## Ô£à Done

### B628 ÔÇö Dedicated canvas right-click menu (never the browser's), with Delete on a deed / parcel `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-reported 2026-07-03 ÔÇö "I should be able to right-click a parcel from a metes-and-bounds and delete itÔÇª it should be a dedicated menu for something on the map. And even a right-click on empty map, catch it before the Chrome menu"; first filed provisionally **B627**, RENUMBERED to **B628** on merge-in of `origin/main` ÔÇö a concurrent session (`claude/chambers-county-gis-down-ttqfnv`) shipped the Chambers-GIS **B627** below; B628 = highest real B# across both files (B627) + 1. Branch `claude/meets-bounds-alignment-9aspnc`, its commits, PR #481, and ALL in-code/test labels + the `verify-b627-context-menu.mjs` harness keep the provisional **B627**.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session.** A right-click **anywhere on the planner canvas now opens OUR menu, never the browser's** ÔÇö the svg root's `onContextMenu` always `preventDefault`s (was: only cancelled an in-progress road draw, so every other right-click leaked the Chrome menu). What the menu offers depends on the target:
- **A markup ÔÇö deed/encumbrance, easement, utility route, traced line, or a neutral line/rect/ellipse/polygon** (wired via `onMarkupContext` on each markup `<g>` + the shared `common` props): **Lock/Unlock** + **Delete**. For a plotted **deed** it also offers **Align to county parcel** (falls back to "Rotate to grid north" with no parcel ÔÇö the B625 action) and Delete reads **"Delete deed + exceptions"**, removing the whole `deedGroup` (boundary + its save-and-except holes) as one unit.
- **A parcel** (existing `parcelMenu`): gained a **Delete parcel** item alongside Merge/Clear (`onParcelContext` now carries the id).
- **Empty canvas**: a **Map** menu ÔÇö **Zoom to fit** + **Paste** (enabled when a shape/overlay is on the clipboard).
- Overlays/elements keep their own menus (each stops propagation before the root handler; the B230 right-click-to-add/delete-vertex capture handler still wins on a selected editable path's edge/corner).
- **Every delete tombstones** (`tombstone(...)` ÔåÆ `deletedIds`) so a cloud/cross-tab union-merge can't resurrect it (B556/B612).
- **Tests:** lint 0 ┬À **2226 tests green** ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b627-context-menu.mjs` **5/5** (right-click a deed opens our Align+Delete menu not the browser's; Delete removes the deed AND its save-and-except hole AND tombstones both; right-click empty canvas opens the Map menu; a canvas `contextmenu` is default-prevented). Added a `data-testid="parcel-outline"` hook (mirrors the B625 `deed-boundary` hook) for the harness.
### B627 ÔÇö Chambers County "can't click / GIS is down": TxGIO disabled the parcels `/query` op ÔÇö route outlines via `/export`, clicks via `/identify` `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high  *(owner-reported 2026-07-03 "figure out why i cant click / why chambers county gis is down"; minted **B627** = highest real B# across both files (B626) + 1 after merging `origin/main` ÔÇö a concurrent session took B625/B626 for the deed-alignment work below; branch `claude/chambers-county-gis-down-ttqfnv`)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped this session.** Chambers County parcels are served by the **TxGIO statewide parcel MapServer** (`feature.geographic.texas.gov/.../Parcels/stratmap_land_parcels_48_most_recent/MapServer/0`), which is also every county's outage fallback (`STATEWIDE_PARCEL_LAYER`). **Root cause (confirmed live):** TxGIO **disabled the layer `/query` + `/find` operations** on that service ÔÇö both now 400 with `"Requested operation is not supported by this service."` (extendedCode -2147220222) ÔÇö even though the layer metadata still advertises `capabilities: Map,Query`. The service's **`/identify` and `/export` ops still work.** Because the app renders parcel outlines with esri-leaflet's **`featureLayer` (which draws by QUERYING)** and identifies a clicked lot with a **point `/query`**, both went dark on Chambers: **no outlines painted AND no lot could be selected** ÔÇö exactly "can't click / GIS is down." Working counties (HCAD/FBCAD, whose `/query` is healthy) were unaffected.
- **The two symptoms, both root-caused to the same dead `/query`:** (1) `makeParcelLayer` ÔåÆ `EL.featureLayer` renders via `/query` ÔåÆ blank Chambers (no lines); (2) `queryAtPoint` ÔåÆ point `/query` ÔåÆ capability error ÔåÆ click selects nothing.
- **Fix ÔÇö outlines via `/export`:** new `makeParcelImageLayer` (an esri `dynamicMapLayer` ÔåÆ server `/export` image, `transparent`+`minZoom`-gated) and a single smart entry point `makeParcelDisplayLayer(url)` that routes **only** the known query-disabled `STATEWIDE_PARCEL_LAYER` to the image overlay; every other (queryable) CAD stays the styleable vector `featureLayer` (which also backs the instant B441 client-side click highlight). Applied at both display sites ÔÇö MapFinder's Select-parcels tool and SitePlanner's in-planner Add-parcel outline. `parcelDisplay.js`.
- **Fix ÔÇö clicks via `/identify`:** `queryAtPoint` now wraps its `/query` in try/catch; on the **specific** capability error (`isQueryCapabilityError`) **and** only for a `/MapServer/<id>` layer, it retries the MapServer **`/identify`** op (`identifyAtPoint`) ÔÇö building a small synthetic map frame around the click and returning the parcel that actually **contains** the point (even-odd PIP) in the same `{geometry:{rings},attributes}` shape. A real timeout/network/other error still surfaces as an outage; FeatureServers are untouched; **self-heals** (the fallback never fires once TxGIO re-enables `/query`). `arcgis.js`. The eager multi-county click path + circuit breaker heal automatically (a successful identify records the source healthy), so the "statewide backup / unavailable" notices clear too.
- **Honest residual (documented, self-healing):** typed **ID/address search** on Chambers stays down until TxGIO restores `/query` (text search needs `/query` or `/find`, both dead ÔÇö `/identify` is spatial-only). The app already degrades to "search unavailable ÔÇö pan/zoom (click the lot) instead," which works. Noted in `counties.js` + surfaced to the owner.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **2222 tests green** (+7 new `queryAtPoint ÔåÆ /identify` fallback tests: capability-error classification, retry-and-select, picks the containing polygon from multiple hits, no-fallback on a non-capability error / on a FeatureServer, null on empty identify) ┬À build green. **The SHIPPED code, run in Node against the LIVE TxGIO service, now selects real Chambers lots** ÔÇö ONEOK Mont Belvieu storage `PROP_ID 46351`, Anahuac ISD `PROP_ID 438`, both with geometry ÔÇö via the identify fallback; **Harris/HCAD confirmed to still use the fast `/query` path** with no needless fallback; the `/export` image returns real transparent parcel linework (200/png). The in-browser click-through (outlines paint + click selects) is owed as **V198** ÔÇö this sandbox's Chromium egress is reset by the agent proxy for every GIS/basemap host (`net::ERR_CONNECTION_RESET`), so the live map can't be driven here; harness `gis-verify/chambers-parcel-click-verify.mjs` is ready for planyr.io/CI.

### B626 ÔÇö "94.91-acre" Grand Port deed plotted as a sliver ÔÇö RESOLVED as stale data, not a current bug `[Site Planner]` (bug/investigation)  *(filed + resolved 2026-07-02 same session; minted **B626** = B625 + 1)*
`[x]` **Resolved ÔÇö no code change needed.** The saved Grand Port project (`sites` id `smqfy2r7pdec`) held a deed markup labelled "94.91 acres" (`e79374qioqog`) that plotted as a 0.38-ac / 119-ft-wide sliver. Owner supplied both source `.docx` files (the "81.95 Save & Except" = Tract 1 94.53 + the 12.584 county carve-out, and the "OLD 94.91 Acre MBs 21484 V3" parent tract). Ran **the exact app path** (`docxText.documentXmlToText` ÔåÆ `metesAndBounds.parseTracts` ÔåÆ `callsToPath`) on both:
- **81.95 file** ÔåÆ Tract 1 (13 courses, closes, 95.40 ac) + 12.584 save-and-except (7 courses, closes, 12.58 ac). Correct.
- **94.91 V3 file** ÔåÆ single tract, **15 courses, closes (30 ft misclosure), 96.03 ac**. Correct ÔÇö every course matched the deed line-by-line (two N87┬░E legs, the "five courses" CenterPoint line, N90┬░E 50ÔÇ▓, S00┬░03ÔÇ▓34ÔÇ│W 1053.10ÔÇ▓, the "four courses" down Reserve A, the Kilgore Pkwy curve, N02┬░39ÔÇ▓44ÔÇ│W 2477.79ÔÇ▓ close).
The **saved sliver's `calls` do NOT match the current parse** (e.g. sliver `N90E 10ÔÇ▓` + `S00┬░03ÔÇ▓34ÔÇ│W 1023.10ÔÇ▓` vs current `N90E 50ÔÇ▓` + `S00┬░03ÔÇ▓34ÔÇ│W 1053.10ÔÇ▓`, plus sliver-only `S67┬░20ÔÇ▓49ÔÇ│W`, `N11┬░34ÔÇ▓10ÔÇ│W 307.52ÔÇ▓` legs) ÔÇö so it was produced by an **older parser/extractor version and/or an earlier "V#" of the deed** (the file is literally "ÔÇªV3"). The current reader is not buggy on these files. **Fix for the owner = re-plot** (delete the stale sliver, re-drop the deed); the ~1% area-over + ~30 ft misclosure is curve-chord + the deed's own real misclosure, honestly surfaced, not an error. (Distinct from B625, the alignment fix, which shipped separately.)

### B625 ÔÇö Plotted metes-and-bounds deed doesn't line up with the county parcel (basis-of-bearings rotation) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-07-02 on the Grand Port project ÔÇö "when I upload the metes and bounds, it doesn't align really with my parcel ÔÇª the metes and bounds and the parcel the GIS data pulls should be aligned equally"; minted **B625** = highest real B# across both files (B624) + 1; branch `claude/meets-bounds-alignment-9aspnc`)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session.** **Root cause (diagnosed, not guessed):** the deed plotter (`callsToPath` in `metesAndBounds.js`) dead-reckons each bearing as an azimuth measured from the plan's "up" = **true north** (the county GIS parcel and the aerial are true-north ÔÇö `arcgis.js` linearizes lon/lat north-up). But a Texas survey states its bearings on **Texas State Plane GRID north** (or an older record bearing), which differs from true north by the meridian **convergence angle**. At the Grand Port site (Ôëê29.80 N, 95.83 W) that convergence is **1.545┬░** (computed from the shared EPSG:2278 projection), which fans out to **~90 ft** of drift over a 3,325-ft boundary line ÔÇö exactly the visible misalignment. The plotted encumbrance was also *move-only*, so the ~1.5┬░ rotation couldn't be corrected by hand.
- **The fix ÔÇö snap the deed onto the county parcel.** New pure module `lib/deedAlign.js`: `solveDeedAlignment(deedRing, parcelRing)` solves the **rigid** rotation+translation (scale **LOCKED** to 1 ÔÇö the deed's surveyed dimensions are ground truth, never rubber-sheeted to a generalized GIS outline) that best overlays the plotted deed on the held parcel, via a full ┬▒180┬░ coarse angle sweep + fine refine minimising symmetric nearest-vertex RMS (copes with any basis of bearings AND a reversed winding). `gridConvergenceDeg(lat,lon)` gives the theoretical grid-north correction for the no-parcel fallback. A deed + its **save-and-except holes** share one `deedGroup` and move as one rigid body, so the holes stay seated in the boundary.
- **UI (SitePlanner.jsx):** right after plotting, if a parcel is loaded and the deed sits >0.25┬░ off it, a one-click **"Align to parcel"** banner appears with the measured offset; the selected deed's panel carries a durable **"Align to county parcel"** button (falls back to **"Rotate to grid north"** with no parcel loaded) + a manual **Rotate┬░** stepper. Every path reports honestly ÔÇö the applied rotation and the residual fit ("agree to within XÔÇ▓"), flagging a loose match rather than silently forcing a bad fit. The deed's hatch fill is now `pointerEvents="all"` so it grabs anywhere inside (was only on the painted hatch lines).
- **Tests:** `test/deedAlign.test.js` (10) ÔÇö the fit recovers a known rotation+translation with ~0 residual, is provably rigid (never scales an 800ÔÇ▓ deed up to a 1000ÔÇ▓ parcel), handles reversed winding; convergence is ~+1.5┬░ at Katy, vanishes on the 99┬░W central meridian, flips sign west of it, and its sign is cross-checked against the projection. **2217 tests green** ┬À build green.
- **Verified end-to-end headless** (`ui-audit/verify-deed-align.mjs`, logged-out): seeded a georeferenced site with a parcel + a deed rotated 2.2┬░ and shoved 365ÔÇ▓ off it (with a save-and-except hole); selecting the deed shows the Align button, clicking it drops the boundaryÔåÆparcel residual **365ÔÇ▓ ÔåÆ 0.00ÔÇ▓**, the hole moves rigidly with it (0.0ÔÇ▓ off), no console errors. The signed-in confirmation on Michael's real Grand Port deed + FBCAD parcel is logged as **V197**.

### B620 ÔÇö Inline line labels (text riding along a line) as an alternative to callouts `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-6"; deferred that session as not-urgent, then picked up + expanded by the owner 2026-07-02; minted **B620** = B619 + 1; branch `claude/site-planner-backlog-impl-rm7ma4`)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** A line/polyline/road/easement can carry an optional `inlineLabel` that renders **along the feature itself** (e.g. `18" SANITARY SEWER` on the line) ÔÇö no separate callout needed. Owner-expanded scope + behavior:
- **Applies to drawn lines/polylines, roads, AND easements**, with **per-feature label density** (owner: "a sewer is small and needs more references, a road far fewer, an easement in between"): the label **repeats along the path** at a per-type spacing ÔÇö line/polyline **150 ft**, easement **350 ft**, road **700 ft** (always ÔëÑ1, centered, when shorter than the spacing). Pure module helpers `inlineLabelPlaces`/`inlineLabelEls`/`inlineLabelAnchorFeet` + `INLINE_LABEL_SPACING`.
- Each instance is **rotated to its local segment**, **auto-flipped** into `[-90,90]` (never upside-down ÔÇö `((a%180)+180)%180; if(a>90)a-=180`), **offset above the centerline**, in the feature's **own color + a white halo**, **LOD-gated** (hides when the on-screen segment can't fit the text; returns on zoom-in), and ÔÇö unlike selection chrome ÔÇö **appears in PNG/PDF exports** (no `data-export="skip"`).
- **Edited two ways:** a panel **"Inline label"** field in each feature's Properties, **and double-click the feature** to type in place (`editInline` state + `beginEditInline`/`commitEditInline`, mirroring the callout editor). Double-clicking a centerline **road** opens the label editor; its type/actions menu stays on **right-click**.
- **Non-sticky by construction** (the key trap): every writer patches the ONE feature (`setMarkups`/`setSelEl`/`commitEditInline`), never `mkStyle`/`liveMarkup`, so a typed label can't bleed into the next drawn shape. Panel fields push **one undo frame per edit** (onFocus), not per keystroke (a review follow-up). **No schema/migration** ÔÇö `inlineLabel` rides through verbatim (`markups: arr(...)`; roads spread through `migrateRoad`).
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **2206 tests** green (+ `bugHuntGuards.test.js` B620 guard: auto-flip math, per-type spacing, own-color+halo, non-sticky, no export-skip, all 3 render sites) ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b620-inline-label.mjs` **12/12** (render on all types, angleÔêê[-90,90] incl. the flipped line, own color + white halo, density gradient 9-line vs 2-road, export survival, LOD hide/restore, double-click editor, typing updates, non-sticky). An adversarial review found no high/medium defects; its two low findings (per-keystroke undo on the new panel fields; boundary-easement label skipping the closing edge) were fixed.

### B624 ÔÇö Typed Start/Finish on a weekend (or holiday) rolls forward to the next working day + toast `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-07-02 immediately after B621/B622 shipped ÔÇö "can I input a weekend? I shouldn't be able to, it should just roll forward with a toast letting me know"; minted **B624** = highest real B# across both files (B623) + 1; same branch, fresh follow-up PR after #472 merged)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session.** The grid Start/Finish cells accepted any parsed date, so a task could sit on a Saturday/Sunday ÔÇö wrong for a working-day schedule. Now the grid date-commit path (`applyUpdate`) runs the parsed date through **`rollForwardToWorkday`** (the B621 helper ÔÇö skips Sat/Sun + `HOLIDAY_SET`); if it moved, a **toast names the reason** ("Start/Finish moved to M/D/YY ÔÇö you picked a weekend" / "ÔÇªa holiday") and the working-day date is what's stored. A working-day entry is unchanged (no toast). Composes with the locks: a rolled Finish still pins as the B622 hard-finish (on a working day); a rolled Start still pins (B615/pinnedStart). The finish-before-start guard runs on the rolled date. The Gantt has no date-editing drag path (bars render from start/end; only columns drag), so the typed cell is the only date input ÔÇö fully covered.
- **Tests:** anti-drift source guard (roll-forward applied on input + the reason-naming toast) + a runtime `rollForwardToWorkday` check (weekendÔåÆMon, weekday-holidayÔåÆforward, working-day unchanged) in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. lint 0 ┬À **2205 tests green** ┬À build green ┬À both babel blocks parse via esbuild.
- **Verified:** logic green here; the live click-through (type a Saturday into Start/Finish ÔåÆ it jumps to Monday with the toast) is folded into **V196** (the CDN-loaded scheduler can't render in the sandbox).

### B621 ÔÇö Duration input model: calendar-real months/years, working-day weeks & days `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-1"; first filed provisionally **B615** but RENUMBERED to **B621** on merge-in of `origin/main` ÔÇö a concurrent session (branch `claude/site-planner-backlog-impl-rm7ma4`) had already shipped B615ÔÇôB620; B621 = highest real B# across both files (B620) + 1. The branch `claude/duration-input-working-calendar-m4chdv`, its commits, PR #472, and ALL in-code/test labels keep the provisional **B615**. Dedup: NOT B159 ÔÇö export-only Gantt density; NOT B256 ÔÇö the O(n┬▓) recompute perf task.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session.** Typed durations now resolve the way a developer means them ÔÇö two measuring sticks keyed to the unit ÔÇö with both the parsed duration and the computed end visible.
- **Recorded BEFORE editing (the two symbols the owner asked me to inspect, not guess):**
  - **(a) current duration-parse + end-date derivation:** `duration` was stored as a plain **integer = inclusive count of working (business) days** ÔÇö no unit concept; `0` = milestone. Parsed in the grid via `applyUpdate` ÔåÆ `parseInt(val)||0` (so `3w`ÔåÆ3). End derived by `calcEnd(start,dur) = addBD(start, dur-1)` (skip Sat/Sun + `HOLIDAY_SET`). End is re-derived from duration in `updateTask`, `previewProject`, `cascadeDates`, `normalizeToV6`, and the holiday-recompute effect. Migration used a boolean `_v6` flag (not a numeric version), idempotent via early-return.
  - **(b) the start-date lock:** the `pinnedStart` boolean ÔÇö `cascadeDates` does NOT re-derive a `pinnedStart` task's start from predecessors (`if (!preds.length || t.pinnedStart) ÔÇª`); it's set implicitly when a Start is typed/dragged and cleared when predecessors are edited. **UI:** an amber **padlock SVG** on the Start cell shown only when pinned; click ÔåÆ unpin + re-apply predecessors.
- **What shipped (mirroring, not guessing):** a small **pure, dependency-free** helper set in `public/sequence/index.html` (mirrored VERBATIM into `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, the tested copy): `parseDurationInput` (3w/3 weeks ┬À 15d/15 days ┬À 2mo/2 months ┬À 1y/1 year ┬À bare numberÔåÆdays ┬À emptyÔåÆmilestone ┬À **junk ÔåÆ {error} so the field shows a visible inline error, never a silent 0**), `addCalendarMonths` (end-of-month clamp: Jan 31 +1mo ÔåÆ Feb 28/29), `rollForwardToWorkday` (plain forward off a weekend/holiday ÔÇö no month-crossing guard, owner decision), `workdaysBetween` (**closed form** ÔÇö full-weeks├ù5 + partial-week remainder ÔêÆ HOLIDAY_SET weekday hits; NO day-by-day loop), and `resolveDuration` (d/w = working days via `calcEnd`; mo/y = calendar add + roll-forward, working-day count **derived** off the span). Week = **5 working days, hard-coded** (6-day weeks are B617, deliberately NOT parameterized here). No monthsÔåÆdays factor anywhere. Reuses the existing `HOLIDAY_SET`/`ensureHolidays` path ÔÇö no new holiday list.
- **Model:** each task keeps its canonical working-day `duration` (every existing consumer unchanged) + the typed intent `durUnit`(`d`|`w`|`mo`|`y`) `durValue`. `cascadeDates`/`rollupParentDates`/`updateTask`/`previewProject`/new-task factory/holiday-recompute all made unit-aware. Duration cell renders the unit (`fmtTaskDuration`) with a derived-working-days tooltip for mo/y; the end date stays visible in the Finish column.
- **Migration `_v6 ÔåÆ _v7` (`normalizeToV7`, idempotent):** stamps `durUnit:'d'`/`durValue:duration` on every legacy task; re-derives via `resolveDuration('d')` which **equals** the old `calcEnd` ÔåÆ zero end-date shift by construction. Wired into all 4 load-pipeline sites. **Ran the real before/after diff on the live production dataset** (scheduler Supabase `hs-v1`, **667 tasks**): **0 real shifts** ÔÇö the only 5 flagged were **parents** rolled up from an intentional Sunday year-end milestone, whose end comes from rollup (unchanged), not `calcEnd`. Reproduced data-safe in `ui-audit/stress/verify-b615-migration.mjs` (18,000 no-shift pairs + parent-rollup + idempotency + the new units).
- **Accepted-on-purpose divergences (not bugs):** the same "7 months" on a Jan vs Jul start yields slightly different working-day counts; "4 weeks" (20 wd) Ôëá "1 month" (calendar).
- **Tests:** +30 in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js` (parser, calendar clamp, roll-forward, closed-form count cross-checked vs a day-by-day reference across 400 holiday-crossing spans, resolver, unit-aware cascade, migration idempotency + no-shift) + anti-drift source/mirror guards. lint 0 ┬À **2201 tests green** ┬À build green ┬À both babel blocks parse via esbuild.
- **Verified:** everything runnable here is green. The live in-app click-through can't run in the sandbox (the scheduler's React/Babel/Supabase CDNs are egress-blocked ÔåÆ `net::ERR_CONNECTION_RESET`), so it's logged as **V196** (harness `ui-audit/verify-b615-b616-scheduler.mjs` ready for an unrestricted browser).

### B622 ÔÇö Finish-date entry sets a hard-finish constraint with a lock toggle `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-2"; renumbered **B616 ÔåÆ B622** on the same `origin/main` merge-in (B615/B616 were taken by the concurrent Site Planner batch); B622 = B621 + 1. Branch/commits/PR #472 + in-code/test labels keep the provisional **B616**.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session.** Typing a Finish date pins it as a **hard finish constraint** and shows a **padlock toggle** on the Finish field ÔÇö the exact icon + interaction as the existing Start lock (which I inspected first: the amber padlock SVG on the Start cell, driven by `pinnedStart`; see B615's recorded findings). The two locks are now visually and behaviorally identical.
- **Semantics:** a locked finish is an absolute date the schedule is measured against ÔÇö the `pinnedEnd` task's `end` is a **fixed point** in `cascadeDates` (successors cascade from it; it never moves when the predecessor chain slips). The start back-calcs from the locked end to preserve the working-day duration (`startForEnd`, the working-day inverse of `calcEnd`). **Precedence:** the locked finish is the constraint; the duration/predecessor-derived finish is **checked against it** ÔÇö if the chain forces a start too late to fit the duration before the locked finish, the task is flagged `finishConflict` **loudly** (a red banner listing the tasks + a red ÔÜá on the row) and the end is **never** silently overwritten or exceeded ÔÇö the user resolves it (move the finish, shorten the duration, or change the dependency).
- **Implementation:** `pinnedEnd` mirrors `pinnedStart` ÔÇö set when a Finish is typed (`updateTask`/`previewProject`), cleared by the padlock click (which re-flows the finish from start+duration). The Finish cell renders the same padlock SVG + tooltip; a conflict adds the ÔÜá + danger color. The holiday-recompute + all load paths preserve a locked finish (never re-derive its end). Confirmed the interaction with `cascadeDates` so the locked finish participates as a fixed point, not a floating output.
- **Tests:** covered by the B615 batch ÔÇö `startForEnd` inverse, pinnedEnd no-preds (end held, start back-calc, no conflict), a conflicting predecessor (conflict flagged, lock held), a non-conflicting predecessor (no flag), and a successor cascading from the locked finish; + anti-drift guards for the lock icon, unlock path, and banner. lint 0 ┬À 2201 green ┬À build green.
- **Verified:** logic green here; the live click-through (type a finish ÔåÆ padlock; slip a predecessor ÔåÆ banner; unlock) is part of **V196** (sandbox can't render the CDN-loaded scheduler).

### B615 ÔÇö Label the color swatches (Text / Fill / Outline) + clarify the callout icon controls `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-1"; minted **B615** = highest real B# across both files (B614) + 1; branch `claude/site-planner-backlog-impl-rm7ma4`)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** The three callout color inputs carried only hover `title=` tooltips. Now each swatch shows a persistent caption ÔÇö **Text / Fill / Outline** ÔÇö beneath it (plus a **Size** caption on the text-size field), and the markup + element panels' fill/stroke fields were relabelled to the same **Fill / Outline** wording (was "Fill color / Line color"). The cryptic align glyphs `ÔñÖ Ôëí ÔñÜ` became clear **Ôçñ / Ôëú / ÔçÑ** with `aria-label`s. Rows stay compact (9.5px caption). `SitePlanner.jsx`. lint 0 ┬À 2173 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b615-b619.mjs` (captions present).

### B616 ÔÇö Callout inline text editor: WYSIWYG with equidistant per-axis margins `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-2"; minted **B616** = B615 + 1; same branch)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** The editor was a fixed 200├ù64 box with flat `5px 7px` padding, so text hugged the top-left. It now mirrors the committed render exactly: auto-sizes to the **live** text with the same `zk`/`charW`/`lineH`/`padX`/`padY` formula, symmetric `padY`(top/bottom)/`padX`(left/right) padding, `wrap="off"` + `white-space:pre` so a line never wraps differently than the non-wrapping committed SVG text. No jump between editing and committed. **Review follow-ups (adversarial pass):** a usable **min floor** (64├ù30 px) so an empty/new or zoomed-way-out edit box can't collapse to a few px (the floor only lifts sub-64px content, where the committed callout is itself tiny ÔÇö no perceptible jump); the active-edit ring is an **`outline`, not a `border`**, so it doesn't consume the content box (a 2px border shrank each axis by 4px and clipped the last line / caret). Headless-verified: left==right padding, top==bottom padding, box auto-sized (w=803,h=156 for the sample, not 200├ù64).

### B617 ÔÇö Line stroke weight holds constant relative to the drawing across zoom (no ballooning on zoom-out) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-3"; minted **B617** = B616 + 1; same branch)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** New pure `strokeZoom(base, zk)` = `clamp(base * zk, 0.6, base*3.5)` with `zk = view.ppf/0.35` (the callout scale). Applied to the stroke-only linear features that ballooned on zoom-out: markup line/polyline, utility routes (utilRoute centerline+corridor, traced/infwater ÔÇö the 18ÔÇ│ sanitary-sewer case), easement + encumbrance strokes/spines, the property (parcel) outline, and roads-as-lines (centerline-road pavement edge + curb stripes, legacy rect-road curbs). Pond (`cartoWater`) + building filled-area outlines and the surface-fill edges keep their deliberate fixed pixel weight (the fill carries the shape) ÔÇö including **polygon-drawn** area elements (building/paving/landscape via the `el.points` branch), which an adversarial-review pass caught being over-scaled and reverted to fixed. Headless: line width == `strokeZoom(2, zk)` at two zoom levels, shrinks on zoom-out, polygon building outline stays fixed, and export weights stay bounded (`printSheet` clamp holds). Second harness `ui-audit/verify-b617-b619-fixes.mjs`.

### B618 ÔÇö Numeric fields: up/down steppers + arrow-key nudge `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-4"; minted **B618** = B617 + 1; same branch. Generalizes the B463 `RotationStepper` spinner/arrow-key pattern to the shared `NumInput`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** `NumInput` gained an **opt-in** `step` (+ optional `coarse`) prop: when set it renders Ôû▓/Ôû╝ spinner buttons and handles ArrowUp/Down (Shift = coarse) while focused, nudging about the committed value (rounded to kill float dust, clamped like a typed commit, commits immediately via `onCommit`). No `step` ÔåÆ the bare `<input>` is unchanged, so existing callers/layout/commit-on-blur/NaN┬▒Infinity rejection are untouched. Enabled on line weight (0.5), text size (1), padding X/Y (1), line spacing (0.1), markup + element width/height/length/depth. Headless: Increase/Decrease buttons present in the panel.

### B619 ÔÇö Neutral handle-based selection (retire the orange recolor) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-5"; minted **B619** = B618 + 1; same branch. Generalizes the B231 pond / B567 neutral-markup no-recolor precedent; distinct from the B587/B588 shared multi-select chrome, which already doesn't recolor.)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** Selecting an object no longer recolors its own stroke/leader to the app accent (the "purple callout turned orange" bug). Every recolor site (`isSel ? PAL.accent` on callout border/leader, markup/utilRoute/traced/encumbrance/easement strokes, parcel outline, and `isSel ? selStroke` on elements/roads in `renderElPx`) now keeps the object's authored color ÔÇö so a live color-picker change shows instantly. Selection is signalled by **white handles with a thin blue (`#2563eb`) border + a thin blue outline** (real hexes, never `var()` ÔÇö the SVG exports to PNG/PDF): box corners/edges for callouts/markup boxes/rect elements (`elSelOutline` is the one outline path, covering locked + polygon elements too), vertex handles for lines/polylines/polygons, a soft blue halo for the move-only utility polylines, and a blue tip grip on callout leaders. All chrome carries `data-export="skip"`; headless confirms 0 blue chrome survives the export strip while the purple content is kept. A **LOCKED road** (grips suppressed, and roads are excluded from the bbox outline since a box around a line is wrong) gets a soft blue halo **along its geometry** so it still reads as selected ÔÇö an adversarial-review catch, headless-verified. Measures stay accent-coloured by design (a redline tool identity, not a per-object color). Source guards added in `bugHuntGuards.test.js` (B617 `strokeZoom`, B619 no-recolor).

### B614 ÔÇö Rebuild the Scheduler task-notes panel: fix "editing a note dismisses the panel" + dated-grid running-log layout `[Scheduler / Notes]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-1" with a full rebuild spec + agreed mockup; minted **B614** = highest real B# across both files (B613) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B613** that a concurrent `main` took for the Fort Bend parcel-layer fix; the branch `claude/scheduler-notes-panel-rebuild-8aowsy`, its commits, PR #470, the in-code `B613 rebuild` labels, the `ui-audit/verify-b613-notes-panel.mjs` harness, and the `schedulerEngine.test.js` "D2" guard all keep the provisional **B613**. Rebuilds `NotesModal` in `public/sequence/index.html`.)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** lint 0 ┬À 2171 tests green ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b613-notes-panel.mjs` **16/16** (mounts the real NotesModal in isolation and drives every interaction). Signed-in cloud round-trip + the live planyr.io before/after logged as **V195** (the scheduler persists to its own Supabase, unreachable logged-out).
- **Triggering bug (repro ÔåÆ expected):** Schedule ÔåÆ open a task's notes ÔåÆ click an existing note's date (or its text) to change it ÔåÆ the whole panel closed ("takes me out"). Expected + now delivered: clicking a date or text edits it **in place** with the panel staying open; the panel closes only on Ô£ò or a deliberate backdrop click.
- **Confirmed mechanism (read the real handlers, not guessed):** cause (b) from the spec ÔÇö an edit-commits-**and-tears-down-on-blur** pattern. Editing a note put its `<textarea>` in `autoFocus` with `onBlur ÔåÆ saveEdit() ÔåÆ setEditId(null)`; moving focus into that row's date `<input>` blurred the textarea, committed, and unmounted the sibling date input mid-click ÔÇö the `mouseup`/`click` then landed on the backdrop (whose `onClick={onClose}` fired because inner `stopPropagation` can't stop a click on an element the render just removed / a native-picker portal click). Exactly the "native-picker/portal click reaching the backdrop" case. Fixed **by construction**.
- **The rebuild (dated-grid running log; the whole panel replaced):** two-column rows ÔÇö compact **M/D** date in a fixed left rail (~54px, plain Schedule-purple `#534AB7` text ÔÇö the AA-safe shade of the module accent `#7F77DD` ÔÇö click to edit), hairline vertical divider, note body; hairline row dividers, no per-note card fill. **Descending (newest first), no sort toggle.** **Add-at-top** composer prepends, dates to today, focuses the body (honors `initChar` quick-entry). **Inline CRUD that never strands you:** click date ÔåÆ native `<input type=date>` (pops wider than the rail ÔÇö fine); click body ÔåÆ edit in place; delete reveals on row hover; commit-on-Enter/blur **saves and stays open**. **Guarded dismiss:** closes only on Ô£ò, on Escape (which first backs out an active edit / a composer date-picker), or on a click whose `mousedown` **and** target are the backdrop element itself (`e.target === backdrop` + a press-started-on-backdrop ref) ÔÇö never a bubbled child / native-picker portal click.
- **Storage contract kept intact:** notes stay `{id, date:"YYYY-MM-DD", text}`; `toShortDate`/`parseFlexDate` untouched; the rail gets a **display-only** `noteShortMD` (M/D) formatter; the native picker reads/writes the stored ISO directly. New sub-components (`NoteRow`, `AddNoteComposer`) defined at **module scope** (B247/B258 focus-loss trap avoided). Every edit maps by note **id** (`notes.map(x => x.id === cur.id ? ÔÇª)`), so an edit structurally can't scramble the other notes ÔÇö the modern successor to the removed free-text by-text matching. All CRUD rides the existing `updateTask ÔåÆ setData` autosave/cloudSync path (B210/B212/B566) + the `<script id="planar-data">` self-save ÔÇö neither bypassed; a failed save surfaces via the existing badge.
- **Also:** the single running-log view supersedes the old **"View as: Free text"** mode, so the now-orphaned toggle was removed from the `NotesColHeader` `┬À┬À┬À` menu (the **Text size** preference is kept and still honored per-note). The drift-guard `schedulerEngine.test.js` "D2" was repointed from the removed free-text matcher to the new id-keyed-edit + guarded-dismiss invariants (the file is never edited to make a test green ÔÇö the feature it pinned was deliberately removed).
- **Dedup:** NOT B253 (the NOW/new-note date stamp ÔÇö left intact), NOT B247 (export-modal focus loss ÔÇö reused its module-scope hoist), NOT the Doc-Review `sheetNotes.js`/`aggregateNotes`/B350 readers (unrelated). **Out of scope (deliberate):** no per-note author/timestamp; no project-level notes log.

### B613 ÔÇö Fort Bend parcel layer: switch off FBCAD's chronically-down self-hosted server, onto FBCAD's own Esri-hosted (ArcGIS Online) parcel service `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug ÔÇö recurring reliability)  *(owner-reported 2026-07-02 ÔÇö "figure out why Fort Bend Appraisal District [layer] is not working wellÔÇª I feel like we've had so much trouble with Fort BendÔÇª maybe we just cache it"; minted **B613** = highest real B# across both files (B612) + 1; branch `claude/fort-bend-appraisal-issues-8j5cp3`. Closes the root cause behind the whole FBCAD trouble family ÔÇö B137/B244/B245/B382/B469.)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** Diagnosed the recurring Fort Bend flakiness to ONE root cause and fixed it at the source rather than adding another layer of fallback. **Every past Fort Bend incident (B137 "can't click a visible lot", B244/B245 the 45s freeze + statewide fallback, B382 "takes forever then won't click", B469 CORS) traces to the app depending directly on FBCAD's SELF-HOSTED server `gis.fbcad.org/serverarcgis2` ÔÇö an on-prem ArcGIS server with no CDN/redundancy that repeatedly 503s, times out, and isn't CORS-clean, and has already migrated servers once (the "serverarcgis2" in the path).** Live-probed 2026-07-02: `gis.fbcad.org` resets the TLS connection on every path (~11.5s hang ÔåÆ reset) ÔÇö the origin server itself, not a proxy denial. Caching alone (the owner's suggestion) would only smooth the DISPLAY tiles; it can't fix click/search when the source's query is down, and the statewide TxGIO fallback's own `/query` was ALSO failing at probe time ("operation not supported"), so both live sources could fail a click simultaneously.
- **The fix ÔÇö repoint Fort Bend to FBCAD's OWN data on Esri's ArcGIS Online cloud.** Research + independent live-probe found FBCAD publishes its parcels as a hosted feature service on its own AGOL org (`services2.arcgis.com/D4saGHECICkCeoJm/.../FBCAD_Public_Data/FeatureServer/0`, layer 0 "FBCAD Public Parcel Data"). It's the SAME authoritative FBCAD data but Esri-hosted (reliable, CORS-open, no key), holds all ~385k county parcels, is **refreshed daily** (last edit 2026-07-02 at probe time ÔÇö fresher than the statewide layer or any snapshot), is natively **EPSG:2278** (State Plane feet ÔÇö our coordinate spine, no reprojection), and carries the full native appraisal schema (OWNERNAME, LANDVALUE/IMPVALUE/TOTALVALUE as real numbers, SITUS, LEGAL, LANDSIZEAC, YEARBUILT, land-use codes, exemptions, deed refs). Verified by direct curl: metadata (capabilities=Query), a Fort Bend point-click query (returns lot R40594 with polygon geometry in feet), and an owner-name search all succeed.
- **Single switch point (`lib/counties.js`):** `COUNTIES.fortbend` (search) and `COUNTIES_MAP.fortbend` (display + click) both repointed at the hosted `FeatureServer/0`; `idField`/`addrField` hints set to `QUICKREFID`/`SITUS` (both auto-detected anyway by `detectField`). `resolveLayerUrl`/`lookupParcels`/`makeParcelLayer`/`queryFeatures` all handle a FeatureServer layer natively ÔÇö no other code change. The old self-hosted URL is retired as primary (kept in a comment). The statewide TxGIO layer **stays** the automatic outage fallback (`statewideFallbackFor` unchanged) and the display hang-guard + circuit breaker (B244/B382) remain the safety net.
- **`lib/appraisal.js`:** added `land_?size_?ac` to the Acreage matcher (FBCAD `LANDSIZEAC`, acres ÔÇö not `LANDSIZEFT`) and `land_?state_?code` to Land use (FBCAD `Land_State_Code`) so the curated appraisal panel fully populates from the new schema; the TxGIO/HCAD mappings are untouched (proven by the unchanged `test/appraisal.test.js`).
- **Verification harnesses** `gis-verify/fbcad-outage-fallback-verify.mjs` + `ui-audit/verify-parcel-resilience.mjs` repointed to the new host; new `gis-verify/fbcad-hosted-primary-verify.mjs` added for the happy-path click.
- lint 0 ┬À **2171 tests green** (counties + appraisal suites unchanged and passing) ┬À build green. The live REST round-trips (metadata + FB point query + owner search) were confirmed here via the agent proxy. The in-browser click-through could NOT be run here ÔÇö the sandbox's Chromium egress currently resets ALL external GIS hosts (services2.arcgis.com AND feature.geographic.texas.gov AND arcgisonline.com alike ÔÇö an environment egress limit, not a code issue), so the browser can't reach the layer ÔåÆ **V194**.

### B612 ÔÇö Delete-tombstone completeness: truck-court / dock-zone, measure, parcel-split, identify-toggle, and callout-blank deletes now record a tombstone (closes the "deleted item comes back + false 'changed in another session' loop" class) `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-07-02 on planyr.io ÔÇö "I remove the truck court on the left sideÔÇª the trailer parking below the building comes back, and the 'changed in another session / Take over editing' banner keeps re-appearingÔÇª I feel like I've dealt with this somewhere else too, with another element"; minted **B612** = highest real B# across both files (B611) + 1; branch `claude/truck-cord-deletion-conflict-1bjjg1`. Extends B276/B556/B596 ÔÇö the SAME tombstone mechanism, applied to the delete handlers that still lacked it.)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02.** Root cause (confirmed in code): `mergeSiteContent` (siteModel.js) unions every drawn collection by id, and only a `deletedIds` tombstone keeps a deletion deleted; separately `wouldThinClobber` (cloudSync.js, B459) blocks a save that drops ÔëÑ2 items with no tombstone to "explain" the drop ÔåÆ the false "changed in another session" banner + the take-overÔåÆresurrectÔåÆre-prompt loop. B556/B596 wired tombstones into `deleteSel`/`deleteDrawing`/`mergeParcels`/`removeParcelById`/`removeOverlay`, but the dock-zone + several sibling delete handlers were never covered ÔÇö exactly the owner's "same issue on another element." (The in-tab write-race half the original handoff feared was ALREADY fixed: B529 `makeWriteSerializer` serializes per-site writes; B480 `fetchSiteForReconcile` does the silent single-row take-over reconcile ÔÇö so the banner the owner still saw was purely the thin-clobber guard, not the CAS version race. No cloudSync change needed.)
Fixes (all reuse the existing `tombstone()` + undo/history plumbing):
  - **Dock-zone deletes** ÔÇö extracted `killSetWithChildren` (the transitive cascade set) out of `removeWithChildren`; `removeFeature` (the owner's "Remove truck court (+ outer)"), `removeOuterDockZone`, and `removeAllDogEars` now tombstone the WHOLE cascade (court ÔåÆ its trailer ÔåÆ its buffer); `removeOuterZoneOnSide` is covered via `removeFeature`.
  - **`splitParkingRows`** ÔÇö the source field is replaced by fresh-uid pieces; tombstone it so a merge can't resurrect the pre-split field overlapping them.
  - **`changeBuildingDock`** ÔÇö a preset change that orphans a side's apron (crossÔåÆsingle/none) tombstones the pruned zones.
  - **Measure deletes** (adversarial-review finding, HIGH) ÔÇö measures carry a stable `uid()` id and count in `contentCount`, so a ÔëÑ2 multi-select delete reproduced the exact false-conflict loop; `deleteSel` (multi + single branches) and both inline "├ù" handlers now tombstone the removed measure ids (resolved from the index). Corrected the stale "measures are index-keyed with no stable id" comment.
  - **`performSplit`** (parcel split) ÔÇö tombstone the source parcel (twin of `splitParkingRows`).
  - **`quickAddAt`** (Identify-tool toggle-off) ÔÇö tombstone the removed lot(s); a multipart lot's ÔëÑ2 drop also stops tripping the guard.
  - **`commitEditCallout`** ÔÇö blanking an existing (already-synced) callout deletes it; tombstone so a merge can't resurrect it.
Undo/redo unaffected: every site calls `pushHistory()` before `tombstone()`, and the history snapshot carries `deletedIds` (undo drops the tombstone + restores the item; redo re-applies). Tests: +2 merge-resurrection cases (dock-zone cascade) + measure / callout / split cases in `storage.test.js`; +4 thin-guard cases (truck-court cascade + 3-measure multi-delete, pre-fix-blocked / post-fix-allowed) in `cloudThinGuard.test.js`. lint 0 ┬À 2171 tests green ┬À build green. Headless logged-out `ui-audit/verify-truckcourt-tombstone.mjs` 6/6 (removeFeature + removeOuterDockZone: every removed id lands in `deletedIds`). A 5-lens adversarial review confirmed the sibling findings and cleared the fix itself. Signed-in end-to-end (banner no longer fires; deletions stay gone after Take over / reload) ÔåÆ **V193**.

### B609 ÔÇö Remove bottom status banner; relocate coordinate readout to floating bottom-left chip `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-7"; minted **B609** = B603 + 6)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02 (branch `claude/site-planner-rail-reorganization-5jmneg`).** Removed the full-width bottom status bar; replaced with a floating dark glass chip pinned at `left:10, bottom:8` inside the canvas area (position:absolute, pointerEvents:none). Shows live EPSG:2278 easting + northing in US survey feet: `projectToGrid(origin.lat, origin.lon)` memoized as `gridOrigin`, then `gridOrigin.x + cursor.x` / `gridOrigin.y + cursor.y` at each cursor move. Chip is suppressed when `cursor` or `gridOrigin` is null (no location set). The px-ratio, the Resize/Rotate hint text, the acreage span, and the ? button were all dropped with the banner. The calibration badge (left:56, bottom:40) and north/scalebar plates (bottom:40) remain at their existing bottom:40 position ÔÇö they don't conflict with the chip's bottom:8 band. Import: `projectToGrid` from `src/shared/coordinates/index.js`. lint 0 ┬À 2148 tests green ┬À build green. Signed-in coordinate display ÔåÆ **V190**.

### B604 ÔÇö Tool rail density pass: kill dead top padding; collapse two-line rows to one line; tighten rhythm `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-02 as "NEW-1"; minted **B604** = B603 + 1)*
`[x]` **Shipped 2026-07-02 (branch `claude/site-planner-rail-reorganization-5jmneg`).** (a) Rail container top padding: `"13px 11px"` ÔåÆ `"4px 11px 13px"` ÔÇö first section now sits just under the AppHeader. Ôû¥ dropdowns are untouched ÔÇö all variants still fully accessible. Net: rail height roughly halved; no scroll needed for the common toolset.

### B598 ÔÇö Parcel tool: no obvious way to EXIT it, and no way to REMOVE parcels from it `[Site Planner]` (UX, owner-requested)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 ÔÇö "on the add parcels tool, test it for user ease, it's not intuitive how to exit it, I'd also like to be able to remove parcels with that tool"; minted **B598** = highest real B# across both files (B597) + 1 ÔÇö B596 (parcel-merge false conflict) and B597 (site-rename revert) were taken by this and a concurrent session; branch `claude/parcel-merge-notification-bug-c7kgoo` restarted from `main` after PR #454 (B596) merged)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À 2109 unit tests green ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact ┬À `ui-audit/verify-b598-parcel-tool.mjs` 11/11 (real Chromium, logged-out) + existing `verify-merge-banner.mjs` 6/6 (merge flow intact).** The Parcel ("add parcels") tool had two usability gaps the owner hit: (1) once in it, every canvas click drops a boundary point, so there was no obvious way OUT ÔÇö the only exits were Esc (undiscoverable) or clicking another tool, and finishing one lot silently auto-switched to Select; (2) no way to delete a parcel from the tool at all (removal was buried at the bottom of a selected parcel's props panel).
- **Exit (fix 1):** the Parcel tool now shows a persistent on-canvas **banner** (mirrors the existing merge/easement banner pattern + z-index/stopPropagation guard) with an explicit **Done** button ÔåÆ back to Select. The tool also **stays active after a lot is closed** (removed `closePoly`'s `setTool("select")`), so several adjacent lots draw in a row (a developer assembling a site from multiple parcels) ÔÇö and "how do I get out?" now has one obvious answer: Done (Esc still works too). Adaptive hint shows point count + how to close; a **Finish ÔÅÄ** + **Undo point** appear once a draft has points.
- **Remove (fix 2):** the banner has a **Draw / Remove** mode switch. In **Remove** mode, clicking a parcel (interior OR boundary ÔÇö both bubble to the canvas handler since `startMoveParcel` early-returns without `stopPropagation` outside Select) deletes it; the lot under the cursor is **highlighted in the danger hue** so it's clear what a click will remove (topmost-wins hit-test via `pointInRing`). Stays in Remove mode to delete several. Also added a per-row **Ô£ò** to each parcel in the Parcels panel ÔÇö the most discoverable spot ÔÇö sharing the same delete path.
- **Shared delete (`removeParcelById`)** mirrors `deleteSel`'s parcel branch exactly: `pushHistory` (undo-able) + filter out + **tombstone** (so a removal sticks across reload / cross-tab / cross-device merge and never trips the thin-clobber guard ÔÇö B556/B596), and drops the id from the current selection / merge pick. `parcelMode` resets to "add" on every `selectTool`/Esc so the tool never re-opens in a stale Remove.
- **Verified (headless, logged-out ÔÇö pure client UI, no auth path):** banner shows Draw/Remove/Done; **Done exits** (banner gone); drawing a lot **adds it and stays in the tool**; **Remove mode deletes + tombstones** the clicked parcel (persisted model: parcel gone, id in `deletedIds`) and stays active; re-entering starts in Draw. Fully verifiable logged-out, so no signed-in `VERIFICATION.md` entry is owed.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (parcelMode state + banner + Remove-mode click + removeParcelById + per-row Ô£ò + closePoly stays-in-tool + Parcel hint), `ui-audit/verify-b598-parcel-tool.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change.

### B597 ÔÇö Formula engine: errors now PROPAGATE through aggregation (Excel parity), instead of silently skipping the bad row `[Scheduler]` (enhancement)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "match excel exactly" ÔÇö accepting the optional skip-vs-propagate decision flagged in B590; minted **B597** = highest real B# across both files (B596) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B596 a concurrent `main` took for the parcel-merge bug below; branch `claude/scheduling-formula-column-anxv35`, fresh from `main`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 errors ┬À 2114 unit tests green (98 in `test/formula.test.js`, +12 B596 cases) ┬À build green ┬À inline-sync guard green ┬À headless E2E `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs` PASS (now also asserts error-propagation live through `window.PlanyrFormula`).** Before: a formula column whose cell errors (`[Price]/[Cost]` with a 0 cost ÔåÆ `#DIV/0!`) was stored as BLANK in the host row map, so `SUM` over that column silently skipped the bad row and showed a quietly-smaller total. After: it matches Excel ÔÇö any consumer of an errored cell re-raises the error.
- **How (engine, `src/shared/formula/formula.js`):** added an ERROR-VALUE sentinel `errVal(code)` + `raiseIfErr` (the engine still THROWS internally; this represents an *already-determined* error living in a cell). It re-raises at every consumption point: the four scalar coercions (`toNumber`/`toStr`/`toBool`/`toDateSerial`), `compareValues`, a bare `[Col]` / `[@Col]` reference, the whole-column collectors (so `SUM`/`AVERAGE`/`MIN`/`MAX`/`PRODUCT`/`COUNT`/`COUNTA` propagate), and the conditional aggregations (`COUNTIF`/`SUMIF`/`AVERAGEIF` ÔÇö on a criteria-range cell or a consumed sum/avg cell). `evaluateFormula` surfaces a trailing error value as `{ok:false}`; `formatValue` renders its code. `IFERROR`/`ISERROR`/`ISNA` trap it as expected.
- **How (host, `public/sequence/index.html`):** `computeFormulaValues` + `previewFormula` now store an errored cell as `PF.errVal(res.error)` (was `PF.BLANK`) in the shared row map, so a downstream column that aggregates it propagates. The errored column's OWN display is unchanged (still shows its code). `errVal`/`isErrVal` exported on `window.PlanyrFormula`; engine re-synced into the inline block.
- **Scoped boundaries (deliberate, documented + tested):** (1) `XLOOKUP`/`INDEX` propagate the SPECIFIC matched/indexed error cell (and `XLOOKUP` an error scanned *before* a match) ÔÇö but `MATCH`'s family-filtered compare-safe scan SKIPS an error cell (use `XLOOKUP` if you need lookup-array error propagation); (2) `SUMIF`/`AVERAGEIF` propagate an error in a *matching* sum cell but not in a *non-matching* row's sum cell (only consumed cells raise); (3) a normal BLANK cell is still skipped ÔÇö only a real error value propagates.
- **Files:** `src/shared/formula/formula.js`, `public/sequence/index.html` (synced engine + host glue), `test/formula.test.js`, `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change. Signed-in scheduler cloud click-through folded into `VERIFICATION.md` V178.

### B594 ÔÇö Bump-out: resizing it slides it off the corner (gap to the dock doors), + it ignores the building's color/opacity `[Site Planner]` (2 bugs)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30, screenshot of a selected, expanded bump-out: "whenever I expand the bump out ÔÇª the dock doors do some weird thing ÔÇª the dock doors shifted away and showed a gap between the bumpout and the dockdoors even though i only expanded the bump out in the opposite direction of the dock door" + "when I change the opacity of the building, it should also change the opacity of the bump outs and same thing with color"; minted **B594** = highest real B# across both files (B593) + 1; branch `claude/trailer-parking-bumpouts-33hfr9` restarted from `main` after PR #452 merged)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À 2095 unit tests green ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact ┬À `ui-audit/verify-bumpout-anchor-style.mjs` 7/7 (real Chromium, logged-out).** Two independent bump-out (dog-ear) fixes:
- **Bug 1 ÔÇö the resize let the bump-out float off its building corner.** A bump-out resize routed through the generic element resize (`refitChildren` ÔåÆ free drag box + `clampToHost`, which re-pins only ONE axis), so on a rotated building dragging a grip slid the bump ALONG the wall, away from its corner ÔÇö opening a gap between the bump and the dock doors (the doors start at the bump's along-edge, which had moved). Even a pure projection drag could shift the along on a rotated bump. **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx` `refitChildren`):** when the resized element IS a dog-ear, update its stored span-along-wall + projection from the new box (`dogEarSize`) and then RE-DERIVE its geometry from the host via `dogEarGeom` ÔÇö so its inner edge stays pinned to the dock face and its outer end to the building corner. Only along / projection change; the bump can never slide off the corner, so the doors stay flush. Reuses the existing pure `dogEarGeom`/`dogEarSize` (B362) ÔÇö no new geometry.
- **Bug 2 ÔÇö a bump-out ignored the building's fill color + opacity.** A bump-out is its own `building`-type element, so a per-building color/opacity override didn't reach it. **Fix (render-time inheritance, owner's mental model "a bump-out is part of the building"):** in `renderElPx` a dog-ear now draws with its HOST building's resolved fill/stroke/fillOpacity (`styleEl = host || el`); the Properties panel resolves + targets the host for a bump-out too (`styleHostOf` ÔåÆ `curStyle`, `setSelEl`, `clearElStyle`), so styling either one keeps the whole unit one colour. Auto-fixes existing bumps (no migration, no stored duplication). Reuses `elStyle` (lib/planStyle.js) unchanged.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx`, `ui-audit/verify-bumpout-anchor-style.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change, no auth path, fully verifiable logged-out (no `VERIFICATION.md` entry owed).

### B596 ÔÇö Merging parcels raised a false "someone else has this open" conflict (merge dropped ÔëÑ2 items with no tombstone) `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-30 on planyr.io ÔÇö "when I tried to merge parcels it gave me a note that someone else had that tab openÔÇª no one did; it only happens when I merge, not when I add"; minted **B596** = highest real B# across both files (B595) + 1; SAME data-loss/tombstone family as B459/B556/B594/B595; branch `claude/parcel-merge-notification-bug-c7kgoo`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À 2105 unit tests green (+3 in `test/cloudThinGuard.test.js`) ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact ┬À `ui-audit/verify-b596-merge-tombstone.mjs` 6/6 (real Chromium, logged-out) + existing `verify-merge-banner.mjs` 6/6 (merge flow intact).** Merging parcels combined them into one but recorded **no tombstone** for the consumed parcels ÔÇö so collapsing 3+ parcels (a net drop of ÔëÑ2 items) tripped the **thin-clobber guard (B459)**, which misread a legitimate active-tab merge as a stale-tab clobber and blocked the cloud save with a FALSE **"This project was changed in another session ÔÇö Take over editing"** banner. Adding parcels GROWS the count, so it never tripped ÔÇö exactly matching the owner's "only when I merge, not when I add."
- **Root cause (`SitePlanner.jsx` `mergeParcels`):** the merge did `setParcels(arr.filter(out the selected) + [newMergedParcel])` with no `tombstone(...)` call. `contentCount` counts parcels, so NÔåÆ1 dropped the count by N-1; for NÔëÑ3 that's ÔëÑ2 unexplained losses, and `wouldThinClobber` blocks a ÔëÑ2 drop with no NEW `deletedIds` to explain it. (A 2-parcel merge drops only 1, under the threshold ÔÇö which is why it sometimes "worked".) This is the **same class** as B556's "phantom conflict": a deliberate active-tab shrink with no tombstone read as a stale clobber.
- **Fix:** the merged-away parcels are genuinely removed (replaced by the new parcel), so **tombstone them** ÔÇö the same invariant every other delete honors (B276/B556). This (1) explains the drop to the guard so no false conflict fires, AND (2) prevents the old parcels being **resurrected** by a reload / cross-tab / cross-device `mergeSiteContent` union (the latent second bug the missing tombstone left). Chose tombstoning over `noteLocalContent` (the B556 undo/redo remedy) precisely because a merge is a real deletion, not a reversible-by-re-add shrink ÔÇö so the stronger anti-resurrection guarantee is correct here. `pushHistory()` already snapshots the pre-merge `deletedIds`, so **undo cleanly drops the tombstones and restores the parcels**; the new parcel's fresh `uid()` can never collide with a tombstoned id (B594 salting).
- **Tests:** 3 new `test/cloudThinGuard.test.js` cases prove the data-layer math (3ÔåÆ1 with no tombstone = wrongly BLOCKED; with the 3 ids tombstoned = ALLOWED; a 2-parcel merge fine either way). Headless `ui-audit/verify-b596-merge-tombstone.mjs` drives the real UI: seed 3 adjacent parcels ÔåÆ shift-select ÔåÆ **Merge parcels** ÔåÆ the persisted model holds 1 parcel with all 3 old ids in `deletedIds`.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (+ tombstone in `mergeParcels`), `test/cloudThinGuard.test.js`, `ui-audit/verify-b596-merge-tombstone.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence-format change.
- **Auth-only tail ÔåÆ `VERIFICATION.md` V186:** the conflict path only fires on a signed-in cloud write (the sandbox is logged-out + CORS-blocks the handshake), so the "no false banner on a real cloud merge" confirmation is a signed-in click-through; the logged-out tombstone proof is done here.

### B593 ÔÇö Trailer parking over-hangs the truck court when bump-outs are added `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30, screenshot annotated "fix the bug where the trailer parking extends past the truck court, make sure it varies like the truck court when i add or subtract bumpouts"; minted **B593** = highest real B# across both files (B592) + 1; branch `claude/trailer-parking-bumpouts-33hfr9`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À 2095 unit tests green (dockZones contract test updated) ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact ┬À `ui-audit/verify-trailer-tracks-court.mjs` 9/9 (real Chromium, logged-out).** B492 pulled the **truck court** (dock-zone 0) IN to the clear dock face between the corner bump-outs, but the **trailer parking** (zone 1) and the buffer (zone 2) kept the FULL wall length ÔÇö so the striped trailer row stuck out past the end of the court (55ÔÇ▓ over at each end in the default 55ÔÇ▓-bump case), exactly the owner's screenshot. Now the whole outward stack tracks the court's clear span, so the trailer "varies like the court" on every bump-out change.
- **Root cause + fix (shared pure geometry, `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/dockZones.js`):** `layoutZoneByKind` gated the bump-out trim (`opts.along` / `opts.alongShift`) to `i === 0`, so only the court honoured it. Removed the `i === 0` gate ÔåÆ ANY zone pulls in to the clear span when given the override; with no override every zone still keeps the full wall (un-bumped sides byte-identical, back-compat held).
- **Wiring (`SitePlanner.jsx`, `relayoutSide`):** now passes the court's resolved span (`courtBumpOpts` ÔÇö the clear face between bumps, capped by an optional manual court length, with the centre shift) to EVERY chain member instead of only zone 0. So trailer + buffer + any appended road/landscape stack at the same width and centre as the court.
- **Tracks on every change, not just "add":** `relayoutSide` already runs on add-bump-outs, remove-all-bump-outs, resize-a-bump-out, and resize-the-building ÔÇö so the trailer follows the court through all of them (the user's "add or subtract bumpouts").
- **Stall count self-updates:** `trailerStalls` reads `floor(w / trailerW)`, so the narrowed strip recounts automatically (e.g. 600ÔÇ▓ÔåÆ490ÔÇ▓ shows 40 trailers not 50) ÔÇö no separate count path.
- **Test:** rewrote the one `test/dockZones.test.js` block that locked in the old "trailer/buffer IGNORE the override" contract to assert they now track the court's span + shift (horizontal and vertical dock walls).
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/dockZones.js`, `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx`, `test/dockZones.test.js`, `ui-audit/verify-trailer-tracks-court.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change. Fully verifiable logged-out (pure client geometry), so no signed-in `VERIFICATION.md` entry is owed.

### B592 ÔÇö Building dimension callout: slide it ALONG the building, keep it ON the building, stop at bump-outs `[Site Planner]` (bug/feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 ÔÇö "I can move it off the building? That's not what I meantÔÇª it should stay on the building, I should just be able to move it along the lengthÔÇª and don't let me take it to where there's bump outs because then 620 feet would no longer be true"; minted **B592** = highest real B# across both files (B591) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B590, which a concurrent `main` took for the deep-cross-dock column-spacing item (commit + PR title still read B590); branch `claude/building-dimension-movement-4v1qo6`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À 2078 unit tests (14 new in `test/dimSlide.test.js`) ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact ┬À `ui-audit/verify-b592-dim-slide.mjs` 12/12 (real Chromium, logged-out).** The red footprint depth dimension (the "620ÔÇ▓" callout) was grab-and-drag via a FREE 2-D `dimOffset` (B146) ÔÇö so it could be dragged clean OFF the building, trailing a dashed leader. The owner never wanted that: it should stay ON the building and only slide ALONG its length, and never onto a corner bump-out (where the real depth differs, so the printed value would be a lie). Fixed by constraining the drag to one axis + a geometry-derived clamp band; same pure clamp runs at render time so legacy/stale offsets self-correct.
- **Pure module `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/dimSlide.js`** (`dimSlideRange` + `clampDimOffset`, 14 unit tests). `dimSlideRange(el, bumps, posF)` returns the slide axis (the LONG/length axis, matching renderElPx's `horizLong = w>=h`), the perpendicular (depth) axis that is pinned to 0, and the `[min,max]` band the along-offset is clamped to. The band is the footprint length minus each corner bump-out's along-span at its end (`endNeg`/`endPos`) ÔÇö i.e. the clear middle run where the depth equals the displayed value. `clampDimOffset` drops the perpendicular component to 0 and clamps the slide component.
- **Drag (`startDimMove`/`dimMove`, `SitePlanner.jsx`):** the valid band (fixed geometry) is resolved once at grab time via the new module-level `dimSlideFor(el, allEls)` (which gathers the host's dog-ears through `bumpsAlongLength` ÔåÆ `dogEarSize().along`); each move slides along the length axis only and clamps ÔÇö so the line tracks the pointer along the building but won't follow it off the shape or onto a bump.
- **Render (`renderElPx`):** the stored offset is run through the same `clampDimOffset(dimSlideFor(...))` for DISPLAY too, so a pre-B592 free-drag offset (off the shape / carrying a depth component) or one stranded by a later resize/bump edit renders back on the footprint; the next drag persists the clamped value. The dashed off-building **leader line was removed** (nothing to bridge once the line always stays on the shape). Applies to buildings (with bump-out exclusion) and, for consistency, to road/paving width callouts (no bump-outs ÔåÆ just the stay-on-the-shape clamp).
- **Reuse, not a parallel pattern:** `footprintDepth`/`horizLong` semantics, `dogEarSize` (B362), and the existing B146 drag flow are reused untouched; only the offset math changed. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change (`dimOffset` field unchanged, just always clamped now).
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/dimSlide.js`, `test/dimSlide.test.js`, `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx`, `ui-audit/verify-b592-dim-slide.mjs` (+ best-effort gesture harness `ui-audit/verify-b592-drag.mjs`). The real click-drag GESTURE can't be driven headless (the documented canvas-drag automation limit) ÔåÆ live confirm tracked as **VERIFICATION.md V184** (logged-out, no auth needed).

### B591 ÔÇö Scheduler bug-hunt batch round 3: 9 more bugs (incl. a self-found regression + a broken Import) `[Scheduler]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "let's do round 3" ÔÇö a third, deeper pass after B584/B585; minted **B591** = highest real B# across both files (B590) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B589 a concurrent `main` took for the Formula-engine debug below; branch `claude/scheduling-more-fixes-r3`; commit + PR title still read B589)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 2064 tests (+9) ┬À build green ┬À headless boot PASS (`ui-audit/verify-scheduler-mixes.mjs`).** A third adversarial hunt (5 region finders over the under-covered surface + a re-audit of my own round-1 Gantt change). Import/export came back **clean**. Every candidate re-verified against the real code; the headless boot caught a scope slip lint/build can't see.
- **HIGH:**
  - **E1 ÔÇö REGRESSION I introduced in B584.** The round-1 Gantt axis-span cap capped the *axis* but left bars / today-line / connectors on absolute dates, so a real >100-year span (a stray 1900 baseline + 2026 tasks) desynced them off the axis (and a 2026ÔåÆ4000 bar still blew the canvas to ~16M px). Replaced with a **today-anchored window** (NOW-30y..NOW+50y) + **clamped `xOf`** (out-of-window dates pin to the chart edge); bar width derives from the clamped endpoints.
  - **C1 ÔÇö global grid shortcuts fired THROUGH open modals ÔåÆ data loss** (Delete behind the Successor prompt cleared/deleted the still-selected cell/row). Added an "overlay open" guard (via a ref).
  - **B1 ÔÇö version-history Restore applied the snapshot RAW**, skipping the normalize+cascade pipeline `importJSON` routes through (B4): a `parentId` cycle ÔåÆ `sortByVisualOrder` infinite-recurses ÔåÆ **white-screen**. Routed Restore through the shared pipeline.
  - **Import was ALSO broken (bonus find):** `importJSON` referenced `normalizeIds`/`ensureContacts`, scoped INSIDE the load effect ÔåÆ **`ReferenceError`, import silently failed**. Extracted one shared `applyLoadedData` (via a ref the load effect sets) used by load / import / restore.
  - **D1 ÔÇö renaming/deleting a Contact left tasks on the stale name and "bounced back"** (owners stored by name; `ensureContacts` re-derives contacts from owners on load, respawning the old name). Rename now rewrites every task's `responsibleParty`; delete clears it.
  - **D2 ÔÇö free-text notes mode re-matched note id+date by ARRAY INDEX**, so a note with a blank line (split in two) shifted every following note's id+date. Now matches blocks by **text**.
- **MEDIUM:**
  - **C2 ÔÇö Delete on a multi-cell selection cleared only the focused cell** (vs the fill path); now blanks every editable, non-locked cell in the span.
  - **D3 ÔÇö a parent's own cost/budget/actual was excluded from its rollup** once it gained a child (invisible + locked but still in saved data/export); rollups now include the node's own value (0 for normal parents ÔåÆ no change).
- **LOW:** **C3 ÔÇö a multi-cell `selRange` went stale after a cascade reordered rows**; a date/dependency commit now clears it.
- **Deferred (latent, no current manifestation):** `normalizeToV6` reconciles `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` only on the one-time `_v6` migration, so a future-added default wouldn't backfill already-migrated blobs. (Noted, not fixed.)
- Cloud/interaction paths owed a signed-in/click-through confirm ÔåÆ **V183**.

### B590 ÔÇö Adversarial debug of the Formula engine, round 2 (post-B589): 6 more real defects found + fixed `[Scheduler]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "keep going" ÔÇö a second hardening pass after B589; minted **B590** = highest real B# across both files (B589) + 1; branch `claude/scheduling-formula-column-anxv35`, fresh from `main`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 errors ┬À 2070 unit tests green (86 in `test/formula.test.js`, +5 B590 regression cases) ┬À build green ┬À inline-sync guard green ┬À headless E2E `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs` PASS + a live in-page spot-check of all 6 fixes through the synced `window.PlanyrFormula`.** A second 4-lens adversarial fan-out (random-formula fuzzer ┬À B589-regression check ┬À host/UI integration sweep ┬À Excel error-prop + edge semantics), each candidate reproduced against the real engine before fixing. 6 confirmed, all fixed + pinned by tests:
- **HIGH ÔÇö a non-finite numeric value leaked out as a successful result.** `1e309`, `SUM(1e308,1e308)`, `TRUNC(0,1e16)` etc. returned `{ok:true, Infinity/NaN}` (and `1e309 > 5` a silent `TRUE`) instead of `#NUM!` ÔÇö the `^`/`POWER`/`+ÔêÆ├ù├À` guards didn't cover literals, aggregations, or the single-arg maths. **Fix (engine):** reject a non-finite number literal at the tokenizer (so an overflow can't even reach a comparison/label), finite-check every numeric function result at the call boundary (`evalCall`), and backstop the contract in `evaluateFormula`.
- **HIGH ÔÇö `MROUND` rounded the half toward zero (float drift).** `MROUND(6.05,0.1)` showed `6.0` not `6.1` (6.05/0.1 floats to 60.4999ÔÇª). **Fix:** `Math.round(n/m + 1e-9)┬Àm` ÔÇö half-away-from-zero with the same epsilon discipline `ROUNDUP`/`ROUNDDOWN` use.
- **MED-HIGH ÔÇö `FLOOR`/`CEILING` to a fractional step landed a whole step off.** `FLOOR(2.4,0.1)` showed `2.3` (2.4/0.1 floats to 23.999). **Fix:** ┬▒1e-9 on the quotient (`Math.floor(n/sig+1e-9)`, `Math.ceil(n/sigÔêÆ1e-9)`).
- **MED ÔÇö a giant operator chain gave a non-deterministic result.** A multi-thousand-term `a+b+c+ÔÇª` (iterative parse, recursive eval) overflowed the JS stack or not depending on the ambient call depth ÔÇö the SAME input could return `#ERROR!` or a value. **Fix:** a `MAX_TOKENS = 1000` cap rejects an over-large formula up front (far above any real formula, far below the overflow boundary), so the verdict is input-determined.
- **MED ÔÇö `TEXT()` mishandled `;`-separated sections** (accounting formats). `TEXT(-1234.5,"#,##0.00;(#,##0.00)")` gave `-1,234.50000000` (8 decimals + a stray `-`) ÔÇö the decimal count scanned across the `;` boundary and there was no section selection or literal-text emission. **Fix:** split on top-level `;`, pick the section by sign (positive;negative;zero), count placeholders within the chosen section only, and emit the section's literal text (`(`, `)`, `$`, `%`) in place ÔåÆ `(1,234.50)`. Single-section formats unchanged.
- **LOW-MED ÔÇö a column named with `[` or `]` was unreferenceable** (`Cost [USD]` ÔåÆ a `[Cost [USD]]` reference tokenizes to the truncated `Cost [USD` + a stray `]` ÔåÆ `#ERROR!`). **Fix (host, `FormulaColumnEditor`):** reject `[`/`]` in a column name with an inline "Name can't contain [ or ]".
- **Confirmed clean / deliberate (no change):** within-formula error propagation is correct (`SUM(1,1/0)`ÔåÆ`#DIV/0!`; `AND`/`OR`/`&`/comparisons propagate; `IFERROR`/`IFNA`/`ISERR` trap correctly); `2^3^2 = 64` is Excel's left-assoc `^`; cross-COLUMN aggregation **skips** an errored cell (stored BLANK) rather than propagating ÔÇö a deliberate host choice (one bad row shouldn't blank a whole roll-up), flagged for the owner as an optional skip-vs-propagate decision, not changed; renaming a referenced column ÔåÆ `#REF!` is a visible-error v1 limitation (rename-propagation deferred).
- **Files:** `src/shared/formula/formula.js`, `public/sequence/index.html` (synced engine + the editor name-guard), `test/formula.test.js`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change. Signed-in scheduler cloud click-through still tracked under `VERIFICATION.md` V178.

### B589 ÔÇö Adversarial debug of the Formula engine (post-B586): 9 real bugs found + fixed `[Scheduler]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "now adversarially debug it"; minted **B589** = highest real B# across both files (B588) + 1; branch `claude/scheduling-formula-column-anxv35`, fresh from `main`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 errors ┬À 2064 unit tests green (80 in `test/formula.test.js`, +8 regression cases) ┬À build green ┬À inline-sync guard green ┬À headless E2E `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs` PASS (now proves the double-count fix on the seed's grouped data: `SUM([Duration])` shows the LEAF total 1217, not the double-counted all-rows 5387).** A 4-lens adversarial fan-out (aggregation/criteria ┬À lookups+range+host ┬À functions+parser ┬À integration/regression), each reviewer reproducing every candidate against the real engine in Node. 9 confirmed, all fixed + pinned by tests:
- **HIGH ÔÇö whole-column aggregation double-counted summary/parent rows.** In a grouped schedule the flattened row list contains a parent (showing the rolled-up sum of its children) AND its children, so `SUM([Cost])` returned ~2├ù the real total (and a collapsed group's children were omitted entirely). **Fix (host, `computeFormulaValues` in `public/sequence/index.html`):** aggregation now reads the project's **LEAF tasks of the full tree** (`proj.tasks` leaves), independent of expand/collapse ÔÇö leaves carry the atomic values; a parent's cell is a pure roll-up. The per-row display still uses each row's own values. `previewFormula` mirrors it.
- **HIGH ÔÇö date-literal criteria never matched.** `COUNTIF`/`SUMIF`/`AVERAGEIF([Dates], ">=2026-03-01")` returned 0 (and the `<>` form a wrong non-zero count) because `parseCriteriaOperand` didn't parse a date string and the numeric-family gate excluded dates. **Fix:** parse date literals ÔåÆ date values; widen the gate to include dates.
- **MED ÔÇö `[@Column]` (this-row) was ignored by `COUNTIF`/`SUMIF`/`AVERAGEIF`/`MATCH`/`INDEX`/`XLOOKUP`** (`colArray` didn't check `atRow`), so `SUMIF([@Cost],ÔÇª)` read the whole column. **Fix:** `colArray` returns a single this-row cell when `atRow`.
- **MED ÔÇö `WEEKNUM`** silently treated every type other than 2 as Sunday-start (so type 11 Monday-start and 21 ISO were wrong) and never returned `#NUM!` for an invalid type. **Fix:** full type table (1/2/11ÔÇô17/21) + `#NUM!` on unknown.
- **MED ÔÇö `[@[Column]]`** (Excel's bracketed-inner structured ref) failed to tokenize (`#ERROR!`); the tokenizer stopped at the first `]`. **Fix:** scan to the `]]` for the `@[ÔÇª]` form.
- **MED ÔÇö `MIN`/`MAX` over a date column returned a raw serial number** instead of a date (so `MIN([Start])` showed `46388`). **Fix:** return a date when every contributor is a date.
- **LOW-MED ÔÇö `MATCH` approximate (type ┬▒1) leaked across type families** ÔÇö a text lookup matched numeric cells (cross-type rank). **Fix:** restrict the Ôëñ/ÔëÑ search to the target's type family.
- **LOW ÔÇö `YEARFRAC`** returned a negative result when end < start (Excel is always non-negative). **Fix:** drop the sign.
- **LOW (latent) ÔÇö `colArray`** decided "unknown column" from `rows[0]` only, so a ragged table could read a real column as `#REF!`. **Fix:** existence check across the union of rows.
- **Verified clean (no bug):** the engineÔåöinline-copy sync, both Babel blocks parse, B585's concurrent F1/F2 engine edits coexist with B586, labelÔåöbuilt-in collisions are editor-blocked, the dependency graph orders aggregate-of-formula-column correctly + flags `SUM([Self])` as `#CIRC!`, performance is acceptable (~171ms at 2000 tasks), and the `%`/lookup/text/info functions hold up. (YEARFRAC basis-1 leap-year denominator left as-is ÔÇö Excel's exact value is implementation-dependent and basis-1 is rarely used.)
- **Files:** `src/shared/formula/formula.js`, `public/sequence/index.html`, `test/formula.test.js`, `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs`. Additive ÔÇö no schema/persistence change. Signed-in click-through still tracked under `VERIFICATION.md` V178.

### B586 ÔÇö Deepen the Formula column to match Excel more: cross-row aggregation, conditional sums/counts, lookups, % operator, +30 functions `[Scheduler]` (enhancement, follow-on to B583)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "deepen the formula tool so it matches excel more"; minted **B586** = highest real B# across both files (B585) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B585 that a concurrent `main` took for the scheduler bug-hunt round 2 below; branch `claude/scheduling-formula-column-anxv35`, fresh from `main` after B583's PR #433 merged)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified this session. lint 0 errors ┬À 2024 unit tests green (70 in `test/formula.test.js`, +14 for the new capability + 1 inline-sync guard) ┬À build green ┬À headless E2E `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs` PASS (now also asserts live `SUM([Cost])=400` + `COUNTIF([Cost],">=100")=2`).** Closes the biggest gaps between the B583 engine and real Excel:
- **Cross-row aggregation over a WHOLE column** (the headline deferred item). A bare `[Column]` inside an aggregator now means the entire column: `SUM([Cost])`, `AVERAGE([Duration])`, `MIN`/`MAX`/`PRODUCT`/`COUNT`/`COUNTA([Column])`. Outside an aggregator a bare `[Column]` is still THIS row (Excel implicit intersection), so `[Cost] / SUM([Cost])` = this row's share of the total. **Conditional**: `COUNTIF`/`SUMIF`/`AVERAGEIF([Column], criteria, [SumColumn])` with operator criteria (`">100"`, `"<>0"`) and `*`/`?` wildcards.
- **Lookups** (column-based, modern set): `XLOOKUP(value, [LookupCol], [ReturnCol], [ifNotFound])`, `INDEX([Column], n)`, `MATCH(value, [Column], [type])`. (VLOOKUP's contiguous multi-column table doesn't fit the per-column model ÔÇö XLOOKUP/INDEX+MATCH supersede it.)
- **Excel `[@Column]` structured reference** = the current row explicitly (so `SUM([@Cost])` is just this row); plus the **postfix `%` operator** (`25%` ÔåÆ 0.25, `[Budget] * 25%`).
- **+~30 everyday functions**: math `AVERAGE COUNT COUNTA SIGN TRUNC EXP LN LOG LOG10 PI QUOTIENT MROUND EVEN ODD FACT`; logical `XOR IFNA TRUE FALSE NA`; info `ISNUMBER ISTEXT ISLOGICAL ISERROR ISERR ISNA ISEVEN ISODD N`; text `SUBSTITUTE REPLACE FIND SEARCH REPT PROPER TEXTJOIN VALUE EXACT CHAR CODE`; date `NOW WEEKNUM ISOWEEKNUM YEARFRAC DATEVALUE`.
