- **Engine mechanics:** range/lookup functions are a new `rng` handler type that reads the whole column from a new `ctx.rows` (all rows) + `ctx.rowIndex`; the host (`computeFormulaValues`) now computes **column-by-column in dependency order across all rows** so an aggregate over ANOTHER formula column sees it filled for every row. `evaluateFormula` defaults `rows` to a single-row view, so the editor preview + a standalone caller still work. The editor's live preview passes the full table too.
- **Bugs caught + fixed during build** (by the new tests): `TRUE()`/`FALSE()` with parens (were bare keywords), `SEARCH` used an anchored full-match regex instead of a substring search, and a blank `""` cell wrongly matched a numeric criterion like `">100"` (string ranks above number) ÔÇö `COUNTIF`/`SUMIF` now ignore blank/text cells for numeric comparisons (Excel behavior).
- **Still deferred (as before):** VLOOKUP's multi-column table form; formula columns writing back into schedule dates; formula columns in the PDF/Gantt export. All additive ÔÇö no persistence/schema change (formula defs still ride `proj.formulaCols`).
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in browser check:** folded into `VERIFICATION.md` V178 (same scheduler cloud path; the aggregation/lookups are headless-verified here, only cloud persistence needs a signed-in click-through).
- **Files:** `src/shared/formula/formula.js`, `test/formula.test.js`, `public/sequence/index.html`, `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs`.

### B585 ÔÇö Scheduler bug-hunt batch round 2: 11 more real bugs (incl. the new Formula column) `[Scheduler]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "look for more" ÔÇö a deeper second pass after B584; minted **B585** = highest real B# across both files (B584) + 1; branch `claude/scheduling-more-fixes-r2`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 2022 tests (+15) ┬À build green ┬À headless boot PASS (`ui-audit/verify-scheduler-mixes.mjs`).** A second adversarial hunt (4 region finders) over the post-B584 `main` ÔÇö focused on the just-merged **Formula column** (B583, ~1.1k lines of new code), the project/duplicate/bridge handlers, the suggestions-apply pipeline, and the grid cost/health paths. Every candidate was re-verified against the real code before fixing. Pure-engine (formula) fixes live in `src/shared/formula/formula.js` AND its verbatim inline copy in `index.html`, locked by runtime tests (`test/formula.test.js`) + anti-drift guards (`test/schedulerEngine.test.js`).
- **HIGH:**
  - **TH1 ÔÇö `renameProject` minted a malformed ghost project for a stale/deleted id** (the shell breadcrumb could post a `nav-rename` for an id deleted on another device). `{...undefined, name}` ÔåÆ a project with no `id`/`tasks` that **white-screens** the schedule on activation. Added the same existence guard `nav-link`/`nav-select` already have.
  - **S1 ÔÇö the suggestion preview LIED for a pinned task gaining a predecessor.** `previewProject` kept `pinnedStart` on a `predecessors` patch while `updateTask` clears it, so the review panel showed the task staying put while Approve moved it (and cascaded downstream) to dates the reviewer never saw. `previewProject` now mirrors `updateTask`.
- **MEDIUM:**
  - **TH2 ÔÇö duplicating a project dropped its column layout + Formula columns.** The copy was built without `...src`, losing `colConfig`/`labelAlign`/`formulaCols`. Now spreads `...src` (deep-copying `formulaCols`) and clears the site link.
  - **S2 ÔÇö `cleanPatchFor` silently dropped a `predecessors` patch.** Its "unchanged?" check used `String(arr)`, and two different object arrays both stringify to `"[object Object]"` ÔåÆ a real dependency change was discarded on Approve. Now structurally compares objects/arrays (cheap `String()` kept for primitives).
  - **S3 ÔÇö the holiday-recascade effect could clobber a concurrent edit.** It built the new projects map from the effect's stale closure and committed `setData(d => ({...d, projects: stale}))`, overwriting any edit (realtime suggestion / autosave merge / rapid second edit) that landed in between. Now recomputes from the live `d`.
  - **F1 ÔÇö a blank cell Ôëá `""` in a formula**, so `IF([Start]="","empty","full")` always returned `"full"` (and disagreed with the engine's own `ISBLANK("")`ÔåÆtrue). A blank now equals `""` (sorts before non-empty text).
  - **G1 ÔÇö a multi-row health/status fill silently did nothing by mouse** (worked only by keyboard). The health-dot mousedown collapsed the selection before the picker committed (the intended capture-ref was dead code). The click now preserves a covering multi-row range.
- **LOW:**
  - **TH3** ÔÇö the `nav-delete` bridge routed you to the Dashboard even when `deleteProject` refused (last project); now only routes on a real delete.
  - **F2** ÔÇö date arithmetic that overflows JS `Date` (e.g. `[Start] + 2e8`) rendered the literal `0NaN-NaN-NaN`; now surfaced as `#NUM!`.
  - **G2** ÔÇö the parent-lock guards tested `col.k==="cost"` only, so **Budget/Actual** (which share `t:"cost"`) could be hand-edited on a summary row (junk persisted to save/export); now lock the whole cost family by type (4 guards).
  - **G3** ÔÇö the range-fill loop bypassed the parent lock for cost/budget/actual, leaking a hidden manual value onto summary rows; now skipped.
- **Checked and NOT bugs (scoped negatives):** the in-app Gantt has **no** drag-to-reschedule (bars only select), so there's no start>end/negative-duration drag bug; the PDF paginator's row-slicing + null-svgEl are sound; the formula engine's operator precedence/associativity, MOD/INT/ROUND-negative, EDATE/EOMONTH/DATEDIF/WORKDAY/NETWORKDAYS, cycle/topo recompute, and XSS-escaping are all correct; formula columns aren't selectable in the PDF exhibit export (so no blank-export gap). One **cosmetic** item left unfixed (not a correctness bug): `insertTaskBelow`/`insertTaskAbove` skip `sortByVisualOrder`, so a row inserted below a *collapsed* parent gets an id out of visual order until the next load normalizes it ÔÇö links stay correct.
- Cloud/cross-iframe paths owed a signed-in confirm ÔåÆ **V181**.

### B584 ÔÇö Scheduler bug-hunt batch: 20 real bugs found + fixed in one pass `[Scheduler]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 "find and debug and ship fixes for the scheduling tool"; minted **B584** = highest real B# across both files (B583) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B568 that a concurrent `main` took for a different scheduler fix; branch `claude/scheduling-tool-mixes-ujsrni`; the commit + PR title still read B568)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1873 tests (+22) ┬À build green ┬À headless boot PASS (`ui-audit/verify-scheduler-mixes.mjs`: the real `/sequence/` page compiles via Babel, renders the 47-task SEED, switches to Gantt, 0 scheduler-code errors).** An adversarial bug hunt (12 region finders + verification) over `public/sequence/index.html` (10.7k lines) + the React shell surfaced 20 concrete, reproducible bugs. All fixed; the pure-engine fixes are mirrored in `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs` and unit-/anti-drift-locked in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. Several cloud/cross-iframe paths can't be driven logged-out ÔåÆ **V179** carries the signed-in click-throughs.
- **HIGH ÔÇö correctness / data-loss / crash / freeze:**
  - **#1 `parseFlexDate` ISO fast-path accepted impossible dates** (`2026-02-30`, `2026-13-01`) ÔåÆ persisted a date the app silently rolled (ÔåÆ Mar 2) or NaN'd, disagreeing with every displayed/cascaded value; slash-form already rejected them. Now the ISO path round-trips the same calendar check. (engine + mirror + runtime test)
  - **#2 stale tab could overwrite newer cloud data** ÔÇö the concurrency guard bailed when `knownRev[k]` was undefined (the seed / offline-fallback load path never sets it), so a seed-derived board could clobber the real cloud row. Now treats an unknown base rev as 0 (matches `checkRemote`).
  - **#5 redo restored the WRONG snapshot** ÔÇö the keydown effect omits `data` from its deps, so undo/redo pushed a STALE closure `data` onto the history/future stacks, losing the redone edit. Now pushes the live `dataRef.current`.
  - **#6 cut+paste a task silently severed every OTHER task's dependency on it** ÔÇö the moved subtree got new ids but external successors kept pointing at the old id, which `renumberTasks` then dropped. Now rewires remaining tasks' predecessors onto the new ids.
  - **#7 editing a name/predecessors/notes cell under a row/range selection silently discarded the edit** ÔÇö `commit()`'s fill loop skipped those columns and returned without ever committing the focused cell. Now commits the focused cell for non-fillable columns.
  - **#9 one far-future date froze the on-screen Gantt** ÔÇö the month/year axis loops + container width were unbounded (a year-4000 typo ÔåÆ ~24k month divs / 16M-px width). Now the span is capped at ~100 years (the SVG export already bounded its loop).
  - **#12 PDF export crashed to a blank page when a selected project had zero visible rows** ÔÇö empty Gantt ÔåÆ null `svgEl` ÔåÆ `cloneNode` threw before the staged DOM was restored. Now the split-Gantt slices are guarded on a non-null `svgEl`.
  - **#17 the owner ContactPicker committed the prefix "ghost" over an exact match** ÔÇö typing "Sam" with "Samuel Jones" on file assigned Samuel. Enter now only ghost-accepts when the typed text isn't already an exact contact.
  - **#20 the nav-request handshake reply stripped `linkedSiteId`** ÔÇö a late reply could desync the shell into falsely showing "no schedule linked" over a linked project (and minting a duplicate on Create). The reply now carries the link fields like the primary emit.
- **MEDIUM ÔÇö correctness / data:**
  - **#4 a phone/narrow-window session overwrote the saved desktop view** ÔÇö the mobile `d.view="grid"` override was baked into persisted data and autosaved cross-device. Now the grid layout is gated at RENDER time only; the saved `view` is never mutated.
  - **#13 Tabloid PDF printed on the wrong paper** ÔÇö `@page{size:11in 17in landscape}` is invalid CSS (lengths + keyword) and never swapped for landscape. Now `@page` uses the explicit, orientation-swapped `pgW/pgR` dims for every size.
  - **#16 approving a suggestion with a blank note appended an empty note row** ÔÇö inflated the task's notes badge/count. Now guarded like `createTask` (note only when typed).
  - **#18 the grid applied conditional-format rules to EXPANDED parents using stale data** ÔÇö so expanding a summary could flip its color, disagreeing with the Gantt/dashboard. Parents now always use rolled child health (collapsed or expanded), like `GanttView`/`MasterView`.
- **LOW ÔÇö rendering / polish:**
  - **#3** persisted grid zoom was ignored on load (always reset to 100%) ÔÇö now adopted from data after the async load.
  - **#8** `autoSizeCol` measured an empty Start/Finish as the literal `"NaN/NaN/"`, inflating the auto-fit width ÔÇö now measures nothing.
  - **#10** dependency arrows to a duration-0 SUMMARY anchored to a phantom diamond center instead of the bracket ÔÇö both y-helpers now test the summary case before the milestone case (matches the bar renderer).
  - **#11** on-screen dependency connectors lacked the export's `isNaN` guard, emitting phantom NaN-coordinate arrows for an unparseable date ÔÇö now mirrored.
  - **#14** a fully-filtered MasterView showed a blank table with no message (the empty-state row was unreachable) ÔÇö now keyed on the displayed-row count.
  - **#15** grouped-by-project arrow-key nav landed the selection on the hidden Project column (outline vanished) ÔÇö selection is now keyed to the rendered `displayCols`.
  - **#19** floating (nth-weekday) holidays were computed one calendar day early in any timezone east of UTC (`fd()`'s UTC slice of a local-midnight date) ÔÇö now serialized with `fdLocal` like every other "today" comparison. (engine + mirror + anti-drift)
- **Verification harness:** `ui-audit/verify-scheduler-mixes.mjs` is self-contained ÔÇö it vendors React/Babel/Supabase locally (the page compiles in-browser, so neither lint nor build parses it) and boots the real page over plain localhost so a JSX slip can't ship green. `ui-audit/.cache-vendor/` is gitignored.

### B583 ÔÇö Excel-style Formula column in the scheduling grid `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "create a column that is a formula column that basically functions like Excel where you can do mathÔÇª copy the most-used Excel functionsÔÇª what would work for ~90% of people"; provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B583** = highest real B# across both files (B582) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B562 that collided with main's grid-keyboard-nav B562 plus the B564ÔÇôB582 burst of concurrent mains landed while this branch was open; branch `claude/scheduling-formula-column-anxv35`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified this session. lint 0 errors ┬À 1850 unit tests green (56 in `test/formula.test.js` + 1 inline-sync guard) ┬À build green ┬À headless E2E `ui-audit/verify-b562-formula-column.mjs` PASS (add column ÔåÆ per-row compute ÔåÆ `#DIV/0!`/`#REF!` surfaced) ┬À a 6-lens adversarial-review Workflow ran and all 6 confirmed bugs were fixed before shipping.** A user can now add a **Formula** column to a project's schedule grid (Columns chooser ÔåÆ "+ Formula column", or right-click a column header ÔåÆ "Add formula columnÔÇª"), type an Excel-like expression, and it computes a value **per task row** from that row's other columns.
- **Per-row calculated column, structured references (not A1).** One formula evaluates for every row, referencing columns by name ÔÇö `[Finish] - [Start]`, `IF([% Complete]>=100,"Done", NETWORKDAYS([Start],[Finish]) & " wd")`. Schedule rows are activities that reorder/insert/delete constantly, so absolute cell addresses (A1) would break; column-name refs survive.
- **Engine = a dependency-free pure lib** `src/shared/formula/formula.js` (tokenizer ÔåÆ precedence-climbing parser ÔåÆ tree-walk evaluator; **no `eval`/`new Function`**). The ~90% function set: math `SUM PRODUCT MIN MAX ABS ROUND ROUNDUP ROUNDDOWN INT MOD CEILING FLOOR POWER SQRT`; logical `IF IFS AND OR NOT SWITCH IFERROR ISBLANK`; date `TODAY DATE YEAR MONTH DAY WEEKDAY EDATE EOMONTH DATEDIF DAYS WORKDAY NETWORKDAYS`; text `CONCAT TEXT LEFT RIGHT MID LEN TRIM UPPER LOWER`; operators `+ ÔêÆ * / ^ &`, comparisons, parentheses. Built generic for Cost Estimating reuse later; wired into Schedule first.
- **Dates honor the project working-day calendar.** `WORKDAY`/`NETWORKDAYS` use the SAME weekends + `HOLIDAY_SET` the schedule cascade uses (injected calendar adapter) ÔÇö not Excel's naive MonÔÇôFri ÔÇö so a formula's working-day count agrees with the duration model. Typed inputs documented: Start/Finish = date, Duration = working days (number), % Complete = **0ÔÇô100**; `date ÔêÆ date ÔåÆ days`, `date + n ÔåÆ date`.
- **Read-only / no feedback loop.** Formula columns are derived display columns (`e:false`) computed in a memo AFTER `cascadeDates`/`rollupParentDates` settle, so they read final dates and can never drive task dates/durations (no circular dependency with the scheduling engine). Inter-formula deps are topologically ordered (`planFormulaColumns`); a reference cycle surfaces **`#CIRC!`**.
- **Errors are visible, never a silent blank** ÔÇö `#REF!` (bad column), `#DIV/0!`, `#VALUE!`, `#NAME?`, `#NUM!`, `#N/A`, `#CIRC!` render red in the cell with a tooltip.
- **Inline editor (no dialog-box edits ÔÇö owner rule)** ÔÇö `FormulaColumnEditor`: a name field + a formula box with **autocomplete** for `[Column]` and `FUNCTION()` names + a **live preview** evaluated against the selected row; validates the name (required/unique/not a built-in name) and the formula (parse error shown live). Edit/delete via the chooser Ô£Ä/­ƒùæ or the header context menu.
- **Persistence** ÔÇö formula defs live on `proj.formulaCols` (additive, rides the existing scheduler cloud-save), visibility/width via the existing `colConfig`. A shared effective column registry (`effectiveColsOf`) merges built-ins + formula cols so `selectedColIdx`/copy/format/keyboard-nav resolve consistently App-wide.
- **Standalone-scheduler delivery:** the scheduler is a self-contained in-browser-Babel HTML file (`public/sequence/index.html`), so the engine is inlined VERBATIM by `scripts/sync-sequence-formula.mjs`; `test/formula-inline-sync.test.js` fails CI on any drift between source and the inlined copy.
- **Adversarial-review Workflow (engine-semantics / parser-security / dates / integration, each finding independently re-verified) found + I fixed 6 real bugs before shipping:** (1) float-noise display (`9999999.99 & ""` ÔåÆ clean, not `9999999.9900000002`); (2) non-finite `+ ÔêÆ * /` now `#NUM!` (was a silent `Infinity`/`NaN` that produced a WRONG TRUE/FALSE in comparisons); (3) `DATEDIF "YD"` across year boundaries (was huge/negative); (4) `TEXT` integer leading-zeros (`"00"`ÔåÆ`"07"`); (5) a parser depth guard + a never-throw-to-host contract (deep nesting returns an error cell instead of a `RangeError` that could blank the grid); (6) a hidden formula column that couldn't be re-shown (`showCol` used the built-ins-only registry). Plus 2 low (CEILING/FLOOR sign mismatch ÔåÆ `#NUM!`, `DATEDIF "MD"` non-negative). All pinned by new tests.
- **Deferred to a follow-up (NOT built, as scoped):** cross-row aggregation (column-wide SUM/COUNT/COUNTIF/SUMIF/AVERAGE), lookups (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP), and formula columns writing back into dates. Formula columns also don't yet appear in the PDF/Gantt exports or the cross-project MasterView (grid-only in v1).
- **ÔÅ│ One browser check pending (`VERIFICATION.md` V173):** the sandbox proxy blocks the scheduler's CDN (React/Babel) AND its own Supabase, so the live cloud-connected scheduler can't be driven here ÔÇö E2E was verified against a locally-vendored copy of those libs + the seed schedule. Signed-in confirm on planyr.io: add a formula column, reload, confirm it persists via the scheduler's backend.
- **Files:** `src/shared/formula/formula.js`, `test/formula.test.js`, `test/formula-inline-sync.test.js`, `scripts/sync-sequence-formula.mjs`, `public/sequence/index.html`, `ui-audit/verify-b583-formula-column.mjs`.

### B580 ÔÇö Print/PDF export: north arrow + scale bar oversized and colliding with plan content `[Site Planner / export]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "the north cardinal direction ends up being massive, same with the scale, way too bigÔÇª should be proportional to the page"; minted **B580** = highest real B# across both files (B579) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B564 that concurrent mains took (B564ÔÇôB579 all landed while this branch was open); AMENDS the B144/B145/B196/B219 furniture lineage rather than a parallel path; branch `claude/drawing-export-quality-6dl678`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the same session. lint 0 errors ┬À 1808 tests green ┬À build green ┬À `ui-audit/verify-export-quality.mjs` 10/10 (real app ÔåÆ File Ôû¥ ÔåÆ Download PDF ÔåÆ measured the actual composed sheet).** Root cause was NOT a regression or stale deploy: the export furniture is sized off `refS = min(frame w,h)` and then the whole plan clone is rescaled into the centi-inch plan box, so the arrow GLYPH was fine (~0.39 in) but the surrounding PLATE (generous padding + a big "N" + heavy type) ballooned to **~0.74ÔÇô0.9 in** on paper (the "massive" read) ÔÇö and it was pinned to the plan-FRAME corners (top-left / bottom-right), i.e. INSIDE the drawing, so the scale-bar plate routinely landed on a building + its dimension labels.
- **Fix (two parts), all export-only ÔÇö the live canvas is untouched:**
  1. **Shrink** (`lib/sheetFurniture.js` `furnitureMetrics`): glyph 0.06ÔåÆ0.05┬ÀrefS, tighter padding (0.7ÔåÆ0.5┬Àfs), smaller type (0.02ÔåÆ0.0165┬ÀrefS), thinner plate/segment strokes, tighter "N"ÔåÆneedle gap. Measured result: north plate **0.55 in**, scale-bar plate **0.38 in** (was ~0.74ÔÇô0.9).
  2. **No-occlude placement** (new pure `chooseFurnitureCorners` + `furnitureLayout({obstacles})`): the export passes the development's content boxes (buildings/pond/parking/road, padded for dim labels) and each plate is placed in the emptiest corner, in DIFFERENT corners. Measured furnitureÔåöbuilding overlap = **0**.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/sheetFurniture.js`, `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (`buildExportSvg` computes obstacles + tags the furniture group `data-furniture`), `test/sheetFurniture.test.js` (+4 no-occlude/compact-plate tests), `ui-audit/verify-export-quality.mjs`. On-screen furniture shrinks ~15% too (shared metrics) ÔÇö a harmless screen/print consistency change.

### B581 ÔÇö Print/PDF export: plan line work bakes in too thick (cartoonish) `[Site Planner / export]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "the line work is all sorts of fucked up, way too thickÔÇª makes it look unprofessional"; minted **B581** = highest real B# across both files (B580) + 1; branch `claude/drawing-export-quality-6dl678`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the same session (`verify-export-quality.mjs`: building object line = 0.60 pt on paper, thinned from authored 2 px).** Root cause: element strokes are authored in SCREEN pixels for the live canvas (building/parcel/pond weight 2, surfaces 1.25, parking/dock striping 0.5ÔÇô0.75). When the plan SVG is cloned and nested into the sheet, those pixel weights are scaled by the sheet's "meet" fit transform ÔÇö a factor that DEPENDS on the zoom the user was at when they hit print ÔÇö so line work baked in heavy and inconsistent.
- **Fix:** a pure `lib/exportStyle.js` (`printStrokeWidth` + `sheetFitScale`) retargets every stroke to a real PHYSICAL drafting weight (object line ÔåÆ **0.6 pt**, hierarchy preserved, floored at 0.32 pt so striping never disappears, capped at 1.5 pt), **independent of zoom**. Applied at export by `restyleExportClone` (walks the clone, skips the `data-furniture` group, scales stroke-width + inline-style stroke + dasharray). Both PDF and PNG use it (PNG against a notional letter-landscape sheet so it matches the PDF). 9 unit tests in `test/exportStyle.test.js` (zoom-independence, hierarchy, floor, cap).
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/exportStyle.js` (new), `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (`restyleExportClone`, wired into `exportPDF`/`exportPNG`), `test/exportStyle.test.js`.

### B582 ÔÇö Print/PDF export: labels should read as exhibit annotation, not screen UI `[Site Planner / export]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "looks cartoonish / undeveloped softwareÔÇª make it more professional"; minted **B582** = highest real B# across both files (B581) + 1; branch `claude/drawing-export-quality-6dl678`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the same session (`verify-export-quality.mjs`: no dark pill / no `data-chip-bg`, chip text = dark ink #1B1E26 + white halo, no `bldgShadow` filter).** In the export the dark "X ac" acreage pill (the on-screen UI chip) and the building drop-shadow read as screen affordances dropped onto a drawing.
- **Fix (export-only, via `restyleExportClone`):** the acreage chip (tagged `data-print-chip="acre"` / `data-chip-bg` / `data-chip-text` on screen) is restyled on paper to **haloed exhibit text** ÔÇö the dark pill `<rect>` is removed and the text becomes dark ink + white casing/halo (the same legibility treatment the pond label uses, B221). Secondary: dock-door aprons lighten (fill-opacity 0.55) and the soft building **drop-shadow filter is dropped** on paper (a blurred shadow is a "software-y" tell on a real exhibit; crisp poch├® instead). The dock door-line "busyness" is also tamed by the B581 stroke pass.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx` (chip/apron tags + `restyleExportClone`), `ui-audit/verify-export-quality.mjs`.

### B579 ÔÇö Document Review robustness batch: stuck detail-popup capture ┬À failed-delete mirror desync ┬À silent PDF-store failure `[Document Review]` (3 bugs)  *(self-found 2026-06-29 by the improve loop's lap-7 hunt; minted **B579** = highest real B# across both files (B578) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified the same session. lint 0 ┬À 1874 tests (+3 anti-drift) ┬À build green.** Three distinct, hand-verified defects from one lap (the hunt's other lens, GIS/layer plumbing, came back clean):
- **Detail popup kept pointer capture on an interrupt (`Stitcher.jsx`).** The Bluebeam-style detail "cloud" popup set pointer capture on `onPointerDown` and released it on `onPointerUp`, but `onPointerCancel` only cleared the drag state ÔÇö so a browser interrupt (blur / tab-hide / OS gesture) mid-pan left the popup holding capture, swallowing every click/drag/wheel with a stuck grab cursor until it was closed and reopened. Same class as B551 on the main canvas; fixed by releasing capture in `onPointerCancel` too.
- **A failed review delete desynced the local mirror (`reviewStore.js`).** `clearDraft` ran *before* the cloud `delete`, so a delete that errored (network/auth) left the cloud row alive but the local resume-mirror cleared ÔÇö the review lingered in the library yet could no longer auto-resume. Now the mirror is cleared **only on a successful delete**, keeping a failed delete in a consistent, retry-able state.
- **A genuine PDF-store failure was silent (`DocReview.jsx`).** When BOTH backends (Drive *and* the Supabase fallback) rejected a dropped PDF ÔÇö not merely `oversize`, which still saves the work layer and flags "re-drop on load" ÔÇö the source stayed permanently keyless, so `buildSnapshot` persisted `sources:[]` and the markups reloaded with **no backdrop**, with no warning. The drop path now surfaces an error banner on a real failure, **gated on `cloudReady()`** so logged-out local-only mode (bytes cached, work mirrored locally) stays quiet.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/doc-review/Stitcher.jsx`, `src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/reviewStore.js`, `src/workspaces/doc-review/DocReview.jsx`, `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (3 anti-drift guards). All three are signed-in / interaction paths the sandbox can't fully drive headless ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION.md V177**.

### B571 ÔÇö Road authority as a rendered, color-coded map overlay `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature)  *(owner request 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "what layer are we usingÔÇª I'd like to see that on the right side as a layer I can visually confirm against"; minted **B571** = highest real B# across both files (B570) + 1 ÔÇö NOT B5264; the B5263/B25431 hits are CSS hex colours, not IDs; branch `claude/multi-road-authority-card-1kqrj1`)*
`[x]` **DONE this session. lint 0 errors ┬À 1805 tests green ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-road-authority.mjs` PASS (card + toggle).** Road authority was the one Site Analysis category with NO map layer, so B190's per-card "Ôùì Map" toggle was suppressed for it ÔÇö nothing on the map to eyeball "Maintained by City" against. Added a drawn, toggleable **road-authority overlay** so the fronting roads appear COLORED BY MAINTAINER.
- **Source = the SAME TxDOT Roadway Inventory FeatureServer the identify reads** (`JURISDICTION_SOURCES.road.url`), so the line you see is the line the card reports from (one source of truth).
- **Render path:** a new `jur_road_authority` row in `lib/layers.js` (`kind:"esriFeature"`), drawn client-side as vector features so each segment is individually styled. `lib/layerRequest.js` `featureLayerOptions` extended to accept a per-feature `styleFn(props, opacity)` + a `fields` allow-list (smaller payload). The style is `roadAuthorityStyle` in `lib/jurisdiction.js`, keyed off the **same `roadAuthority(RDWAY_MAINT_AGCY, HSYS)` decode** the card uses ÔÇö no parallel mapping, no drift. Wired into the shared `overlays`/`setOverlays` + `syncOverlayLayers`; the `defaultOverlayState()` picks it up automatically (overlay state is rebuilt from `ALL_LAYERS` each session, so no migration). On an opacity change the esriFeature `setOpacity` re-applies the **style function** (not a flat `{opacity}`) so the per-maintainer colors survive AND newly-fetched tiles keep them.
- **B190 suppression lifted:** `buildRoadFinding` now sets `mapLayer:"jur_road_authority"`, so the Road authority card gets a "Ôùì Map" toggle (status `info` when roads found) ÔåÆ frames to the site + paints the overlay, like every other constraint card.
- **Color encoding ÔÇö a NEW categorical palette, NOT drawn from the locked palettes** (project-status coral/blue/amber/grays, module accents Site #1D9E75 / Schedule #7F77DD / Review #EF9F27 / brand #D85A30): City `#C2185B` ┬À County `#6A1B9A` ┬À State-TxDOT `#00838F` ┬À Toll/managed `#283593` ┬À Federal `#827717` ┬À **Unknown = neutral gray `#546E7A`, drawn DASHED** (distinguished by pattern, never by a faded/low-opacity line ÔÇö hierarchy via weight, per the theming rule). A unit test asserts the palette never reuses a locked hex.
- **Legend:** `ROAD_AUTHORITY_LEGEND` rendered inline in the Layers panel (`LayerPanel.jsx`) when the layer is on ÔÇö small named color swatches (Unknown shown dashed).
- **Performance** (the one real risk ÔÇö dense statewide layer): gated to **minZoom 14** (never paints at metro scale) + fetches only the two style fields; esri-leaflet does its own viewport-bbox tiled fetching on top. Display-only screening overlay on the EPSG:4326 basemap ÔÇö never feeds the editable layer / measurements / the 2278 site frame; carries the screening note + vintage stamp like the other GIS layers.
- **ÔÅ│ V176:** the headless harness verifies the cardÔåÆtoggle wiring with shimmed data, but the actual **colored-line PAINT on the map + the legend swatches** need a browser that can reach `services.arcgis.com` (esri-leaflet's own XHR) ÔÇö this sandbox only allowlists basemap tiles, so the live paint is logged to VERIFICATION.md V176 (logged-out is fine; it's an egress gap, not auth).
- **Dedup:** net-new; reuses the B93/B94 source + the B190 toggle machinery, no new backend/tables/keys.

### B94 ÔÇö Road / right-of-way maintenance authority ÔÇö now PER-ROAD (name + authority + route) `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature)  *(core shipped 2026-06-15; PER-ROAD amendment closed 2026-06-29 per the owner "there are usually multiple roadsÔÇª the card should become a per-road list", folding this chat's earlier provisional "NEW-1"; branch `claude/multi-road-authority-card-1kqrj1`)*
`[x]` **Closed this session by the per-road amendment. lint 0 ┬À 1805 tests green ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-road-authority.mjs` PASS.** The frontage path already returned every distinct fronting *authority* (the multi-desk case ÔÇö a lot fronting a state highway AND a city street already reported both); the remaining gap was **presentation/granularity** ÔÇö the card collapsed it to one `authorities.join(" ┬À ")` value. Fixed to a true per-road list:
- **`identifyRoadAuthority` (jurisdiction.js)** now carries each segment's **road name** (added `STE_NAM` / `HWY` / `TOLL_NM` to the road source `fields`, mirrored in `sources.js`) via a pure `roadDisplayName` (local-street name ÔåÆ title-case; coded `HWY` "SL0008" ÔåÆ "SL 8" via `formatHighway`; else toll name; a bare numeric inventory id is NEVER shown as a name) + each segment's **frontage length** (`polylineLengthMeters`). Segments of the SAME road **merge into one row** (key = name, else route ÔÇö so "Greens Rd" in 3 inventory segments collapses to one), frontage length sums, the highest road class (lowest `F_SYSTEM`) wins the tie-break, and rows are ordered **longest abutment first, then by class** (arterial before local).
- **`buildRoadFinding` (siteAnalysis.js)** emits a **header roll-up** ("Maintained by <X> (all roads)" when every road shares one authority, else "Mixed ÔÇö N roads") + **one row per fronting road** ("<road name> ÔåÆ <authority>"; route + FHWA class in the expandable detail). Any unclassifiable segment shows an explicit **"Unknown"** (the honest-unknown rule applied per road). Zero matches ÔåÆ the honest "No roads matched within 40 m ÔÇö screening only," never a blank.
- **Carries `mapLayer:"jur_road_authority"`** ÔåÆ the card gets the new "Ôùì Map" toggle (see **B571**).
- **+13 unit tests** (formatHighway / roadDisplayName / polylineLengthMeters / roadAuthorityStyle / frontage per-road merge+ordering+Unknown; buildRoadFinding per-road rows + roll-up + map toggle) and the headless harness confirms the real card renders the multi-road list ("Maintained by ┬À Mixed ÔÇö 4 roads" ÔåÆ IH 45 = State (TxDOT) ┬À Greens Rd = City ┬À Aldine Mail Rd = County ┬À Private Dr = Unknown).
- **Optional tail (NOT a blocker):** "supplement with per-jurisdiction county-road / city-street layers where published" ÔÇö largely moot, since the authoritative statewide TxDOT inventory already includes local streets (HSYS=LS) + county roads (HSYS=CR) with their maintenance-agency codes; revisit only if a specific city publishes richer attribution.
> **Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/festive-davinci-0oco2n`): built on the shared connector, verified + calibrated.** Backbone = TxDOT `TxDOT_Roadway_Inventory/FeatureServer/0` (polyline). `identifyRoadAuthority(lng,lat)` buffers the click by 40 m, then returns the **nearest** segment (`polylineDistMeters`) with its maintenance authority. **`RDWAY_MAINT_AGCY` calibrated from the live distinct HSYS├ùagency cross-tab:** 1 = State/TxDOT, 2 = County (CR), 4 = City (LS), 7ÔÇô15 = Federal (HSYS=FD), 5/6/16 = toll/managed-lane; an unrecognized code falls back to the HSYS class and otherwise reads an honest "Unknown."
> **Parcel-frontage added 2026-06-15 (same branch):** `identifyRoadAuthority` also takes the parcel ring ÔåÆ returns EVERY distinct fronting authority (buffers the parcel polygon by the tolerance), not just a clicked point's nearest segment. **Validated live** against the TxDOT endpoint: a downtown-Houston parcel box ÔåÆ 8 segments within 40 m, distinct authorities **City + State (TxDOT)**.
### B570 ÔÇö Fold the Deed / Title (metes & bounds) tool into the Parcel group; rename "Boundary" ÔåÆ "Parcel" `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "put the deed/title metes-and-bounds tool into boundary as a tool, and rename Boundary ÔåÆ Parcel"; minted **B570** = highest real B# (B569) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B567 that collided with mainÔÇÖs scheduler-remap B567; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1822 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless 10/10.** Consolidated the two front doors the earlier laps created into one clean home, per the owner:
- **Renamed the right-rail "Boundary Ôû¥" group button ÔåÆ "Parcel Ôû¥"** (reverses the B108 ParcelÔåÆBoundary rename, at the owner's explicit request). The group now reads Parcel Ôû¥ ÔåÆ { Draw new parcel ┬À Deed / Title ÔÇö metes & boundsÔÇª ┬À Split a parcel }.
- **Folded the Deed / Title tool INTO the Parcel menu** ÔÇö the standalone row-1 "Deed / TitleÔÇª" rail launcher (B543) is removed; its entry now lives in the Parcel menu (carrying the document glyph), opening the SAME reader/plotter modal (`setTitleOpen(true)` now fires from exactly one place). "Draw new parcel" (freehand) is untouched.
- Anti-drift guard rewritten (`test/deedTitleRail.test.js`): the tool lives in the Parcel menu, the standalone `tool-deed` launcher is gone, the group reads "Parcel", and the reader opens from one place. Headless `scratchpad/verify-curves-parcel.mjs` confirms the Parcel menu + entry live.

### B569 ÔÇö Render metes-and-bounds curves as their TRUE arc, not a straight chord `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "do whatever is truly accurate"; minted **B569** = highest real B# (B568) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B566 that collided with mainÔÇÖs B566; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1822 tests (+4) ┬À build green ┬À headless (plotted boundary = 55 vertices, visible arcs).** A curve course used to plot as the straight long-chord between its endpoints; now it's reconstructed as the real circular arc.
- **`arcChordPoints(p0, p1, meta)`** (new export in `lib/metesAndBounds.js`): from the curve's stated radius / central angle / turn (already parsed into `curveMeta`), rebuilds the circle and walks the minor arc from start to chord-end, tessellating ~1 point per 4┬░ (8ÔÇô48 pts). Derives R from chord+╬ö when radius is absent; falls back to the straight chord when there's no usable radius/angle or the geometry is degenerate (chord > 2R). Frame-correct: +x east, +y south; `turn:"R"` puts the centre on the traveller's right.
- **`callsToPath` emits the arc points** for a curve course (ending exactly at the chord endpoint, so the next course continues unchanged) ÔÇö so `buildEncumbranceMarkup`, the easement strip, the modal preview, and the plotted polygon ALL follow the true curve. Closure/misclosure are unaffected (endpoints unchanged). Fixed a latent bug: the save-and-except tie POB was read by `callsToPath(tie)[tie.length]`, which breaks once a call emits many points ÔÇö now takes the last path point.
- **Honest UI:** the modal warning changed from "plotted as straight chords" to "reconstructed as true arcs" (and flags any curve with no stated radius/angle that still falls back to a chord); the call list marks each curve "Ôñ¥ (arc)" vs "Ôñ┐ (chord)".
- **Tests:** 4 geometry tests (a 90┬░ curve bulges off the chord by the sagitta on the correct side for R/L; ends at the chord endpoint; chord fallback). Verified across all 5 real deeds (each 5ÔÇô6 curves; e.g. the 94.91 boundary tessellates 15 courses ÔåÆ 55 path points) with 0 oracle mismatches (course bearings/distances unchanged).
### B568 ÔÇö Scheduler: a duplicate task id silently scrambled the dependency graph on load (`renumberTasks` remap) `[Scheduler]` (bug ÔÇö data integrity)  *(self-found 2026-06-29 by the improve loop's lap-6 hunt; minted **B568** = highest real B# across both files (B567) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered three times as concurrent mains took B564 (deed reader), B566 (cloud badge / callout padding) and B567 (live color picker))*
`[x]` **DONE + verified the same session. lint 0 ┬À 1830 tests ┬À build green ┬À scheduler JSX transpile OK (`ui-audit/jsxcheck-sequence.mjs`).** `renumberTasks` built its old-idÔåÆnew-id remap with `tasks.forEach((t,i) => { map[t.id] = i+1; })` ÔÇö **last occurrence wins**.
- **Why it bites:** if a project's persisted tasks carry a **duplicate id** (the app once minted dup ids before `addTask` switched to `maxId+1`, and `normalizeIds` does NOT dedup before the remap ÔÇö its B550 cycle-break uses a `byId` map but passes the full task array on to `renumberTasks(sortByVisualOrder(tasks))`), the second occurrence overwrote `map[id]`. A parent/predecessor reference to that id then remapped to the **stray copy lower in visual order** instead of the original task ÔÇö silently corrupting the dependency graph on load (wrong cascade dates, dependencies pointing at the wrong task, no warning). The in-code comment at `addTask` already flagged this as a known risk ("a duplicate id poisons renumberTasks' remap table, scrambling parent links").
- **Fix:** `if (!(t.id in map)) map[t.id] = i + 1;` ÔÇö **first occurrence wins**, so a reference resolves to the original task in visual order. **No change for clean unique-id data** (every other caller already passes unique ids). Applied to BOTH the live source (`public/sequence/index.html`) and its verbatim test mirror (`ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`).
- **Tests:** 2 behavioral (dup-id predecessor remaps to the first occurrence; clean data unaffected) + 1 anti-drift guard asserting the first-occurrence form exists in source AND mirror. `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`.
- **Files:** `public/sequence/index.html`, `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. The scheduler can't boot headless (its own Supabase backend is unreachable in-sandbox), so the live signed-in confirmation that a real schedule's dependencies survive load is **VERIFICATION.md V175**.
### B567 ÔÇö Color pickers don't apply live; the color only updates after the picker closes `[Site Planner + Document Review / markup]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-29, arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B567** = highest real B# across both files (B565) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered twice from a provisional B562 then B565 as concurrent `main`s took those IDs; branch `claude/markup-hit-area-color-picker-kfm284`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À tests green ┬À build green ┬À headless ALL-PASS (`ui-audit/verify-markup-fixes.mjs`).** Repro: select any object ÔåÆ open its color control ÔåÆ click a swatch ÔÇö the object did NOT recolor until you dismissed the picker. **Root cause:** every color control is a native `<input type="color">` wired to `onChange`, which in Chromium fires **only when the OS palette closes**; the live event while you click/drag swatches is `input`. So the canvas couldn't reflect the color until dismissal.
- **Fix ÔÇö wire the live `input` event, with single-step undo:**
  - **Site Planner** (`SitePlanner.jsx`): a `livePick(apply)` helper spreads `onFocus`/`onInput`/`onChange` onto each of the **12** native color inputs (markup line/fill, callout text/fill/line, element fill/stroke, parcel fill, pond existing/added fill, the two "Element default colors"). `onInput` commits the color **live**. Undo stays one-step: exactly ONE history frame is pushed per picking **session** ÔÇö lazily on the first change, reset on focus, via `pickSnapRef` (survives re-renders; only one input is active at a time) ÔÇö so the live mutators (`liveMarkup`/`liveCallout`/`liveTypeStyle`/`setDetLive`, plus the already-history-free `setSelEl`/`setSelParcel`) must NOT push their own. The old inline `pushHistory()` in the element/parcel color handlers moved into `livePick`.
  - **Document Review** (`PropertyPanel.jsx` `ColorControl` ÔåÆ `DocReview.jsx` `onPropChange`): the shared control now fires `onInput` (flagged `{live:true}`) for the live recolor and `onChange` (`{live:false}`) for the commit; the host coalesces a live burst into one undo frame keyed on the property (`colorSessionRef`).
- **WYSIWYG follow-through (the half that would otherwise hide the fix):** a SELECTED neutral markup in the Site Planner used to repaint its stroke with the accent tint (`isSel ? PAL.accent : m.stroke`), which **masked** a live stroke-color change. Selected neutral markups (line/polyline/polygon/rect/ellipse) now render their **real** color (`nStroke`), cued by the existing grips + a faint width bump (`nsw = sw + 1`) ÔÇö matching the shared `MarkupRenderer`'s deliberate choice. Semantic markups (encumbrance etc.) keep the accent tint. Verified headless: the selected line recolors to the picked color on `input` (before any close) and a single Ôîÿ/Ctrl-Z reverts it.
- **Left alone:** the ParcelDrawing swatch ROW already applies on click (fixed palette, not the native picker); range sliders already fire live. Guards in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. All logged-out-verifiable ÔÇö no auth/cloud surface, no V### pending.

### B566 ÔÇö Callout / text-box horizontal padding too tight by default `[Site Planner + Document Review / markup]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-29, arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B566** = highest real B# (B565) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered twice from a provisional B561 then B564 as concurrent `main`s took those IDs; same branch as B567)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À tests green ┬À build green ┬À headless (`ui-audit/verify-markup-fixes.mjs`: new-default left/right inset 13.8px vs the old padX:8 box 4.3px).** Repro: a freshly placed Callout / Text box (a text box is a callout with `noLeader:true`) had too little left/right margin ÔÇö text butted close to the side edges and looked cramped, while the equal top/bottom 8px read fine because the line-height cushions it.
- **Fix:** in `SitePlanner.jsx` `calloutStyle`, the default **`padX` is now `?? 14`** (was `?? 8`); `padY` stays `?? 8` ÔÇö horizontal a touch more generous than vertical (the natural label/chip look). padX resolves to screen px at default zoom (`padX * zk`, `zk = ppf/0.35`), so 14 reads as comfortable side breathing room. The per-callout **Padding X / Y** field stays user-editable; this only moves the default, so existing callouts with an explicit padX are untouched.
- **Doc Review parity:** the shared `MarkupRenderer.jsx` callout used one `pad = 4` for both axes (~6px horizontal inset); split into `padX = 8, padY = 4` so its horizontal inset is likewise the more generous of the two.

### B565 ÔÇö Surface the metes-and-bounds plotter as a first-class entry in the Boundary tool group `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-1"; minted **B565** = highest real B# (B564) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B563 that collided with mainÔÇÖs restore-correctness B563; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À build green ┬À headless (Boundary Ôû¥ ÔåÆ Plot from metes & bounds ÔåÆ modal opens).** Adds a **"Plot from metes & boundsÔÇª"** item to the existing **Boundary Ôû¥** `AnchoredMenu` in the right-side Tools rail, between "Draw new parcel" and "Split a parcel". It opens the EXISTING Title-reader / M&B modal (`setTitleOpen(true)`) ÔÇö no second modal, no second parser, no curve-parsing change. Reuses the whole B564 flow (drop a deed *or* paste a legal description ÔåÆ `parseTracts` ÔåÆ plot in the EPSG:2278 real-feet frame, persisted through the existing save path). "Draw new parcel" (freehand) is kept untouched.
- **Audit-first (per the owner's gate):** confirmed the Boundary menu (`toolMenu`/`boundaryAnchor`, `placement:"left"` ÔåÆ it lives in the RIGHT rail, menu flies left into the canvas) and the modal-open mechanism (`titleOpen`); matched the live layout, not the stale README.
- **Contradictions flagged (owner asked):** (1) NEW-1's premise that the M&B flow is "reachable only from inside the modal" is now partly stale ÔÇö **B543 (shipped earlier this same session) already added a row-1 "Deed / TitleÔÇª" Tools-rail launcher**; this Boundary-menu entry is a complementary second front door (both kept, as the owner intended). (2) The curve-guardrail/B25 caution is **moot** ÔÇö curve handling was already implemented + tested under B564 this session (the owner's own note allows this). (3) README is stale (still claims "no login"; the app has Supabase email/password auth) ÔÇö noted, not changed here.

### B564 ÔÇö Drop a deed (.docx) into the Deed/Title reader ÔåÆ read & plot its metes-and-bounds boundary `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 with 5 real sample deeds, "make it so it can read these by me just dropping in the files"; minted **B564** = highest real B# (B563) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B562 that collided with mainÔÇÖs importLegacy B562; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1781 tests (+26) ┬À build green ┬À headless 8/8 (real .docx dropÔåÆplot, 0 page errors).** Follow-on to B543 (the rail launcher): the metes-and-bounds plotter only accepted pasted text and its parser only read tidy "N 45 E, 150 ft" calls ÔÇö it could read NONE of the owner's real surveys. Now you **drop a Word deed onto the modal** and it reads + plots the boundary.
- **Browser `.docx` reader** `src/shared/files/docxText.js` (no dependency): parses the ZIP central directory and inflates `word/document.xml` with the platform-native `DecompressionStream('deflate-raw')`, then turns WordprocessingML into text (one line per `<w:p>`). `readDeedFile` dispatches `.docx` / `.txt`. Pure + Node-testable.
- **Real-deed parser rewrite** `lib/metesAndBounds.js`: handles SPELLED-OUT bearings ("North 87┬░04'16" East"), a governing leg distance a paragraph away from its bearing ("ÔÇªfor a total distance of 1773.49 feet") while SKIPPING "passing at X" waypoints / "(0.14 feet left)" offset notes / monument tie calls ("ÔÇªbears ÔÇª, 1.73 feet"); CURVE courses via their long-chord bearing+distance (keeps radius/central-angle/arc/turn); and MULTI-TRACT **SAVE-AND-EXCEPT** (new `parseTracts` ÔÇö splits only on a real uppercase line-start header, never lower-case prose; a COMMENCING tie is kept so the hole can be placed). `parseCalls` stays back-compatible (returns the main tract).
- **Drop zone + multi-tract plot** in `SitePlanner.jsx`: Section 2 of the Title modal gains a drag-drop / click drop zone (`readDeed`); the summary reads honestly ("15 calls parsed ┬À closes (misclosure 30.0ÔÇ▓) ┬À +1 save-and-except", or "does NOT close ÔÇö gap XÔÇ▓"). "Plot on canvas" draws the boundary as a polygon (gap on the last edge when it doesn't close ÔÇö never silently a corridor) plus each save-and-except tract as a dashed-red hole positioned from its commencing tie off the same POB.
- **Verified course-for-course against an independent ground-truth transcription of all 5 deeds (0 mismatches).** Tests: `test/docxText.test.js` (5) + `test/metesAndBoundsDeeds.test.js` (12, incl. all 5 real `.docx` fixtures under `test/fixtures/deeds/`) + the existing 9 back-compat. Headless `scratchpad` dropÔåÆplot 8/8.
- **Adversarial-review hardening (14 confirmed findings fixed before merge):** `governingDist` leg-end rule now also excludes "passing"/tie distances (a tie ending in "to" could win); `parseTracts` SAVE-AND-EXCEPT cut requires the segment to actually start a new traverse (`COMMENCING`/`BEGINNING at`), rejecting upper-case prose without breaking a long real header; `pathCloses` tolerance tightened to Ôëñ0.5% of run capped at 50 ft (2% masked real misclosure on big tracts; keeps the 5-ft small-lot floor); `CHORD_RE` broadened ("which bears"/"bearing of") with a curve still flagged on fallback; bearings with minutes/seconds ÔëÑ 60 rejected; tract label from a real heading/acreage not body prose; `.docx` reader bounds-checks every ZIP offset + reframes any RangeError/inflate fault as a friendly message (EOCD comment-length validated, codepoint range-guarded); modal resets stale deed state on open; `readDeed` has a request-token guard against overlapping reads; the easement path warns honestly that it doesn't carve exceptions. Re-validated: 0 oracle mismatches, 26 tests, lint 0, build green.

### B563 ÔÇö Version restore was a partial restore: it never re-applied `parcelDrawings` `[Site Planner / version history]` (bug ÔÇö restore correctness)  *(self-found 2026-06-29 by the improve loop's lap-4 hunt; minted **B563** = highest real B# across both files (B562) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified the same session. lint 0 ┬À tests green ┬À build green.** `restoreVersion` explicitly re-applied 8 collections (parcels, els, measures, callouts, markups, underlay, sheetOverlays, deletedIds) but **silently skipped `parcelDrawings`** ÔÇö the per-parcel attached drawings (PDF/image markups).
- **Why it bites:** `parcelDrawings` rides its OWN persistence path (`persistDrawings` ÔåÆ a `saveSite` merge), deliberately kept off the main autosave snapshot. The stored version DOES capture it (`sigOf` + `snapshotVersion` save the full `createSiteModel`), so a restore should bring it back ÔÇö but the restore only touched the main-path setters. Result: after restoring an older version, every other collection reverted while `parcelDrawings` stayed at the CURRENT value ÔÇö a **mixed-version canvas**. Worse, the very next line `noteLocalContent(siteId, v)` recorded the restored version `v`'s drawings as the conflict baseline, so **live state Ôëá recorded baseline**, which could mis-fire the cross-session "newer version elsewhere" guard on the next push.
- **Distinct from undo/redo:** the undo/redo snapshot intentionally excludes `parcelDrawings` (geometry-only undo). A full version RESTORE is a different operation ÔÇö the user expects the entire saved state back.
- **Fix:** restore + durably persist the version's drawings via `persistDrawings(v.parcelDrawings || [])` (sets state AND saves AND debounces the cloud push ÔÇö a bare `setParcelDrawings` would update the display but not persist, reverting on reload). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (B563).
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/SitePlanner.jsx`, `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. The live signed-in confirmation (attach a drawing ÔåÆ save a version ÔåÆ change drawings ÔåÆ Restore the earlier version ÔåÆ the old drawing set returns and survives reload; cloud version history is unreachable headless) is **VERIFICATION.md V173**.

### B562 ÔÇö `importLegacyIntoCloud` timestamp compare wasn't type-safe (third sibling of B559) `[Site Planner / cloud sync]` (bug ÔÇö data loss)  *(self-found 2026-06-29 by the improve loop's lap-4 hunt; minted **B562** = highest real B# across both files (B561) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified the same session. lint 0 ┬À tests green ┬À build green.** B559 made the newer-wins timestamp comparison type-safe with `toMs()` at two sites (the `mergeNewer` in `siteModel.js` and `pruneMigratedLegacy` in `storage.js`) but **missed a third sibling**: `importLegacyIntoCloud` (the login-time copy of on-device sites into the cloud) still used the raw `(existing.updatedAt || 0) >= (local.updatedAt || 0)`.
- **Why it bites:** `createSiteModel` keeps whatever `updatedAt` it's handed (`p.updatedAt || Date.now()`), so a legacy/imported record can carry an **ISO string** while the cloud cache holds a **number**. `number >= "2025-ÔÇª"` coerces the string to `NaN`, and `anything >= NaN` is always `false` ÔåÆ the "cloud already newer, skip" guard silently fails ÔåÆ an **older** local copy can be pushed over a **newer** cloud copy (cross-device work loss) on the one-time legacy import.
- **Fix:** one line ÔÇö `toMs(existing.updatedAt) >= toMs(local.updatedAt)`, mirroring the B559 fixes. `toMs` was already imported. Anti-drift guard added in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (B562).
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/storage.js`, `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B561 ÔÇö SiteÔåÆSchedule switch is buggy: flashing, placeholder name, raw id, false "newer version" banner `[Cross-module / Schedule]` (bug ÔÇö regression in B493)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 ÔÇö "switching from the Pappadoupolos site plan into the schedule is super buggyÔÇª flashing between the name and 'pick a site'ÔÇª it gave the project a random name that was just letters and numbersÔÇª and it says a newer version was saved on another device which isn't true"; minted **B561** = highest real B# across both files (B560) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B560 a concurrent `main` (header-selector consolidation) took)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the same session. lint 0 errors ┬À 1793 tests green ┬À build green ┬À `ui-audit/verify-cross-module-link.mjs` 11/11 (4 new anti-regression checks).** Four symptoms, ONE root cause in the B493 cross-module wiring (`src/workspaces/scheduler/Scheduler.jsx`):
- **Flashing (whole screen + top-left name) = a two-effect feedback loop.** The *carry-in* effect (routeÔåÆiframe: `planar:nav-select-by-site`) and the *sync* effect (iframeÔåÆroute: `onProjectChange(linkedSiteId)`) fought each other: arriving on site A while the iframe's last-active schedule was linked to a different site B made the sync effect rewrite the route to B, which re-fired the carry-in toward A, which re-fired the sync toward BÔÇª every `onProjectChange` ÔåÆ `navigate()` ÔåÆ hashchange re-rendered the whole workspace ÔåÆ flash. **Fix:** the sync effect now only adopts the iframe's active link into an **empty** route (`projectId == null`) ÔÇö provably loop-free (once set, it's inert; it never overrides a routed project). A user switching schedules in the breadcrumb still carries up, via a one-shot `selectSchedule()` (wired to `onSelectProject`), not a reactive effect.
- **Placeholder "Select a project" for ~2 s = no last-known-good.** The breadcrumb read only the iframe's nav-state (empty until the iframe boots). **Fix:** when the route carries a project, the crumb shows the schedule linked to it OR the routed project's real name as last-known-good ÔÇö never the iframe's transient active schedule (which may belong to a different project mid-carry-in).
- **Random letters/numbers as the name = the raw `group_id` leaking.** `routedSiteName` fell back to the raw `projectId` when the site list wasn't warm, and that string was shown AND posted as the created schedule's `name`. **Fix:** the name resolves to a real name only (no id fallback); the resolution panel + its Create action are gated on a resolved name, so a schedule can never be shown/created named a group_id (`LinkSchedulePanel` also disables Create defensively).
- **False "a newer version was saved on another device" = a self-conflicting save burst.** The oscillation/redundant carry-in fired a burst of `setData` in the embedded app ÔåÆ overlapping auto-saves ÔåÆ the scheduler's optimistic-save guard (`cloudRev > knownRev`) tripped against the tab's OWN in-flight write. **Fix:** the `planar:nav-select-by-site` handler (`public/sequence/index.html`) no-ops when that schedule is already active ÔÇö no needless `setData`/save ÔÇö and killing the oscillation removes the burst.
- **Files:** `src/workspaces/scheduler/Scheduler.jsx`, `src/workspaces/scheduler/components/LinkSchedulePanel.jsx`, `public/sequence/index.html`. Verified headless: anti-oscillation (route stays on A, no flip to B), breadcrumb shows the routed name (no placeholder/id), no raw-id ever rendered or postable as a name, and the user push-up (picking a B-linked schedule routes to B) all pass. The live signed-in confirmation (no flash + no false "newer version" banner against the scheduler's own backend, unreachable headless) is **VERIFICATION.md V172**.

### B560 ÔÇö Consolidate the header project/plan selector; remove the duplicate project name `[Shell / header]` (task)  *(owner-requested 2026-06-29, provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B560** = highest real B# across both files (B559) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered twice as concurrent mains took B558 (PR #418, persistence-reprompt) and B559 (round-14 timestamp fix, below); branch `claude/header-selector-consolidation-ktqxib`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1790 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless 7/7 (`ui-audit/verify-header-consolidation.mjs`).** In plan mode the project name rendered **twice** in Row 1: the left breadcrumb (`Map / ­ƒöÆ <Project> Ôû¥`) AND a standalone center group (`<Project> Ôû¥ ÔÇ║ <Plan> Ôû¥`). Collapsed to one ÔÇö the header now reads **`Map / ­ƒöÆ <Project> Ôû¥ / <Plan> Ôû¥`**.
- **Audited the binding first:** both "Grand Port"s bound to the SAME state (`siteLabel` in `SitePlanner.jsx`); the left breadcrumb is `currentProject={{id:groupId,name:siteLabel}}`, the center group reused the same `siteLabel`. The plan selector binds to local `planLabel`/`plansHere`/`siteId` ÔÇö moving it within the same component keeps its binding intact.
- **Removed** the standalone center Site switcher from `plannerCenterContent` (now `plannerPlanCrumb`); `centerContent={null}` so the center/right of Row 1 simply clears (no replacement element). Orphaned `siteReps`/`siteMenu`/`siteAnchor` deleted.
- **Moved** the Plan switcher into the breadcrumb as a new optional `planSlot` (threaded `AppHeader` ÔåÆ `ProjectBreadcrumb`), rendered after the project crumb with the SAME `/` separator + crumb geometry (height 24 / pad 0 8 / radius 6) so the three segments read as one breadcrumb (hierarchy by weight, not by fading per the text-hierarchy rule). The whole plan dropdown (switch/rename/new/duplicate/delete ┬À Save now ┬À Version history) is unchanged and still carries `title="Switch or rename plan"` (the e2e harnesses key on it).
- **No rename regression:** the breadcrumb is now the only project control, so its rename is wired (`onRenameProject={renameProjectFromHeader}`) to route the CURRENT project through `commitSiteLabel` ÔÇö keeping the Row-1 label live the way the old inline Site-name editor did; other projects rename their site group directly. Switch + new + delete + the Private lock are all preserved.
- **Verified:** project name appears exactly 1├ù in the header ┬À old "Switch or rename site" dropdown gone ┬À plan switcher present + opens (New plan / Save now) ┬À breadcrumb order Map ÔåÆ Project ÔåÆ Plan ┬À plan crumb left-anchored beside the project ┬À clean boot. All paths are visible logged-out (same code path signed-in) ÔÇö no auth-only surface, so no V### needed.

### B574 ÔÇö Overlay header buttons render at inconsistent sizes `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-1"; minted **B574** ÔÇö renumbered B560ÔåÆB564ÔåÆB566ÔåÆB574 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ┬À 1808 tests ┬À build green.** The hide (eye) / lock / remove (├ù) buttons in an expanded overlay row were visibly different sizes ÔÇö the eye carried `padding:"5px 7px"` while lock/├ù used the bare `chip` style, and lock/├ù were **emoji** (­ƒöÆ/Ô£ò) whose glyph metrics never matched the eye's inline SVG. **Fix:** one shared square **`iconBtn`** style (30├ù30, padding 0, flex none, centered) for all three, and the emoji swapped for inline-SVG `LockIcon`/`UnlockIcon`/`XIcon` matched to the eye icon's 13px box ÔÇö so the three are now provably identical (headless: all three 30├ù30, square, equal). Titles `Hide overlay`/`Show overlay`/`Remove` preserved (the B276/B277 regression harness still drives them). Verified in `ui-audit/verify-overlay-panel-controls.mjs` (B574 checks) + `verify-overlay-delete-hide.mjs` 13/13 regression.

### B575 ÔÇö Opacity: numeric input alongside the slider `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-2"; minted **B575** ÔÇö renumbered B561ÔåÆB565ÔåÆB567ÔåÆB575 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ┬À 1808 tests ┬À build green.** Kept the range slider; added a two-way-bound numeric percent field to its right. The stored value is a 0.1ÔÇô1.0 float (slider `min 0.1 max 1 step 0.05`); the field shows it as an **integer percent** (`Math.round(opacity*100)`) and writes typing back as `n/100` **clamped to 10ÔÇô100%**. Dragging the slider updates the number; typing updates the slider + overlay live. Implementation holds a raw **draft** string while the field is focused (so a half-typed value isn't clobbered by the controlled round-trip) and clears it on blur so the field follows the overlay again. Verified: shows 85% for 0.85; typing 40 ÔåÆ 0.40 live; below-range 5 clamps to 10% (0.1) and re-displays 10 after blur. `data-testid="overlay-opacity-pct"`.

### B576 ÔÇö Scale control: dead "customÔÇª" rebuilt as Bluebeam-style page-distance = real-distance entry `[Site Planner]` (bug + feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-3"; minted **B576** ÔÇö renumbered B562ÔåÆB566ÔåÆB574ÔåÆB576 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ┬À 1808 tests (+18 unit) ┬À build green.** The old scale `<select>` skipped its own `custom` value and only revealed the custom input when the overlay *already* sat at a non-preset size ÔÇö so picking "customÔÇª" did nothing. It was also a feet-per-inch model with the page side hardcoded to 1ÔÇ│, so only `1ÔÇ│=XÔÇ▓` scales were expressible. **Rebuilt to Bluebeam Revu parity:** a preset dropdown (Engineering `1ÔÇ│=10/20/30/40/50/60/80/100ÔÇ▓` + Architectural `1/8ÔÇ│ ÔÇª 1ÔÇ│=1ÔÇ▓-0ÔÇ│`, grouped) where picking a preset populates page=1 + real=preset and **CustomÔÇª** reveals editable paired fields **`[page][unit Ôû¥] = [real][unit Ôû¥]`** (page in/ft, real ft/in/m; page side accepts decimals AND fractions ÔÇö `0.5` or `1/2`). The pageÔåÆreal ratio is the single source of truth: `feetPerInch = realFt / pageIn` feeds the **untouched** `applyOverlayScale`/`ftPerPx` model, so `0.5ÔÇ│=60ÔÇ▓ ÔåÆ 1ÔÇ│=120ÔÇ▓` is now expressible (was impossible). The custom/preset distinction is driven off **explicit editor state** (`ovEdit`), not the overlay's current size, so Custom always reveals the fields; the dropdown **round-trips** to the matching preset when the size lands on one, else shows Custom with the fields populated. Resolved scale still drives EPSG:2278 sizing and survives reload (only `ftPerPx` changes). **Extended `lib/overlayScale.js`** (not forked): pure `parseDistanceInput` (decimals/fractions/mixed), `feetPerInchFromPair`, `SCALE_PRESETS` + `feetPerInchForPreset`, `matchScalePreset`; **+18 unit tests** (`test/overlayScale.test.js`, 30 total green). Headless: round-trips to eng-30; `1/4ÔÇ│=1ÔÇ▓` ÔåÆ `1ÔÇ│=4ÔÇ▓`; Custom `0.5ÔÇ│=60ÔÇ▓` and `1/2ÔÇ│=60ÔÇ▓` both ÔåÆ `1ÔÇ│=120ÔÇ▓ ┬À 4320ÔÇ▓ wide`. **Review-driven precision guard:** the custom fields default-DISPLAY a rounded value but COMMIT full-precision defaults, and the page/real inputs carry **per-field dirty flags** so an idle focusÔåÆblur of an un-edited field never re-commits the rounded display string (which would have quantized e.g. a metric `1ÔÇ│=1m`/3.2808 to 3.3). Headless: idle focus+blur leaves the scale exactly put.

### B577 ÔÇö Overlay panel cleanup: remove Width, Reset rotation, and obsolete instructional text `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-4"; minted **B577** ÔÇö renumbered B563ÔåÆB567ÔåÆB575ÔåÆB577 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ┬À 1808 tests ┬À build green.** Removed: (1) the **Width [input] ft ´╝ï/´╝ì** row (scale now owns sizing; the live width still shows in the "Now Ôëê ÔÇª ┬À NÔÇ▓ wide" readout); (2) the **Reset rotation** button (the Rotate stepper stays); (3) the drop-zone hint tail ÔÇö "Drag it to move; set size, rotation & opacity below, then Lock it to draw on top." and the obsolete "(Sizing to the drawing scale comes next.)" parenthetical (B576 *is* that) ÔÇö keeping the core "Drop a site-plan PDF or image here or on the map ÔÇö or browse." plus the real-behavior "White paper is knocked out so the map shows through." sentence; (4) the redundant "Scale to the drawing ÔÇö sizes the sheet to true real-world feet." line above the scale control (the "Sheet: ÔÇª" line beneath it stays). Verified each is gone from the overlay card / drop hint while the kept lines remain.
- **Review-fix folded in (image overlays):** "scale now owns sizing" holds for **PDFs** (which carry a `sheet`, so the new scale picker renders) but NOT raster images ÔÇö the picker is gated on `o.sheet` and `openOverlayFile` returns `sheet:null` for PNG/JPG (no physical-inch dimension). So the Width row (+ ┬▒10% nudge) is **kept for `!o.sheet` (image) overlays** as their one numeric size control. Caught by the post-ship adversarial review. Headless: image overlay keeps Width + has no scale picker; sheet overlay unchanged.

### B578 ÔÇö Overlay filename gets its own full-width row `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-5"; minted **B578** ÔÇö renumbered B564ÔåÆB574ÔåÆB576ÔåÆB578 as concurrent mains kept taking the id)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified. lint 0 ┬À 1808 tests ┬À build green.** The filename chip was `flex:1`, sharing the header row with the eye/lock/├ù buttons and truncating with a single-line ellipsis. Moved it to its **own full-width row above the action buttons** (`width:100%`, `whiteSpace:normal`, `overflowWrap:anywhere`, line-height 1.35) so a long name **wraps** and is fully readable; the hide/lock/├ù controls drop to their own row beneath (done together with B574's button-sizing fix, same header). Button `textContent` stays exactly the filename (the overlay-row regression harness matches it). Verified: a 72-char name takes ~full panel width (248/268px), wraps to 3 lines (63px tall), and sits above the action buttons.

     shared-markup / storage-read / shell-routing / date-handling / coverage-logic). 9 raw ÔåÆ 9 "confirmed"
     by the panel, but on my own re-read only ONE survived ÔÇö the rest were over-confirmed false positives /
     by-design and were REJECTED: crossSections (NO producer ÔÇö always empty; round-5 already refuted),
     pdf.js width fallback (modern pdf.js always sets .width; proposed fallback Ôëê font height, not run width),
     measure.js dimension-in-rollup (dimension is category:"shape" ÔÇö rollup deliberately sums measure-tools;
     including it would double-count), DocReview docIntent "race" (the `=== undefined` one-shot guard can't be
     overwritten ÔÇö misread), coverage hydrate bounds (already validates all four via `.every(Number.isFinite)`),
