     (a 10-mi UI cap is a defensible deliberate choice). -->

### B559 ÔÇö Timestamp comparisons assumed a number ÔåÆ a string `updatedAt` silently picks the OLDER copy (merge + legacy-prune) `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ÔÇö latent data-loss) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 14, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B558 a concurrent main took for the take-over-editing fix below)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1789 tests ┬À build green.** `mergeSiteContent` (siteModel.js newer-wins) and `pruneMigratedLegacy` (storage.js) compared `updatedAt` with `>=` assuming a ms number ÔÇö but `createSiteModel` keeps whatever it's given (`p.updatedAt || Date.now()`), so an imported/legacy record can carry an ISO **string**, and `"2025-ÔÇª" >= 1718ÔÇª` is a silent **false**. In the merge that would pick the OLDER copy as "newer" (scalar/meta from the stale side; union still keeps drawn work, but the wrong meta + a wrong prune could strand/drop a newer edit). **Fix:** a shared exported `toMs(v)` coercion (stringÔåÆ`Date.parse`, numberÔåÆitself, else 0) applied at both comparison sites. Unit tests (`test/siteModel.test.js`: toMs + a newer-ISO-vs-older-number merge) + anti-drift guard. Latent (updatedAt is ~always a number in practice) but the consequence is data-loss, so worth the cheap guard. **This lap's hunt was mostly false positives (7 of 9 rejected on re-read) ÔÇö this is the one genuine find.**
### B558 ÔÇö Phantom "changed in another session / Take over editing" re-prompt after a delete (no real 2nd tab) `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-29 on planyr.io: "I click take over editing and a second later it still offers itÔÇª I'm not even sure another tab has it open"; the decisive clue ÔÇö "if I MOVE a building it doesn't pop back up, but if I DELETE one it does"; minted **B558** = highest real B# across both files (B557) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B556 two concurrent mains took (B556 mobile properties-panel, B557 round-13 batch); branch `claude/take-over-editing-reprompt-fxteik`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1786 tests (8 new) ┬À build green ┬À headless 6/6 (`ui-audit/verify-delete-tombstone.mjs`).**
- **Root cause (ONE bug, not two ÔÇö explains BOTH the re-prompt AND the "no other tab" phantom):** the banner re-appearing is the **blue cloud-CONFLICT banner** (`cloudConflict`, "changed in another session"), driven by the **B459 thin-clobber guard** (`wouldThinClobber` in `cloudSync.js`) ÔÇö NOT the amber Web-Locks read-only banner. That guard blocks a push that drops **ÔëÑ2 items the cloud still has with no delete-tombstone to explain the drop**, treating it as a stale-tab clobber. The bug: **`deleteSel` removed the building AND all its bonded children (dog-ears / sidewalks / parking, `attachedTo`) from `els` but recorded NO tombstone** ÔÇö only `removeOverlay` (B276) ever tombstoned. So deleting one building dropped ÔëÑ2 items unexplained ÔåÆ the guard FALSELY rejected the save as a cross-session conflict, with **no second session anywhere**. A **MOVE** keeps the item count (`lost = 0`), so it never tripped ÔÇö exactly the owner's move-vs-delete tell. Secondary latent bug from the same gap: "Take over editing" union-merges the cloud copy back, **resurrecting the just-deleted building** (the never-ending loop).
- **The whole CLASS, not just building-delete (owner asked "what else causes this?"):** any DELIBERATE local op that shrinks the plan by ÔëÑ2 vs the last-synced baseline with no tombstone. Found + fixed all of them:
  - **Deletes** (building+assembly, multi-select, parcel, callout, markup, parcel-drawing, stranded-zone auto-prune on reshape) ÔåÆ now **tombstone** the removed ids (mirrors `removeOverlay`/B276). New shared `tombstone(ids)` helper in `SitePlanner.jsx`; wired into `deleteSel` (both branches), `deleteDrawing`, and the strand-prune. This both clears the false conflict AND makes deletions stick through `mergeSiteContent` (no resurrection).
  - **Undo of a multi-element add** (drawing a building adds building+parking+sidewalk in ONE undo frame ÔåÆ one Ctrl-Z drops ÔëÑ2), **Redo**, **cancelled drag**, and **Restore to a thinner version** ÔåÆ tombstones are wrong here (a redo must re-add the items), so instead the active tab rebases its own thin-clobber baseline via new **`noteLocalContent(id, model)`** (`cloudSync.js`, re-exported through `storage.js`), called from `applySnapshot` (undo/redo/cancel) and the version-restore handler.
- **Why the 8 South stale-clobber protection (the whole point of B459) is untouched:** `noteLocalContent` only ADJUSTS an existing baseline and is called ONLY on an explicit user undo/redo/restore ÔÇö a passive STALE background tab never does those, so its baseline stays at the cloud's fuller count and its thin push still BLOCKS. The cross-session CAS **version** guard (B314) also still rejects a genuine concurrent change. Proved by a dedicated unit test.
- **NOT triggers (verified):** move / resize / rotate / group / add / split-parcel / vertex-delete / cross-tab+takeover merges (all union-up or count-neutral).
- **Files:** `SitePlanner.jsx` (`tombstone` helper + `deleteSel`/`deleteDrawing`/strand-prune tombstoning; `noteLocalContent` in `applySnapshot` + restore), `lib/cloudSync.js` (`noteLocalContent`), `lib/storage.js` (re-export). Tests: `test/cloudThinGuard.test.js` (+4 ÔÇö undo rebase, restore rebase, never-fabricate-baseline, 8-South-still-blocks) + the headless harness.
- **PR #418 ÔåÆ main.** Live signed-in confirmation (the conflict banner itself only renders when cloud-active) owed as **V171**.

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 13 (B557 ÔÇö lap 1 of 4 owner-requested laps): a 6-lens Workflow (print-export /
     gis-builders / calculators / auth-profile / layer-status / a11y-2). 8 raw ÔåÆ 7 confirmed; all 7
     clean low-risk fixes shipped as one umbrella. print-export, gis-builders, and calculators came
     back CLEAN (no wrong output/requests/numbers ÔÇö consistent with the round-12 yield audit).
     Renumbered B556ÔåÆB557 ÔÇö a concurrent main took B556 for the mobile properties-panel fix below. -->

### B557 ÔÇö Round-13 robustness + accessibility batch (auth null-guards ┬À layer-lifecycle cleanup ┬À LayerPanel/menu ARIA) `[Site Planner / robustness + a11y]` (bug umbrella)  *(bug-hunt round 13, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B556 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1786 tests ┬À GIS audit OK ┬À build green.** Seven small, confirmed, low-risk fixes from the lap-1 hunt:
- **Auth email null-guards** ÔÇö `Shell.jsx` (pill title + dropdown) and `AuthPanel.jsx` (Profile tab) rendered `user.email` unguarded ÔåÆ a missing email would show "undefined". Now `user?.email || "(no email)"`/`""`, matching the codebase's existing defensive pattern.
- **Layer-lifecycle cleanup (`layers.js`)** ÔÇö (HIGH) `attachFeatureRetry` scheduled a backoff `setTimeout(refresh)` never cleared when the layer toggled off ÔåÆ a timer fired `refresh()` on a detached layer; now `onRemove` clears it (Leaflet calls onRemove on `map.removeLayer`, chained to the original). `reportCacheAge`'s async cache-age fetch now checks `lyr._map` before `onStatus`, so it can't report age on a since-removed layer.
- **LayerPanel ARIA** ÔÇö the group collapse buttons (Map layers / Jurisdictions / Utility evidence) and the "Show N layers with no local data" reveal button gained `aria-expanded` + a descriptive `aria-label`.
- **ProjectBreadcrumb manage menu** ÔÇö the portal Rename/Delete menu gained `role="menu"`/`role="menuitem"` + the backdrop `role="presentation"`.
- Anti-drift guards in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. Pure-additive ARIA + defensive guards ÔÇö no behavior change for the happy path.

### B556 ÔÇö On a phone, tapping an element auto-opens the properties panel (buries the canvas) `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 on iPhone; minted **B556** = highest real B# across both files (B555) + 1; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1782 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless 2/2.** On a phone the left menu is a full overlay (`position:absolute`, ~320px, zIndex 1100) that buries the canvas, so the Bluebeam-style "select ÔåÆ open its inspector" auto-open was the wrong default there ÔÇö a single tap to select an element/parcel covered the drawing. Two narrow-gated changes (Ôëñ760px, the existing `narrow` state; desktop unchanged):
  - **Auto-open gated (the fix).** The selectionÔåÆpanel effect now early-returns `if (narrow && !leftPanel)` ÔÇö on a phone with the panel closed, tapping just **selects** (handles/rotation/resize controls still appear) and does NOT pop the overlay; the user opens Properties from the **Element** tab when they want it. If a panel is **already** open it still re-syncs to the new selection. One guard covers all selection kinds (`el`/`callout`/`markup`/`parcel`) ÔÇö verified by the adversarial review (the per-handler `startMoveEl/Markup/Callout/Parcel` paths all route through this one effect).
  - **View-shift reconciliation (review-found, B556 finding #1).** The pan-compensation effect (which offsets the drawing when the desktop panel resizes the canvas) was rewritten to track the **exact** px shift applied in a ref and reconcile to the desired value on `[leftPanel, narrow]` ÔÇö so the phone overlay never shifts the drawing, and a mid-session **rotation** (crossing the 760px breakpoint with a panel open) can't leave a residual sideways offset. Replaces the old `prevPanelOpen` open/close toggle.
- **NOT changed (deliberate, confirmed by the review):** explicit **create** actions still open their panel ÔÇö dropping/importing a drawing or duplicating an overlay (`setLeftPanel("overlay")`), finishing an easement/markup draw. Those are deliberate, not incidental taps; the overlay move/scale/rotate tap handlers never opened the panel to begin with.
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-mobile-no-autopanel.mjs` 2/2 ÔÇö phone keeps the panel CLOSED after selecting a building (handles still show); desktop still OPENS it (unchanged). Adversarial review workflow (6 findings ÔåÆ 3 confirmed; 2 already-handled/no-op, 1 view-shift fix applied; overlay finding dismissed as a wrong-premise false positive). The on-device feel (tap to select, then open Properties manually) is owed as **V170**.

### B555 ÔÇö Planner two-finger pinch is "super buggy" on mobile Safari `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 once actually planning a parcel on mobile Safari; minted **B555** = highest real B# across both files (B554) + 1; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1782 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless 3/3.** The Site Planner's pinch-zoom ran on **pointer** events. iOS Safari's multi-touch pointer-event support is unreliable ÔÇö it routinely fires a spurious `pointercancel` on the first finger the instant a second touches, which tore down the pinch mid-gesture and let a one-finger pan fight it (the "super buggy" feel: jumpy, dropping out, sticking). Rewrote the gesture on **native touch events** (`onTouchStart/Move/End/Cancel` on the canvas `<svg>`):
  - `e.touches` always carries every active finger each frame, so the gesture never "loses" a finger the way pointer events did on iOS.
  - View updates are throttled to **one per `requestAnimationFrame`** so a heavy plan doesn't re-render on every raw `touchmove` (the other half of the jank).
  - On the 2nd finger landing, any one-finger pan that just started is torn down **and the view restored** to its pre-pan offset, so the pinch can't jump in.
  - While ÔëÑ2 fingers are down (`touchCountRef`), the pointer-driven pan/draw/vertex-edit handlers all bail, so nothing competes with the pinch. Single-finger pan/draw are untouched (the guard is false for one finger).
  - The canvas already sets `touch-action: none`, so the browser's own pinch-zoom is suppressed and the (passive) React touch handlers need no `preventDefault`. Blur/visibility recovery (`abortGesture`) also clears the new pinch state + cancels any pending frame. The old pointer-pinch refs (`pinchRef` etc.) are left vestigial only where `abortGesture` still clears them.
  - Reuses the shared `pinchZoom`/`midpoint`/`distance` engine (`shared/viewport/viewportTransform.js`) ÔÇö the math was already correct; only the event source changed.
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-planner-pinch.mjs` (CDP multi-touch, 390├ù844) 3/3 ÔÇö pinch-out zooms in, pinch-in within the same gesture zooms back out, a second pinch still works (not stuck). The actual iOS flakiness only reproduces on a real device (headless Chromium's pointer multi-touch is well-behaved) ÔåÆ real-device confirm owed as **V169**.

### B554 ÔÇö Mobile Safari: pinch pops the tab overview; left menu open by default; Select-parcels button covered `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-28 on mobile Safari; minted **B554** = highest real B# across both files (B553) + 1 ÔÇö the B25431/B5263/B3340 hits in this file are CSS hex colours, not IDs; branch `claude/mobile-safari-pinch-menu-3gon8x`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À build green ┬À headless 6/6.** Three mobile complaints, all fixed:
- **Pinch opened all Safari tabs.** A two-finger pinch on the page was hitting Safari's own page-zoom / "show all tabs" gesture instead of zooming the map. `index.html` now `preventDefault`s `gesturestart`/`gesturechange`/`gestureend` (registered before React, `{passive:false}`) and the viewport meta adds `maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no, viewport-fit=cover`. Safari's `gesture*` events are SEPARATE from the touch/pointer events Leaflet (map) and the planner SVG canvas use for their own pinch-zoom, so in-app zoom is unaffected ÔÇö only the browser's page-zoom/tab-grid is suppressed.
- **Left "Your sites" menu opened by default.** `MapFinder.jsx` now defaults the sites panel **closed** on a phone (Ôëñ760px `matchMedia`); desktop keeps its persisted state. Tap the "Your sites" header to open it.
- **"Select parcels" button covered.** On a phone the three top panels (centered search pill `maxWidth: calc(100% - 540px)`, the left sites list, the right layers box) overlapped and buried the Select-parcels button. Added a phone reflow (mirrors the planner's B113 Ôëñ760px mode): the search pill becomes a **full-width top bar** (raised z-index), the sites + imagery/layers panels drop **below** it as collapse-to-tap chips (mutually exclusive when opened so they don't stack), the parcel-info card spans full width (its old `calc(100% - 540px)` went negative on a phone), and the Leaflet +/- zoom control moves to **bottom-left** (it was peeking out behind the bar once the side panels moved). Desktop layout is byte-for-byte unchanged (every phone style is `narrow ?`-gated).
- **Verify:** `ui-audit/verify-mobile-mapfinder.mjs` (390├ù844, logged-out) 6/6 ÔÇö Select-parcels visible/on-screen/not-covered, Your-sites collapsed by default + tap header present, bar clears the layers chip. The real iOS-Safari pinch confirm can't run in headless Chromium (no `gesture*` events) ÔåÆ owed as **V168**.

<!-- ROUND 11 (scheduler hardening) ÔÇö the focused B550 lap (filed in round 10): break parentId cycles
     at the data-load boundary so the Scheduler's tree-walks can't hang the tab on corrupt data. -->

### B550 ÔÇö Scheduler: a parentId cycle in loaded data could hang the tab (recursive tree-walks) `[Scheduler / robustness]` (bug ÔÇö hardening)  *(filed round 10; fixed round 11, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1782 tests ┬À JSX parse OK ┬À build green.** `public/sequence/index.html` has many recursive parentÔåÆchild tree-walks. **Sharpened analysis vs the original filing:** `sortByVisualOrder`'s `walk(null)` actually CANNOT loop on a cycle ÔÇö a cyclic node's parent chain never reaches `null`, so it's unreachable from the null-root walk (it's just orphaned). The genuine hang is the **arbitrary-root** walks ÔÇö `getSubtreeIds` (outdent) and the per-op `walk()` closures (delete/indent/insert) ÔÇö which START at a specific node that can itself be inside a cycle (e.g. `1ÔåÆ3ÔåÆ2ÔåÆ1`), infinite-looping. **Fix (one place protects all):** a cycle-break pass at the data-load boundary in `normalizeIds` ÔÇö for each task, walk its ancestor chain with a `seen` Set; if it loops, null that task's `parentId` (degrade to a root). After load the in-memory data is acyclic, so EVERY downstream walk terminates. The UI already prevents *creating* a cycle live, so the load path was the real exposure. Deliberately did NOT add a `visited` guard to `sortByVisualOrder` ÔÇö it can't loop on a cycle anyway, and the guard would have changed duplicate-id handling (dropping a dup ÔåÆ data-loss regression). Faithful copies in `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`; tests in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js` (3-task cycle broken, valid hierarchy unchanged, arbitrary-root-walk safety) + anti-drift guard. No persistence migration (load-time normalize, lossless ÔÇö a cyclic task just becomes top-level).

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 10 (B551/B552 fixed; B550 filed): a 7th 6-lens Workflow (scheduler-logic /
     keydown-in-input / geometry-correctness / event-binding / import-parse / shared-markup). 8 raw ÔåÆ
     7 confirmed; 2 clean live bugs shipped, the scheduler cycle-hang class filed (B550, defensive +
     high-churn). My two fixes were minted B548/B549 then RENUMBERED to B551/B552 when a concurrent
     main took B548/B549 (+ V166) for the building-panel work below; V166ÔåÆV167. TWO rejected on my own
     re-read: ringCentroid "Math.abs(a)" (the signed area is REQUIRED ÔÇö the cross-term sign cancels
     num/denom; abs() would INVERT the centroid for CCW rings, introducing the very bug claimed) and a
     MarkupRenderer callout-baseline "too low" (6px descender room isn't a clip ÔÇö cosmetic non-defect). -->

### B551 ÔÇö Stitcher left pointer-capture held on a blur/visibility abort ÔåÆ frozen grab cursor `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ÔÇö event binding) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 10, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B548 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1774 tests ┬À build green.** The pan-interrupt recovery (`abortGesture`) needs the pointerId to `releasePointerCapture`, but the window-blur / visibility-hidden handlers called it with NO argument, so `pid != null` was false ÔåÆ capture was never released. Alt-tabbing mid-pan left the SVG with a frozen grab cursor that swallowed clicks until refocus. **Fix:** store `pointerId` in `drag.current` on pan-start and have `recover` pass `drag.current.pointerId` so the capture is actually released. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ Runtime eyeball (pan + alt-tab ÔåÆ no stuck cursor) ÔåÆ V167.**

### B552 ÔÇö pendingLegacyCount over-counted vs the list/import (the B128 count-mismatch class) `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ÔÇö count) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 10; fixed same lap; renumbered from a provisional B549 a concurrent main took)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1774 tests ┬À build green.** `pendingLegacyCount` looped raw localStorage keys and counted records with a missing/falsy normalized id, but `importLegacyIntoCloud` (and `pendingLegacySites`) skip those ÔÇö so the "N sites to import" badge could read 3 while only 2 actually import (the B128 symptom). **Fix:** `pendingLegacyCount` now returns `pendingLegacySites(uid).length`, so the count can never disagree with the list or with what import copies. (Likely resolves the root of the filed B128 ÔÇö left B128 open pending an owner repro to confirm.) Anti-drift guard added.

### B548 ÔÇö Building dimensions mislabeled + mis-bound: "Width/Depth" ÔåÆ dock-relative "Length/Depth" `[Site Planner / massing panel]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-1"; minted **B548** = highest real B# across both files (B547) + 1 ÔÇö twice-renumbered as concurrent merges took the lower IDs first (B542 Files-button, B543 Deed/Title, then B544/B545 bug-hunt round 9); branch `claude/building-dimensions-binding-andglj`)*
`[x]` **DONE + shipped this session ÔÇö fix + headless V166. lint 0 ┬À 1746 tests ┬À build green.** The Selected ┬À Building panel bound "Width"ÔåÆ`el.w` and "Depth"ÔåÆ`el.h` (raw axes), so a tall footprint (`w 328 ├ù h 1159`) printed the impossible **Depth 1159**. The dock-relative depth (`footprintDepth`, B417/B418) was already used by the canvas red dimension but never by the panel.
- **Fix:** added pure `footprintAxes(el)` (which raw axis is depth vs length, off `dockSidesFor`) + `footprintLength(el)` in `lib/dockZones.js`; refactored `footprintDepth` onto the shared resolver (byte-identical output). The building panel now shows **Length** (= dock-parallel wall, `footprintLength`) and **Depth** (= dock-perpendicular span, `footprintDepth`); each edit drives the mapped physical edge via `resizeSelEl({ [ax.length|ax.depth]: n })`. The reported building now reads **Length 1159 / Depth 328**. The dock-doors readout repointed from `max(w,h)` to `footprintLength` (count unchanged, now explicit). "Width" retired for buildings (paving/pond keep their own labels).
- **No persistence migration** ÔÇö buildings store only `w`/`h`; this is display + edit-binding only, stored geometry untouched and reinterpreted via the dock axis at render (lossless). Dock-door RENDERING (~L9663) already arrays doors along the long wall ÔÇö left correct. Rotation independence verified (359┬░ leaves Length/Depth unchanged).
- **Verified:** `test/dockZones.test.js` +4 (footprintAxes/footprintLength incl. the exact 328├ù1159 case) ┬À full suite **1746 green** ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green ┬À headless **V166** (`ui-audit/verify-b548-b549-building-panel.mjs`, 11/11). Reachable logged-out ÔåÆ fully verified here, nothing signed-in pending.
- **Dedup:** not a re-file of B417/B418 (those made the CANVAS depth dock-relative; this is the PANEL finally adopting it). Distinct from B121/B225/B226 (dimension-callout overlap/visibility).

### B549 ÔÇö Reorganize the Selected ┬À Building panel into 4 concept groups; promote Loading; compact dock features `[Site Planner / massing panel]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-2"; minted **B549**; sibling of B548 ÔÇö same branch/PR; twice-renumbered as concurrent merges took the provisional B543 then B545)*
`[x]` **DONE + shipped this session ÔÇö headless V166. lint 0 ┬À 1746 tests ┬À build green.** The flat, interleaved building inspector is now one grouped block (`SitePlanner.jsx`), real-building-only (`isBuilding`), in four weight/size-labeled groups (hierarchy by weight/size, never fading ÔÇö house rule):
- **Footprint** ÔÇö Length, Depth (the B548 dock-relative pair, bare labels). **Loading** ÔÇö Docks promoted as the section anchor, with the dock-feature controls beneath it (Dock zones / Car parking / Bump-outs, the Add-layer choosers, per-zone depths). **Structure** ÔÇö Clear height, Slab (auto Ôçä override affordance kept). **Placement** ÔÇö Rotation, moved to the end.
- **Compacted** the three dock-feature rows from the tall label + sub-label + two-big-buttons block into single-line `label ┬À [ÔêÆ] count [´╝ï]` steppers (sub-caption ÔåÆ button tooltip); standardized affordances. Nothing dropped ÔÇö full field inventory preserved (Footprint-area + Dock-doors readout + Pin/Delete stay at the bottom). Dog-ear (bump-out) selection keeps its prior Width/Depth + Rotation + Docks path unchanged.
- **"Rear-load" never existed** in the code (only Single-load / Cross-dock / No docks) ÔÇö nothing removed; "No docks" stays (a legitimate spec-building state).
- **Verified:** headless **V166** asserts the four groups render in order with Rotation last and every control present, alongside B548's checks (11/11); 1746 tests green; lint 0; build green; `SitePlannerApp` still its own lazy chunk.
- **Dedup:** net-new; no prior Open/Done item covers a building-panel reorg (checked both files + origin/main; the lower IDs were taken by the Files-button removal, Deed/Title rail, and bug-hunt round 9).

     empty-collection / number-display / export-safety / effect-correctness). 7 raw ÔåÆ 6 adversarially
     confirmed; 4 shipped this lap. TWO were REJECTED on my own re-read: a FileBrowser "await refresh()"
     (its reqRef already guards stale state ÔÇö a micro-opt, not a defect) and a DocReview boot-effect dep
     add (the effect is intentionally run-once via a `booted` ref, so the dep change is a no-op AND would

### B544 ÔÇö PDF export leaked a blob URL via an 8-second revoke timer `[Site Planner / export]` (bug ÔÇö resource leak) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 9, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1772 tests ┬À build green.** The Download-PDF path created an object URL and revoked it on an 8s `setTimeout`, so rapid re-exports (or navigating away) accumulated blob URLs in memory. **Fix:** revoke immediately after the synthetic `click()`, matching the proven PNG-export path in the same file (which already revokes inline). Anti-drift guard added.

### B545 ÔÇö MapFinder address search had no request-token ÔåÆ a slow result clobbered a newer search `[Site Planner / map]` (bug ÔÇö stale response) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1772 tests ┬À build green.** `goAddress`/`selectParcelAt` awaited geocode + parcel-identify with no generation guard, so two quick searches raced: a slow first could land after the second, setting `parcelInfo` (and ADDING a parcel) for the wrong address, and its `finally` cleared the shared busy flag mid-second-search. **Fix:** an `addrTokRef` generation counter ÔÇö `goAddress` claims a token and drops its result if a newer search started; `selectParcelAt` takes the token and neither applies info NOR adds the (stale) parcel once superseded. Reuses the codebase's existing cancelled-flag pattern. **ÔÅ│ Rapid-search runtime check ÔåÆ V165.**

### B546 ÔÇö Stitcher calibration could display a non-finite scale ("1\" Ôëê Ôê×'") `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ÔÇö number display) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1772 tests ┬À build green.** The composite-scale readout gated on `ftPerUnit ?`, but `Infinity`/`NaN` are truthy, so a corrupt loaded `ftPerUnit` (or a future bad calibration) would render "Scale set ┬À 1\" Ôëê Ôê×'". **Fix:** `Number.isFinite(ftPerUnit) && ftPerUnit` on both the color and the text; plus a belt-and-suspenders `u >= 1` guard in `commitCalibrate` so a near-zero span can never produce `Infinity` (mirrors `doCalibrate`, also catches NaN). Anti-drift guard added.

### B547 ÔÇö Stitcher takeoff rollup could show "NaN ac ┬À NaN sf ┬À NaN ft" `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug ÔÇö number display) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 9; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1772 tests ┬À build green.** The stitched-takeoff totals rendered `totals.areaSf`/`totals.distFt` unguarded; one degenerate measure (NaN from malformed/loaded geometry) propagates through the reduce and shows "NaN" for the whole rollup. **Fix:** `Number.isFinite` guard on each total, falling back to "ÔÇö" (matches the per-measure display's conditional). Anti-drift guard added.

<!-- ROUND 8 (UX honesty) ÔÇö cleared the last two small round-5 filed items: geocoder error
     distinction (B540) + cloud-badge popover persistence (B541). -->

### B540 ÔÇö Address geocoder conflated "service unreachable" with "address not found" `[Site Planner / geocode]` (bug ÔÇö honesty/UX)  *(filed round 5; fixed round 8, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1761 tests ┬À build green.** `geocodeAddress` returned `null` for BOTH a real "no candidates" result AND any network failure ÔÇö so when the lookup host was down/blocked, the user was told the address doesn't exist (and might abandon a valid one). **Fix (B540 contract):** track `reachedAny` (did any provider respond OK?). Return `{lat,lon,label}` on a hit, `null` only when a provider was REACHED and authoritatively found nothing, and `{ error }` when NO provider was reachable. Both callers updated to show the distinct "lookup unavailable" message vs "not found": `MapFinder.goAddress` + `SitePlanner.addByAddress` (each gained `if (hit && hit.error) ÔÇª`). `test/geocode.test.js` updated (the old "null on throw" test now asserts `{error}`) + 3 new contract tests. Anti-drift guard added.

### B541 ÔÇö Cloud-sync badge popover closed itself on benign sync-state churn `[Shared / UI]` (bug ÔÇö UX)  *(filed round 5; fixed round 8)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1761 tests ┬À build green.** `CloudSyncBadge` keyed its error-boundary on `String(state)`, remounting the whole badge (and discarding the inner `Badge`'s open popover) on every save-cycle transition. **Fix:** the boundary is no longer remounted via a `key`; instead it takes a `resetKey` prop and clears a transient crash in `componentDidUpdate` when it changes. So the popover survives state transitions (the Badge's own effect still closes it when the state stops being actionable) while crash-recovery is fully preserved (a crash clears on the next state change, as before) and the badge still always renders the live `state`. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ Signed-in eyeball that the detail popover stays open across a save cycle ÔåÆ V164** (same signed-in session as the B528/B529 check).

### B543 ÔÇö Promote the Deed / Title reader (Schedule B + metes-and-bounds) to a first-class tool-rail entry `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 as "NEW-1"; minted **B543** = highest real B# across both files (B542) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B542 that collided with the already-merged ­ƒùé Files-button-removal B542; branch `claude/deed-title-tool-rail-ldvtxe`)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1749 tests (+7) ┬À build green ┬À headless 12/12.** The Title reader & metes-and-bounds plotter (`setTitleOpen(true)`) was fully implemented but its only launcher was a menu item buried at the bottom of the top-toolbar **File Ôû¥** (export) dropdown ÔÇö semantically wrong (reading a deed / plotting a boundary is not an export action) and undiscoverable. **Promoted it to a first-class rail launcher**, no modal-logic change:
- **Rail launcher** added to the bottom of the right-rail "Tools" group (after the Boundary `AnchoredMenu`, before `railHdr("Site elements")`): shared `rbtn(false)` style, `<ToolIcon id="deed" />` glyph, two-line anatomy like Building/Road ÔÇö label **"Deed / TitleÔÇª"** + sub-label "Schedule B ┬À metes & bounds". `onClick` = `if (narrow) setMobileTools(false); setTitleErr(""); setTitleOpen(true);`. It's a **launcher, not a tool**: never calls `selectTool`, never gets active styling, no keyboard shortcut.
- **New `deed` glyph** in `ICON_PATHS` ÔÇö a folded-corner document page over text lines, same `currentColor`/stroke style as the other tool icons (a missing case would render the button blank).
- **Old export-menu launcher removed cleanly** ÔÇö deleted the "Title reader / metes & boundsÔÇª" `menuItem` **and** its preceding divider from the export `AnchoredMenu`; Export PNG + Download PDF remain, no dangling separator. `setTitleOpen(true)` now fires from exactly one place (relocated, not duplicated).
- **Edge cases honored:** phone/narrow rail dismisses the scrim (`setMobileTools(false)`) so the `zIndex:3000` modal isn't shown over a dimmed overlay; renders unconditionally (the modal handles the missing-API-key case internally).
- **Dedupe:** distinct from **B180** (the Files repository, which only *reuses* the metes-and-bounds parser for title-commitment ÔåÆ boundary) and from the existing Title-reader modal work (B25/B26 parser, B48 client key, "Plot as easement") ÔÇö purely a launcher relocation/discoverability fix.
- **Tests:** `test/deedTitleRail.test.js` (render-free anti-drift guard, bugHuntGuards-style) ÔÇö asserts the `tool-deed` rail launcher exists, opens the modal, has the glyph, and is not a tool mode; and that the export menu no longer lists the title-reader item. Live headless run (`scratchpad/verify-b542.mjs`, logged-out, seeded site): launcher present/visible/labelled/glyphed/not-active, click opens the modal (Plot on canvas), File Ôû¥ no longer lists it but keeps Export PNG / Download PDF, 0 page errors.

<!-- ROUND 7 (concurrency) ÔÇö the careful lap for the two HIGH save-race bugs (B528/B529) that
     rounds 4-5 filed and deferred as "needs its own pass". Fixed via a SHARED per-id write
     serializer so the two modules can't drift, with a unit test that models the exact race and
     proves the genuine cross-device guard is NOT weakened. -->

### B528 ÔÇö Doc Review: overlapping same-id cloud saves false-tripped the self-conflict guard ÔåÆ autosave lockout `[Doc Review / persistence]` (bug ÔÇö concurrency) ÔÇö HIGH  *(filed round 4; fixed round 7, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1752 tests ┬À build green.** `usePersistence`'s `writeNow` (debounce flush + visibility/unmount/manual flush) could fire a second cloud write for the same review id BEFORE the first resolved; both read the same `reviewVersions[id]`, so the loser failed the CAS and was reported as a foreign "conflict" ÔÇö which trips `canCloudSave` and silently stops autosave until reload (no data loss, but alarming + sticky). **Fix:** a per-id write serializer (`src/shared/cloud/serializeWrites.js` `makeWriteSerializer`) wraps `upsertReview` ÔåÆ the second write for an id WAITS for the in-flight one, so it reads the version that write threaded back and the CAS succeeds. The genuine cross-device guard (`casUpsert`) is untouched. Unit test `test/serializeWrites.test.js` models the exact race (serialized ÔåÆ both succeed; na├»ve ÔåÆ one false-conflicts) AND asserts a real stale version still conflicts. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ Signed-in runtime confirm (rapid edit + tab-hide doesn't flip to "saved elsewhere") ÔåÆ V164.**

### B529 ÔÇö Site Planner: overlapping same-id cloud saves false-tripped the self-conflict guard `[Site Planner / persistence]` (bug ÔÇö concurrency) ÔÇö HIGH  *(filed round 4; fixed round 7)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1752 tests ┬À build green.** Same root cause in `cloudSync.cloudUpsert` (Site Planner). **Fix:** the SAME shared serializer wraps `cloudUpsert` per site id, so serialization also keeps the B459 thin-clobber content baseline (`siteContent`/`siteTombs`) fresh for the second write. Lifting the latch into one shared helper means the two modules can't drift. Covered by the shared `test/serializeWrites.test.js` + an anti-drift guard. **ÔÅ│ Signed-in runtime confirm ÔåÆ V164.**

<!-- ROUND 6 (a11y) ÔÇö shipped the B530ÔÇôB532 accessibility group that rounds 4-5 filed but deferred
     (cleanly-actionable work shouldn't keep getting deferred). Escape-to-close + dialog roles on the
     two scrim modals; keyboard-reachable rows/section headers. B530 verified live headless. -->

### B530 ÔÇö AuthPanel modals: no Escape-to-close, no dialog role `[Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ÔÇö accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6, 2026-06-27)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified LIVE (headless). lint 0 ┬À 1736 tests ┬À build green.** The shared `Wrap` (sign-in / Profile / Settings / password views all route through it) had no Escape handler and no dialog semantics. **Fix:** a window `keydown` Escape listener mounted while open (cleaned up on unmount) + `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Account"` ÔÇö one change covers every AuthPanel view. **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b530-modal-escape.mjs` drives a real Chromium (Supabase-configured build) ÔåÆ modal opens as a dialog, Escape closes it, repeatably (5/5). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B531 ÔÇö Reviews row + planner Section header weren't keyboard-reachable `[Doc Review + Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ÔÇö accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1736 tests ┬À build green.** Click-only `<div>`s: the `ReviewsBar` review row and the `SitePlanner` left-rail `Section` header. **Fix:** both got `role="button" tabIndex={0}` + an Enter/Space `onKeyDown` mirroring the `onClick` (the Section header also `aria-expanded={open}`; the row an `aria-label`). The Reviews bar TOGGLE was already a real `<button>` ÔÇö only the row/header needed it. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ The signed-in row-activate + the Section-header keyboard-toggle eyeball ÔåÆ V163.**

### B532 ÔÇö SiteReviewModal: no Escape-to-close `[Site Planner / a11y]` (bug ÔÇö accessibility)  *(filed round 4; fixed round 6)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1736 tests ┬À build green.** The unsaved-on-device review modal closed only by ├ù / scrim-click. **Fix:** an Escape `keydown` listener (closes with the current `savedCount`) + `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label` ÔÇö the same pattern B530 verified live. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ The legacy-gated Escape eyeball (needs pending on-device sites) ÔåÆ V163.**

### B542 ÔÇö Remove the now-redundant ­ƒùé Files button + ProjectFilesDrawer from the Site Planner banner `[Site Planner / Library]` (cleanup)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "get rid of the files option in the top banner as it's likely now redundant" ÔÇö follow-up to B496 once the Library tab shipped; branch `claude/library-drive-module-fpzane`; minted **B542** = highest real B# (B541) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À full suite 1732 tests ┬À build green ┬À `ui-audit/verify-library-tab.mjs` 8/8 (real Chromium, logged-out ÔÇö Library tab + Review empty state unaffected).** The Site Planner's row-1 **­ƒùé Files** pill opened the old `ProjectFilesDrawer` slide-over ÔÇö a second, in-context copy of the file browser that the new **Library** tab (B496) now does in full. Redundant, so removed.
- **`[x]` Removed in `SitePlanner.jsx`:** the ­ƒùé Files `<button>`, the `<ProjectFilesDrawer>` render, the `filesOpen` state, and the `import` ÔÇö that button was the drawer's ONLY trigger, so the drawer was fully orphaned.
- **`[x]` Deleted the orphaned `src/workspaces/doc-review/components/ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx`** (no remaining importer). The shared file-storage data layer (`reviewStore`/`autofiling`/`fileFacts`) is untouched ÔÇö Library + Review still use it.
- **`[x]` Retired the obsolete B527 guard** in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` (it read the now-deleted drawer file for a dark-mode theming check ÔÇö moot once the component is gone).
- The shared `onOpenReviewInDocReview` Shell handler stays (Library uses it); its now-unused SitePlanner prop is harmless. Logged-out-verifiable (pure planner UI) ÔÇö no signed-in path owed.

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 5 (B533ÔÇôB539 fixed; B540ÔÇôB541 filed): a 5th 6-lens Workflow (units-geometry/
     async-errors/parse-robust/react-correctness/coords-projection/storage-migration). 13 raw ÔåÆ 11
     adversarially-confirmed real; the 7 clean crash-safety/correctness ones shipped this lap, 2
     medium ones filed (B540 geocode contract, B541 badge popover). TWO confirmed findings were
     REJECTED on my own re-read: a LayerPanel "missing cleanup" (the implicit arrow-return ALREADY
     provides React's cleanup ÔÇö no-op fix) and an arcgis feetToLatLng param-order "fix" (a cosmetic
     refactor across 8 coordinate call sites ÔÇö pure flip-the-map risk for zero functional gain;

### B533 ÔÇö 3DEP elevation converted metres with the international foot, not the US survey foot `[Site Planner / units]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 5, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** `elevation.js` used `M_TO_FT = 3.280839895` (international foot) while the whole project ÔÇö EPSG:2278 State Plane, `FT_PER_M` in `shared/coordinates` (B57b) ÔÇö is US survey feet (3937/1200). A ~0.0002% drift (~0.66 ft per 100 m), low-impact today (elevation is transient/screening-only) but inconsistent with every other foot in the app and a latent error if a ditch invert is ever persisted. **Fix:** `M_TO_FT = 3937 / 1200`. Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B534 ÔÇö Stitcher boot-resume IIFE had no rejection handler ÔåÆ unhandled promise on a resume failure `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** The mount effect that resumes the last stitch session ran an async IIFE with no `.catch`; a throw from `loadReview`/`currentUid`/`reconcile`/`loadStitch` was an unhandled rejection. **Fix:** `.catch(() => {})` on the IIFE ÔÇö `loadStitch` owns its own `busy` via `finally`, so swallowing falls cleanly to the empty stitcher instead of a half-started boot. Anti-drift guard added.

### B535 ÔÇö DocReview boot-resume IIFE had no rejection handler ÔåÆ unhandled promise `[Doc Review]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** Same class as B534 in `DocReview.jsx`: the boot-resume IIFE guarded only the localStorage reads; `currentUid`/`loadReview`/`reconcile`/`loadSingleReview` awaited outside any catch. **Fix:** `.catch(() => {})` ÔÇö `loadSingleReview` owns its own "OpeningÔÇª" overlay via `finally`, so a resume failure falls to the empty state (the documented intended behavior). Anti-drift guard added.

### B536 ÔÇö Stitcher addGroup: one failed page-render silently aborted the WHOLE group-drop (no sheets, no message) `[Doc Review / Stitcher]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** `renderPageToImage` inside addGroup's page loop had no per-page guard; a single corrupt page threw past the loop (the `finally` cleared `busy`, so not a stuck spinner ÔÇö but `setPlaced` never ran, so a drop of N pages produced NOTHING with no explanation). **Fix:** per-page try/catch collects `renderFailed`, skips the bad page, and the notice now reports "N page(s) couldn't render (re-drop to retry)" ÔÇö or, if every page failed, an explicit "couldn't render ÔÇª re-drop" instead of a silent no-op. Anti-drift guard added.

### B537 ÔÇö buildFilingPlan crashed ("not iterable") on a set missing pageNums `[Doc Review / auto-filing]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests (+2) ┬À build green.** `disciplineSplit.buildFilingPlan` spread `...s.pageNums` in three places (the `allSeen` reduce + the `claimed`/`entries` build) with no guard; a malformed `decision.sets` entry lacking `pageNums` threw and crashed filing. **Fix:** `...((s && s.pageNums) || [])` at all three sites ÔåÆ a missing array degrades to empty, not a throw. Behavioral tests in `test/disciplineSplit.test.js` (single + multi-discipline malformed input ÔåÆ no throw).

### B538 ÔÇö sheetScale fracToNum allowed a zero denominator ÔåÆ Infinity/NaN `[Doc Review / scale]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** `fracToNum` divided user-supplied callout text without a zero-denominator guard ("5/0" ÔåÆ Infinity, "0/0" ÔåÆ NaN). Downstream `parseSheetScale` range checks neutralize it today, but the helper should not leak non-finite values. **Fix:** return 0 when the denominator is 0 (both mixed and plain fraction branches). Anti-drift guard added.

### B539 ÔÇö currentAccessToken: one corrupt auth-token blob aborted the whole scan ÔåÆ signed-in user looked signed-out to the keepalive push `[Auth / Storage]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 5; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1733 tests ┬À build green.** `supabase.currentAccessToken` wrapped the WHOLE key-scan loop in one try/catch, so a single corrupt/truncated `sb-*-auth-token` blob threw at `JSON.parse` and returned null ÔÇö missing any later valid session (the B452 keepalive unload-flush would then push as anonymous ÔåÆ a contributing factor to "edits not saved"). **Fix:** parse each entry inside its own try/catch so a bad key is skipped and the scan continues. (The agent's first-pass fix ÔÇö a type-check after JSON.parse ÔÇö was rejected: the throw happens AT JSON.parse, before any check; only a per-iteration try works.) Anti-drift guard added.

     concurrency/theme-contrast/a11y/overlay-stitch/module-loader). 12 findings adversarially
     confirmed; the 6 mechanical/low-risk ones shipped this lap (incl. B527 drawer theming), the
     remaining 5 larger/riskier ones filed (2 HIGH save-race fixes B528/B529 + an a11y group
     B530ÔÇôB532 for dedicated careful laps). overlay-stitch came back clean. -->

### B522 ÔÇö Mapillary edge proxy crashed (uncaught 500) on a no-body upstream status (204/304) `[Edge / GIS]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 4, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1725 tests ┬À build green.** `functions/api/mapillary/[[path]].js` forwarded the upstream status verbatim into `new Response(JSON.stringify(...), {status})`; a 204/304 (body forbidden) throws a TypeError ÔåÆ uncaught platform 500, the exact hard error this graceful proxy promises never to emit. **Fix:** clamp a no-body/out-of-range status to 502 before responding (honest "Mapillary HTTP N" message kept). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`.

### B523 ÔÇö /api/file edge proxy crashed on a no-body upstream status (204/304) `[Edge / Doc Review]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1725 tests ┬À build green.** Same class as B522 in `functions/api/file.js` (dormant-gated behind `DOC_FILING_URL`). **Fix:** clamp the forwarded status to 502 for a no-body/empty-body upstream (a 200-with-empty-body no longer reports a self-contradicting "ok:false ÔÇª HTTP 200"). Anti-drift guard added.

### B524 ÔÇö Library workspace had no loader skin ÔåÆ generic "LoadingÔÇª" caption `[Shell / loader]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1725 tests (+1) ┬À build green.** `LOADER_SKINS` had no `library` entry, so the shipped Library workspace fell back to the generic gantt skin + "LoadingÔÇª". **Fix:** added a `library` skin ("Opening libraryÔÇª"). Test in `test/moduleLoaderTheme.test.js`.

### B525 ÔÇö ProjectBreadcrumb cloud-unreachable warn row hardcoded a light-amber box (light slab in dark mode) `[Shared / theming]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1725 tests ┬À build green.** The warn row inside the token-themed breadcrumb dropdown hardcoded `#fef3c7`/`#92400e`, so it read as a light slab in dark mode (B341 trap). **Fix:** repointed to `var(--surface-page)` + `var(--warn-text)` border/text. Anti-drift guard added.

### B526 ÔÇö ToolRail was permanently dark regardless of theme (dark slab between light chrome in light mode) `[Shared / theming]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 4; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1725 tests ┬À build green.** The Doc Review tool rail hardcoded `#191613`/`#ece7db`/`#9b9482` + dark border/divider, so in light theme it was a dark slab sandwiched between light chrome ÔÇö the eye-strain case B318 forbids. **Fix:** repointed CHROME/INK/MUTED + border + divider to theme tokens (`--surface-raised`/`--text-primary`/`--text-secondary`/`--border-default`); the active-button text stays near-black on the light amber module accent (correct in both themes). Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ Light + dark eyeball of the rail ÔåÆ V162.**

### B527 ÔÇö Project Files drawer rendered a white slab with invisible text in dark mode `[Doc Review / theming]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH (visible)  *(bug-hunt round 4, 2026-06-27; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À tests green ┬À build green.** `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx` already had a token-backed `PAL`, but ~14 scattered spots hardcoded light hexes ÔÇö the drawer panel + refresh/select/place/refile/queue-row backgrounds at `#fff`, the drop-zone/needs-filing/place-plan boxes at light creams (`#faf8f3`/`#fffbeb`/`#f3f6f4`/`#faf9f5`), and light text (`#92400e`/`#b45309`/`#2c2a26`/`#4b5563`/`#6b7280`). In dark mode the panel stayed white while `--text-primary` went light ÔåÆ **white-on-white invisible content**; the B341 trap at full scale. **Fix:** repointed every background to `var(--surface-raised)`/`var(--surface-page)`, amber labels to `var(--warn-text)`, danger ├ù/fail to `var(--danger-text)`, success/on-map/spatial to `var(--success-text)`, the "both" tag to `var(--status-active)`, and chip-on-accent text to `var(--on-accent)`. Solid-green action buttons (File/Place) keep white-on-green ÔÇö readable in both themes (green doesn't theme). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. **ÔÅ│ Light + dark eyeball of the open drawer ÔåÆ V162.**

<!-- Bug-hunt ROUND 3 (B517ÔÇôB521): a 3rd 6-lens Workflow (markup-engine/pdf-ocr/print-export/
     mobile/calculators/request-builders), each finding adversarially confirmed; all fixed same lap.
     Two lenses (pdf-ocr, calculators) came back CLEAN ÔÇö correctly reported, nothing invented. -->

### B517 ÔÇö Site Planner: oil & gas / pipeline MAP OVERLAY still on the retired Harris-clipped TxRRC host ÔåÆ false all-clear `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 3, 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1720 tests ┬À GIS audit OK ┬À build green.** B369 migrated the Site Analysis *count* path to the authoritative statewide RRC service, but the *map overlay* (`layers.js` txrrc_wells/txrrc_pipe) still requested `www.gis.hctx.net/.../TXRRC/{Wells,Pipelines}` ÔÇö a Harris-County republication ~99.8% incomplete outside Harris. So the panel could report thousands of wells while the map painted ~14 (the documented Chambers Co. 14-vs-8,014 false-clean) ÔÇö a genuine industrial-siting hazard. The CI guard only scanned siteAnalysis.js/jurisdiction.js, never layers.js, so the overlay was missed. **Fix:** repointed both overlays to the registry's authoritative `gis.rrc.texas.gov/.../RRC_Public_Viewer_Srvs/MapServer` (wells = sublayer 1, pipelines = sublayer 13), matching `sources.js` + the analysis path. Anti-drift guard (`test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`) asserts the retired host is gone + the authoritative one present. **ÔÅ│ Over-aerial eyeball (well/pipe density on a Chambers/Gulf-coast site) ÔåÆ V161.**

### B518 ÔÇö Site Planner: 5 raster overlays 400'd at the GIS cache proxy (hosts missing from the allowlist) ÔåÆ no outage caching `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1720 tests ┬À build green.** `gisProxyEnabled()` is on by default, so every raster layer is built pointing at the cache proxy ÔÇö but `ALLOWED_GIS_HOST_RE` omitted `hctx.net` (HCFCD ROW), `nationalmap.gov` (3DEP elevation), and `harcresearch.org` (MUD boundaries), so the proxy returned HTTP 400 and those layers paid a wasted round-trip every paint then fell back to a direct, **uncached** fetch (zero B445 outage resilience). **Fix:** added the three hosts to the allowlist (anchored regex keeps the anti-spoof property). Unit tests assert the hosts pass + spoofs still blocked (`test/gisProxyCore.test.js`). (Complements B517, which moved the TxRRC overlays off hctx.net onto the already-allowlisted texas.gov.)

### B519 ÔÇö Scheduler: exhibit/PDF dependency arrows drew NaN coordinates for an unparseable date `[Schedule / export]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1720 tests ┬À build green ┬À esbuild parse OK.** `buildGanttSVG`'s arrow guard skipped only MISSING (falsy) dates, not a truthy-but-unparseable one (e.g. an imported `2026-13-99`) ÔÇö the bar renderer correctly blanks such a task, but the arrow code still ran `xOf()` ÔåÆ NaN and emitted a garbage `<path d="MNaN,ÔÇª">` into the exported SVG (web snapshot + print PDF). **Fix:** extended the guard with `isNaN(pd(...))` on both endpoints, matching the bar renderer (`public/sequence/index.html`).

### B520 ÔÇö Site Planner / shared header: fixed notification banners overflow phone width ÔåÆ action button off-screen `[Site Planner / mobile]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1720 tests ┬À build green.** Every fixed top banner used `position:fixed; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); maxWidth:<bare px>` (680/740/620/720) with no viewport cap, so on a ~390px phone the box spilled past both edges and its right-pinned action/dismiss button (Take over editing / Ô£ò / Retry) was unreachable. **Fix:** changed all six SitePlanner banners + the AppHeader conflict banner to `maxWidth: min(<px>, calc(100vw - 16px))` (desktop unchanged; matches the modals' existing `92vw` pattern). **ÔÅ│ Phone-width tap-through of a banner button ÔåÆ V161.**

### B521 ÔÇö Shared markup: hitMarkup couldn't select a 2-corner closed box (rect/ellipse/cloud/snapshot) except on its diagonal `[Shared / Markup]` (bug) ÔÇö MED (latent)  *(bug-hunt round 3; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1720 tests (+2) ┬À build green.** The shared `hitMarkup` gated the closed-shape interior/outline tests on `pts.length >= 3`, but Document Review commits rect/ellipse/cloud/snapshot as exactly 2 opposite corners ÔÇö so only the diagonal segment was hittable; the rendered shape was almost entirely un-clickable. Latent today (no production path imports `pickMarkup` yet ÔÇö DocReview still carries its own picker), but a real contract violation in the shared engine meant to be adopted. **Fix:** `hitMarkup` now expands a 2-corner closed box to its axis-aligned interior + outline (ellipse via the normalized equation, so a bbox corner correctly misses). Unit tests in `test/markupHitTest.test.js` (interior, each edge, ellipse-rejects-corner). Tightens the shared module toward its documented contract before DocReview migrates onto it.
     react-effects), each finding adversarially confirmed; all filed AND fixed the same lap. Two
     candidate findings were REFUTED by the skeptic (elevation.crossSections has no producer; the
     doc-review setProjectStatus path is dead code) and correctly not shipped. -->

### B511 ÔÇö Site Planner: pruneMigratedLegacy dropped a NEWER logged-out site by id alone ÔåÆ silent data loss `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 2, 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests (+3) ┬À build green.** `pruneMigratedLegacy` (runs in `pullCloud` on every sign-in) deleted any on-device legacy site whose id merely EXISTED in the cloud map, with no timestamp check ÔÇö so: create a site logged-out ÔåÆ sign in + migrate (legacy copy kept) ÔåÆ sign out ÔåÆ edit it logged-out ÔåÆ sign back in ÔåÆ the NEWER local edit was deleted before the migration modal (`pendingLegacyCount`, which keeps a legacy id newer than cloud) could ever surface it. **Fix:** prune only when `cloudMap[id].updatedAt >= legacy[id].updatedAt` (the inverse of `pendingLegacyCount`'s predicate), so B473's duplicate-reclamation still works but a newer local copy survives. Exported the function + 3 unit tests (`test/storage.test.js`: keeps-newer, prunes same/older, keeps cloud-absent). **ÔÅ│ Signed-in end-to-end flow ÔåÆ V160.**

### B512 ÔÇö Doc Review: parseSheetScale read a rise:run SLOPE/grade ratio (or a clock time) as the drawing scale ÔåÆ silent mis-calibration `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests ┬À build green.** Step 4's bare `1:N` match had no scale-context guard, so a civil/grading sheet printing "MAX SLOPE 1:4" / "1:3 TYP" (or even "1:30 PM") was auto-calibrated to a garbage scale ÔÇö every measurement off by orders of magnitude ÔÇö on the common "scale-bar-only + slope notes" sheet. **Fix:** the ratio match now skips any `1:N` whose surrounding text carries a slope/grade keyword (slope/grade/pitch/batter/ratio/H:V/typ/max/min) or that is immediately followed by AM/PM; a real "SCALE 1:200" / bare scale-box "1:100" still parses (engineer/arch/NTS steps run first). Unit test in `test/sheetScale.test.js`. (`src/shared/files/sheetScale.js`.)

### B513 ÔÇö Doc Review: parseRevision dropped the value for the common "REVISION NO. 3" / "REV. NO. 5" forms `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests (+2) ┬À build green.** The revision regex had no slot for an intervening NO./NUMBER/# label, so the most standard title-block wording filed with no revision tag. **Fix:** allow an optional `no.`/`number`/`#` token between the rev-word and the value, keeping the rev-word `\b` (so the "REVISIONS" heading isn't read as "Rev S") and the value `\b` (so "revision label" isn't "Rev L"). Unit tests in `test/titleBlockParse.test.js`. (`src/shared/files/titleBlockParse.js`.)

### B514 ÔÇö Doc Review: findDates accepted impossible calendar days (Feb 30, Apr 31) `[Doc Review / parsing]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests (+1) ┬À build green.** `valid()` capped the day at 31 for every month, so a garbage date (e.g. "02/30/2025") could become the auto-named filing date. **Fix:** real per-month day count + leap-year rule. Unit test (incl. non-leap 02/29 rejected, leap 02/29/2024 kept). (`src/shared/files/titleBlockParse.js`.)

### B515 ÔÇö Site Planner: coverage SR set aliased ESRI WKID 102739 (Texas CENTRAL/EPSG:2277) to 2278 ÔåÆ layer failed CLOSED `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests (+1) ┬À build green.** 102739 is a DIFFERENT projection (Central zone) than 2278/102740 (South Central); a Central-zone regional extent run through the 2278 math produced a garbage bbox and marked the layer out-of-coverage across all of Texas ÔÇö violating the module's fail-OPEN guarantee. **Fix:** removed 102739 from `STATEPLANE_2278`, so an unknown SR returns null ÔåÆ unknown ÔåÆ available (fails open). Unit test in `test/coverage.test.js`. (Latent today ÔÇö all current regional layers are South Central.) (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/coverage.js`.)

### B516 ÔÇö Scheduler: PDF-preview postMessage listeners skipped the origin check (one persists to saved data) `[Schedule / security]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt round 2; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1716 tests ┬À build green ┬À esbuild JSX transform of the index.html block OK.** Three preview-iframe message handlers (`planarToggleCollapse`, `planarColResize`, `planarPreviewHeight`) lacked the `e.origin !== window.location.origin` guard the nav/toolbar bridges enforce ÔÇö so a cross-origin page framing `/sequence/` could postMessage `planarColResize` and mutate the user's PERSISTED export column widths. **Fix:** added the same-origin guard to all three (the legitimate same-origin blob preview iframe is unaffected). (`public/sequence/index.html`.) **ÔÅ│ Cross-origin attempt confirmation ÔåÆ V160.**

### B504 ÔÇö Site Planner: truck-court paving double-subtracted a corner bump-out footprint ÔåÆ yield understated impervious/coverage `[Site Planner / yield]` (bug)  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1707 tests (+4) ┬À build green.** Since B492 `relayoutSide` trims a same-side truck court IN to the clear face between corner bump-outs (`usableCourtSpan` ÔåÆ the court element's `w`/`h` already excludes the bump column), but the yield loop (`SitePlanner.jsx`) STILL subtracted the bump's full footprint (`e.w*e.h`) from `paving` when a same-side court existed ÔÇö a stale de-double-count whose overlap no longer exists post-B492. Net: paving/impervious understated by the bump area (~3300 sf on the 55├ù60 default) and `open` overstated by the same; only fired when a building had both a same-side truck court AND a corner bump-out. **Fix:** removed the spurious de-double-count block ÔÇö the court area already excludes the bump, and the bump (type `building`) is counted in `bldg`, so there's no remaining overlap to remove. Premise locked by new `usableCourtSpan` unit tests in `test/dockZones.test.js` (along = full ÔêÆ bumpStart ÔêÆ bumpEnd, asymmetric shift, 1-ft floor). **This clears the entire 2026-06-27 bug-hunt queue (B500/B510, B501ÔÇôB509).**

### B505 ÔÇö Doc Review: Stitcher `loadStitch()` rejection unhandled ÔåÆ noise + stuck `pendingStitch` `[Doc Review / stitch]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1703 tests (+anti-drift) ┬À build green.** `loadStitch()` awaits PDF/Drive/Storage I/O that can reject, but its caller `loadStitch(loadReq).then(() => onConsumeLoad())` had no `.catch` ÔÇö a corrupt/partial PDF surfaced an unhandled rejection AND `onConsumeLoad()` never ran, so the load stayed half-consumed (`loadedId` already set ÔåÆ effect won't re-fire). **Fix:** added `.catch(() => onConsumeLoad && onConsumeLoad())` so the request is always consumed (`Stitcher.jsx`). Anti-drift guard in `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js`. ÔÅ│ Live confirmation of the corrupt-PDF path ÔåÆ V159 (needs a deliberately-bad file).

### B506 ÔÇö Site Planner: device-full autosave `pushModelToCloud().then` had no `.catch` ÔåÆ stuck "saving" badge `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1703 tests ┬À build green.** In the B473 device-full autosave branch the `.then` set "saving" but had no `.catch`; the degrade path awaits a raw supabase upsert that can reject on a transport error, leaving the badge spinning forever (the watchdog is on a different push path). **Fix:** appended `.catch(() => { setSaveStatus("unsaved"); setSavedToCloudOnly(false); setCloudSaveFailed(true); })` (`SitePlanner.jsx`). Anti-drift guard added. ÔÅ│ Live confirmation needs a forced transport failure ÔåÆ V159.

### B507 ÔÇö Site Planner: manual "Save now" device-full `pushModelToCloud().then` had no `.catch` ÔåÆ stuck banner `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1703 tests ┬À build green.** Same class in the manual Save-now handler: a rejected push left "Saving to your accountÔÇª" on screen and never tripped the loud failed state. **Fix:** appended `.catch(() => { setSaveNowMsg(""); setLocalSaveFailed(true); setSavedToCloudOnly(false); })` (`SitePlanner.jsx`). Anti-drift guard added. ÔÅ│ ÔåÆ V159.

### B508 ÔÇö Site Planner: LayerPanel relevance control hardcoded warm-dark hex instead of theme tokens (B341 trap) `[Site Planner / theming]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1703 tests ┬À build green.** The Relevance (Show all/Dim/Hide) active segment used `#3b3a36`/`#fbfaf6` and the stale-age stamp used `#b45309` ÔÇö hardcoded, despite the file's own header rule to use theme tokens (the dark active fill barely separated from the dark frosted panel in dark mode; `contrast-audit.mjs` can't see inline hex). **Fix:** repointed the active segment to `var(--accent)`/`var(--on-accent)` and the stamp to `var(--warn-text)` (`components/LayerPanel.jsx`); no layout change. Anti-drift guard asserts no hardcoded hex + the tokens are used. ÔÅ│ Dark-mode eyeball of the Relevance control ÔåÆ V159.

### B509 ÔÇö Markup: PropertyPanel number/range/enum/color controls had no accessible name `[Shared / Markup / a11y]` (bug) ÔÇö MED  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1703 tests ┬À build green.** Non-bool rows render the caption as a detached `<div>` with no `<label>`/`htmlFor`/`aria-label`, so NumberControl/RangeControl/EnumControl/ColorControl exposed no accessible name (a screen reader announced only "number edit"/"slider"/"combo box") ÔÇö affecting every editable markup property in both workspaces. **Fix:** threaded the schema `label` into each control as `aria-label` (`src/shared/markup/PropertyPanel.jsx`). Anti-drift guard asserts all four controls receive `label` and apply `aria-label`. ÔÅ│ Screen-reader pass ÔåÆ V159.

### B501 ÔÇö Scheduler: deleting a task never recomputed parent roll-ups / cascade ÔåÆ stale summary dates `[Schedule]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1699 tests (+4) ┬À build green ┬À esbuild JSX transform of the 625 KB index.html block OK (board still parses).** `deleteTask`/`deleteTasks` (`public/sequence/index.html`) filtered the removed rows then `renumberTasks(...)` with NO recompute ÔÇö every other structural handler (indent/outdent/paste/move) wraps with `recomputeAfterStructureChange = rollupParentDates(cascadeDates(...))` (the exact class B487 fixed). So a deleted child left its parent's summary bar/row showing a span for work that no longer existed until an unrelated edit re-triggered a rollup. **Fix:** both delete paths now wrap the filtered list with `recomputeAfterStructureChange(...)` before `renumberTasks`. New behavioral unit test (delete the later child ÔåÆ parent end shrinks 06-26ÔåÆ06-22) + an anti-drift guard asserting both handlers carry the recompute wrap (`test/schedulerEngine.test.js`, against the mirrored engine `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`). **ÔÅ│ Running-app click-through ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V158** (the board can't boot headless here ÔÇö vendored React/Babel absent; covered by the unit test + esbuild parse).

### B502 ÔÇö Scheduler: InlineDate stripped the year ÔåÆ silently rewrote a non-current-year date to this year on commit `[Schedule]` (bug) ÔÇö MED, data corruption  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1699 tests ┬À build green ┬À esbuild parse OK.** `InlineDate` (`public/sequence/index.html`) rendered month/day only; on commit `parseFlexDate` defaulted the missing year to the current year, so just opening + Enter/blur mutated a `2025-ÔÇª` date to `2026-ÔÇª` with no warning. **Fix:** seed the display with `toShortDate(value)` (M/D/YY ÔÇö keeps the year, reads back losslessly via `parseFlexDate`), matching every other date editor in the file. Anti-drift guard added. **ÔÅ│ Running-app click-through ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V158.**

### B503 ÔÇö Scheduler: MasterView `fmtDate` lacked the malformed-date guard `fmtD` got in B489 ÔåÆ could render NaN/NaN/yy `[Schedule]` (bug) ÔÇö low  *(bug-hunt 2026-06-27, adversarially confirmed; fixed same lap)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1699 tests ┬À build green ┬À esbuild parse OK.** The dashboard's inline `fmtDate` split on `-` with no presence check (unlike the B489-hardened module-level `fmtD`), so a non-ISO value rendered `NaN/NaN/26` (and `y.slice(2)` could throw on a missing year). **Fix:** added `if (!y||!m||!d) return ""` before formatting. Anti-drift guard added.

### B510 ÔÇö Markup: Dimension tool showed "ÔÇö" instead of its calibrated length `[Shared / Markup]` (bug) ÔÇö HIGH  *(filed AND fixed the same lap per STANDING RULE #1; surfaced by the 2026-06-27 bug-hunt Workflow ÔÇö found by two independent hunters, adversarially confirmed)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1691 tests (+1) ┬À build green.** The Dimension tool is declared `measureOutput:"length"` (`tools.matrix.js`) and rendered via `measureLabel` in `MarkupRenderer.jsx`, but `measureValue` (`src/shared/markup/measure.js`) had no `dimension` branch ÔÇö it fell through to the generic return with no `lengthFt`, so `measureLabel` printed **"ÔÇö"** for every calibrated Dimension (the tool's whole purpose ÔÇö a ticked line labelled with its real length ÔÇö was broken). Verified empirically: pre-fix `measureLabel({kind:"dimension",pts:[{0,0},{10,0}]}, 2)` returned `"ÔÇö"` while the identical distance returned `"20.0 ft"`. **Fix:** `measureValue`'s distance branch now also handles `dimension`, preserving `m.kind` so it reports the calibrated `lengthFt` while callers still render the ticked variant. Deliberately NOT added to `MIN_MEASURE_PTS`/rollup ÔÇö a dimension is an annotation, not a takeoff total, so totals are unchanged. New regression test in `test/takeoff.test.js` (`measureLabel` ÔåÆ "20.0 ft" calibrated / "set scale" uncalibrated / no-throw on empty). Pure-function fix; the on-canvas label is the visible win.

### B500 ÔÇö Pond grading tool produced invalid slope contours + floor (acute-corner spikes, no valid floor, silent mis-report) `[Site Planner / detention]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-27 on the live triangular pond off Hwy 6 N: "the pond drawing tool ÔÇª calculates how the pond curvature/slopes would work ÔÇª does not seem like it worked whatsoever"; civil-engineer read attached; provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B500** = highest real B# (B499) + 1; branch `claude/pond-storage-site-plan-pzkjnf`)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À full suite 1694 tests (+19 new: pondOffset 8, pondContours 11) green ┬À build green ┬À `ui-audit/verify-pond-contours.mjs` 9/9 (real Chromium, logged-out) on the actual acute-triangle repro.** The B497 contours reused the codebase's hand-rolled `offsetPolygon` (per-edge miter/bevel), which **spiked and self-intersected at acute corners**, returned a single ring (no pinch-off / no split), re-formed a bogus same-winding polygon past half-width (so the `aBottom===0` over-taper guard **silently misfired** ÔåÆ wrong area/volume, no warning), and `smoothRing` then rounded the garbage. Root-and-branch fix:
- **`[x]` Robust offset via clipper-lib** (new `lib/pondOffset.js`, pure/unit-tested): `offsetInward(ring, dist)` runs Clipper's `ClipperOffset` with **round joins** (rounds contour corners ÔÇö also how earthwork actually grades) in the planner's **feet frame** (no reprojection), normalising orientation so a negative delta always shrinks. Returns an **array of rings**: `[]` on clean pinch-off, `[r]` normally, `[r1,r2,ÔÇª]` when a basin splits ÔÇö never a spike, never throws (degenerate/bowtie input ÔåÆ `[]`). `maxInwardOffset(ring)` binary-searches the max inscribed reach.
- **`[x]` `pondContours` rewritten** (`lib/pondGeom.js`): each level carries `rings[]`; stops at pinch-off; reports `feasible` + `maxDepth = maxInwardOffset/slope`. `smoothRing` (Chaikin) **deleted** ÔÇö round joins already produce true smooth geometry; smoothing distorted areas.
- **`[x]` `detentionStorage` rewritten** (SitePlanner.jsx): stage areas from `offsetInward`; **average-end-area volume** integrated only over slabs that actually exist (a basin that daylights before design depth no longer inflates the number); returns `feasible`/`maxDepth`. 1-entry memo so panel+label+chip don't re-run the offsets each frame.
- **`[x]` Fail LOUD on infeasibility** (silent-failure rule): renderer draws the real partial contours **plus** a red on-canvas `ÔÜá Too deep ÔÇö max Ôëê NÔÇ▓ at S:1`; panel states the exact max gradeable depth and that the volume shown is for that achievable basin; the on-plan "Holds ÔÇª" line reports the **achievable** depth `(max)`, not the over-stated design depth.
- **Untouched on purpose:** `offsetPolygon` stays for **parcel setbacks** (per-edge variable offset a uniform buffer can't express) and `expandPolygon` for pond "push banks out" ÔÇö neither is the reported bug.
- **Dep:** added `clipper-lib` (CAD-grade polygon-offset engine) to the lazy `SitePlannerApp` chunk (+~29 kB gzip). *(Investigated the civil-engineer-suggested JSTS first; jsts 2.12 ships no package entry point ÔåÆ unimportable under Vite/vitest, so used clipper-lib, which is purpose-built for offsetting and imports cleanly ÔÇö same robust round-join + multipolygon + pinch-off behaviour.)*
- **ÔÅ│ Visual-over-live-aerial eyeball ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V157** (headless draws over the gray surface, not live tiles).

### B124 ÔÇö Data loss: saved work disappeared / reverted to a prior state on Schiel Road and JFK `[Persistence]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS, critical
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö root cause fixed + shipped to `main` (PR #81) and DEPLOYED to live planyr.io; confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial agents both clean, refuter re-ran the test suite).** The core defect was in `pullCloud` (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/storage.js`): it rebuilt the per-user cache from the **cloud list alone**, silently dropping any local-only site the cloud hadn't returned (a not-yet-pushed/slow-network/new site), so the resume's `loadSite(cur)` missed and bounced to the map ÔÇö "my work disappeared," re-firing on a tab-focus `SIGNED_IN` re-emit. Fixed: `pullCloud` now **merges** local+cloud via the pure, unit-tested `mergePulledSites` (storage.js ~L75) ÔÇö local-only work is kept **and re-pushed**, newer-wins on overlap; a redundant same-user `SIGNED_IN` re-emit is skipped; sign-out no longer wipes the per-user cache; cloud confirmed authoritative (data was never deleted in Supabase ÔÇö verified). Remaining ongoing-loss *incidents* are tracked separately (B134/B473 track), not this drop. No new code owed for B124.

### B125 ÔÇö Make cloud save status visible and write failures loud ÔÇö no silent failures `[Persistence]` (feature)
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö shipped (PR #81 + the B473/B474/B485 persistence track); confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial both clean, refuter re-ran 15/15 persistence tests + build).** All core asks are implemented in the tree: a **loud, dismissible red cloud-save-failure banner** + "Retry now" + a ~6s stall watchdog (`SitePlanner.jsx` `cloudSaveFailed`/`cloudPushWithWatchdog`/`retryCloudSave`, `role="alert"`); a **persistent header save-status indicator** (`headerSaveState` ÔåÆ `AppHeader`); a **silent-no-op guard** (drawn-count read-back ÔåÆ `local-save-failed` banner); a **durable IndexedDB fallback** (`lib/localDb.js`, self-healing, cloud authoritative) wired into `storage.js` (write-through + hydrate/migrate + raster eviction); and **beforeunload/visibility synchronous flush** + `flushRegistry.js` + doc-review parity. The genuinely-open remnants are separate filed follow-ons B125 does not require (B479 perf, B483 100%-full-localStorage edge, B474's owner-agreed deferral). Only auth-only/signed-in live-browser verification remains (not blocking).

### B36 ÔÇö Map/GIS minor robustness `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö all five sub-items shipped; confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27, verify + adversarial both clean, refuter ran lint/test 57/57).** (a) statewide-TxGIO county mislabel ÔÇö real fix: `countyAtPoint` (true point-in-county via the TxDOT layer, `lib/jurisdiction.js`) + `COUNTY_NAME_TO_KEY`, relabel wired in `MapFinder.addParcelHit` with the query point threaded through all call sites; unit-tested. (b) evidence-layer opacity slider ÔÇö shipped PR #33. (c) `largestRing` (max-|area|, winding-agnostic so a hole can't win) + area-weighted `ringCentroid` + multipart `outerRingsLngLat` (`lib/arcgis.js`); unit-tested. (d) multi-county straddle ÔÇö moot by design (a point is in one county ÔåÆ `hits[0]`; multi-parcel adds each lot per click). (e) AbortController ÔÇö moot (evidence/address fetches serialized + busy-gated). The non-configured-county (Austin/DFW) relabel edge is carved into the parked B150, not B36. Only a live browser confirmation of the (a) relabel path remains (not blocking).

### B57 ÔÇö Coordinate/units consistency (core verified clean) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö closed by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27). lint 0 ┬À 1684 tests ┬À build green.** The core units/coordinate audit was already verified clean (FT_PER_DEG, lat/lng order, aerial aspect, ppfToZoom inversion, takeoff px┬▓ÔåÆft┬▓, metes-and-bounds, north-up Y-flip), and sub-items (a) underlay Calibrate disabled for a from-map underlay and (c) address-search projecting via the shared 365223 model both shipped in the 2026-06-15 follow-up. The last open sub-item **(b)** is now fixed: `FT_PER_M` in `src/shared/coordinates/index.js` was the **international** foot (`1/0.3048`) while `PROJECT_CRS.unit` is `us-ft` (EPSG:2278) and the projection math uses `US_FT_M = 1200/3937` ÔÇö a ~2 ppm inconsistency. Changed `FT_PER_M` to the **US survey foot** (`3937/1200`) so the lone `metersToFeet` consumer matches the grid; `FT_PER_M`/`metersToFeet` have no other consumers (grep-confirmed), so zero behavioral impact on live code. `test/coordinates.test.js` updated to assert the us-ft value (the two assertions had enshrined the international foot). No headless/UI surface; logged-out-N/A.

### B485 ÔÇö Phone header clips its controls instead of scrolling sideways `[Shared header / mobile]` (bug)  *(surfaced 2026-06-27; minted **B485** = highest real B# across both files (B484) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B481** a concurrent `main` (#373) took)*
`[x]` **DONE + merged (PR #376, branch `claude/resume-planner-persistence-36zqh1`); confirmed + archived by the verify-and-reconcile loop (2026-06-27).** The shared two-row `AppHeader` overran phone width and clipped its controls under `overflow:hidden` + flex-shrink. Fix: new `useNarrow()` (matchMedia `max-width:760px`) gives each header row `overflow-x:auto` (bar hidden via `.no-hscrollbar` in `index.css`) with natural-width zones below the breakpoint so nothing clips ÔÇö it scrolls sideways exactly as V11 specifies; the brand drops to the mark to reclaim width; every change is `narrow ?`-gated so desktop is byte-identical. Verified: lint 0 ┬À full suite green ┬À build green ┬À `ui-audit/verify-phone-layout.mjs` (real iPhone-13 emulation) 10/10 with a regression guard that fails the pre-fix build.
