### B497 ÔÇö Pond storage on the plan: stage contour lines + on-canvas storage, communicated extremely well `[Site Planner / detention]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "add a feature where pond storage calculations is shown on the site plan with contour lines and is communicated extremely well", + two refinements: "make the contours smooth like a pond would be, ie no sharp angles" and "the edge of the pond is where the slope starts when I draw it, no ÔÇª 5ÔÇ▓ buffer"; branch `claude/pond-storage-site-plan-pzkjnf`; minted **B497** = highest real B# (B496) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from provisional **B493ÔåÆB496** as concurrent `main` PRs kept taking B493/B494/B495/B496 while this was in flight; the commit/PR still read B493)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À full suite 1656 tests (+15 new) green ┬À build green ┬À NEW `ui-audit/verify-pond-contours.mjs` 12/12 (real Chromium, logged-out).** A placed detention pond now shows its **basin shape and storage right on the plan**, not just in the side panel:
- **Stage contour lines (the topographic depth rings).** Each ring is the inward offset of the top-of-bank footprint at a depth below the top ÔÇö exactly the construction `detentionStorage` already uses (`offsetPolygon(ring, slope*down)`), so the rings and the storage numbers come from one source and can never disagree. The **water surface** ring is emphasized (solid + light tint, "WS" label), the **basin floor** dashed ("Floor" label); intermediates are thin. Labels read as **real elevations** when a top-of-bank elevation is entered (e.g. "WS 95.0"), else **depth below top** ("WS ÔêÆ1.0ÔÇ▓"). Water anchored to the top of its ring, floor to the bottom, so the callouts frame the centred pond name instead of stacking on it.
- **Smooth, pond-like rings (owner refinement).** A pure **Chaikin corner-cutting** pass (`smoothRing`) rounds the rings at draw time ÔÇö display-only; every area/volume number still reads from the true (un-smoothed) offset polygons.
- **No buffer (owner refinement).** The drawn edge **is** the top of bank where the side slope starts (`down=0` = the footprint); nothing inserts an offset.
- **On-plan storage line.** The pond label gains "Holds N ac-ft ┬À DÔÇ▓ deep" (same `detentionStorage` the panel reads), gated with the rings.
- **Default ON, zoom-aware, degrades cleanly.** Contours show automatically (`det.contours !== false`), with a panel toggle to hide; an **auto** ring interval (~4ÔÇô6 rings, user-overridable) + an optional **top-of-bank elevation** input. The overlay is gated by `detailLabelVisible` (the B149 LOD floor) and skips sub-pixel rings (anti-flicker), so a site-overview stays clean and it reveals on zoom-in. An **over-taper** pond (side slopes meet before full depth) stops at the last valid ring and shows a "Ô£ò slopes meet" marker ÔÇö the on-canvas echo of the panel's existing `aBottom===0` warning.
- **Reuse, no new primitives:** new pure helpers `pondContours` / `smoothRing` / `autoContourInterval` / `contourLabelPoint` live in the existing `lib/pondGeom.js` (15 new unit tests; `offsetPolygon` injected to keep the module React-free); rendering rides the existing `renderElPx` pond branches + the existing `layoutLabels` pool; the model fields (`det.contours`, `det.contourInterval`, `det.tobElev`) are additive/lossless (no migration). One signed-in/visual-over-live-aerial click-through is logged at **V155**.

### B496 ÔÇö Promote file storage to its own **Library** workspace tab (stop overloading Review) `[Library / Doc Review / Shell]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "create a library or drive module instead of trying to do too much with the review module"; owner picked the name **Library** + "move browsing fully into Library"; branch `claude/library-drive-module-fpzane`; minted **B496** = highest real B# (B495) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B493** that concurrent `main` PRs took (B493 cross-module link + B494/B495) while this was in flight; the commit/PR/branch still read B493)*

Lifted it into its own top-level workspace so Review goes back to "open one drawing and mark it up."
- **`[x]` New `library` workspace** (`src/workspaces/library/Library.jsx` + `components/FileBrowser.jsx` *moved* git-tracked from `doc-review/components/`). Registered in the Shell `WORKSPACES` registry, `modulePrefetch`, `route.js` slug maps (`#/library`, `#/project/<id>/library`, `#/all/library`), and the AppHeader `MODULES` tab + accent maps. Lazy-loaded ÔÇö opening Review never pulls Library in and vice-versa.
- **`[x]` Module accent** `--accent-library` **teal `#0E7490`** (index.css light+dark, `palette.js`, `moduleAccent.js`) ÔÇö chosen dark enough that **white-on-teal clears WCAG AA in BOTH themes** (5.36:1), unlike Review's amber which needs dark on-accent. The 5 new pairs are registered in `contrast-audit.mjs` (all PASS). FileBrowser's own chips/drop-zone repointed from `--accent-review` ÔåÆ `--accent-library`/`--on-accent-library`.
- **`[x]` Data layer stays shared, NOT moved.** `reviewStore` / `autofiling` / `fileIndex` stay in `doc-review/lib` (project-scoped, canvas-independent); Library imports them cross-workspace ÔÇö the SAME established pattern as Site Planner importing `ProjectFilesDrawer`. No new backend, tables, or keys.
- **`[x]` Review slimmed down.** Removed the `FileBrowser` import + the `browsing` landing state. With nothing open Review shows a clean empty state ("No drawing open" + a teal **Browse the Library** button + Open PDFÔÇª); the header **­ƒùé Library** button + the open-error banner button now switch to the Library tab (`onShellSwitch("library")`); boot now resumes the last open drawing on mount (project deep-links included, keeping the wrong-project guard). Clicking a file in Library opens it in Review via the **existing** Shell `onOpenReviewInDocReview` intent (already used by the Site Planner files drawer).
- **Left intact (not "Review doing too much"):** the Site Planner's slide-over `ProjectFilesDrawer` (in-context map peek) still opens files into Review unchanged.
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in file-flow click-through ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V154** (the file list is account-gated; logged-out + headless fully green here).

### B495 ÔÇö Flexible outward "layer stack": choose what goes behind a building (road / landscape buffer / sidewalk / parking) via an "Add layer Ôû¥" chooser `[Site Planner / dock features]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "add options to switch parking behind a building for a road, or a landscape buffer behind parking ÔÇª or a road behind the trailer parking's buffer"; owner confirmed: keep the fast "+" default AND add a dropdown; all four layer types; branch `claude/sidewalks-truck-courts-bump-outs-0u7o6v`; minted **B495** = highest real B# (B494) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B493 that concurrent `main` PRs took for B493/B494)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 ┬À full suite 1655 tests (+8) green ┬À build green ┬À NEW `ui-audit/verify-zone-catalog.mjs` 6/6 (real Chromium, logged-out).** The two hardcoded "stack outward from a building wall" sequences (dock = courtÔåÆtrailerÔåÆbuffer; rear = sidewalkÔåÆparkingÔåÆrows) are now extensible: the fast ´╝ï keeps its preset, and a new **"Add layer Ôû¥"** chooser appends a specific catalog layer.
- **`[x]` Data-driven catalog** (`lib/dockZones.js` `ZONE_CATALOG` + `catalogDepthDefault`): court/trailer/buffer/sidewalk/parking/road, each with element type, default depth, valid sides (`dock`/`nondock`/`any`), and a `terminal` flag. **Road is terminal** (nothing stacks behind it); **landscape buffer is one concept** (`type:"landscape"`, `buffer:true`) shared with the dock buffer; trailer/court stay dock-only (B416).
- **`[x]` Generalized layout** `layoutZoneByKind(b,side,i,depths,kinds,opts)` lays an ARBITRARY chain (strip/trailer; a road along a wall is geometrically a strip ÔÇö its curbs are a render detail). `layoutZone` is now a thin wrapper ÔåÆ **byte-identical** for the court/trailer/buffer sequence (parity test guards it).
- **`[x]` Generic `prevZone` chain** (SitePlanner `nextInChain`/`chainFromHead`/`dockChainOnSide`): the dock sequence + any appended layers are ONE linked list rooted at the wall. `nextInChain` follows `prevZone` and **falls back to the legacy `forCourt`/`forTrailer` bonds**, so old saved plans walk identically with **no migration**. `relayoutSide` lays the whole chain; `removeWithChildren` + `strandedZoneIds` cascade on `prevZone`; LIFO "ÔêÆ" peels the outermost (appended road ÔåÆ buffer ÔåÆ trailer ÔåÆ court).
- **`[x]` "Add layer Ôû¥" chooser** in the building's Dock-features panel: two rows ÔÇö **"Behind the dock stack"** (road / landscape buffer, only once the court stack exists) and **"Rear / non-dock sides"** (sidewalk / parking / landscape / road) ÔÇö each applying to every eligible side. Dock appends join the relayout chain (`noFit`); non-dock appends are placed flush beyond the outermost element as ordinary wall-kids (the proven `fitKid` resize path; `road` added to `WALL_KID_TYPES`). Sidewalk/parking reuse the existing `addSidewalkSide`/`addParkingRowSide`.
- **Verified headless:** seeded a cross-dock building, built the preset stack, then via the chooser appended a **road** (bonds `prevZone`ÔåÆbuffer, carries `travelW`, sits flush beyond the buffer at full 600ÔÇ▓ wall) on both dock sides, a **landscape buffer** on the rear sides, and confirmed LIFO removal peels the road first (buffer kept). Pure planner canvas ÔåÆ no signed-in path owed.
- **Known limitation (filed, minor):** a DOCK-appended layer's depth edited via the generic element editor reverts to its catalog default on the next building-resize relayout (it's not yet wired to a `zd` editor). Non-dock appended layers (wall-kids) edit normally. Follow-up only; the add/place/remove flow is complete.

### B494 ÔÇö Schedule module loading overlay sticks for a full 9 s "sometimes" (slow/buggy ready signal) `[Scheduler / loader]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "fix the loading icon for the scheduling module, seems kind of slow or buggy sometimes"; minted **B494** = highest real B# (B493) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B493 a concurrent `main` took for the cross-module-link feature)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/pdf-viewer-quality-x40e5a`). lint 0 ┬À 1641 tests ┬À build green ┬À NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-scheduler-loader.mjs` 3/3 (+ diagnostic `observe-scheduler-loader.mjs`).**
- **Repro (headless, observed):** switch to the Schedule module ÔåÆ the "assembling schedule" loading overlay (`ModuleLoader`, B224) sat visible for the FULL **9 s** before cross-fading out, regardless of how fast the embedded `/sequence/` app was. Root cause: the overlay only dropped on the embedded app's FIRST `planar:nav-state` message, whose ONLY backstop was a lone `setTimeout(ÔÇª, 9000)`. So whenever that first signal was slow or missed ÔÇö a network hiccup, the embed's deps loading slowly, the embed not yet interactive ÔÇö the loader stuck for a flat 9 seconds (the "slow/buggy sometimes").
- **Fix (`Scheduler.jsx` + a tiny responder in `public/sequence/index.html`):** make the ready signal **reliable + bounded**. (1) **Handshake** ÔÇö on the iframe's `onLoad` the shell posts `planar:nav-request` (retried ~every 380 ms for ~2.3 s, in case the embed's own listener isn't attached yet); the embedded app answers by re-emitting `nav-state` from `latestData` (a no-op until its data has loaded), so a missed/slow first emit can no longer strand the loader ÔÇö it drops the instant the embed is interactive. (2) **Bounded fallbacks** ÔÇö reveal the embed ~2.5 s after its document loads even if it never answers (a slow/broken embed shouldn't hold a full-screen spinner), with a 6 s absolute backstop if `onLoad` itself never fires. `markReady()` + a ref make every path idempotent (one cross-fade). The proactive `nav-state` fast path is unchanged, so a clean boot still drops the loader immediately.
- **Verified:** `verify-scheduler-loader.mjs` 3/3 (real built shell, the embed stubbed via request-interception so the handshake/fallback paths actually run ÔÇö the real embed's CDN is blocked in the sandbox so its iframe never finishes loading here): a **responsive** embed drops the loader at **~520 ms** via the handshake (was 9000 ms); a **silent** embed is bounded to **~3000 ms** (onLoad+2.5 s), not 9 s, and doesn't drop prematurely. Diagnostic `observe-scheduler-loader.mjs` shows the sandbox worst case (embed never signals, `onLoad` never fires) improved 9.5 s ÔåÆ 6.5 s (the backstop).
- **Follow-up filed (deliberately deferred):** the embed still `await`s a Supabase round-trip (no local cache) before first paint ÔåÆ genuine first-load instant-ness is **B495**.
- **ÔÅ│ Live/signed-in check ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V153** (the real embedded app's `nav-request` responder needs its CDN + Supabase, blocked here).

### B493 ÔÇö Connect projects across modules: a schedule linked to a site (also CLOSES B477) `[Cross-module / Schedule Ôåö Site Ôåö Review]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "connect the projects within modules, like I have pappadoupolos in schedule and site planner but I don't have a way to connect themÔÇª or create a schedule connected to a site"; branch `claude/cross-module-project-connections-bemcqi`; minted **B493** = highest real B# across both files (B492) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B491 that concurrent `main` PRs took for B491/B492 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/cross-module-project-connections-bemcqi`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1629 unit tests green ┬À build green ┬À NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-cross-module-link.mjs` 4/4.**
- **The gap:** a project = a Site Planner **site group** (`group_id`). SiteÔåöReview already follow the same project (Review reads the shared `sites` table by `project_id=group_id`), but the **Schedule** is the embedded `/sequence/` iframe with its OWN backend + numeric project ids ÔÇö "Pappadoupolos" there had no link to the site. This ships the link + the project-aware Schedule tab, which also **closes B477** (the project-selection half of the bridge).
- **Owner shape (from Q&A):** linking is **suggest-and-confirm** (offer a same-named match + manual pick, never auto-link); the **payoff is the top-header tabs carrying the project** (no new jump buttons); **all three modules** read as one connected project.
- **Where the link lives (mirror; schedule authoritative):** the pairing is stored on the schedule project (`linkedSiteId`/`linkedSiteName`, free in the already-serialized `hs-v1` blob); a lightweight HINT (`scheduleProjectId`/`scheduleProjectName`, Site Model **schema v8ÔåÆ9**, additive) is mirrored onto the site so the Site Planner shows "has a schedule" without booting the iframe. The two live in separate backends; the Shell brokers the mirror write.
- **Navigation:** `Scheduler.jsx` now honors the routed `projectId` ÔÇö posts `planar:nav-select-by-site` to activate the linked schedule, and pushes the active schedule's `linkedSiteId` back up via `onProjectChange` so the Site/Review tabs carry the same project. Land on a site with no linked schedule ÔåÆ a `LinkSchedulePanel` resolution card: **Create a schedule for this site** / **Link an existing one** (same-named suggestion + manual picker).
- **Files:** `siteModel.js` (v9 fields + migrate), `storage.js` (`setScheduleLink`/`scheduleLinkOf`), `shared/projects/projectModel.js`+`projects.js` (surface the hint + pure `suggestNameMatch`/`normalizeProjectName`), `public/sequence/index.html` (nav-state carries the link; new inbound `nav-select-by-site`/`nav-link`/`nav-create-linked`; outbound `link-changed`; `addProject` accepts name+link), `scheduler/lib/navState.js` (`findBySiteId` + carry link through `sanitizeProjects`), `scheduler/Scheduler.jsx` + new `scheduler/components/LinkSchedulePanel.jsx`, `app/Shell.jsx` (`onScheduleLinkChanged` ÔåÆ mirror), `shared/ui/ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx` (­ƒôà connectedness chip). +28 unit tests across navState/projects/siteModel.
- **Verified:** the new React-wrapper path end-to-end headless (panel ÔåÆ suggested match ÔåÆ link mirrors `scheduleProjectId` onto the site store ÔåÆ panel closes once linked) via the same-origin bridge contract; the embedded `index.html` edits parse clean (Babel, zero page errors).
- **ÔÅ│ Live-edge (signed-in, both backends):** the genuine cross-iframe round-trip needs the Schedule's own Supabase (unreachable from the sandbox) ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md **V152**.
### B158 ÔÇö Replace inline Ô£ò delete button with right-click context menu on site list rows `[Site Planner / UI]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B158)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 errors ┬À 1613 tests ┬À build green ┬À NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-b158-site-row-context-menu.mjs` 9/9 (real Chromium, logged-out).** Most of the target state had **already shipped under B168** ÔÇö the inline single-click Ô£ò on a site row was removed (only the non-destructive Ôèò locate icon remains on hover) and the right-click menu (`statusMenu` in `MapFinder.jsx`, opened from a card row OR a map marker) already carries both **RenameÔÇª** (ÔåÆ inline-edit rename flow) and **Delete projectÔÇª** (ÔåÆ the existing confirmation modal), positioned at the cursor and clamped to the viewport, dismissing on click-outside. The one unmet acceptance criterion was **Escape-to-close**: the menu closed on click-outside but ignored Escape.
- **Fix:** added a `useEffect` (keyed on `statusMenu`) in `MapFinder.jsx` that listens for `keydown` and closes the menu on `Escape`, matching the click-outside behavior the B158 acceptance calls for.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-b158-site-row-context-menu.mjs` 9/9 ÔÇö site row renders; Ôèò locate present on hover; no inline Ô£ò; right-click opens the menu with Rename + Delete project; **Escape closes it**; click-outside closes it; re-open + Rename opens the inline edit input.
- **Note on the original spec:** B158 proposed a brand-new two-item portal menu, but the existing `statusMenu` (status picker + share + Rename + Delete) is the established one-menu pattern (B7/B168 deliberately consolidated to "one menu, not two"), so the fix completes that menu rather than forking a second one. The Ôèò locate icon behavior is unchanged.

### B492 ÔÇö Bump-outs vs. sidewalks & truck courts: sidewalk should span the full building side; truck court should pull in between bumps + be length-editable `[Site Planner / dock features]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "sidewalks should default to the full side of the building when I add or edit bump outs ÔÇª I can't edit truck courts length along the dock and [it should] get pulled in when I put a bump out"; branch `claude/sidewalks-truck-courts-bump-outs-0u7o6v`; minted **B492** = highest real B# (B491) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B488** that concurrent `main` PRs (#377 PDF-viewer / #380 schedule-output / pen-test) took for B488ÔÇôB491 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. lint 0 errors ┬À full suite 1625 tests (+11) green ┬À build green ┬À NEW `ui-audit/verify-b492-bump-sidewalk-court.mjs` 11/11 (real Chromium, logged-out).** Three coupled defects in how a building's corner **bump-outs** (dog-ears) interact with its **sidewalks** and its **truck court**, fixed at the shared geometry so the canvas + the panel agree:
- **`[x]` Sidewalk now spans the FULL building side incl. the bump-out.** Was: a bump-out grew the perpendicular sidewalk by a *fixed* 60ÔÇ▓ (`adjustSidewalkForBump`), ignoring the bump's real projection and not following a later resize. Now: new pure `sidewalkSpanForBumps(b, swSide, bumps)` (`lib/dogEar.js`, unit-tested) recomputes the strip's run + along-centre from the wall length **plus every bump-out that lengthens that wall**; `sidewalkFullRunPatch`/`relayoutBumpSidewalks` (SitePlanner) re-lay it on bump **add / remove / resize** and when a sidewalk is added to a wall that already has bumps ÔÇö preserving the strip's thickness + outward offset. (Headless: left/right sidewalks 300ÔÇ▓ÔåÆ**420ÔÇ▓** with two 60ÔÇ▓ bumps; back to 300ÔÇ▓ on removal.)
- **`[x]` Truck court PULLS IN to the clear dock face between the bump-outs.** Was: `layoutZone` hard-locked the court to the full wall (`along = b.w/b.h`), so the paving overlapped the bump corners (only the dock *doors* pulled in). Now: pure `usableCourtSpan(full, bumpStart, bumpEnd)` + an optional 5th `opts` arg on `layoutZone` (zone 0 only) pull the court in to `full ÔêÆ bumpStart ÔêÆ bumpEnd`, centred on the clear face ÔÇö the **same `bumpAlong` model the door renderer already uses**, so doors + court agree. Trailer/buffer keep the full wall (a 60ÔÇ▓ bump doesn't reach them). Wired via `courtBumpOpts` in `relayoutSide` (so add-zone / depth-edit / building-resize all benefit) + an explicit re-lay on bump add/remove/resize. (Headless: court 600ÔÇ▓ÔåÆ**490ÔÇ▓** with two 55ÔÇ▓ bumps, stays centred.)
- **`[x]` Truck-court length is now USER-EDITABLE along the dock.** New **ÔÇ£Truck court length (ft)ÔÇØ** inline field on the selected court (next to its depth), `setCourtLengthAll`/`courtLengthShown` store `alongLen` per dock side; relayout caps a typed length to the clear face so it never re-overlaps a bump. (Headless: set 300ÔÇ▓ ÔåÆ court along-span = 300ÔÇ▓.)
- **Reuse, no new primitives:** reuses `bumpSidewalkSide`, `dogEarSize`, `SIDE_N`, `rot2`, `swSide`, `findCourtIn`, `relayoutSide`, `outwardUnit`; the old fixed-increment `adjustSidewalkForBump`/`isBumpSidewalk` were deleted (no remaining callers). Logged-out-verifiable (pure planner canvas) ÔÇö no signed-in path owed.

### B489 ÔÇö Schedule module: output (export / dashboard / Gantt render) bugs `[Schedule / output]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "now check output bugs and ship fixes"; the output counterpart to B487's input batch; branch `claude/schedule-input-bugs-jtfplb`; minted **B489** = highest real B# (B488) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1627 tests (+14) ┬À build green ┬À esbuild JSX OK ┬À NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` 7/7 + input harness `verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 (no regression).** A fan-out review of every output path the scheduler PRODUCES (web/JSON/PDF export, the on-screen Gantt, the cross-project Dashboard) found defects where the output disagreed with the data or showed a stale brand. Fixed at the output boundary; pure logic extracted + unit-tested.
- **`[x]` Web Snapshot (.html) + JSON export ÔÇö stale brand & unguarded values.** The snapshot hardcoded a **"Hillwood Schedule"** heading, `<title>planar`, and a `hillwood-schedule-export.html` filename (and JSON `hillwood-schedule.json`) regardless of project ÔÇö all retired brands (CLAUDE.md: brand is **Planyr**). Now the title is the real project name (single) or **"Planyr Schedule"** (multiple), the filename is `planyr-schedule-<date>.html` / `planyr-schedule.json`; percent guards `greenÔåÆ100`/`||0` (was `undefined%`), duration uses `Number(t.duration)||0` (no bare "d"), and the status color is `escapeHtml`'d.
- **`[x]` PDF / Print Exhibit Gantt (`buildGanttSVG`) ÔÇö NaN geometry + wrong shapes.** An **unscheduled** task (blank/invalid dates) produced `NaN` SVG coords (an invisible, broken row) ÔÇö it's now skipped and tagged **"Unscheduled"** like the on-screen Gantt. A **duration-0 parent** drew as a single milestone diamond ÔÇö it now renders as a **summary bracket** (the `isParent` branch is checked before `isMilestone`). Dependency **arrows** skipped plain-number predecessors (seed/imported shape) ÔÇö they now `normPreds` like the table. The exhibit **table %Done** now matches the bar's `greenÔåÆ100` convention.
- **`[x]` On-screen Gantt ÔÇö duration-0 parent.** Same B2 fix: a duration-0 parent renders as a bracket, not a diamond (`(isMilestone && !isSummary)`).
- **`[x]` Dashboard / reports (`MasterView`) ÔÇö parent status wrong + stale.** Parent rows ran `computeDisplayHealth` on their own stale `health` instead of the **worst-of-children rolled** status ÔÇö so a parent of a red task showed gray, got **dropped from the "at-risk" filter**, and sorted wrong. Now uses a shared `computeRolledHealth` (extracted to module scope; the project grid's `rolledHealthMap` reuses the SAME helper so the two can't drift), attaches `_disp` per row, and the memos recompute on `data.settings`/`NOW` so overdue/due-soon + CF-rule edits stay live.
- **`[x]` `computeDisplayHealth` overdue rule** no longer flags a **100%-complete** task overdue (was only guarded behind the `completeGreen` toggle); **`fmtD`** guards a malformed (non-ISO) date so it never prints `NaN/NaN/yy`.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` 7/7 ÔÇö board boots; web snapshot carries the Planyr brand (not Hillwood/planar), the right filename, and no `undefined%`/`NaN`/bare-`d`; the Gantt view and the Dashboard both render rows with no page errors. Plus `computeRolledHealth` units (incl. a random-tree match against the prior inline algorithm) + anti-drift string guards in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. *(Sandbox quirk: the board's React/Babel CDNs are closed to the browser here, so the harness used pre-downloaded copies via `SEQ_VENDOR=<dir>`; in CI/normal envs run it with no env var.)*
- **ÔÅ│ Two running-app click-throughs ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V151** (the PDF/Print exhibit's "Unscheduled" tag + summary-bracket render; a parent's rolled color/at-risk inclusion in the Dashboard) ÔÇö not cleanly drivable headless (the PDF preview), covered here by unit + anti-drift + the boot/render checks.
- **Reviewer scope notes (NOT bugs):** Gantt bars use calendar-day spans by design (correct); the leaf-only rolled **cost** sum is the documented B212 model (left as-is). The Web Snapshot exporting all projects (vs the PDF's selected subset) is now honest via the multi-project "Planyr Schedule" title.
- **Follow-up (owner, same session) ÔÇö export filenames now match the Site Planner.** New shared `scheduleExportName(projects[, date])` (mirrored to `scheduler-engine.mjs`, 6 unit tests) produces the Site Planner's exact PDF/PNG format (`src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/printSheet.js` `sheetFileName`): **"YYYY.MM.DD {Project} - Schedule"** (single project ÔåÆ its name; multiple ÔåÆ Planyr; filesystem-illegal chars stripped via the same rule). Applied to all three exports ÔÇö Web Snapshot `.html`, JSON `.json`, and the PDF/Print Exhibit `<title>` (which the browser's "Save as PDF" uses as the filename). Replaces the prior `planyr-schedule-<iso>.html` / `planyr-schedule.json` / cover-label `<title>`. Headless `verify-schedule-output-bugs.mjs` updated + 7/7.

### B488 ÔÇö Document Review PDF viewer quality: wire pdf.js support assets (fonts/CMaps/ICC/WASM) + supersample the detail layer + deeper zoom & snappier settle `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task ÔÇö quality)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 "improve the PDF viewer qualityÔÇª I'm sure it's lacking somehow"; minted **B488** = highest real B# (B487) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B486 that a concurrent `main` (#374/#376) took for the team-RLS + schedule-input fixes)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/pdf-viewer-quality-x40e5a`). lint 0 ┬À 1599 tests ┬À build green ┬À NEW headless `verify-pdfjs-assets.mjs` 9/9 (incl. a fixÔåöcontrol A/B) + `verify-docreview-viewer.mjs` 13/13 (no regression).**
- **ROOT GAP:** `loadPdf` called `getDocument({ data })` with NONE of pdf.js v6's asset URLs ÔÇö but v6 ships its render-support assets as on-disk folders (`pdfjs-dist/{standard_fonts,cmaps,iccs,wasm}`) that are NOT bundled into the worker. Verified against the installed worker source: without `wasmUrl` BOTH the WASM and the JS-fallback image decoders fail to load (both build their URL from `wasmUrl`), so **JBIG2 (scanned B&W) and JPEG2000/JPX (scanned colour/aerial) images decode to BLANK**; ICC colour is explicitly disabled (`warn("No ICC color space support due to missing wasmUrl")`); Symbol/ZapfDingbats + CID/CJK glyphs drop without `standardFontDataUrl`/`cMapUrl`. Exactly the surfaces a survey/scan review hits.
- **FIX:** (1) new inline vite plugin `pdfjsAssets()` (`vite.config.js`) exposes the four folders at `<base>pdfjs/<folder>/` ÔÇö streamed from node_modules in dev (`configureServer`), emitted verbatim into the build (`generateBundle`, fixed `fileName` ÔåÆ no content-hashing) so they stay in lock-step with the installed pdfjs version (no committed, drift-prone blobs). (2) `loadPdf` (`doc-review/lib/pdf.js`) passes `cMapUrl`+`cMapPacked` / `standardFontDataUrl` / `iccUrl` / `wasmUrl` derived from `import.meta.env.BASE_URL` (root-absolute ÔåÆ resolves identically on the main thread, inside the worker, and under any deploy subpath). Production-safe: no `/* /index.html` SPA catch-all and the `/assets/*` rule never matches `/pdfjs/*` (B449), and Cloudflare serves real static files before consulting `_redirects`.
- **ALSO (crispness/responsiveness, same module ÔÇö high-value low-risk set from the audit synthesis):** the DETAIL layer now supersamples to a 2├ù density floor even on a 1├ù monitor (`DETAIL_DENSITY_TARGET`/`DETAIL_DENSITY_CAP`, `renderBudget.backingScale`, still budget-clamped) for cleaner anti-aliased CAD linework/text ÔÇö the residual "softer than Bluebeam" gap left after B415 (which only restored *device* density); the settle debounce dropped **140ÔåÆ90 ms** (sharp detail returns sooner after a gesture); `VIEW_MAX` **6ÔåÆ10** (read the finest survey bearing/dimension text); the whole-page backdrop floor `BACKDROP_PX_BUDGET` **8e6ÔåÆ16e6** (the soft floor *during* a gesture on a retina E-size sheet rises Ôëê73ÔåÆ103 dpi; no-op on a 1├ù monitor; under Safari's single-canvas cap); the detail pan-halo margin **0.25ÔåÆ0.40** (a decisive flick-pan stays sharp to its leading edge); and the high-dpi density cap **2ÔåÆ2.5** (a 3├ù/4├ù panel renders a bit denser). +4 budget unit tests (15 total).
- **Verified:** `verify-pdfjs-assets.mjs` 9/9 ÔÇö serves all four asset types (200; wasm as `application/wasm`); the pdf.js **WORKER** fetches `FoxitDingbats.pfb` + `FoxitSymbol.pfb` from `/pdfjs/standard_fonts/` (a CONTROL with the getDocument wiring reverted drops to 6/8: no worker font fetch + logs "Cannot load system font: ZapfDingbats/Symbol"); zero degradation warnings; sheet non-blank; detail backing = **2.00├ù** on a 1├ù display. `verify-docreview-viewer.mjs` 13/13 ÔÇö zoom-anchor/pan/sheet-switch/fit/count/drag/text all unaffected (also repointed its stale "Library" boot to the `#markup` route, B418).
- **Follow-ups filed (deliberately deferred):** remaining render-engine polish ÔåÆ **B489**; an optional-content (OCG) layer toggle ÔåÆ **B490**.
- **ÔÅ│ Live-edge:** production serving of `/pdfjs/*` on planyr.io ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md **V149**. Verified this session against the Cloudflare **branch-preview** deploy (same Pages project + build that serves planyr.io): all four `/pdfjs/*` asset types return 200 with correct content-types (`application/wasm`, `application/x-font-type1`, `application/vnd.iccprofile`, `application/octet-stream`) and none falls through to the SPA `index.html`; the post-merge planyr.io curl is the last confirmation.

### B487 ÔÇö Schedule module: input edits silently dropped, mangled, or left stale state `[Schedule / inputs]` (bug batch)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-27 ÔÇö "find all the bugs you can as it relates to the inputs for the schedule module and fix them and ship"; branch `claude/schedule-input-bugs-jtfplb`; minted **B487** = highest real B# (B486, the concurrent team-RLS fix) + 1 ÔÇö the B526 hit in BACKLOG-DONE is a CSS hex, not an id)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified. lint 0 ┬À 1613 tests (+16) ┬À build green ┬À esbuild JSX transform OK ┬À NEW headless `ui-audit/verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 (real Chromium, logged-out).** A fan-out review of every user-input path in the embedded scheduler (`public/sequence/index.html`) found six places where a reasonable typed value was silently lost, mangled, or left the model inconsistent. All fixed at the input boundary; the engine itself was already hardened (B246ÔÇôB251).
- **`[x]` Predecessor edits validated (self / unknown / cycle).** New pure `validatePredEdit(tasks, id, parsed)` (in index.html + mirrored to `ui-audit/stress/scheduler-engine.mjs`, 7 unit tests): drops self-references (existing Fix #16), references to task ids that don't exist in the project (were stored + displayed as a live dep but did nothing ÔÇö `cascadeDates` filtered them), and references that would close a **circular dependency** (AÔåÆBÔåÆA produced silently-arbitrary dates) ÔÇö each surfaced with a toast. Wired into `applyUpdate`.
- **`[x]` Grid date input hardened.** A date cell can now be **cleared** (empty ÔåÆ `""`, was silently reverted); an **unreadable** date gives a toast ("Couldn't read that dateÔÇª") instead of silently reverting; and **Finish before Start** is rejected with a toast instead of silently collapsing the task to a 0-day milestone and discarding the typed date.
- **`[x]` Finish on an unscheduled task.** Setting a Finish on a task with no Start used to set `duration = ""` (rendered a bare "d") and the typed finish was then wiped by the cascade (`calcEnd("",ÔÇª)===""`). It now anchors a 1-day task on that date (start=end, duration=1). Added a defensive duration-cell render guard so a stray empty duration can never show a bare "d".
- **`[x]` Indent / outdent / paste now recompute roll-ups.** `indentTask`, `outdentTask`, `indentTaskById`, `outdentTaskById`, and `pasteTaskAfter` changed `parentId` but never ran `cascadeDates`/`rollupParentDates`, so the old and new parents kept **stale summary start/end/duration** until the next in-subtree date edit. New shared `recomputeAfterStructureChange` (= `rollupParentDates(cascadeDates(...))`, the same recompute `updateTask` runs) is applied in all five handlers before renumber.
- **`[x]` Master ("All tasks") grid duration clamp.** It lacked the project grid's `Math.min(100000, ÔÇª)` upper bound, so a fat-fingered duration there could freeze the layout. Clamp added to match.
- **`[x]` Export cover fields escaped.** The PDF/print cover **Date** (`cfg.docDate`) and **Prepared for** (`cfg.preparedFor`) were interpolated raw while their four siblings used `escapeHtml` ÔÇö a typed `<`/`&`/quote corrupted the cover (and was an injection vector into the generated preview). Both now use `escapeHtml`.
- **Verified here (logged-out headless):** `verify-schedule-input-bugs.mjs` 8/8 ÔÇö board boots with all fixes in (proves the whole Babel block still parses), and self-ref / nonexistent-id / circular-dependency / Finish-before-Start / unreadable-date each toast with no uncaught errors. Plus `validatePredEdit` units + `rollupParentDates` move-rollup unit + anti-drift string guards in `test/schedulerEngine.test.js`. *(Sandbox quirk: the board's React/Babel CDNs are closed to the browser here, so the harness used locally pre-downloaded copies via `SEQ_VENDOR=<dir>`; in CI/normal envs run it with no env var.)*
- **ÔÅ│ Three running-app click-throughs ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V148** (indent roll-up widening visible in the grid; Finish-on-blank-task showing "1d"; export-cover escaping in the generated PDF) ÔÇö not drivable as cleanly headless, covered here by unit + anti-drift + the boot check.
- **Scope note (NOT a bug):** the on-screen Gantt has **no bar drag/resize** (bars are click-to-select only; the only chart drag is whole-canvas pan, and the Snap/Free toggle drives the gridÔåöGantt split divider) ÔÇö so there's no second date-input path that could disagree with the typed/`addBD` path. New/inserted tasks are intentionally born blank (unscheduled until given a date or predecessor). Both confirmed during the review.

### B486 ÔÇö SECURITY: a non-owner team member could RE-HOME a shared project to another team `[Site Planner / Team / RLS]` (bug, security)  *(found 2026-06-27 by an owner-requested Team Workspaces bug audit; minted **B486** = highest real B# (B485) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fix WRITTEN + committed (`db/team_rehome_guard.sql`); ÔÅ│ owner must RUN it in Supabase to activate (like every team migration). lint 0 / build green (SQL-only + docs ÔÇö no app code).**
- **The bug:** `db/team_sharing.sql`'s `update own or team ÔÇª` policies enforce sharing via WITH CHECK, whose second branch `(team_id is not null and is_team_member(team_id))` only checks the RESULTING team_id. RLS WITH CHECK can't see the OLD row, so it can't distinguish "kept the same team" from "moved to a different team." A NON-OWNER member who belongs to two teams could therefore UPDATE the owner's shared row and change its `team_id` from team X to team Y (a team the member is on) ÔÇö re-homing the owner's project and cutting team X (and the owner, if not on Y) out of access. Contradicts the documented owner-only re-home/unshare intent. (Unshare-to-null was already owner-gated ÔÇö only re-home-to-another-team slipped through.)
- **Why it's not an emergency (but should be run before teams go live):** it requires a member on two teams with the project shared to one ÔÇö and the team feature isn't actively used yet (no invites sent; B406's signed-in round-trip is still owed, V118). So no real team with overlapping members exists today; the exposure is latent.
- **The fix (`db/team_rehome_guard.sql`):** a BEFORE UPDATE trigger `guard_team_rehome()` on `sites`/`doc_reviews`/`file_facts` that raises `42501` if `team_id` changes and `old.user_id <> auth.uid()` ÔÇö i.e. only the row OWNER may change `team_id` (share/unshare/re-home); teammates edit CONTENT freely (team_id unchanged ÔåÆ allowed). Idempotent; SECURITY INVOKER (reads OLD/NEW + auth.uid(), no RLS bypass). Audited the rest of the team surface too (teams.js, teams.sql helpers + invite-claim, casUpsert owner-preservation, team_storage.sql) ÔÇö all sound; this was the one real gap.
- **ÔÅ│ Owner step (handed over + OWNER-TODO):** run `db/team_rehome_guard.sql` in the Supabase SQL editor. Verify (2 accounts) ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V147.

<!-- archived 2026-06-26: B437/B472/B475/B476 were already fixed + merged to main but left stale in BACKLOG.md Open; moved here per the move-to-DONE-on-ship rule. -->

### B437 ÔÇö Callout tool missing from the Document Review rail (matrixÔåörail drift) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug/feature)  *(found 2026-06-24 by the B432 loop)*
`[x]` **DONE (wired by a prior session; VERIFIED + archived 2026-06-26).** `callout` is now in `DocReview.jsx`'s `TOOLS` rail with its full `point`-mode placement (first click = leader tip/target, second click = text-box anchor ÔåÆ inline editor ÔåÆ commit), double-click to edit, and rendering via the shared `MarkupRenderer` (the same callout path the Site Planner uses). The stale Open entry described the gap as still-open; it isn't.
- **Verified 2026-06-26:** matrixÔåöschema conformance for `callout` passes (`schemaForMarkup({kind:"callout"})` keys === `propsForTool("callout")` ÔÇö both `[align,bold,fill,fillOpacity,fontColor,fontSize,italic,lineHeight,padding,stroke,underline]`), `callout` is in `toolsForWorkspace("doc")`, and headless `ui-audit/verify-b437-callout.mjs` 6/6 (logged-out, fixture PDF): the `tool-callout` rail button is present and ARMS (`aria-pressed=true`), no JS crash. (The two-click draw + inline editor isn't drivable by synthetic CDP events headless ÔÇö same limitation noted for paste in V120 ÔÇö so it's exercised by the auth-gated `e2e/markup-tools.spec.js` Section B, which now arms callout instead of skipping it.)

### B472 ÔÇö Restore the parking-field split as a true EXPLODE into individual rows + aisles `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1" ÔÇö "with a parking field selected there's no longer a tool to split it into its rows + drive aisles; it worked before"; minted **B472** = highest real B# across both files (B471) + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/restore-parking-split-tool-hgdg7f`).** *NOTE ÔÇö this session was push-to-branch only; no PR/merge to `main` was opened (next session/owner merges). Kept in Open until merged.*
- **GROUNDED THE REPORT FIRST (git log/blame).** The split control was **NOT removed** ÔÇö not in the markup-engine unification (B423ÔÇôB432) the brief flagged, nor anywhere else (zero removing commits). `splitParkingRows` + the pure `splitParkingPieces` (B130) were intact and wired in **two** places (the Parking panel + the right-click "Parking" menu). So the "lost in the consolidation" hypothesis is **refuted**; nothing was reintroduced as a fork.
- **The REAL gap (why the owner saw it as gone/wrong):** the existing tool split a field into double-loaded **MODULE** elements ÔÇö each piece *still* bundled 2 rows + 1 aisle ÔÇö so a 2-module field gave a **2-element** result, and it was hidden below 3 rows. That is exactly the owner's complaint: *"do not collapse the aisle into a row or force a 2-element result."*
- **Fix ÔÇö a true explode into the field's constituent bands.** New pure `explodeParkingBands(h, sd, ai)` in `lib/parking.js` returns the ordered `{kind:"row"|"aisle", depth}` sequence (rows pair into double-loaded modules exactly as the field was built ÔÇö aisles = Ôîêrows/2Ôîë; depths always sum to `h`, so stall count + pavement are preserved). `splitParkingRows` now maps each piece to an **independent, separately selectable element**: a **stall row ÔåÆ `type:"parking"`** (one-row depth, renders a single row) and a **drive aisle ÔåÆ `type:"paving"`** (a plain lane). Counts scale: single-loaded (1 row+aisle) ÔåÆ **2**, double-loaded ÔåÆ **3** (row, aisle, row), 8-row field ÔåÆ **12** (8 rows + 4 aisles).
- **Behaviour decisions made explicit (per the brief):** *destructive but cleanly UNDOABLE* ÔÇö replaces the parent + `pushHistory()` so Ctrl+Z restores it (never vaporizes geometry); pieces are editable-layer geometry (never touches any imported backdrop); child centres are derived from the parent edges (rotated local offsets, depths sum to `h`) so **no coordinate drift**; curbs auto-suppress at the new seams via the existing `edgeAbutsPaving` (parking/paving are both paved neighbours). The control is now offered whenever the field explodes to **ÔëÑ2 real pieces** (was ÔëÑ3 rows), and a degenerate/empty field (or a bare already-exploded single row) **surfaces a loud notice instead of a silent no-op** (and the gate hides the button there too). Renders for **already-saved** fields, not just freshly-drawn (reads only persisted `h/w/rot/cx/cy/cfg`).
- **Dedup:** net-new ÔÇö NOT B130/B131 (parking *generation* + boundary clip) and NOT B416 ("Split **a parcel**" = lot boundary). REUSES the B130 `lib/parking.js` math + the `carStalls` band model; the B130 `splitParkingPieces` (module split) + its tests are left intact (no fork).
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ┬À **1560 tests** (+9 `explodeParkingBands`/no-op) ┬À build green ┬À headless **V138** (`ui-audit/verify-b472-explode-parking.mjs`, **9/9**): a double-loaded module ÔåÆ 3 elements (2 parking rows + 1 paving aisle, depths sum to 60ÔÇ▓); a 6-row field ÔåÆ 9 elements; a bare exploded row no longer re-offers the control.

### B475 ÔÇö Markup breadcrumb switcher and the Markup Library read different project sources and can diverge `[Doc Review / Shared header]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-26 as "NEW-1"; minted **B475** = highest real B# across both files (B474) + 1 ÔÇö the B25431/B5263 hits in BACKLOG-DONE.md are CSS hex colours, not IDs)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/markup-switcher-cache-divergence-ceprhv`).** *NOTE ÔÇö push-to-branch only this session; no PR/merge to `main` opened. Kept in Open until merged.*
- **Repro (grounded in code, not guessed):** sign in on a device that hasn't opened the Site Planner this session (cold/empty local cache), go straight to Markup, open the breadcrumb "Ôû¥" project switcher ÔåÆ it showed no/stale projects, while the Markup **Library / Files** drawer listed the real cloud projects. Same `sites` table, two read paths: the breadcrumb runs **uncontrolled** in DocReview (no `projects` prop) ÔåÆ `shared/projects/projects.js listProjects()` ÔåÆ `loadSitesList()` ÔåÆ the Site-Planner local cache (`planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>`, only filled after a Site-Planner cloud pull). The Library uses `reviewStore.listProjects()` ÔåÆ a **live Supabase** query. Purely a source/freshness divergence (IDs are consistent ÔÇö `groupId` === `group_id`), contradicting B191's "no parallel project store" intent.
- **Fix (option 2 from the hint ÔÇö warm the cache, don't fork into a controlled list):** new `warmProjectsIfEmpty()` in `projects.js` ÔÇö when signed in **and** the on-device project cache is empty, it runs the SAME `pullCloud(uid)` the Site Planner runs on login (no-ops logged-out or already-warm; never throws), populating the shared cache both paths read. `ProjectBreadcrumb`'s uncontrolled mount effect now kicks it off once and re-`refresh()`es when it lands. New `activeUid()` getter on `storage.js`. Chosen over passing a controlled `projects` prop because that path drops the breadcrumb's rename/delete + cross-tab `storage`-event refresh and mismatches the project shape (no `updatedAt`/`relTime`) ÔÇö warming fixes the actual root cause and benefits every uncontrolled host with no behavior change.
- **Dedup:** net-new; touches the shared breadcrumb + projects helper only (no change to controlled mode / Schedule). Verified: lint **0 errors** ┬À **1571 tests** ┬À build green (DocReview still its own lazy chunk).
- **ÔÅ│ One signed-in spot-check (owner, planyr.io):** on a fresh tab/device, open Markup signed-in without touching the Site Planner first ÔåÆ the breadcrumb switcher now lists the same projects as the ­ƒùé Files drawer. (Sandbox can't reproduce ÔÇö sign-in is blocked there; the cold-cache path is signed-in-only.)

### B476 ÔÇö "At-risk save" messaging promised automatic re-sync on reconnect that doesn't happen `[Shared header]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-26 as "NEW-2"; minted **B476** = B475 + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session (same branch as B475).** *Push-to-branch only; kept in Open until merged.*
- **Repro:** with a failed cloud write (save badge "unsaved" ÔåÆ breadcrumb saveState "error"/"offline"), the breadcrumb toast + dropdown banner + the `CloudSyncBadge` tooltip all said the changes "will sync **automatically when you reconnect**." But there is **no** `online`/reconnect listener and no background retry anywhere in `src/` ÔÇö a failed write is only re-attempted on the next edit (debounced `writeNow`) or on tab close (the dirty-gated flush). Reconnecting and doing nothing else never triggered a sync ÔåÆ false reassurance about a data-safety state.
- **Fix (option B from the hint ÔÇö soften the wording to match reality; the honest, complete, low-risk fix for a LOW-severity messaging bug, chosen over wiring a new `online` retry into the just-stabilized B473 save paths):** all three strings now read "ÔÇªthey'll sync **the next time you make a change or close this tab**." Files: `ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx` (the `flagIfAtRisk` toast + the dropdown at-risk banner) and `CloudSyncBadge.jsx` (the offline tooltip ÔÇö same false promise, fixed for honesty even though the report only named the breadcrumb).
- **Dedup:** net-new; messaging-only, no behavior change. Verified: lint 0 ┬À 1571 tests ┬À build green. (If a true reconnect-retry is wanted later, that's a separate enhancement ÔÇö a `window` "online" handler re-triggering the pending `writeNow` in each workspace's persistence.)

### B481 ÔÇö Large site-plan overlay didn't re-render after reload (stranded on the "Re-add" placeholder despite the raster being in IndexedDB) `[Site Planner / overlay]` (bug)  *(Cowork signed-in verification 2026-06-26, "NEW-1" ÔÇö a 7.5 MB overlay showed "Re-add ÔÇª not synced to this device" after reload though its ~10 MB raster sat in IndexedDB; minted **B481** = highest real B# (B480) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1597 tests ┬À build green ┬À NEW headless `verify-b480-overlay-large.mjs` 8/8 + `verify-b474-overlay-idb.mjs` 7/7 (no regression).**
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in):** load a >~5 MB image as a sheet-overlay ÔåÆ it stores in IndexedDB (`raster:<proj>:overlay:<id>`, ~10 MB) with localStorage small ÔåÆ reload ÔåÆ overlay shows the "Re-add ÔÇª not on this device" placeholder, even though the bytes are in IndexedDB *on this device* and the record carries an `idbKey`. The same large image as the **aerial underlay re-rendered fine** (separate, simpler effect).
- **ROOT CAUSE (not Cowork's guess of "gates on storageKey" ÔÇö the effect DOES try `idbKey` first, SitePlanner.jsx:3551).** The overlay rehydrate effect had a `cancelled`-flag stranding race: a SLOW `idbGet` of a multi-MB raster was discarded whenever any other setState re-ran the `[sheetOverlays]` effect before the read resolved ÔÇö the result was dropped AND never re-fetched, so the overlay sat src-less forever. A small raster resolves before any re-render (why the 7/7 small-overlay test always passed); the underlay uses a separate effect (why it worked).
- **FIX:** apply each rehydrate result via a functional patch-by-id guarded on `!x.src` (safe across re-runs ÔÇö fills only an overlay still present + still src-less), and DROP the `cancelled` guard so a slow `idbGet` is never stranded. Plus the placeholder now reads "Loading drawingÔÇª" when an `idbKey` OR `storageKey` exists (recoverable) and only "Re-add ÔÇª image not on this device" when neither does.
- **Verified:** `verify-b480-overlay-large.mjs` 8/8 ÔÇö a 2.1 MB overlay (idbKey only, no storageKey, heavy src off the cap) re-renders after reload + view churn, no placeholder. Original 7/7 overlay test unchanged.
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in re-confirm of Cowork's exact 7.5 MB case ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V146.**

### B482 ÔÇö Cold Review tab: project switcher shows "No projects yet" though the projects exist `[Doc Review / shared header]` (bug)  *(Cowork signed-in verification 2026-06-26, "NEW-2"; the B475/V140 cold-cache fix resurfacing on the direct-to-Review path; minted **B482**)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1597 tests ┬À build green; signed-in cold-tab re-confirm ÔåÆ V146 (the warm only fires signed in).**
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in):** open a fresh tab straight into Review (`/project/<id>/markup`) without opening the Site Planner first ÔåÆ the project "Ôû¥" switcher shows "No projects yet ÔÇö start one below" though localStorage holds all projects (26 known). A *warm* load (Site opened first) lists them ÔÇö so it's cold-vs-warm, not the lock.
- **ROOT CAUSE:** `ProjectBreadcrumb` (uncontrolled) called B475's `warmProjectsIfEmpty()` **only once on mount** ÔÇö but on a cold tab `isCloudActive()` is usually still false at mount (auth hasn't resolved), so the warm no-opped, and it **never retried**. Opening the switcher only re-read the still-empty cache.
- **FIX:** warm the cache when the switcher **opens** too (auth is settled by then), not just on mount, via a shared `warmThenRefresh()`; a `warming` flag shows "Loading projectsÔÇª" instead of the misleading "No projects yet" while the one-time cloud pull is in flight. No-ops fast when logged out or already warm; the logged-out path is byte-identical (warm returns false ÔåÆ "No projects yet" as before).
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in cold-tab re-confirm ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V146.**

### B480 ÔÇö "Take over editing here" was a pointless loop (conflict ÔåÆ take over ÔåÆ bounce to map ÔåÆ same conflict); lock/conflict scoping confirmed PER-PLAN `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, HIGH PRIORITY)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-26 live on planyr.io ÔÇö "it's a fucking pointless loop ÔÇª it needs to be site specific"; minted **B480** = highest B# (B479) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1597 tests (+3) ┬À build green ┬À headless `verify-readonly-takeover.mjs` 7/7 (no regression).**
- **Repro (owner, signed-in):** editing a plan that was ALSO open in another tab on the same computer ÔåÆ the cloud version-CAS (B314) correctly flagged "changed in another session" + offered "Take over editing here." Clicking it ran `window.location.reload()`, which (a) bounced to the MAP (out of the plan) and (b) re-entered the version race with the still-open other tab, so reopening the plan re-flagged the SAME conflict ÔåÆ an endless take-over ÔåÆ map ÔåÆ conflict loop that never resolved.
- **ROOT CAUSE:** `takeOverEditing` reconciled a conflict by reloading ("boot union-merge"). The reload left the planner AND, because the other tab still held the lock / kept the cloud version ahead, the reloaded tab's stale optimistic-version token (`siteVersions[id]`) still mismatched the cloud ÔåÆ conflict again.
- **FIX ÔÇö reconcile IN PLACE, never reload:** Take over now (1) steals the per-plan Web Lock + broadcasts a yield so the other same-browser tab steps down to read-only and STOPS pushing; (2) calls the new focused `reconcileSiteFromCloud(id)` (`cloudSync.fetchSiteForReconcile` ÔÇö a single-row fetch that refreshes `siteVersions[id]` to the cloud's CURRENT version + returns the cloud copy; deliberately NOT `pullCloud`, whose fire-and-forget re-push could race our own push and re-trigger the conflict); (3) unions the other session's content into the canvas (nothing lost from either side); (4) clears the conflict ÔÇö the state change drives a single autosave push at the fresh version ÔåÆ it lands, this tab becomes the active editor, and you never leave the planner.
- **Scoping CONFIRMED already correct (owner's 2nd ask) ÔÇö PER-PLAN, not module-wide.** The editor lock name is `planyr-editor:<siteId>` (`setProject(siteId)`, SitePlanner.jsx ~1629) and the cloud version-CAS is keyed `siteVersions[<siteId>]`. Two people on DIFFERENT projects/plans share NEITHER ÔåÆ zero contention; only two sessions on the SAME plan contend (correct ÔÇö that's exactly the case being arbitrated). No code change needed; verified in code.
- **Tests:** `test/reconcileSite.test.js` (3) ÔÇö the version-token refresh that breaks the loop. Existing read-only take-over headless 7/7 (no regression ÔÇö the logged-out lock hand-off path is unchanged).
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in confirm ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V145** (the cloud-conflict reconcile only fires signed in; the sandbox is logged-out).

### B485 ÔÇö B473/B474 adversarial review hardening: close the IndexedDB-offload data-loss gaps the audit found `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner asked 2026-06-26 to "double check the persistence implementation ÔÇª world-class"; an 8-dimension adversarial review raised 35 findings, all 35 confirmed by independent verifiers, 0 refuted; this ships the safe/high-value data-loss + honesty fixes, defers the perf refactors to B479. **Renumbered B477ÔåÆB485** to clear a collision: a concurrent session had already taken B477 for the Schedule cross-module-nav bug (still Open). Shipped via PR #370, whose title still says "B477" ÔÇö the backlog number is the canonical one.)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1583 tests (+9) ┬À build green ┬À headless: existing B474 suite 7/7├ù3 + `verify-new-site-save` 10/10 (no regression) + NEW `verify-b474r-underlay-confirm.mjs` 7/7 (proves the headline fix).** The review found that B473/B474, while curing the original loss, opened narrower IndexedDB-specific data-loss paths. Root cause of most: **a raster's inline `src` was dropped from the saved record on the mere PRESENCE of an `idbKey`, never confirming the fire-and-forget `idbPut` actually committed** ÔÇö and the aerial underlay had no second home (cloud Storage) to mask a failed/slow/evicted stash.
- **`[x]` Confirm-before-strip (the headline, #2/#4/#6/#7/#10).** All three stash sites (`onUnderlayFile`, `addOverlayFile`, `addDrawingFromRaster`) now attach `idbKey` to the record **only after `idbPut` resolves true**; until then `src` stays inline (the safe localStorage copy `dropIdbBackedSrc` won't strip). A failed/slow/in-flight stash can no longer delete the only on-device copy. Proven headless with IndexedDB writes forced to throw: the record keeps `src`, no `idbKey`, the aerial survives reload (`verify-b474r-underlay-confirm.mjs`).
- **`[x]` IndexedDB connection self-heal (#1/#3).** `localDb.js openDb()` nulls the cached `dbPromise` on every non-success path (a transient `onblocked`/`onerror`/throw no longer poisons IndexedDB for the whole session), and registers `db.onversionchange` (close + drop so another tab's future schema bump / `deleteDatabase` isn't blocked) and `db.onclose` (reopen on the next op after a browser force-close/eviction).
- **`[x]` Underlay cloud-Storage backup (#5/#16, the pre-existing cross-device gap).** New `uploadUnderlayDataUrl` in `overlayStorage.js` (uid-first key `ÔÇª/site-underlay/ÔÇª`, reuses the private bucket + RLS); `onUnderlayFile` uploads the downscaled data-URL when signed in (`storageKey`), and the rehydrate effect gains a cloud fallback after the idb miss. The aerial ÔÇö previously the ONE raster with no cross-device / post-eviction recovery ÔÇö now restores on a second device. (Cross-device underlay loss was pre-existing, not a B474 regression ÔÇö `slimForCloud` always stripped it; now there's a real backup.)
- **`[x]` `persist()` request (#9).** `idbPersist()` (`localDb.js`) asks the browser to keep our IndexedDB durable (not best-effort/evictable); called once at boot in `SitePlannerApp`. IndexedDB is now the version-ring's home + the underlay's local cache, so eviction protection matters.
- **`[x]` Leak cleanup ÔÇö `idbDelete` was dead code (#13/#15/#22/#23/#24).** New `idbDeleteByPrefix` (cursor); `deleteSite` evicts `raster:<id>:*`; `deleteDrawing` / `removeOverlay` (ref-counted like `storageKey`) / the underlay Remove button evict their entries. No more orphaned raster bytes accumulating forever.
- **`[x]` `backupNow` honesty (#14).** `writeHistoryAll` now returns ONLY the synchronously-verified localStorage result ÔÇö it no longer counts the fire-and-forget idb write it can't confirm, so the Restore safety gate can't claim a backup that may not exist (and wipe the canvas). Deep history still lands in IndexedDB; this governs only what we CLAIM.
- **`[x]` Save-banner honesty (#8/#17/#18).** The conflict / read-only / cloud-failed banners ("safe on this device") are gated on `!localSaveFailed` so a failed device write never shows a reassuring message beside the truthful red one; the device-full cloud fallback clears any stale amber and routes a cloud CONFLICT to the conflict UI instead of a never-clearing "will retry" banner.
- **`[x]` Honest underlay re-drop prompt (#16).** A missing aerial that can't be recovered from idb OR cloud now shows a "couldn't recover ÔÇö re-drop the screenshot" prompt instead of an invisible `<image>`.
- **Tests (+9):** `test/idbRobustness.test.js` (prefix-delete, deleteSite eviction, backupNow honesty), `saveFallbackCloud.test.js` (mixed idb-backed + inline over-quota strip), `storage.test.js` (explicit IndexedDB-ABSENT faithfulness assertion), `overlayStorage.test.js` (underlay key + safe null paths).
- **ÔÅ│ Signed-in / second-device checks ÔåÆ VERIFICATION.md V144** (cross-device underlay restore, `persist()` grant, the device-full + conflict edge) ÔÇö the sandbox is logged-out so these can't be self-driven here.

### B478 ÔÇö Resume-into-planner: a signed-in deep link / refresh bounced to the finder (route stripped to `#/`) `[Site Planner / Persistence / Routing]` (bug, HIGH PRIORITY)  *(Cowork re-confirmed ÔØî 2026-06-25 on planyr.io ÔÇö see VERIFICATION.md V13/V28; minted **B478** = highest real B# across both files (B477) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + LIVE ÔÇö filed, fixed, logged-out-verified, AND merged to `main` the SAME session (PR #368, branch `claude/v13-resume-into-planner`).** The signed-in end-to-end confirm is owed as `VERIFICATION.md` V143 (the async-gap repro needs Supabase, which the sandbox lacks).
- **Repro (Cowork, signed-in on planyr.io):** open 8 South (URL `#/project/smqiljx5fngg/site`, planner rendered) ÔåÆ `location.reload()` ÔåÆ at both ~2 s and ~7 s the app **bounced to the finder**, breadcrumb "Select a project", URL **stripped to `#/`**; a cold navigate straight to a project URL likewise bounced. Data durability is fine (the site stays intact + reopenable) ÔÇö purely the resume UX. Re-confirms the 2026-06-20 finding; HIGH-PRIORITY and long-standing.
- **ROOT CAUSE (pinned in `SitePlannerApp.jsx`):** on a **signed-in** deep link/refresh the user's cloud sites aren't in the local store at the first synchronous render (auth + `pullCloud` are async), so `activeSiteId` is momentarily null even though the route names a project. Two boot reactions then destroyed the resume **before** the pull finished: (1) the active-projectÔåÆURL sync wrote that transient `null` over the route, **stripping `#/project/<id>/site` ÔåÆ `#/`** (ÔåÆ finder); (2) the "tidy a dangling `currentSite`" cleanup **nulled the pointer** because the cloud site only *looked* absent. (Logged-out / no-Supabase never reproduced it ÔÇö the local store is synchronous, so there's no gap; which is exactly why the sandbox + every logged-out self-test missed it.)
- **FIX:** a `bootResolved` gate (new pure `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/bootResume.js`) holds BOTH the URL sync and the dangling-pointer cleanup until the first auth + pull settles (true from the start when there's no Supabase to wait on, so logged-out is byte-identical). The resume target is now one shared, unit-tested `pickResumeTarget` (used by the first-render boot AND the post-pull resume, so they can't drift). The `bootResolved=true` flip is batched with the resume's `setActiveSiteId`, so the URL-sync effect only ever sees the resolved view.
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ┬À **1585 tests (+11 `test/bootResume.test.js`)** ┬À build green ┬À logged-out headless **8/8** (`ui-audit/verify-resume-into-planner.mjs` ÔÇö deep-link + reload stays in the planner, route intact, `currentSite` preserved, 0 page errors) + `verify-new-site-save.mjs` **10/10** (no regression). **The signed-in async-gap repro can't run in the sandbox (no Supabase ÔåÆ no gap), so the signed-in confirm is owed ÔÇö `VERIFICATION.md` V143.**
- **Dedup:** net-new; the resume regression sat under V13/V28 (which referenced B124/B125/B134 ÔÇö those were the *data-durability* halves, already Ô£à). This is the *resume-into-planner* half. Distinct from B477 (Schedule project carry).

### B471 ÔÇö Saving stalled with no banner / couldn't take over; cross-session+device conflict now has a real "Take over" + auth-transition tracing `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug/robustness)  *(owner reported a recurrence 2026-06-25 right after B466 shipped ÔÇö "it's literally not savingÔÇª I should be able to take overÔÇª maybe open on another PC"; minted **B471** = highest B# (B470) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7 ÔÇö the read-only take-over path is intact after the handler reorder).** Owner correction taken on board: it was **NOT** a second tab on his PC. The exact incident cause is **un-pinned and I won't pretend otherwise** ÔÇö "no banner + a fresh tab fixed it" most fits a STALE loaded build (an old tab from before B455/B458/B459/B460 + this batch deployed), whose recurrence is self-resolved by the now-deployed fixes on reload; a silent token lapse is ruled out (a real SIGNED_OUT bounces to the map, a 401 shows the red banner ÔÇö neither matches "still in the plan, nothing obvious"). Rather than build a speculative banner for a cause that doesn't match the symptoms, this closes the two real gaps + makes the next occurrence precisely diagnosable:
- **B466's take-over only reached SAME-browser tabs (Web Locks are per-browser).** A plan open in another SESSION/DEVICE blocks saving via the version-conflict path (B314 CAS), which only had a passive "Reload" button. `takeOverEditing` now handles BOTH: same-browser ÔåÆ steal the Web Lock + push in place; cross-session/device conflict ÔåÆ flush to the device mirror then reload, whose boot UNION-merges (this device Ôê¬ the other session ÔÇö nothing lost, B453) and resumes saving at the fresh version. The conflict banner now reads "your changes **aren't reaching the cloud** ÔÇª **Take over editing here**" (was "Reload"), so there is ONE consistent take-over for every blocked-save reason.
- **Auth transitions are now traced** ÔÇö `reportClientEvent("auth-signed-in"/"auth-signed-out")` in `SitePlannerApp.applyUser` (only on a real change; the same-user re-emit returns early). The cloud-save path is gated on being signed in, so a silent session change was previously invisible to telemetry; now a "saving stopped after my session changed" report is diagnosable after the fact via `window.pfTelemetry.recent()` / `public.client_errors`.
- **Honest limit (deferred, not built):** true simultaneous multi-device single-editor locking (forcing a remote PC read-only) needs a cloud editing-lease (heartbeat + realtime) ÔÇö the deferred per-site-sharing / multi-device territory. An **inaccessible** remote tab is idle and, post-B460, doesn't advance the cloud version, so it can't block this device anyway; the conflict take-over covers the actively-editing-elsewhere case. Work is never lost (device mirror B458 + boot union-merge B453).

### B464 ÔÇö Read-only/locked tab silently accepted edits that never synced; the read-only signal was too easy to miss `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1"; minted **B464**; EXTENDS B455 + B458)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests (+11) ┬À build green ┬À V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7, logged-out).** **Audited first, FLAGGED a contradiction:** on current `main` (post-B458/B455) a read-only tab's IMMEDIATE local-mirror write + version snapshot run UNCONDITIONALLY (`writeMirror`, SitePlanner.jsx ~L1461) ÔÇö only the CLOUD push is gated (~L1477). So the report's "snapshots froze / never persisted anywhere" is NOT reproducible on current main; it predates B458 (shipped earlier 2026-06-25), which made the device mirror immediate. The REAL gap: those read-only edits never reach the CLOUD, and the B455 read-only banner was a passive `role="status"` strip easy to work past for an hour.
- **Fix:** the read-only banner is now LOUD + actionable (`role="alert"`, `data-testid="readonly-banner"`): it states the changes are saved on this device but **aren't syncing to the cloud**, that **reloading won't help** while the other tab is open, and offers **"Take over editing here"** (B466). The immediate local mirror (B458) is kept as the recovery net ÔÇö read-only edits are never lost, just not synced until you take over or close the other tab.
- **Telemetry (B468):** an edit whose cloud push is suppressed now emits a `save-suppressed` event, so a silent non-syncing stretch is diagnosable after the fact without DevTools.
- **Deduped:** EXTENDS B455 (the Web Locks lockout) + B458 (immediate mirror); not a re-file.

### B465 ÔÇö Cloud indicator showed green "synced" while the tab was read-only and NOT saving `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-2"; minted **B465**)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests ┬À build green; signed-in indicator glance ÔåÆ Cowork (logged-out the badge correctly reads "local").** **Reproduced in code:** `headerSaveState` (SitePlanner.jsx ~L6040) had NO read-only branch ÔÇö a read-only tab fell through to `return cloudActive ? "synced" : "local"` ÔåÆ the green cloud-check badge, even though read queries (the connection) succeed but writes don't. Exactly the owner's "the cloud indicator stayed green the whole time."
- **Fix:** `headerSaveState` returns a new `"readonly"` state when `cloudActive && readOnly && !cloudConflict`. `cloudBadgeView("readonly")` (shared `CloudSyncBadge`) = amber + a distinct **lock** glyph + an actionable popover titled "Read-only ÔÇö not saving" that explains why and what to do ÔÇö never the green save-badge. Unit-locked in `cloudSyncBadge.test.js` (readonly differs from synced on BOTH glyph and color; title says "read-only"). Connection Ôëá write-capability is now the contract; the salient state ("your edits aren't saving") is loud, not green.

### B466 ÔÇö "Opened in another tab" recovery was broken: reload didn't take the lock; no way to take over or force-save `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug/feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-3"; minted **B466**; depends on B464)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). V (`ui-audit/verify-readonly-takeover.mjs`, 7/7): two tabs of one browser context share Web Locks ÔåÆ tab B goes read-only, "Take over editing here" makes B active AND hands tab A down to read-only via the cross-tab bus.**
- **`editorLock.takeOver()`** (`src/shared/presence/editorLock.js`): STEALS the Web Lock (`{steal:true}`) so this tab is unambiguously the editor even if the other tab is gone or on an older build, THEN broadcasts a `yield` on a BroadcastChannel so the prior holder steps down to read-only cooperatively. Steal-first-then-broadcast so the prior holder's re-request queues behind the steal. Degrades open where Web Locks is unavailable. (+3 unit tests: steal-makes-active, bus hand-off, degrade-open no-op.)
- **`takeOverEditing()` (SitePlanner):** the banner button calls `takeOver`, then flushes the live canvas to the device mirror AND force-pushes to the cloud (`cloudPushWithWatchdog`) ÔÇö so the pent-up un-synced work reaches the cloud immediately. The B459 thin-clobber + B314 CAS guards still protect that push (a genuine conflict surfaces the loud BLOCKING banner + reload-merge; never a silent overwrite), so takeover-and-push is safe.
- **Reload-recovery premise CORRECTED (flagged):** "work done before reload is lost on reload" is mitigated by B458 ÔÇö a read-only tab's edits ARE written to the device mirror immediately, so boot's union-merge restores them; the remaining gap (cloud sync) is what takeover closes. The brief's "manual Save now / force flush" is subsumed by "Take over editing here" (the only SAFE way to push from a read-only tab is to first own the lock; the device mirror already keeps the work, so a user is never stranded). The B313 presence banner copy was updated to the single-active-editor reality.

### B467 ÔÇö Restore's "can be undone" guarantee was unverified; Restore wasn't lock-gated `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-4"; minted **B467**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests (storage.test.js +3) ┬À build green.** The Version History dialog promises "your current version is backed up too, so a restore can be undone," but that relied on the autosave's shape-DEDUPED snapshot firing after the restore ÔÇö which can no-op (same collection shape, different content) ÔåÆ restore overwrites current work with no real undo.
- **Fix (`storage.js` + `restoreVersion`):** `snapshotVersion(model, { force })` now **returns whether it persisted** and `force` bypasses the shape-dedup; new `backupNow(id)` force-snapshots the current stored state and returns true only when a backup actually persisted (or there's genuinely nothing at risk ÔÇö empty/missing record). `restoreVersion` (a) is **lock-gated** ÔÇö a read-only tab can't Restore (loud message + the takeover way out); (b) flushes the live canvas, then **verifies `backupNow` before replacing** ÔÇö if the backup can't be written (device storage full) it CANCELS the restore loudly + emits a `restore-backup-failed` telemetry event, rather than destroying current work on a false promise. Fits the verify-it-persisted / no-silent-success discipline.

### B468 ÔÇö Lockout / suppressed-save / write-failure / zero-row-delete events now leave a telemetry trace `[Site Planner / Observability]` (task) ÔÇö EXTENDS B279  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-5"; minted **B468**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests (clientErrors.test.js +2) ┬À build green; signed-in row-write ÔåÆ Cowork.** The whole 8 South lockout diagnosis required live DevTools spelunking because nothing about it was traceable. Fixed: `reportClientEvent(kind, message, extra)` in `clientErrors.js` records structured NON-error events to the existing `public.client_errors` table ÔÇö **no schema change / no owner SQL step** (stamped with a per-page-load `TAB_ID` embedded in the message so multi-tab contention is reconstructable) ÔÇö surfaced via `window.pfTelemetry.recent()` + `pfTelemetry.tab`. Wired at the exact gates B464 touches: `readonly-enter`/`readonly-leave` + `readonly-takeover` (SitePlanner lock onChange + takeover), `save-suppressed` (the autosave cloud-push gate), `cloud-conflict` (CAS 409 + thin-clobber) / `cloud-write-failed` / `delete-zero-rows` (cloudSync). Fail-safe is the whole point ÔÇö every path swallows its own errors (unit-tested: never throws on a circular / null / empty payload), reuses B279's dedup + per-session cap.

### B469 ÔÇö Fort Bend FLOODZONE layer blocked by CORS; route the health probe through the existing GIS proxy `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-6"; minted **B469**)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`). lint 0 ┬À 1528 tests (gisProxyCore.test.js +2) ┬À build green; live "load a Fort Bend parcel ÔåÆ no CORS errors" ÔåÆ Cowork/prod.** **Audited first: the server-side GIS proxy already EXISTS (B445) and `fortbendcountytx.gov` is already in its allow-list; the export-image raster already proxies + renders via a CORS-exempt `<img>`.** The 6├ù CORS console errors + `net::ERR_FAILED` were `probeService` (layers.js) fetching `?f=json` **directly** (bypassing the proxy) ÔÇö `arcgisweb.fortbendcountytx.gov` sends no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, so the browser killed the probe.
- **Fix (`layers.js`):** `probeService` is now **direct-first, proxy-on-CORS-failure** (refactored into `probeOnce`): the many CORS-clean agency hosts take NO extra hop and never depend on the proxy being deployed, while a host that throws (CORS/network) retries through the same-origin B445 cache proxy (`/api/gis-cache/svc/<b64>?f=json`), which fetches it server-side and returns the JSON same-origin ÔÇö so the service can still be health-checked AND its `fullExtent` read for the coverage engine. `fb_contours` marked `kind: "dynamic"` for clarity. Round-trip unit-locked (the proxied Fort Bend probe decodes back to the real request for an allowed host). The "one proxy adapter" the brief asked for = the existing single B445 module (paired with its SWR Drive cache); no new server, no regression for CORS-clean hosts.

### B470 ÔÇö pdf.worker "TT: undefined function" Trusted Types warning ÔÇö triaged benign `[Document Review / PDF]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-7", low priority; minted **B470**; CLOSED benign)*
`[x]` **CLOSED ÔÇö triaged benign, no code change (branch `claude/exciting-wright-yb6ruz`).** Confirmed the repo enforces **no** Content-Security-Policy and **no** `require-trusted-types-for` directive anywhere (checked `public/_headers`, `index.html` meta, all `functions/`, `vite.config.js`). So the `TT:` log from `pdf.worker.min.mjs` is pdf.js's own internal Trusted Types feature-PROBE during worker bootstrap (it looks for a TT policy to harden itself; with no enforcing policy it falls back to the normal path) ÔÇö purely informational, no render impact, matching the owner's "PDF rendering appears to work." Adding a TT policy solely to silence a harmless log would add complexity and risk breaking the worker, so no change is warranted; re-open only if a strict CSP is ever introduced (then pdf.js needs its `pdfjs` policy allow-listed).

### B461 ÔÇö Right-click context menu for site-plan overlays (Copy / Duplicate / Paste / z-order / Lock / Align to base / Delete) `[Site Planner / overlay]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-1"; minted **B461**; surface confirmed by the owner ÔÇö see below)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/wizardly-mccarthy-oi8xl7`). lint 0 ┬À 1517 tests ┬À build green ┬À V137 (`ui-audit/verify-b461-b462-overlay-menu.mjs`, 13/13, logged-out).** **Flagged + corrected the premise:** the brief tagged this `[Document Review]` and assumed an "overlay & version-compare surface" there ÔÇö that surface does **not exist** in Doc Review yet (unbuilt Track-2 item). Asked the owner which "overlay" he meant; he **chose the Site Planner's site-plan overlays** (`sheetOverlays`), the one real object matching the brief (a transform layer + a source-file reference + lock + align). Built there.
- **Menu (`ovMenu`):** a portalled, viewport-clamped floating menu at the cursor (mirrors the element `typeMenu` / B127 portal pattern), opened by `onOverlayContext` from the **canvas overlay `<g>`** (unlocked) AND its **Overlay-panel row** (so a locked overlay ÔÇö pointer-inert on the map ÔÇö is still reachable to unlock). Entries: **Copy, Duplicate, Paste** (Paste disabled on an empty clipboard), **Bring to front / Send to back** (disabled at the ends; draw order = array order), **Lock / Unlock**, **Align to base edgeÔÇª** (disabled when locked or no parcel), **Delete**. Right-click `preventDefault`+`stopPropagation` so the canvas pan/marquee never eats it.
- **Copy/Duplicate/Paste touch only the overlay's transform layer + its source *reference*, never the source bytes:** a copy/dup/paste shares the same `src` + `storageKey` (no re-import). `removeOverlay` is now **ref-counted** ÔÇö it only deletes the cloud source object when no OTHER overlay still points at that `storageKey`, so deleting one of a duplicated pair can't orphan the sibling's image. Copy ÔåÆ `overlayClip` ref; Paste lands at the cursor (B417 pattern) or offset; `Ctrl/Ôîÿ C/V/D` wired for a selected overlay (separate from the element clipboard; selecting an overlay sets `sel=null`, so no collision).
- **Delete is count-verified (silent-failure rule):** `removeOverlay` refuses if the id isn't present, and a `setTimeout` re-reads `stateRef` to confirm the row actually went ÔÇö a no-op delete flashes a loud "ÔÜá Delete didn't take" warning instead of a phantom success.
- **Single-overlay note:** the Site Planner selects one overlay at a time (`selOverlay`), so the menu is single-target (the brief's multi-select-for-Delete/z-order is moot until overlay multi-select exists). Every op is undoable (`pushHistory`). Esc clears the menu + any armed align.

### B462 ÔÇö "Align to base edge" for overlays, reusing the Site Planner buildingÔåÆparcel align `[Site Planner / overlay]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-2"; minted **B462**; depends on B461)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified with B461 (V137 above; rot 0 ÔåÆ 30┬░ on a 30┬░-rotated parcel). lint 0 ┬À 1517 tests ┬À build green.** The "Align to base edgeÔÇª" menu entry **reuses the existing buildingÔåÆparcel primitive ÔÇö no second align written:** it arms `ovAlignBase`, and the next click on a parcel boundary (caught in `startMoveSheetOverlay` / `startMoveParcel` / the background handler, the same three consumption points as the element `alignFor`) runs `alignOverlayToParcelEdge`, which finds the nearest parcel edge via `segDist` and snaps the overlay's rotation parallel to it via `snapParallel` (the very functions the buildingÔåÆparcel "Align rotationÔÇª" uses).
