- **Edge-snap = rotation only** (translation + rotation, scale unchanged); the **2-point translate+rotate+scale** path is the overlay's existing "Align to map" (`solveSimilarityLSQ`), left in place. Rotation is a persisted real-world angle (degrees on the model) so the alignment survives zoom/pan/reload; the base parcel is never mutated. **Guards:** refuses a **locked** overlay with a visible reason; rejects a **degenerate** (too-short) edge rather than applying a NaN rotation; "no parcel to align to" flashes a clear message; one undo step; re-aligning replaces the prior rotation.

### B463 ÔÇö Replace the rotation slider with a numeric stepper (hundredth-degree, shared widget) `[Doc Review + shared markup engine]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-25 as "NEW-3"; minted **B463** = highest real B# (B460) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/wizardly-mccarthy-oi8xl7`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1517 tests (+11 unit) ┬À build green ┬À lazy chunks intact ┬À headless V (`ui-audit/verify-b463-rotation-stepper.mjs`, 12/12, logged-out).** The owner's "verify first" was right: the ONLY actual rotation **slider** in the app was the Site Planner overlay's "Rotate" (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~L7416); the markup + element panels already used a NumInput (element even had ┬▒1┬░ spinners, B431). Document Review has no rotation control today. So this was a real swap on the overlay + a unify on the other two, NOT a one-for-one replacement everywhere.
- **New shared widget `src/shared/ui/RotationStepper.jsx`** ÔÇö THE one rotation control the whole app imports (overlay panel, markup panel, element panel today; any future Doc-Review rotatable object next). Behavior to the owner's spec: **type-to-set** up to two decimals (hundredth-degree); commit on **Enter / blur / spinner** only (never live per keystroke); **Ôû▓Ôû╝ spinners nudge ┬▒1┬░**, in-field ArrowUp/Down = ┬▒1┬░, Shift+Arrow = ┬▒0.1┬░; **normalize/wrap into [0,360)** on commit (370 ÔåÆ 10, ÔêÆ5 ÔåÆ 355); **display Ôëñ2 decimals but store full precision and step the stored value** so repeated nudges never drift; **empty on blur reverts** to the last committed value (never zeroes); **garbage flashes the field red + reverts** (never silently clamps to 0); a **locked** target disables the input AND the spinners with a visible reason (refuses, doesn't fail). Pure helpers `normalizeDeg`/`parseRotationInput`/`formatDeg` exported + unit-tested (`test/rotationStepper.test.js`, 11). Inline editor only (owner rule); theme tokens only (WCAG AA).
- **Wired into all three Site Planner rotation controls** (overlay slider ÔåÆ stepper; markup NumInput ÔåÆ stepper; element NumInput+spinners ÔåÆ stepper). Each commit is **one undo step** (the hosts' `patchOverlay`/`setSelMarkupGeom`/`rotateAssemblyTo` already `pushHistory`); rotation is a persisted real-world angle (degrees on the model), so it survives reload. The now-orphaned `bumpRot` helper was removed; `spinBtn`/`NumInput` stay (still used elsewhere).
- **Multi-select note (owner asked to confirm):** the stepper's contract already gives his recommended behavior ÔÇö a **typed value = absolute set** (`onCommit`), a **spinner press = ┬▒1┬░ delta** (`onStep`). The current Site Planner rotation panels are single-selection (selEl/selMarkup/one overlay), so absolute-vs-delta only matters once multi-select rotation is wired; the widget is ready for it.

### B457 ÔÇö Recover lost buildings on 8 South / Plan 1 (one-time) `[Persistence]` (task) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-9"; CLOSED 2026-06-25)*
`[x]` **CLOSED ÔÇö unrecoverable, owner declined further pursuit.** Cowork's Phase-B account-wide sweep (signed in, planyr.io) found 17 of the user's plans intact, but **8 South / Plan 1** (id `smqiljx5fngg`) is gone everywhere reachable: cloud row = 0 buildings (version 40), on-device history = 5 snapshots all 0 buildings (Jun 17ÔÇô18), device mirror = 0 buildings. The free Supabase tier has no PITR, so the overwritten cloud state isn't server-recoverable. The only untried avenue was another device's local Version History; **the owner said he doesn't care about recovering 8 South ÔÇö close it out** (2026-06-25). The recurrence is prevented by B458 (immediate mirror) + B459 (no thin-clobber) + B460 (no spurious conflict) + B453/B455 (boot union-merge + conflict lockout).

### B460 ÔÇö Opening/reloading a plan re-pushed it and bumped `version` with no edit ÔåÆ spurious "changed in another session" conflicts `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, UX)  *(Cowork Phase-B flag #2, 2026-06-25; minted **B460** = highest real B# (B459) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1506 tests (storage.test.js +2 net) ┬À build green.** Data-safe before the fix (B459's CAS + content guard means the re-push can't clobber), but the version churn was firing a benign-but-scary loud "reload to merge" banner in any OTHER open tab/device.
- **Root cause:** `mergePulledSites` `toPush` re-pushed a row when `(local.updatedAt) > cloud.updatedAt` ÔÇö and B458's immediate per-edit mirror write advances the local `updatedAt` on every edit while the cloud push is debounced/lagging, so EVERY reload re-pushed identical content, bumped `version`, and made other sessions see a (false) conflict.
- **Fix (`storage.js`):** new stable `contentSig(model)` (drawn collections sorted by id + tombstones, as JSON; both sides are `createSiteModel`-normalized so identical content hashes equal). `toPush` now re-pushes ONLY when the row is cloud-absent OR `contentSig(merged) !== contentSig(cloud)` ÔÇö i.e. the merge actually ADDED/CHANGED/DELETED content. `map[id]` is the union (Ôèç cloud) so this never pushes a thinner row; a no-op re-open pushes nothing (no version churn ÔåÆ no spurious conflict). A real add/move/**tombstoned delete** still re-pushes (delete propagation preserved ÔÇö explicit regression test). Dropped the obsolete `updatedAt >` and `contentCount >` clauses.
- **Live confirm (no spurious conflict on a benign two-tab re-open) ÔåÆ Cowork / VERIFICATION.md V135.**

### B459 ÔÇö The cloud save guards the version NUMBER but not CONTENT, so a stale/thin tab silently overwrote a fuller cloud row `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS ÔÇö the actual 8 South 5-building killer)  *(owner pushed back on the 400ms-debounce story 2026-06-25 ÔÇö "a 400ms gap can't lose 5 buildings I watched save"; he was right; minted **B459** = highest real B# (B458) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + unit-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1504 tests (cloudThinGuard.test.js +9) ┬À build green.** Owner's skepticism was correct and is the key insight: the 400 ms debounce (B458) can only ever lose the ONE edit in flight ÔÇö it cannot explain losing 5 buildings placed over minutes and watched save. Losing 5 needs a SECOND failure, which I'd wrongly waved off as "benign" (the Q3 re-push); Cowork independently refused to take it on faith.
- **Root cause (proven by code audit):** the CAS guard (`optimisticUpsert.js` `casUpsert`) is `WHERE id=ÔÇª AND version=ÔÇª` ÔÇö it compares the version SCALAR only, **never the `data` content**. So a tab holding a stale/thin model (e.g. road-only after a bad load / the deploy-crash reload) at a version that still MATCHES the cloud would PATCH right over a fuller cloud row (5 buildings ÔåÆ road-only) and bump the version, badge still "Saved" (it did save ÔÇö the wrong, thinner copy). There was **no fetch-merge and no content check** anywhere on the write path.
- **Fix (`cloudSync.js`):** alongside `siteVersions` we now remember the **content baseline** we last synced per site ÔÇö `siteContent` (item count) + `siteTombs` (tombstone count) ÔÇö populated by `cloudList` (from the cloud row) and advanced on every successful write. New pure `wouldThinClobber(model, baseCount, baseTombs)` blocks a push that would drop **ÔëÑ2 items the cloud still has with no delete-tombstone to explain it** (`lost = (baseCount ÔêÆ newTombstones) ÔêÆ contentCount(model) ÔëÑ 2`). `cloudUpsert` returns `{ ok:false, conflict:true, thinned:true }` on a hit ÔåÆ the existing **B455 loud + blocking** conflict banner fires; because the write is REFUSED, the cloud stays intact and the next reload union-merges the fuller copy back (no loss). The keepalive forced-reload push (B452) is guarded the same way. Legit incremental **undo** (Ôëñ1 unexplained drop/push) and any **tombstoned delete** pass through untouched.
- **Why ÔëÑ2, not ÔëÑ1:** an undo-of-a-synced-add drops exactly 1 non-tombstoned item ÔÇö common; the stale-tab clobber drops the whole divergence (5+) in one push. The threshold cleanly separates them; a rare large legit drop trips a recoverable "reload to merge", never silent loss.
- **Closes the loop with B458/B453:** B458 keeps the on-device mirror current; B453 union-merges on boot; **B459 stops the cloud being thinned in the first place.** All three together make the 8 South loss-shape impossible.
- **Live signed-in two-tab / stale-tab clobber repro ÔåÆ Cowork Phase B / VERIFICATION.md V134** (self-tests are logged-out; the clobber path needs auth + the cloud).
- **Follow-up noted:** Document Review's `reviewStore` cloud upsert has the same version-only CAS shape (different content model) ÔÇö mirror the guard there next (low priority; the owner's loss was a Site Planner plan).

### B458 ÔÇö Autosave debounced the on-device mirror too, so a reload-within-400ms lost the edit everywhere `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(follow-up to B452/B453 after Cowork's live Phase-A diagnosis on prod, 2026-06-25; minted **B458** = highest real B# (B457) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE + headless-verified (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1495 tests (storage.test.js +3) ┬À build green ┬À V133 (`ui-audit/verify-immediate-mirror.mjs`, 6/6, logged-out).** This is the REAL structural fix the 8 South building-loss needed ÔÇö B452 (keepalive on the app's own forced reload) and B453 (boot union-merge, verified correct) were necessary but not sufficient.
- **Root cause (Cowork reproduced on prod, confirmed against the merged code):** the Site Planner autosave (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~L1438) debounced **everything** by 400 ms ÔÇö the localStorage mirror (`planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>` / `:v1`) AND the version-history snapshot were both written only inside `setTimeout(saveSite, 400)`. So a reload/crash within 400 ms of an edit lost the building from cloud, the on-device mirror, AND history **at once**, and boot's union-merge (`mergePulledSites`/`mergeSiteContent`, correct per B453) had nothing fuller locally to restore. *Push-abandoned, structural ÔÇö not a CAS conflict* (matches Cowork's mechanism conclusion; Document Review already split immediate-mirror from debounced-cloud in `usePersistence.js`, so only the Site Planner was the laggard).
- **Fix:** the autosave effect now writes the device mirror **immediately** on every edit, decoupled from the debounced cloud push. `storage.js` `saveSite(partial, { skipHistory })` gained an option; the **immediate** write is a DEFAULT save (history ON ÔÇö `snapshotVersion` backs up the prior version before overwriting, the B126 thinning safety net, AND makes the rollback snapshot reload-safe), coalesced to ~50 ms so a same-shape drag can't thrash `writeSites` (and `snapshotVersion`'s count-based sig-dedup already skips same-shape frames). The **settle tick** (400 ms) keeps the cloud push and passes `{ skipHistory: true }` so it can't double-snapshot. Conflict/read-only/CAS gates on the cloud push are unchanged; the immediate local write always runs (a local save is always safe ÔÇö it's the whole recovery net).
- **Flagged correction vs the approved plan:** the plan put `skipHistory` on the immediate write; that would have lost the prior version on a thinning edit (the B126 safety net). Moved history ONTO the immediate write instead ÔÇö strictly more correct AND makes history reload-safe too. (Unit-test'd: immediate-then-settle backs up the prior version exactly once per shape.)
- **Reconciled Cowork's other audit Qs (no code beyond B458):** Q2 boot does union-merge (it was only moot because the input was debounced away ÔÇö B458 fixes the input). Q3/Q4 the version-creep-on-open is the benign CAS-guarded boot re-push (`pullCloud` `toPush`) ÔÇö can't clobber a fuller row; left as-is (tightening it risks under-pushing).
- **Live signed-in cloud round-trip (survive-a-reload with a real cloud row) ÔåÆ Cowork Phase B / VERIFICATION.md V133** (self-tests run logged-out).

### B449 ÔÇö SPA fallback serves index.html for a missing /assets/* chunk (masks a 404 as a text/html MIME error) `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; shipped same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests ┬À build green. Live-edge 404 confirmation delegated to Cowork (the sandbox's vite-preview can't reproduce Cloudflare's edge ÔÇö verified it serves index.html for a missing /assets path, which is exactly the masking bug, but only Cloudflare honors `_redirects`).** Repro: requesting a content-hashed chunk a newer deploy deleted (`/assets/SitePlannerApp-<deadhash>.js`) returns 200 + index.html (text/html) instead of a real 404 ÔåÆ the browser tries to parse HTML as a JS module and dies with "Expected a JavaScript module script ÔÇª MIME type text/html" / "Failed to fetch dynamically imported module".
- **Root cause:** the Cloudflare Pages project answers unmatched paths with index.html (SPA not-found handling), which catches missing `/assets/*` too. There was no in-repo routing file to stop it.
- **Fix:** added **`public/_redirects`** (copied to `dist/` like `public/_headers`) with **NO** `/* /index.html 200` catch-all ÔÇö Planyr uses HASH routing (`#/ÔÇª`, `src/app/route.js`), so every real navigation already lands on a static file and no catch-all is needed. A single rule `/assets/*  /404.html  404` forces a missing chunk to a real 404 (Cloudflare serves real static files BEFORE `_redirects` rules, so it can NEVER 404 a live asset ÔÇö only a deleted one). Added a tiny dependency-free **`public/404.html`**. Confirmed `_redirects`/`_headers`/`404.html` all emit to `dist/`. The brief's prescribed catch-all was the WRONG fix here (it would *cause* the masking); flagged + corrected.
- **Deduped:** related to B221/B239 (stale-chunk recovery) but distinct ÔÇö this is the server-side 404 honesty those rely on.

### B450 ÔÇö Chunk-recovery dead-ends with no escape when the FRESH build is also missing the chunk `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug, follow-up to B239)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests (chunkReload.test.js +8) ┬À build green. The live escape-screen render is a Cowork preview/prod check (the sandbox e2e harness `verify-chunk-reload.mjs` is timing-flaky on the heavy full-app reload ÔÇö confirmed it fails identically on unmodified `main`, so not a regression; the pure decision is deterministically unit-tested).** Repro: parked on "A new version of Planyr is ready / Reload to update"; clicking re-runs `reloadFresh()`, the fresh index.html still references a 404ing chunk, the 10s cooldown suppresses the retry ÔåÆ the same dead screen returns.
- **Fix:** the cache-buster `?_r=` param is now the "already tried a fresh reload" signal. New pure `recoveryStage(arrivedViaFreshReload, now, lastReloadAt)` ÔåÆ `reload | stuck | cooldown`; `installChunkReloadGuard` captures `hasReloadParam(win)` BEFORE `stripReloadParam` tidies it and only auto-reloads on `reload` (so `stuck`/`cooldown` surface to the ErrorBoundary instead of looping ÔÇö one automatic attempt, then a manual escape). `ErrorBoundary.jsx` shows a distinct **"Planyr is finishing a deploy ÔÇö try again in a minute"** message whose **Try again** clears the cooldown stamp (`clearReloadGuard`) before reloading. (`src/app/chunkReload.js`, `src/app/ErrorBoundary.jsx`.)

### B451 ÔÇö Set Vite `base:"/"` for the Cloudflare root deploy `[App Shell / Deploy]` (task, hardening)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). build green; built index.html references `/assets/ÔÇª` (absolute); `/sequence/` + all three lazy chunks resolve at root.** `vite.config.js` set `base: command === "build" ? (process.env.PLANYR_BASE || "/") : "/"` ÔÇö production is Cloudflare Pages at the domain root, so root-absolute asset URLs resolve identically on every page (the old relative `"./"` was a retired-GitHub-Pages artifact; `PLANYR_BASE` env-gates a subpath build if ever resurrected). Hardening, not the B449 cause; done regardless.

### B452 ÔÇö A forced reload drops unsaved work: `reloadFresh()` / the ErrorBoundary reload don't cloud-flush first `[App Shell / Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-4"; shipped same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests (flushRegistry +5, keepalivePush +4) ┬À build green. Survive-a-forced-reload is a Cowork signed-in check (self-tests run logged-out, so the cloud-push path needs auth).** Corrected-scope note (flagged vs the brief): the LOCAL half already runs ÔÇö `reloadFresh` navigates via `location.replace`, which fires `beforeunload`/`visibilitychange`, and the Site Planner already saves localStorage synchronously there. The real gap was the **cloud** push (debounced/async, abandoned mid-navigation).
- **Fix:** new cross-workspace **`src/app/flushRegistry.js`** (`registerFlush`/`flushAll`); `reloadFresh` calls `flushAll()` before navigating. Site Planner + Doc Review register a flusher that does the synchronous local save PLUS a **keepalive cloud push** (`keepaliveCasPush` in `optimisticUpsert.js` ÔÇö a `fetch({keepalive:true})` PATCH that outlives the navigation, **version-guarded** exactly like `casUpsert` so it can never clobber a newer row, and never inserts; reads the token synchronously via `currentAccessToken()`). `keepaliveCloudPush`/`keepaliveFlushSite` (sites) + `keepaliveFlushReview` (doc_reviews). Gated off when read-only/conflict (B455). The synchronous local save + the next-load boot merge (B453) remain the guarantee; this shortens the lost window.

### B453 ÔÇö Boot reconciliation must UNION-merge the on-device mirror with the cloud pull `[Persistence]` (bug, DATA-LOSS)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-5"; verified same session)*
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö verified ALREADY-CORRECT + locked with a regression test (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests (storage.test.js +1) ┬À build green.** Flagged vs the brief: this is **already implemented**. `saveSite` writes the on-device autosave mirror to `planarfit:sites:cloud:<uid>` ÔÇö the SAME key `pullCloud ÔåÆ mergePulledSites` reads as `existing` ÔÇö and the boot path builds the map from the local cache FIRST, folds each cloud row in via `mergeSiteContent` (union-by-id), and re-pushes anything fuller (`toPush`). So a building present in EITHER copy survives a reload (B124/B126/B127). Added the explicit incident-shape regression test (5 local buildings + a road vs a road-only/0-building cloud row ÔåÆ all 5 survive AND `toPush` heals the cloud). No code change needed; documented the verified-already finding.

### B454 ÔÇö Root-cause: how a building-loss got past the active B314 CAS guard `[Persistence]` (bug, diagnostic)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-6"; investigated same session)*
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö written root cause + audit (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`).** Audited every write to `public.sites` / `public.doc_reviews`:
- **(a) CAS-bypass via a raw `.upsert` ÔÇö RULED OUT.** The only raw `.upsert` of the `data` column are the degrade fallbacks in `cloudSync.js`/`reviewStore.js`, which fire ONLY when the `version` column is absent (migration ran 2026-06-20, so they don't). `sharing.js` raw updates touch `team_id` only (never `data`). The merge re-push (`pullCloud` `toPush`) and `setProjectStatus` both route through the guarded `cloudUpsert`. No unguarded `data` write exists.
- **Most likely cause = (b) conflict-then-strand during the concurrent deploy.** The fuller (5-building) state existed only in a tab whose every save **conflicted** (a racing/thin tab advanced the version), so the buildings stayed local-only there; meanwhile a thin tab that DID hold the current version won the CAS and wrote the road-only row. The conflict banner was **dismissible and didn't block**, so editing continued, every save re-conflicting. Then the **forced reload** (the chunk-crash incident) landed in a context whose durable local cache was the thin copy, so the boot merge had nothing fuller to union. Secondary possibility: an erroneous `deletedIds` tombstone (the only way `mergeSiteContent`'s union drops a building). `mergeSiteContent` proven to union `els` by id otherwise.
- **Closed by:** **B452** (keepalive cloud push on forced reload) + **B455** (conflict is now loud+blocking, and the Web Locks read-only lockout stops a stale/thin tab winning the CAS over the active one). **B453** is the recovery net. **B456** explains why the snapshots looked unusable (the "0 buildings" label hid recoverable history). Reconcile with Cowork's live two-tab repro before final conclusions; the fixes are correct either way. Regression coverage: the B453 incident-shape test + the B455 `canCloudSave` gate test.

### B455 ÔÇö Conflict + at-risk saves must be loud AND blocking; finish the deferred read-only lockout `[Persistence]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-7"; built same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests (editorLock.test.js +11, canCloudSave gate) ┬À build green. The live single-active-editor lockout is a Cowork two-tab signed-in check.** Three parts, mirrored into Site Planner AND Doc Review:
- **(1) Conflict loud + BLOCKING.** A `casUpsert` conflict now PAUSES further cloud autosave until reload (the local save still runs, so work is preserved + reload union-merges it). Site Planner's conflict banner lost its dismiss Ô£ò (blocking) and is reworded ("Saving is pausedÔÇª Reload to merge"); Doc Review gates the same via the pure `canCloudSave(status, readOnly)`.
- **(2) Web Locks single-active-editor lockout (the deferred half of B313).** New `src/shared/presence/editorLock.js` (`createEditorLock` + pure `lockRole`): only ONE tab holds the editor lock per project; a second tab on the same plan/review goes **read-only** and can't push over the active one ÔÇö the direct structural fix for the stale-tab clobber. Degrades OPEN where Web Locks is unavailable; hands off automatically when the active tab closes. Site Planner shows a read-only banner; both gate the cloud push (+ keepalive) on `readOnly`.
- **(3) Unconfirmed-save watchdog.** A cloud write that doesn't resolve within ~6s escalates to the loud B125 banner with Retry (Site Planner) / flips the badge red (Doc Review). Tied to the in-flight write, so sustained editing never false-triggers it.
- Builds on B125/B313/B314; extends B313, doesn't rebuild it.

### B456 ÔÇö Version History unusable for picking a restore point: "0 buildings" on every row, indistinguishable timestamps `[Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-8"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/youthful-volta-ns1n75`). lint 0 ┬À 1468 tests (storage.test.js +5) ┬À build green.** Root cause: the row label used the snapshot's stored `mainBuildingCount`, which excludes attached additions AND dog-ears ÔåÆ it read "0 buildings" while buildings existed (crash-class: it hid recoverable work), and rows minutes apart looked identical.
- **Fix:** new pure `summarizeVersion(model)` computes a real content summary ("12 parcels ┬À 1 road ┬À 5 buildings ┬À 3 markups") from the **stored full model** (so OLD snapshots benefit too) counting REAL buildings (`isBuilding`, excludes only dog-ear sub-pieces). `listVersions` now returns `{ at, buildings, summary }` and de-dupes adjacent same-second/same-shape rows; the dialog renders the summary and a **seconds-precision** timestamp so two backups in the same minute are distinguishable. (`lib/storage.js`, `SitePlanner.jsx`.)

### B445 ÔÇö Site-plan overlay drop target has no affordance + the panel isn't a dropzone `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 via screenshot as "this does not allow anything to actually be dropped in, should work like enterprise software"; minted **B445** = highest real B# across both files (B444) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/hopeful-turing-1x24sg`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1425 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V131 (`ui-audit/verify-overlay-dnd.mjs`, 7/7).**
- **Repro (owner):** Site Planner ÔåÆ open the "Site-plan overlay" panel ÔåÆ its copy says "Drop a site-plan PDF onto the map (or browse)", but dragging a file over the panel gives **zero feedback** and releasing it does nothing (or the browser opens the PDF in a new tab) ÔåÆ reads as broken / "doesn't allow anything to be dropped in."
- **Root cause (confirmed in code):** the canvas wrapper (`wrapRef`, `SitePlanner.jsx`) already HAD a working `onDragOver`/`onDrop` ÔåÆ `addOverlayFile`, but (1) there was **no drag-hover affordance** so the live zone looked dead, and (2) the **left panel itself** (where the cursor goes ÔÇö the "Add site planÔÇª" button + copy live there) had **no drop handlers**, so a release there hit the browser default. On narrow screens the panel is `position:absolute; zIndex:1100`, outside the `wrapRef` subtree, so its drops could never reach the canvas handler.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`), reusing the existing ingestion path (`addOverlayFile` ÔåÆ `openOverlayFile`/`uploadOverlayFile`) + the Doc-Review `FileBrowser` dropzone pattern:**
  1. **Panel is now a real dropzone** ÔÇö the overlay `Section`'s button + copy are wrapped in a dashed-border `<div>` that highlights on hover (accent border + `PAL.accentSoft` bg, anti-flicker via `e.currentTarget === e.target`), routes drop ÔåÆ `addOverlayFile`, and shows "Drop to add this site plan" while dragging. The button stays the click-to-browse path.
  2. **Canvas "drop to place" hint** ÔÇö a full-bleed dashed overlay (`pointerEvents:none`, above the SVG's `zIndex:1`) mounts only during an active file drag, with a centered "Drop site plan to place it on the map" pill.
  3. **Window-level guard** ÔÇö a `useEffect` blocks the browser's default file-drop everywhere (`dragover`/`drop` preventDefault when the drag carries Files), so releasing a few pixels off-target no longer navigates away / opens the PDF (the core "nothing happens / data loss" feeling).
- **Edge cases:** multi-file drop takes the first file + `flashWarn`s ("one overlay placed at a time"); panel `onDrop` `stopPropagation`s so it can't double-fire to the canvas; all new colors use the existing `PAL` theme tokens (no raw hex), keeping WCAG AA.
- **Deduped:** distinct from the Doc-Review file-drop dropzones (`ProjectFilesDrawer`/`FileBrowser`) ÔÇö this is the Site Planner *site-plan overlay*, a separate surface; the pattern was reused, not the component.

### B446 ÔÇö Dropping/opening a file gives no clear signal it registered `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; fixed same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). lint 0 ┬À 1434 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V131 (`ui-audit/verify-open-feedback.mjs`, 6/6, logged out).** Owner repro: drop a PDF (especially with a doc already open, or open from the Files panel) ÔåÆ no immediate, unmistakable sign anything is happening; it can look like nothing registered ("silence is a crash").
- **Root cause:** `setBusy(true)` existed but the only "OpeningÔÇª" text rendered in the EMPTY state. The B294 drop-over-open path and the `openReview`/Files-panel/`loadSingleReview` paths render no empty state, so they showed no loading signal at all; a non-PDF reject set `err`, which the canvas never displays, and a null drop / `loadReviewÔåÆnull` could no-op silently.
- **Fix:** a canvas-level **"Opening &lt;name&gt;ÔÇª" overlay** (`data-testid="opening-overlay"`, spinner + filename, reuses the `spin` keyframe) driven by `busy`, rendered in BOTH the empty-state and the canvas branches (so it covers the prior sheet on a drop-over-open). `busy`/`busyLabel` are now set on EVERY entry path ÔÇö `openFile`, `openReview`, `loadSingleReview` ÔÇö and cleared in token-guarded `finally`s so a rapid switch can't let an older load's finally hide the newer overlay. **Silence-is-a-crash:** `openFile` surfaces a null drop + a non-PDF reject + a read failure on the always-visible `openErr` banner (role="alert") AND the inline `err`; `openReview` surfaces a missing-row and a `loadReviewÔåÆnull`. A superseded (older) load stays intentionally silent ÔÇö the newer load owns the feedback.
- **Deduped:** a GAP in **B294** (which only wired the drop *handler*, not feedback) + the **B405/docIntent** open paths ÔÇö not a duplicate of any.

### B447 ÔÇö Switching between files is flaky and "forgets which file I was on" `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). Code complete + logged-out paths verified V131; the auth-only switch-determinism check (two saved cloud reviews) logged for a signed-in live pass in VERIFICATION.md V131.** Owner repro: open A, switch to B, switch back to A ÔåÆ A's backdrop/markups/calibration/view/breadcrumb don't reliably return; clicking the file list feels like it drops the doc you were on.
- **Root cause (the clobber):** the debounced autosave's pending cloud write is cancelled the instant a switch changes the persistence `deps` (the effect cleanup clears the timer), so the outgoing review's last edit lived ONLY in the synchronous localStorage mirror. `openReview` then loaded the **stale cloud copy** by id and never reconciled with that mirror ÔÇö so switching back overwrote the newer local state with the older cloud one.
- **Fix:** `openReview` now (1) `await saveNow()` to **flush the outgoing review's pending write** before the incoming load begins, and (2) loads the incoming via `reconcile(await loadReview(id), readDraft(uid, id))` so it picks up that file's newer local mirror if the cloud is behind ÔÇö exactly the pattern the resume-on-mount path already uses. The lazy-mount **resume effect was confirmed to already stand down** for an in-workspace switch (its `booted`-once guard + `projectId` early-return + `bootDocIntentRef` mean it never races an explicit `openReview`), matching the cross-workspace docIntent contract the brief asked to mirror.
- **Depends on B448** (the in-memory bytes cache) so a switch-back is reliable even while the file's first upload is still in flight.
- **Deduped:** a GAP/regression of **B52** (the load-supersede token, intact) + the resume/docIntent fix ÔÇö not a duplicate.

### B448 ÔÇö Backdrop vanishes on switch/reload while a fresh drop's upload is still in flight `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; fixed same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/vibrant-pascal-wkg50i`). New pure `lib/sessionBytes.js` + 5 unit tests (1434 total); logged-out render-from-cache verified V131; the keyless-mid-upload signed-in confirm logged V131.** Owner repro: drop a PDF, then immediately switch files (or reload) before the Drive/Supabase upload resolves ÔåÆ the source is still **keyless** (no storageKey/driveKey), so the backdrop can't be re-fetched and you land on the re-drop banner / a blank canvas with orphaned markups.
- **Fix:** a session-lifetime **byte cache** (`sessionBytes`, module-scoped Map keyed by `srcId`, capped FIFO at 8) holds the dropped **File** (never a raw ArrayBuffer ÔÇö pdf.js transfers a buffer to its worker and detaches it; a File re-reads cleanly). `openFile` caches the bytes the moment a srcId is minted; `fetchSourceBytes` checks the cache **before** `classifySource`/Drive/Supabase, so a dropped backdrop stays viewable for the rest of the session regardless of upload state. Survives the lazy DocReview workspace unmounting/remounting (a tab switch mid-upload). Only when the bytes are truly absent does the precise B405 re-drop banner show.
- **Deduped:** net-new ÔÇö the safety net that makes B447's switch-back reliable mid-upload; complements (does not replace) B405's source-state taxonomy.

### B439 ÔÇö Rename / delete projects from the breadcrumb switcher (right-click + kebab) `[Header-Nav / Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; built same session per STANDING RULE #1)*
`[x]` **DONE + verified (branch `claude/beautiful-archimedes-0qjr3w`). lint 0 ┬À 1416 tests (4 new) ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b439-b440-project-manage.mjs` 7/7 (Site path).** Triggered by the owner's live report: "i cant change names or delete in the schedule module."
- **What shipped:** every project row in the Row-1 switcher dropdown (`ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx`) now carries a hover-revealed kebab `Ôï»` **and** a right-click menu (both open the same menu) ÔåÆ **Rename** (inline `<input>` in the row; Enter/blur commit, Esc cancel, trim, empty/whitespace rejected ÔåÆ keeps prior name) + **Delete** (a two-step inline confirm, never `window.confirm`/`prompt` per the owner rule). The menu is a SECOND portal layer (zIndex 5000/5001) above `AnchoredMenu`'s click-away backdrop (4000/4001), so a click inside it never closes the parent dropdown (the documented gotcha ÔÇö handled by stacking, not an outside-click exclusion list).
- **New props** `onRenameProject(id,newName)` / `onDeleteProject(id)` (threaded through `AppHeader.jsx`). Uncontrolled (Site/Markup) ÔåÆ drives the store directly + `refresh()`; controlled (Schedule) ÔåÆ the workspace's callbacks (B440).
- **Store layer:** `projects.js` gains `renameProject`/`deleteProject` thin wrappers; `storage.js` gains **`deleteSiteGroup(groupId)`** ÔÇö deletes every plan in the group locally + cloud, returning an aggregated `{ ok, removed, error? }`. Honest-failure rule honored: a signed-in delete that matches **zero rows** (RLS/ownership mismatch) is reported as `ok:false` (would otherwise reappear on reload claiming "deleted"); the breadcrumb surfaces it as a toast. Deleting the **active** project routes to the all-projects view.
- **Tests:** `test/deleteSiteGroup.test.js` (4) ÔÇö group rename relabels every plan, group delete removes only that group + reports `removed`, unknown-group no-op, single-plan case. Unique ids per test to respect the B372 resurrection tombstone.
- **Deduped:** distinct from **B158** (still Open) ÔÇö that's the YOUR SITES *sidebar* row Ô£ò; this is the *breadcrumb switcher dropdown* (different component; a "project" here = a whole Site group).

### B440 ÔÇö Extend the Schedule iframe bridge for rename / delete `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (feature) ÔÇö depended on B439  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; built same session)*
`[x]` **DONE (branch `claude/beautiful-archimedes-0qjr3w`). Code complete + symmetric with the existing nav bridge; signed-in/online LIVE check logged in VERIFICATION.md (the embedded scheduler can't boot in the offline sandbox ÔÇö CDN + its own Supabase backend are blocked, the same limitation `verify-schedule-picker.mjs` hits here, so the bridge half isn't headless-verifiable).** This is what the owner actually reported broken.
- **The brief's "verify first" CONFIRMED:** the embedded app (`public/sequence/index.html`) ALREADY had internal `renameProject`/`deleteProject` (the latter with a snapshot + `window.confirm`). So B440 was just command-plumbing, exactly as the `Scheduler.jsx` comment implied ÔÇö NOT a from-scratch in-app rename/delete.
- **What shipped:** `Scheduler.jsx` passes `onRenameProject ÔåÆ post planar:nav-rename` and `onDeleteProject ÔåÆ post planar:nav-delete` (alongside the existing nav-select/dashboard/new). The embedded app handles both: `nav-rename` ÔåÆ `renameProject(id, name.trim())` (re-guards empty); `nav-delete` ÔåÆ `deleteProject(id, { skipConfirm:true })` then routes to the Dashboard (reports) if the deleted project was active. `deleteProject` was refactored to accept `{ skipConfirm }` so the breadcrumb's inline confirm doesn't double-prompt with the embedded `window.confirm` (standalone scheduler UI untouched ÔÇö still confirms). Both re-emit `planar:nav-state`, so the breadcrumb updates automatically.

### B442 ÔÇö Merge/Clear banner buttons unclickable; canvas grab-cursor + pan bleeds through `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 via screenshot+voice as "NEW-1"; minted **B442** = highest real B# across both files (B441) + 1)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1421 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`, 6/6).**
- **Repro (owner):** Select tool ÔåÆ Shift-click 2+ parcels ÔåÆ the floating "N parcels selected ┬À Merge parcels ÔÅÄ ┬À Clear" banner appears ÔåÆ hovering Clear showed the canvas **grab hand** (not a pointer), and clicking it **panned the map** instead of clearing the selection; Merge was likewise dead.
- **Root cause (confirmed in code):** the banner is a sibling of the SVG canvas under `wrapRef`, and the SVG carries `zIndex:1` while the banner had **no z-index** (auto). A positioned `zIndex:1` paints *over* an auto sibling, so the transparent canvas sat on top of the banner ÔÇö it swallowed the clicks and showed its own `cursor:grab`. The buttons' own `cursor:pointer` never won because the press never reached them.
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`, merge-banner block ~L6871):** gave the banner an explicit **`zIndex:6`** (clears the SVG's `1`, below the slide-in tool rail) so its buttons are the topmost element ÔÇö clicks land, the pointer cursor applies, Clear runs `setCombineSel([])`, Merge runs `mergeParcels()`, neither starts a pan. Added defensive `onPointerDown`/`onMouseDown` `stopPropagation` on the banner container (belt-and-suspenders for any future reparenting under a pointer-handling ancestor ÔÇö the z-index is the actual fix). Applied the **identical guard to the sibling parcel-edge easement banner** (`tool==="easement" && easeMode==="parceledge"`), which had the same latent bug.
- **Dedup:** NOT **B271** (that fixed interrupted-gesture recovery via pointercancel/blur ÔÇö a stuck-capture *recovery* path); this was a plain missing z-index on a normal press. NOT **B441** (that was MapFinder county-map identify *latency*; this is the planner's banner stacking). Cross-referenced both.

### B443 ÔÇö Shift-click parcel multi-select unreliable / "needs several tries" `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; minted **B443**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). Headless-verified: 4 interior shift-clicks ÔåÆ "4 parcels selected" on the first try (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`).**
- **Repro (owner):** with the Select tool, Shift-clicking parcels to add them to the merge selection often didn't register ÔÇö it took several clicks.
- **Root cause (confirmed in code ÔÇö broader than first hypothesized):** **B420** makes a parcel's *interior* click-through ÔÇö only its ~12px boundary hit-stroke grabs the press. So a Shift-click on the parcel **body** misses the parcel and falls through to the canvas background handler `onBgDown`, whose `tool==="select" && e.shiftKey` branch started a **marquee select** (not a merge-toggle). The pick only registered when the click happened to land on the thin boundary line ÔåÆ the "click a couple times" feel. (Secondary, rarer: when a parcel is *unlocked + selected*, the capture-phase vertex-insert `onCanvasVtxDownCapture` could steal a Shift-click meant to merge a neighbor sharing that edge.)
- **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):**
  - `onBgDown` select+shift branch (~L1990): before starting a marquee, ray-cast (`pointInRing`) the press against the parcels; if it lands inside one, `toggleMerge(hit.id)` + select it and return. Topmost (last-drawn) parcel wins, matching the boundary-stroke path. Gated on `settings.parcelSelect` (respects the "Select parcels: off" browse mode). Marquee still works on genuinely empty canvas.
  - `onCanvasVtxDownCapture` (~L2508): the Shift+edge vertex-insert now yields when the press is inside *another* parcel's body ÔÇö that's a merge-pick on a neighbor, not an edit of the selected path ÔÇö letting it fall through to the merge-toggle.
- **Verified after B442** (a residual stuck pointer-capture from the banner bug could have masked this) on a clean canvas.

### B444 ÔÇö Selection-count text overlapped/garbled by the Merge button in the merge banner `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 as "NEW-3"; minted **B444**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped (branch `claude/gallant-euler-sk8kwd`). Headless-verified the count label and Merge button bounding boxes do not intersect (`ui-audit/verify-merge-banner.mjs`).**
- **Repro (owner screenshot):** the banner read "4 parce**s** selected" ÔÇö the red "Merge parcels" button appeared to sit *on top of* the count text.
- **Root cause:** NOT a flex-layout defect ÔÇö it was a **symptom of B442's z-index bug.** The banner sat *behind* the SVG, so the canvas's own parcel area-labels and strokes painted *over* the banner text, garbling "parcels" ÔåÆ "parces". A flex row never overlaps its own items.
- **Fix:** resolved by B442's `zIndex:6` (the banner now paints on top, crisp). Hardened anyway: wrapped the count in a `<span>` and added `whiteSpace:nowrap` to the banner so the label can never wrap or be crowded by the buttons at any count. Headless check asserts the label's and Merge button's rects are disjoint with 4 selected.

### B441 ÔÇö Parcel selection feels laggy: paint the highlight optimistically on click, confirm via identify in the background `[Site Planner / map]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-24 from a ChatGPT thread as "NEW-1"; minted **B441** = highest real B# across both files (B440) + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/hopeful-brown-q7q4jo`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1421 tests ┬À build green.**
- **Repro (owner):** clicking a lot on the live county map took a variable delay ÔÇö sometimes a couple seconds ÔÇö between the click and the parcel highlighting. Root cause (confirmed in code): `MapFinder.handleClick` did `setBusy(true)` then `await identifyParcelEager(...)` ÔÇö a network identify against the county ArcGIS REST service(s) ÔÇö and only painted the highlight + pushed the selection AFTER that promise resolved. The variable multi-second lag was county-server round-trip latency, not client compute.
- **Fix ÔÇö optimistic local highlight, then reconcile (no network on the felt path):**
  - The county parcel outlines are already drawn client-side as esri-leaflet vector `featureLayer`s (`makeParcelLayer`). New `optimisticHitAt(latlng)` hit-tests those already-loaded features (cheap `getBounds().contains` reject ÔåÆ ray-cast on the outer rings) and returns a hit shaped like an identify hit. New pure `geoJsonToEsriFeature` (arcgis.js) converts the display layer's GeoJSON polygon into the same esri-shaped `{geometry:{rings},attributes}` the highlight/select path already consumes ÔÇö so the on-screen outline feeds the **exact same** `addParcelHit` pipeline (one source of truth, B233/B22 keys intact). Prefers a real county over the statewide TxGIO backup (mirrors identify's source priority), then the tighter parcel when several overlap.
  - `handleClick` now paints the optimistic highlight **synchronously, before** the identify starts ÔåÆ the click feels instant. The `await identifyParcelEager(...)` still runs in the background as the authoritative confirm (still feeds the circuit breaker via `onSettled` ÔåÆ `recordSourceResult`, B244 unchanged). On return it **always rolls back the optimistic outline and rebuilds from the identified geometry** (full-res + real account/address attrs), so the IMPORTED parcel is never the simplified display outline. No flash ÔÇö the remove+re-add happen in one synchronous turn after the await.
  - **Reconcile cases:** identify confirms the same parcel ÔåÆ seamless real-geometry swap; identify returns a different/refined parcel (multipart per B36c, or a statewide-backup relabel) ÔåÆ optimistic rolled back, authoritative added (multipart still highlights every tract); identify finds nothing / errors ÔåÆ optimistic highlight **visibly rolled back** + the existing "No parcel right there" / "server isn't responding" message (B245 wording preserved).
  - **Instant local toggle-off:** new `selectedHitAt(latlng)` ÔÇö a click inside an already-highlighted parcel now deselects it with **zero** network round-trip (we already have its geometry).
- **Edge cases handled:** display layer zoom-gated (`PARCEL_MINZOOM` 14) ÔåÆ when nothing's loaded under the click, `optimisticHitAt` returns null and we fall back to the original await-identify-with-busy behavior (never fabricate a highlight from nothing). Optimistic add only fires when the parcel isn't already highlighted, so a rollback can never strip a pre-existing selection. `selectParcelAt` (address-search select, B233) shares the same reconcile pipeline; the optimistic step is click-only (an address select already runs after a `flyTo`, so there's no felt lag there).
- **Tests:** +3 unit tests for `geoJsonToEsriFeature` (Polygon flatten + attrs, MultiPolygon flatten, null/non-polygon) round-tripping through `outerRingsLngLat` (test/arcgis.test.js, 19 in that file). lint 0 errors ┬À full suite 1421 green ┬À build green.

### B436 ÔÇö e2e: open a PDF so the per-tool rail-arm assertions actually execute `[Doc Review / Markup, Infra]` (task) ÔÇö follow-up from B432/B280  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ┬À build green ┬À mechanism verified headless (`ui-audit/verify-b436-fixture-pdf.mjs`, 8/8).**
- **The gap:** the B280 fixture seeds a *site*, not a *doc_review with a PDF*, so on CI the Review module opened to the file browser and the tool rail never rendered ÔåÆ Section B's per-tool `aria-pressed` tests all skipped (green, but the ARM path ÔÇö the heart of NEW-9 ÔÇö wasn't exercised; only Section A schema conformance was).
- **Fix (code-only, deploy-independent):** added a tiny valid 1-page fixture PDF `e2e/fixtures/sample.pdf` (generated with a correct xref; parses in pdf.js ÔÇö 612├ù792). Section B's `beforeEach` now `setInputFiles` it onto the always-rendered header file input (`input[type="file"][accept*="pdf"]`); `openFile()` parses it and mounts the canvas so `markup-rail` renders, then each `tool-<id>` test arms and asserts `aria-pressed="true"`.
- **Robustness:** a tool the matrix lists before it's deployed is **skipped, not failed** (`btn.count()` guard) ÔÇö the matrix can lead the live build; likewise the whole per-tool test skips gracefully if the rail never renders (storage hiccup / older deploy). Targets the file input by attribute (not a new testid) so it works against the current production build.
- **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b436-fixture-pdf.mjs` drives the local preview LOGGED-OUT (the parse ÔåÆ canvas ÔåÆ rail path needs no auth; only the best-effort background upload does) and confirms the rail renders + line/rect/arc/dimension arm. lint 0; local e2e 22 passed + 22 skipped (auth-gated still skip without creds locally). The auth-gated arm runs for real against the deploy in CI.

### B432 ÔÇö Per-tool matrix assertions + loop driver in CLAUDE.md `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ÔÇö NEW-9, closes the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ┬À 1390 tests ┬À build green. V127.**
- **`e2e/markup-tools.spec.js` upgraded** with two sections:
  - **Section A ÔÇö matrix Ôåö propertySchema conformance (no auth, no browser).** For every tool row in `toolsForWorkspace("doc")` (except `drawMode:"mode"` rows), asserts `schemaForMarkup({kind:id}).map(s=>s.key).sort()` equals `propsForTool(id).sort()`. Runs in the same Playwright process via pure-JS imports; if propertySchema drifts from the matrix the loop flags it before any browser opens. Imports: `TOOL_MATRIX`, `propsForTool` from `tools.matrix.js`; `schemaForMarkup` from `propertySchema.js`.
  - **Section B ÔÇö per-tool rail arm (auth-gated, B280 account).** A `test.describe("per-tool rail arm")` block generates one test per doc-workspace non-mode tool row. Each test clicks `getByTestId("tool-<id>")` and asserts `aria-pressed="true"`. Gracefully skips (with a precise explanatory message) when the tool rail isn't visible (no PDF open / B280 fixture not seeded yet). The rail is already wired with the correct `data-testid` and `aria-pressed` attributes (ToolRail B330).
  - The old "pending wiring" stub loop is replaced ÔÇö the loop is now real (Section A fires immediately; Section B fires as soon as the B280 seeded account can open a PDF).
- **Loop driver section added to CLAUDE.md** (before `## KEY DECISIONS`): documents the matrix rule (Ôøö never edit to pass a test), how both sections work, what a red row means + how to fix it, and how to grow assertions as each tool ships.
- **B423 umbrella is now complete** (B424ÔÇôB432 all done). The engine is live, the spec is machine-checkable, and the loop is encoded.

### B431 ÔÇö Unified interaction model + edit handles `[Doc Review / Markup + Site Planner]` (feature) ÔÇö NEW-8, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ┬À 1390 tests ┬À build green. ÔÅ│ signed-in round-trip ÔåÆ V126.**
- **ParcelDrawing `window.prompt` ÔåÆ inline `numEdit`** (the explicit plan blocker). `calibrateFrom` in `ParcelDrawing.jsx` now sets `calBox` state `{pts, px, sx, sy, val, err}` instead of calling `window.prompt`. A positioned inline div renders at the line's midpoint: `Length (ft)` label, input (Enter = commit, Esc = cancel), "Set" button, error message on invalid. `onDown` while calBox is open cancels it. Matches the DocReview inline calibrate (B304) in UX.
- **`shouldPan` already reused** ÔÇö `viewportTransform.js`'s `shouldPan` rule was already imported and used in DocReview (B329); no new wiring needed.
- **Vertex drag handles in DocReview (B431).** When a markup is selected in the Select tool, small white circles with accent outline render at each vertex (skipped for pen/highlight which have too many pts). Clicking within 8 screen-px of a grip starts a single-vertex drag (`vtxDragRef`) instead of a full-markup move. `onMove` translates only the grabbed vertex; `onUp` commits via `pushHistory` + `setMarkups`. The `vtxPreview` state drives a live preview during the drag, and passes into the MarkupRenderer call the same way `dragPreview` does.
- **Shift snap (45┬░) during TWOPOINT drawing.** In `onMove`, when `draft && TWOPOINT.has(tool) && e.shiftKey`, the cursor is snapped to the nearest 45┬░ interval from the first point before `setCursor`. In `onDown` for the second-point commit, the same snap is applied before `commit`. So Shift held = perfectly horizontal/vertical/diagonal line constraints for Line, Rect, Ellipse, Dimension, and Calibrate tools.
- **Reuse mode + set-as-default already done** (B428 `propStyle` sticky defaults ÔÇö DocReview stays on the same tool after commit, doesn't revert to Pan).
- **Dedup:** convert-segment-to-arc and rotate/resize handles are deferred (roadmap ÔÇö complex, low daily use); the high-value pieces (vtx handles, Shift snap, inline calibrate) ship this session per the standing rule.

### B429 ÔÇö New tools: Arc, Dimension, Pen, Highlight, Eraser, Snapshot `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) ÔÇö NEW-6, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). lint 0 ┬À 1390 tests ┬À build green. ÔÅ│ signed-in draw round-trip ÔåÆ V125.**
- **6 new tools** added to DocReview tool rail and wired end-to-end:
  - **Arc** (`multiPoint`): click start ÔåÆ click end ÔåÆ click a point on the curve; auto-commits on 3rd `onDown`. Renders as a quadratic B├®zier (`Q ctrl`) where ctrl = 2┬Àp3 ÔêÆ (p1+p2)/2. Live preview during first two points.
  - **Dimension** (`twoPoint`): drag end-to-end; renders line + two perpendicular witness ticks (┬▒7 px) + a centred calibrated length label. Added to `MEASURE_KINDS` in MarkupRenderer so it gets the label from `measureLabel`.
  - **Pen** (`freehand`): pointer-capture press-drag; accumulates pts on `onMove`, commits on `onUp` (min 2 pts). Renders as a stroked open polyline.
  - **Highlight** (`freehand`): identical gesture to Pen. `TOOL_DEFAULTS` forces `stroke:#fbbf24`, `strokeWidth:12`, `opacity:0.35` (clamped to Ôëñ0.5 in renderer so it stays translucent even if the user overrides).
  - **Eraser** (`region`): drag a rubber-band box; `eraseInBox` removes any `pen`/`highlight` markup whose pts intersect the box ÔÇö never touches other kinds. Hit-test treated as rect-interior so a partly-drawn eraser box shows a live rectangle.
  - **Snapshot** (`region`): drag a box; commits a `snapshot` markup. Renders as a dashed rect + a camera emoji at the centroid.
- **Arrow** = arrowhead toggle on Line (already in `arrowStart`/`arrowEnd` property column via B428 matrix ÔÇö no standalone rail button needed per locked owner decision).
- **Classification sets** (`TWOPOINT`/`MULTIPOINT`/`FREEHAND`/`REGION`) extended to include all new tools; pointer handlers dispatched through them uniformly.
- **`TOOL_DEFAULTS`** map provides per-tool style overrides between `propStyle` (user sticky) and column defaults; currently used for Highlight.
- **`MarkupRenderer.jsx`** extended: arc (quadratic bezier, optional arrowheads), dimension (line + ticks + label), pen (stroked polyline), highlight (wide translucent path), snapshot (dashed rect + emoji).
- **`verify-b429.mjs`** headless smoke: Review tab loads, no JS crash. Tool buttons not visible logged-out (tool rail only shows when a PDF is loaded) ÔåÆ ÔÅ│ V125.

### B426 ÔÇö Shared `MarkupRenderer.jsx` + `PropertyPanel.jsx`; DocReview left property panel `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ÔÇö NEW-2 cont./NEW-3, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-24; minted **B426** = B425 + 1)*

Handles 12 kinds: distance/polylength/perimeter/area/count (measures) + rect/cloud/ellipse/line/polyline/polygon/text (shapes). Reads style via `readProp`/`propertySchema` (matrix drives defaults). Local `cloudPath()` (same algorithm, module-local). Replaces DocReview's inline `draw()` function; the host SVG just maps `pageMarks.map(m => <MarkupRenderer ... />)`.
- **`src/shared/markup/PropertyPanel.jsx`** ÔÇö pure property editor driven by `schemaForMarkup(markup)`. Sub-controls: ColorControl/NumberControl/RangeControl/BoolControl/EnumControl. WCAG AA theme tokens only (no raw hex). Host wires `onChange(key, value)` ÔåÆ `writeProp`. `"Select a markup"` / `"No editable properties."` empty states.

PropertyPanel appears at the bottom of the left sheet-list panel when a markup is selected (scrollable 220px area, `Properties / <kind>` header). Old inline `draw()` function removed; SVG layer uses `<MarkupRenderer>` with optional `dragPreview` overlay.

### B434 ÔÇö Map marker: building/shield silhouette ÔåÆ precision pin, progress folded into the ground ring `[Site Planner / map]` (feature) ÔÇö amends B161; depends on B433  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-2"; minted **B434**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/stoic-albattani-8bmtp0`). lint 0 errors ┬À 1335 tests ┬À build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-precision-pin.mjs` 18/18.**
- **What:** replaced the zoomed-out site marker (`buildingPinIcon` ÔåÆ renamed **`sitePinIcon`**, `MapFinder.jsx`) with the **precision pin**: a color **bulb** on a short vertical **stalk** seated over a **ground ring** (a survey-monument read). The **ground-ring center is the anchor** ÔÇö it sits exactly on the site coordinate (replaces the old shield bottom-tip anchor). Zoomed-in parcel/plan render unchanged; the tiny left-rail list markers stay simple token discs.
- **Salience gradient realized here:** size tracks importance (Pursuit largest ÔåÆ Dead smallest, via the token `tier`), plus each token's `mapOpacity` (settled stages recede). The open-site emphasis is kept (**1.15├ù** + a subtle drop-shadow on that one pin only ÔÇö no per-pin shadows, they flash on re-render).
- **Progress ÔÇö folded in, NOT dropped.** B161's building carried a progress arc (derived from status until a real `progress_pct` lands ÔÇö B163). The shield had silently dropped it (B365); the precision pin **restores it as the ground-ring sweep** 0ÔÇô100% from 12 o'clock, **same source** (`STATUS_PROGRESS` = pursuit 10 ┬À active 60 ┬À onhold 30 ┬À complete 100 ┬À dead 0). A faint full track keeps the ring readable at 0%.
- **Anchor is fixed for every tier:** the ground-ring center is placed at the viewBox bottom edge so it maps to the hit-box bottom-center (`iconAnchor = [HIT_W/2, HIT_H]`, Leaflet margins `-17/-46`) at every size ÔÇö the art can shrink without the anchor drifting, and the tap target stays generous (fixed 34├ù46 hit box). Verified the ring's lower half overflows symmetrically about the point (`overflow:visible`).
- **Colorblind cue:** the glyph (ÔÇû/Ô£ô/Ô£ò) rides inside the bulb for the settled stages; Pursuit/Active are glyphless discs (color + size + sweep carry them). `statusGlyph` dropped the now-dead flag/pulse cases.
- **Verified (V123, `ui-audit/verify-precision-pin.mjs`, 18/18, chromium-1228, 0 page errors):** bulb+stalk+ring structure; anchor = ring center; sweep ordering Complete 31.42 > Active 18.85 > On-hold 9.43 > Pursuit 3.14; size tiers 20.8 > 18.7 > 17.1 > 15 > Dead 13.3; solid bulb + white keyline (never hollow); correct coral/blue/amber/gray fills; **no `#E24B4A`**; Dead (filtered in) = solid gray Ô£ò, dimmed (opacity 0.5), smallest. Retired the superseded `ui-audit/verify-b365-markers.mjs`; refreshed the stale `verify-b232-b236.mjs` color constants.
- **Deduped:** amends **B161** (the marker shape it replaces ÔÇö its progress encoding restored, not lost) + depends on **B433** (reads the corrected tokens). NOT B365 (which this supersedes shape-wise).

### B433 ÔÇö Correct status palette: solid fills, salience-ordered, Dead off red `[Site Planner / UI]` (task) ÔÇö amends B234 (`statusTokens.js` is the single source); touches B320  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-24 as "NEW-1"; highest B# was B422 when filed, but a concurrent `main` (#321, the Shared markup engine batch) took B423ÔÇôB432 while this was in flight, so renumbered to **B433** = the real next free ID ÔÇö code/harness/test tags moved with it)*

The brief was drafted against the *pre-B365* baseline ("Pursuit is a hollow dashed blue ring", "Active was green #639922"), so it overlapped. The genuine RESIDUAL work this session: (a) take **Dead off red**, (b) stand up a dedicated **alert-red token**, (c) drop the now-redundant Pursuit/Active glyphs, (d) make Dead a **solid** disc (it was a hollow outline). The "reconcile the drifted duplicate `STATUS_COLOR` in `ProjectLibrary.jsx`" item was already moot ÔÇö that constant/file no longer exists (`#E24B4A` lived in exactly two spots, `statusTokens.js` + `index.css`), so there was no parallel implementation to fold.

Repointed the three real red consumers off the (now-gray) `--status-dead` ÔåÆ `--danger`: the **cloud-off badge** (`CloudSyncBadge`), the **failed-layer dot** (`LayerPanel`), the **delete ├ù** (`ReviewsBar`) ÔÇö so they keep their red while Dead goes gray. `--status-dead` ÔåÆ gray in both themes; `test/cloudSyncBadge.test.js` updated to expect `--danger`; a `--danger` pair added to `ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` (passes ÔëÑ3:1 both themes).
