- **Standing rules added to CLAUDE.md (KEY DECISIONS):** (1) map markers always solid + white keyline, never hollow on the aerial; (2) salience monotonic PursuitÔåÆDead; (3) red reserved for alert/error, never an inert state.
- **Deduped:** amends **B234** (extended the single token source in place) + touches **B320** (the dark `--status-*` variants); the residual of **B365** (not a re-do). Net-new token: `--danger`.

### B281 ÔÇö CI: Playwright on the deploy + failure auto-files an @claude issue `[Infra / CI]` (feature) ÔÇö the B423 loop's alarm  *(2026-06-23; built the SAME session as B278)*
`[x]` **Built. New workflow `.github/workflows/e2e.yml` mirrors the proven idempotent `gis-drift.yml` pattern.** Runs `npm run e2e` against the deploy (`E2E_BASE_URL` secret; `github.event.inputs.base_url` override for manual runs) on a weekday schedule + `workflow_dispatch`. On failure ÔåÆ opens (or BUMPS ÔÇö never duplicates) a single `@claude` issue labeled `e2e-drift` with the report tail (last 5 KB) + the "do NOT edit the matrix to pass" rule; on green ÔåÆ auto-closes any open `e2e-drift` issue. Uploads the Playwright HTML report as an artifact (14-day retention). Permissions `contents:read` + `issues:write`. **This is the verifier that drives the autonomous implementÔåÆtestÔåÆfixÔåÆadvance loop** for the remaining tool rows (B426+). Extends `build.yml` (the required "build" check), doesn't replace it. Gated on B280's secrets ÔÇö without them the auth specs skip, the smoke still runs (a useful signal, not a false alarm). **Dedup:** NET-NEW; first real filing of the long-parked `@claude` e2e workflow.

### B278 ÔÇö Playwright regression harness against the deploy `[Infra / E2E]` (task)  *(2026-06-23; built this session ÔÇö the B423 loop's verifier)*
`[x]` **Built + the logged-out smoke RUNS GREEN locally (2/2, headless Chromium driving the real built app). 106 vitest files / 1376 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.**
- `@playwright/test` added; `playwright.config.js` targets `BASE_URL` (a deploy) OR, unset, builds + serves `vite preview` on :4173 itself. Chromium launches with `--ignore-certificate-errors` + `ignoreHTTPSErrors` (the TLS-inspecting-proxy flag every `ui-audit` harness uses). `npm run e2e` script added; artifacts git-ignored.
- **`/e2e`:** `helpers.js` (`signIn` via the seeded B280 account, `openModule`, a `hasAccount` guard so auth specs SKIP cleanly without the secrets ÔÇö never a false fail); `smoke.spec.js` (logged-out: app boots, shell renders, SiteÔçäReview switch ÔÇö runs anywhere, **verified 2/2 in-sandbox**); `markup-tools.spec.js` (auth-gated; **the home of the NEW-9 per-tool loop** ÔÇö imports `toolsForWorkspace` so the suite is generated FROM the matrix; per-tool arm+draw+panel assertions fill in as tools land, B426+). `e2e/README.md` documents both run modes.
- **Stable `data-testid`s added** (the only app-code change): `module-tab-<id>` on the workspace tabs (`AppHeader`), and `tool-<id>` on every shared `ToolRail` button (`ToolRail.jsx`, pass-through). eslint: `e2e/**` added to the Node-globals override.
- **Ôøö In-sandbox limit (documented, not a harness bug):** the AUTH-gated specs only green against a deploy + the B280 account (CI secrets) ÔÇö neither exists in the sandbox. The logged-out smoke is what's self-verifiable here, and it passes. **Dedup:** NET-NEW ÔÇö not the ad-hoc `ui-audit/*.mjs` scripts; this is the deterministic regression suite, reusing their cert-proxy flag. (The preview-env "Cloud on" blocker was already RESOLVED 2026-06-20 ÔÇö previews use the production env.)

### B425 ÔÇö Shared markup engine: the PURE layer (geometry, model, measure, hit-test, property schema, tool registry) `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ÔÇö NEW-2, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-23; minted **B425** = B424 + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + unit-tested the SAME session. lint 0 ┬À 106 test files / 1376 tests ┬À build green. No behavior change yet ÔÇö this is the shared foundation the host wiring (B426+) builds on; the existing ~101 suites stay green as the regression guard.**
- **New shared module `src/shared/markup/` (mirrors the purity discipline of `shared/viewport/viewportTransform.js` ÔÇö pure math/policy, no React/DOM):**
  - `geometry.js` ÔÇö point primitives lifted verbatim from `takeoff.js` (`dist`, `pathLength`, `polyArea`, `centroidOf`, `midOfPath`, `pointInPoly`) + the Site Planner drawing helpers (`rot2`, `snap45`, `projToSeg`) + `bboxOf`. Unit-agnostic (the ft/page-unit seam lives in `measure.js`).
  - `markupModel.js` ÔÇö reconciles the TWO host storage forms so shared code reads any markup: Document Review's flat `pts`, the Site Planner's `{a,b}` line AND its centre-box `{cx,cy,w,h,rot}` (ÔåÆ `boxCorners`). `ptsOf`/`setPts`/`translate`/`bboxOfMarkup`/`minPtsOf`/`isClosed` + `sanitizeMarkup`/`sanitizeMarkups` (moved here from `takeoff.js`).
  - `measure.js` ÔÇö the RELOCATED measure engine. `measureValue`/`measureLabel`/`MIN_MEASURE_PTS`/`canCommitMeasure`; the **unit-scale seam** is the `ftPerUnit` arg (Site passes 1 / feet-native, Doc passes its per-page calibration, Stitcher its set calibration). `rollup` GENERALIZED to accept either the legacy `calByPage` object OR a `(m)=>ftPerUnit` function ÔÇö back-compatible. Adds the `polylength` (open polyline) measure kind.
  - `hitTest.js` ÔÇö **completes the B155 shared tranche** (no `hitTest` existed before; hit-testing was 3 inline ways). `pickMarkup`/`pickMarkupIndex` (top-most wins; handle ÔåÆ edge ÔåÆ filled interior; filter for locked) + `hitEditPath` (vertex 9 px beats edge 11 px ÔÇö the Bluebeam rule from `SitePlanner.jsx:2462`). Tolerances are screen px ├À `view.scale` (equal grab at any zoom).
  - `propertySchema.js` ÔÇö `schemaForMarkup` derives the panel's control list FROM the matrix (panel can't drift from spec) + reconciles canonical keys Ôçä the Site Planner's legacy field names (`strokeWidth`Ôåö`weight`, `strokeStyle`Ôåö`dash`, `fontColor`Ôåö`color`) via `readProp`/`writeProp`, so neither host renames stored data.
  - `toolRegistry.js` ÔÇö the one sanctioned divergence: `ARM_POLICY = {site:"revert", doc:"reuse", stitch:"reuse"}` + `nextToolAfterCommit`; `SITE_TOOL_ALIAS` maps `mline`/`mrect`/ÔÇª Ôçä canonical ids.
- **`doc-review/lib/takeoff.js` is now a thin re-export shim** ÔåÆ every historical import (`DocReview.jsx`, `Stitcher.jsx`, 4 test files) resolves unchanged from the shared engine; all 50 takeoff/stress/rollup tests pass against the relocation.
- **New tests (5 files):** `markupModel` (both host forms), `markupHitTest` (per-kind rules + top-most + vertex-beats-edge, seeded from both hosts), `propertySchema` (matrix-consistency + keyÔåöfield), `toolRegistry` (arm-policy + alias), plus the relocated measure coverage. **Dedup:** net-new shared module; completes B155's unbuilt shared tranche.

### B424 ÔÇö The markup tool├ùproperty MATRIX as committed data (`tools.matrix.js`) `[Site Planner + Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ÔÇö NEW-1, part of the B423 umbrella  *(2026-06-23; minted **B424** = B423 + 1)*
`[x]` **Built + conformance-tested the SAME session. The single source of truth the engine's property panel is DRIVEN by and the NEW-9 tool tests are GENERATED from.**
- `src/shared/markup/tools.matrix.js`: `TOOL_MATRIX` (one row per tool ÔÇö `id`, `label`, `hint`, `category`, `drawMode`, `closed`, `measureOutput?`, `properties[]`, `workspaces[]`), `PROPERTY_COLUMNS` (the control universe with type/label/default/range), `DRAW_MODES`/`CATEGORIES`/`WORKSPACES` vocabularies, + pure accessors (`toolById`, `propsForTool`, `toolsForWorkspace`, `measureTools`, `isClosedTool`, `columnMeta`). Encodes the Bluebeam-parity TARGET (a tool a workspace doesn't yet implement still has its row, so the loop has a goal). Arrow is a Line arrowhead toggle, not a row (owner decision).
- **Ôøö The refinement loop NEVER edits this file to make a red test go green** ÔÇö the matrix is the spec; a failing assertion means the CODE is behind it. The matrix changes only on explicit owner direction. (Pinned in the file header.)
- `test/toolMatrix.test.js` (14 tests): every row well-formed; ids unique; every category/drawMode/workspace/property token in its declared vocabulary; closed shapes pairing fill+fillOpacity; arrowheads only on open shapes; measures declare a known output; accessors round-trip. **Dedup:** net-new (no prior matrix/spec item).

### B421 ÔÇö Markup z-order ("Arrange"): Bring to Front / Forward / Send Backward / to Back via Bluebeam shortcuts + a right-click menu `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B421** = highest B# across both files (B420) + 1; deduped net-new)*
`[x]` **Filed AND built + verified + shipped to main the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/gallant-turing-mcempv`). lint 0 ┬À 1335 tests ┬À build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-b421-arrange.mjs` 17/17 (V122).**
- **What:** when two markups overlap on a sheet, control which draws on top ÔÇö Bluebeam's four Arrange ops on the selected markup (Bring to Front, Bring Forward, Send Backward, Send to Back), reachable two ways from ONE shared core: keyboard chords and a new right-click context menu. Net-new ÔÇö there was no z-order control before.
- **Core (one engine, both entry points):** new pure module `src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/arrange.js`. `reorderWithinPage(markups, id, mode)` permutes the selected markup within its **same-page** siblings (draw order IS z-order ÔÇö the overlay paints `pageMarks` in array order, so the last same-page entry is on top), then splices the reordered group back into the global array **without disturbing other sheets' markups** (they keep their absolute slots, so an arrange never silently shuffles another sheet's stack). Returns the SAME array reference on a no-op (already at that end / a lone markup / unknown mode) so a top/bottom arrange skips the history push + state write. `arrangeFlags(markups, id)` reports `{page, count, index, atTop, atBottom}` for the greying. `DocReview.arrange(mode)` wraps it with `pushHistory()` so every arrange is one undoable step (B303) and persists for free (markups are already saved/mirrored).
- **Keyboard:** the existing `onKeyRef` handler gains an arrange branch **before** the `if (mod) return` early-out (which previously handed every other modified key back to the browser, so a branch after it would never fire). Gated on `mod && sel`; matches **`e.code`** (`BracketRight`/`BracketLeft`), NOT `e.key` ÔÇö Shift turns `e.key` `]`ÔåÆ`}`, which would silently miss the Bring-to-Front chord. Ôîÿ works via the existing `e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey`; the INPUT/TEXTAREA guard already blocks firing mid-text-edit. `]`=up / `[`=down, Shift=jump to the end: `Ôîÿ/Ctrl+]` Forward ┬À `Ôîÿ/Ctrl+Shift+]` Front ┬À `Ôîÿ/Ctrl+[` Backward ┬À `Ôîÿ/Ctrl+Shift+[` Back.
- **Right-click menu:** new `onContextMenu` on the viewport runs the existing `hitTest` (B33) at the cursor ÔÇö a hit selects that markup and opens the menu at the cursor; a **miss** does nothing (no `preventDefault`, so the native browser menu shows over blank canvas ÔÇö never an empty custom menu). The menu is **portalled to `<body>`** (`createPortal`, the same escape-the-clip pattern as `AnchoredMenu`/the Site-row context menu) so the canvas's `overflow:hidden` + stacking can't clip it; cursor-positioned + viewport-clamped; dismissed on click-away / right-click-away / **Escape**. Items: the four Arrange ops (with platform-aware shortcut hints), a divider, then **Edit textÔÇª** (text notes only, reuses the B293 inline editor) and **Delete** (reuses the existing `pushHistory` + filter-by-id). Arrange items **grey out** at the top (Front/Forward) / bottom (Back/Backward) of the stack; a lone markup disables all four. Not opened while an inline editor / calibrate entry is active.
- **Scope / parity:** built in `DocReview.jsx` (the single-sheet markup surface). **Skipped `Stitcher.jsx` deliberately** ÔÇö it renders only `measures` (distance/area), with no overlapping fillable markups (rect/cloud/text), so an Arrange menu there would be empty/no-op. Toolbar buttons for Arrange are out of scope (shortcuts + right-click only) ÔÇö an easy follow-up.
- **Tests:** `test/arrange.test.js` ÔÇö **14 unit tests** (all four ops; single-step Forward/Backward are inverses; SAME-reference no-ops at the ends / lone markup / unknown id / unknown mode; other-sheet slots preserved through an interleaved-page reorder; input not mutated). Headless **V122** (`ui-audit/verify-b421-arrange.mjs`, **17/17**): three overlapping rects, every chord + every menu item reorders correctly verified BOTH via the DOM order AND the persisted localStorage draft, each undoable, greyed at top/bottom, blank-canvas right-click shows no menu, Edit-text only on a text note, Delete works, and the order **survives a full reload**. 0 page errors.
- **Deduped:** net-new ÔÇö NOT B397 (Scheduler-Gantt z-order, a different surface), NOT the CSS stacking-context fixes (B66 et al.), NOT B33/B374 (markup hit-testing, which this REUSES). NEW-2 from the same brief (named markup **Layers**) is filed as **B422** ÔÇö a ROADMAP STUB (design pass required before build), left Open, NOT built.

### B420 ÔÇö Parcel grabs only by its boundary/setback, not its interior (free the interior for building work) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1", live; minted **B420** = highest real B# across both files (B419) + 1 ÔÇö B416ÔÇôB419 were taken by concurrent PRs #313/#314/#316 while this was in flight; branch/commits/code tags first read B417, renamed on the merge-in of main)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/vigilant-cerf-6rvxga`). lint 0 ┬À 1320 tests ┬À build green; new regression harness 7/7.**
- **Repro (owner, live):** in Select mode, clicking anywhere inside a lot's interior selected the parcel and popped the parcel menu ÔÇö even when you were trying to edit a building/element sitting in that lot; a press in the open interior near (but not on) a footprint grabbed the parcel. **Expected:** a parcel is grabbable only by its boundary edge or its setback line; the interior is click-through (presses fall to whatever element is painted on top, else the background pan ÔÇö exactly as on empty canvas). Appearance unchanged; hit-area change only.
- **Root cause (code-confirmed):** the visible parcel `<polygon>` (`SitePlanner.jsx`, parcels render block) carried `pointerEvents="all"`, so the whole fill was a hit target even when `fill:"none"`; both `startMoveParcel` and `onContextMenu` ÔåÆ `setParcelMenu` fired from any interior point.
- **Fix ÔÇö boundary-only hit model:** the visible polygon is now `pointerEvents="none"` (its fill never captures, even when a translucent fill is toggled on ÔÇö boundary-grab stays consistent regardless of fill state; deliberately NOT `"painted"`, which would re-arm the interior whenever a fill is set). A companion transparent **fat hit-stroke** on the same ring (`stroke="rgba(0,0,0,0.001)"`, `strokeWidth={12}`, `pointerEvents="stroke"`) carries `startMoveParcel` + the context menu ÔÇö reusing the B146 easement-edge fat-stroke technique. ~12 px is **zoom-independent** because `f2p` (`worldToScreen`) already projects feetÔåÆscreen pixels, so the polygon is drawn in pixel space (the existing easement-edge picker uses the same flat `12`; no division by zoom needed). The **setback outline** gets the same fat hit-stroke for active parcels (owner explicitly wanted "boundary OR setback"); a shared `onParcelContext(e,id)` opens the parcel menu from either grab target.
- **Preserved (re-verified, no regression):** B310 click-vs-drag + B311 "Select parcels" toggle (both keyed on `startMoveParcel`, which now fires from the boundary instead of the fill), Shift-click-to-merge (`toggleMerge`), align-edge picking (`alignToParcelEdge`), unlocked-parcel drag-to-move, and B230 shared vertex editing (it hit-tests at the svg root via `editablePath`/`hitEditPath`, independent of the fill, so vertex handles + insert/delete are untouched). The B311 tooltip was reworded "click a lot" ÔåÆ "click a lot's edge or setback line" (label only, no logic change).
- **Distinct from B155/B156 (the inverse, not a contradiction):** those make small markup-shape *interiors* grabbable (correct for annotations). A parcel is a *container*, so boundary-grab is the right inverse. **Layers on B310/B311**, does not replace them.
- **Verified (V121):** new `ui-audit/verify-b420-parcel-boundary-grab.mjs` **7/7** ÔÇö interior click does NOT select, boundary click selects, setback-line click selects, right-click parity (interior opens no menu, boundary opens it), no page JS errors. Repointed `verify-b310-b311` / `verify-edge-runs` / `verify-edge-runs-irregular` off the now-dead interior-click / `pointer-events="all"` selectors ÔåÆ all green; `verify-b221-b222` (a pond element on a parcel still selects + its vertices insert/delete 4ÔåÆ5ÔåÆ4ÔåÆ3), `verify-parcel-split-control`, `verify-b383-add-parcel`, `verify-parcel-resilience` all still pass.
- **Deduped:** net-new. NOT B155/B156 (markup interiors ÔÇö the inverse), NOT B310/B311 (gesture/toggle ÔÇö preserved), NOT B146 (reuses its technique), NOT B213 (inactive parcels already draw no setback line).

### B419 ÔÇö Rename the module accent token `markup` ÔåÆ `review` (names only, values locked) `[Doc Review / theming]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-3"; minted **B419**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). lint 0 ┬À 1282 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b417-b419.mjs` 11/11.**
- Pure token-name rename; every hex value byte-for-byte unchanged. `src/index.css` (`:root` + `[data-theme="dark"]`): `--accent-markup`ÔåÆ`--accent-review`, `--on-accent-markup`ÔåÆ`--on-accent-review`, `--accent-markup-text`ÔåÆ`--accent-review-text` (#EF9F27 / #412402 / #8A5410 light / #EF9F27 dark). `src/shared/theme/palette.js`: `accentMarkup`ÔåÆ`accentReview`, `accentMarkupText`ÔåÆ`accentReviewText` (LIGHT + DARK).
- Every consumer repointed: `AppHeader` `ACCENT_FILL`/`ACCENT_TEXT`, `DocReview.jsx` (­ƒùé Files chip), `FileBrowser.jsx` (5 refs). CI's WCAG guard updated too ÔÇö `ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` (5 pairs) + `test/contrast.test.js` (allowlist regex `Markup underline`ÔåÆ`Review underline`). `--warn-text` left untouched (separate saving/offline amber).
- Verified zero orphaned `accent-markup`/`accentMarkup` refs in `src`/`ui-audit`; runtime probe confirms `--accent-review` ÔåÆ #EF9F27 and `--accent-markup` ÔåÆ "" (gone). **Dedup:** net-new (a pure rename of the B316ÔÇôB319/B341 tokens, no value change).

### B418 ÔÇö Module user-facing label ÔåÆ "Review" + pin the naming in CLAUDE.md `[Doc Review]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; minted **B418**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). headless 11/11.**
- The module's canonical user-facing name is now **"Review"** everywhere it self-names: the row-2 **tab** (`AppHeader` MODULES), the loader caption (`moduleLoaderTheme.js` "Loading markupÔÇª"ÔåÆ"Loading reviewÔÇª"), the Shell workspace label + error-boundary text (`Shell.jsx` "Document Review"ÔåÆ"Review"), and the empty-state heading (`DocReview.jsx`). Internal id `doc-review`, the folder, the `/markup` route + prefetch keys, and the `markups` data model all UNCHANGED (route test still green). Accent-token rename is B419.
- **Grounded the report:** NEW-2 assumed the tab read "Markup" and warned to leave a separate "­ƒôü Library" button alone ÔÇö but the live code had moved on (B404 renamed the tab "Markup"ÔåÆ"Library"; the old ­ƒôü Library door is now the ­ƒùé Files drawer). So the only "Library" string left WAS the module tab; renaming it "Library"ÔåÆ"Review" RESOLVES the tab-vs-Files-drawer collision B404 introduced. Follow-on to B404 (done), not a dup.
- **CLAUDE.md pinned** under a new "Module naming" note: user-facing "Review"; internal id `doc-review`; folder `src/workspaces/doc-review/`; token `--accent-review`; historical names "Markup"/"Document Review"/"Library" all mean this module.

### B417 ÔÇö Paste lands at the cursor in BOTH the Site Planner and Review canvases `[Site Planner + Doc Review]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B417**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1; shipped to main this session (branch `claude/gifted-shannon-ycachr`). lint 0 ┬À 1282 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless 11/11. One live copyÔåÆpaste click-through logged in VERIFICATION.md V120.**
- A pasted copy drops **centered under the mouse** (Bluebeam). **Site Planner** (`SitePlanner.jsx`): a `lastPtrFt` ref records the live cursor in WORLD feet on every canvas move (ref-only, no setState in the hot path); `pasteClip()` anchors there via the existing `snapPt` (grid/snap toggle + held-Alt bypass), setting a rect's `cx/cy` and translating a polygon so its bbox center sits under the cursor; a null/non-finite ref falls back to the old fixed offset (never a no-op). Ctrl/Ôîÿ+D duplicate keeps its fixed offset (duplicate Ôëá paste).
- **Review** (`DocReview.jsx`): net-new copy/cut/paste (there was none) ÔÇö a component `clip` ref + `copyMarkup`/`cutMarkup`/`pasteMarkup` wired into `onKeyRef` (Ôîÿ/Ctrl+C/X/V; only `preventDefault` when they act, else native). Paste clones with a fresh `uid()`, sets `page` to the current sheet, centers the markup's bbox on the live `cursor` (text = its single point), with a fixed page-unit fallback. Markups stay on the editable overlay ÔÇö the immutable PDF backdrop is untouched.
- Centering math is a shared, unit-tested helper `src/shared/geometry/pasteGeom.js` (`centerOn`/`bboxCenter`, 9 tests) so both canvases agree and it can't silently regress. Scope: single-element clipboard; repeated Ctrl+V restamps at the cursor. **Verified:** placement math unit-tested; headless boots the planner + confirms the Ctrl+V wiring no-ops on an empty clipboard. The interactive copyÔåÆpaste click-through pends a real-browser pass (selecting a canvas element via *synthetic* pointer events isn't reliable headless) ÔÇö V120. **Dedup:** net-new (builds on, not a dup of, the fixed-offset `pasteClip`/`duplicateEl`).

### B416 ÔÇö "Split a parcel" control unreachable where users now look ÔÇö surface it in the Parcel panel `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1", live; minted **B416** = highest B# across both files (B415) + 1)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/affectionate-wright-gf3kgt`). 1273 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green; new regression harness 11/11.**
- **Repro (owner, live):** "no way to reach the Split tool ÔÇö the 'Split a parcel' control is not shown on screen."
- **Grounded the report against the live code FIRST (the brief's own instruction ÔÇö find why it stopped rendering before touching downstream).** The geometry path (`performSplit` ÔåÆ `lib/polygonSplit.js`) is intact (B128, unit-tested) and so is the rail control: the right-rail **Boundary Ôû¥** flyout still renders **Draw new parcel + Split a parcel** (unchanged since 2026-06-21 per `git blame`; headless **scenario A passed on the unfixed build** ÔÇö the rail item is present, opens, and arms split). So this was **NOT** a deleted/ungated control ÔÇö none of the three suspected failure shapes (button gone / menu won't open / item removed) held.
- **Real cause ÔÇö a discoverability regression from the B383 reorg.** B383 surfaced parcel operations (**´╝ï Add parcel**, and **Merge parcels** already lived there) into the left-hand **Parcel panel**, which is now where a user reaches for parcel actions. **Split is the inverse of Merge but was left only in the rail menu** ÔÇö so a user looking in the panel finds Add + Merge but no Split, and reads it as "Split is gone." The brief anticipated exactly this: *"if the entry points were reorganized, Split must get a deliberate home in that same reorg, not be left orphaned."*
- **Fix ÔÇö surface "Ô£é Split a parcel" in the Parcel panel, beside Merge** (`SitePlanner.jsx`, the `leftPanel === "parcel"` Section): a full-width chip shown whenever `parcels.length >= 1` (you need a parcel to split; Merge stays `> 1`), active-styled when `tool === "split"`, calling the SAME `selectTool("split")` as the rail ÔÇö no new pipeline, no second split path. The rail Boundary Ôû¥ ÔåÆ Split is kept unchanged (the drafting-tools home); this adds the panel home, it doesn't move it. Helper line mirrors the tool hint (click points ÔåÆ double-click/Enter ÔåÆ divides in two).
- **Smoke-tested the full path live, per the brief** (it could have been hidden because half-broken ÔÇö it wasn't): activate ÔåÆ point capture (`snapSplit`) ÔåÆ finish (`finishActiveDrawing` on double-click/Enter) ÔåÆ commit (`performSplit`) splits one parcel into two end-to-end.
- **Regression guard added (the brief's explicit ask ÔÇö a "control silently disappears" / silence-is-a-crash class can't recur uncaught):** `ui-audit/verify-parcel-split-control.mjs` asserts Split is reachable from BOTH the rail AND the panel, each arms the tool, and the end-to-end cut splits 1ÔåÆ2. Hardened so a missing control reports a clean fail instead of crashing the run. **11/11.**
- **Deduped:** net-new. NOT B128 (concave-cut split *geometry* ÔÇö a different, intact concern), NOT B96 (the shared Enter/double-click finisher), NOT B130's "Boundary" rename (which *created* the rail menu). No existing Open/Done item covered Split's UI reachability.

### B414 ÔÇö Doc Review (Library) sheet flashes white on zoom-settle ÔÇö double-buffer the re-raster `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; first filed as B412, but a concurrent `main` (PR #311) took B412/B413, so **renumbered B414** = the next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/confident-edison-lpbco9`). 1273 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green; headless-verified.**
- **Repro:** open a sheet in the Library/Markup canvas, zoom in/out; ~140 ms after the gesture settled the sheet flashed blank/white before the sharp render appeared (Bluebeam never flashes on the same file).
- **Root cause (code-confirmed):** the single visible canvas was re-rastered on every zoom settle; reassigning `canvas.width/height` clears it to transparent, revealing the white page-box `<div>` (`background:"#fff"`) behind it, and the `page.render()` that refills it is async (pdf.js worker) ÔåÆ a visible blank gap each settle.
- **Fix:** `pdf.js` `renderInto` now rasterises into an OFF-SCREEN canvas and blits the finished frame onto the visible canvas (`drawImage`) in one synchronous step ÔÇö the compositor never sees the cleared canvas. It bails on `isStale()` so a superseded render never blits a stale frame (B40 guard kept; off-screen also sidesteps the same-canvas throw). The biggest contributor in practice is B415's backdrop (the whole-page layer no longer re-rasters on zoom at all), but the double-buffer makes EVERY swap (page change + the detail settle) flash-free.
- **Verified:** `ui-audit/verify-b414-b415-render.mjs` D1 (backdrop backing dims unchanged across a zoom ÔåÆ can't blank) + D3 (detail layer polled through a settle, never caught blank ÔÇö min opaque centre pixels > 0). Foundation for B415.

### B415 ÔÇö Doc Review (Library) render is softer than Bluebeam on large sheets ÔÇö viewport-clipped two-layer rendering `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; first filed as B413, renumbered past main's B412/B413 (PR #311) to **B415**; depends on B414)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/confident-edison-lpbco9`). 1273 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green; headless-verified.** Net-new architectural item that **supersedes the whole-page raster path B247/B265/B327/B329** (extends that path, does not fork a second renderer).
- **Repro:** Planyr's linework/text was visibly softer than Bluebeam side-by-side, most pronounced on large (E-size) civil sheets and when zoomed in.
- **Root cause:** the viewer rasterised the ENTIRE page into one canvas at a single density; the ~24 MP backing-store budget (`renderBudget.backingScale`, B327/B247) was spent across the whole sheet, so on a big sheet density dropped below full (even <1├ù) everywhere to stay under budget ÔÇö even though only a small window is visible. Bluebeam rasters only the visible region at full resolution.
- **Fix (two-layer, additive over B414; the page-box + SVG-overlay CSS model is byte-for-byte unchanged, so markup/zoom-anchor geometry is untouched):**
  - **Backdrop** ÔÇö the whole page at a FIXED, zoom-independent density (`backdropDensity`, ~8 MP budget) painted once per page, filling the page box. It never re-rasters on zoom (so it can't flash white) and is always present as a no-white floor.
  - **Detail** ÔÇö only the visible page-rect (+ a 25% margin) at full device dpr (cap 2), budget-bounded on the REGION not the page (reuses `backingScale` on the region size), re-rastered on pan/zoom settle (double-buffered via B414) and positioned over the backdrop. Because the detail backing store is ~viewport-sized, the budget stops forcing low density ÔåÆ on-screen resolution stays native at any sheet size/zoom.
  - Pure region/density math (`visibleRegion`, `tileCovers`, `backdropDensity`, `BACKDROP_PX_BUDGET`) in `renderBudget.js` (+13 unit tests in `test/renderBudget.test.js`); region render path (`renderInto` with a `transform` offset + canvas-clip) in `pdf.js`; two-canvas viewport + settle-driven `renderBackdrop`/`renderDetail` in `DocReview.jsx`. The B40 race guard + B39 destroy are kept; the imported drawing stays a display-only backdrop.
- **Verified (`ui-audit/verify-b414-b415-render.mjs`, 11/11, E-size sheet @ deviceScaleFactor 2):** zoomed to 2.6├ù, the **detail layer renders at 2.00├ù native device density** while the OLD whole-page raster would be **0.95├ù at the same zoom ÔåÆ 2.11├ù sharper**; backdrop fills the page box and stays opaque through the zoom; markup overlay still draws. Regression: the B329 viewport harness (`verify-markup-viewport.mjs`) still 13/13 (pan / cursor-anchored zoom / calibration survival / measurement geometry / sheet-switch), plus `verify-docreview-viewer` 13/13 and `verify-delete-markup` 10/10.

### B412 ÔÇö Title-block reader: read bare sheet codes + stop mislabeling civil sheets (MATCH LINE / project banner / "C-14 SHEET NUMBER") `[Doc Review / reader]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-23 with a screenshot of wrong Markup labels + a real GPL topo upload; minted **B412** = highest B# (B411) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE this session on branch `claude/planner-drive-file-testing-r0vh2a` ÔÇö 1264 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green; verified on the owner's real GPL topo slice + 7 new unit tests.** Fixes the shared positional reader `src/shared/files/sheetMeta.js`, so both the Markup sidebar AND the Stitcher benefit.
- **Bare sheet-code read (the topo unblock).** A scanned/reference sheet prints its number as a big standalone "C-2" with the "SHEET NO." label drawn as graphics, not text ÔÇö the label-anchored `parseSheetNumber` read it as "". New `prominentSheetCode` in `readSheetNumberInZone`: when no labeled number is found in the title-block zone, take the TALLEST lone sheet-code-shaped token that carries a real discipline letter-prefix (`disciplineFromSheetNumber`), so "C-2" is read but the set page-count "46" (no prefix) is not. On the owner's file C-2/C-3/C-4 now read (confidence 1.0) ÔåÆ the three topo pages group as one **"Topographic Survey ┬À C-2ÔÇôC-4 ┬À 3 sheets"** set (was 3 separate singles).
- **Title scoped to the title-block zone (the GPL civil mislabels).** `readSheetTitle` now restricts candidates to the band (or, with no band, the right/bottom edge strip ÔÇö the same discipline the number read uses) instead of scanning the whole page; so a large drawing-area "MATCH LINE ÔÇª" annotation and the big "GRAND PORT LOGISTICS" project banner can no longer outscore the real title. Falls fully open only for older callers with neither band nor dims.
- **Reject match-line + field-label rows.** `isTitleish` drops any "MATCH LINE/for continuation" line and any title-block FIELD-label row (new `looksLikeFieldRow`, matched ANYWHERE in the line, not just at `^`) ÔÇö so a merged "C-14 SHEET NUMBER" is rejected and the real title wins with the number read separately.
- +7 unit tests (`test/sheetMeta.test.js`): bare-code read vs page-count, NTS scale, the 3-sheet grouping, MATCH-LINE/banner/field-row title rejection. **Remaining stitch gap ÔåÆ B413** (these sheets carry no match-line text/scale, so AUTO-alignment is data-limited ÔÇö its own item).

### B410 ÔÇö Auto-filing: per-page discipline + multi-discipline split into separate PDFs `[Doc Review / auto-filing]` (feature)  *(owner-requested 2026-06-23: "test all the Drive files for sheet-title/file-name recognition, and if a drawing has multiple disciplines, split it up properly"; minted **B410** = highest B# (B409) + 1)*
`[x]` **DONE this session ÔÇö engine + read path + byte-split actuation all shipped on branch `claude/planner-drive-file-testing-r0vh2a`. 1257 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green; validated against 7 real Drive PDFs + a real end-to-end byte carve.** Owner picked "split into separate PDFs" (AskUserQuestion, 2026-06-23), which is built.
- **Problem (found by testing real Drive files):** the filing read looked at only the first 2 pages and stamped ONE discipline on the whole file ÔåÆ real misfiles: a Mesa Mechanical set filed Architectural (cover named the architect); a Jacintoport make-ready filed Fire Sprinkler off its cover. And a combined set (Architectural with a bound-in Structural set) filed as one discipline.
- **Engine ÔÇö `src/shared/files/disciplineSplit.js` (pure, 13 tests):** PREFIX-FIRST per-page discipline (a sheet code's letter prefix, e.g. `A5.00`ÔåÆArchitectural / `M-1`ÔåÆMechanical, is the engineer's own filing code ÔåÆ overrides a keyword tally that stray cross-refs skew ÔÇö fixed the real `A5.00`-read-as-Structural case) + STICKY smoothing (covers/notes/unreadable pages join their block; a lone codeless misread snaps to two agreeing neighbours) ÔåÆ discipline segments + merged sets; a set is standalone only with ÔëÑ2 pages OR its own code (no junk 1-page filings). `dominant` = majority vote (the single-file answer, fixes the first-2-pages misfile). `buildFilingPlan` makes a COMPLETE page partition (orphan/cover pages ride with the dominant ÔÇö no page lost).
- **`titleBlockParse.disciplineFromSheetNumber()`** exported as the single prefixÔåÆdiscipline source (classifyDiscipline's fallback reuses it).
- **Read path ÔÇö `doc-review/lib/pdf.js` `extractAllPagesText` + `localRead.js`:** reads EVERY page's text, classifies each, returns majority `discipline` + `multiDiscipline` + per-discipline `sets` (page ranges) + `numPages` on the filing decision.
- **Actuation ÔÇö `doc-review/lib/pdfSplit.js` (pdf-lib, lazy-loaded; 6 tests):** `splitPdfByPlan` carves the source into one clean PDF per discipline ("Bergstrom Phase 2a - Structural.pdf"), each filed in its own folder via `FileBrowser`/`ProjectFilesDrawer` (both drawers); single-discipline files file as before; a split failure falls back to single-file filing (never worse).
- **Real-file validation:** make-ready Fire SprinklerÔåÆArchitectural; Mesa Mechanical ArchitecturalÔåÆMechanical; **Bergstrom Arch IFP byte-split into Architectural (9p) + Structural (pages 5ÔÇô8) as two PDFs**; surveys/fire unchanged. Residual gaps tracked in **B411**.

### B407 ÔÇö Record the Supabase org/project naming convention in CLAUDE.md `[Infra]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B407** ÔÇö B405 was the max when filed, but a concurrent `main` (#305/#306) took B406 for Shared team workspaces, so B407 is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Filed AND done the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/blissful-hopper-zcijkh`).** Doc-only ÔÇö no code paths touched. The owner had already applied the Supabase-side rename himself; this item is purely recording the agreed scheme so future env work references the right project and the "Planar vs Planyr" confusion is logged as resolved.
- **Convention recorded in CLAUDE.md's "## Supabase" section** (right beside the existing build-time-env note): **org = `Planyr`**; **live/production project = `planyr-production`** (AWS `us-east-1`, the old "Site Planar" dashboard label, serving planyr.io); **`planyr-staging` reserved** for a future second environment. Rationale captured: one app ÔçÆ the org carries the brand and projects are named by environment (lifecycle stage), not by feature ÔÇö readable as the commercial/VC direction adds a staging env, and no redundant "Planyr / Planyr" nesting.
- **Safety fact captured (the whole point of the doc):** renaming a Supabase org/project **display label is cosmetic** ÔÇö it does NOT change the project ref, the API URL (`VITE_SUPABASE_URL`), or the anon key, so **no rebuild** and cloud features don't break. The client binds to the **immutable 20-char project ref** in the URL, never the display name; only a *different* project (new ref) or rotated keys would change the build-time env.
- **Two-project reality captured (corrected mid-session after a code check ÔÇö prevents a data-loss footgun):** the suite spans TWO live Supabase projects ÔÇö the main app (ref `lyeqzkuiwngunutlkkmi`, via Cloudflare `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`) AND the **Scheduler** (`/sequence/`), which **hardcodes a DIFFERENT ref `ksetjztkplttbcehyicv`** in `public/sequence/index.html` (tables `planar_data`/`planar_history`/`planar_suggestions`). So the us-west-2 "Planar" project is most likely the Scheduler's LIVE backend, not a spare. The doc now says: match each dashboard project's ref against BOTH live refs before any rename/delete; rename live projects for what they are (the Scheduler's is NOT `planyr-staging`), delete only a project matching neither ref. Plus a noted open question: whether to consolidate the Scheduler onto the main project. Renaming stays cosmetic; deleting a live project is irreversible.
- **README reconciled:** the README "Deploy secrets" note references Supabase only via "the anon key" (no project name), so nothing there needed changing. No build/test impact (Markdown only).

### B207 ÔÇö Google Drive storage backend behind the adapter ÔÇö LIVE & DEPLOYED `[Server]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B204, renumbered **B207** ÔÇö `main` took B203ÔÇôB205; closed out 2026-06-23)*
`[x]` **DONE ÔÇö code complete + unit-tested AND provisioned + deployed live in production.** Drive is the active storage backend on planyr.io: store/fetch/list/move/rename **bytes only** in a Workspace Drive, the substrate beneath auto-filing (which writes through it; the queryable index records stay in Supabase Postgres). Moving storage to Drive **removes the Supabase 50 MB-per-file ceiling** on the happy path.
- **Code (built across sessions; a prior session advanced it past the original "scaffold", confirmed by a 2026-06-23 ground-truth read ÔÇö NOT a rebuild):** the thin Drive REST `client` (`server/storage/backends/driveClient.js` ÔÇö create/get/list/move/rename/delete + share-link on `drive.file`; OAuth refreshÔåÆaccess-token via `server/oauth/googleAuth.js`); the Planyr-keyÔåöDrive-id map persisted on **Supabase Postgres** (`server/storage/idStoreSupabase.js` + migration `server/storage/db/drive_files.sql`, own-row RLS) so Cloud Run / Function scale-to-zero can't wipe it; the same-origin **`POST/GET/DELETE /api/files`** handler (`functions/api/files.js`) that validates the Supabase Bearer JWT server-side (`server/auth/supabaseAuth.js`) before any Drive op and honors `X-Planyr-Key/-Folder/-Name`; the one-time OAuth consent flow (`functions/api/auth/google/{start,callback}.js`); a guarded Drive round-trip smoke test (`functions/api/drive/selftest.js`). Tests: `test/driveClient.test.js`, `test/idStoreSupabase.test.js` (incl. bindÔåÆresolve persistence end-to-end), `test/storageAdapter.test.js` (backend swap + 404/not-connected degrade).
- **Item (4) "Cloud Run + proxy" met more simply:** Drive byte-I/O runs IN the Cloudflare Pages Function (`functions/api/files.js`) ÔÇö same-origin, no CORS, the sanctioned thin-Pages-Function pattern (same as the OAuth callback) ÔÇö instead of a separate Cloud Run hop. The frontend keeps its same-origin `/api/files` (`reviewStore.storeSource` Drive-first/Supabase-fallback, `downloadFromDrive`/`deleteFromDrive`); a 404/503 degrades to the Supabase fallback (no regression).
- **Provisioned + deployed (owner-verified 2026-06-22, Cloudflare Pages "planyr" ÔåÆ Production):** `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` (plaintext, existing OAuth client `263720424027-ÔÇª`), `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` (secret), `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN` (secret), `PLANYR_STORAGE_BACKEND=drive`, `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (un-prefixed server-side auth pair; the `VITE_`-prefixed frontend copies are also present). All carried by the green Production deploy **912de2b** ("Library tab rename"). The temporary `PLANYR_SELFTEST_TOKEN` gate was **removed** after use (correct ÔÇö it's a world-triggerable Drive writer otherwise). Decisions held: OAuth **Internal** (skips Google verification + the 7-day refresh-token expiry), scope **`drive.file`** (least privilege), all creds **server-side only** (never a `VITE_` var, same isolation as the APS key).
- **Ôøö Operational guardrail ÔÇö DO NOT re-run the OAuth setup or re-mint `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`.** A fresh consent mints a NEW refresh token that no longer matches the deployed secret and would take Drive filing offline. The setup is complete; treat the deployed token as load-bearing.
- **Only optional follow-up:** a signed-in end-to-end re-confirmation on planyr.io (drag a fresh PDF into Project Files ÔåÆ expect no "Drive copy failed" + a new `drive_files` row). This already passed (2026-06-20 notes); re-run only for a post-deploy re-confirmation. Owner/cohort signed-in test (not a logged-out Cowork self-test).
- **Follow-up (separate, still open):** route the adoption targets (`overlayStorage.js`, `reviewStore.uploadSource/downloadSource`) through the adapter now that live creds are verified end-to-end ÔÇö a deliberate later pass, not part of standing up the backend.

### B405 ÔÇö Files browser: opening a file failed with one conflated/silent banner ÔÇö split into a precise taxonomy `[Document Review]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2"; minted **B405** ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B402 that a concurrent `main` (#297) took for a Scheduler item; folds in the standalone "oversize banner copy" item)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + unit-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/brave-brown-jb1n2l`).**
- **Grounded against the real repo FIRST (the brief was written blind).** The corrected open path is `FileBrowser.open(f)` ÔåÆ `onOpenReview(row)` ÔåÆ `openReview` ÔåÆ `loadReview(row.id)` (re-fetches the FULL Postgres record by id) ÔåÆ `loadSingleReview` ÔåÆ `fetchSourceBytes`. So the brief's "the Files-browser reshape drops storageKey/driveKey" hypothesis does **NOT** hold at the open hop ÔÇö the lightweight browser row is never the `src`; the full record (with `data.sources[]` carrying the keys) is re-fetched. Confirmed and flagged rather than rebuilding the (working) upload path.
- **Real defect = the read failure was ONE bucket, and one case was SILENT.** `fetchSourceBytes` returned silently on a missing source (`if (!src) return` ÔåÆ a blank canvas, no banner) and collapsed every other cause into a single `"Couldn't fetch ÔÇª"`, so oversize / never-stored / stored-but-unreachable / signed-out all read identically (and the oversize copy said "was too large to store in the cloud", which would go stale once B207's Drive cutover lifts the cap).
- **Fix ÔÇö one taxonomy, four hard-distinct states** (`src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/sourceState.js`, new ┬À pure ┬À **15 unit tests** `test/sourceState.test.js`): `classifySource` + `sourceUnavailableMessage` + `fileWarn`. States: **oversize** (names the 50 MB per-file cloud limit ÔÇö durable, never "coming soon") ┬À **not-stored** (a source record with no key ÔåÆ re-open to UPLOAD; this is the old silent path, now surfaced) ┬À **fetch-failed** (had a key but bytes didn't come back ÔåÆ transient/retryable "couldn't load just now") ┬À **signed-out** (sign in to view). `DocReview.fetchSourceBytes` classifies pre-download and, after a keyed download returns nothing, distinguishes fetch-failed vs signed-out; every line ends "(your markups are saved)".
- **"Re-open it" already uploads-and-rebinds (verified by reading the path, no change needed):** the banner's "Re-open fileÔÇª" ÔåÆ `openFile` reuses the srcId on a same-name re-drop and never changes `reviewId`, so markups stay bound to the same document id (no orphaning), `storeSource` re-uploads, and `buildSnapshot` persists the new key.
- **One vocabulary across surfaces:** `FileBrowser` + `ProjectFilesDrawer` queue warns now call the shared `fileWarn` (was three drifting copies).
- **Live caveat (the brief's own note):** the universal "every file fails" symptom is a LIVE storage/auth class the logged-out sandbox can't reproduce (most likely the `doc-review-files` Storage bucket/RLS not provisioned, or legacy keyless rows) ÔÇö the code fix makes whatever the live cause is **name itself** precisely + offer a working recovery; a signed-in pass on the deployed site (cohort/owner) confirms the render. lint 0 errors ┬À 1228 tests ┬À build green ┬À `DocReview` lazy chunk intact.

### B404 ÔÇö Rename the Document Review module tab "Markup" ÔåÆ "Library" `[Document Review]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1"; minted **B404** ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B401 that a concurrent `main` (#297) took for a Scheduler item)*

CSS-token contrast labels (`--accent-markup-text`) left as-is.

### B403 ÔÇö Silently persist & restore ALL export-screen state (no badge) `[Scheduler / Export]` (feature)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-5"; minted **B403**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).** On reopening the PDF/Print Exhibit, the user's last choices come back, defaulting to them, with **NO badge** (no "restored" indicator).
- **Persisted (localStorage `planar:exportPrefs:v1`, per browser/user):** page size, orientation, margins (preset + custom), name-align, every column toggle (ID/Task Name/Start/End/Duration/Status/% Done/Owner/Predecessors/Cost), Header/Cover options, the **expand/collapse state of each sidebar section**, and the **timescale/pan** state (`timeUnit`/`zoomMul`/`panFrac`) from B361.
- **Written synchronously** (not debounced) so closing the modal right after a change still saves it ÔÇö the earlier debounce dropped the last change on a quick close.
- **Per-user/session was used** (localStorage); column **width** keeps riding `data.exportColWidths` (B392, cloud-synced). `docDate` intentionally defaults to "today" each open rather than freezing a stale exhibit date; task selection/collapse stay data-specific (not persisted).
- `loadExportPrefs`/`saveExportPrefs` + an `EXPORT_PERSIST_KEYS` whitelist; the `cfg`/`secOpen`/`headerOpen` initializers overlay the saved values. Verified: 11├ù17ÔåÆPortrait + Owner off + Margins collapsed all restore after close+reopen, no badge.

### B402 ÔÇö De-conflict co-dated labels + route dependency curves into the TOP (12 o'clock) of the target `[Scheduler / Export]` (bug)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-4"; minted **B402**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).**
- **Connector reroute (curves KEPT):** in `buildGanttSVG`, the source still departs from its bar center, but the curve now terminates at **12 o'clock ÔÇö the top-center of the target glyph** (new `topOf()` per kind: diamond top vertex / summary bracket top / leaf bar top), with a vertical final control point (`ctrl.x == end.x`) and a **down-pointing arrowhead** descending into the top. Clears the endpoint date label that sits at 9 o'clock. NOT converted to orthogonal ÔÇö stays a cubic b├®zier (B396 preserved). The "Dep. arrows" toggle still turns them fully off.
- **Label de-collision:** the in-chart names are collected during the row pass, then a post-pass nudges any overlapping label boxes apart vertically (capped, defensive) before emitting them on a top `nameLayer`. A pure no-op when nothing overlaps (the common per-row case), so existing layouts are untouched. Reinforced by B401's right-edge framing room, which removes the right-cluster pile-up at its source.
- Verified: 87/87 connectors curved + on-palette, 87/87 vertical entry, 87/87 down arrowheads; V114 (print connectors curved + no-NaN) still PASS.
- **Follow-up fix (same session, owner-reported "clearly not working"):** the first pass entered at the target's geometric **center-top**, so on a long bar the connector swept far across to the bar middle (messy diagonals). Corrected to enter at the **connection x** (start for FS/SS, end for FF/SF) at the top edge, with adaptive departure/descent handles (small lead for near rows ÔåÆ no hump). Re-verified V116 + the SEED 87-connector view; all drop straight into the top right where the link lands.
- **Follow-up #2 (owner refinement ÔÇö natural arcs, not rigid):** forcing a strict 12-o'clock (vertical) entry and a 3-o'clock (horizontal) exit looked stiff. Now the connector **leaves from the source's BOTTOM edge** (Ôëê4ÔÇô6 o'clock ÔÇö clears the date at the source's side) and **arrives at the target's TOP edge** (Ôëê9ÔÇô12 o'clock ÔÇö clears the date at the target's side), with tangents that follow the natural travel direction (a short link drops near-vertically; a long horizontal link enters at a shallow angle from the side). The arrowhead is oriented along the entry tangent. Re-verified **V116 17/17** (87/87 curved, 87/87 arrive-from-above, 87/87 leave-downward, arrowheads point into the target).

### B401 ÔÇö Export default time window auto-fits all labels (extend the frame, never move the labels) `[Scheduler / Export]` (bug)  *(arrived as the corrected owner "NEW-2"; minted **B401**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V116, 16/16) the SAME session (branch `claude/vigilant-brown-5dqcog`).** On open, the default visible window auto-extends its edges so every start/end label sits on the sheet without clipping ÔÇö **framing only, no label repositioned** (no gutter, no flip). `buildGanttSVG` measures each label's natural (unclamped) overhang past the chart edges using the SAME `placeGanttLabel` helper as the render, converts the widest left/right overhang to lead/trail days, and extends `[minD,maxD]` ÔåÆ the visible window. **Capped at ~22% of the natural span per side** so a long label can't crush the schedule into a sliver (it clamps/flips as before past the cap). Default-only: the timescale/pan controls (B361) still adjust from there. Verified: no name clips (x0=10/x1=312 in W=322) and the earliest bar has lead room (x=17, not 0).

### B361 ÔÇö Export Gantt time-axis controls: discrete scale selector + continuous timescale zoom + horizontal pan + fix the dead preview toolbar (PDF/Print Exhibit) `[Scheduler / Export]` (feature + bug)  *(renumbered from a colliding B159 ÔåÆ B361, 2026-06-21; **amended 2026-06-22** to absorb the corrected owner "NEW-1")*

- **Dead/intermittent toolbar FIXED:** the floating preview toolbar (was the broken `float`/`position:sticky`-inside-a-flexbox "+ 82% Fit Move" from B400 ÔÇö clicks no-op, intermittently visible) is rebuilt as an **absolute overlay pinned to the top-right of a non-scrolling wrapper** (the scroller is now a child) ÔåÆ always visible, always clickable (`elementFromPoint` returns the buttons themselves).
- **Discrete time-scale selector** (the original B361 ask): a sidebar **Time scale** SegBtn ÔÇö **Fit ┬À Qtr ┬À Mo ┬À Wk ┬À Day** ÔÇö sets `cfg.timeUnit`; re-renders the preview; export-only.
- **Continuous timescale** (amended NEW-1): toolbar **Time ÔêÆ/+** multiplies `cfg.zoomMul`. The discrete unit and the continuous multiplier feed **one `vis` span** in `buildGanttSVG` (`opts.timeUnit`/`zoomMul`/`panFrac`), so they compose and never fight.
- **Pan** (amended NEW-1): "Move" renamed **"Pan"** (hand cursor); a drag slides the visible time window (`cfg.panFrac`, an offset from a **today-centered** default landing when zoomed in). Page **Zoom** (ÔêÆ/%/+) and **Fit** (reset zoom + axis) kept. Off-window names are suppressed so zoom/pan don't pile labels at the edge.
- **Distinct from B160** (the whole-split divider ratio ÔÇö left Open). Verified: toolbar clickable; "Mo" drops month rules 46ÔåÆ10, Time+ composes 10ÔåÆ9, Pan-drag shifts the window, Fit restores to 46.

### B400 ÔÇö Exported Gantt: whole timeline fits (viewBox) + Move/zoom drag-pan in the preview `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped as "NEW-4" 2026-06-22; minted **B400** ÔÇö concurrent session took B393ÔÇôB396)*

### B396 ÔÇö Dependency connectors: keep the CURVE, bind it to the bars (revert B395's orthogonal elbow) `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner feedback right after B395 shipped: "the serpentine things were fine, i preferred those, revert, the issue was just that they weren't bound to anything")*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** B395 had replaced the curved (cubic-b├®zier) dependency connectors with orthogonal elbows ÔÇö but the owner preferred the curves; the real defect was that the connectors **floated at the row's vertical mid-line** while the on-screen bars sit at the **bottom** of the row, so a curve looked unbound (it left from / arrived at empty space above the bar).
- **Reverted to the curved b├®zier** in both render paths (`GanttView` `depLines` render + `buildGanttSVG`); removed the `depElbow`/`depElbowD`/`DEP_DIRS` elbow router entirely.
- **The actual fix ÔÇö bind to the bar:** each endpoint's **y is now the bar's true vertical center per kind** (`depYCenter` in `GanttView`; `ycOf` in `buildGanttSVG`) ÔÇö leaf bars at the row bottom, summaries on their span, milestones on the diamond ÔÇö instead of the old `ROW_H/2` row-center. So a curve now cleanly leaves the predecessor bar and enters the successor bar. x still anchors to the correct bar edge per type (FS/SS/FF/SF); arrowhead direction by `x2ÔëÑx1`. Pure geometry from the row index, so off-screen virtualized rows still compute.
- **Supersedes B395's visual** (the elbow); keeps the SS color `#7c3aed`, dashed `4 3`, layer order (z3, under bars), and the blank-date guard (`verify-unscheduled-deps` still 1/1, no phantom NaN).
- **Verified:** build green ┬À JSX OK ┬À headless **V114** (`ui-audit/verify-gantt-labels-deps.mjs`, updated): every connector is a curve (not an elbow), **0** NaN, and every endpoint binds to the bar band (below the row mid-line) on screen + in the print SVG, across right/center/left.

### B393 ÔÇö Gantt label alignment (Left / Center / Right) + uniform near-black ink with weight hierarchy + never-on-fill auto-fit `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; first minted B390, **renumbered B393** ÔÇö a concurrent `main` (PR #293, the exhibit-columns batch) took B390ÔÇôB392 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** Gantt task/summary/milestone names now have a user-selectable **Left / Center / Right** alignment, render in ONE uniform near-black ink with hierarchy carried by **font weight**, and auto-fit so they're **never painted on the bar fill** ÔÇö fixing the reported defect where centered names sat on the fill and got covered (the top-level summary "Utilities"/"Electric Transmission" overlapping their span).
- **Uniform ink + weight hierarchy:** every in-chart label uses `GANTT_INK = #1a1a1a` (both render paths). The **B210 depth-navy was REMOVED from the name TEXT** (that fade-by-depth was the "grey" Michael spotted ÔÇö exactly the B320 ban). Prominence is now weight: `ganttNameWeight` ÔåÆ top summary **800** ÔåÆ child summaries **700/600** ÔåÆ leaves **500** (milestones 600; explicit `task.bold` lifts to ÔëÑ700). No text fades toward the background.
- **Bracket GRAPHIC keeps its ramp (verified NOT inverted):** `["#2B3340","#46506A","#6E7790","#8A93A8"][min(level,3)]` ÔåÆ depth 0 (top) = #2B3340, darkest/thickest, lighter/thinner as nesting deepens ÔÇö correct. The "seemed backwards" report was the navy on *text*, now gone. Span/leg magnitudes were compressed a touch (SPAN `[6,5,4,4]`, LEG `[0.16,0.12,0.09,0.07]┬ÀROW_H` on screen; matching compression in print) so the always-above caption has clear air within the row ÔÇö the ramp itself is unchanged.
- **Placement helper `placeGanttLabel`** (pure, in `index.html`, used by BOTH paths; mirrors the overflow-outside logic of `site-planner/lib/labelLayout.js` ÔÇö can't import, separate iframe bundle): **Leaf** ÔÇö Right (default) = name after the bar end, flips Left then lifts above when it would hit the right clip; Left = before the bar start, lifts above near the left edge; Center = on a **contrast plate**, overflows to a caption above when wider than the bar. **Summary** ÔÇö caption **ALWAYS above** the thin span (Left=above left leg, Center=above midpoint, Right=above right leg), clamped in the row band so the topmost summary can't ride into the timeline header. **Milestone** ÔÇö Left=before / Center=above / Right=after the diamond. Data labels (start/end/owner/%) are cleared via `padBefore/padAfter` so the name never overlaps them.
- **Scope & persistence:** view-level, per project ÔÇö `proj.labelAlign` written via `writeLabelAlign` (a mirror of B212's `writeGridCfg`), riding the existing cloud-save/autosave path; default `"right"`. Control surfaced in the Settings panel ("Task name alignment", 3-way, edits the active project).
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ┬À 1201 tests ┬À build green ┬À JSX OK ┬À headless **V114** (`ui-audit/verify-gantt-labels-deps.mjs`) ÔÇö pure-helper unit checks (weight ramp, summary-always-above, leaf after/flip/plate/overflow, milestone before/after/above) + live DOM (every name `rgb(26,26,26)`, "Utilities" caption mode=above & above the bar band, summary bolder than leaf) across **right/center/left**, 0 page errors. Existing `verify-unscheduled-deps` (1/1) + `verify-scheduler-no-crash` (6/6) + `verify-scheduler-bugfixes` still green.

### B394 ÔÇö Printed/PDF Gantt now emits in-chart task names; honors alignment + uniform ink `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; first minted B391, **renumbered B394** ÔÇö PR #293 collision)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** The PDF/print exhibit showed task names in the left grid columns but **none on the Gantt bars** ÔÇö `buildGanttSVG` never emitted the in-chart name layer that `GanttView` draws. It now does, consuming the **same** `placeGanttLabel` helper, alignment, `GANTT_INK`, and `ganttNameWeight` as on-screen, so the two paths can't drift.
- **Uniform black here too** (no B210 depth-navy on summary names ÔÇö the bracket rects keep their navy, the `<text>` is `#1a1a1a`); names escaped via the existing `escapeHtml` (B248).
- **Sidebar controls:** added a **"Bar names"** toggle (default **ON**) + a **"Name align"** Left/Center/Right segment to the PDF export Gantt section (seeded from the active project's `labelAlign`), wired through `opts.barNames` / `opts.labelAlign`. **Checked B159/B160 first:** the export `cfg.columns` governs the grid TABLE name COLUMN, not in-chart bar labels ÔÇö no conflict/duplication.

- **Verified (part of V114):** `buildGanttSVG` called directly in-browser ÔÇö print SVG emits ÔëÑ6 names in `#1a1a1a`, **0** navy on any `<text>`, includes summary + milestone names, "Bar names" off drops them, and the print connectors are orthogonal with no NaN.

### B395 ÔÇö Dependency arrows route as clean orthogonal elbows, not serpentine zig-zags `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-3" 2026-06-22; first minted B392, **renumbered B395** ÔÇö PR #293 collision)*
`[x]` **ÔÜá´©Å SUPERSEDED by B396 (same session):** the owner preferred the original curved connectors ÔÇö B396 reverts the orthogonal elbow and instead fixes the real defect (binding the curve's endpoints to the bar centers). This block records what B395 shipped before that feedback. ÔÇö **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified + shipped the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/zen-ramanujan-14sak9`).** The dashed dependency links were single cubic b├®ziers with direction-flipped handles that wandered into multi-bend serpentine shapes. Replaced the path generation in BOTH the `GanttView` `depLines` useMemo and the `buildGanttSVG` connector emitter with a deterministic **orthogonal elbow router** (`depElbow` + `depElbowD`, pure, shared) keyed on dependency type via `DEP_DIRS` (FS `[1,1]` / SS `[-1,1]` / FF `[1,-1]` / SF `[-1,-1]` exit/entry directions).
- **Shape:** short exit stub off the predecessor edge ÔåÆ a single vertical run ÔåÆ a horizontal into the successor edge, arrowhead pointing along the entry direction. A **normal forward link is Ôëñ2 bends**; a **backward link** (the successor's entry edge sits on the far side) routes out ÔåÆ vertical ÔåÆ back across ÔåÆ in (Ôëñ4 bends), around the bars ÔÇö never diagonally through them.
- **Edge cases:** milestone endpoints anchor to the **diamond tip** (center ┬▒ half-width in the travel direction), not a bar edge; endpoints are pure geometry from `tasks.indexOf`, so a link whose row is scrolled out of the virtualized window still computes and clips at the viewport; adjacent rows give a minimal vertical run; many-row spans give one straight vertical run, not per-row jogs. Kept the layer/paint order (connectors z3, under bars) and the per-type colors incl. SS `#7c3aed`; still dashed `4 3`.
- **Verified (part of V114 + the existing `verify-unscheduled-deps`):** `depElbow` unit-checked per type (every segment orthogonal for forward AND backward geometry, forward Ôëñ2 bends, backward Ôëñ4, no NaN in `depElbowD`); the live on-screen scenario draws 4 connectors all orthogonal/no-NaN incl. a backward link routing around; the print path likewise. `verify-unscheduled-deps` still passes (exactly 1 connector, no phantom NaN paths) ÔÇö the blank-date guard is intact.

### B392 ÔÇö Drag-to-resize columns in the PDF/Print Exhibit `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-3" 2026-06-22; filed B387, **renumbered B392** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (PRs #290/#291/#292) took B385ÔÇôB389 while this exhibit batch was in flight, so B390ÔÇôB392 are the real next free IDs; branch commit titles still read B385/B386/B387)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** The exhibit columns can now be tuned by dragging a column's right edge in the preview ÔÇö the same interaction as resizing a column in the live schedule grid ÔÇö with the preview reflowing live and the tuned layout persisting.
- **Handles + live drag (`buildPDFHtml`, `public/sequence/index.html`):** every column header in the **preview** (never the print output ÔÇö `@media print{.col-rs{display:none}}` + emitted only when `forPreview`) carries an absolutely-positioned `.col-rs` `col-resize` handle on its right edge. A small preview-only script tracks the drag and resizes the dragged column **live across every paginated page** (sets the width on all `th[data-k]` + each `.split-table`/`.split-gantt`), trading the delta against the Gantt ÔÇö which keeps `EXHIBIT_MIN_GANTT` (240px) ÔÇö and clamping to the column's `[min,max]`. On release it posts `{type:'planarColResize',key,w}` to the parent.
- **Persistence (`PDFExportModal`):** a `planarColResize` message handler stashes the width as a per-column override (`cfg.colWidths[key]`), flushes the preview immediately (like the collapse-toggle path), and writes through `setData` to **`data.exportColWidths`** so a tuned exhibit comes back next time the modal opens (`cfg.colWidths` is seeded from it). A **Reset** affordance under the Columns section clears the overrides back to content-fit. (Persisted at schedule-data level ÔÇö the exhibit renders one shared column layout across all selected projects, so a per-project-keyed store wouldn't apply; this is "comes back the way you left it" at the right granularity.)
- **Starts from B390's auto-fit:** an override wins for its column; everything else stays content-fit. Drag handles render only in the split (Gantt) view, where the table-vs-Gantt trade-off is well-defined (the reported screenshot).
- **Pagination fix bundled:** the paginator rebuilds the split and the Gantt container had **lost its `.split-gantt` class** (a no-class `div`); restored it so the live width-trade (and any `.split-gantt` selector) lands on the real post-paginate element instead of pushing/overflowing the chart.
- **DEDUPE / RECONCILE with B160 (left Open):** B160 is a single *ratio slider* for the whole table-vs-Gantt split; B392's **per-column** drag now changes the table block's total directly and the Gantt auto-takes the remainder (ÔëÑ MIN_GANTT) ÔÇö complementary, not a duplicate. B392 sets the default total from content-fit; if B160's slider is built later it sets the overall budget B392's columns fit within. Added a reconcile note to B160 in `BACKLOG.md`; not built here (it wasn't in this drop and is a distinct control).
- **Verified:** see the shared verification note under B390.

### B391 ÔÇö Year-boundary divider lines on the exhibit Gantt timeline `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; filed B386, **renumbered B391** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B385ÔÇôB389 while this batch was in flight)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** A multi-year exhibit Gantt (the example spanned 2025ÔåÆ2027) had no visual break between calendar years.
- **Root cause (read the code, not the brief):** `buildGanttSVG`'s month gridlines were drawn **behind** the opaque per-row background bands (`<rect width=svgWidth fill=bg>`), so in the chart **body** they were invisible ÔÇö only the dark header strip showed month labels. There were effectively no body gridlines at all, year or month.
- **Fix:** a new `yearLines` string drawn **over** the row bands (in the return, `${gridLines}${rows}${yearLines}${arrows}${todayLine}`): a heavier solid **medium-slate `#7b8290`, 1.3px** vertical at each Jan 1 (`yearMarkers`, the existing `m.mo===0` filter), so the split between years reads at a glance. Solid slate keeps it distinct from the red **dashed** Today line and the colored **dashed** dependency arrows. Also **emphasized the January month-axis labels** (bold + white vs the faint `#e8e8e8` other months) so each year's start is legible on the axis itself (doubles as the year label). A single-year exhibit (no January boundary in range) gets no divider ÔÇö correct.
- **Scope:** the exhibit only (the explicit request ÔÇö "the Gantt's result ÔÇª should at least have divisions between the years"). Mirroring onto the on-screen `GanttView` was deferred (different render path; not requested).
- **Verified:** see the shared verification note under B390.

### B390 ÔÇö PDF/Print Exhibit table columns mis-sized: oversized Task-Name gap + truncated Start/End/Duration `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; filed B385, **renumbered B390** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (PRs #290/#291/#292) took B385ÔÇôB389 while this batch was in flight, so B390ÔÇôB392 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified (V113, 10/10) + merged via PR #293 the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/practical-edison-ggcm1k`).** Three symptoms, **one** cause: in the split-view (table + Gantt) exhibit the columns were sized by **fixed widths, not fitted to content** ÔÇö a large dead band between TASK NAME and START, Start/End rendering clipped (`06/2ÔÇª`, `09/0ÔÇª`), and DUR clipped (`1ÔÇª`, `4ÔÇª`).
- **Root cause:** the split table used `table-layout:fixed` at a hardcoded ~50%-of-page width, with fixed per-column px from `COL_DEFS` and the **Task-Name column at `w:null`** ÔÇö so Name absorbed ALL the leftover width (the giant gap) while the fixed **42px** Start/End and **28px** Duration columns were too narrow for `MM/DD/YY` / multi-digit durations and clipped via `overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis`.
- **Fix ÔÇö content-fit width model.** New pure, DOM-free module-scope helpers (above `buildPDFHtml`, so preview + print + tests agree): `approxTextPx(str,fs)` approximates rendered width from per-character advances for the exhibit's Inter face (deliberately generous so a fixed-format value never clips); `layoutExhibitCols(specs,opts)` lays columns out from their content-fit base, lets a user drag-override win, clamps each to `[min,max]`, makes the **table block exactly its columns' total**, and gives the Gantt the rest ÔÇö never below `EXHIBIT_MIN_GANTT` (240px), shrinking the **flexible** columns (name/owner/predecessors/cost) toward their min, then a uniform scale, only if the columns would crowd the chart. `buildPDFHtml` measures each visible column's widest value across **all** selected projects (the exhibit stacks them under one shared header) via a plain-text `cellText` + a `COL_FIT` profile (font px, clamp range, flex flag, the Status dot+gap extra), then sizes the split from the result.
- **Per the brief:** the short fixed-format columns (Start/End/Duration/%/Status) are **NOT flexible** ÔÇö a date is never the value that gets clipped to make room; only Name/Owner/Predecessors/Cost cap + ellipsize. **Respects the column on/off checkboxes** (only the turned-on `colDefs` are measured/sized). Tables-only mode (no Gantt) is **untouched** ÔÇö its auto-layout already content-fits, so the bug was split-only; the fix is scoped there.
- **Shared verification (B390/B391/B392):** lint **0 errors** ┬À **1210 tests** (1201 + 9 new in `test/exhibitCols.test.js`, with a faithful copy in `ui-audit/stress/exhibit-cols.mjs` + anti-drift guards) ┬À build green ┬À JSX transpiles (`jsxcheck-sequence`) ┬À `Scheduler` lazy chunk intact. Headless **V113** (`ui-audit/verify-exhibit-columns.mjs`, **10/10**, drives page ÔåÆ sequence iframe ÔåÆ preview iframe on the real app): Start **and** End dates render in full with no ellipsis clip (`12/27/26`, `07/23/25`, `02/02/27`); Duration full (`389d`, `21d`); Task-Name content-capped (no runaway gap, name=280px); Gantt keeps a real share (322px); year-boundary divider lines present; a resize handle drags **live** (53ÔåÆ87px) and the new width **round-trips/persists** through the parent re-render. Screenshot: `ui-audit/screens/exhibit-fixed-zoom.png`. (Re-verified after merging `main` ÔÇö main's B388 Schedule-toolbar lift moved the Export control into the shell header, so the harness opens it there; still 10/10.)

### B389 ÔÇö Move the Light/Dark/System theme picker into account ÔåÆ Settings `[Header/Nav ┬À Theme]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat 2026-06-22: "put the light v dark option under profile settings"; minted **B389**)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/inspiring-bohr-46gql9`).** The Light/Dark/System picker that lived in the row-1 ÔÜÖ gear (B342) now lives in the **account ÔåÆ Settings** panel (`AuthPanel`), next to Change password ÔÇö where a signed-in user expects their preferences.
- **One picker, one home, no duplication:** extracted the picker UI into a shared `src/shared/theme/ThemePicker.jsx` (self-contained ÔÇö reads/sets the ThemeProvider via `useTheme`, theme tokens only, no raw hex). `AuthPanel`'s Settings tab renders it; the row-1 gear (`AppHeader` `SettingsMenu`) now renders the SAME component (no copy-paste).
- **Signed-out access preserved (B342):** the row-1 gear is kept **only when signed out** (`{!accountActive && <SettingsMenu/>}`) ÔÇö a logged-out visitor can still switch theme, a signed-in user finds it in account ÔåÆ Settings, and it's never duplicated. `accountActive` is `!!user` from the Shell, threaded to every AppHeader, so the gate is reliable. (Owner trade-off, flagged: signed-out is now the only place the standalone gear shows; signed-in is account ÔåÆ Settings.)
- **Code:** new `ThemePicker.jsx`; `AppHeader.jsx` (gear ÔåÆ shared picker, gated on `!accountActive`, dropped the now-unused `mode`/`setMode`/`THEME_OPTS`); `AuthPanel.jsx` (Settings tab renders `<ThemePicker/>`). Pure relocation ÔÇö the ThemeProvider, the `data-theme` mechanism, and the System matchMedia listener are unchanged.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **1201 tests** ┬À build green; headless **V112** (`ui-audit/verify-b389-theme-in-profile.mjs` + `theme-in-profile-harness.{html,jsx}`, **7/7** ÔÇö signed-out gear present, signed-in gear gone, the Settings-tab picker offers Light/Dark/System and clicking Dark/Light actually flips `<html data-theme>`, 0 page errors). The B387 (9/9) + B388 (17/17) harnesses still pass ÔÇö the logged-out gear they rely on is preserved.

### B388 ÔÇö Lift the Schedule toolbar into the unified AppHeader over the iframe bridge `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (feature) ÔÇö built on B387  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-2" 2026-06-22; filed B386, **renumbered B388** ÔÇö a concurrent `main` (PRs #289/#290) took B385/B386 while this was in flight, so B387/B388 are the real next free IDs. The branch commit titles still read "B385/B386" ÔÇö written before the collision surfaced; the canonical IDs are **B387/B388**.)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fully built + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/inspiring-bohr-46gql9`).** Schedule's two stacked header rows are now one: the embedded Gantt app's action toolbar (`public/sequence/index.html`) is LIFTED up into the shell's unified `AppHeader` Row 2 over the existing postMessage bridge (B203), so Schedule matches Site/Markup ÔÇö one header, no double toolbar.
- **What moved up:** the **Grid/Split/Gantt** toggle + **review inbox** (unread badge) ÔåÆ the B387 `toolbarCenter` slot; **zoom (ÔêÆ/%/+), export, save, version history, contacts, automation, format, settings** ÔåÆ `toolbarContent` (right). Module tabs stay left.
- **The architectural worry was UNFOUNDED:** the embedded app already renders its panels (Settings/Format/Automation/Contacts/History/PDF) as **self-positioned `position:fixed` modals/drawers toggled by boolean state ÔÇö never anchored to their toolbar buttons** (they take only `data/setData/onClose`). So lifting the *trigger buttons* to the shell is clean: the buttons post commands; the panels still open inside the iframe exactly as before. Only the Export dropdown was button-anchored ÔÇö its menu is now rendered shell-side (reusing `AnchoredMenu`) and each item posts the chosen export.
- **Bridge extended, not rebuilt:** iframe ÔåÆ shell adds `planar:toolbar-state` (view, section, zoom %, zoomable, reviewCount, reviewOpen, saveStatus/savePulse/fileLinked, activePanel) with its own signature-dedup; shell ÔåÆ iframe adds `planar:view-set / review-toggle / zoom / export / save / history / contacts / automation / settings / format`, handled by a second `inShell`-gated listener in the App. **Strict same-origin guards kept** (exact-origin target + `planar-seq`/`planar-shell` source discriminators) ÔÇö not loosened for the new types. The iframe is the **single source of truth**: the shell controls only display reported state + post intent; the unread badge is the reported count (never hardcoded), and `toolbar.ready` gates rendering so a control never shows a fabricated value before the iframe reports.
- **Hide-in-embed:** `.in-iframe .app-header { display:none }` (the whole header) now that the shell hosts the toolbar ÔÇö re-widening what B381 had narrowed (B381 narrowed it only because the toolbar had no shell home *yet*; now it does). Standalone `/sequence/` is untouched ÔÇö the hide is scoped to `.in-iframe` and both bridge halves are gated `inShell` (headless-confirmed).
- **Brief gotchas honored:** two settings gears stay separate (Row-1 app/theme gear B342 vs the lifted Schedule Settings); the lifted save is the embedded app's own indicator (Row-1's cloud badge is not wired for Schedule ÔåÆ no redundancy); Fullscreen (F) collapses the header so the lifted controls vanish with it for free; "share" in the brief = the existing **Contacts** control (no speculative control invented, per B381's lesson).
- **Code:** new `src/workspaces/scheduler/components/ScheduleToolbar.jsx` (`ScheduleCenter` + `ScheduleActions`, shell theme tokens, reuses `AnchoredMenu`); `Scheduler.jsx` (tracks reported `toolbar` state, feeds the two AppHeader slots, posts commands via the existing same-origin `post`); `public/sequence/index.html` (toolbar-state emit + command listener, both `inShell`-gated; CSS hide). Consumes B387's `toolbarCenter`.
- **Deduped:** net-new, the completion of the B203 bridge. NOT B203 (the *nav* half) nor B381 (which *restored* the in-iframe toolbar ÔÇö B388 relocates it). NOT the concurrent `main` **B386** (the Scheduler dependency-connector bug ÔÇö different surface, just an ID collision). **Supersedes B381's harness** `verify-schedule-toolbar.mjs` (retired ÔÇö it asserted the in-iframe toolbar is visible, the opposite of B388).
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **1201 tests** ┬À build green ┬À JSX OK (`jsxcheck-sequence`) ┬À `Scheduler`/`AppHeader` lazy chunks intact; headless **V111** (`ui-audit/verify-b388-toolbar-lift.mjs`, **17/17** ÔÇö embedded toolbar hidden, every lifted control renders, two gears separate, cross-frame round-trips (GanttÔåÆzoom appears, zoom 17%ÔåÆ33%, Settings opens the iframe panel + button reflects reported state), badge never fabricated, 0 page errors, AND standalone `/sequence/` toolbar still intact).

### B387 ÔÇö Add an optional center toolbar zone to AppHeader Row 2 `[Header/Nav]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped from chat as "NEW-1" 2026-06-22; filed B385, **renumbered B387** ÔÇö a concurrent `main` (PR #289) took B385 while this was in flight; canonical IDs for this pair are B387 + B388)*
`[x]` **Filed AND fixed + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/inspiring-bohr-46gql9`).** Row 2 of the shared header (`src/shared/ui/AppHeader.jsx`) was a two-zone flex ÔÇö module tabs (left) + `toolbarContent` (right). Added an optional third **center** slot: a new `toolbarCenter` ReactNode prop, rendered between the tabs and `toolbarContent`, mirroring how Row 1 already centers `centerContent` between its left/right zones.
- **Layout:** with `toolbarCenter` present, Row 2 becomes 3 zones ÔÇö left tabs (`flex:1`, basis-0), center group (`flex:0 1 auto`, shrink-to-content), right toolbar (`flex:1`, justified end) ÔÇö so the center is TRULY centered the same way Row 1 is (verified center mid-x **719** vs row mid-x **720**). The row `flex-wrap`s (center/toolbar flow to a second line; `minHeight:44` lets it grow) on a too-narrow viewport instead of overlapping ÔÇö never absolute positioning.
- **Generic + additive:** Site and Markup pass no `toolbarCenter`, so their headers render the ORIGINAL 2-zone layout **byte-for-byte** (the whole 3-zone branch is gated on the prop; the per-tab wiring was factored into one `moduleTabButtons` const reused by both branches, so no duplication).
