     errors, build green. The Dashboard/Map home crumb was deliberately KEPT (see B357). -->

### B357 ÔÇö AppHeader row hierarchy + de-clutter the Markup project zone `[App Shell]` (feature)  *(parallel-branch handoff "NEW-1" 2026-06-21; minted **B357**)*
`[x]` **Done + headless-verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/hopeful-dirac-aqjlqb`).** The two header rows read as equal weight (measured **35px vs 36px** ÔÇö the root cause of "which row matters?") and the centre crowded. Fixes: **Row 2 (the workspace tools row) raised to 44px**, Row 1 left at 35px ÔÇö the tools row now visibly carries the weight (verified `[35,44]`); the per-module accent/underline on the active tab is unchanged, so primacy comes from height + the existing weight, never a low-contrast gray (text-hierarchy rule respected, AA intact). **De-duped the project name:** the Markup centre zone was rendering `meta.title` while the Row-1 breadcrumb already shows the project ÔÇö the centre copy is removed, so the project name has ONE home (the breadcrumb). **Files stays put** (the ­ƒùé pill is a Row-1 shelf by B180 ÔÇö not relocated). Shared `AppHeader` height change re-tested across Site / Schedule / Markup; the floating multi-tab warning nudged 70ÔåÆ84px for the taller header. **Decision ÔÇö the labeled home crumb (Ôûª Dashboard / Map) was KEPT, not dropped** as NEW-1 suggested: removing it conflicts with B192 ("the labeled crumb is primary") and B204 (Site's "Map" label), and it's a shared component (Site relies on the "Map" crumb). The higher-impact de-clutter (title de-dup, ReviewsÔåÆRow 2 B360, Library removed B359, truthful chip B358) lands without it; trivially revisitable if the owner wants the minimal `[logo] / [project]` look. `src/shared/ui/AppHeader.jsx`, `DocReview.jsx` centerContent.

### B358 ÔÇö Truthful save "cloud" chip ÔÇö kill the "Not saved" cry-wolf `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(parallel-branch handoff "NEW-2" 2026-06-21; minted **B358**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/hopeful-dirac-aqjlqb`).** The owner's "the cloud for files is fucked up" was the save badge in **`ReviewsBar`** (the handoff guessed `ProjectBreadcrumb` ÔÇö the breadcrumb only ever shows an at-risk ÔÜá; the actual badge is in ReviewsBar, confirmed against the tree). It rendered **"Not saved" with nothing open / nothing to save** ÔÇö a flag that cries wolf trains the eye to ignore the one state that matters; it also had no `conflict` state (B314 conflicts fell through to "Not saved"). Replaced with a **truthful little cloud chip** driven by one pure `chipFor(status, signedIn, idle)`: **idle (nothing to save) ÔåÆ no chip at all**; **saving** ÔåÆ cloud-Ôåæ + "SavingÔÇª" (pulsing amber); **unsaved** ÔåÆ cloud-Ôåæ "Unsaved"; **conflict** ÔåÆ cloud-Ô£ò "Sync conflict" (loud red, never silent); **saved OR signed-in** (cloud-backed, mirroring the Site Planner's "Synced Ô£ô") ÔåÆ calm green cloud-Ô£ô "Saved"; **signed-out with content** ÔåÆ honest "On this device" (a monitor glyph ÔÇö saved here, can't sync), not a false "Not saved". Colors are theme tokens (legible in light/dark per B341). Headless (logged-out): empty Markup shows **no** save chip; once a PDF is open it reads "On this device". The signed-in "Saved"/`saving`/`conflict` states (sandbox blocks sign-in) are locked by `test/reviewsBadge.test.js` (7 truth-table tests). `components/ReviewsBar.jsx`. **NB:** the separate **"Cloud off"** pill (Shell `authControl`) is a *different* indicator ÔÇö it only appears when Supabase isn't configured for the build (so it shows in the sandbox preview); on a cloud-configured planyr.io it's the account/Sign-in pill. Left as-is per the handoff's "don't conflate"; if it's visible in production, the cloud env vars aren't wired into that deploy (a config item, not this code).

### B359 ÔÇö Retire the redundant ­ƒôü Library door (Files subsumes it) `[Doc Review / Files]` (task)  *(parallel-branch handoff "NEW-3" 2026-06-21; minted **B359**)*
`[x]` **Done + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/hopeful-dirac-aqjlqb`).** "Library" (­ƒôü ÔåÆ `ProjectLibrary.jsx`, the older B14 projectÔåÆdiscipline tree) and "Files" (­ƒùé ÔåÆ `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx`, the newer B180 tagged-index drawer) were two doors onto the same `doc_reviews` index (both `listReviews` ÔåÆ `openReview`). The Files drawer already **groups by discipline** and has an **All-projects / cross-project** toggle ÔÇö so it covers everything Library offered, plus saved views / document-class tagging / place-on-map / the auto-filing ingestion queue. Removed the ­ƒôü Library button + its drawer + `libraryOpen` state from **both** `DocReview.jsx` **and** `Stitcher.jsx` (the handoff said "nothing else imports it" ÔÇö the tree showed the Stitcher did too; both handled). The Stitcher still opens saved reviews via its **Reviews Ôû¥** dropdown, so nothing is stranded. `ProjectLibrary.jsx` was then fully orphaned (no importers) and **deleted**. No feature loss; the lazy chunk split holds. Headless: no "Library" button in either the single-sheet header or the Stitcher toolbar.

### B360 ÔÇö Move "Reviews" out of Row 1 into the Markup tools row `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task)  *(parallel-branch handoff "NEW-4" 2026-06-21; minted **B360**)*
`[x]` **Done + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/hopeful-dirac-aqjlqb`).** `ReviewsBar` was mounted in `AppHeader`'s Row-1 `saveSlot` (only DocReview fills it ÔÇö it was never cross-module). Moved `<ReviewsBar>` into Row 2's `toolbarContent`, beside Open PDF / Stitch; its (now truthful, B358) save chip rides with it. DocReview's `saveSlot` is now empty ÔÇö correct, not a regression. Wiring (status/signedIn/meta/onMeta/onOpen/onNew, + new `idle={isEmpty()}`) unchanged. Markup-scoped (Site/Schedule untouched). Headless: the "Reviews" button now resolves inside Row 2 (index 1), not Row 1.
### B356 ÔÇö Invisible site-name in the header + a whole-app sweep so it can't happen elsewhere `[App / Theming]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-21: "I can't see the project name here ÔÇª it's gray text on their gray background ÔÇª go through the entire site and check the rest so this doesn't happen"; filed **B355** but renumbered **B356** ÔÇö a concurrent `main` (#265, lettered plan concepts) took B355 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified BOTH themes 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/gallant-mccarthy-k6sqmr`).** Root cause was the B341 theme-flip trap again: the Site Planner **site-name button hardcoded white text** ÔÇö `hdrTab(12.5, "#fff", 600)` ÔÇö on chrome that now themes light, so white-on-near-white was invisible. `contrast-audit.mjs` couldn't catch it because it only checks the **token pairs defined in `index.css`**, and this colour was a literal passed into a style helper (invisible to a `color:` grep, too).
- **Headline fix:** site-name ÔåÆ `PAL.chromeInk` (theme-aware). The same trap also hit the **signed-out "Sign in" avatar** (white "ÔÇ║" on the light pill ÔåÆ `var(--chrome-text)` on `var(--chrome-bg)`) and the **"current" marker** in the site/plan switcher menus (plain accent on the soft-accent fill = 3.8:1 ÔåÆ a new theme-aware `accentStrong`, 7.3:1 light / bright in dark). Also made `ModuleLoader` theme-aware (was `#6b7280` on a hardcoded `#f6f8fa`, 4.26:1).
- **Whole-app sweep (the owner's real ask):** new `ui-audit/contrast-sweep.mjs` walks the **rendered DOM** of every workspace in **both themes**, reading each text node's computed colour vs its effective (first opaque ancestor) background, compositing alpha, applying the AA floor by size/weight. Catches colours set ANY way (helpers, inheritance) ÔÇö which static analysis cannot. It found exactly the avatar + "current" issues; all now green.
- **Dark-theme hardening:** the sweep + a contrast calc showed **every hardcoded semantic colour** (`#b45309` amber, `#b3361b`/`#b91c1c` red, `#15803d` green, `#0e7490` teal, `#7c3aed` purple, `#1d4ed8` blue) fails AA on a **dark panel** (2.2ÔÇô3.4:1) while passing on a light one. Added theme-aware **`--success-text` / `--danger-text` / `--info-text`** tokens (+ palette.js mirror; `--warn-text` already existed) and rerouted the semantic TEXT that sits on **themed** panels (SitePlanner inspector, DocReview takeoff + drop-zone, LayerPanel, SiteAnalysis findings, MapFinder status menu) to them. **Left untouched (correct):** fixed-light-banner text, **fixed-`#fff` panels** (Stitcher + Project Files/Library drawers ÔÇö self-consistent + readable; their not-theming-to-dark is a separate cosmetic item), and SVG/map data-colour tables. **SiteReviewModal** (a fixed-dark modal) had its own too-dim subtitles (`#4a6080` 2.5:1) lightened to AA on its dark bg.
- **Guards:** the 3 new tokens are registered in `contrast-audit.mjs` (8 new pairs, all PASS both themes) so a palette edit can't silently re-introduce a low-contrast pair; `contrast-sweep.mjs` is the runtime net for the helper/inheritance class the static audit can't see.
- **Verified (V96):** `contrast-sweep.mjs` ÔåÆ **0 low-contrast elements** in planner / site-menu / Markup / Schedule, **light AND dark**; `contrast-audit.mjs` all pairs PASS; **1041 unit tests**; lint **0 errors**; build green; screenshots show "Katy Logistics Park" clearly visible in both themes.

### B354 ÔÇö Markup yield panel miscounts redline annotations as "uncalibrated measurements" `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21 "keep going" ÔÇö Markup-tool stress test; minted **B354** ÔÇö next free after B353)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/stitch-tool-stress-test-1tvw5b`).** Stress-testing the Markup yield panel turned up a real miscount. The panel warns **"N measurement(s) on uncalibrated sheets are excluded"** from `rollup(markups, calByPage)` ÔÇö but `rollup` was handed the WHOLE markup list (measurements **and** redline annotations: rect / cloud / line / arrow / text), and any non-measurement kind on an *uncalibrated* sheet fell into `measureValue`'s generic branch (`calibrated:false`) and incremented `uncal`. So drawing a redline cloud or adding a text note on an uncalibrated sheet made the panel falsely warn "1 measurement(s) ÔÇª excluded" when **zero** measurements were made (and could mislead a reviewer into thinking a real takeoff was dropped). `rollup` now tallies **only** the measurement kinds (`distance/perimeter/area/count`, derived from the existing `MIN_MEASURE_PTS` so there's one source of truth) and skips everything else (and null entries) outright. Totals were already correct (redlines never matched the area/perimeter/distance branches); only the `uncal` warning was wrong. `lib/takeoff.js`. **New `test/markupRollupStress.test.js` ÔÇö 6 adversarial tests** (redlines don't inflate `uncal`; redlines on a calibrated sheet aren't summed; a true uncalibrated measurement still counts; all four measure kinds total correctly; null/unknown-kind entries don't crash; a non-finite page calibration excludes the measure). Existing 16 takeoff tests still green; full suite **994 tests**, lint 0 errors, build clean. Pure-logic fix ÔÇö no live-browser verification needed.

<!-- 2026-06-21: owner chat ÔÇö "make the stitch tool great on all the other stuff: keep the notes in the
     legend shown but get ALL of them in case they change by page; and like Bluebeam, click a detail
     reference number ÔåÆ pull up a cloud of that detail without leaving the drawing." Minted **B350** (next
     free after B349). Filed AND built + headless-verified the SAME session per STANDING RULE #1. -->

### B350 ÔÇö Stitcher: aggregated notes/legend across the set + Bluebeam-style detail-callout "cloud" `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21 "make it great on all the other stuff"; minted **B350**)*
`[x]` **Built + headless-verified 2026-06-21.** Two upgrades to the stitch tool, both backed by pure shared readers so they're unit-tested away from pdf.js:
- **Notes/legend, captured across the whole set (never lost to the crop).** The auto-stitch crop (B338) hides each sheet's title-block band ÔÇö where the **general notes / legend** usually live. A new pure reader `src/shared/files/sheetNotes.js` (`parseNotes` finds each `GENERAL/GRADING/KEYED NOTES`, `LEGEND`, `ABBREVIATIONS` block; `aggregateNotes` merges every placed sheet's blocks, **dedupes** repeated boilerplate, and **flags a note that didn't appear on every sheet** with the sheet(s) it came from ÔÇö so a note that **changes page to page** is surfaced, not buried). Wired into `readSheetMeta` (each page now carries `notes`) and shown in the pinned **Composite key** as an expandable "Notes & legend ┬À N" section.
- **Click a detail reference ÔåÆ the detail pops up as a "cloud" (Bluebeam-style).** A new pure reader `src/shared/files/detailRefs.js` reads detail-callout **bubbles** (a detail id stacked over a sheet code ÔÇö "5 / A-3" ÔÇö plus inline `5/A-3` and `SEE DETAIL 5 ON SHEET A-3` forms; conservative ÔÇö a plain fraction can't match) and where details are **defined** (`DETAIL 5`, `SECTION A-A`). Each callout renders as a clickable hotspot ring on the placed sheet; clicking it **pulls up that detail in a floating cloud popup** that renders the referenced sheet (reused from a placed sheet, or rendered on demand from a loaded PDF), **centered on the named detail** when the target sheet labels it ÔÇö pan/zoomable, so you never leave the current drawing. Honest fallback: a target that isn't in the set yet says so. New toolbar **Details** toggle; hotspots only grab clicks in Pan mode (measure/align/calibrate pass through).
- **Persistence:** placed sheets now carry `sheetNumber`/`detailRefs`/`detailAnchors`/`notes` through save/load. **Scope:** all inside the doc-review workspace; lazy chunk split intact. **Tests:** 14 new unit tests (`test/detailRefs.test.js` 8, `test/sheetNotes.test.js` 6); full suite green (1029 with main folded in); lint 0 errors; build green. **Headless:** `ui-audit/verify-b350.mjs` 11/11 (notes aggregated + the C-5-only note flagged; the "5/A-3" hotspot opens a "Detail 5 ┬À Sheet A-3" cloud rendering the A-3 sheet) + `verify-b335-b339.mjs` 13/13 no-regression.
### B352 ÔÇö Scanned-sheet OCR: read image-only drawings into the auto-assembly pipeline `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (feature)  *(owner-requested 2026-06-21 right after B335ÔÇôB339: "Add ocr capabilities if I don't have that"; minted **B352** ÔÇö was B340's OCR tail; a very hot `main` (B341ÔÇôB351 + V88ÔÇôV92) consumed every earlier number this session)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/loving-newton-t7gzpq`).** Fills the dormant OCR seam B336/B337 left wired, so a **scanned / image-only** drawing (no text layer) now reads, groups, stitches, crops, and auto-calibrates through the SAME pipeline as a vector PDF.
- **How:** `doc-review/lib/ocr.js` renders a no-text page to a canvas (`renderPageToOcrCanvas`, pdf.js, density-capped Ôëñ24 MP) and runs **Tesseract.js** (WASM, in its own worker) to recover the text **with per-word bounding boxes**, then `wordsToItems` converts those boxes into the exact `{ str, x, y, w, h }` page-unit items `sheetMeta.readSheetMeta` (B336) already consumes ÔÇö so OCR'd pages feed the title-block/scale/match-line reader identically. Low-confidence words are dropped (consistent with "never auto-guess").
- **Lazy + cheap on the common path:** `createOcrRunner` only spins up the worker when a no-text page is actually hit (a normal vector set pays nothing); the Tesseract orchestrator is a separate ~16 KB chunk and the WASM core + English model load from a pinned CDN (jsDelivr ÔÇö reachable from Cloudflare Pages) only on first use, then browser-cached. The drawing's pixels never leave the browser. The Stitcher shows a "Reading scanned sheet (OCR)ÔÇª" status while it runs.
- **Verified:** **7 unit tests** (`test/ocr.test.js` ÔÇö the bboxÔåÆpage-unit conversion, lazy single-worker reuse + dispose, fail-soft, and a scanned title block flowing through `readSheetMeta` to the right sheet number / title / scale / match-line) **+ a LIVE headless run** (`ui-audit/verify-b352-ocr.mjs`, **V93**): the real Tesseract engine loads from jsDelivr in Chromium and reads a "scanned" title block + match line ÔÇö "GRADING PLAN / SHEET NO. C-5 / SCALE: 1\"=40' / MATCH LINE - SEE SHEET C-6" ÔÇö recovering C-5, the title, the 40-scale, and the C-6 target. lint 0 ┬À full suite green ┬À build green; DocReview lazy chunk intact, Tesseract split into its own on-demand chunk. **OCR-path stress-hardened the same session ÔÇö see the B352 stress addendum below.**
> **Dedup:** this is B340's OCR tail, pulled forward on owner request; B340 keeps the 3 remaining computer-vision tails (graphic scale-bar, geometric edge-match, legend symbol-union). Distinct + complementary to main's stitch-engine stress-tests and Stitcher-component bug sweeps (this session) ÔÇö those predate OCR and never touch `ocr.js`.

**Stress addendum (owner: "stress test it") ÔÇö B352.** Adversarial pass over the OCR layer (the stitch-engine stress tests predate OCR and didn't cover it). It turned up + fixed one real **non-finite poison** (the same class the engine stress-test fixed in `stitchGeom`): a **non-finite bbox coordinate** (NaN/┬▒Infinity ÔÇö which a noisy scan can emit) slipped past `wordsToItems`' `x1 <= x0` guard (`NaN <= NaN` is `false`), pushing a NaN item that would poison the whole reader (title-block density ÔåÆ NaN, line sort ÔåÆ undefined). `wordsToItems` now requires all four box coords **finite** + the box non-inverted, guards a 0/NaN/negative/Infinity render scale and non-finite page dims, and coerces non-string OCR text. New `test/ocrStress.test.js` ÔÇö **15 tests** across malformed boxes, broken Tesseract shapes (`extractWords`), every orchestration failure (render/recognize/worker-creation throw ÔåÆ null; all-low-confidence ÔåÆ graceful no-text record), the OCRÔåÆ`readSheetMeta`ÔåÆgrouping integration (a garbage scan stays standalone; an unreadable number isn't force-merged; clean scans group through NaN noise), and a **300-iteration randomized fuzz** asserting no non-finite item ever leaks and `readSheetMeta` always returns a finite [0,1] confidence. **LIVE stress** (`ui-audit/verify-b352-ocr.mjs`, 9/9): the real engine reads a clean scan AND ÔÇö the dangerous case ÔÇö emits **0 words on a blank page and on pure noise (no hallucinated sheet number/title)**, and a small-font degraded render doesn't throw. Full suite **959 tests**, lint 0, build clean.

### B351 ÔÇö Auto-filing matcher: close two browser-path MISFILE vectors `[Doc Review / Auto-file]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21 "keep going" ÔÇö stress-testing adjacent to the stitch tool; minted **B351** ÔÇö next free after B350)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/stitch-tool-stress-test-1tvw5b`).** Continuing the "stress-test / look up how to break things" pass into the auto-filing project matcher (same risk class as the stitcher: **a misfile is worse than an unfiled file**). The browser matcher (`matchProject.js` `scoreProjectInText`) searches the sheet's raw text, so it had two false-positive vectors the server twin (`server/filing/matcher.js`, which compares *extracted fields* with exact equality) avoids by construction ÔÇö both could **auto-route a drawing onto the wrong project**:
- **Short IDs matched as a substring of the separator-stripped whole document** ÔÇö `normId(wholeText).includes(id)` matched a 4-digit job / 6-digit parcel number as a coincidental substring spanning unrelated words (e.g. job `"1045"` inside `"ÔÇª210455ÔÇª"`, or a parcel inside a longer run). Now matched by **boundary-aligned equality** against `idWindows` (the normId of each run of Ôëñ5 *adjacent* tokens), so a real id split by punctuation/space (`1234567-000-001`, `1234567 000 001`, `KG-2025`) still matches but a coincidental digit run can't manufacture a confident match.
- **Scattered name words scored 0.8 (auto-filed)** ÔÇö a 2-word common name like *Katy Grand* whose words merely appear *somewhere* on an unrelated Katy-area / Grand-Parkway sheet auto-routed there. A full-**phrase** hit still scores 0.9 (auto-files), but a scattered all-words hit now scores **0.55 ÔÇö below the 0.6 auto-file floor**, so it can corroborate another signal via noisy-or but never auto-route by itself ("never auto-guess").
- Also hardened `scoreProjectInText` to tolerate a `null` entry in the projects list (the `= {}` default only caught `undefined`).
- **Untouched:** the shared `decide()` thresholds (`minConfidence 0.6` / `minMargin 0.15`) ÔÇö only the client-specific text-search *scoring method* changed, so the decision contract stays in parity with the server matcher. **New `test/matchProjectStress.test.js` ÔÇö 11 adversarial tests** (substring-id rejection + real split-id recall, scattered-name-no-autofile + phrase recall + corroborated match, empty/junk/ambiguous/huge-blob/malformed-input). Existing 8 matcher tests still green; full suite **968 tests**, lint 0 errors, build clean. (Auto-filing is gated dormant behind `VITE_AUTOFILE_ENABLED`, so this is precision hardening on a not-yet-lit path ÔÇö no production behavior change.)

<!-- 2026-06-21: SECOND pass of the stitch-tool bug sweep (owner: "merge and then look for more"), after
     B343ÔÇôB347 merged via #246. Minted **B348/B349** (next free after B347). Both in the Stitcher COMPONENT,
     fixed + headless-verified (`ui-audit/verify-stitch-bugs.mjs` now 14/14, incl. these two). -->

### B348 ÔÇö Inline Calibrate box detaches from its line on wheel-zoom `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21 "look for more"; minted **B348**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** The Calibrate entry box stored its screen position **once** (computed at open time). `onDown` blocks pan while it's open, but `onWheel` is NOT blocked ÔÇö so zooming to read the drawing while typing the known length left the box stranded away from its two-point line. The box now derives its screen position from the stored **world** points via the live pan/zoom each render (`calPos`), so it tracks the line under any zoom. `Stitcher.jsx` (`doCalibrate` stores points only; render computes `calPos`). Headless confirmed the box follows a wheel-zoom.

### B349 ÔÇö Composite key shows a meaningless "N units/ft" instead of the drawing scale `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug, minor)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21 "look for more"; minted **B349**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** The pinned composite key read `Scale set ┬À {round(1/ftPerUnit)} units/ft` ÔÇö i.e. **page-points per foot** (~2 for a 1"=40' sheet), which reads as nonsense to a reviewer. Since the world frame is always identity-scale (world unit = page point), `ftPerUnit ├ù 72` is the real **feet per paper inch**, so the key now reads `Scale set ┬À 1" Ôëê 40'` ÔÇö the engineer's scale the reviewer recognises. `Stitcher.jsx` composite-key readout. Headless confirmed `1" Ôëê 40'` for the auto-calibrated set and that `units/ft` is gone.

     `main` landings consumed those numbers (PR #244 theming-contrast took B341/B342; a sibling stitch
     STRESS-TEST session ÔÇö below ÔÇö also took B341) ÔÇö so renumbered to **B343ÔÇôB347**. These five are all in

### B343 ÔÇö Stitcher tray hides a file whose grouping failed (its pages become unreachable) `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; first filed B341, renumbered **B343** ÔÇö main #244 took B341/B342)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** The background readÔåÆgroup (B335) sets a file's `groups` to `[]` on any read exception. The tray's grouped view rendered `p.groups.map(...)` ÔåÆ **zero entries**, so a file that failed to group showed nothing and the user had no way to add its pages (the "all pages" toggle was their only escape, with no hint it was needed). Now a file is shown in grouped mode only when its read produced **ÔëÑ1 logical sheet** (`hasGroups`); an empty/failed group result falls back to the raw per-page list for that file (groupSheets always yields ÔëÑ1 run for a readable page, so empty == failure). `Stitcher.jsx` `trayItems`/`anyGroups`.

### B344 ÔÇö Removing the world-frame (index-0) stitch sheet strands a leftover un-aligned sheet `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; first filed B342, renumbered **B344** ÔÇö main #244 took B341/B342)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** The first placed sheet is the world frame (`aligned:true`); the Align button + "Not aligned" chip are gated on `i > 0`. Removing the frame promoted a still-`aligned:false` sheet to index 0, where it **lost its Align button yet stayed measurement-blocked** (`measureOverUnaligned`) ÔÇö an unrecoverable stuck state (measuring errored "Align that sheetÔÇª" with no Align affordance). New `removeSheet` helper promotes the new first sheet to the frame (`aligned:true`) on removal, restoring the "index-0 is the frame" invariant. Also guarded `onDown`'s align path against a sheet removed mid-align (was a `sheet.M` null deref). Headless: add frame + un-aligned sheet ÔåÆ remove frame ÔåÆ survivor is framed, no stranded "Not aligned".

### B345 ÔÇö Stitcher ┬▒ zoom buttons anchor on the world origin, not the viewport centre `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug, minor)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; first filed B343, renumbered **B345**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** The wheel zoom is cursor-anchored (shared `zoomAround`, B329) but the ┬▒ buttons just scaled `view.zoom` with pan untouched, so content slid toward the top-left corner on every click (cf. the single-sheet B290). New `zoomBtn(factor)` routes the ┬▒ buttons through `zoomAround` about the **viewport centre**. Headless confirmed a fixed point's offset from centre scales ~1.2├ù per click.

### B346 ÔÇö Stitcher distance read-outs round to whole feet (inconsistent with area's 2-dp acres) `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug, minor)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; first filed B344, renumbered **B346** ÔÇö the stitch counterpart of B296)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** B296 gave the single-sheet tool one-decimal linear feet, but the Stitcher's on-canvas distance label and the "Distance" takeoff total still used whole-foot `f0` (a 150.6 ft line read "151 ft"). Added `f1` and used it for both. Headless confirmed a decimal-foot read-out.

### B347 ÔÇö Seeded auto-stitch fallback gives no endpoint-order cue (reverse clicks flip the sheet 180┬░) `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; first filed B345, renumbered **B347**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21.** When auto-stitch (B337) can't place a sheet but knows its seam side, manual Align is **pre-seeded** with the moving sheet's two seam endpoints (`detectedEndpointsFor`) and the user clicks the two matching points on a placed sheet. Nothing showed WHICH seed was "first" vs "second", so clicking the matching points in reverse order silently rotated the sheet 180┬░ via `solveM`. The seeded flow now draws **numbered markers (1, 2)** at the moving sheet's seed endpoints (its current position), so the click order is unambiguous. Additive render only; no geometry change.

<!-- Ô£à DONE 2026-06-21 (branch claude/focused-davinci-kkytxf). Follow-up to PR #242 (which shipped the
     drop-a-set ÔåÆ group/auto-stitch/crop/auto-calibrate feature in the STITCHER). #242 left the single-
     sheet MARKUP sidebar as a flat "Sheet N" list, so B266 (real sheet labels there) was never delivered
     on that surface. This PR brings the real sheet # + title + the logical-group collapse to the Markup
     sidebar, REUSING #242's shared engines (sheetMeta.readSheetMeta + sheetGroups.groupSheets +
     sheetRead.statedCalibration ÔÇö no duplicate modules). Delivers the open B266 + the new B348. 7/7
     headless (ui-audit/verify-markup-sheet-labels.mjs) + #242's viewer suite still 13/13 (no regression);
     846 tests, lint 0, build green. VERIFICATION V91. Backstory: a parallel session had built an
     equivalent FULL implementation (PR #243) just before #242 merged; on the owner's "compare, then
     salvage extras" call, the duplicate engines/Stitcher work were discarded and only this genuinely-
     missing Markup-sidebar piece was kept, rebased onto #242. -->

### B266 ÔÇö Sheet sidebar labels from the title block (real sheet # + name, not "Sheet N") `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; delivered 2026-06-21 as a follow-up to #242)*
`[x]` **Shipped.** The single-sheet Markup sidebar now shows each sheet's real **sheet # + title** read
from its title block (e.g. "C-5 ÔÇö GRADING PLAN", "COVER SHEET"), not generic "Sheet N". Built on #242's
shared reader (`sheetMeta.readSheetMeta` ÔÇö which locates the title-block band first, so a "SEE SHEET X"
cross-ref or a detail/grid ref can't masquerade as the sheet number, the crux this item called out).
**No-auto-guess:** a sheet with no detected title block / no readable number falls back to "Sheet N"
(gated on `meta.titleBlock || meta.sheetNumber`, so a stray body-text line is never surfaced as the
label). OCR for scanned sheets stays the shared remaining slice (tracked under B267 + #242's dormant OCR
seam in `sheetRead.js`). 7/7 headless (`ui-audit/verify-markup-sheet-labels.mjs`, V91); #242's viewer
suite still 13/13.

### B348 ÔÇö Collapse the Markup single-sheet sidebar into logical sheets (parity with the Stitcher) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived 2026-06-21; minted **B348** ÔÇö next free after B347)*
`[x]` **Shipped (follow-up to #242).** #242 collapses a dropped set into logical sheets in the STITCHER
tray; this brings the same collapse to the single-sheet **Markup** sidebar: consecutive pages sharing a
plan type + a contiguous sheet-number run show as one expandable entry ("Grading Plan ┬À C-5ÔÇôC-7 ┬À 3
sheets"); cover/notes/one-offs stay standalone. Reuses #242's `sheetGroups.groupSheets` (the same engine
the Stitcher uses, so the two surfaces agree) + surfaces the per-sheet stated-scale auto-calibrate (┬ÀÔëê)
via `sheetRead.statedCalibration`. The sidebar count reads "N sheets ┬À M pages" so the collapse is
visible. Verified: a 4-page set ÔåÆ 2 logical entries, the group expands to its members (V91).
### B341 ÔÇö Stress-test the drawing stitch tool: harden the auto-stitch against bad PDF sets `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (task)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21: "stress test the stitch tool, look up how to break things and implement strategies to do so"; minted **B341** ÔÇö next free after B340)*
### B350 ÔÇö Stress-test the drawing stitch tool: harden the auto-stitch against bad PDF sets `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (task)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21: "stress test the stitch tool, look up how to break things and implement strategies to do so"; **renumbered twice in a very hot number space** ÔÇö first shipped as B341 (PR #248), bumped to B348 when a concurrent `main` kept B341 for theming-contrast (PR #244), then bumped again to **B350** when THREE more sessions collided on B348 (PR #255 calibrate-box, a Markup-sidebar feature, and this). B350 was the next free after B349 at this push; the other two B348 entries belong to other already-merged sessions and are left as-is. Code comments + tests + harness all carry B350.)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/stitch-tool-stress-test-1tvw5b`).** Adversarial pass over the stitch core (`stitchGeom.js`, `autoStitch.js`, `sheetGroups.js`, `sheetMeta.js`) ÔÇö drove each engine with the inputs a messy real-world PDF set throws at it and fixed four ways it could **fail silently** (the worst kind here: a bad transform flings a sheet off-canvas, or ÔÇö worse ÔÇö butts two drawings together at the wrong size/place and the takeoff reads a confident wrong number). All four follow the owner rule **"a wrong stitch is worse than an un-stitched one"**: when a signal is unreliable the sheet is left **unplaced ÔåÆ manual-Align safety net**, never auto-guessed.
- **Non-finite endpoints (NaN/Infinity) used to slip past the degenerate-baseline guard** ÔÇö `Math.hypot(NaN) < 1` is `NaN < 1` ÔåÆ `false`, so a NaN coordinate (bad read / mis-built drawing area) reached `solveM`, produced a NaN matrix, and poisoned the sheet transform **and** the whole composite's bbox (every `Math.min/max` over it ÔåÆ NaN). `alignBaselinesDegenerate` now rejects any non-finite point. (`stitchGeom.js`)
- **Contradictory match-line labels produced a wrong (overlapping/mirrored) stitch** ÔÇö if both sheets claimed the seam on their "right", `buildAdjacency` trusted it. It now treats the **geometric opposite** as the source of truth and **drops the edge** when a sheet's own label contradicts it (ÔåÆ manual Align). (`autoStitch.js`)
- **Mismatched page sizes were silently rescaled** ÔÇö a similarity fit from two endpoints will shrink/blow up a half-size detail page to make the seam meet. `autoPlaceGroup` now rejects a placement whose implied scale strays past **`MAX_STITCH_SCALE` (┬▒25%)** and leaves the sheet for manual Align. (`autoStitch.js`)
- **Grouping chained across a major rollover** ÔÇö the packed `ordinal` (major┬À100+minor) made `C-1.99 ÔåÆ C-2.00` look consecutive (and could collide two codes onto one ordinal). `consecutiveCodes` now compares the parsed major/minor by numbering level, so a rollover starts a new logical sheet. (`sheetGroups.js`)
- **New `test/stitchStress.test.js` ÔÇö 29 adversarial tests** across 8 break-strategy groups (non-finite/degenerate coords, contradictory seams, size mismatch, cycles/duplicates/self-refs/missing targets, sheet-code edge cases, reader junk/empty/huge input, a **300-iteration randomized fuzz** asserting the partition + finiteness + scale-band invariants, and the measure-over-unaligned guard). Full suite green (**879 tests**, lint 0 errors, build clean). Pure-engine + DI hardening, so no live-browser verification needed; the manual-Align safety net and text-layer path are unchanged. **(Round 1 ÔÇö PR #248, merged 2026-06-21.)**

**Round 2 ÔÇö reader / grouping / calibration path (follow-up, same B350).** After round 1 hardened the placement geometry, a second adversarial pass over the text reads and takeoff math turned up one real functional gap and locked in the rest:
- **A set numbered past sheet 99 was invisible to grouping AND auto-stitch** ÔÇö `parseSheetNumber` capped the major at `\d{1,2}`, so `C-100` read as `""`, while `parseSheetCode` (sheetGroups) and the match-line `SHEET_REF` (sheetMeta) both already accepted 3 digits. A sheet with no readable number can't be chained by contiguity or found in the seam graph's by-number index, so a `C-100ÔÇªC-110` plan set never grouped or stitched. Bumped the **label-anchored** read to `\d{1,3}` (still requires a "SHEET NO." label, so the `A195` grid-ref guard is intact). (`titleBlockParse.js`)
- **New `test/stitchReaderStress.test.js` ÔÇö 13 tests**: 3-digit sheets now read + group + auto-stitch end-to-end (incl. through the wired `readSheetMeta`); date reading rejects impossible months/days/years and picks the newest under a huge-blob load; takeoff math (`dist`/`polyArea`/`pathLength`/`centroidOf`/`measureValue`/`measureLabel`) degrades on too-few / collinear / concave / coincident / non-finite input and **never fabricates a number** ÔÇö a non-finite calibration reads a safe sentinel ("set scale" / "ÔÇö"), never a plausible-looking figure. Full suite green (**892 tests**, lint 0 errors, build clean).

**Round 3 ÔÇö LIVE interactive stress (headless browser).** Rounds 1ÔÇô2 fuzzed the pure engines; round 3 attacks the one surface they can't reach ÔÇö the real React interaction layer ÔÇö with a new headless harness **`ui-audit/verify-stitch-stress.mjs`** (V92, **14/14 checks, 0 page errors**) driving a synthetic 4-page civil set: **(S1)** a degenerate manual Align (two coincident reference clicks) is refused and the sheet is **not flung**; **(S2)** measuring over a not-yet-aligned sheet is **blocked** and commits nothing; **(S3)** an interrupted pan (window `blur` mid-drag) doesn't crash or stick; **(S4)** ┬▒ zoom-button spam **clamps** at 5%/800%; **(S5)** a 45-click random fuzz across all tools never crashes the Stitcher. The hypothesized "remove a sheet mid-Align ÔåÆ deref `undefined.M`" crash was found **already guarded** (the sibling session's B344 fix, `if (!sheet) { setAlign(null); ÔÇª }` in `onDown`) ÔÇö so this round adds a permanent interaction regression net rather than a new fix. No source change; harness + V-log only.

### B336 ÔÇö Sheet-metadata reader: title block, scale, match-line labels `[Doc Review]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B336**)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/loving-newton-t7gzpq`).** The shared positional reader the whole batch consumes. New `extractPageItems` (pdf.js) keeps each text run's x/y; pure `src/shared/files/sheetMeta.js` finds the **title-block band** (text-density on the right/bottom edge), the **sheet title** (largest wordy line in the band, label/data rows skipped), the **stated scale** (reuses B267 `parseSheetScale`), and every **MATCH-LINE label** with its position + orientation (page-edge ÔåÆ vertical/horizontal cut). Reuses B312's `titleBlockParse` for discipline/number/revision/date ÔÇö no second/parallel reader; surfaces a `confidence` instead of guessing. `doc-review/lib/sheetRead.js` glues pdf.js ÔåÆ reader ÔåÆ grouping with a **dormant injectable OCR seam** for scanned pages (ÔåÆ B340). **12 + 6 unit tests** (`test/sheetMeta.test.js`, `test/sheetRead.test.js`).
> **Dedup:** folded the CLAUDE.md roadmap "read its title block" (auto-filing) ÔÇö extends the shipped filing readers with the positional layer they lacked, doesn't duplicate them.

### B335 ÔÇö Drop a whole set ÔåÆ auto-group pages into logical sheets (collapsed list) `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B335**)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21.** Pure `src/shared/files/sheetGroups.js` collapses consecutive pages that share a plan type (discipline + `item`) AND a contiguous sheet-number run into logical sheets ("Grading Plan ┬À C-5ÔÇôC-7 ┬À 3 sheets"); cover/general-notes stay standalone; conservative (under-groups rather than mis-merges). The Stitcher tray now shows **logical sheets** (read+grouped in the background after a drop), with an **"all pages"** safety-net toggle and a "Reading sheetsÔÇª" state. Clicking a grouped plan adds every page at once (auto-stitched, B337). **7 unit tests** (`test/sheetGroups.test.js`); headless V87 confirmed a 4-page set ÔåÆ 2 logical entries.

### B337 ÔÇö Automatic match-line stitching `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B337**)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21.** Pure `doc-review/lib/autoStitch.js` builds the seam graph from B336's match-line labels and places each sheet by feeding the shared seam's two endpoints into the **existing `solveM()`** similarity fit (`stitchGeom.js`, B300) ÔÇö BFS from the most-connected anchor, the whole group then slotted to the right of existing content. A label-less sheet stays **unplaced** and drops to the 2-point manual Align **pre-seeded** with its detected seam endpoints (half the clicks); manual Align remains the safety net. **5 unit tests** (`test/autoStitch.test.js`); headless V87 confirmed 3 sheets auto-stitch with **coincident seams** (╬öx Ôëê 0.78┬Àwidth, not the old +40 gap).
> **Dedup:** this WAS the roadmap's "automatic match-line detection later," now specified ÔÇö folded, built on `solveM`. The geometric edge-line middle-fallback (when labels are missing) is deferred ÔåÆ B340.

### B338 ÔÇö Crop title blocks + single merged legend on stitched plans `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; arrived as "NEW-4"; minted **B338**)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21.** On a grouped composite each sheet is **clipped to its drawing area** (the B336 title-block band hidden) so the drawings butt cleanly, with a **"Crop blocks"** toggle. One **pinned "Composite key"** panel lists the group merged + the auto-set scale ÔÇö rendered as an overlay (not baked into the raster) so it stays readable at any zoom. Grouping/crop metadata now rides the saved stitch (additive, back-compatible) so a reload keeps the cropped composite + key. Headless V87 confirmed 3 clipPaths + the pinned key + the toggle. The graphical legend **symbol-union** is deferred ÔåÆ B340.

### B339 ÔÇö Auto-calibrate from the sheet's stated scale `[Doc Review]` (feature)  *(owner chat 2026-06-21; arrived as "NEW-5"; minted **B339**)*
`[x]` **Shipped + verified 2026-06-21.** When a grouped plan is added, `groupCalibration` (`doc-review/lib/sheetRead.js`) reads the group's stated scale and sets the composite `ftPerUnit` automatically ÔÇö gated on a standard plot size (`detectSheet`) so a resized/half-size sheet can't mis-scale the set; manual Calibrate still overrides. Reuses `overlayScale.js` (`parseSheetScale`/`ftPerPointForScale`). Headless V87 confirmed the composite auto-calibrated from "1\"=40'" (takeoff "Calibrated", key "Scale set").
> **Dedup:** the SINGLE-SHEET half already shipped (B267 `autoDetectScales`); this added the stitcher/grouped-composite half. The graphic scale-bar fallback is deferred ÔåÆ B340.

<!-- 2026-06-21: coworker-chat batch NEW-1..NEW-4 ÔÇö Document Review FILE-STORAGE consistency after the
     "Drive is the primary file home" direction. Minted **B321ÔÇôB324** (highest B# across both files was
     B315 ÔÇö B25431 / B3340 in this file are CSS hex colours, not IDs). Filed in BACKLOG.md Open AND fixed
     + verified the same session per STANDING RULE #1 (branch `claude/bold-bohr-hohz2x`).
     Dedup before filing:
       ÔÇó NEW-1 (B321): the PRIMARY claim ("there is no Drive delete anywhere") was ALREADY shipped under
         the B207 Drive wiring ÔÇö `deleteReview` already removes `sources[].driveKey` via `deleteFromDrive`.
         So B321 is only the RESIDUAL the brief flagged: loadReviewÔåÆnull (network) left keys=[] ÔåÆ nothing
         cleaned. Distinct from B38(a) (re-file orphaning on upsert).
       ÔÇó NEW-2/3/4 (B322/B323/B324): net-new. NOT duplicates of the single-sheet VIEWER batch B288ÔÇôB296
         (zoom/pan/markup interaction) nor the STITCH-guard batch B300ÔÇôB302 (align/measure-commit) ÔÇö those
         never touched which storage backend a source goes to, nor the autosave gating. -->
### B321 ÔÇö `deleteReview` orphans the Drive copy when the cloud record can't be read `[Doc Review / Files]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-21; minted **B321**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/bold-bohr-hohz2x`).** Deleting a review no longer leaves source bytes orphaned in Google Drive (or Supabase Storage) when the cloud record read misses.
- **Already shipped (B207 Drive wiring):** the brief's primary point ÔÇö "there is no Drive delete anywhere in the code" ÔÇö was already addressed: `deleteReview` removes every `sources[].driveKey` through `deleteFromDrive` (a `DELETE /api/files?key=ÔÇª`) alongside the Supabase `BUCKET.remove(keys)`.
- **Residual fix (this session):** if `loadReview(id)` returns `null` (a transient network miss), `sources` was `[]`, so **neither** the Supabase objects **nor** the Drive objects were removed. `deleteReview` now falls back to the synchronous **local mirror** (`readDraft(uid, id)`, which carries the same `sources[]` incl. `storageKey` + `driveKey`) to recover the file list, so cleanup still runs. Best-effort and in parallel; it never blocks the row delete, and the legacy `<uid>/<id>/` folder sweep is unchanged.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 788 tests ┬À build green** (8 new in `test/docPersistence.test.js`); doc-review lazy chunk intact. The storage path is auth-gated (cloud + Drive), so the unit tests assert the graceful-degrade contract; a signed-in delete-with-Drive is the live check (logged in VERIFICATION).
> **Dedup:** primary already done under B207; this is the loadReview-null residual. Distinct from B38(a) (upsert re-file orphaning).

### B322 ÔÇö "Open PDFÔÇª" and every Stitcher sheet upload to Supabase only, never Drive `[Doc Review / Stitch + Files]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-2" 2026-06-21; minted **B322**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/bold-bohr-hohz2x`).** Interactively-opened single sheets and **all** stitched sheets now go through the **same Drive-first / Supabase-fallback path** as filing, instead of Supabase-only.
- **One shared storage policy:** added `reviewStore.storeSource(srcId, blob, { projectId, discipline, fileName })` ÔÇö push to Google Drive (the primary home); only if Drive doesn't take it, fall back to Supabase Storage. `fileNewReview` was refactored to call it too, so there's a single source of truth for "where a source's bytes live" (no more two slightly-different copies of the policy).
- **Call sites:** `DocReview.openFile` and `Stitcher.openFiles` now call `storeSource` (were `uploadSource` ÔåÆ Supabase only) and persist `driveKey`; both `buildSnapshot`s carry `driveKey`; `Stitcher.loadStitch` reads back Drive-first then Supabase (`DocReview.fetchSourceBytes` already did). 
- **Why it matters:** (a) these files now live in Drive like filed ones (the "Drive is the primary file home" direction); (b) they **bypass Supabase's 50 MB per-file cap on the happy path** ÔÇö an E-size sheet or a large multi-sheet set that used to silently flag `oversize` ("re-drop on load") now stores in Drive; (c) stitched sheets carry a `driveKey`, so a stitched set can read its sheets back from Drive.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 788 tests ┬À build green** (`storeSource` degrade-path test); doc-review lazy chunk intact. The full Drive round-trip needs the (not-yet-deployed) Drive backend + sign-in, so it's the live check; the fallback to Supabase keeps today's behaviour until Drive is on.
> **Dedup:** net-new; the viewer (B288ÔÇôB296) and stitch-guard (B300ÔÇôB302) batches never touched the storage backend choice. Pairs with B321 (Drive delete) and B323 (keyless-source window).

### B323 ÔÇö A source saved with a null key during the upload window becomes unfetchable on a quick reload `[Doc Review]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-3" 2026-06-21; minted **B323**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/bold-bohr-hohz2x`).** A file opened and then reloaded **before its upload resolved** no longer strands the backdrop with a pointer the loader can't fetch.
- **Cause:** `openFile`/`openFiles` set the source into state immediately with `storageKey:null` (and no `driveKey`), which made it an autosave dep; the debounce + the synchronous local mirror could capture that **keyless** source before the upload's `.then(setSource w/ key)` ran. A reload in that window saved a record pointing at a source with no key ÔåÆ `fetchSourceBytes` couldn't fetch it ÔåÆ a permanent "re-open it to view", even though the bytes may have landed.
- **Fix:** `buildSnapshot` now persists a source only when it's actually stored ÔÇö `isStoredSource(s) = !!(s.storageKey || s.driveKey || s.oversize)`. While a just-opened/added file is still uploading (keyless), it's **omitted** from the saved `sources`, so a mid-upload reload can't save a broken pointer; the markups still save, and once the upload resolves the source is persisted normally (its key change re-triggers autosave). Applied to both the single-sheet and the per-sheet Stitcher snapshots. (`oversize` still counts as "stored" ÔåÆ the existing graceful "re-drop on load" path is unchanged.)
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 788 tests ┬À build green** (`isStoredSource` unit tests: keyed/drive/oversize ÔçÆ persistable; keyless/absent ÔçÆ not); doc-review lazy chunk intact.
> **Dedup:** net-new; shares the `isStoredSource` predicate with B322's snapshots. Backdrop-only loss (markups were always safe).

### B324 ÔÇö A genuine edit made within the post-open suspend window isn't flagged dirty ÔåÆ never reaches the cloud `[Doc Review]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-4" 2026-06-21; minted **B324**)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/bold-bohr-hohz2x`).** An edit made right after opening/resuming a review (inside the ~1.5 s programmatic-load window) is now mirrored, flagged dirty, and reaches the cloud.
- **Cause:** the autosave effect early-returned for the whole suspend window (B19), skipping **both** the local mirror **and** `dirtyRef=true`. The unmount/hide flush gates its cloud upsert on `dirtyRef`, so an edit in that window never reached the cloud (lost cross-device, or entirely on a tab crash before flush). The window is armed at load-start, so it usually elapsed harmlessly during a large PDF's fetch ÔÇö it bit on small/cached PDFs.
- **Fix:** the per-tick decision is now a pure, unit-tested `lib/autosavePlan.js ÔåÆ planAutosave({enabled, empty, loadEcho, suspended})`. The programmatic-load **echo** is identified precisely by a `loadEchoRef` flag (armed by `suspendSave`, consumed on the echo tick) rather than by the blunt time window, so it's still skipped ÔÇö but a **genuine edit**, even inside the window, now **mirrors + flags dirty** (so it's recoverable on-device AND flushes to the cloud); only its **debounced cloud re-save** stays suppressed while suspended, preserving B19's "don't re-stamp the just-loaded snapshot" intent (and B44's "no re-upsert of unchanged data on a mode toggle").
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 788 tests ┬À build green** (`planAutosave` unit tests cover: load echo ÔçÆ consume only; edit-in-window ÔçÆ mirror+dirty, no debounce-save; edit-out-of-window ÔçÆ mirror+dirty+save; blank/disabled ÔçÆ nothing).
> **Dedup:** net-new; interacts with B19 (suspendSave) + B44 (dirty-gated flush), both preserved. Not covered by any open item.

### B325 ÔÇö Stitcher pan crash: "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'panX')" when a pan is interrupted `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-21: "its broken, i was on the stitcher and it broke"; minted **B325** ÔÇö a hot `main` had reached B324)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/awesome-feynman-i7wypf`).** The pan handler's `setView` updater read `drag.current` **inside** the deferred updater. That updater runs in React's render phase, which for a continuous `pointermove` can be deferred a tick; a discrete event in between ÔÇö `pointerup`, `pointercancel`, or the **B270** blur/visibility gesture-abort ÔÇö nulls `drag.current` first. Reading the ref then dereferenced null and threw in the render phase ÔåÆ the Document Review **error boundary** caught it ("hit an error and couldn't load") and the whole stitcher went down. A real user hits this by tab-switching / losing focus / a pointer-cancel mid-pan. **Fix:** capture the drag origin into a local and close over it via a pure `panTo()` helper in `lib/stitchGeom.js`, so an aborted gesture can't crash the deferred updater. **Verified:** 4 unit tests (`test/stitchGeom.test.js` ÔÇö math, null-ref-survival, + a teeth test that the OLD ref-reading pattern throws `/panX/`) and a headless harness (`ui-audit/verify-b325-pan.mjs`: a normal pan moves the world; 12 mid-pan aborts ÔåÆ no error boundary, 0 `panX` errors; pan still works after). lint 0 ┬À build green. VERIFICATION **V84**.

### B341 ÔÇö Contrast regression from the scheme change; full-app audit `[Site / shared / theming]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 "Backlog mode"; arrived as "NEW-1"; first filed B321, renumbered **B341** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B321ÔÇôB340)*
`[x]` **Fixed + audited + verified this session (branch `claude/dreamy-fermi-qqyt9j`).** After the
B316ÔÇôB320 scheme change, several chrome elements rendered too light to read. **Root cause:** B318 flipped
the chrome from always-dark to **themes-with-the-app** (light theme ÔåÆ light chrome), and migrated
`index.css` + `AppHeader` + `palette.js` + the per-file `PAL` *objects* to tokens ÔÇö but a set of
**chrome-region components kept inline hardcoded old-dark-chrome hexes** (`#ece7db` cream text,
`#fff` active text, `#9b9482`/`#8a8473` warm grays, `#2e2a23` borders, `rgba(255,255,255,0.0x)` fills,
a hardcoded-dark status-bar bg, and pale dark-chrome badge colors `#86efac`/`#fbbf24`). On the new
**light** chrome those became cream/white-on-white; one (the planner status bar) was dark-text-on-a-
still-hardcoded-dark-bg. The two reported cases ÔÇö the **user-name pill** and the **Dashboard/Map
toggle** ÔÇö were exactly this class, so the fix audited the **whole tree**, not just the three reported spots.
- **Audit method (as briefed):** diffed the scheme commit (`32a1b8a`) to see which tokens changed,
  then grepped every consumer of the retired hexes; built a **programmatic WCAG checker over every
  defined token pair** in both themes (`ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs`, parses the real `index.css`) +
  a **live headless probe** that reads the computed text/bg of the actual chrome elements
  (`ui-audit/verify-b341-contrast.mjs`).
- **Token system itself was sound** ÔÇö every actionable pair clears AA in both themes; the only WARNs
  are documented exceptions (locked Site/Schedule brand fills, only ever used as ÔëÑ3:1 underline +
  unused `--on-accent-*` text tokens; the amber Markup underline whose state is also carried by its
  passing text; owner-exempt subtle borders). So the regression was purely the unmigrated components.
- **Fixes (repointed to tokens):** `Shell.jsx` (account pill + dropdown), `ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx`
  (crumbs + dropdown; **AppHeader now passes the `-text` accent token**, never the fill, for the
  "current"/"New project" labels ÔÇö fill-as-text was 3.4:1), `SitePlanner.jsx` (status bar bg, Files
  button, left-rail + tool-rail + snap/parcel active text, hdrTab chips, save-slot warn color),
  `DocReview.jsx` (header title + Files + chrome buttons), `Stitcher.jsx` (toolbar dividers/buttons),
  `ReviewsBar.jsx` (badge colors + dropdown), `LayerPanel.jsx` (warm grays ÔåÆ tokens; it rides the
  themed surface-overlay so it was dark-on-dark in dark mode), and the reusable `Field` label.
- **Two new tokens** (both themes, AA-checked): **`--on-accent`** (text ON the global accent fill ÔÇö
  white in light, near-black in dark because the B319 dark accent `#F26B3A` is too light for white at
  only 3.0:1) and **`--warn-text`** (legible amber for saving/unsaved/offline badges, replacing the
  dark-chrome `#fbbf24`).
- **Before ÔåÆ after (reported + key siblings), contrast on the light chrome:**

  | Element | before | after (light / dark) |
  |---|---|---|
  | User-name / Sign-in pill text | `#ece7db` on `#FFF` Ôëê **1.2:1** | **16.66 / 15.11** |
  | Dashboard┬ÀMap crumb (active) | `#fff` on `#FFF` = **1.0:1** | **16.66 / 15.11** |
  | Dashboard┬ÀMap crumb (idle) | `#9b9482` Ôëê **3.0:1** | **11.2 / 10.4** |
  | Planner status bar text | dark-on-dark Ôëê **1.3:1** | **9.6ÔÇô14.3 / 8.2ÔÇô16** |
  | Markup "Saved Ô£ô" badge | `#86efac` Ôëê **1.4:1** | **5.3 / 11** |
  | Active module tab + inactive tabs | (ok) | 5.3ÔÇô6.9 / 8ÔÇô10.3 |

- **Deliverables:** `ui-audit/contrast-audit.mjs` (token-pair report, exits non-zero on any actionable
  fail) + **`test/contrast.test.js`** (3 tests ÔÇö both themes pass their floor; the WARN set stays
  locked) so a future palette edit that drops a pair below AA fails CI instead of the eye. Module
  accents stayed confined to the tab row (no accent bled into body areas).
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ┬À **783 tests** (3 new) ┬À build green ┬À doc-review + SitePlannerApp lazy
  chunks intact ┬À headless **both themes** all chrome elements clear WCAG AA (**V84**); screenshots
  `ui-audit/screens/b341-chrome-{light,dark}.png` + `theme-markup-{light,dark}.png`.

### B342 ÔÇö Move the display-theme picker (Light / Dark / System) out of the open header into Settings `[shared / theming]` (task)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 "Backlog mode"; arrived as "NEW-2"; first filed B322, renumbered **B342** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B321ÔÇôB340)*
`[x]` **Done + verified this session (branch `claude/dreamy-fermi-qqyt9j`).** The Light / Dark / System
control used to sit open in `AppHeader` row 1 as a 3-button segmented control. It now lives behind a
**Settings gear (ÔÜÖ) in row 1** that opens an `AnchoredMenu` popover ("DISPLAY THEME" ÔåÆ Light "Always
light" / Dark "Always dark" / System "Match your computer", active row checked).
- **Theme state/store untouched** ÔÇö the gear popover renders the same `useTheme()`-wired options; only
  the control's *placement* changed. Persistence stays per-user in `localStorage['planyr.theme']` as
  before.
- **Edge case handled:** the "System" live OS-preference listener (`matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:
  dark)')`) lives in **`ThemeProvider`** (app-root context), NOT in the relocated control ÔÇö so it keeps
  updating mid-session after the move, verified by inspection + the existing theme tests.
- **Kept reachable signed-OUT** (deliberate): the gear is always present, so the theme switch never
  depends on the signed-in account dropdown (and the sandbox self-tests run logged-out). The only
  header consumer of the old control was AppHeader itself ÔÇö confirmed nothing else depended on it.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À 783 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless logged-out (**V84**,
  `ui-audit/verify-b342-settings.mjs`): segmented control gone, gear opens the popover, choosing Dark ÔåÆ
  `data-theme="dark"` + persisted, back to Light ÔåÆ `data-theme="light"`; screenshot
  `ui-audit/screens/b342-settings-popover.png`.

### B316 ÔÇö Light / dark / system theme with cool-gray light palette `[AppHeader / theming]` (feature) ÔÇö foundation-scale  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-20; arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B316** ÔÇö next free after B272)*
`[x]` Add a theme selector with three modes ÔÇö **Light**, **Dark**, **System** (`prefers-color-scheme`). App is currently hard-locked to one look; this adds the switch. **Both modes are net-new full surface palettes** (owner decision 2026-06-20, see below): Light = cool gray-white surfaces; Dark = a genuine dark-*surface* mode (dark content panels), **retiring** today's warm cream/paper work area.
> **Ô£ô DECISION (owner, 2026-06-20) ÔÇö "build a true dark mode":** the app today is **not all-dark** ÔÇö content surfaces are a warm **cream/paper** ("drafting") palette (`body #efeadf`, panels `#f6f3ec`, ink `#26231e`) under **dark graphite chrome** (top bars + rail, `#14110e`/`#191613`). Asked whether "keep the existing dark unchanged" meant preserve today's cream-content + dark-chrome look, or build a real dark-*surface* mode, the owner chose the latter: build a **genuine dark-surface** Dark mode (dark content panels) and **retire the cream**, alongside the cool-gray Light. **Consequence for scope:** *both* themes are net-new surface palettes ÔÇö the only "existing dark" that survives is the dark **chrome** (bars/rail); the dark **content-surface** values (dark equivalents of surface-page / surface-raised / borders / text) **don't exist yet and must be designed**, and every one of the ~886 color spots needs a proper two-way (lightÔåödark) mapping, not a one-way "add light." This increases effort vs. preserving the current look and reinforces the multi-pass-foundation plan below.

> **­ƒöü UPDATE 2026-06-21 ÔÇö design RESOLVED + decomposed into B317ÔÇôB320 (building this session).** The owner ran the theming design through a ChatGPT visual pass; the returned spec settles every open question here. Implemented as four sibling children of this umbrella: **B317** (infrastructure), **B318** (full palette + theme-aware chrome), **B319** (work-surface canvas/sheet), **B320** (contrast + status). B316 stays as the umbrella/record. **Canonical visual reference: `planyr-theming-mockup.html`** ÔÇö match its *rendered* result, not just raw hexes. ÔÜá Owner named that file as the reference but it is **not in the repo** as of this update ÔÇö request a copy to keep in-repo + diff against. **Two assumptions in THIS item are now reversed/resolved:** (1) ~~"the dark chrome survives unchanged in both themes"~~ ÔåÆ **chrome now themes WITH the app** (light theme = light chrome, dark theme = dark chrome); a permanently-dark bar in an otherwise-white app forces constant pupil readjustment ÔÇö worst case for eye strain (see B318). (2) the net-new dark surface palette **and** the two-value accent-text question are now **fully specified** in B318. Retire the warm cream, the old always-dark warm chrome (`#14110e`/`#191613`), and the ember accent (`#e8590c`).

**Mechanics (from the owner's spec):**
- Drive theming off `data-theme="light|dark"` on `<html>`. All colors as CSS variables, both themes fully defined.
- **System** resolves via `prefers-color-scheme` and must update **live mid-session** ÔÇö attach a `matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` change listener.
- Persist the choice (light/dark/system) in **localStorage as source-of-truth on load**; mirror to the `profiles` row (NEW-1) for cross-device. localStorage wins on first paint to avoid a network round-trip.
- **FOUC gotcha (do this FIRST):** an inline script in `index.html <head>` that reads the stored choice (or system pref) and sets `data-theme` **before React mounts** ÔÇö otherwise every load flashes the wrong theme. ("FOUC" = flash of un-themed content.)
- Theme control lives in the **account dropdown (NEW-2)** or **Settings** ÔÇö consolidate, don't duplicate.
- Files likely touched: `index.html` (inline pre-paint script), a `ThemeProvider`/context, the token stylesheet (`src/index.css`), the account dropdown (NEW-2), the `profiles` mirror via the NEW-1 hook.

**Light-mode surface tokens (cool gray-white ÔÇö owner-verified legible):**

| Token | Light value | Dark value |
|---|---|---|
| surface-page | `#F3F5F8` | existing locked dark |
| surface-raised (cards) | `#FFFFFF` | existing locked dark |
| border-default | `#E1E5EB` | existing |
| border-strong (hover) | `#CDD3DC` | existing |
| text-primary | `#1B1E26` | existing |
| text-secondary (muted) | `#565E6E` | existing |
| text-tertiary (hints) | `#8B92A1` | existing |

> ÔÜá **Per the decision above, "Dark value = existing" for the *surface* rows is now a net-new true-dark surface palette to be designed** (dark page / dark cards / dark borders / light-on-dark text), NOT the current cream. Only the dark **chrome** (bars/rail) pre-exists. The owner's Light column is fixed; the Dark surface column needs its cool-neutral dark equivalents specced as part of the build.

**Accent pair model:** each module accent is **two tokens** ÔÇö a **fill/brand** value (unchanged, locked hexes ÔÇö these already live in `src/shared/ui/moduleAccent.js` as `MODULE_ACCENT`) and an **on-light text** value (tuned, AA-passing as foreground). The `-text` token is theme-dependent.

| Module | fill (both modes) | text ÔÇö light mode | text ÔÇö dark mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| Site | `#1D9E75` | `#0F6E56` | `#5DCAA5` |
| Schedule | `#7F77DD` | `#534AB7` | `#AFA9EC` |
| Markup | `#EF9F27` | `#8A5410` | `#EF9F27` |

- **On-fill text rule (both modes):** text/icons **on** an accent fill ÔÇö Site & Schedule fills carry **white** (`#FFFFFF`); **Markup (amber) fill carries dark text** (`#412402`). White-on-amber is unreadable; enforce wherever a status/tab chip uses the amber fill.
- **Usage rule:** accent-as-**fill** (tab band, active indicator, status-chip bg) ÔåÆ **fill** token. Accent-as-**foreground** (colored text, thin icons, links) ÔåÆ **`-text`** token, never the fill token.
- **Edge case ÔÇö status palette:** the locked status tokens (Pursuit / Active / On Hold / Complete / Dead) need the same discipline. For each status color used as text/icon on a light surface, **derive programmatically** the nearest variant that clears **AA ÔëÑ 4.5** as foreground on `#FFFFFF`/`#F3F5F8`; keep originals as fills with the same on-fill rule. **Flag any status color whose foreground variant can't reach 4.5** for review (don't hand-pick).
- **Constraint (unchanged):** module accents stay confined to the **top tab row only** ÔÇö light mode doesn't change that.

> **Build plan / scope (grounds the "foundation-scale" tag) ÔÇö REUSE, don't rebuild:**
> The app has **no central theming layer today**: ~**886 hardcoded hex literals across ~30 files**, **9 local `PAL` objects** (`SitePlanner.jsx`, `MapFinder.jsx`, `AuthPanel.jsx`, `ParcelDrawing.jsx`, `DocReview.jsx`, `Stitcher.jsx`, `ProjectLibrary.jsx`, `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx`, `ReviewsBar.jsx`), inline styles throughout, plus literals inside `src/index.css`. `MODULE_ACCENT` (`moduleAccent.js`) is already the single source for the **fill** colors ÔåÆ extend it with the `-text` counterparts rather than re-listing hexes. `BrandMark` already takes a `surface="dark|light"` prop (theme-aware) ÔåÆ just feed it the active theme. The Scheduler is an **iframe** (`public/sequence/index.html`) with its **own** colors ÔåÆ needs its own token sheet or a postMessage theme bridge (separate surface).
> Recommended sequence: **(1)** define the full token set for both themes in `:root` / `[data-theme]`; **(2)** FOUC inline script; **(3)** `ThemeProvider` + `matchMedia` live listener + localStorage persistence; **(4)** migrate the 9 PALs + inline literals + `index.css` literals to consume tokens (the bulk ÔÇö do it per-workspace, verifying each surface); **(5)** AA-derive the status palette; **(6)** theme the Scheduler iframe; **(7)** wire the control into NEW-2/Settings + the NEW-1 cross-device mirror. A shell-only/partial theme that leaves the canvas/map/doc-review hardcoded would be a **misleading half** (per the "finish the job" rule) ÔÇö so this ships as a deliberate multi-pass foundation, ideally after NEW-1 + NEW-2 land.

     B316 ÔåÆ next free B317). NOT duplicates of B316: B316 stays as umbrella/record with an UPDATE
     note pointing here; the resolved design lives in these children. Canonical visual reference =

     mockup file but it is NOT in the repo as of filing ÔÇö request a copy to keep in-repo + diff
     against at verification. "Ship together" ÔÇö all four merge as one chain this session. -->

### B317 ÔÇö Light / Dark / System theme infrastructure `[Global / theming]` (feature) ÔÇö child of B316; build FIRST  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 via ChatGPT spec; arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B317**)*
`[x]` Build the theming foundation every module inherits. **Ships together with B318ÔÇôB320; build B317 first (they depend on it).**
- **Token system:** CSS custom properties driven by `data-theme="light|dark"` on `<html>`. Components reference **tokens only**, never raw hex. **Extend** the existing locked token set (`:root` vars in `src/index.css` + the per-file `PAL` objects) rather than starting fresh ÔÇö repoint them at tokens.
- **Three modes:** Light, Dark, System. **System** follows the OS via `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` and must **live-update** when the OS flips ÔÇö attach a `change` listener, don't read once at load.
- **Persistence:** store the choice in `localStorage['planyr.theme']`; **default System** on first run.
- **No theme flash (FOUC):** apply the resolved theme in a tiny inline `<head>` script **before first paint**, so the page never flashes the wrong colors before React boots.
- **Switch UI:** three-way control in Settings and/or the **row-1 settings menu in `AppHeader`**.
- Files: new `ThemeProvider`/context, the global token stylesheet (`src/index.css`), `AppHeader`, `index.html` (pre-paint script).
> Lineage: the **infrastructure half of B316**. Finalizes B316's FOUC/ThemeProvider/matchMedia/localStorage mechanics. localStorage is source-of-truth on load; the `profiles` cross-device mirror stays a later additive follow-on, not a prerequisite.

### B318 ÔÇö Full theme palette + theme-aware chrome `[Global / AppHeader]` (feature) ÔÇö depends on B317; child of B316  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 via ChatGPT spec; arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B318**)*
`[x]` Replace the retired warm-cream work surface **and** the old always-dark chrome. **Chrome now themes WITH the app** ÔÇö this deliberately **reverses** B316's "dark chrome in both themes" assumption: a dark bar in an otherwise-white app (bright office) forces constant pupil readjustment, the worst case for eye strain. **Light theme ÔåÆ light chrome; Dark theme ÔåÆ dark chrome.** Depends on B317.

**Surfaces:**

| Token | Light (fixed) | Dark (new) |
|---|---|---|
| surface-page | `#F3F5F8` | `#14161B` |
| surface-raised | `#FFFFFF` | `#1D2027` |
| border-default | `#E1E5EB` | `#2A2E37` |
| border-strong | `#CDD3DC` | `#3A3F4B` |
| text-primary | `#1B1E26` | `#E8EBF0` |
| text-secondary | `#353B49` | `#CAD0DA` |
| text-tertiary | `#4B5263` | `#A4ABB8` |

**Chrome (themes with app):**

| Role | Light | Dark |
|---|---|---|
| chrome-bg | `#EAEEF3` | `#111319` |
| chrome-bg-elev | `#FFFFFF` | `#171A21` |
| chrome-divider | `#D7DDE5` | `#262A33` |
| chrome-text | `#1B1E26` | `#ECEFF4` |
| chrome-muted | `#353B49` | `#A6ADBA` |
| chrome-tab-inactive | `#454C5C` | `#C2C8D2` |
| save-badge | `#0F6E56` | `#7FD8B8` |

- **Module fills (fixed, both themes):** Site `#1D9E75`, Schedule `#7F77DD`, Markup `#EF9F27`. **On-fill text:** white for Site/Schedule; **dark `#412402` for Markup** ÔÇö never white on amber.
- **Active-tab accent TEXT swaps by theme** (tabs sit on chrome): light chrome ÔåÆ `#0F6E56` / `#534AB7` / `#8A5410`; dark chrome ÔåÆ `#5DCAA5` / `#AFA9EC` / `#EF9F27`. (Resolves the two-value accent-text question: dark-on-light set = light chrome; light-on-dark set = dark chrome.)
- **Accents only in the module-tab row** (active tab text + 2px underline) ÔÇö never a work-surface fill.
- **Retire** the old warm chrome (`#14110e`/`#191613`) and the **ember** accent (`#e8590c`). NB `src/index.css :root` hardcodes `--chrome/--chrome-line/--chrome-ink/--chrome-muted/--accent` to warm/ember + a `rgba(232,89,12,ÔÇª)` focus ring, and `Shell.jsx`/`AppHeader.jsx` hardcode `#14110e`/`#2e2a23`/`#c9c3b4` ÔÇö all move to per-theme tokens.

### B319 ÔÇö Work-surface theme behaviors: canvas + Markup sheet `[Site, Markup]` (feature) ÔÇö depends on B317/B318; child of B316  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 via ChatGPT spec; arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B319**)*
`[x]` The drafting canvas and the document viewer need their own dark behavior, distinct from panel surfaces. Depends on B317/B318.
- **Site canvas dark tokens:** `canvas-bg #0E1014`, `grid-minor rgba(232,235,240,0.05)`, `grid-major rgba(232,235,240,0.10)`; lift **parcel stroke to `#2FBE90`** on dark.
- **Drafting/selection accent dark variant:** the selected-element + dimension-line **red-orange is a work-surface color, NOT a module accent** ÔÇö the "accents don't change" rule doesn't apply: light `#C2410C` ÔåÆ dark **`#F26B3A`** (`#C2410C` too dim on the dark canvas).
- **Markup in Dark:** the **mat** (around the drawing, `#cfc8ba` today at `DocReview.jsx:539` + `Stitcher.jsx:343`) darkens to **`#0E1014`**; the **drawing sheet stays white** ÔÇö it's the **immutable backdrop**, and inverting a construction sheet misrepresents it. Direct application of the standing immutable-backdrop principle, not a one-off.
- Files: Site Planner canvas renderer (`SitePlanner.jsx`); Markup viewer mat/sheet container (`DocReview.jsx`, `Stitcher.jsx`).

### B320 ÔÇö Contrast + text-hierarchy pass `[Global / theming]` (task) ÔÇö depends on B317/B318; child of B316  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-21 via ChatGPT spec; arrived as "NEW-4"; minted **B320**)*
`[x]` Apply the dark text ramp (B318) and lock the rule behind it. Depends on B317/B318.
- **Standing rule (also added to `CLAUDE.md`):** build text hierarchy through **weight, size, and uppercase letter-spacing ÔÇö never by fading text toward the background.** Low-contrast gray body/label text is an eye-strain problem in bright offices and is **disallowed**. Subtle grays stay correct **only** for borders, the drafting grid, and the semantic "Complete" status badge.
- **Status badges need dark-surface variants.** The canonical `STATUS_TOKENS` (`src/shared/ui/statusTokens.js`: pursuit `#378ADD`, active `#639922`, onhold `#BA7517`, complete `#888780`, dead `#E24B4A`) are tuned for white cards; saturated values go muddy on `#1D2027`. Add a lighter dark-mode variant per status (mockup direction: Active ÔåÆ `#2FBE90`, Complete ÔåÆ `#8B92A1`). NB a drifted duplicate `STATUS_COLOR` lives in `ProjectLibrary.jsx:13` ÔÇö reconcile while here.
- **Verify all body text clears WCAG AA (ÔëÑ 4.5:1)** against its surface in **both** themes; flag any accent/status that fails and propose the fix rather than shipping it silent.

### B312 ÔÇö Auto-filing reads the title block with plain code first; AI only as a fallback `[Document Review / Files]` (feature) ÔÇö owner direction on B299  *(owner 2026-06-20: "a ton of this could be done without an LLMÔÇª is this gonna cost tokens?"; minted **B312** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B300ÔÇôB311)*
`[x]` **Built + tested + shipped this session.** Re-architected auto-filing (B299) to **"plain code first, AI only as fallback"** so the common case is **free, instant, and needs no cloud or API key** ÔÇö exactly the owner's point that reading a title block, picking the latest date, and recognizing "ALTA survey" is ordinary code, not an LLM job.
- **Tier 1 ÔÇö deterministic, in the browser, $0 (the default now):** for any PDF with an embedded text layer (most CAD-exported vector drawings), read the text with the existing `extractPageText` (pdf.js, the same reader B267 uses) and parse the filing fields with pure code:
  - `src/shared/files/titleBlockParse.js` (pure, **17 unit tests**): `classifyDiscipline` (a keyword table ÔÇö **ALTA** ÔåÆ Survey/ALTA Survey, boundary/topo ÔåÆ Survey, grading/paving/drainage ÔåÆ Civil, fire sprinkler, landscape, environmental, geotechÔÇª; sheet-number prefix as the tie-break), `findDates`/`latestDate` ("search all dates, pick the newest" = the issue date, across MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, "June 30, 2025", "30 June 2025"), `parseSheetNumber` (labelled-first, conservative ÔÇö returns "" rather than grab a grid ref), `parseRevision`, `readTitleBlockText`.
  - `src/shared/files/matchProject.js` (pure, **8 unit tests**): `matchProjectInText` **searches the sheet text for each named project's identifiers** (name phrase, or exact parcel/account/job number) and reuses the B299 "never auto-guess" decision (confident single ÔåÆ matched; none/ambiguous ÔåÆ needs-filing tray). Mirrors `server/filing/matcher.js`'s thresholds.
  - `src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/localRead.js` glues them to pdf.js (lazy-imported) ÔåÆ a decision in the SAME shape the server returns.
- **Tier 2 ÔÇö AI fallback, only when Tier 1 finds NO text** (a scanned/image-only sheet) **and** the backend is enabled. So tokens are spent only on the hard minority, not every file. `autofile` (`lib/autofiling.js`) is now local-first with an injectable `localRead`; `capturePlacementFacts` uses a new `forceServer` flag (placement is genuinely an AI/CV step).
- **On by default, safely:** the provider now exposes `autofileReady = true` (Tier 1 always works in the browser ÔÇö no key, no deploy), so the drawer auto-files dropped drawings out of the box. A **confident match auto-routes**; **anything else falls back to today's behavior** (the active project, or the holding tray) ÔÇö so the only behavior change is that confident matches now file themselves, and a wrong confident match is still re-filable from the row. `backendReady` stays gated on `VITE_AUTOFILE_ENABLED` (the honest "AI can also read scanned sheets / capture placement" signal).
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 743 tests (53 new: titleBlockParse 17 + matchProject 8 + autofiling tiering) ┬À build green; doc-review lazy-chunk split intact.** Headless on the built app (`ui-audit/verify-b299-autofiling.mjs`): the Files drawer opens, the new modules (parser, matcher, lazy-pdf localRead) evaluate in a real browser, **0 page/console errors**. **Real-sheet accuracy** (the keyword table + name match against the owner's actual KG B1 / Jacintoport drawings) is the live acceptance check ÔÇö logged as **VERIFICATION V79** (needs a signed-in drop of a real construction sheet; the deterministic logic itself is exhaustively unit-tested).
> **Dedup:** does NOT replace the B299 AI reader (`server/filing/`) ÔÇö it puts a free deterministic front-end on it and demotes it to a fallback. Reuses `extractPageText` (B267) for text, the B299 matcher logic/thresholds, the B299 file-facts index + drawer wiring. The AI path stays the answer for scanned sheets + placement-fact capture.
