### B310 ÔÇö Accidental parcel selection while panning the planner canvas `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; first filed B300, renumbered **B310** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B300ÔÇôB309)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/blissful-mccarthy-dkozi0`).** With parcels rendered, grabbing the planner canvas to pan kept registering as a parcel select ÔÇö `startMoveParcel` (`SitePlanner.jsx`) fired `setSel` on pointer-**down** and `e.stopPropagation()` blocked the background pan, so a press anywhere on a lot selected it whether the gesture was a click or the start of a drag. Navigating across lots felt like constantly clicking into them.
- **Fix ÔÇö click-vs-drag discrimination on the parcel layer.** A press on a **locked** parcel (the default every county-pulled / drawn lot carries ÔÇö created `locked:true` in all six creation paths) now starts a **PAN** (`drag.current.mode:"pan"`) tagged with the parcel id + the press origin (`tapParcel`, `downX/downY/downT`), instead of selecting. On pointer-**up** (`onUp`) it counts as a deliberate **click ÔåÆ select** only if travel Ôëñ `PARCEL_CLICK_SLOP_PX` (**5px**, measured in SCREEN px so it's zoom-independent) **and** the press was < `PARCEL_CLICK_MS` (**400ms**); any larger/longer movement panned and leaves the selection untouched. Uses Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture` (already the canvas's model) so the up fires even if the pointer left the polygon, and the gesture joins the existing `abortGesture` recovery (`capturePidRef`) so an interrupted parcel-pan can't strand the grab cursor (B271-class).
- **Preserved:** a deliberately **unlocked** parcel still grabs-to-move on press (you unlocked it to reshape/move it ÔÇö matches elements); the full-polygon hit area is unchanged (we gate on gesture *intent*, never shrink the target); Shift-click-to-merge and align-edge picking still work. Space / the Pan tool remain the always-on pan escape hatches.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 712 tests ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.** Headless on the built app (`ui-audit/verify-b310-b311.mjs`, logged-out, seeded one locked parcel): a deselectedÔåÆ**click selects** (strokeÔåÆ#c2410c / width 3); a **drag of 95px pans and does NOT select** (stroke stays #5b6650 / width 2, the parcel's on-screen box shifted +95px), **0 page JS errors**. Logged as **VERIFICATION V78**.

### B311 ÔÇö "Select parcels" mode toggle (non-hijacking default) `[Site Planner]` (feature) ÔÇö layered on B310  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; first filed B301, renumbered **B311**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/blissful-mccarthy-dkozi0`).** A **Row-2 toolbar toggle** ("Select parcels", next to Snap, shown only when the plan has parcels) as a hard-off fallback for pure browse/measure workflows where zero accidental selection is wanted. **Non-hijacking default = ON:** click a lot to select it, dragging always pans (normal selection works out of the box thanks to B310, so the toggle isn't the primary gate). **OFF:** a press on a parcel is click-through ÔÇö `startMoveParcel` returns *without* `stopPropagation`, so the event falls through to the background pan exactly as if it landed on empty canvas; a click never selects. Turning it OFF also drops any current parcel selection so browse mode is clean.
- **Persistence:** stored as `settings.parcelSelect` (default `true` in `DEFAULT_SETTINGS`), so it rides the existing per-project Site Model `settings` save/restore path ÔÇö saved per project, restored on reopen. No new store, no localStorage key.
- **Verified ÔÇö same harness (`ui-audit/verify-b310-b311.mjs`):** default reads **ON** (`aria-pressed=true`); flipping it **OFF** ÔåÆ a click no longer selects (parcel stays unselected); flipping it back **ON** ÔåÆ a click selects again; **OFF persists across a full page reload** (per-project). lint 0 ┬À 712 tests ┬À build green. Logged as **VERIFICATION V78**.

### B315 ÔÇö Undo (Ctrl+Z) unreliable after moving a building `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as coworker-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-21; renumbered B310ÔåÆB313ÔåÆ**B315** as a very hot `main` twice consumed the number ÔÇö B310/B311 parcel click-vs-drag + B312 auto-filing, then B313/B314 multi-tab + optimistic-concurrency)*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/ecstatic-ritchie-ihzkym`).** Moving a building then pressing Ctrl+Z now reverts the **entire** move in one step, every time (was: sometimes nothing, sometimes a partial revert).
- **Root cause (confirmed by reading, NOT guessed ÔÇö the brief's hypothesis #2, the race):** the drag-move transaction boundary was already correct ÔÇö `startMove`/`startMoveParcel`/`startGroupMove` push history **once** at drag-start, nothing during the drag, nothing on pointer-up (so hypothesis #1, per-frame pushes, did **not** apply). The defect was that `stateRef` ÔÇö the single source both for the snapshot pushed onto the undo stack AND for the baseline `undo()` compares against (the B32 no-op dedup `histKey(stateRef.current)` check) ÔÇö was updated in a **passive `useEffect` (no deps)**. A ref written in a passive effect lags the committed render by a paint, so a quick undoÔåödrag sequence could snapshot/compare a **stale** state ÔåÆ revert to the wrong frame (partial) or skip the frame in the dedup loop (does nothing). Timing-dependent ÔçÆ intermittent, matching the fuzzy repro.
- **Fix:** (1) `stateRef.current` is now assigned **during render** instead of in a passive effect, so `pushHistory`/`undo`/`redo` always read the TRUE current state ÔÇö the race window is gone. (2) Extracted the stack + dedup into a **pure, unit-tested** module `lib/history.js` (`createHistoryStack({ keyOf })` ÔåÆ push/undo/redo/drop/canUndo/canRedo), matching the `stitchGeom.js` precedent; `undo`/`redo` take the live state **explicitly**, so the stale-ref class of bug can't recur. (3) **Interrupted-drag edge case** (brief's list): Esc / lost-focus / pointer-cancel **mid-move** now reverts to the pre-drag snapshot stashed on `drag.current` and drops the dangling frame (`cancelActiveMove` in `abortGesture`), so a cancelled drag leaves no half-recorded command.
- **Edge cases covered + verified:** one drag = one undo (full revert); redo restores; two moves = two undos (one each); multi-select/group move = one push = one undo (geometry-agnostic stack); independent transactions don't clobber each other (a move-undo doesn't touch an unrelated vertex edit); Esc-mid-drag cancels cleanly.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **full suite green** (11 new in `test/history.test.js`, incl. a deterministic repro of the stale-baseline failure mode) ┬À build green. Headless on the built app (`ui-audit/verify-b315.mjs`, chromium-1194, logged-out): drag a building 300px ÔåÆ **one Ctrl+Z snaps it fully back, ╬ö=0.0px** (not partial); redo; two-moves-two-undos; Esc-mid-drag revert; **0 JS errors**.
> **Dedup:** net-new. **Related but distinct from B32 (done)** ÔÇö B32 stopped no-op edits from polluting the stack (the *dedup* the `while`-loop does); B315 is the *stale-baseline race* that made even a clean move-frame revert wrong/skipped. NOT a duplicate of any open item; no open item covered undo-after-move. Kept as ONE item (the brief said split only if the diagnosis turned out to be redo- or multi-select-specific ÔÇö it wasn't; the race affects all undo, redo + multi-select already work as separate transactions).

### B308 ÔÇö Server-side Mapillary token proxy so the street-level layer works for all users `[Site Planner / functions]` (feature)  *(coworker-chat "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; first filed B303 then renumbered **B308** ÔÇö concurrent `main` landed a Doc Review batch on B303ÔÇôB307, so both can't share the number)*
`[x]` **Built + shipped 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** The "Poles & hydrants from street imagery" layer now works for **every visitor with no per-user token**, and the Mapillary access token never reaches the public bundle or any client request URL ÔÇö it lives only as an encrypted Cloudflare Pages Secret the owner set (`MAPILLARY_TOKEN`, Production). Resolves the audit's *token-in-bundle* + *token-in-URL* LOW findings; honours the KEY DECISION "Mapillary token is a secret."
- **Pages Function** `functions/api/mapillary/[[path]].js` reads the token from the runtime binding `context.env.MAPILLARY_TOKEN` (NOT `import.meta.env`/a `VITE_*` var) and forwards to the Graph API. **Guardrails** (pure helpers in `functions/api/mapillary/_proxy.js`, unit-tested): allow-list ONLY `map_features` + fields `id,object_value,geometry`; **Origin check** (same-origin / `*.planyr.io` / `*.planyr.pages.dev`, foreign Origin ÔåÆ 403); bbox/limit validation; short edge-cache (`max-age=300`) to cut quota. **Dormant-graceful:** no token (e.g. a Preview deploy, which doesn't have the secret) ÔåÆ 503 + empty `data`, the client shows "not available here", never a hard error.
- **Client:** new leaflet-free `lib/mapillaryClient.js` (`mapillaryRequestUrl`/`pickDetections`); `evidenceLayers.fetchMapillary` calls the same-origin `/api/mapillary/map_features` with **NO `access_token`** by default (a pasted token = an optional power-user override straight to Mapillary). Removed the no-token gate in `mapillaryLayer` + `syncOverlayLayers` and the `needsSetup` flag on the layer; the LayerPanel box is now "Works automatically ÔÇö (Advanced) use your own token". `.env.example` + README document the secret-lives-in-Cloudflare rule.
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **722 tests** (10 new, `test/mapillaryProxy.test.js`: allow-list, Origin, limit-clamp, and the client default carrying no token) ┬À build green; **0 `MLY|` token literals + 0 `VITE_MAPILLARY_TOKEN` in `dist/`**. Headless on the built app (`gis-verify/mapillary-proxy-verify.mjs`): toggling the layer with no token ÔåÆ it loads via **same-origin `/api/mapillary/map_features`**, **0 token leaks** in any request URL, **no** direct `graph.mapillary.com` call, graceful degrade in preview, **0 JS errors**.
- **ÔÅ│ LIVE confirm (the one piece the sandbox can't do ÔÇö a Pages Function needs the Cloudflare runtime):** on planyr.io Production after the next deploy, zoom ÔëÑ 16 over a covered area ÔåÆ pole/hydrant dots render via `/api/mapillary/ÔÇª`, token nowhere in page source/bundle/URLs. Logged in VERIFICATION. (The owner already set the secret, so this is just the deploy + a coworker eyeball.)
> **Dedup:** net-new; builds on B285/B286 (Mapillary rename + gating). The cleanup half (drop the dead `VITE_MAPILLARY_TOKEN` read) stays as **B309** `[~]`, held until this is confirmed live.

### B303 ÔÇö Undo / redo for Document Review markups + calibration `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20 owner chat; provisionally B283, renumbered **B303** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B283ÔÇôB302; batch B303ÔÇôB307)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/stoic-faraday-th9mp8`).** Repro: place/delete/move a markup or measurement, press Ctrl/Ôîÿ-Z ÔåÆ nothing happened; the workspace had no history at all, so a mis-edit was unrecoverable. **Fix:** **Ctrl/Ôîÿ-Z undo, Ctrl/Ôîÿ-Shift-Z + Ctrl-Y redo** in BOTH `DocReview.jsx` (single sheet) and `Stitcher.jsx` (stitched set), plus on-canvas **ÔåÂ / ÔåÀ** buttons (disabled when there's nothing to undo/redo).
- **Pattern reused, not reinvented:** mirrors the Site Planner's `pushHistory` ÔÇö `pastRef`/`futureRef` of editable-state snapshots taken **by reference** (DocReview `{markups, calByPage, calInfo}`; Stitcher `{measures, ftPerUnit}`), with a `histKey` signature so `undo` **skips frames identical to the current state** ÔÇö the **B32/B105 lesson** that a stray push can't make Ctrl-Z look like a no-op. `pushHistory()` fires only on a REAL mutation; the background stated-scale auto-scan (B267) deliberately does NOT push. History resets when the document context changes (open new PDF / load / new review).
- **Covers `main`'s viewer edits too:** a markup **move** (B293, committed on pointer-up) and a **text create/edit/delete** (B293 inline editor) each push one frame, so the new editing affordances are undoable as well as plain draws.
- **Poly-vertex placement:** mid-draw, Ctrl-Z (and Backspace/Delete) step back the **last placed vertex** of a polygon/area/count draft before falling through to full undo / delete-selection.
- **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b303-b307.mjs`): draw a rect ÔåÆ Ctrl-Z removes it ÔåÆ Ctrl-Shift-Z and Ctrl-Y each restore it; undo disabled before any edit; same proven in the Stitcher.

### B304 ÔÇö Manual Calibrate validates input (no more silent mis-calibration) `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20 owner chat; provisionally B284, renumbered **B304**; batch B303ÔÇôB307)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/stoic-faraday-th9mp8`).** Repro: Calibrate ÔåÆ click two points ÔåÆ at the length prompt type a non-decimal form (e.g. **"1/8"** or **"1:240"**) ÔåÆ `parseFloat` returned **1** and the sheet calibrated to a wrong scale with NO warning, silently poisoning every downstream measurement; gibberish ("abc") silently did nothing. **Root cause:** `parseFloat(window.prompt(...))` stops at the first non-numeric char, and the invalid branch `return`ed with no feedback.
- **Fix:** replaced `window.prompt` with an **inline on-canvas entry box** (honoring the owner "no dialog boxes" rule, like `main`'s B293 text editor) backed by a new tested parser **`parseFeet`** (`lib/parseLength.js`). It validates the WHOLE string: accepts plain feet (`120`, `120.5`, `120'`), **feet-and-inches** (`38'-7 3/4"`), and explicit fractional feet (`12 1/2 ft`); **rejects** scale ratios (`1:240`), bare ambiguous fractions (`1/8`), non-numeric junk, and zero/negative ÔÇö each with a clear inline message. A bad entry keeps the box open to fix; a blank cancels. Applied in BOTH `DocReview.jsx` and `Stitcher.jsx`.
- **Dedup:** distinct from **B267/B268** (auto-calibrate / scale cross-check) ÔÇö the **manual-entry** path they don't cover. Clears the **calibration** half of the B155/B156 "no dialog boxes" note; `main`'s B293 already converted the **text** prompt, so only ParcelDrawing's calibrate prompt remains under B155.
- **Verified:** unit ÔÇö `test/parseLength.test.js` (7 cases incl. the exact "1/8" / "1:240" / "abc" bugs). Headless ÔÇö the inline box opens (no prompt), **rejects "1/8" and stays uncalibrated**, rejects "1:240" as a ratio, and **accepts "100" ÔåÆ sheet calibrated** (single sheet + Stitcher).

### B305 ÔÇö Markup toolbar overcrowds; header buttons wrap into uneven multi-line labels `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3" 2026-06-20 owner chat; provisionally B285, renumbered **B305**; batch B303ÔÇôB307)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/stoic-faraday-th9mp8`).** Repro (confirmed live at 1568px wide): the header's "Open anotherÔÇª", "Stitch sheets Ôû©", "Library" buttons wrapped their labels onto 2ÔÇô3 lines at uneven heights; the single toolbar row was too crowded and worsened as the window narrowed. **Fix (the B117/B118 density treatment):** every toolbar button now carries **`white-space: nowrap`** (labels never break mid-word) + tightened padding; the three file-action buttons are condensed (`OpenÔÇª`, `Stitch Ôû©`, `­ƒôü Library`) with full-text `title`s; sections are grouped with thin dividers (file ┬À tools ┬À undo/redo ┬À zoom). `DocReview.jsx` + `Stitcher.jsx`.
- **Verified headless:** the Open / Stitch / Library buttons compute `white-space:nowrap` and render as a **single 25px line** (Ôëñ34px) at 1440px ÔÇö no wrapping. Screenshot `screens/b303-b307.png`.

### B306 ÔÇö Sheet paging: Prev/Next buttons + keyboard `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-4" 2026-06-20 owner chat; provisionally B286, renumbered **B306**; batch B303ÔÇôB307)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/stoic-faraday-th9mp8`).** Repro: on a 32-sheet set the only way to change sheets was clicking each entry in the left list ÔÇö no Prev/Next, and arrow / PageUp / PageDown didn't flip sheets. **Fix** (`DocReview.jsx`): a **ÔÇ╣ N / total ÔÇ║** pager above the sheet list, plus **ÔåÉ / ÔåÆ and PageUp/PageDown** keyboard paging (gated so they don't fire mid-draw or mid-calibrate-entry), and the sheet list **auto-scrolls to keep the current sheet in view** (`scrollIntoView`). A shared `goToPage` keeps the current zoom (matching `main`'s viewer behaviour) and drops any in-progress draft/selection.
- **Verified headless:** Next advances 1/4ÔåÆ2/4; ÔåÆ / ÔåÉ page 2/4ÔåÆ3/4ÔåÆ2/4; the indicator + active-sheet highlight track.

### B307 ÔÇö Measurement labels sit at the path midpoint / true centroid `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-5" 2026-06-20 owner chat; provisionally B287, renumbered **B307**; batch B303ÔÇôB307)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/stoic-faraday-th9mp8`).** Repro: a Distance label rendered on the first endpoint (`pts[Math.floor((len-1)/2)]` resolves to `pts[0]` for a 2-point line); an Area label sat at the vertex average, which can fall outside a concave (L-shaped) region. **Fix** (new pure helpers in `lib/takeoff.js`): **`midOfPath(pts, closed)`** anchors distance/perimeter labels at the true **arc-length midpoint**; **`centroidOf(pts)`** anchors area labels at the **area-weighted centroid**, and when that lands outside a concave shape it drops to the midpoint of the widest interior scanline span so the label stays **inside**. Wired into `DocReview.jsx` (distance/perimeter/area) and `Stitcher.jsx` (area). Coexists with `main`'s B296 (the label's 1-dp numeric format) ÔÇö B307 is the label's POSITION.
- **Verified:** unit ÔÇö `test/takeoff.test.js` (midpoint of a 2-pt line + an L; closed wrap-around; square centroid; concave-L centroid proven interior by ray-cast). Headless ÔÇö a horizontal distance label renders at **xÔëê454** (midpoint Ôëê450), not Ôëê250 (pts[0]).
### B300 ÔÇö Stitcher alignment has no degenerate-baseline guard; coincident clicks fling the sheet with a garbage transform `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20 owner chat; first filed B288 then renumbered **B300** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B288ÔÇôB299; batch B300ÔÇôB302)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/admiring-hypatia-xjv8a1`).** Repro (numerically verified): in Stitch, aligning a sheet means clicking two matching points; if the two clicks on the moving sheet (or the two reference points) land on ~the same spot, `solveM`'s `lb = Math.hypot(vb) || 1` **masked the zero baseline** and returned an extreme similarity transform (e.g. `solveM(b1==b2, A1, A2 100 apart)` ÔåÆ ├ù100 scale, e/f Ôëê ÔêÆ500), throwing the sheet far off-canvas at huge scale ÔÇö silently, and Stitch has no undo. **Fix:** extracted the pure stitch geometry (`fwd`/`inv`/`solveM`/`sheetBBox`) out of `Stitcher.jsx` into a unit-testable **`lib/stitchGeom.js`**, and added **`alignBaselinesDegenerate(b1,b2,A1,A2)`** + `MIN_ALIGN_BASE`, which rejects an alignment whose moving-sheet baseline `|b2ÔêÆb1|` **OR** reference baseline `|A2ÔêÆA1|` is below a small threshold (mirrors DocReview's Calibrate "line too short" guard). The align commit checks it **before** `solveM`: on a degenerate baseline it shows the banner ("Those two points are too close together ÔÇö pick two points farther apartÔÇª") and restarts the alignment at step 0, leaving the sheet **untouched**; only a healthy baseline applies the transform (and now also stamps the sheet `aligned:true`, see B301).
- **Verified:** 8 unit tests (`test/stitchGeom.test.js`, incl. the runaway-scale case the guard blocks + `solveM` scale/rotation recovery + `fwd`/`inv` round-trip) and **headless on the built app** (`ui-audit/verify-b300-b302.mjs`, chromium-1228): a degenerate Align shows the banner and the sheet's `<g transform>` is **unchanged** (not flung); a real Align still succeeds. lint 0 ┬À build green; DocReview lazy chunk still splits (stitchGeom rides inside it).
> **Dedup (batch B300ÔÇôB302):** net-new ÔÇö no existing item covers the stitch alignment math. NOT main's single-sheet-viewer **B288ÔÇôB296** (zoom/pan/markup-edit) nor its auto-filing **B299** (server-side title-block read), all different surfaces; distinct from **B181/B182/B183** (the *map* placement cascade) and **B130/B131** (parking). Shares `lib/stitchGeom.js` with B301.

### B301 ÔÇö Measuring over a not-yet-aligned sheet silently uses the composite scale ÔåÆ wrong quantities `[Doc Review / Stitch]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20 owner chat; first filed B289 then renumbered **B301**; batch B300ÔÇôB302)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/admiring-hypatia-xjv8a1`).** Repro: add a second sheet (it drops at identity scale, offset to the right) and draw a Distance/Area over it **before** pressing Align ÔÇö the measurement applied the single composite calibration (ft per world unit, defined by sheet 1), so if that sheet's real scale differed the reading was **silently wrong** (and even at a shared scale its position/rotation could be off). **Fix:** each placed sheet now carries an **`aligned`** flag ÔÇö the first sheet *is* the world frame (auto-aligned `true`); every later sheet starts `false` and flips to `true` only when its Align completes (B300's commit path). Unaligned sheets are **flagged visually** ÔÇö an amber dashed "ÔÜá Not aligned ÔÇö click Align" overlay on the canvas, plus a "ÔÜá Not aligned ÔÇö measurements may be off" chip + amber-emphasized Align button in the Placed-sheets list. Committing a Distance/Area whose points fall over an unaligned sheet (**`measureOverUnaligned`** / `sheetContains` in `lib/stitchGeom.js`) raises a **warning banner** ("Heads up: that measurement is over a sheet that isn't aligned yet ÔÇö Align it firstÔÇª"). **Warn, not block** (the app's "surface it loudly, let the user decide" principle). The flag is persisted in the stitch snapshot and restored on load ÔÇö older saves default their non-base sheets to aligned, so nothing already-saved is falsely flagged.
- **Verified:** unit tests for `sheetContains` + `measureOverUnaligned` (warns only over an `aligned:false` sheet; never over the base or with no sheets) and **headless**: a fresh 2nd sheet shows the canvas overlay + the panel chip, a measurement over it shows the warning, and a successful Align clears both. lint 0 ┬À build green.
- **Ôåæ Superseded by B316 (2026-06-21, owner call "don't let it measure on uncalibrated things"):** the **warn** became a **hard block** ÔÇö a Distance/Area point over an un-aligned sheet is now refused at click time (`blockedOverUnaligned`, nothing commits), and the chip reads "Not aligned ÔÇö Align before measuring." `measureOverUnaligned`/`aligned`-state unchanged. See B316 / V83.

### B302 ÔÇö Degenerate 2-point Area (0 sf) and 2-point Perimeter (single segment) are committable `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3" 2026-06-20 owner chat; first filed B290 then renumbered **B302**; batch B300ÔÇôB302)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/admiring-hypatia-xjv8a1`).** Repro (numerically verified): `finishDraft` committed Area/Perimeter at ÔëÑ2 points, but a real polygon needs ÔëÑ3 ÔÇö a 2-point Area saved as **0 sf** (shoelace) and a 2-point Perimeter drew closed yet measured only the single segment (10 ft for a 10-unit span at 1 ft/u, not the 20 ft a closed loop implies), leaving meaningless zero/half entries in the takeoff list. **Fix:** added **`MIN_MEASURE_PTS` + `canCommitMeasure(kind,n)`** to `lib/takeoff.js` (area/perimeter ÔëÑ3, distance 2, count 1 ÔÇö matching what `polyArea`/`pathLength` actually need) and gated **both** finish paths on it ÔÇö Enter/`finishDraft` and (after merging main's B291 phantom-point fix) double-click/`onDbl` ÔÇö so a 2-point Area/Perimeter is a no-op (the draft is cleared) whichever way you finish, consistent with the existing count min-point guard. Other kinds (distance/rect/cloud/calibrate) are unaffected.
- **Verified:** unit tests for `canCommitMeasure` (the ÔëÑ3 / 2 / 1 contract) + the degenerate-geometry rationale (2-pt area = 0; a 2-pt "closed" path = one 10-unit segment, not 20; a real 3-pt loop closes 3+4+5=12) and **headless**: a 2-point Area finished by Enter or double-click commits nothing and the takeoff stays "No measurements yet", while a 3-point Area commits one polygon. lint 0 ┬À build green.
> **Dedup:** minor severity (clutter, not corrupted totals ÔÇö a 0-sf area contributes 0 to the rollup), filed with B300/B301 but a separate surface (`takeoff.js` / single-sheet Markup). Complements main's **B291** (which stripped the double-click's phantom points) by also enforcing the ÔëÑ3 minimum on that finish path.

### B299 ÔÇö Auto-filing: drop a drawing ÔåÆ it reads the title block ÔåÆ matches a project ÔåÆ files itself `[Document Review / Server + Files]` (feature)  *(owner HANDOFF 2026-06-20; first built as B270, renumbered through a very hot `main` that consumed B270ÔÇôB298 ÔÇö incl. a different **B270** (Project Files drop-zone queue) and a different **B297** (account profiles) ÔÇö so it landed at the real next free ID **B299** at merge time)*
`[x]` **Built + wired + tested this session (branch `claude/sleepy-archimedes-gts4wa`).** The auto-filing **backend tranche**: drop a PDF ÔåÆ a server-side title-block read ÔåÆ match to one of the named projects ÔåÆ auto-route + auto-name, with a "needs filing" tray for anything it can't confidently match. Server-side because the read uses an **API key that must never reach the browser** (KEY DECISIONS) ÔÇö it lives in the `/server` compute layer (Cloud Run), distinct from Supabase. Status mirrors the convert (B238) / Drive (B207) backends: **code complete, fully unit-tested, wired through the UI, gated dormant** until the owner provisions Cloud Run + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + `DOC_FILING_URL`.
- **Title-block reader** `server/filing/titleBlockReader.js` ÔÇö mirrors the client `titleReader.js` request shape (model `claude-opus-4-8`, adaptive thinking, a `json_schema` output, the PDF as a base64 `document` block) but runs **server-side** and calls the Messages API over **raw `fetch` with an injectable `fetchImpl`** (like `server/convert/aps.js`), so the Cloud Run image stays dependency-free and the read is unit-testable without a key. **One read, two payoffs:** the same pass captures the FILING fields (project / number / address / parcel / discipline / sheet # / title / revision / date) AND the PLACEMENT-readiness facts (scale callout + bar, north arrow, drawn boundary, stated coord system, labeled dimensions) in the shape of `src/shared/placement/placementFacts.js`. Pixel-geometry (scale-bar length in px, dimension endpoints) is deliberately left to the later CV step (B268/B183) ÔÇö the read captures presence + printed values, never guessed geometry. `parseStatedScaleFeetPerInch` handles engineer's (`1"=40'`) + architectural (`1/4"=1'-0"`) scales, NTSÔåÆnull.
- **Matcher** `server/filing/matcher.js` (pure) ÔÇö scores each project on parcel/account (exact) ÔåÆ job number (exact) ÔåÆ address (token overlap) ÔåÆ name (token overlap), combined noisy-or; **never auto-guesses** (a misfiled drawing is worse than an unfiled one). A confident SINGLE project ÔåÆ `matched`; **no / multiple / low-confidence** ÔåÆ `needsFiling` with a reason (`no-readable-identifiers` / `no-match` / `ambiguous` / `low-confidence`). Thresholds tunable via env. `scoreProject` is transparent (lists the signal it matched on). Auto-name `server/filing/naming.js` mirrors the canonical `reviewStore.composeTitle` "<Project> - <Item> - YYYY.MM.DD".
- **Service + HTTP** `server/filing/filingService.js` (read ÔåÆ match ÔåÆ decision + the index row) + `server/filing/server.js` (`POST /file` body = PDF bytes, `X-Planyr-Projects` header = base64 JSON; `GET /health`) ÔÇö honest status codes (200 real decision / 400 / 413 / 422 unreadable / 503 not-configured), **never a 200 with a junk decision**. `Dockerfile` (no native binary, no npm install ÔÇö built-ins only) + `README.md`. The reader/service/server are injectable end-to-end (mirrors `dwgConvert.test.js`).
- **File-facts index (Supabase Postgres, NOT /server)** ÔÇö `src/workspaces/doc-review/db/file_facts.sql`: `public.file_facts` (one small row per filed drawing: project / discipline / sheet / revision / date / match confidence / needs-filing / placement jsonb), RLS private-by-default identical in shape to `doc_reviews`, indexed `(user_id, project_id, discipline, doc_date desc)` for the north-star "map ÔåÆ project ÔåÆ discipline ÔåÆ latest set" without re-reading the PDF. Client I/O `reviewStore.upsertFileFacts/listFileFacts` (degrade gracefully if unmigrated) + pure helpers `lib/fileIndex.js` (`toFactsRow`, `mergeFactsIntoReviews` ÔÇö surfaces captured placement on the drawer's review rows).
- **capturePlacementFacts wired (deliverable #4)** ÔÇö `src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/autofiling.js` is the real `createIndexProvider`: `capturePlacementFacts` + `autofile` ride the SAME server read. `backendReady` reflects `VITE_AUTOFILE_ENABLED` honestly (false by default ÔåÆ UI never claims "auto-detected" while dormant). Same-origin proxy `functions/api/file.js` (verifies the Supabase session, forwards to Cloud Run `DOC_FILING_URL`; 503 until set). The drawer drop handler auto-files when `backendReady`, else the **exact** prior manual path (a 404/503 is a graceful SKIP, like the Drive path) ÔÇö **no regression**.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 602 tests (52 new: `test/docFiling.test.js` 37, `test/autofiling.test.js` 15) ┬À build green; doc-review lazy-chunk split intact.** Headless on the built app (`ui-audit/verify-b299-autofiling.mjs`): the Project Files drawer opens, the new `autofilingProvider`/`fileIndex` imports evaluate in a real browser, **0 page/console errors**, auto-filing dormant by default (no spurious "it files itself" behavior). The signed-in dropÔåÆreadÔåÆfile round-trip + the live read are the deploy-gated checks (sandbox blocks sign-in + no key) ÔÇö logged as **VERIFICATION V74**.
- **Dedup:** this is the backend that fills the `createIndexProvider.capturePlacementFacts` seam B180/B181 stubbed and the auto-filing roadmap item; it WRITES THROUGH the Drive substrate (B207) and feeds the placement cascade (B182/B183). Distinct from B266/B267/B268 (the in-Markup sheet-label/scale siblings of the read pass) ÔÇö those share the title-block read principle but run browser-side on the open sheet; this is the filing index. **Owner to-do (deploy, when ready):** `gcloud run deploy` `server/filing/` + set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (server-side) + point `DOC_FILING_URL` at it + set `VITE_AUTOFILE_ENABLED=1` + run `db/file_facts.sql` once.

<!-- 2026-06-20: filed from chat AND shipped same session (branch claude/eloquent-edison-6anmlb). Owner-dropped
     pair "NEW-1"/"NEW-2" (account names + identity pill). Blind to this file ÔåÆ first labeled B271/B272, but
     concurrent `main` had consumed everything through **B296** (pointer-lockout B271, main-thread-stall B272,
     then the B273ÔÇôB287 + B288ÔÇôB296 GIS / Doc-Review-Markup runs), so per the backlog rule these were
     renumbered to the real next free IDs **B297/B298**. **One owner action: run the migration**
     `src/workspaces/site-planner/db/profiles.sql` once in the Supabase SQL editor (creates the profiles
     table + trigger + RLS + backfill). -->

### B297 ÔÇö First/last name capture at signup + a real `profiles` table `[Auth / Backend]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; first labeled B271, renumbered **B297** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B271ÔÇôB296)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eloquent-edison-6anmlb`).** Names are now persisted in a queryable **`public.profiles`** table (one row per `auth.uid()`), not just in auth `user_metadata` ÔÇö the scalable foundation for the B2B direction (org/role/prefs later). The signup form already collected First/Last/Org; that path is unchanged (still seeds `signUp({ options: { data } })`), but a DB **trigger** now copies those into the table so there's one queryable source of truth for display.
- **Migration (idempotent, run once in the Supabase SQL editor):** `src/workspaces/site-planner/db/profiles.sql` ÔÇö the `profiles` table (`id uuid pk ÔåÆ auth.users on delete cascade`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `org`, `updated_at`); **RLS private-by-default** (the same select/insert/update own-row shape as `public.sites`/`public.doc_reviews`, `auth.uid() = id`, no cross-user reads, no delete ÔÇö the row lives/dies with the user); a `handle_new_user()` **SECURITY DEFINER trigger** on `auth.users insert` that inserts the matching row from `raw_user_meta_data` (the trigger route avoids the client-side follow-up-insert race ÔÇö the row always exists before the user can read it); and a **backfill** that seeds rows for all existing users (null names are fine ÔÇö the UI falls back, see B298). `on conflict (id) do nothing` throughout so re-running is safe.
- **Client I/O + hook (additive, reuses the existing anon client + session ÔÇö no new keys):** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/profile.js` (`loadProfile`/`saveProfile`, mirrors `cloudSync.js` shape; throws on a real fetch error so "no row" Ôëá "offline"; `saveProfile` also best-effort syncs the name into auth `user_metadata` so the table and the auth record never disagree and the offline fallback stays fresh) and `src/shared/profile/useProfile.js` ÔÇö the `useProfile(user)` hook returning `{ profile, loading, displayName, firstName, org, initial, reload, save }`, with a stale-fetch guard (a slow load can't show the previous user's profile) and an optimistic local update on save so the pill refreshes instantly.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 572 tests (562 + 10 new) ┬À build green.** The never-blank display chain is pure-extracted (`displayNameFor`/`firstNameFor`/`orgFor`/`initialFor`) and unit-tested in `test/profileDisplay.test.js` (profile wins over metadata; First Last ÔåÆ first ÔåÆ last ÔåÆ metadata ÔåÆ email; whitespace-only trims to email; empty only when there's no user). The actual cloud round-trip (real profiles table read/write while signed in) needs a signed-in click-through ÔÇö the sandbox proxy CORS-blocks auth ÔÇö logged as **VERIFICATION V73**.
> **Dedup:** builds on the existing email/password auth (Supabase Phases 2ÔÇô4) and the `AuthPanel` signup form (which already had the name fields). Distinct from the Site Model `status` reuse (B14) ÔÇö this is per-*user* identity, not per-*project* lifecycle. The deferred `planarfit:* ÔåÆ planyr:*` localStorage rename is untouched (separate item).

### B298 ÔÇö Identity pill shows the user's name + an account dropdown `[App Header]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; renumbered **B298**, next free ID after B297)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eloquent-edison-6anmlb`).** The Row-1 account control is now a **name pill that opens an account dropdown** instead of jumping straight to the auth modal. Name comes from the `useProfile` hook (B297); the display is **never blank** ÔÇö "First Last" ÔåÆ first name alone ÔåÆ last name alone ÔåÆ signup metadata ÔåÆ email.
- **Dropdown (reuses `AnchoredMenu`, the same portal menu primitive as the project breadcrumb ÔÇö escapes the header's stacking/clipping context, lines up under the pill, white panel for consistency):** an identity header (avatar + name + **organization** when present + email) then **Profile**, **Settings**, **Sign out** (each with a small line icon). Sign out acts directly from the menu; Profile/Settings open the account modal on the matching tab. Logged-out, the pill stays a one-click **"Sign in"** (no empty dropdown). **Esc closes the menu** ÔÇö added to `AnchoredMenu` itself, so every consumer (account menu, breadcrumb, rail flyouts) gets it.
- **Account modal upgraded (`AuthPanel.jsx`) to a tabbed Profile/Settings panel** ÔÇö *consolidate, don't duplicate*: **Profile** edits First/Last/Org (saved to the `profiles` table via `useProfile.save`, with an optimistic pill refresh; email shown read-only); **Settings** hosts **Change password** (reusing the existing `updatePassword` flow ÔÇö previously only reachable logged-out via "Forgot password") + the cloud-sync note. Sign out is always available in the panel footer too. (Theme control, "NEW-3", is **not** in this task ÔÇö Settings is structured so it drops in there when it lands; no dead/placeholder UI was shipped.)
- **Verified headless ÔÇö `ui-audit/verify-b297-b298.mjs`, 14/14, 0 JS errors.** Seeded a well-formed local Supabase session (far-future expiry ÔÇö supabase-js reads the session from localStorage with no network, sidestepping the sandbox's CORS block) to drive the **real signed-in UI**: the pill shows "Mike Abbott"; the dropdown opens with Profile/Settings/Sign out + the email + the organization; **Esc closes it**; Profile opens the modal pre-filled (First/Last/Org) with "Save profile"; Settings shows "Change password". Logged-out: the "Sign in" pill opens the modal ÔåÆ Sign up shows the First/Last fields. Screenshots captured. lint 0 ┬À 572 tests ┬À build green; the workspace lazy-chunk split is unaffected (shell-level change). The real cloud profile save while signed in is the same **V73** signed-in click-through as B297.
> **Dedup:** the pill already *showed* the metadata name and the modal already had a logged-in "account view" ÔÇö this replaces the click-opens-modal behavior with a real dropdown and re-points the name at the profiles table. Reuses `AnchoredMenu` (B127) rather than a new popover.

### B282 ÔÇö Opening a file from the global Project Files panel no-ops on the first click when launched from another workspace `[Doc Review]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-20, repro'd in the signed-in session; arrived as "NEW-1"; first filed B270 then renumbered **B282** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B270ÔÇôB281 (incl. a different B270, the Project Files drop-zone queue) while this was in flight, so B282 is the real next free ID. "NEW-1" is only a scratch label ÔÇö B180/B206/B266/B270 also "arrived as NEW-1".)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/wonderful-galileo-abbbus`).** From the Site side, opening the global **­ƒùé Files** panel (with a project like Jacintoport open) and clicking a filed document (e.g. the MEP set) switched to the **Markup** tab but landed on the empty **"Open or drop a construction PDF"** placeholder ÔÇö the document did **not** open; it stayed empty even after a multi-second wait, with **no console errors** (silent). The breadcrumb also reset to "Select a project". Clicking the **same file a second time** (now that Document Review was mounted) opened it correctly. 
- **Root cause (confirmed via the repro).** Document Review is a **lazy-loaded workspace** ÔÇö the Shell renders only the active workspace (`React.lazy`/`Suspense`), so DR's code doesn't mount until you first enter the tab. The **global** Project Files drawer lives in the Site Planner (`SitePlanner.jsx`), and its open handler was `onOpenReview={() => onShellSwitch?.("doc-review")}` ÔÇö it **switched workspaces but threw away the clicked review row entirely**. So in the same tick the app switched to DR *and* dropped the open request; DR mounted fresh and booted to its default/resume state, never opening the clicked file. The second click worked because DR was by then already mounted and its **own** in-workspace drawer wires `onOpenReview={openReview}` (the real handler). (The in-workspace path was never broken ÔÇö only the cross-workspace one.)
- **Fix ÔÇö persist the open-intent across the mount, mirroring the existing `navIntent` pattern.** New **`docIntent`** channel in `Shell.jsx`: `openReviewInDocReview(row)` stashes a **token-stamped** `{ kind:"open-review", row }` and *then* switches to `doc-review`; the Shell passes `docIntent` + `onOpenReviewInDocReview` to every workspace (parallel to the `navIntent`/Dashboard plumbing). The Site Planner's global drawer now calls `onOpenReview={(row) => onOpenReviewInDocReview?.(row)}` (threaded `SitePlannerApp` ÔåÆ `SitePlanner`), handing the **whole review row** up. `DocReview` consumes the pending intent in a mount/update effect (`openReview(row)`), guarded by a **module-scoped `lastConsumedDocToken`** (same technique as `SitePlannerApp`'s `lastConsumedNavToken`) so a plain tab switch-back never re-fires the last open.
- **No race with resume.** DR's boot effect (resume-the-last-review) now **stands down** when a fresh open-intent is present at mount (captured once via `bootDocIntentRef`), so the two async loads can't fight and silently land the wrong file.
- **Breadcrumb carries through.** `markupProject` is **seeded from the fresh intent** at mount (handling both a `listReviews` row's `project_id` and a loaded record's `projectId`), and `openReview` re-confirms it after load ÔÇö so the project context no longer flashes to "Select a project".
- **Can't fail silently again.** `openReview` is hardened: it wraps `loadReview` in try/catch and, when the row can't be loaded (removed row / cloud unreachable), shows a **visible dismissible error banner** ("Couldn't open ÔÇª") with a **Browse FilesÔÇª** retry, instead of the previous bare `if (!rec) return`.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 550 tests ┬À build green; the DocReview / SitePlannerApp / ProjectFilesDrawer lazy chunks all still split (workspace isolation intact).** Headless smoke on the **built** app (`ui-audit/verify-new1.mjs`): Document Review **mounts and remounts cleanly** with the new `docIntent` prop + ref capture + intent effect + hardened `openReview` + error banner ÔÇö **zero JS errors** on a fresh mount AND after an unmount/remount. The signed-in **first-click open** itself (global Files ÔåÆ click a real filed set ÔåÆ DR opens it on the *first* click, breadcrumb shows the project) is **auth-gated** ÔÇö the sandbox proxy blocks sign-in and this build has no Supabase creds, so the panel can't list real files headless ÔÇö logged for a live signed-in confirm on planyr.io as **VERIFICATION V70**.
> **Dedup:** net-new ÔÇö the only cross-workspace "open a file into Document Review" handler is the global `ProjectFilesDrawer` rendered by `SitePlanner` (the in-workspace DR drawer + `ReviewsBar` + `ProjectLibrary` already call `openReview` directly and were unaffected). Distinct from **B180** (the Files tagged-index/saved-views feature this rides on) and from the silent-no-op family (**B82** calibrate, **B32** undo, the map-click open in B66) ÔÇö those are unrelated surfaces. Reuses the **B191ÔÇôB193** `navIntent` cross-workspace pattern rather than inventing a new mechanism. Distinct from the concurrently-landed **B270** (Project Files drop-zone + processing queue) ÔÇö same `ProjectFilesDrawer` file, unrelated concern (file ingestion vs. cross-workspace open).

<!-- 2026-06-20: owner-dropped chat batch NEW-1..NEW-5 (Site Planner GIS layers ÔÇö coverage detection +
     a coverage-aware picker + the Mapillary rename/gating + jurisdiction-vector retry). Concurrent `main`
     consumed B274ÔÇôB282 (save-concurrency, overlay delete/visibility, telemetry/CI, Project Files first-click) while this was in
     flight, so per the backlog rule this batch took the real next-free IDs **B283ÔÇôB287** (filed AND
     shipped same session, branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`). Root motivation: the City-of-Houston utility
     layers load green
     (HTTP 200) but paint blank outside the city, which read as "broken" ÔÇö verified live 2026-06-20 they
     work fine over Houston. NEW-5 deduped against the existing retry/backoff (raster tiles `withTileRetry`,
     metadata probe `fetchWithRetry`): the gap was the esri-leaflet FeatureServer *query* itself. -->

### B283 ÔÇö Coverage detection engine + honest per-layer status states `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; minted **B283** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B273)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** New **`lib/coverage.js`** engine answers, per layer, whether its DATA reaches the current view ÔÇö so a layer that loads green but paints blank outside its region reads as "no data here," not "broken" (the COH water/sewer/storm root cause). Each layer is tagged **national | statewide | regional** (`LAYER_SCOPE`). For a **regional** layer the engine reads the service's own **`fullExtent` from the existing `?f=json` health probe** (`probeService` now captures it ÔÇö **no extra request**), reprojects it to lat/lon, buffers it by the nearby radius (NEW-2/B284), and intersects it with the viewport ÔåÆ **`in` / `out` / `unknown`**. The three honest states (`displayCoverage`): **data-in-view**, **covers-region-but-empty-here**, **out-of-coverage**. National/statewide are **always in-coverage** (their empty/painted state still comes from the in-view query). **Fails open everywhere** ÔÇö missing extent or errored probe ÔåÆ treated as available, never hidden.
- **Real reprojection (the crux):** services publish their extent in mixed SRs ÔÇö verified live 2026-06-20 that **HCFCD/City-of-Houston use EPSG:2278 (Texas State-Plane, US ft)** while AGOL layers use **Web Mercator (3857)**. So the shared `src/shared/coordinates/index.js` stub's reserved **`projectToGrid`/`gridToProject`** are now **implemented** (Lambert Conformal Conic 2SP per EPSG GN 7-2), **validated against pyproj to <1e-4┬░**; `coverage.js` dispatches 4326 / Web-Mercator / 2278 and reprojects all 4 extent corners to safely enclose the conic footprint. This is a read-only screening use ÔÇö the planner keeps its own per-site feet frame.
- **HARD RULE honoured + unit-tested:** coverage is a **picker-only** signal and **never** alters a layer's request bbox/where/extent. The request-spec builders live in a leaflet-free **`lib/layerRequest.js`** that takes **no coverage input by construction**; `test/coverage.test.js` asserts the COH request always carries its **full pinned sublayer set [0,1] and no where/bbox**, coverage-independent.
- **Wired** into the map finder + planner (recompute coverage on map move / view change, debounced, + when the nearby-range pref changes; regional extents prefetched once via the health probe and cached). **Tests:** `coverage.test.js` (27) + projection assertions in `coordinates.test.js` + `layerRequest.test.js` ÔÇö **609 tests green**, lint 0, build green, lazy chunks intact. **Verified live headless** (`gis-verify/coverage-picker-verify.mjs`, V71): panning north out of Houston flipped **HCFCD (State-Plane path) + H-GAC ETJ (Web-Mercator path)** to "No data in this area," 0 JS errors.

### B284 ÔÇö Coverage-aware layer picker: relevance modes + nearby range `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) ÔÇö depends on B283  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; minted **B284**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** The Layers panel (`components/LayerPanel.jsx`) now consumes B283's coverage. A **"Relevance"** control ÔÇö **Show all / Dim / Hide** (**Dim** is the default) ÔÇö plus an adjustable **"nearby range"** slider (default **2.5 mi**, the buffer fed back into B283) decide how out-of-coverage layers are presented:
- **Dim:** out-of-coverage layers sink to the bottom of their group, grayed, with a **"No data in this area"** caption ÔÇö still one-click toggleable.
- **Hide:** they collapse behind a **"Show N layers with no local data here"** toggle (reveal shows them dimmed).
- A layer that is currently **ON is never demoted** (you always see what you've enabled). The mode + radius are **shared across both the map-finder and planner panels and persisted** (`coverage.js` prefs with pub/sub, `planarfit:relevance:v1`), so a change in one panel reflects in the other live.
- **List ordering/visibility ONLY ÔÇö never the rendered map** (the map is driven by `overlays` + `syncOverlayLayers`, untouched). `normalizeMode`/`normalizeRadius` clamp + default; unit-tested. **Verified live** (V71): the control + slider render, Dim is default, and the dim/"No data in this area" treatment fires on a real pan out of region.

### B285 ÔÇö Rename "Street-level detections (Mapillary)" to plain language `[Site Planner / GIS]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-3" 2026-06-20; minted **B285**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** The layer is renamed **"Poles & hydrants from street imagery"** with the sublabel **"Detected in crowdsourced street-level photos. Needs a free access token."**; the **"Mapillary"** brand (which the owner didn't recognize) is **demoted to a small `Source: Mapillary` attribution note** (`EVIDENCE.mapillary` in `layers.js`; LayerPanel renders the new `sublabel` + `source`). **Verified live** (V71): the new name + sublabel + source render and the old "Street-level detections" name is gone.

### B286 ÔÇö Mapillary layer was inert ÔÇö gated clearly as "needs setup" + excluded from the default set `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-4" 2026-06-20; minted **B286**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** The layer fired zero requests + showed a red "failed"-style "Add a Mapillary token" message as shipped ÔÇö it never worked. **Chose option (b): keep it, but gate it honestly** (not (a) bake a token ÔÇö that violates the secrets rule and ships a shared rate-limited key; not (c) remove ÔÇö its detections can be richer/fresher than OSM later, and the owner can have it gone with one word). A new **`needsSetup` flag** marks it as **not part of the default working set** (the relevance picker sorts a tokenless instance to the bottom as "Needs setup"), and the no-token state now reads **"Not configured ÔÇö add a free access token to enable this layer"** via a distinct **`unconfigured`** status (a gray "needs setup" dot, **not** the alarming red "failed") in `syncOverlayLayers` + `evidenceLayers.mapillaryLayer` ÔÇö and **no error toast** fires. **Note:** poles already come from OSM and hydrants from OSM + City-of-Houston, so this layer is redundant-but-optional today; say the word and it's removed entirely. **Verified live** (V71): toggling it (no token) reads "Not configured", no red failure.

### B287 ÔÇö Retry/backoff for transient 5xx on jurisdiction VECTOR services (City ETJ, County boundaries) `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-5" 2026-06-20; minted **B287**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/busy-allen-ofgy9l`).** Live 2026-06-20 the City ETJ (H-GAC FeatureServer) and County-boundaries (TxDOT/AGOL) layers threw intermittent **503s on their geojson queries**. **Deduped against the existing retry/backoff** and found the gap: raster tiles already retry via `withTileRetry` and the `?f=json` metadata probe via `fetchWithRetry`, but **esri-leaflet's FeatureLayer issues its own GeoJSON query with no retry** ÔÇö so one transient blip dropped the whole vector layer to "failed." New **`attachFeatureRetry`** (`layers.js`) wires a pure **`featureRetryDecision`** policy (`layerRequest.js`) onto every `esriFeature` layer: on a `requesterror` it re-issues via `refresh()` with **exponential backoff** (400ÔåÆ800ÔåÆ1600 ms), holds the dot at "loading" while retrying, reports "failed" **only after `max` retries**, and resets on a successful `load`; a **hard 4xx is permanent** (no retry). The transient set (`429, 500, 502, 503, 504`) is now **one shared constant** (`TRANSIENT_STATUS`) across raster/metadata/vector. **9 unit tests** (`layerRequest.test.js`); lint 0 ┬À 609 tests ┬À build green.

### B279 ÔÇö Production error telemetry ÔåÆ Supabase table `[Infra / Telemetry]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20 owner chat; filed **B279** in the B278ÔÇôB281 Infra tranche ÔÇö jumped past B271ÔÇôB277 consumed by concurrent `main` + PR #217; batch comment lives in BACKLOG.md)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/trusting-hawking-1c4d6g`).** The app now self-reports runtime errors so silent production failures stop being invisible until someone stumbles on them. New **`src/shared/telemetry/clientErrors.js`**: three global sources ÔÇö window `error`, window `unhandledrejection`, and Vite's `vite:preloadError` ÔÇö plus the React **ErrorBoundary** (`componentDidCatch`) all funnel into one fire-and-forget sink that inserts a row into a new **`public.client_errors`** table via the EXISTING anon Supabase client (the "Supabase table, not Sentry" decision ÔÇö no new vendor, no new keys). Installed in `main.jsx` before render; the Shell tags each row with the active workspace (`setTelemetryModule`).
- **Session-optional + no read hole (RLS):** `src/shared/telemetry/client_errors.sql` makes the table **INSERT-only** for `anon` + `authenticated` (no SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE policy), so an error **during login ÔÇö before any session exists ÔÇö still records**, and clients can write but can never read the table back (admins read via the dashboard / service role). `user_id` defaults to `auth.uid()` (null when anonymous); `WITH CHECK (user_id is null or user_id = auth.uid())` blocks forging another user's id. **Run the SQL once in the Supabase SQL editor** (idempotent; same pattern as `doc_reviews.sql`).
- **Fail-safe + storm guard:** every path swallows its own errors and never rethrows into the app (telemetry that throws is worse than none). `decideReport` (pure, unit-tested) suppresses an exact-duplicate signature within 10 s and caps sends to 20/min, so a render-loop error storm becomes a handful of rows, not thousands. Messages/stacks truncated (2 k / 8 k). NOT capture-phase, so blocked tiles / broken images don't spam it.
- **Build provenance:** `vite.config.js` bakes the short git SHA into the bundle (`__BUILD_ID__` define, read via a typeof guard ÔåÆ "dev" under dev/test) and stamps it on every row, so a report traces back to the exact deploy.
- **Dedup (the filing's own correction held):** the `vite:preloadError` listener it "pairs with" already existed and shipped (**B221** `installChunkReloadGuard`, `src/app/chunkReload.js`); this **adds** a telemetry write for that event through its OWN listener (chunkReload.js left untouched + pure). NET-NEW otherwise ÔÇö no prior telemetry / Sentry item in either file.
- **Verification:** lint **0 errors** (only the pre-existing `SitePlanner.jsx`/`MapFinder.jsx` warnings); **587 tests** (12 new in `test/clientErrors.test.js` ÔÇö storm-guard dup/rate/window, row shaping, message/stack extraction + truncation, signature); `npm run build` green, all three workspace lazy chunks intact, `__BUILD_ID__` fully replaced (no leftover token). **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-telemetry.mjs`, **6/6** on the built bundle via `vite preview`, re-run clean after merging the latest `main`): all three global sources captured with the correct `source` tag, a duplicate suppressed, build id baked in, and the page stays alive after firing synthetic errors (fail-safe). The live **cloud insert** (signed-in, after the one-time SQL) is the one auth-only path the sandbox can't exercise logged-out ÔåÆ logged in **VERIFICATION V69**.
> Independent of the B278 (Playwright) ID-collision concern; built + shipped this same session per the owner's "ship ship and then build whatever you need to." B278 / B280 / B281 stay Open (CI/secret-bound ÔÇö see their `Ôøö Verify-blocker` notes).

### B277 ÔÇö Overlay visibility toggle (hide without deleting) `[Document Review / Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-4" 2026-06-20; minted **B277** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B270ÔÇôB273 while this was in flight; batch B274ÔÇôB277)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/peaceful-ride-k41y6e`).** Added a per-overlay eye / eye-slash toggle to the Site-plan overlay panel (`SitePlanner.jsx`) so a placed overlay can be switched OFF the map without deleting it ÔÇö the standard layers-UI affordance (Bluebeam/ArcGIS/Photoshop). Distinct from B272's delete (hide Ôëá delete): a hidden overlay stays in the panel, listed + recoverable.
- **Render:** the canvas overlay map skips an overlay with `visible === false` (returns null) ÔÇö off the map, still in the panel with the eye-slash to bring it back. **Persistence:** `visible` rides in the overlay record (saved + cloud-slimmed + content-merged like every other overlay field), so a hidden overlay stays hidden across reload (and, signed in, across devices); it's also folded into the undo/redo signature so a hide/show is undoable.
- **Data shape was already reserved (B131):** `isOverlayPrintable = o.src && o.visible !== false` was shipped "forward-compatible with a future per-overlay hide," so hiding an overlay **also drops it from Print/PNG export automatically** (WYSIWYG) ÔÇö no extra wiring. Inline `EyeIcon`/`EyeOffIcon` SVGs render crisply everywhere.
- **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-overlay-delete-hide.mjs`, the built app, logged-out): hide removes the overlay from the map but KEEPS it listed; the hidden state PERSISTS across reload (the record carries `visible:false`); show brings it back. lint 0 ┬À 549 tests ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp`/`DocReview` lazy chunks intact.
> **Dedup:** B131 (print-overlay master toggle) reserved the `visible` semantics; this implements the per-overlay UI toggle it anticipated. Pairs with B276 (delete persistence) ÔÇö same overlay surface, shipped together. (B314/B313, the data-integrity pair from the same batch, shipped this session too.)

### B276 ÔÇö Overlay delete doesn't persist across reload `[Document Review / Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-20, "Jacinto Port"; arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B276** ÔÇö concurrent `main` consumed B270ÔÇôB273; batch B274ÔÇôB277)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/peaceful-ride-k41y6e`).** Deleting a placed overlay (the owner's "Jacinto Port" sheet) made it vanish, but it RETURNED on reload. **Root cause (as the backlog predicted):** the cross-copy reconciler `mergeSiteContent` (`siteModel.js`) **unions `sheetOverlays` by id**, so when the post-delete local copy (overlay gone) is merged with a copy that still has it ÔÇö the cloud copy on the next `pullCloud`/`mergePulledSites`, or another tab via the `storage` fold ÔÇö the deleted overlay is **resurrected**. Exactly the documented B126 union-merge trade-off ("a delete in only one copy can reappear once ÔÇö per-item tombstones are the fully-correct future hardening"); `removeOverlay` only dropped it from local state, nothing recorded that the deletion was deliberate.
- **Fix ÔÇö per-item tombstones (`deletedIds`), the B126 hardening, now built:** an additive Site Model field (`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 5ÔåÆ6) holding ids the user deliberately deleted. `mergeSiteContent` unions the `deletedIds` from BOTH copies and **drops any tombstoned id from every unioned collection**, so a deletion wins over presence in either copy ÔÇö across reload, tab-sync, and device-sync. `removeOverlay` records the tombstone; it's threaded through the save effect, the live/undo refs, the cross-tab `storage` fold, `applySnapshot`, and version-restore so it persists and undo correctly resurrects (the tombstone rolls back with the overlay). Bounded + deduped (Ôëñ5000 ids).
- **General but scoped:** the mechanism works for any collection (a tombstoned building stays deleted too) but is wired only at the overlay delete site for now; other delete handlers can adopt `setDeletedIds` trivially. **No regression:** collections not yet recording a tombstone keep the existing recoverable union behavior; `slimForCloud`/`slimForHistory` pass `deletedIds` through untouched (only inline rasters are stripped). **Composes with B297** (optimistic concurrency, shipped this session): a delete already rides the normal save, so it'll advance B297's version like any edit.
- **Verified:** unit ÔÇö `test/storage.test.js` (6 new B276 cases: tombstone in either copy wins, set-union, no-regression on non-tombstoned items, generalizes to a building, end-to-end through `mergePulledSites`). Headless ÔÇö `ui-audit/verify-overlay-delete-hide.mjs` (built app, logged-out): delete persists across reload and the record carries the `deletedIds` tombstone. lint 0 ┬À **549 tests** ┬À build green; lazy chunks intact.
> **Dedup:** net-new bug; the fix is the per-item tombstone B126 explicitly deferred. Distinct from B260 (overlay *scale* misread, done). Pairs with B277 (visibility), shipped together.
### B313 ÔÇö Multi-tab open warning (same project open in another browser tab) `[App Shell]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; minted **B313** ÔÇö renumbered B275ÔåÆB298ÔåÆB313 as a very hot `main` reached B311; pairs with B314 + the already-live B276/B277)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/peaceful-ride-k41y6e`).** A non-blocking header banner now warns when the SAME project is open in another tab of the same browser ÔÇö "This project is open in another tab. Editing it in more than one tab can conflict ÔÇö work in a single tab to be safe." ÔÇö so a two-tab clash isn't a surprise. Same-browser only; cross-DEVICE conflicts are B314's server guard.
- **How:** `src/shared/presence/multiTab.js` ÔÇö a BroadcastChannel (same-origin cross-tab bus) presence tracker. Each tab announces its open project (`hello`/`here`/`update`/`bye` + a heartbeat); a pure `summarizePresence` reduces the live peer set into the banner state (conflict only when another tab is on the *same* project; stale peers pruned by TTL). Wired into the shared `AppHeader` via a `useMultiTab(projectId)` hook (BOTH workspaces get it); the banner clears the moment the other tab closes/navigates (its `bye`, or the TTL). Degrades to a silent no-op where BroadcastChannel is unavailable.
- **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-multitab.mjs`, 4/4, the built app, two real tabs in one browser context): Tab A alone shows nothing; opening Tab B on the same project raises the banner in BOTH; closing Tab B clears A's banner. Plus **10 unit tests** (`test/multiTab.test.js`) incl. a real two-instance BroadcastChannel round-trip (same-project ÔåÆ conflict; different project ÔåÆ present-but-no-conflict; clears on leave). lint 0 ┬À build green; lazy chunks intact.
> **Dedup:** the VISIBLE companion to B127 (which made two same-browser tabs silently *converge*) ÔÇö surfaces the situation, doesn't replace the merge. **Optional, deliberately not built:** the Web Locks single-active-editor *lock* (non-leader tabs go read-only) ÔÇö the spec marked it optional; the banner is the required warning, the read-only lockout is an invasive follow-up. Pairs with B314 (the cross-device half).

### B314 ÔÇö Reject stale saves (optimistic concurrency on the save path) `[Platform / Persistence]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; minted **B314** ÔÇö renumbered B274ÔåÆB297ÔåÆB314 as a very hot `main` reached B311; pairs with B313)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/peaceful-ride-k41y6e`); migration RUN by owner 2026-06-20 ÔåÆ goes ACTIVE on deploy.** Two sessions editing the same project no longer silently last-write-wins: a save carries the `version` it last synced, and the DB applies it ONLY if the stored version still matches (a single atomic conditional UPDATE), bumping it. A stale write (another session advanced the row) is **REJECTED as a conflict**, not applied ÔÇö the Site Planner shows a loud blue "this project was changed in another session ÔÇö reload before saving" banner with a **Reload** button. No silent clobber; work is never lost (the edit is saved on-device, and the reload union-merges it via mergeSiteContent, then re-pushes the combined result).
- **Reusable primitive (foundation for the deferred multi-user feature):** `src/shared/cloud/optimisticUpsert.js` ÔÇö a pure `casUpsert` compare-and-swap over `(user_id, id, version)`, used by BOTH `public.sites` (cloudSync.js) and `public.doc_reviews` (reviewStore.js). Version tokens are tracked per-tab in the cloud layer (populated on list/load, advanced on each write, cleared on delete / user-switch), so a conflict propagates back as a typed `{ conflict:true }` the UI turns into the reload prompt. Doc Review surfaces it on its save badge (the persistence hook gains a `conflict` state).
- **Graceful degradation = merge-safe:** before the migration ran, the conditional write returned "column version does not exist" and the client fell back to a plain upsert (today's EXACT behaviour) ÔÇö so saving never regressed; the guard was simply dormant. (The detector requires the error name the *version* column, so doc_reviews' optional library columns can't be mistaken for it and wrongly disable the guard.)
- **Verified:** **16 unit tests** (`test/cloudConcurrency.test.js`) cover the whole matrix ÔÇö conflict (0 rows), success (+version), degrade (un-migrated), brand-new insert, unique-violationÔåÆconflict, never-throws, and the not-the-version-column guard. The live cloud conflict path needs sign-in (the sandbox blocks auth logged-out), so it's logged for a signed-in click-through (VERIFICATION V79). lint 0 ┬À build green; lazy chunks intact.
> **Ô£à MIGRATION RUN (owner, 2026-06-20):** `src/workspaces/site-planner/db/optimistic_concurrency.sql` was run in the Supabase SQL editor (added the `version` column to `sites` + `doc_reviews`). The guard goes ACTIVE on this deploy. Composes with B276's tombstones (a delete advances the version like any write).

### B271 ÔÇö Canvas could get stuck unclickable behind a frozen grab/hand cursor when a pointer gesture was interrupted `[Core / Site Planner + Doc Review]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-20 via chat, with a screenshot of the "Claude is debugging this browser" banner; arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B271** ÔÇö real next free ID after concurrent `main` consumed B270)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/gifted-lamport-miamoc`).** Owner hit a state where the whole app went unclickable ÔÇö a hand cursor over everything, clicks doing nothing ÔÇö while every other website kept working. It coincided with a browser devtools/remote-debugger session attaching to the tab (the on-screen "Claude is debugging this browser" banner), but the fragility is **ours**: Planyr was the only page that locked up, so a latent bug in our pointer handling is the real cause (the attachment merely exposed it).
- **Root cause.** The planner canvas (`SitePlanner.jsx`) starts a pan/drag by capturing the pointer (`setPointerCapture`) and setting `panning` + `drag.current`, and relied **only** on `onPointerUp` (`onUp`) to release capture and clear that state. But the browser fires **`pointercancel`** (not `pointerup`) when a gesture is interrupted ÔÇö a devtools/remote-debugger session attaching, an OS gesture takeover, or the window losing focus mid-drag ÔÇö and the canvas had **no `onPointerCancel` handler and no blur/visibility recovery**. So the cleanup never ran: `panning` stayed `true` (cursor stuck on the select-mode grab/grabbing hand) with pointer-capture still held ÔåÆ the canvas swallowed every click. Other sites don't hold global pan/drag state, which is exactly why only Planyr broke. The same capture-pan pattern (same latent lockup) existed in the Doc Review **Stitcher** and the **ParcelDrawing** canvas.
- **Fix ÔÇö four independent recovery paths where there were zero** (`SitePlanner.jsx`, `doc-review/Stitcher.jsx`, `site-planner/components/ParcelDrawing.jsx`):
  1. **`onPointerCancel` on each canvas** ÔåÆ an `abortGesture()` that releases pointer-capture (via `e.pointerId`) and clears `drag.current` / `panning` / the in-progress marquee + draw drafts (it **aborts**, never commits a half-finished gesture).
  2. **Window `blur` + `visibilitychange`(hidden) recovery** ÔåÆ same teardown, **plus** it drops the Space hold-to-pan flag (`spaceRef`/`spacePan`) so a missed Space key-up can't leave the hand cursor on. Mirrors the existing Shift-reset-on-blur effect.
  3. **Escape** now also tears the gesture down (release capture + clear `drag`/`panning`/`spacePan`) ÔÇö a manual safety hatch on top of the draft/menu reset it already did.
  4. **`onBgDown`/`onUp` bookkeeping** ÔÇö record the captured pointer id (`capturePidRef`) so blur/Escape can release a capture they didn't see start, cleared on normal release.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 543 tests ┬À build green; lazy chunks (`SitePlannerApp`/`DocReview`) intact.** New headless test `ui-audit/verify-pointer-recover.mjs` drives a **real** pointer on the **built** app: a pan shows `grabbing`; **window blur** mid-pan recovers the cursor to `grab`; a **`pointercancel`** mid-pan recovers to `grab`; the canvas is still interactive afterward (a fresh pan re-engages); no uncaught errors. Before the fix, both interruptions left `grabbing` stuck (the lockup).
- **Dedup:** folds into the established pointer-capture discipline already in `onUp` (`setPointerCapture`/`releasePointerCapture`), not a parallel mechanism. The "ghost portal overlay" hypothesis was investigated and **disproved** as the cause: the full-canvas inline-editor catchers (`numEdit`/`editCallout`) already commit-and-clear on pointerdown and are bounded by their own state ÔÇö the stuck pointer-capture / stuck-pan path is what produced the lockout. Secondary "main-thread stall" hypothesis tracked separately as **B272** (investigated; no actionable bug; low priority).
<!-- 2026-06-20: filed from chat AND shipped same session (branch claude/quirky-bardeen-xje4nv). Arrived
     as amendments ("Amendment A"/"Amendment B") to a parallel chat's "NEW-1" (the persistent drop zone +
     processing queue). That base "NEW-1" was filed in the OTHER chat and is blind to this file ÔÇö it was
     nowhere in this repo's history (grep across all refs: no "processing queue"/"persistent drop
     zone"/"concurrency pool"; no open PR touching the Project Files drop zone), so per the backlog rule
     ("items pasted from another chat are blind... assign the real next B#") I minted it. It first took
     B260, but concurrent `main` landed B246ÔÇôB269 (the overlay B260, the Drive cutover, and the Doc
     Review B265ÔÇôB269 batch) while this was in flight, so on merge it was renumbered to the real next
     free ID **B270** (highest B# across both files is now B269). The owner's instruction was to fold
     Amendments A & B into NEW-1's spec when assigning its B# ÔÇö done below: the B270 entry IS the
     canonical spec (base reconstructed from the existing ProjectFilesDrawer drop-zone surface + the two
     amendments), with A & B as first-class spec bullets. A future sibling filing of NEW-1 should dedupe
     against B270, not mint a new ID. -->

### B270 ÔÇö Project Files: persistent drop-zone + processing queue (multi-file ingestion + persistent upload tray) `[Site Planner / Document Review ÔÇö Project Files]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" from a parallel chat ÔÇö only Amendments A & B reached this session; minted **B270**, the real next free ID after concurrent `main` consumed B246ÔÇôB269)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/quirky-bardeen-xje4nv`).** Turned the Project Files drop zone (the shared `ProjectFilesDrawer`, opened from Row 1 across workspaces) from a one-file-at-a-time loop that reported errors through a `window.alert` (a KEY-DECISION violation) into a **persistent processing queue with an upload tray**. Pure, browser-free model + derived view in **`src/shared/files/uploadQueue.js`** (`QUEUE_STATUS`, `makeQueueItem`/`makeQueueItems`, `splitQueue` (the derived two-group view), `hasPendingDemote`, `runPool` (concurrency pool), `isAcceptedFile`; **12 unit tests**, `test/uploadQueue.test.js`). UI wiring in `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx` (`UploadTray` + `QueueRow` + the ingest/pipeline/demote logic); demote slide/fade in `index.css`.
- **Folded spec ÔÇö Amendment A (multi-file ingestion is first-class).** Drop, the native file picker (`multiple`), AND clipboard paste all funnel through one `ingest()` that iterates the file list and creates **one independent queue item per file**, each with its own `uploadId`, run through a small **concurrency pool** (3 at a time) ÔÇö so dropping 8 files yields **8 independent concurrent rows**, not one batch row and not a serialized chain. **Mixed types in one action:** PDFs enter the pipeline; unsupported types get a clear **per-file rejection row** (`rejected`, with a Dismiss ├ù) ÔÇö never silently discarded.
- **Folded spec ÔÇö Amendment B (persistent tray, not a vanishing toast).** Filed items do not disappear from under the user. On success a row goes to a calm muted **`done`** state (green check, reduced weight); after a ~3s confirmation beat it **demotes** from the active **PROCESSING** group into a collapsible **"Recently filed ┬À N"** group via a subtle slide/fade ÔÇö never an abrupt removal. The trail **collapses by default once it has more than ~3 entries** and has a **Clear** affordance. `needs_filing` (filed but unrouted ÔÇö a **Triage** button jumps to the holding area's one-click confirm) and `failed` (a **Retry** button) **never auto-dismiss** ÔÇö they stay in the active group until the user acts. **Net:** active group = in-flight + exceptions (live to-do); recently-filed = an auditable, clearable trail. The two groups are a **derived view over one queue array** (filter by status + a `filedAt` timestamp), not two lists ÔÇö the demote is a status/timestamp transition, exactly as the implementation note specified.
- **Graceful degrade kept:** an oversize (>50 MB) or upload-failed file still files the work layer and the row carries a non-fatal "re-drop on open to view" warn (it is `done`, not `failed`); a Drive-copy failure is a soft warn too. "Drive not enabled yet" stays silent (B207 best-effort).
- **Verification:** lint clean on the changed files (the 13 repo warnings are all pre-existing in `SitePlanner.jsx`); **549 tests** (537 + 12) green; `npm run build` green; the doc-review/`ProjectFilesDrawer` lazy chunk split still holds (28 kB chunk). Real-browser headless: (a) the existing `verify-files-refile.mjs` still PASSes (drawer mounts, **0 JS errors** ÔÇö the new imports/components load), and (b) drove a **mixed 3-file pick** through the real component ÔåÆ **"PROCESSING ┬À 3"**, two PDF rows + one **"Not a PDF" rejection row** with Dismiss, Retry buttons, **no `window.alert`** (screenshot captured). The `done`ÔåÆRecently-filed demote / >3 collapse / Clear need a *successful* cloud filing (= sign-in, which the sandbox blocks logged-out) ÔÇö that path is unit-tested (`splitQueue`/`hasPendingDemote`/`RECENT_COLLAPSE_AT`) and logged for a signed-in click-through in VERIFICATION (**V66**).
> **Dedup ÔÇö build on what's shipped, don't fork:** this upgrades **B180**'s drop zone (the saved-views drawer) and reuses **B217**'s "needs filing" holding area + one-click confirm (Triage routes there ÔÇö no second filing UI). **Auto-file-by-title-block** (read title block ÔåÆ match a project ÔåÆ auto-route/auto-name; low-confidence ÔåÆ holding area) is **NOT** this item ÔÇö it stays the **backend tranche** (B180 ÔÅ│ + the auto-filing roadmap item), stubbed behind `createIndexProvider`; B270 is the browser-only ingestion/queue surface it will feed. Filing still files under the active project (or the holding area) until that lands.

### B265 ÔÇö Doc Review (Markup) PDF render is blurry ÔÇö honour devicePixelRatio `[Doc Review / Markup]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; renumbered **B265** as concurrent `main` consumed B246ÔÇôB264; batch B265ÔÇôB269, the other four filed Open: B266/B267/B268/B269)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/friendly-euler-hw8v1t`, PR #205).** Sheets in Markup rendered soft/illegible ÔÇö note text unreadable at fit-to-page, only marginally better zoomed ÔÇö because `renderPageToCanvas` (`src/workspaces/doc-review/lib/pdf.js`) sized the canvas backing store to `(scale ├ù base)` with **no devicePixelRatio factor** and gave the canvas no explicit CSS size, so on a HiDPI/Retina display the browser **upscaled a 1├ù-density bitmap** to fill 2├ù physical pixels = blur. (Same HiDPI class as the Site Planner's B169/B170 basemap fix ÔÇö different surface: the PDF.js canvas, not Leaflet tiles.)
- **Fix:** the page now renders the backing store at `scale ├ù dpr` (`dpr = min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2)`) while the canvas's **on-screen (CSS) size stays at the logical `scale├ù` size** (`canvas.style.width/height`), so the dense bitmap is sampled down ÔåÆ crisp text. A new `backingScale()` caps the backing store to a **~24 MP budget** (drops dpr toward 1├ù on a huge E-size sheet at deep zoom) so the denser render can't OOM, and **never goes below 1├ù** (never worse than before). Zoom already re-renders at the new scale (true re-rasterization); this makes every render dpr-aware.
- **Overlay geometry unchanged by construction:** `renderPageToCanvas` still returns `dims.w/h = floor(base ├ù scale)` ÔÇö byte-identical to before ÔÇö and that's what both the wrapper box and the markup SVG overlay bind to, so measurements/markups land exactly where they did.
- **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new2-dpr.mjs`, the REAL built viewer driven at two pixel densities with a generated 1-page PDF): at **deviceScaleFactor 2** the backing store is **2.000├ù** the CSS size (1054ÔåÆ2108 px) = crisp; at **deviceScaleFactor 1** it's **1.000├ù** (unchanged); the **on-screen size is identical across both densities** (1054├ù1364) ÔåÆ overlay geometry provably unaffected. lint 0 ┬À 543 tests ┬À build green; DocReview + pdf lazy chunks intact. The crispness *gain* only manifests on real HiDPI hardware (headless DPR-forcing proves the 2├ù mechanism); logged for a live retina eyeball as **VERIFICATION V65**.
> **Dedup:** net-new for the Doc Review canvas ÔÇö **B169/B170** (basemap HiDPI/retina) and the map-finder over-zoom fixes are the *Site Planner basemap-tile* siblings of the same principle, not this surface. The Stitcher's `renderPageToImage` is a separate path (fixed 2├ù + a placed-image transform), intentionally left as-is. Filed alongside **B266/B267/B268/B269** (sheet labels, auto-calibrate, scale cross-check, sample-fixture cleanup) which stay Open ÔÇö this was the cleanly-fixable, verifiable one of the batch.

### B260 ÔÇö Dropped site-plan overlay placed 10ÔÇô30├ù too large when its printed scale is misread ÔåÆ the drawing's title block blankets the map ("file name all over the map") `[Site Planner / overlay]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-20 with a screenshot of "Jacinto"; arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B260** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B246 (dock-zone build-out, PR #204) and B247ÔÇôB259 (Scheduler backlog) while this was in flight, so B260 is the real next free ID after B259)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/sweet-wozniak-txzr3e`).** Owner dropped a landscape drawing onto the planner and a huge rotated string ÔÇö the sheet's title block, ~`BV-255-2024-LD1 ÔÇª ARCH ASSET FOR ÔÇª` ÔÇö covered nearly the whole aerial; only the parcel (`26.33 ac`) looked right.
- **Diagnosis (corrected the hand-off's guess).** It is **NOT** a world-scaled *text label*: every label path in the planner is screen-space or px-capped ÔÇö element labels `11┬Àls`, parcel acreage chip `12┬Àls`, callouts are boxed + capped at their own `size`, all markup/measure labels are fixed px, and the overlay's name **placeholder** is a hard-coded `fontSize={13}` (confirmed in a headless seed: the placeholder text stays 13 px even when its dashed rect is huge). The giant string is the rasterized overlay **IMAGE** itself. `addOverlayFile` auto-applied a scale note read off the sheet (the B73 `trueScale` path ÔåÆ `ftPerPointForScale(detectedScale)`) with **no sanity check on the resulting size**. A landscape sheet commonly prints a **vicinity / key-map scale** (e.g. 1ÔÇ│=600ÔÇ▓) next to the plan scale (e.g. 1ÔÇ│=30ÔÇ▓), and `parseScaleNote` returns the **first in-range match**, so the vicinity scale can win ÔåÆ `ftPerPx` 10ÔÇô30├ù too big ÔåÆ `imgW┬ÀftPerPx┬Àppf` renders the sheet (and its title block) enormous, spilling far past the viewport. (The drawing's white paper is knocked to transparent, so on a dark aerial the only thing that reads is the dark title-block text ÔÇö which is why it looks like "just the file name, splattered.") **Reproduced headless:** a seeded overlay at the misread 1ÔÇ│=600ÔÇ▓ rendered **14279├ù9519 px** on a 1440├ù900 view.
