- **Fix 1 ÔÇö guard the auto-scale (root cause).** New pure, browser-free `chooseOverlayScale()` in `lib/overlayScale.js`: trust the read scale **only** when it lands the sheet at a sane on-screen size (**Ôëñ4├ù / ÔëÑ0.04├ù the viewport width**); otherwise fall back to the existing "size to fit" (~60% of the view) and report a `too-big`/`too-small` reason. The read scale is **still stored** on the overlay, so the panel's "Read from sheet: 1ÔÇ│=XÔÇ▓ **[Apply]**" still offers it as a one-click when the user wants it. `addOverlayFile` now routes through it and, on a rejected scale, flashes an **honest, actionable** note ("ÔÇªits printed scale would place it far too large ÔÇö sized it to fit instead; set the exact scale or 'Trace a length' in the Site-plan overlay panel"). No silent mis-place.
- **Fix 2 ÔÇö one-click rescue.** New **"Size to view"** button in the Site-plan overlay panel: resizes the selected overlay to ~60% of the current view and recenters it ÔÇö recovery for an already-splattered overlay (the owner's existing "Jacinto" file) or any manual scale slip. Distinct from "Fit view" (which zooms the canvas), with tooltips on both to disambiguate.
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 538 tests ┬À build green; lazy-chunk split intact.** `chooseOverlayScale` has **7 new unit tests** (`test/overlayScale.test.js`: trusts a correct 1ÔÇ│=30ÔÇ▓, rejects a misread 1ÔÇ│=600ÔÇ▓ as too-big, rejects a deep-zoom tiny render as too-small, falls back with no/non-standard scale, the fit fallback Ôëê 60% of the view, NaN/zero-divide defense). Headless on the **built** app (`ui-audit/verify-overlay-fix.mjs`): **(A)** dropping an image runs the real import path ÔåÆ overlay lands **535 px** wide on a 1440 px view (sane, not splattered); **(B)** a seeded splattered overlay (**14279 px**) shrinks to **535 px** with one "Size to view" click (before/after screenshots). **Honest gap:** PDF rasterization can't run in the sandbox Chromium (pdf.js `getOrInsertComputed` is unsupported there), so the PDF scale-read path is covered by the unit tests + the image-path E2E rather than an end-to-end PDF drop ÔÇö logged for a real-browser confirm as **VERIFICATION V63**.
- **Dedup:** distinct from **B148** (oversized world-space *summary banner* ÔÇö a genuine screen-vs-world text bug) and builds on **B73** (the overlay scale tooling this hardens). The hand-off's "world-scaled label, scaled to the geometry's bounding box" hypothesis was investigated and **disproved** ÔÇö no label path produces it; the cause is overlay-image scaling. The "Place on map" cascade (`shared/placement/placeOnMap.js`, B182) remains a stub (`onPlaceOnMap` only closes the drawer) and emits no label, so there is **no** second "auto-place emits a bogus label" item.

<!-- 2026-06-20: owner dropped his old "Planar ÔÇö Engineering Backlog" (a 2026-05-26 code review,
     re-verified 2026-06-19) for the **Scheduler** app ÔÇö the in-browser-Babel Gantt tool at
     `public/sequence/index.html` (the old "Planar"/"Planar_Fit" = Planyr Scheduler module). Asked to
     "log this" and explicitly "don't worry about the email feature." Filed the whole thing under
     `[Scheduler]` with real next-free IDs **B247ÔÇôB259** ÔÇö provisionally B246ÔÇôB258, renumbered +1 after
     concurrent `main` took **B246** (dock-zone build-out, PR #204) while this was in flight; their own
     B#/M#/P# tags kept as orig-refs.
     Per STANDING RULE #1, filed AND fixed the safe wins this same session: **B247ÔÇôB253 shipped**
     (moved here); **B254ÔÇôB259** are the decision/refactor/perf/optional/project items, left in
     BACKLOG.md (Open + Later) with reasons. Email feature excluded per owner. The doc's "Done since
     the review" (orig B2 multi-device guard, B6 main escaping, B8 split-view col widths) was already
     shipped ÔÇö not re-filed. Branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`; headless-verified VERIFICATION **V62**. -->

### B247 ÔÇö Scheduler Export modal: Header/Cover fields drop focus after every keystroke `[Scheduler / UI]` (bug)  *(orig "B3/M4" in the owner's Planar backlog; provisionally B246, renumbered **B247** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B246 (dock-zone build-out, PR #204) while this was in flight, so B247 is the real next free ID after B246)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** In Export ÔåÆ "Header / Cover", typing in Exhibit / Title / Prep. by / Prep. for / Date / Conf. tag dropped focus after each character (one keystroke at a time). **Cause:** `Field` (and `Toggle`/`SegBtn`/`Row`) were defined *inside* `PDFExportModal`, so every render minted a new component identity ÔåÆ React unmounted the live `<input>` and mounted a fresh one, taking focus with it. **Fix:** hoisted `Field`/`Toggle`/`SegBtn`/`Row` to module scope (all props-only ÔÇö no closure over modal state) above `PDFExportModal`; their JSX usages are unchanged (now resolve to the stable module-scope identity). `SHdr` was deliberately left inside the modal ÔÇö it closes over `setSecOpen`/`secIsOpen`, so hoisting it would need prop-threading for zero user benefit (perf-only). **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-scheduler-bugfixes.mjs`, V62): typed "ALTA & Topo Survey ÔÇö Phase 2" character-by-character into the Title field ÔÇö full value intact, focus retained, 0 console errors.

### B248 ÔÇö Scheduler "Export Report" (HTML) doesn't escape user strings `[Scheduler / export]` (bug)  *(orig "B6┬¢")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** The older `exportHTML` path still did raw template interpolation, so a task named "ALTA & Topo Survey" or "Budget > $1M" (or any `<`/`>`/`&` in name/owner) corrupted the downloaded HTML report. `buildPDFHtml`/`buildGanttSVG` already used the module `escapeHtml` helper; this was the last raw path. **Fix:** wrapped every interpolated user string in `exportHTML` ÔÇö project name, task id, task name, start/end dates, the status label, and owner ÔÇö in `escapeHtml(...)`. Numbers (duration, %) left as-is.

### B249 ÔÇö Deleting a custom status crashes views that still reference it `[Scheduler / status]` (bug)  *(orig "B1")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** Create a custom status ÔåÆ apply to tasks ÔåÆ delete it, and several views went blank (the % Done cell, the HTML export) because the deleted key was dereferenced as `HEALTH[t.health].dot` with no fallback. **Fix, two halves:** **(a) Reassign on delete** ÔÇö `removeOption` now walks every project's tasks and sets `health: "gray"` ("Not Started") on any task still carrying the deleted key *before* filtering the status out (same `setData`), so no task is ever left pointing at a missing key. **(b) Defensive lookups** ÔÇö guarded the two genuinely-unguarded sites with `(HEALTH[ÔÇª] || HEALTH.gray)` (the `exportHTML` row and the % Done cell), matching the `|| HEALTH.gray` pattern already used in ~10 spots. The project-header lookup (`HEALTH[projHealth(cur)]`) was already safe ÔÇö `projHealth` only ever returns base keys.

### B250 ÔÇö JSON import skips the normalization/migration pipeline `[Scheduler / import]` (bug)  *(orig "B4")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** `importJSON` did `setData(parsed)` raw, skipping the migration the cloud/seed **load** path runs ÔÇö so an old or partial backup could load with predecessors in the old shape, missing settings, and un-cascaded dates. **Fix:** route the import through the *same* pipeline as load: `ensureContacts(normalizeIds(ensureHolidays(normalizeToV6(parsed))))`, then rebuild `HOLIDAY_SET` and run `cascadeDates ÔåÆ rollupParentDates` per project (mirrors the seed-load branch exactly). The pre-import `pre-import` snapshot is preserved. A current backup re-imports byte-identically (the migrations are idempotent); an old/partial one loads cleanly with defaults filled in.

### B251 ÔÇö New/renamed custom status renders one beat late `[Scheduler / status]` (bug)  *(orig "B5/M3")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** Adding or renaming a custom status often didn't show in pickers until you clicked around. **Cause:** `HEALTH`/`HK`/`HDARK` are module-level mutable globals rebuilt by `rebuildHEALTH(...)` inside the `[data]` effect; the rebuild mutates the globals but doesn't itself re-render, so the render that processed the change read stale values. **Fix (the low-risk option A):** track a `prevHealthKey` ref of the serialized `customHealth`+`healthLabelOverrides`; after `rebuildHEALTH`, when that key actually changes, bump a small `setHealthTick` once to force a re-render that re-reads the fresh globals. Keyed so it fires only on real status-config changes, and loop-safe (the tick isn't a `[data]` dependency, so it can't re-trigger the effect) ÔÇö mirrors the existing holiday-recascade guard in the same effect. **Verified headless** (V62): boots clean, no "Maximum update depth" loop, 0 console errors.

### B252 ÔÇö Undo history holds 50 full board snapshots `[Scheduler / perf]` (task)  *(orig "P3")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/zen-bohr-7xgk6q`).** Every edit pushed a full deep copy of `data` onto the undo stack, capped at 50 (~50├ù the board's JSON held in memory). **Fix:** lowered the cap to 20 (`slice(-19)`) ÔÇö undo still works through many steps, memory footprint cut ~60%. (The bigger "snapshot only the active project / store inverse diffs" options stay deferred under B256/roadmap; not needed yet.)

### B253 ÔÇö "Today" stays fresh across midnight / tab-left-open `[Scheduler / dates]` (bug) ÔÇö already resolved  *(orig "B7")*
`[x]` **Already done in current code; verified 2026-06-20, no change needed.** The owner's doc predated the fix. `NOW` is now a live module value refreshed on `focus`, `visibilitychange`, and a 60s `setInterval`, each bumping a `todayTick` that forces the re-render that re-reads it; `computeDisplayHealth` (overdue/due-soon) and the Gantt "today" line read `NOW`; `NotesModal` stamps `toShortDate(NOW)` / `parseFlexDate(dateStr) || NOW` at open time. So the today line, overdue colouring, and new-note date all roll over without a reload. Filed for the record so the old finding is closed.

### B254 ÔÇö Split-view Snap/Free chip lingers ~5s after releasing the divider `[Scheduler / UI]` (bug?) ÔÇö resolved as won't-change  *(orig "B9"; minted **B254**)*
`[x]` **Resolved 2026-06-20 (no code change ÔÇö owner decision).** The owner's doc suspected the `setTimeout(() => setDragging(false), 5000)` (public/sequence/index.html) was a 500ms typo. **Inspected first:** the chip is an *interactive* Snap/Free toggle (fixed portal, top-right); the ~5s keeps it clickable after a drag so you can flip snap mode for the next drag without re-grabbing the divider ÔÇö i.e. the delay serves a real purpose, not an obvious typo. Raised it to Michael; he said **"don't worry about the chip thing"** ÔåÆ leave the 5s as-is. Closed so it isn't re-raised. No change shipped.

### B261 ÔÇö Add an explicit "Group" tool (Bluebeam-style, no content lock) `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; minted **B261** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B246, so B261ÔÇôB264 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eager-keller-1ueisx`).** A deliberate Group replaces the old implicit drag-to-bond. A persistent group = a shared **`groupId`** on ÔëÑ2 els/markups (additive Site-Model field; rides `els`/`markups` losslessly, no migration). It is DISTINCT from the temporary multi-selection (marquee / Shift-click; evaporates on the next click) AND from the building-host **`attachedTo`** assembly (a rigid childÔåÆhost bind with resize-refit).
- **Commands:** select ÔëÑ2 ÔåÆ **Group** (toolbar **Ôè× Group**, the element context menu's "Group selection (N)", or **Ctrl+G**) stamps a fresh `groupId`. **Ungroup** via toolbar **Ôèƒ**, context menu, or **Ctrl+ÔçºG**. **Duplicate group** (Ctrl+D on a group, or context "Duplicate group") clones every member + each member's attached children with remapped ids and a fresh shared `groupId`; a LONE paste/duplicate strips `groupId` (added to `ORPHAN_TAGS`) so a single copy starts standalone.
- **Grouping means "they stay together," nothing more** (owner clarification 2026-06-20): a single click on any member selects the WHOLE group, and move / copy / delete / arrow-nudge then act on all of them ÔÇö basically how a building + its attached features already travel together, extended to arbitrary peers (e.g. a road + an easement). A dashed enclosing box labelled "Ôè× Group" marks it as one unit. **There is deliberately NO whole-group resize** ÔÇö scaling everything at once "isn't how you plan buildings" (site elements are real feet). To resize a piece, drill into it (below). (An earlier build added uniform group-resize handles; removed per the owner ÔÇö `startGroupResize`/`scaleMarkup` deleted, the box is now a pure indicator.)
- **No content lock:** double-click a member to "drill in" (`drillId`) and edit it in place (resize / retype / restyle) WITHOUT ungrouping; right-click still opens the actions menu; members keep their own properties. Grouping happens ONLY from the explicit command ÔÇö never from a plain drag or click.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b261-b264.mjs`): group two elements ÔåÆ "Ôè× Group" box renders with **no resize handles** ÔåÆ dragging one member moves **both** (108px each) ÔåÆ Ungroup removes the box ÔåÆ members move independently again; **0 page errors**. lint **0 errors** ┬À **537 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Logged **VERIFICATION V64**.
> **Dedup:** no prior open Group item. Supersedes the *bonding* half of the B114 snap/bond work (the Shift-dragÔåÆ`attachedTo` bond is removed ÔÇö see **B262**). The owner-mentioned "earlier split-snap item" was the B114 family (already done), not a separate open item; the still-open **B115** (keyboard-shortcuts pass, owner-gated) listed the now-removed Ôçº-drag bond gesture ÔÇö its shortcut table is updated here.

### B262 ÔÇö Keep snap as pure alignment only (no relationship) `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; minted **B262**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eager-keller-1ueisx`).** Removed the implicit drag-to-bond entirely: the Shift-drag ÔåÆ `alignSnap` ÔåÆ `attachedTo` path, the green "+" attach hint, and the bond-on-drop in `onUp` are gone, and the now-unused `alignSnap` / `flushContact` / `isAxisRect` helpers + `canAttach` / `bondTarget` / `bondRot` were deleted (net ÔêÆ2 lint warnings). Dragging an element now only MOVES it; with snap on, the ambient flush-snap (`edgeSnapCenter`) only ALIGNS position (grid + flush against neighbours, AutoCAD-style) and never creates a group or attachment. The building-host `attachedTo` assembly (docks / parking / sidewalks) and the explicit "Attach toÔÇª" command are unchanged.
> **Self-verified headless** (V64): with snap ON, dragging a building flush against a parking field then away leaves the parking untouched (╬ö=0px ÔÇö no bond).
> **Dedup:** pairs with **B261** (grouping moved to the explicit Group tool); narrows **B114** (the snap toggle + Alt-bypass stay; only the bond half is removed).

### B263 ÔÇö Snap pref is silently global & sticky across sessions/projects `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3" 2026-06-20; minted **B263**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eager-keller-1ueisx`).** Snap was persisted in `localStorage` (`planarfit:snap`) and re-applied on every load, so it could be ON without the user enabling it this session ("I never clicked snap"). It's now a **per-SESSION** pref: it defaults **OFF** every time the app opens, is remembered only within the current browser-tab session (`sessionStorage` ÔÇö survives plan/project switches this session, resets on a fresh tab/session), and `loadSnapPref` retires the old global `localStorage` key on read. The toolbar pill makes state explicit (**"Snap off"** / **"Snap 10ÔÇ▓ on"** with a green dot) and its tooltip clarifies snap only aligns position ÔÇö it never groups or bonds (so with **B262** no group/bond-type move can fire from this flag).
> **Self-verified headless** (V64): a fresh session ÔåÆ button reads "Snap off" and no global `localStorage` key exists; toggling on writes `sessionStorage` only.

### B264 ÔÇö No way to delete a single plan; only "delete site + all plans" `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-4" 2026-06-20; minted **B264**)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eager-keller-1ueisx`).** Added a per-plan delete to the **Plan Ôû¥** menu: each plan row carries a **Ô£ò** that arms an inline **"Delete '<name>'? Delete / Cancel"** confirm (no `window.confirm` ÔÇö honours the no-dialog-edits rule). The **last** plan in a site never shows a Ô£ò (removing the whole site stays the map/finder's job). App-side `deletePlan` removes just that plan; if it was the plan currently open it switches to a sibling. A new `deletedSelfRef` suppresses EVERY save path for the plan being deleted (the debounced autosave, the leave/unmount `persistOrDrop`, the `beforeunload`/visibility flush, and `flushSite`) so the unmounting planner can't immediately re-write the row we just deleted.
> **Self-verified headless** (V64): a 2-plan site ÔåÆ Ô£ò shows the inline confirm ÔåÆ Delete removes one plan (the **current** plan was deleted ÔåÆ it switched to the sibling and did NOT resurrect) ÔåÆ the lone remaining plan has no Ô£ò; **0 page errors**.

### B246 ÔÇö Employee/non-dock side build-out: "´╝ï" adds sidewalk ÔåÆ parking rows (mimics the dock stack) + restore the lost sidewalk `[Site Planner]` (bug/feature)  *(owner-reported, chat ÔÇö "still missing the option to addÔÇª rows of parking. I can get the first row, but I should be able to get [more]ÔÇª mimic how the dockside stuff is set up, but for the employee side. AlsoÔÇª when I press plus, I've lost my sidewalk." Filed provisionally as B243, renumbered **B246** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B243ÔÇôB245.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/practical-faraday-9mmfch`).** Two gaps after B242: the non-dock-side on-canvas "´╝ï" added a **single** parking row then disabled (couldn't add more), and the **sidewalk** that the pre-B242 progression placed against the wall was gone. **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** a shared `growEmployeeSide(b, side, dir)` walks the non-dock side like the dock stack ÔÇö **"´╝ï": sidewalk ÔåÆ first parking row ÔåÆ another row ÔåÆ ÔÇª** (`addSidewalkSide` ÔåÆ `addParkingRowSide` ÔåÆ `growParking(+1)`); **"ÔêÆ"** reverses (row ÔåÆ row ÔåÆ remove parking ÔåÆ remove sidewalk). Wired to **both** the on-canvas non-dock "´╝ï/ÔêÆ" pair (per side, with a live title: "Add a 5ÔÇ▓ sidewalk" ÔåÆ "Add a parking row" ÔåÆ "Add another parking row") **and** the panel **Car parking** row (applies to every non-dock side). So car parking now grows unlimited rows like the dockside zones, and the sidewalk is back as the first step. (Non-dock side = the short ends, plus the long side opposite single-load docks.)
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-dock-zones.mjs`, rebased on latest `main`): on a non-dock side the 1st "´╝ï" restores the **sidewalk**, the 2nd adds the **first parking row**, the 3rd **deepens the field by another row** (27px ÔåÆ 38px on screen); the panel + on-canvas dock-stack and per-zone flows all still pass; **0 page errors** (`dock-zones-parking.png`). lint **0 errors** ┬À **532 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Logged **VERIFICATION V55** (updated).
> **Dedup:** extends B242 (on-building controls); reuses the existing `addSidewalkSide` / `addParkingRowSide` / `growParking` so the employee side stays consistent with the row-stepping used by the Car Parking element elsewhere.

### B244 ÔÇö Resilient county parcel fetch with TxGIO statewide fallback `[Site Planner / parcels]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1" 2026-06-20; minted **B244** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B239ÔÇôB243 (Schedule stale-chunk hardening, brand icons, dock-zone fixes, PDF download) while this was in flight, so B244/B245 are the real next free IDs after B243)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/focused-darwin-8gkesb`).** When a county's own appraisal-district endpoint is down, parcel selection now degrades gracefully instead of hanging. Trigger was the 2026-06-19 FBCAD outage: `gis.fbcad.org/serverarcgis2` returned 503 then **total timeouts on every path** (layer, `/rest/services`, `/rest/info` ÔÇö the whole ArcGIS server unresponsive), so a Fort Bend click **froze the tab ~45s** with no answerer. What shipped:
- **Hard timeout (the freeze fix).** `fetchJson` in `lib/arcgis.js` now wraps every county GIS request in an **`AbortController` with an 8 s cap** (`PARCEL_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS`) ÔÇö an indefinite hang becomes a prompt, typed `timeout` failure so the fallback can take over. Covers **every** parcel surface automatically (map click-select, address-search select, in-planner identify) since they all route through `fetchJson`/`queryAtPoint`.
- **Per-source circuit breaker.** New `lib/sourceHealth.js` (pure, injectable clock, **7 tests**): after **3 consecutive failures** a source's breaker **opens for a 5 min cooldown** (`recordSourceResult`/`isSourceOpen`/`sourceCooldownMs`), then auto-resumes (half-open) so a recovered server is picked back up without a reload. `filterHealthyCandidates` drops an open primary from the click's candidate list **but never the statewide source** ÔÇö so we stop re-hammering a dead host while coverage always survives. A success resets the streak (a blip never trips it). Wired into `MapFinder.handleClick` + `selectParcelAt`: candidates are health-filtered before the query and `identifyParcelDetailed`'s new per-source outcomes feed the breaker.
- **Fallback chain (primary ÔåÆ TxGIO statewide).** B137 already appended the statewide TxGIO layer (`feature.geographic.texas.gov/.../stratmap_land_parcels_48_most_recent`) as a universal click candidate; this makes the chain *robust* (timeout + breaker above) and adds the **search-side** fallback: `lib/parcelQuery.js` `lookupParcels` tries the county's own CAD and, on a typed outage, retries the TxGIO layer **scoped to that one county**.
- **County-scoped statewide search.** `lib/counties.js` `statewideFallbackFor(county)` returns the TxGIO layer + `county='<NAME>'` scope (HARRIS / FORT BEND; Chambers already rides TxGIO so it has no separate backup). The shared, injection-safe `buildParcelWhere` (`parcelQuery.js`, **10 tests**, ports the B47 identifier guard) ANDs the scope **only when the layer actually has a `county` field** (self-healing for a pasted single-county URL) so an ID/address search can never match a like-named parcel in another county. Click-to-select is a point query and needs no scope.
- **Honest source labeling.** When a hit comes from the statewide layer while a real CAD existed for that spot, MapFinder now shows an amber **"Statewide backup source ÔÇö <County> county's own parcel server is unavailable; ÔÇª may lag county updates"** notice (a click banner + a badge in the address-search info card). The county name is read from the parcel's own `county` attribute (`findAttr(attrs, /^county$/i)`), title-cased.
- **Field mapping / normalization.** `lib/appraisal.js` `APPR_FIELDS` extended to cover TxGIO's column names (`legal_area`/`gis_area` ÔåÆ Acreage, `mkt_value` ÔåÆ Total value, `stat_land_use` ÔåÆ Land use, `year_built` ÔåÆ Year built) so a backup parcel surfaces the **same curated rows** as one from its home county (`test/appraisal.test.js`, **6 tests**). FBCAD's *native* field names can't be captured/mapped yet ÔÇö its server is down ÔÇö so that mapping is the one explicitly-deferred sliver (the generic detectors already read TxGIO's `prop_id`/`situs_addr`/`owner_name` fine).
- **Verified ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 532 tests ┬À build green; lazy-chunk split intact.** **Live headless run (`gis-verify/fbcad-outage-fallback-verify.mjs`):** with `gis.fbcad.org` intercepted as 503 (the real outage), a Sugar Land / Fort Bend click **selected the real TxGIO lot (prop_id 40594, county "FORT BEND") in 1.2 s** ÔÇö HCAD answered empty, FBCAD's 503 never froze the click ÔÇö and the **"Statewide backup source" notice rendered** (`gis-verify/fbcad-outage-fallback-verified.png`). Logged **VERIFICATION V61**; no owner action.
> **Dedup vs B137:** B137 made TxGIO a *queryable candidate* so a Fort Bend lot *can* be answered (coverage). B244 is the **resilience layer on top** ÔÇö the timeout that stops the hang, the breaker that stops re-hammering, the honest backup labeling, the search-side county-scoped fallback, and the TxGIO field normalization. Also distinct from **B233** (which added the "source unavailable Ôëá no-parcel-here" split ÔÇö B244 *feeds* that split with typed failures, see B245) and **B36e** (AbortControllers on *evidence* fetches, a different, busy-gated path). **Note (honest):** the search-side fallback lives in the canonical `runLookup` seam, which the in-planner lookup panel doesn't currently mount (the live search is the map address box, a point query that's inherently single-parcel) ÔÇö the mechanism is built + unit-tested at the seam so it's correct if/when that panel returns.

### B245 ÔÇö Validate the ArcGIS response BODY, not just the HTTP status `[Site Planner / parcels]` (bug) ÔÇö prereq for B244  *(arrived as "NEW-2" 2026-06-20; minted **B245** ÔÇö next free ID after B244)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/focused-darwin-8gkesb`).** ArcGIS routinely returns **HTTP 200 with a JSON `{error:ÔÇª}` body** (e.g. 499 Token Required) for a failed query; the old `fetchJson` did already throw on `j.error`, but it threw a *generic* `Error`, so a caller couldn't tell a **server failure** from a healthy **"no parcel at this point."** Now `lib/arcgis.js` throws a typed **`ParcelFetchError { kind, status, unavailable:true }`** classifying every failure ÔÇö `timeout` (aborted), `http` (non-OK status), `arcgis` (200 + error body), `network` (fetch/CORS throw) ÔÇö while a **healthy empty feature list still returns `null`, never an error** (no-parcel-here is a valid answer, not a failure). `identifyParcelDetailed` already separated `responded` vs `errors` for the B233 "unavailable vs none" card; it now also returns **per-source outcomes** (`sources:[{county, ok, hit, error}]`) so the B244 circuit breaker can mark exactly which source failed. `humanizeError` gives each `kind` its own plain wording. This is the prereq that makes B244's fallback trigger reliably (a 200-with-error-body now *counts* as a failure, not a masked success). **Tests:** `test/arcgis.test.js` extended (+7: emptyÔåÆnull, 200+errorÔåÆ`arcgis`/499, 503ÔåÆ`http`, networkÔåÆ`network`, the timeout-abort path via fake timers, per-source split, humanized wording). lint 0 ┬À 532 tests ┬À build green.
> **Dedup:** extends **B233** (typed "couldn't reach any service" vs "no parcel here") from the address-search card down into the fetch layer, and reuses the same body-parse principle the layer **`probeService`** (`lib/layers.js`) already applies (HTTP 200 + `.error` = failed) ÔÇö now applied to the parcel **query** path and surfaced as a typed error. Folded into the B244 ship (one branch); kept as its own item because it's the reusable classification primitive every parcel surface now leans on.

### B243 ÔÇö Replace browser print with a Planyr-composed PDF download (no print dialog, white background, exact page size) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B228, renumbered **B243** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B228ÔÇôB242 (dock-zone stack, DWG service, map-finder search, brand icons, ÔÇª) while this was in flight, so B243 is the real next free ID after B242)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/eloquent-mendel-jioa4q`).** Owner reported (3 screenshots) that **Print** opened a blank `about:blank` window and the output carried a browser date/time header (top-left), an `about:blank` URL (bottom-left), a `1/1` page number (bottom-right), a cream page background, and Letter-sized content floating in a corner of a Tabloid sheet. **Inspected first (as asked):** the path ALREADY did the hard part ÔÇö `printPDF` composed the WHOLE sheet (title block + plan + B197 buildings table + metrics) as ONE SVG at the exact declared page size (B200) ÔÇö and the ONLY defect was the final DELIVERY: `window.open("","_blank")` + `win.print()` handed that sheet to the **browser's** print dialog, which injects the header / `about:blank` / page number (none strippable by CSS or code), and the cream was `PAL.paper` (`#f4f1ea`, the on-screen page colour) bleeding into the sheet fill **and** the plan-clone background rect.
- **Fix = keep the composition, replace the delivery with a real PDF we build ourselves.** New pure, dependency-free writer `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/imagePdf.js` (`jpegToPdf`): assembles a one-page PDF (catalog ÔåÆ pages ÔåÆ page ÔåÆ image XObject ÔåÆ content stream ÔåÆ Info) at an **exact physical size** (`MediaBox = inches ├ù 72 pt`), embeds the page raster with `/DCTDecode` (the JPEG bytes verbatim, no re-encode), with a **byte-accurate xref** and binary-safe assembly (builds a `Uint8Array`; the JPEG never passes through a UTF-16 JS string). No jsPDF/pdf-lib ÔÇö zero bundle weight, matching the `printSheet.js` pure-lib pattern; unit-tested in `test/imagePdf.test.js` (10 tests: xref offsets land on each object, MediaBox in points, DCTDecode dims, paren/backslash title escaping, bad-input guards).
- **`printPDF` ÔåÆ `exportPDF`** in `SitePlanner.jsx`: the old `window.open`/`win.print()` machinery is **gone**. It now forces **white** paper (new `paper` arg to `buildExportSvg` for the plan-clone bg + `pal:{ÔÇªPAL,paper:'#ffffff'}` for the sheet ÔÇö the two cream sources), inlines the aerial with `dropOnFail=true` so a CORS-blocked image can't taint the canvas (the vector plan still exports), rasterizes the composed sheet at **300 DPI** (`PDF_DPI`, capped by `PDF_MAX_PX = 22M` px ÔÇö Tabloid@300 Ôëê 16.8M, under the cap), fills the canvas white first (JPEG has no alpha), JPEG-encodes at q0.92, wraps with `jpegToPdf`, and **downloads** `"<date> <project> - <plan>.pdf"` (B201 naming). Generating the PDF ourselves is the mechanism that removes the injected chrome; the declared page size kills the Letter-on-Tabloid float. Raster (vs a vector SVGÔåÆPDF converter) is deliberate: the plan uses `feDropShadow` filters, which the browser renders faithfully into the raster but most SVGÔåÆPDF converters drop ÔÇö full WYSIWYG fidelity over crisp-vector-text, and 300 DPI keeps text crisp for print anyway.
- **UI:** File Ôû¥ item ÔåÆ **"Download PDF / pick frameÔÇª"**; the print-frame toolbar action ÔåÆ **"Download PDF"** (+ tooltip); a **"Preparing PDFÔÇª"** pill (new `exportingPDF` state + a `spin` keyframe in `index.css`) replaces the old blank "Preparing printÔÇª" window. The two `exportPNG` alerts that said "use Print to PDF" now say "Download PDF". Paper/orientation (Letter/Tabloid ├ù landscape/portrait) and the **B131** overlay toggle are unchanged.
> **Self-verified headless** (**V60**, logged-out, against the built `dist/`): **(1)** real app ÔÇö `ui-audit/verify-pdf-download.mjs` drove File Ôû¥ ÔåÆ Download PDF / pick frameÔÇª ÔåÆ Download PDF and captured the ACTUAL file: `2026.06.20 Mesa Logistics - Plan 1.pdf`, a valid `%PDF`, **numPages 1**, page **exactly 792├ù612 pt**, **ZERO pop-up windows**, **0** errors. **(2)** pipeline ÔÇö `ui-audit/verify-pdf-export.mjs` ran the exact rasterÔåÆJPEGÔåÆ`jpegToPdf` path for Letter (792├ù612) and Tabloid (1224├ù792), each a valid 1-page PDF that paints an image; all four paper corners white, **no cream field** (Ôëñ1 stray warm pixel = JPEG chroma-edge artifact), the **building drop-shadow + aerial `<image>` survive** the raster, table/metrics crisp at 300 DPI (`live-download-page.jpg`, `pdf-export-letter.jpg`). lint **0** ┬À **496 tests** ┬À build green (re-verified after merging the latest `main`); existing `verify-print-live.mjs` still passes with the renamed menu item; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Dedup:** NO existing open item covered this. **B131** (overlay-in-print toggle ÔÇö done) is PRESERVED (the `printOverlay` checkbox still gates the placed overlay in the export). **B50** (export/print robustness) is partly SUPERSEDED for the print path: its "don't strand a blank PreparingÔÇª window on a serialization throw" guard is moot now that there's no window; PNG export keeps its own try/catch + alerts, untouched. **B159/B160** are the *Scheduler* Gantt PDF/Print export (a different module/app), not this Site Planner path. The single-SVG sheet composition (**B200**) + buildings table (**B197**) + filename (**B201**) are reused unchanged ÔÇö only the final delivery step changed.

### B242 ÔÇö On-building "´╝ï / ÔêÆ" controls for the whole dock stack + uniform panel rows `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-reported, chat ÔÇö "bring my plus buttons back to the buildingÔÇª plus and minus next to each other for every one of them, except the bump-outs." Filed provisionally as B240, renumbered **B242** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B240/B241 while this was in flight.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/practical-faraday-9mmfch`).** The owner's model: the building is the hub and every dock-side piece is part of it, so the add/remove controls belong **on the building, on the map** ÔÇö and he was "missing the plus buttons" because the old on-canvas dock node was a single toggle that flipped to a lone "ÔêÆ" once a court existed (the "´╝ï" to add the *next* zone had moved onto the sub-pieces). **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):**
- **On-canvas "´╝ï / ÔêÆ" PAIRS on the building** (new `featPair` + `glyphPlus`/`glyphMinus`, replacing the single-toggle `featNode` for these). Each **dock side** gets a pair that walks the zone stack **out/in** (´╝ï = next outward zone court ÔåÆ trailer parking ÔåÆ buffer; ÔêÆ = remove the outer zone), **per side** (independent ÔÇö never the old "stuck adding courts" problem). Each **non-dock side** (short ends, and the long side opposite single-load docks) gets a **car-parking ´╝ï / ÔêÆ**. Each **dock corner** keeps a single **bump-out** toggle ÔÇö the one exception the owner called out ("you only add one thing there"). The old `courtAddNodes` / `trailerAddNodes` far-edge nodes are removed (folded into the dock-side pair). Still gated by `FEAT_BTN_MIN_PX` (B225) so they never cluster when zoomed out, and shown for the selected/hovered building (B226).
- **Building-level controls are now per-side too:** `addDockZone` / `removeOuterDockZone` grow / shrink **every** dock side by one ring (each side adds/removes its own next/outer zone), so an uneven building is never stuck.
- **Uniform panel rows:** the Dock-features panel now lists **Dock zones / Car parking / Bump-outs**, each a label + a consistent square **´╝ï / ÔêÆ** pair aligned right (bump-outs: ´╝ï, plus ÔêÆ only when present) ÔÇö fixes the "different sizes / odd layout." Below: the zone **depth** rows (inline-editable), outward order.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-dock-zones.mjs`, rebased on the latest `main`): panel "´╝ï"/"ÔêÆ" walk the stack on **both** dock sides (courtÔåÆtrailerÔåÆbuffer, 2 each; then peel back); the **on-canvas pair** adds a court on **one** side then walks it to trailer and removes it (per-side); inline buffer depth 15ÔåÆ40 grows both bands; car parking adds on the ends; **0 page errors**. The per-zone court editor (B239) still works. Screens `dock-zones-full.png` / `dock-zones-oncanvas.png` / `dock-zones-parking.png`. lint **0 errors** ┬À **486 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Logged **VERIFICATION V55** (updated).
> **Dedup:** extends B228/B229 (the stack) + B239 (per-zone court add); reuses B225/B226's `FEAT_BTN_MIN_PX` + `featActiveId` gating so the new pairs inherit the anti-clutter / one-building rules.

### B241 ÔÇö Coral brand palette tokens + a reusable BrandMark component `[App shell]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B231 ÔåÆ B240, renumbered **B241** ÔÇö next free ID after B240; concurrent `main` advanced through B230ÔÇôB239 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/inspiring-feynman-c3ggew`).** Added the coral palette as design tokens in two mirrored places: CSS custom properties in `src/index.css :root` (the `--coral-*` / `--brand-*` group) and a JS token module `src/shared/brand/tokens.js` (`BRAND`, for inline-styled chrome that can't read CSS vars). Tokens: coral-base `#A8482B` (sides `#963F26`/`#823620`), coral-mid `#DC6B42` (`#C85F3A`/`#B25431`), coral-top `#F8946A` (`#E8825A`/`#D6744E`); linework-on-dark grid `#E89A78`, glass-edge `#F0A888`, wire `#FBB89A`; surfaces ink `#15171C`, cream `#F4EFE6`; wordmark `#F4F1E9` (on dark) / `#2A211C` (on light).
- **`src/shared/brand/BrandMark.jsx`** renders the mark inline as SVG (crisp at any size, theme-aware): `variant` (`auto`|`favicon`|`mark`; **auto = simplified Ôëñ32px, full mark above** ÔÇö the responsive-logo rule), `wordmark` (the horizontal "<mark> planyr" lockup), `surface` (`dark`/`light` ÔåÆ wordmark colour), `tile`, `size`. Geometry mirrors `brand/*.svg` (shared with B240's favicons).
- **The unified header's logo slot** (`src/shared/ui/AppHeader.jsx`) now consumes `<BrandMark>` instead of the old ad-hoc accent-square glyph, so the product logo is the real coral mark (fixed; the per-module accent still drives the tabs + breadcrumb). Plugs into the B104/B10 single-header work.
> lint 0 errors ┬À 481 tests pass ┬À build green; the `AppHeader` shared chunk still splits cleanly (BrandMark rides in it). Self-verified headless (V59): the live built header renders the coral stack + "planyr" wordmark on the dark chrome.

### B240 ÔÇö Replace the favicon and app icon with the new Planyr coral mark `[App shell]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B230 ÔåÆ B239, renumbered **B240** ÔÇö concurrent `main` advanced through B230ÔÇôB239 (Bluebeam vertex editing, detention pond, map-finder tranche, stale-chunk hardening, dock-zone fixes) while this was in flight, so B240/B241 are the real next free IDs after B239; PR #197 still says "B230/B231")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/inspiring-feynman-c3ggew`).** Swapped the old inline orange-rectangle data-URI favicon for the new Planyr coral brand mark across the app. Canonical artwork is the source of record in `brand/`: `planyr-favicon.svg` (simplified solid coral stack on a rounded dark `#15171C` tile ÔÇö the small/favicon lockup) and `planyr-mark.svg` (full finish: gridded base ┬À glass middle ┬À wireframe top ÔÇö the display lockup); both copied into `public/` (`favicon.svg`, `planyr-mark.svg`).
- **Raster set generated** from the favicon SVG: `public/favicon.ico` (multi-size **16/32/48**, PNG-embedded) and `public/apple-touch-icon.png` (**180├ù180**, dark full-bleed tile for iOS home screens). `index.html <head>` now references `favicon.svg` (preferred), `favicon.ico` (legacy fallback), and `apple-touch-icon.png`; the old data-URI icon reference was removed.
- **Dependency-free generator** `brand/generate-icons.mjs`: drives the sandbox Chromium with `--screenshot` (rendering onto a tall canvas and cropping, since headless caps usable viewport height) and assembles the PNG/ICO by hand (zlib only). Re-run when the artwork changes, then commit `public/`. Documented in `brand/README.md` (the "logged in a folder" home for the source artwork + palette + regen steps).
> Responsive-logo rule honoured (favicon/Ôëñ32px = simplified solid; large = full mark ÔÇö also applied in-app by B241's `BrandMark variant="auto"`). Self-verified headless (V59): the favicon SVG, the 48px ICO frame, and the 180px apple-touch render the full three-tier stack (not blank/clipped); `vite preview` serves all four icon files (200 + correct content-types) and the built `dist/index.html` references resolve under the root deploy. **Live-tab caveat:** favicons cache hard ÔÇö a stale open tab may keep the old icon; hard-refresh to confirm.

### B239 ÔÇö Schedule module still dead-ends after a deploy: harden stale-chunk recovery (cache-busting reload + chunk-aware ErrorBoundary) `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug) ÔÇö follow-up to B221  *(owner-reported 2026-06-20, chat: "my scheduling module not workingÔÇª this is obviously a huge deal"; minted B228, renumbered **B239** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B228ÔÇôB238 (dock-zone stack, Bluebeam vertex editing, map-finder batch, two-backend doc note) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B238)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/optimistic-brahmagupta-ubw407`).** The owner still hit the Sequence/Schedule module on the workspace error screen with `Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: ÔÇª/Scheduler-<hash>.js` **even though B221 had shipped** the stale-chunk auto-reload. **Confirmed the module itself is NOT broken** (headless `ui-audit/diagnose-scheduler.mjs` scenario A: the embedded Gantt renders **44 task rows** the moment its chunk loads ÔÇö the earlier "blank iframe" reading was just measured before the in-browser Babel transpile of the ~692 KB `/sequence/` doc finished). The residual failure was two gaps in B221's *recovery path*, both fixed here:
- **The recovery reload wasn't guaranteed-fresh.** B221's auto-reload (and the boundary's manual reload) used a plain `location.reload()`, which the browser can satisfy from its OWN cached copy of the stale `index.html` ÔÇö so the reload lands right back on the previous build's deleted chunk ÔåÆ second `vite:preloadError` ÔåÆ cooldown ÔåÆ dead-end. The no-cache `public/_headers` can't retro-fix an `index.html` a long-lived/returning tab already hard-cached. **Fix:** new `reloadFresh()` in `src/app/chunkReload.js` navigates to the same path with a throwaway `?_r=<ts>` query ÔÇö a **distinct cache key** the browser MUST fetch from the server ÔÇö via `location.replace` (no back-button trap), so it always pulls a fresh `index.html` and the current chunk names; `stripReloadParam()` tidies the param off the address bar on the recovered load (called at guard install). The `vite:preloadError` guard now calls `reloadFresh` (loop-guard/cooldown unchanged).
- **The ErrorBoundary's PRIMARY button was a no-op for this error.** "Try again" just `reset()`s ÔåÆ re-renders the same lazy import ÔåÆ re-requests the same dead chunk ÔåÆ fails identically (the dead-end the owner kept hitting). **Fix:** `ErrorBoundary.jsx` is now chunk-aware via a shared `isChunkLoadError()` (matches the dynamic-import failure phrasings across Chrome/Firefox/Safari + the SPA-fallback "expected JS, got text/html" MIME error). For a chunk error it reframes the screen as **"A new version of Planyr is ready"** with a single PRIMARY **"Reload to update"** (cache-busting) button ÔÇö your work is saved. Genuine render crashes keep the Try again / Reload page pair (both reloads now cache-busting too).
- **`public/_headers` unchanged** ÔÇö already correct from B221 (no-cache HTML, immutable hashed `/assets/*`); it's the *forward* guarantee, while `reloadFresh` is the *escape hatch* for tabs that cached the old HTML before it took effect.
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ┬À **443 tests** (`test/chunkReload.test.js` 4ÔåÆ11: `isChunkLoadError` cross-browser matrix, `reloadFresh` cache-key/replace, `stripReloadParam`) ┬À build green; `dist/_headers` still lands at the deploy root and all three lazy chunks emit hashed under `/assets/`. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/diagnose-scheduler.mjs` + `ui-audit/verify-chunk-reload.mjs`, **all green** on the built bundle via `vite preview`): **(A)** a normal Schedule click mounts the iframe and renders 44 task rows; **(B)** a chunk that 404s once then recovers ÔåÆ the guard cache-busting-reloads and lands in the module; **(C)** a permanently-missing chunk surfaces the **"A new version of Planyr is ready"** screen (no reload loop), and clicking **"Reload to update"** performs a real cache-busting reload ÔÇö captured nav trail `"/" ÔåÆ "/?_r=<ts>" ÔåÆ "/"` (the param added, then stripped). `verify-chunk-reload.mjs` still **3/3** (reload-once, cooldown holds, re-arms).
> **Owner note:** nothing to configure ÔÇö the fix ships with the next deploy. Because `reloadFresh` cache-busts, even a browser still holding the *old* `index.html` escapes on the first recovery instead of dead-ending. Logged **VERIFICATION V58**.
> **Dedup:** a hardening of **B221** (same stale-chunk-after-deploy family ÔÇö NOT a new bug class, and explicitly NOT a Scheduler/iframe logic bug, which was ruled out by the 44-row render). Distinct from the render-crash path the ErrorBoundary already handled (B205-era). No change to the lazy-chunk split or the iframe app.

### B238 ÔÇö Scaffold the DWGÔåÆDXF conversion service in /server (LibreDWG primary, APS fallback dormant) `[server]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B228, renumbered **B238** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B227ÔÇôB236 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B237)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/kind-johnson-oqzqco`).** Stood up the conversion **compute** service as a new `server/convert/` workspace alongside the existing /server storage/oauth scaffold ÔÇö a tiny, dependency-free Node HTTP service that turns a **DWG** into a **DXF**, targeting **Google Cloud Run** (scale-to-zero). Endpoints: `POST /convert` (body = DWG bytes ÔåÆ DXF bytes, `X-Convert-Engine` header) and `GET /health`. Engine policy (`convertService.js`): **LibreDWG primary** (`libredwg.js` ÔÇö spawns `dwg2dxf`, treats LibreDWG's error *bitmask* correctly: a non-empty DXF + non-critical code is success-with-warning, a critical code or no output is failure) ÔåÆ **Autodesk APS Model Derivative fallback** (`aps.js`) only when **`APS_ENABLED`** is on. **APS is DORMANT by default** (account not provisioned): real 2-legged auth is implemented + the full Model-Derivative pipeline is documented inline as a provisioning fill-in, but every dormant/unconfigured path returns a clear `{ok:false}` ÔÇö never a throw, never a false success. **When LibreDWG fails and APS is off, the HTTP layer returns an explicit error (422), never a 200 with a junk body** ÔÇö the same no-silent-failure contract as the storage adapter (it **reuses `server/storage/result.js`**, no parallel result type). Status map: 200 (DXF) ┬À 400 (no/empty body) ┬À 413 (over `MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES`) ┬À 422 (unreadable drawing) ┬À 503 (engine binary missing). All config via **server-side env only** (`config.js`: `PORT`, `LIBREDWG_BIN`, `CONVERT_TIMEOUT_MS`, `MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES`, `APS_ENABLED`, `APS_CLIENT_ID/SECRET`) ÔÇö never a `VITE_` var, never committed. **Dockerfile** is a production-correct multi-stage build: stage 1 compiles **LibreDWG 0.13.4 from source** (it isn't in the Debian/Ubuntu repos), static-linked so stage 2 (`node:22-bookworm-slim`, non-root `node` user) carries just the ~MB `dwg2dxf` binary; no npm install (Node built-ins only).
> **Verified this session ÔÇö built the image and ran the container for real.** `GET /health` ÔåÆ 200 `{ok,service:"dwg-convert",engine:"libredwg",apsFallback:false}`. `POST /convert` with two **real** sample drawings: **example_2000.dwg** (582 KB) ÔåÆ **200**, a valid `AutoCAD Drawing Exchange Format, version 2000` DXF (1.27 MB), `X-Convert-Engine: libredwg`; **example_2013.dwg** ÔåÆ **200**, valid DXF. **Failure path (the important one):** garbage (non-DWG) bytes with APS off ÔåÆ **422** `{"ok":false,"error":"DWGÔåÆDXF conversion failed in LibreDWG and the APS fallback is disabled (APS_ENABLED is off): ÔÇª"}` ÔÇö explicit, never a silent success; empty body ÔåÆ **400**; `GET /convert` ÔåÆ **405**. **+26 unit tests** (`test/dwgConvert.test.js`: config/APS-dormant parsing, LibreDWG success/warning/critical/spawn-fail/no-output, the engine-policy matrix incl. "APS never consulted when off", APS guard + auth shape, and the live HTTP server's status codes). lint **0 errors** ┬À **462 tests** ┬À `vite build` green; **isolation confirmed ÔÇö `grep` of `dist/` finds zero conversion-service code** (the public bundle never imports `/server`).
> **Follow-on (NOT this item):** actual `gcloud run deploy` waits on the GCP project being provisioned in the Google Cloud Console; the service + Dockerfile are proven locally now. The Dockerfile's `CURL_INSECURE` build-arg exists ONLY to build behind this repo's sandbox TLS-inspecting proxy (CLAUDE.md); production/Cloud Build omits it ÔåÆ full TLS verification.
> **Dedup / inspect-first:** checked before writing ÔÇö `/server` already held the B206ÔÇôB209 Drive storage adapter + the B207 Google OAuth helper, so this is **additive** (`server/convert/` only); no existing /server code changed. The browser/Supabase side calls this over the network in the backend tranche (distinct from the Supabase user-data backend).

### B237 ÔÇö Record the two-backend architecture in CLAUDE.md `[infra]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B227, renumbered **B237** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B227ÔÇôB236 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B236)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/kind-johnson-oqzqco`).** Reconciled the existing **"Two backends ÔÇö don't conflate"** section of `CLAUDE.md` **in place** (no duplicate section) so the data layer and the compute layer can never be confused: **(1) Supabase ÔÇö the data/auth/storage layer (BUILT):** cloud Postgres, email/password auth, RLS, cloud save/load; the frontend talks to it **directly with the anon key**, which is safe in the browser **because RLS protects it** (a request only ever sees the signed-in user's own rows); the permanent home for user data. **(2) `/server` on Google Cloud Run ÔÇö the compute layer (built in-repo, not yet deployed):** scale-to-zero containers running **DWGÔåÆDXF conversion** (LibreDWG primary ÔåÆ APS Model Derivative fallback, dormant behind `APS_ENABLED`) and **Google Drive auto-filing + bytes I/O** (the file-facts index itself lives in Supabase Postgres; the one-time OAuth *consent* callback is a thin same-origin Cloudflare Pages Function, the heavy compute is Cloud Run). Documented that **all third-party secrets (APS key, Google credentials, Supabase service-role key) stay server-side only, walled off from the public Cloudflare Pages deploy**; the only Supabase key that reaches the frontend is the RLS-protected **anon** key. The two layers talk **over the network** ÔÇö Supabase the permanent data home, `/server` additive compute added in the backend tranche.
> **Also fixed a stale contradiction** surfaced while reconciling: the Workflow section still said *"Deploy = GitHub Pages (production)"* and *"Cloudflare Pages is optional and deferred"* ÔÇö both false since the Pages-pipeline retirement (already recorded as DONE near the end of CLAUDE.md). Updated to **Cloudflare Pages = production (planyr.io)**, with per-branch previews noted as the separate optional Cloudflare concern, so the doc no longer contradicts itself about where the public deploy (the thing `/server` is walled off from) actually lives.
> **Dedup:** the section already existed ÔÇö this **enriched it in place** (Cloud Run / scale-to-zero / LibreDWGÔåÆAPS detail + the Cloudflare-Pages walling), it did not add a second copy. Doc-only change: lint 0 ┬À 462 tests ┬À build green (unaffected).

### B236 ÔÇö Per-layer GIS data-age / source-vintage stamp `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-5"; minted **B236** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B225ÔÇôB231 while this was in flight, so B232ÔÇôB236 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/tender-goldberg-ax4n5w`).** Each GIS layer now shows a low-visual-weight **"as of: &lt;source vintage&gt;"** line in `components/LayerPanel.jsx` (rendered when the layer is on), kept **deliberately distinct** from the existing **"refreshed Xm ago"** cache-age stamp by the layer name ÔÇö the two are never conflated. The vintage is the source's **own** effective/publication date or maintenance cadence, from a curated, central `LAYER_VINTAGE` map + `layerVintage(id, cfg)` in `lib/layers.js` (a per-config `vintage` override wins; else the map; else `null`). **Honest by rule:** per-panel / per-area datasets say what actually varies ("Effective date varies by FIRM panel", "Survey date varies by NWI project area", "LiDAR collection varies by county (USGS 3DEP)"); continuously-maintained registries say "continuously updated / current edition"; **any layer with no entry renders "vintage unknown" (italic) ÔÇö never a fabricated date** (same family as "source unavailable Ôëá not present"). **Scope:** source-vintage only; the "last refreshed" half rides the planned GIS-cache SWR work and is NOT built here.
> **Dedup:** the **standalone, ship-now** version of the data-age surfacing already specced inside **B96** (GIS layer cache, stale-while-revalidate) ÔÇö when caching lands, fold the vintage stamp + the refreshed-age stamp into one surface rather than duplicating. **Self-verified headless** (**V57**, `ui-audit/verify-b232-b236.mjs`): toggling FEMA on shows "as of: Effective date varies by FIRM panel". lint 0 ┬À 448 tests ┬À build green; lazy chunk split intact.

### B235 ÔÇö Rework "Your Sites" left rail: chips as filters, type-to-filter, collapsible status groups `[Site Planner / UI]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; minted **B235** ÔÇö next free ID after B234; depends on B234)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/tender-goldberg-ax4n5w`).** The status chips are now **positive multi-select filters** (`statusFilter` set): tap one to show only that state, multi-select; none selected = show everything. The filter drives **both** the list **and** the map pins, so "show only Active" focuses both at once (replaces the old hide-on-map `hidden` set). Added a **type-to-filter** text box (`nameFilter`, case-insensitive substring on the site/plan name) at the top of the list. The list is grouped under **collapsible status section headers** (`groupCollapsed`, persisted to `planarfit:sitesGroups:v1`): **Active / Pursuit / On Hold expanded, Complete / Dead collapsed by default** ÔÇö settled projects become expand-on-demand instead of rows you scroll past. An active name filter **force-expands** matching sections so a match in a collapsed (settled) group is never hidden. Section headers + chips render from the **B234 tokens**; the panel header count shows `shown/total` when a filter is active. One shared `siteRow()` renderer feeds every section.
> **Dependency:** consumes the B234 status tokens (one color + glyph per state). **Self-verified headless** (**V57**): type-to-filter narrows + surfaces a match inside a collapsed group; Complete collapses by default and expands on click; the Active chip narrows the map 7ÔåÆ2 pins. lint 0 ┬À 448 tests ┬À build green.

### B234 ÔÇö Unify status color + glyph as shared tokens across chips, list markers, and map pins `[Site Planner / UI]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B234** ÔÇö next free ID after B233)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/tender-goldberg-ax4n5w`).** New `src/shared/ui/statusTokens.js` (`STATUS_TOKENS` + `statusToken()` + a `darken()` shade helper) is the **single source** of status visuals ÔÇö each lifecycle state gets **exactly one color + one marker shape/glyph**: **Pursuit** blue `#378ADD` hollow ring Ôùï, **Active** green `#639922` filled dot ÔùÅ, **On Hold** amber `#BA7517` pause ÔÇû, **Complete** gray `#888780` check Ô£ô, **Dead** red `#E24B4A` ├ù (label struck through). MapFinder.jsx's three formerly-divergent sources ÔÇö `STATUS_STYLE` (chips/list/boundary), `BUILDING_COLORS` (pins), `STATUS_GLYPH` ÔÇö were deleted; the chips, list-item markers, map building-pins, saved-site boundary lines, **and** the right-click status menu now all read the tokens (so the three surfaces can't drift again). Reassignment is intentional: Complete moved **greenÔåÆgray** so green reads as Active only; settled states recede, decision states stand out. **Module accents** (Site `#1D9E75` / Schedule `#7F77DD` / Markup `#EF9F27`, `moduleAccent.js`) are now confined to the tab row ÔÇö the pins no longer borrow `#EF9F27`/`#1D9E75` for status (the mismatch this closes).
> **Dedup:** **amends B161** (the building pin now pulls its body fill from these tokens instead of a hardcoded `BUILDING_COLORS`); the **progress arc** (B161/B163, construction/analysis %) stays a **separate encoding** and is not part of this status palette. Supersedes the ad-hoc per-surface status colors. **Self-verified headless** (**V57**): all five pin colors correct; `#EF9F27`/`#1D9E75` confirmed **absent** from pins. lint 0 ┬À 448 tests ┬À build green.

### B233 ÔÇö Address search should select the parcel and surface its info `[Site Planner / map]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B233** ÔÇö next free ID after B232; depends on B232 for centering)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/tender-goldberg-ax4n5w`).** After geocoding (B232), `selectParcelAt(latlng, label)` runs the **same parcel pipeline a click uses** ÔÇö `identifyParcelDetailed` (new in `lib/arcgis.js`) ÔåÆ `addParcelHit` (a shared highlight+select helper **extracted** so click-select and address-select are identical) ÔÇö so the parcel under the point is highlighted, added to `selected[]` (the existing "Plan site" flow then works unchanged), and an **info card** drops in under the search pill with the **parcel ID + key appraisal attributes** (owner / land+improvement+total value / land use / legal) + the measured acreage. The appraisal labeller was extracted to `lib/appraisal.js` (`apprRows`/`apprVal`/`findAttr`, formerly inline in SitePlanner.jsx) so the planner's "Appraisal data" panel and this card share **one** source ÔÇö not a parallel pipeline. **"Source unavailable"** (no parcel service responded ÔÇö `responded === 0`) is a **distinct** card state from **"no parcel at this point"** (a service answered, no hit).
> **Dedup:** reuses the existing parcel-query + selection pipeline (`identifyParcelAcross`/`outerRingsLngLat`/the highlight) and the planner's appraisal labeller ÔÇö no parallel system. **Self-verified headless** (**V57**): searching "19630 Crossbranch Dr, Katy, TX" recentered and produced a "­ƒôì CROSSBRANCH" card (Account 1403613 ┬À 0.27 ac ┬À land/improvement/total values ┬À land use ┬À legal) with the parcel highlighted + "Plan this site ÔåÆ".

### B232 ÔÇö Address search doesn't recenter the map on "Go" `[Site Planner / map]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B232** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (PRs #188/#189/#190/#191) took B225ÔÇôB231 (building feature-buttons / Yield donut / dock-zone stack / Bluebeam vertex editing) while this was in flight, so B232ÔÇôB236 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/tender-goldberg-ax4n5w`).** Confirmed the impl note first: the geocode handler (`goAddress` in MapFinder.jsx) **already** flew the camera, so the bug was the **geocoder itself**, not unwired plumbing. The old code called OSM Nominatim with only `countrycodes=us` and **no viewport bias**, which routinely returns nothing for a bare house-number street address ("19630 Crossbranch") ÔÇö so the map silently stayed put. Replaced with `geocodeAddress(q, center)`: tries the **Esri World Geocoding Service** `findAddressCandidates` first (keyless single lookup, the same ArcGIS family the app already uses for imagery/parcels, far better at exact US street addresses) **biased to the current map center** via `&location=`; falls back to **Nominatim biased to a ~0.6┬░ viewbox** around the center. On a hit, `flyTo([lat,lon], 18)` ÔÇö parcel-level zoom. Failure stays honest (the "couldn't find that address" toast).
> **Dedup / scope:** a separate failure path from B233 ÔÇö this is the **geocodeÔåÆcamera** half; B233 is the parcel identify/select/info half. **Self-verified headless** (**V57**): "19630 Crossbranch Dr, Katy, TX" ÔåÆ Go recenters the map on the parcel (not a no-op).

### B230 ÔÇö Replace always-visible "+" midpoint handles with Shift-click / right-click vertex insertion (Bluebeam-style) `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B221, renumbered **B230** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (PRs #184/#186/#188/#189/#191) took B221ÔÇôB229 (lazy-chunk recovery, Schedule fixes, building-button gating, Yield-panel redesign, dock-zone stack) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B229; the implementing commit + the PR still say "B221")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/funny-franklin-hrk815`).** The permanent "+" midpoint handle that every editable edge used to carry is **gone in every state** (selected, moving, all of them); editing now follows the Bluebeam model. Built **once in a shared vertex-editing layer** so all editable geometry inherits it ÔÇö **detention ponds, parcels, polygon elements, measurement polygons/lines, markup poly/line, and easement paths** ÔÇö not per element type.
- **Shared core (new):** `editablePath()` resolves the current selection to a uniform `{ layer, id, pts, closed, min }`; `hitEditPath()` returns the nearest vertex / nearest edge within a **screen-pixel** tolerance (9px vert / 11px edge ÔåÆ zoom-independent grab radius); `insertVtx()` and `deleteVtx()` are single dispatchers keyed by `layer` (parcel/el/measure/markup/ease), each using functional `setState` so they always act on current state. Wired through **three capture-phase handlers** on the canvas `<svg>` (`onPointerDownCapture` / `onContextMenuCapture` / `onPointerMoveCapture`) so the same interaction reaches every layer **before** its own handlers, **without overlaying any new hit target** ÔÇö a normal click/drag/select is untouched.
- **Add a vertex:** **Shift+click an edge** (away from a corner) drops a control point exactly at the click point on that segment; **right-click an edge** ÔåÆ portal "Add control point". **Delete a vertex:** **right-click a vertex** ÔåÆ portal "Delete control point"; **Delete/Backspace** removes the active control point. Deletion is blocked below the geometry minimum (**3 for a polygon**), and the menu item reads "(min reached)" + disables there.
- **Discoverability:** a faint **candidate-insertion dot** snaps to the nearest point on the edge under the cursor on **edge-hover during edit**, and brightens while **Shift** arms the insert ÔÇö replacing exactly what the permanent "+" advertised, without the clutter. The **active control point** (the Delete target) renders inverted.
- **Kept:** the square corner handles (the real vertices) ÔÇö shown only when selected, drag behavior unchanged. The five `add*Vertex` "+" handlers and the per-handle Shift-click-delete branches were removed (superseded). **Tie-break rule:** a **corner always wins over its adjacent edges** (`hitEditPath` returns both at a corner; a vertex right-click deletes, never adds), mirroring the insert rule `edge && !vertex` ÔÇö this was the one real bug found in headless testing and fixed before merge.
- **Portal menu:** the Add/Delete-control-point menu is `createPortal`-mounted at `document.body` (z 6000/6001) so it can never be clipped or trapped behind the canvas / tool-rail stacking contexts; it reuses the app's existing `menu`/`menuPanel`/`menuItem` styling.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b221-b222.mjs`, logged-out, live Harris aerial, a polygon detention pond in a parcel): after selecting the pond ÔÇö **0** "+" midpoint handles, **4** square vertex handles; **Shift+click an edge ÔåÆ 4ÔåÆ5** vertices with the candidate dot shown while Shift held; **edge-hover (no shift) shows the dot**; **right-click a vertex ÔåÆ portal "Delete control point" ÔåÆ 5ÔåÆ4**; **Delete key ÔåÆ 4ÔåÆ3**; repeated deletes **stop at 3** (polygon min guard). **0 console errors.** Screenshots `ui-audit/screens/b221-pond-selected.png` + `b221-after-edits.png` eyeballed (squares only, no plus marks). Logged **VERIFICATION V56**. lint **0 errors** ┬À **436 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Dedup:** AMENDS the now-done Bluebeam-editing items **B141** (editable vertices on the measurement tools) + **B147** (markup/shape vertex-edit for lines/polylines/polygons), which had introduced the "+" midpoint handle, and addresses the clutter **B215** flagged (the "+" stacking at the edge midpoint). Distinct from the open **B155/B156** (whole-shape interior hit-testing + hover highlight) ÔÇö those select/hover a shape; this inserts/deletes control points on the already-selected shape.

### B231 ÔÇö Restyle the detention pond to a modern "Cartographic" water-body treatment `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B222, renumbered **B231** ÔÇö next free ID after B230; concurrent `main` took B221ÔÇôB229; the implementing commit + the PR still say "B222")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/funny-franklin-hrk815`).** The detention pond no longer reads as dated clip-art. The decorative wavy-line water hatch, the saturated outline (which read as the Markup-accent orange ÔÇö the wrong signal for water), and the monospace label are all replaced with a modern cartographic water-body style (how Mapbox / ArcGIS Pro render water). Implemented as a **named, reusable style token** so future water/basin features inherit it; **scoped to the pond element type** (never auto-applied to other polygons).
- **Token (`lib/planStyle.js`):** the pond `TYPE` is now `{ fill:"#5B97A5", stroke:"#2C5D6B", weight:2, cartoWater:true }` (replaced the old `water:true` wavy-hatch flag); `elStyle` exposes `cartoWater`. One token, reusable.
- **Fill (`renderElPx`):** a **radial steel-teal gradient** `#grad-water` (added to `<defs>`) that **deepens toward the center** ÔÇö `#2F6675` at the middle ÔåÆ `#5B97A5` at the edge ÔÇö at ~80% opacity, so a pond reads as water *with volume*, not a flat cutout. The old `pat-water` wavy pattern is deleted. Applies to both the polygon and rectangular pond branches.
- **Outline:** `#2C5D6B`, **2px at a constant screen-pixel width** (the canvas is drawn in pixel space, so the stroke never hairlines when zoomed out nor fattens when zoomed in); **3px when selected**. **No orange anywhere on the water body** ÔÇö selection is signaled by the thicker teal stroke + the (transient, orange) editing handles, not a colour change on the feature itself.
- **Label:** switched monospace ÔåÆ **Inter** (the app's proportional UI sans), **dark slate `#0E2E36` with a white casing/halo** (always, so it stays legible over busy aerial), two lines kept ÔÇö name above ("Detention Pond" / "Existing Detention Pond"), area sub-line below ("0.47 ac ┬À 20,272 sf") ÔÇö centered on the centroid (or the existing baseline anchor in expansion mode). Threaded through the shared LOD/label renderer via a per-candidate `carto` flag, so **only the pond label changes**; every other element label stays mono.
- **Map overview:** `MapFinder.jsx` resolves element fills through the same `elStyle`, so the map's pond pins inherit the flat steel-teal fill + teal outline automatically (no wavy, no orange) ÔÇö no second code path; the expansion baseline ghost stroke was recoloured to match.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b221-b222.mjs`): `#grad-water` exists with stops **`#2F6675` ÔåÆ `#5B97A5`**; **no `#pat-water`**; the pond fill uses `url(#grad-water)`; the **`#2C5D6B`** outline is present; the label uses **Inter** + fill **`#0E2E36`** reading "Detention Pond / 2.14 ac ┬À 93,100 sf". Screenshot `ui-audit/screens/b221-pond-selected.png` eyeballed ÔÇö steel-teal body with a deeper center, crisp teal edge, haloed sans label, no hatch, no orange. **0 console errors.** lint 0 ┬À 414 tests ┬À build green.
> **Dedup:** AMENDS the pond rendering from **B132/B139** (expansion baseline + ghost) and **B140/B157** (on-map pond area labels ÔÇö the two-line name+area is preserved). Distinct from **B219** (cartographic restyle of the map *furniture* ÔÇö north arrow + scale bar); this is the *water body* itself.

### B239 ÔÇö Restore the per-zone "´╝ï" to add trailer parking / buffer from the selected court (regression from B228/B229) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported, angry ÔÇö "I literally can't add the 50 foot trailer parking anymore. I lost that ability." Filed provisionally as B230, but concurrent `main` took B230ÔÇôB238 while this was in flight, so renumbered to the real next free ID **B239**.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/practical-faraday-9mmfch`).** B229's panel reorg **removed the "´╝ï 50ÔÇ▓ trailer parking (far side)" button** that lived on a selected truck court and replaced it with a depth-only editor ÔÇö so selecting the court no longer offered any way to add the trailer parking. Worse, the building-level "´╝ï" advances **every dock side together at the min level**, so on a cross-dock building with a court on only ONE side it stays stuck adding *courts* to the empty side and never reaches trailer parking on the side that has a court. Net effect: the owner could not add the 50ÔÇ▓ trailer parking at all. **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** the selected dock-zone panel (court / trailer / buffer) now shows, beside the inline depth field, a **"´╝ï Add <next outward zone>"** button (court ÔåÆ "´╝ï Add trailer parking", trailer ÔåÆ "´╝ï Add buffer") that adds the next zone **on that one dock side** via `addZoneOnSide`, plus a **"´╝ì Remove <this zone>"** (cascades to outer zones). This restores the exact per-side "´╝ï" workflow that existed before B228/B229, independent of the other dock side; the building-level unit "´╝ï/ÔêÆ" stays as the grow/shrink-everything convenience.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-dock-zones.mjs` regression ÔÇö all checks still pass ÔÇö plus a focused repro: cross-dock building with a court on the RIGHT only ÔåÆ select the court ÔåÆ "´╝ï Add trailer parking" appears ÔåÆ adds trailer on the **right side only** (count 1, not both) ÔåÆ select the trailer ÔåÆ "´╝ï Add buffer" ÔåÆ adds buffer; **0 page errors**). lint **0 errors** ┬À **481 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Logged **VERIFICATION V55** (updated).

### B229 ÔÇö Dock Features panel: add-controls on top, stacked zone list `[Site Planner / UI]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B222, renumbered **B229** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B221ÔÇôB227 while this was in flight, so B228/B229 are the real next free IDs)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/practical-faraday-9mmfch`, ships with B228).** The selected-building **Dock features** panel is reorganised: **add-controls on top** ÔÇö the dock-zone stack **"+ / ÔêÆ"** (label names the next/outermost zone, disabled at empty/full), a **car-parking (ends)** add+remove, and the **bump-outs** control kept in the top group but **visually distinct** (its own dashed-divided sub-section, purple accent, "footprint modifier" note). **Below them, the active dock-face zones listed in outward order** (1. Truck court ÔåÆ 2. Trailer parking ÔåÆ 3. Buffer), each row an **inline editable depth field** + the **LIFO "ÔêÆ"** (active only on the outermost row). The shared **footprint / bump-out total / dock-doors summary stays at the bottom.** A selected court/trailer/buffer shows a focused "Dock zone" depth editor (length + rotation are stack-controlled). The right-click building menu mirrors the same add-next / remove-outer actions.

### B228 ÔÇö Building-anchored dock-zone stack with LIFO +/ÔêÆ `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B221, renumbered **B228** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B221ÔÇôB227 (PRs #186 lazy-chunk reload, #188 B225/B226 feature-button visibility, #189 B227 yield panel, #184 B222ÔÇôB224 Schedule) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B227)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/practical-faraday-9mmfch`).** The building footprint is now the control hub for the dock-side site elements: a single **"+"** adds the next zone **outward** from the dock face, a single **"ÔêÆ"** peels the **outermost** one off (LIFO). Fixed outward order **truck court ÔåÆ trailer parking ÔåÆ buffer**; "+" walks out (court ÔåÆ trailer ÔåÆ buffer), "ÔêÆ" walks in (buffer ÔåÆ trailer ÔåÆ court).
- **Reused, not duplicated:** the court stays the existing `truckCourt` paving strip and trailer parking the existing far-side `forCourt` striped row ÔÇö the change unifies their geometry under one pure layout and adds the buffer. **Buffer is the new zone** ÔÇö a sage `landscape` clear strip (buffer/setback) bonded to its trailer via a new `forTrailer` tag, labelled "<d>ÔÇ▓ Buffer".
- **One geometry source:** `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/dockZones.js` (`DOCK_ZONES`, `zoneDepthDefaults`, `layoutZone`) positions all three zones flush-outward from the building face, full wall length, trailer rotated so its stalls run along the wall. Used by add / remove / inline depth-edit / **building-resize** (the rewritten `refitChildren` re-lays the stack), so depths survive a resize and outer zones always stay flush. **12 unit tests** (`test/dockZones.test.js`): order, configurable defaults, flush-outward stacking (gap-free, no overlap), vertical-side rotation, building rotation.
- **Editable depths inline** (US survey feet, EPSG:2278), defaults **135 / 50 / 15** and **user-configurable** in **Setup ÔåÆ Dock zones** (`settings.truckCourtD/trailerParkD/bufferD`) ÔÇö not hardcoded.
- **Creation rerouted through the building "+":** trailer parking + buffer now add from the building panel (and the on-canvas court "+" ÔåÆ trailer "+" ÔåÆ buffer "+" chain). **Car parking** is **also** added from the building panel but occupies the **ends (non-dock short sides)** and is tracked as its **own element outside** the LIFO stack, with its own remove. **[assumption flagged for Michael: should car parking instead join the LIFO removal order? ÔÇö shipped as a separate control.]**
- **Both long sides:** zones **mirror to every dock face with doors** (cross-dock = both); the "+"/"ÔêÆ" operate on the whole dock-zone set as a **unit** (one "+" advances every dock side; "ÔêÆ" peels the outermost off every dock side). **[assumption flagged for Michael ÔÇö shipped this way.]**
- **Bump-outs are NOT in this stack** ÔÇö a footprint modifier, behaviour unchanged; only relocated in the panel (B229).
- **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-dock-zones.mjs`, logged-out, cross-dock building): "+"├ù3 ÔåÆ 2 courts / 2 trailers / 2 buffers, outward order courtÔåÆtrailerÔåÆbuffer, "+" disabled at full; inline buffer depth 15ÔåÆ40 grew the band with outer zones staying flush; building resize 600ÔåÆ760 kept all zones attached, full-width and flush; "ÔêÆ"├ù3 peeled bufferÔåÆtrailerÔåÆcourt; car-parking-ends added a field; **0 page errors**. lint **0 errors** ┬À **448 tests** ┬À build green; the `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk split still holds. Logged **VERIFICATION V55**.
> **Dedup:** NOT B71 (per-element trailer curb 6ÔÇ│ÔåÆ12ÔÇ│) or B78 (stall-layout freeze guard); orthogonal to main's B225/B226 (those size-GATE these feature buttons ÔÇö visibility; this is the zone stack the buttons drive). Reuses the court (`truckCourt`/`makeStrip`), far-side trailer (`forCourt`/`oppTrailerGeom`) and bump-out (`dogEar`/`placeDogEars`) machinery; the orphaned old one-shot helpers (`addTruckCourt`, `addStripSide`, `makeOppTrailer`, `TRUCK_COURT_D`) were removed.

### B227 ÔÇö Redesign Site Yield panel: composition donut, identity icon, grouped rows, semantic palette `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B225, renumbered **B227** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#188) minted B225 + B226 (building feature-edit-button visibility) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B226)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/jolly-turing-n8rmzy`).** Presentational reskin of the Site Yield panel ÔÇö **no engine / data-model / RLS / persistence change** (it stays per-user/private, reading the exact values the planner already computes). New self-contained `YieldPanel` component (+ a scoped `YIELD_PAL` semantic palette) replaces the old generic `<Section title="Site yield">` in `SitePlanner.jsx`; the generic `Section` and every other panel are untouched. Layout topÔåÆbottom: **(1)** a header row = a **parcel-with-footprint identity glyph** (terracotta lot outline `#C0532E` + filled footprint + a half-opacity outbuilding) in a 32 px soft `#F6E7DD` tile + the tracked "SITE YIELD" label + a collapse chevron (its own `useState`, mirroring `Section` ÔÇö collapse/expand preserved). **(2)** the three existing KPI cards (Site ac ┬À Building abbreviated "227k" ┬À Coverage %), card `#F2ECE1`, tabular-mono. **(3)** a new **composition donut + legend**: how the site partitions into Building / Paving / Open-green / Detention, derived from engine OUTPUTS only ÔÇö `buildingPct = coverage`, `pavingPct = max(0, impervious% ÔêÆ coverage%)`, `detentionPct = detentionSf/siteSf├ù100`, `openPct = 100 ÔêÆ the rest (clamp ÔëÑ0)` ÔÇö so the four always sum to 100 and the ring closes. SVG (~100 px, 13 px stroke): faint `#ECE3D5` track + four `stroke-dasharray` arcs, group rotated ÔêÆ90┬░ to start at top, butt caps, contiguous (`dashoffset = ÔêÆ╬ú earlier arcs`); center = site acreage over "acres". Legend beside it always renders the **Detention row even at 0 %** with a muted hollow zero-state swatch (`#DCE5EB`/`#C2D2DC`) ÔÇö zero Ôëá absent. **(4)** a hairline divider. **(5)** **grouped detail rows** (Land┬Àsage / Building┬Àterracotta / Parking┬Àtaupe / Stormwater┬Àblue), each a semantic dot + tracked label over label-left/mono-value rows, all wired to live values; the prior extras are preserved in their natural group (bump-out sub-line + inactive-parcel note under Land/Building; the easement encumbrance readout as a subordinate trailing "Easements" block). **One semantic colour = one meaning** across the donut arcs, the legend swatches, and the group dots. Out of scope by request: the headroom/limit bars + scheme-compare view (set aside in chat).
> **Self-verified headless** (**V54**, `ui-audit/verify-yield.mjs`, logged-out, two seeds against the local `dist/` build): the four donut arcs sum to **100.0 % of the circumference in both seeds** (ring closes); seed A (29.25 ac ┬À 226,576-sf building ┬À paving only, no pond) renders 18 / 13 / 0 / 69 with **no detention arc**, seed B (+ parking + pond) paints all four incl. the dusty-blue detention slice; the legend %s match the detail rows; the **Building KPI shows `227k` while the Building row shows the full `226,576 sf`**; the Detention legend row shows **0 % explicitly** (zero-state swatch) rather than disappearing; seed B's Parking group reads live **Car stalls 220 ┬À 0.97/1k sf**; the header collapse toggle hides/restores the body; **0 page errors** (screens `yield-panel.png` + `yield-panel-detention.png`). lint **0 errors** ┬À **436 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk split intact.
> **Dedup:** distinct from the existing Yield-panel content items ÔÇö the easement encumbrance readout (Done), the "Excludes N inactive parcel(s)" note, and the road-cost takeoff Section (left as its own collapsible `<Section>` directly below, untouched). Those are *numbers*; this was a *presentation* pass over the same values, so no metric or persisted field changed.

### B226 ÔÇö Show feature-edit buttons only on the selected/hovered building `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B226** ÔÇö next free ID after B225)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/lucid-heisenberg-mjhpw3`).** **Dedup finding:** the per-building feature-add buttons ALREADY rendered only on the *selected* building (gated on `sel.kind==='el' && el.id===sel.id`), never "on every building in view" ÔÇö so that half of the arriving ask was already true. Implemented the rest as the requested SINGLE visibility rule: the buttons render for exactly ONE building ÔÇö `featActiveId =` the selected one, else (in select mode) the one HOVERED ÔÇö and only when the **B225** footprint-size gate passes.
