Net rule: **buttons visible iff (footprint large enough on screen) AND (building selected OR hovered).** **Hover** (`SitePlanner.jsx`: new `hoverElId` state + a block in `onMove`) is computed **position-based** (the topmost building whose footprint bbox contains the cursor), NOT via SVG `onPointerEnter/Leave` ÔÇö because the buttons sit INSIDE the footprint, an enter/leave trigger would flicker the buttons off the instant the cursor moved toward one; the position test keeps the hover alive. It only runs while idle in select mode with **nothing selected** (selection otherwise drives the buttons), so there's no per-frame churn once you've clicked. Hover previews the add-buttons only ÔÇö resize/rotate grips stay selection-only.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b225-b226.mjs`, logged-out): with nothing selected, the not-hovering baseline = **0** buttons, hovering the building reveals its **8** (`b226-hover.png` shows the add-buttons with NO selection grips ÔåÆ pure hover), and moving off hides them (**0**). So the buttons appear on the ONE active building only, gated by the same size rule. Logged **VERIFICATION V53**. lint 0 ┬À 414 tests ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Dedup:** same render condition as **B225** (folded into one rule, per the arriving note "Combine with NEW-1 into one visibility rule"). Sibling of **B156** (hover-highlight the markup under the cursor) ÔÇö that's the markup-layer version; this is the building-handle case, position-based for the same flicker reason. Distinct from selection hit-testing (**B155**).

### B225 ÔÇö Hide building feature-edit buttons when the on-screen footprint is too small (anti-cluster) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B225** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B220, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/lucid-heisenberg-mjhpw3`).** Zooming OUT on a building with feature-add buttons shrank its footprint while the fixed-pixel +/ÔêÆ buttons (truck-dock / sidewalk / bump-out / parking-row ÔÇö each inset ~22px inside a wall, r=9) stayed full size, so the cluster grew larger than the footprint and spilled past the edges into an unreadable pile (the owner's screenshot). **Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`):** each feature-add button is now gated on the building's RENDERED on-screen footprint size in PIXELS (`el.w/el.h * view.ppf`) against a tunable module const `FEAT_BTN_MIN_PX = 72` (Ôëê the ~22px inset + 9px radius clearance on each side, plus a small legibility margin ÔÇö opposite buttons overlap below ~68px). The gate is **per-axis**: a wall's inset +/ÔêÆ needs its PERPENDICULAR on-screen dimension ÔëÑ 72px (top/bottom gated on height-px, left/right on width-px); the corner bump-outs need BOTH axes. So a long/narrow on-screen footprint keeps its long-side buttons and drops only the cramped short-end ones ÔÇö the overlap case the arriving note flagged ("also check") is handled inherently, no collapse-menu needed ÔÇö and a fully-small footprint drops ALL of them (no spill). Applied to all three feature-add node groups (`sideAddNodes`, `courtAddNodes`, `parkingAddNodes`). **Resize/rotate grips are intentionally NOT gated** (still usable on a small building ÔÇö the spec says "only the editable feature buttons are suppressed"); the map's **Building Pin + Progress Arc live in `MapFinder.jsx`** (a different component) and are **untouched**, so they stay visible at every zoom level, as required.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-b225-b226.mjs`, logged-out, 400ÔÇ▓├ù300ÔÇ▓ cross-dock building): swept the wheel zoom from large to tiny; at every non-ambiguous step the drawn button count matched the rule predicted from the measured footprint px ÔÇö **8** while large (494├ù370 ÔÇª 127├ù95px), **2** (long-side sidewalks only) at **81├ù60px** (height < 72 Ôëñ width ÔåÆ per-axis collision handling), **0** once tiny (Ôëñ 64├ù48px). Screens `b225-zoomed-in.png` / `b225-partial-longside.png` / `b225-zoomed-out.png`. Logged **VERIFICATION V53**. lint 0 ┬À 414 tests ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Dedup:** distinct from **B149** (LOD tiering ÔÇö which *dimensions/labels* draw at a given zoom) and **B121** (label-overlap engine) ÔÇö those govern annotations; this governs the *interactive edit handles*. Combined with **B226** into ONE visibility rule (size-gate AND selected-or-hovered). Reuses the **B30** precedent of pixel-projected (not feet-based) thresholds.

### B224 ÔÇö "Schedule-assembling" themed loading animation, reusable engine across modules `[Scheduler]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; provisionally B220, renumbered **B224** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#183, #185, #187) took B218ÔÇôB221 while this was in flight, so the real next free block was B222ÔÇôB224)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/awesome-pascal-zlb19p`).** A reusable, per-module themed loader replaces the bare "Loading workspaceÔÇª" text. New `src/shared/ui/ModuleLoader.jsx` (one animation engine) + pure, unit-tested theming `src/shared/ui/moduleLoaderTheme.js` (skin + label) over the shared `src/shared/ui/moduleAccent.js` (`MODULE_ACCENT` extracted to a React-free module; `AppHeader` re-exports it for back-compat). **Schedule skin** = a Gantt building itself: zebra row bands fade in topÔåÆbottom (the real banding tints), ghost task bars wipe in leftÔåÆright staggered (~100 ms/row) in the accent **`#7F77DD`**, milestone diamonds pop at bar ends, and a soft vertical **playhead** sweeps leftÔåÆright; the assemble loop fades at its boundary so it repeats seamlessly, then **cross-fades** (not a hard cut) into the real Gantt. **Site Planner skin** = a parcel outline **stroke-drawing itself** + building footprints fading in + a north-arrow, in the Site accent **`#1D9E75`** ÔÇö same engine, different grammar. **Accessibility:** honors `prefers-reduced-motion` (drops the cascade + sweep for a static skeleton + gentle pulse). **Threshold:** holds invisible for ~250 ms so fast loads never flash it. **Wired** as the Shell `Suspense` fallback (themed by active module ÔÇö covers the chunk-fetch phase) AND as a cross-fading overlay in `Scheduler.jsx` over the iframe until its first `planar:nav-state` (covers the heavy iframe boot ÔÇö the actually-slow phase), with a 9 s safety timeout so it can never stick.
> **Self-verified headless** (**V52**, `ui-audit/verify-loader-prefetch.mjs`): on Schedule nav the loader shows (`role="status"`, `aria-label="Assembling scheduleÔÇª"`, 12 elements in `#7F77DD`, SVG present ÔÇö `loader-schedule.png`) then cross-fades to the iframe; with `prefers-reduced-motion` the cascade + playhead are dropped (**0 animated bars, no playhead** ÔÇö `loader-reduced-motion.png`). +5 tests in `test/moduleLoaderTheme.test.js` (per-module skin/accent, fallback, threshold). **Dedup:** the loader reuses `MODULE_ACCENT`, so it can never drift from the tab/brand colors. **Pairs with B223** (that shrinks the wait; this covers the residual). lint 0 ┬À 419 tests ┬À build green; lazy chunk split intact.

### B223 ÔÇö Speed up Schedule module load `[Scheduler]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; provisionally B219, renumbered **B223** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#183, #185, #187) took B218ÔÇôB221)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/awesome-pascal-zlb19p`).** Profiled first: the Schedule tab embeds the standalone Gantt app (`public/sequence/index.html`, ~692 KB) in an iframe, so the dominant navigation cost is **doc-fetch** (downloading + parsing that HTML each visit), not the trivial `Scheduler.jsx` chunk, and not layout/paint. **Shipped the prefetch (the measured win):** new `src/app/modulePrefetch.js` ÔÇö `prefetchModule(id)` warms a workspace's lazy chunk (same dynamic-import specifier Vite already code-splits) **and, for Schedule, injects `<link rel="prefetch" href="/sequence/">`** so the iframe doc is cached before the click. Wired two triggers: **idle** (`prefetchOnIdle(["scheduler","doc-review"])` via `requestIdleCallback` in `Shell.jsx`, after first paint) and **hover-intent** (`ModuleTab`'s `onMouseEnter` in `AppHeader.jsx`). Lazy-loading still gates the first boot ÔÇö nothing prefetches until idle. **Confirmed already-satisfied (no change needed):** both `GridView` and `GanttView` already **virtualize** (row windowing by scroll, `startIdx`/`endIdx` + slice), `GanttView` already `useMemo`s its dep-line/month/year layout, and the app already paints from an inline `__PLANAR_DATA__` seed before the Supabase sync (so the cloud round-trip is off the critical first paint and the silent-failure save guard is intact).
> **Self-verified headless** (**V52**, `ui-audit/verify-loader-prefetch.mjs`): the `/sequence/` prefetch link is injected after boot-idle; no boot console errors. **Pairs with B224.** lint 0 ┬À 419 tests ┬À build green; `Scheduler` (1.79 kB) / `DocReview` / `SitePlannerApp` chunks stay separate.

### B222 ÔÇö Row color fill hides Gantt bars `[Scheduler]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B218, renumbered **B222** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#183, #185, #187) took B218ÔÇôB221)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/awesome-pascal-zlb19p`).** Applying a row color fill made the task bars on that row disappear from the Gantt. **Root cause was a color collision (b):** in `GanttView` (`public/sequence/index.html`), `task.rowColor` was overloaded ÔÇö the same field that paints the row background (`rowBg`) was also the bar fill via `task.rowColor || <neutral>` for **leaf bars, summary brackets, AND milestone diamonds** ÔÇö so a fill repainted every on-row datum its own color and it vanished into its own background. **Fix:** each datum now keeps its **own identity hue independent of the row fill** ÔÇö leaf bars + milestone diamonds = neutral gray `#94a3b8`, summary brackets = depth-navy ÔÇö and carries a **hairline edge** (`#475569`, a slate darker than the fill) so it stays legible on any fill; notably the **solid/complete leaf bar gained a 1px border** (it was borderless and was the one that fully disappeared on a matching fill). The row fill is now pure background decoration. **Paint order verified + documented** (z-index): row fill (row `<div>`, z auto, bottom) ÔåÆ grid/today/dependency lines (z2ÔÇô4) ÔåÆ bars/milestones (z5, data layer) ÔåÆ bar + name labels (z6ÔÇô7, top). Holistic: covers bars, diamonds, links, and labels.
> **Self-verified headless** (**V51**, `ui-audit/verify-rowfill-bars.mjs`, logged-out seed): with a leaf task seeded a row fill, the fill paints the row bands (`rowBands:2`) with **zero bar-sized collisions** (`barCollisions:0`); the bar keeps `#94a3b8` + the `#475569` 1px edge (`rowfill-bars-crop.png` shows the marker clearly on the tinted row). **Dedup:** distinct from B210ÔÇôB212 (the prior Gantt tranche ÔÇö brackets / status-as-fill / columns). The PDF/Print export path (`buildGanttSVG`) already paints bars neutral gray/navy and never reads `rowColor` for bars ÔÇö no collision there, left untouched. lint **0 errors** ┬À **419 tests** ┬À build green; lazy chunk split intact.

### B221 ÔÇö Lazy workspaces fail to load after a deploy: "Failed to fetch dynamically imported module" (stale content-hashed chunk) `[App Shell / Deploy]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; provisionally B218, renumbered **B221** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (PR #183) took B218 (dead header controls) + B219 (map-furniture restyle) and a parallel session took B220 (map-finder over-zoom) while this was in flight, so B221 is the real next free ID after B220. The branch's first commit + the PR were opened as "B218". Deduped against the PDF.js `import()` items (B72/B67/B180 lazy-load a heavy library on first use ÔÇö unrelated to the stale-chunk-after-deploy failure here).)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/cool-ptolemy-2lb1js`).** Root cause confirmed as the standard Vite stale-chunk-after-deploy problem (NOT a logic bug inside DocReview, and NOT a broken/partial build ÔÇö the build is green and all three workspace chunks emit cleanly under `/assets/`): an already-open tab is holding the *previous* deploy's `index.html`, which references content-hashed filenames like `/assets/DocReview-<oldhash>.js`; once Cloudflare serves the new build those files are gone, so switching into a not-yet-loaded workspace 404s and throws the user onto the workspace error screen. A hard reload fixes it because it re-fetches the current `index.html` (and its new hashes). Fix, applied globally so it covers **every** lazy route (Site Planner, Document Review, Sequence), not just DocReview:
- **Auto-reload-once on dynamic-import failure** ÔÇö `src/app/chunkReload.js` (`installChunkReloadGuard`, wired in `src/main.jsx` before render) listens for Vite's `vite:preloadError` window event and reloads the page once. **Loop guard:** the time of the last auto-reload is stamped in `sessionStorage` (`planyr:chunkReloadAt`); a second preloadError within a 10 s cooldown does **not** reload ÔÇö a genuinely-missing chunk (a broken/partial deploy) falls through to the existing `ErrorBoundary` instead of reload-looping. The stamp self-expires so a *later, separate* deploy re-arms a fresh one-time recovery ÔÇö deliberately more robust than a boolean flag cleared on mount, which would either race the reload (auto-loop if the default workspace's chunk is the missing one) or reload on every click of a truly-broken module (never letting its error show). The pure decision (`shouldReloadAfterPreloadError`) is unit-tested.
- **Cloudflare cache headers** ÔÇö `public/_headers` (copied verbatim to `dist/_headers` at the deploy root): `/assets/*` (the content-hashed, immutable build files) get `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`; the HTML entry points (`/`, `/index.html`, `/sequence/`) get `Cache-Control: no-cache` so a new deploy's asset hashes propagate on the next load instead of being pinned behind a hard-cached HTML.
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors ┬À **418 tests** (incl. the new `test/chunkReload.test.js`, 4) ┬À build green; `dist/_headers` lands at the deploy root and the three lazy chunks (`DocReview-*`, `Scheduler-*`, `SitePlannerApp-*`) emit hashed under `/assets/`. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-chunk-reload.mjs`, **3/3** against the built bundle on `vite preview`): a first `vite:preloadError` reloads the page; a second within the cooldown does NOT (no loop); pushing the stamp past the cooldown reloads again (re-armed).
> **Owner note:** the `_headers` cache rules take effect automatically once Cloudflare redeploys (on merge to `main`); nothing to configure. **Dedup:** distinct from the PDF.js `import()` lazy-loading in B72/B67/B180 (those defer a heavy library until first use ÔÇö a feature), and from B205-era ErrorBoundary work (that catches *render* crashes; this catches *chunk-fetch* failures and recovers before the boundary is reached).

### B220 ÔÇö Esri imagery shows "Map data not yet available" placeholder on over-zoom (map finder) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B218, renumbered **B220** ÔÇö concurrent `main` PR #183 took B218 (dead header controls) + B219 (map-furniture restyle) while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B219. The implementing commits on this branch still say "B218".)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/quirky-mayer-s3n0pm`).** On the Site Planner **map finder**, zooming in past the area's native imagery ceiling with Esri selected filled the whole canvas with gray "Map data not yet available" tiles while street labels still rendered on top. **Root cause (confirmed):** Esri World Imagery serves those placeholder tiles as **HTTP 200** (not errors), so Leaflet's error-tile fallback never fires; on a retina/HiDPI display `detectRetina` (added in B170 for sharpness) fetches one zoom level **higher** than the display zoom (zoomOffset +1), so a plain `maxNativeZoom: 19` let deep zoom request **z20** = the placeholder. **B182 already fixed this exact bug, but only on the planner-canvas backdrop** (`SitePlanner.jsx` GEO_BASEMAP detail layer's `detailMaxNative`); the **map-finder** layer (`MapFinder.jsx`) got `detectRetina` from B170 but **never the matching clamp** ÔÇö an incomplete original fix across the two call sites, not a refactor revert. **Fix (`MapFinder.jsx`):** the imagery `L.tileLayer` now clamps `maxNativeZoom` to `L.Browser.retina ? bm.maxNative - 1 : bm.maxNative` for **every source in the Imagery dropdown** (Esri 19ÔåÆ18, USGS 16ÔåÆ15 on retina), keeping `maxZoom: 21` so Leaflet **upscales** the deepest real tile past native instead of fetching placeholders. The labels/reference overlay (World_Transportation) is capped at `maxNativeZoom: 19` so it can't diverge above the imagery at deep zoom (the "labels float over gray" tell). Durable code comments left at the `BASEMAPS` config + both layer sites so a future restructure can't silently drop them. **Verified headless** (`gis-verify/mapfinder-overzoom-verify.mjs`, the map finder driven deep over a Katy parcel at DSF 1 and DSF 2): max imagery tile zoom requested = **z19 at both densities** (was z20 on retina), max labels zoom = **z19**, **0** placeholder/past-native responses ÔÇö logged **VERIFICATION V50**. lint **0 errors** ┬À **414 tests** ┬À build green; lazy chunks intact.
> **Dedup:** RECURRENCE/completion of **B182** (done ÔÇö planner-canvas placeholder fix) across the second call site; builds on **B170** (detectRetina) + **B169** (blur diagnostic). Distinct from **B171** (gated high-res-source eval ÔÇö correctly NOT triggered; the imagery source isn't the problem) and **B162** (label zoom gating).

### B219 ÔÇö Professional cartographic restyle of the map north arrow + scale bar `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B219** ÔÇö next free ID after B218)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/quirky-cannon-ve2ppz`).** The on-screen north indicator and scale bar read as cartoonish (a chunky solid filled arrowhead; a heavy, thick segmented bar). Restyled the **shared furniture primitives** in `lib/sheetFurniture.js` ÔÇö one source of truth for BOTH the live canvas and the print/PNG export, so both inherit the upgrade with no fork. The **north arrow is now a classic two-tone surveyor's needle**: a slim elongated kite split on its spine, east half filled in a single neutral ink, west half a hairline outline, with a small "N" above in restrained weight ÔÇö no compass rose, no bright fill, no 3D. The **scale bar is now a thin segmented cartographic bar**: alternating filled/empty quarters, hairline strokes, 0 / mid / max tick numbers centered under their ticks, a muted "FEET" label, units in feet to match the EPSG:2278 (Texas State Plane, US survey feet) spine. Both sit on a **subtle warm semi-opaque backing** (`rgba(249,248,244,0.84)`) so labels stay legible over busy aerial imagery without reading as a hard white box. Sizing + anchoring are unchanged (the B196 always-visible DOM-overlay corners ÔÇö north bottom-left clear of the calibration pill, scale bottom-right under the zoom stack), so the visibility guarantees are preserved; this is a pure visual pass.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to2.mjs`, logged-out, over a live Harris-county aerial): the furniture renders as the needle (**2 `<path>` halves, 0 `<circle>`**) + the segmented bar (6 `<rect>` = plate + 4 segments + north plate), with the FEET + N labels and the new subtle plate fill; corner screenshots confirm the cartographic look, legible over the imagery; **0 console errors**. `test/sheetFurniture.test.js` (18) stays green **unchanged** ÔÇö the restyle deliberately preserved every invariant the suite asserts (rect/segment count, no compass rose, FEET/N labels, modest ~0.5 in arrow height, north-up-vs-bearing rotation). lint 0 ┬À 414 tests ┬À build green; the `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk split still holds.
> **Dedup:** distinct from **B144/B145** (export furniture clipping/sizing) and **B173/B196** (always-visible on-screen placement) ÔÇö those fixed *visibility / no-clip*; this is the *aesthetic* pass over the same shared `sheetFurniture.js` primitives, so screen and export both get it without a second code path.

### B218 ÔÇö Remove the dead hamburger menu and settings gear from the unified header `[Header/Nav]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B218** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B217, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-20 (branch `claude/quirky-cannon-ve2ppz`).** The hamburger (Ôëí, far-left of Row 1) and the settings gear (ÔÜÖ, just left of the account block) were both **pure placeholders** ÔÇö neither had an `onClick`, a nav-drawer / responsive toggle, or a route (the hamburger was literally commented "placeholder ÔÇö no behavior yet"), and a repo-wide search found **no external selector** targeting either. Removed both controls from `src/shared/ui/AppHeader.jsx`, plus the now-unused `IconBtn` helper and the `MUTED` color constant it consumed (so lint stays at 0 unused-var errors). Row 1 reflows cleanly: logo / Map crumb / project selector stay left-aligned (the left zone keeps its intended `paddingLeft:12` ÔÇö no orphaned padding), the save badge + account block stay right-aligned. AppHeader is the **single shared header for every module**, so the removal applies to Site, Schedule, and Markup at once.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to2.mjs`, logged-out): on **both** the Site and Schedule modules the header has **0** `[aria-label="Menu"]` and **0** `[aria-label="Settings"]` controls, the planyr wordmark + module tabs still render, and there are **0 console errors**. lint 0 ┬À 414 tests ┬À build green; the `AppHeader` chunk split still holds.

### B217 ÔÇö Project Files "needs filing": wire the one-click confirm (re-file an unfiled doc) `[Document Review / Files]` (bug ÔåÆ feature)  *(follow-up to B180; provisionally B189, renumbered **B217** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B189ÔÇôB216 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B216. Surfaced by comparing a parallel duplicate PR (#167, closed) against the already-shipped B180ÔÇôB183.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/friendly-hamilton-nwdxbd-b189`). The shipped B180 drawer had the **"needs filing" holding area** (banner + a "Needs filing" view) but **no actual one-click confirm** ÔÇö the NEW-1 spec calls for "a 'needs filing' holding area with one-click confirm for low-confidence / no-match," and the view only offered open/place/delete, so an unfiled file could only be re-filed by opening it and editing its Reviews fields. Fix: a new **`refileReview(id, {projectId, project, discipline})`** in `reviewStore.js` (loads the full record, updates only the filing fields, re-upserts; work layer + source bytes untouched, storageKey stays valid) + an inline **`RefileRow`** (project + discipline picker + **File** button) shown on every **unfiled** file row in `ProjectFilesDrawer.jsx`, so the holding area is actionable wherever an unfiled file appears. **Never auto-guesses** (a misfiled drawing is worse than an unfiled one ÔÇö the user confirms each). Additive + private-by-default (rides the existing RLS write path). **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-files-refile.mjs`, logged-out): the drawer + the new control render with zero JS errors; the signed-in re-file round-trip is logged for a live check (rides the proven `upsertReview` path). lint 0 ┬À build green ┬À full test suite green.
> Context: this is the one genuine gap found when comparing the closed duplicate **PR #167** against the already-merged **B180ÔÇôB183** (the parallel session's equivalent, more-capable implementation ÔÇö N-sample cross-check, `shared/placement/` layout, `stubIndexProvider` seam). Everything else in #167 duplicated shipped work, so only this piece was salvaged forward.

### B216 ÔÇö Harden edge-run setbacks for IRREGULAR parcels: concave inward placement + gentle-curve grouping `[Site Planner]` (bug/task) ÔÇö follow-up to B214/B215  *(owner-requested edge-case pass 2026-06-19; minted **B216** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B215, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-edison-7gdn3y`).** Edge-case testing on irregular parcels (L-shapes, flag lots, curved frontage, dense survey boundaries, triangles) surfaced two issues, both fixed. **(1) Concave inward placement (refines B215):** the setback pill / boundary-dim fan chose the inboard side by stepping *toward the parcel centroid* ÔÇö but on a concave / L-shaped / flag-lot parcel the centroid can sit in a notch (or outside the polygon), so a pill was thrown to the WRONG side (outside the lot). Replaced with a **point-in-ring test** (a small step that must land INSIDE the boundary) via a new `inwardScreenNormal` helper (computed in feet, mapped to screen), used by the per-side pill, the per-segment pill, AND the run-length dimension ÔÇö correct on any shape. **(2) Gentle-curve grouping (refines B214):** `edgeRuns` now chains each edge to the **PREVIOUS** edge's bearing (was the run's FIRST edge), so a gently curved frontage (a few degrees per segment) or survey noise on a nominally-straight line groups into **ONE** side (one setback, one pill, one dimension) instead of many; a genuine corner still breaks the run, and a TIGHT curve (each segment turning beyond tolerance) correctly stays per-segment.
> **Tests:** +2 `edgeRuns` unit tests (gentle-curve groups; tight-curve splits ÔÇö **14** in `test/edgeRuns.test.js`). New headless harness `ui-audit/verify-edge-runs-irregular.mjs` (L-shape, flag lot, tight + gentle curved frontage, dense 12-vertex survey boundary, triangle) ÔÇö **19/19**: every setback pill lands inside the lot (the concave fix), every run-length dim outside, no NaN, and the shallow arc groups to 4 sides (not 17). lint **0 errors** ┬À **411 tests** ┬À build green; the original **B213ÔÇôB215 acceptance still 7/7** (the chain change keeps the 3-segment-east-side case at 4 sides). Verified **V48**. **Dedup:** not a new feature ÔÇö hardens the B214 run-grouping + B215 fan placement for irregular geometry; same canonical `pc.setbacks` model.

### B215 ÔÇö Stacked/occluded dimension labels at shared parcel-edge anchors (boundary dim + setback pill + "+" all at the edge midpoint) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted **B215** ÔÇö next free ID after B214)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-edison-7gdn3y`, commit `cc735e9`).** The parcel-edge boundary dimension, the setback value pill, and the add-vertex "+" handle no longer stack at the same edge midpoint. Each logical SIDE now gets **one run-length dimension placed OUTBOARD** of the edge (`parcelEdgeLabels` rewritten over `selRuns` + `runLabelAnchors`), and the **setback value pill is placed INBOARD** (toward the setback line it describes) ÔÇö fanned ~25 px apart along the edge normal, so neither occludes the other. The add-vertex "+" stays at the segment midpoint (it is the add-vertex handle, not a stack-expander) and now reads clearly once the dims move off it. No setback-line *length* label is ever drawn (the only length label is the boundary/run dimension), so there is nothing redundant to suppress.
> **Dedup / dependency:** built **with B214** ÔÇö run-grouping collapses the per-segment dimension stack into one label per side; B215 adds the boundary-vs-setback inboard/outboard fan that run-grouping alone does not. Distinct from **B121** (element NAME/area label overlap, `labelLayout.js`) and **B149** (zoom LOD) ÔÇö those are the centred element-label pool; this is the parcel-edge dimension/setback/handle pool. Self-verified headless (**V47**, `ui-audit/verify-edge-runs.mjs`): 4 run-length dims (one per side), each fanned 25 px from its pill.

### B214 ÔÇö Edit setback across a multi-segment side in one action (edge "runs") `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B214** ÔÇö next free ID after B213)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-edison-7gdn3y`, commit `cc735e9`).** New pure, unit-tested `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/edgeRuns.js` groups a parcel's **contiguous near-collinear edges** (┬▒7┬░ of the run's first-edge bearing, wrap-around-aware) into **runs** = logical sides, so a side a survey digitized as ~10 segments reads as ONE side. On the selected parcel the on-canvas setback editor shows **one pill per side**; editing it applies the value across **every segment of the run at once** via `setRunSetback`, writing the **canonical per-edge `pc.setbacks` array** (what `setbacksOf`/`constraintsOf`/`developableArea` read ÔÇö no parallel store) which `offsetPolygon` already miters into one continuous setback line. A **"By side / Per segment" toggle** (shown only when a side has >1 segment) plus **Alt-click a side pill** give the single-edge override for notches/jogs. The run also drives the single run-length dimension (B215). Easement `restrictsBuildings`/`restrictsPaving` flags already feed the same model via `exclusionZonesOf`, so both setbacks and easement exclusions reach the (stubbed) `developableArea` verdict engine ÔÇö structurally integrated.
> **Dedup:** reuses the contiguous-edge-run idea already shipped for parcel-edge easements (`buildParcelEdgeStrip` + `easeEdges`), here auto-grouped by bearing and applied to setbacks. Distinct from **B146** (interactive ELEMENT dimension callouts). **+12 unit tests** (`test/edgeRuns.test.js`). Self-verified headless (**V47**): selecting a parcel with a 3-segment east side shows **4 pills (S, E-run, N, W), not 6 edges**; "Per segment" ÔåÆ 6; toggling back ÔåÆ 4.

### B213 ÔÇö Inactive parcel still shows its setback line, acreage label, and edge/boundary dimensions (active-state not inherited by anchored elements) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B213** ÔÇö first filed as B206, then twice renumbered as concurrent `main` (PR #175 then PR #177) took B206ÔÇôB212 in the same window; this Site-Planner trio landed at the next free block **B213ÔÇôB215** to keep IDs unique)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-edison-7gdn3y`, commit `cc735e9`).** Anchored chrome now inherits a parcel's active state (the B100 `active !== false` convention): the setback-outline loop and the acreage-chip `parcelLabels` map both **skip inactive parcels**, and the selected-parcel run-length dimensions + setback pills are gated on `selRuns` (null for an inactive parcel), so an inactive parcel draws **no setback line, no acreage chip, and no edge/setback chrome when selected**. Parcel-edge easements are now **stamped with their source `parcelId` at creation** (`makeEasement`/`finishEaseEdges`); the render hides ÔÇö and the encumbrance tally (`easeAll`) drops ÔÇö any easement anchored to an inactive parcel. Centerline/boundary and pre-existing untagged easements have no tie and stay visible (safe default).
> **Site-analysis clause verified, not rebuilt:** the dissolved union feeding Site Analysis already filters `p.active !== false` (the `rings` in the `leftPanel==="analysis"` block), and `siteSqft`/coverage already exclude inactive ÔÇö confirmed in code. **Dedup:** the follow-on bug to **B100** (it added the flag + dimmed the boundary; this propagates the flag to the anchored chrome). Self-verified headless (**V47**): with one active + one inactive parcel + two easements, exactly **1 acreage chip, 1 setback line, 1 easement** render (the inactive parcel's chrome + its anchored easement are hidden).

### B212 ÔÇö Configurable table columns (show / hide / add / reorder), MS Project / P6 style `[Scheduler / Table]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B196, renumbered **B212** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B194ÔÇôB209 (Site Planner trailer-label/scale-bar/print/storage tranches) while this was in flight, so the real next free IDs were B210ÔÇôB212)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-keller-wphsna`).** The Schedule's left table panel (`GridView` in `public/sequence/index.html`) columns are now user-customizable. **Show / hide / add / reorder / resize** ÔÇö reorder (header drag) and resize (header-edge drag + double-click auto-size) already existed; this adds show/hide + an available-field set. **Two entry points, both portal-mounted at document root** (the established z-index pattern): a discoverable **"Ôè× Columns" button** pinned to the table's top-right opening a field chooser (`GridColumnsChooser` ÔÇö *Shown columns* with Ôû▓Ôû╝ reorder + Ô£ò hide, the Task-name column **locked**-visible; *Available ÔÇö click to add*; *Reset to default*), **plus** a right-click header **context menu** (`GridHeaderMenu` ÔÇö *Hide this column* / *Insert column Ôû©* submenu of hidden fields / *ColumnsÔÇª*). **Available fields** add **% Complete** and the **cost/budget** trio (**Budget, Cost, Actual / Spend**); cost columns render **blank** for empty cells and **roll up to parents** (`rolledBudget`/`rolledActual` mirror `rolledCost`) ÔÇö never an error on a project with no money entered. The new fields **ship hidden by default**; the End column label is aligned to **"Finish"** (MSP/P6 term; the `end` key is unchanged). **Per-project persistence:** column config (visible set, order, widths) lives at `projects[pid].colConfig` and rides the **existing cloud-save path** (`writeGridCfg` ÔåÆ `setData` ÔåÆ autosave); each project is **independent**, new/other projects start from `DEFAULT_GRID_COLS`, and legacy global `gridColOrder`/`gridColWidths` migrate in on first read (`readGridCfg`). Keyboard column nav (arrow/Tab, in-grid and in-cell-edit) **clamps to the visible columns** so the cursor can't wander into hidden fields; SplitView's divider column-snap follows the visible order. COLS stays the master registry + selection index space, so cell selection / range / copy are unchanged.
> **Self-verified headless** (logged-out seed; `ui-audit/verify-sequence.mjs` + interaction probes): chooser opens and adding **Budget**/**Actual** makes them appear (header count 9ÔåÆ11); the header context menu opens with the **Insert** submenu listing hidden fields; and **per-project independence holds** ÔÇö Budget added to *Goose Creek* is **absent** on *Bee Sand Development* and **persists** on return. 0 runtime console errors; the B210/B211 bars are unaffected. **Logged-in cloud round-trip** rides the identical `setData`ÔåÆ`cloudSync` path as task edits ÔÇö the sandbox proxy blocks sign-in, so a signed-in pass is the one remaining confirmation (logged-out, the existing "changes will not save" banner keeps persistence honest, no silent revert). lint **0 errors** ┬À **352 tests** ┬À build green. **(Optional/future, flagged not-built per the brief:** auto-default cost columns visible for Pursuit-lifecycle projects ÔÇö left manual.)
> **Dedup:** mirrors **MasterView's** existing `visCols` show/hide pattern (`masterCols`), generalized to the per-project table with reorder + an explicit chooser/menu. Distinct from **B159** (export task-name toggle) and **B163** (a `progress_pct` data-model field). No new store ÔÇö reuses the project record + the cloud-save path.

### B211 ÔÇö Task status as fill treatment (hollow / partial / solid) `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B195, renumbered **B211** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B194ÔÇôB209 while this was in flight)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-keller-wphsna`).** Task status moved **off the color channel onto a single continuous fill treatment** in both Gantt render paths (on-screen `GanttView` + the PDF/print `buildGanttSVG`). Every task bar keeps **one identity hue** (the existing task **gray `#94a3b8`**, or an explicit per-task `rowColor` override); status reads as **0ÔåÆ100% complete**: **not started (0%)** = hollow rounded pill (outline in the hue, required else invisible); **in progress** = partial fill (solid portion = `percentComplete`, lighter remainder behind it, thin outline around the whole); **complete (100%)** = solid fill. A **min-width floor** keeps a 0% bar from collapsing to a sliver. Fill fraction derives from `percentComplete` (Complete ÔåÆ solid regardless). The **red / paused / overdue** signal stays in the **row background + the table** (untouched), so status never collides with the navy hierarchy. Milestone diamonds follow the same monochrome language (gray, filled when complete, hollow otherwise).
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-sequence.mjs`): DOM probe finds **11 solid + 24 outlined** gray task bars and **0** bars using the old health colors (yellow/red/green); 0 runtime errors. Palette preserved (the one purple `SS` dependency line `#7c3aed` untouched). lint 0 ┬À 352 tests ┬À build green.

### B210 ÔÇö Summary bars as depth-stepped brackets `[Scheduler / Gantt]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B194, renumbered **B210** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B194ÔÇôB209 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sharp-keller-wphsna`).** Replaced the heavy solid summary bars (a thick slab + barely-visible triangle endcaps) with a thin **bracket**: a navy horizontal **span** with **straight-down vertical legs** at each end (no triangles), in both render paths (`GanttView` + `buildGanttSVG`). WBS nesting depth is encoded by **three cues that step together, top-of-tree strongest** ÔÇö **darkness** (navy `#2B3340` ÔåÆ `#46506A` ÔåÆ `#6E7790` ÔåÆ tint), **span thickness** (7/5/4/4 px), and **leg length** (a fraction of row height) ÔÇö the ramp **stops deepening at ~4 levels** (the table's indented name carries depth from there). The summary **name centers just above the span** in the bar's own level-navy (no thick fill to print white onto); no per-level label indentation (bars are date-positioned). An explicit `rowColor` override still wins.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-sequence.mjs`): DOM probe finds **36 navy bracket elements** (Ôëê a dozen brackets ├ù span+2 legs); a detail screenshot confirms the shape ÔÇö label above a thin span with a straight-down leg, **no triangle**. 0 runtime errors. lint 0 ┬À 352 tests ┬À build green.

### B205 ÔÇö Remove the redundant "center map" control in the Site module `[Site Planner / UI]` (task)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-19, "maybe get rid of the center map button"; minted **B205** ÔÇö next free ID after B204)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/elegant-bohr-16zcag`).** The owner clarified (screenshot) that the "center map" button was the **planner header's center `ÔÇ╣ Map` back-button** (`plannerCenterContent` in `SitePlanner.jsx`, which called `onBackToMap`). Once **B204** renamed the Row-1 breadcrumb home crumb to **"Map"** (which already routes to the map via the same `onBackToMap`), the planner had **two "Map"s** ÔÇö so the center one was redundant. Removed the `ÔÇ╣ Map` button; the breadcrumb "Map" crumb is now the single way back. Also updated the empty-state hint ("Pick a parcel from the map (ÔÇ╣ Map)" ÔåÆ "(the "Map" button, top-left)") so it doesn't reference the deleted control. No behavior lost ÔÇö `onBackToMap` is still wired to the breadcrumb crumb; the site/plan switchers + Files pill in the center are untouched.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-mapcrumb.mjs`, **4/4**, booted into the planner via a seeded site + currentSite): no visible `ÔÇ╣ Map` / "Back to the map finder" button remains, the breadcrumb crumb reads "Map", and clicking it returns to the map finder. 381 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green. **Dedup:** resolves the B191 "name shows in both the left breadcrumb and the center switcher" known follow-up for the *Map* half; pairs with **B204** (which created the single "Map" label).

### B203 ÔÇö Schedule project picker showed Site Planner projects; scheduling projects inaccessible `[Scheduler / Header-Nav]` (bug) ÔÇö CRITICAL  *(owner-reported 2026-06-19; minted **B203** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#169ÔÇô#172) took B194ÔÇôB202 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B202)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/elegant-bohr-16zcag`).** Owner: in the **Schedule** module the Row-1 project picker listed **Site Planner** sites, and his scheduling projects (Goose Creek, Kilgore - Grand Port / "Grandport", Eight South a.k.a. vSAN, Pursuits, Papadopoulos) were unreachable.
> **Diagnosis first (the silent-data-loss rule) ÔÇö data was NEVER lost.** The Schedule workspace embeds a separate scheduler app in an **iframe** (`public/sequence/index.html`); that app stores ALL projects as one cloud record (`hs-v1`) in its **own** Supabase project (`ksetjztkplttbcehyicv`), cloud-first with the baked `__PLANAR_DATA__` only as a first-run seed. Inside the iframe the scheduler **hides its own header** (`.in-iframe .app-header{display:none}`), which is where its project picker + Dashboard/Projects toggle lived. The B191ÔÇôB193 shared breadcrumb that replaced it reads `listProjects()` ÔåÆ the **Site Planner** site store and routes every action into the Site Planner ÔÇö so Schedule showed Site projects and couldn't open scheduler ones. A wiring bug, not a data bug.
> **Fix ÔÇö make the Row-1 picker module-aware via a postMessage bridge** (no second store, no schema change): the embedded scheduler now emits its OWN nav state (project list + active project + section) to the shell (`planar:nav-state`) and accepts select / dashboard / new-project commands back (`planar:nav-*`), same-origin-targeted. `ProjectBreadcrumb` accepts an optional **controlled `projects`** list (falls back to `listProjects()` when omitted, so Site/Markup are unchanged); `Scheduler.jsx` feeds in the iframe's projects and posts commands to it instead of bouncing into the Site Planner. Opened directly (not in the shell) the scheduler keeps its native header ÔÇö no regression.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-schedule-picker.mjs`, **8/8**, logged-out with the scheduler cloud blocked ÔåÆ it fell back to its seed, which still flowed through the bridge): Site crumb "Map" lists Site projects; Schedule crumb "Dashboard" lists the SCHEDULER's projects (Goose Creek / Grand Port / Bee Sand) and **not** the seeded Site projects; selecting a project switches it inside the iframe; the Dashboard crumb opens the scheduler's reports view. 381 tests (full suite, post-merge with `main`) ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green. **NOTE for the owner:** the headless run is offline/logged-out so it showed the scheduler's 3 *seed* projects; the live signed-in/online app will list his real set (the bug never touched that cloud record).
> **Dedup:** direct follow-up to **B191ÔÇôB193** (the shared breadcrumb) ÔÇö fixes their Site-only assumption for Schedule; reuses the same `AppHeader`/`ProjectBreadcrumb`, adds only the module-aware list + the iframe bridge. Paired with **B204** (module-contextual home label).

### B204 ÔÇö Module-contextual home crumb: "Map" in Site, "Dashboard" in Schedule `[Header/Nav]` (task)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-19; minted **B204** ÔÇö next free ID after B203)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/elegant-bohr-16zcag`).** Row 1 showed "Dashboard" in every module. The home crumb now reflects the active module's landing view: **Site ÔåÆ "Map"** (the all-projects map IS its dashboard), **Schedule ÔåÆ "Dashboard"** (the scheduler's reports view); Markup keeps "Dashboard" (label TBD per the owner). Label text only ÔÇö `ProjectBreadcrumb` gained a `homeLabel` prop (default "Dashboard"); the breadcrumb structure (`<home> / <project>`) is unchanged. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-schedule-picker.mjs`): Site crumb reads "Map", Schedule crumb reads "Dashboard." Paired with **B203**. lint 0 ┬À build green. **Dedup:** rides on B191/B192's crumb; only the label is parameterized.

<!-- These six print/export items first filed as B191-B196, but concurrent `main` (#169-#171)
     minted B191-B196 for the project switcher / trailer labels / scale bar. Renumbered to the
     real next free IDs B197-B202 at merge time per the permanent-ID rule. The implementing
     commit 8e19081 + branch name predate the renumber; the diff itself is unchanged. -->

### B197 ÔÇö Print buildings/site data table `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B197)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commit `8e19081`).** The printed sheet now carries a **BUILDINGS** data table anchored on the right, next to the title block: one row per building (row keyed by the building's stable `el.id`), columns **name / sf / clear height / slab**. Clear height + slab are read from the building's stored properties (B198) ÔÇö never recomputed in the print routine ÔÇö and the table is part of the single-SVG composition (B200), so it scales with the sheet. Built in pure `lib/printSheet.js` (`buildBuildingTableSvg`); headless-rendered (`ui-audit/verify-print-sheet.mjs` ÔåÆ `ui-audit/screens/print-sheet.png`). 15 printSheet tests.
> **Dedup:** distinct from B131 (overlay-in-print toggle); reuses B198's effective-property selector for the values.

### B198 ÔÇö Auto-assign clear height & slab thickness as building properties by square footage `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B198)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commit `8e19081`).** `clearHeight` + `slabThickness` are now first-class building properties (available to any module), auto-assigned from the building's footprint sf via an **editable per-plan rule** (`settings.buildingRules`) plus an **optional manual override** stored on the element (`clearHeightOverride` / `slabThicknessOverride`; override wins, auto recomputes on sf change when unset). Upper tier inclusive at each boundary ÔÇö `<140k ÔåÆ 32'/6"`, `[140k,600k) ÔåÆ 36'/7"`, `>=600k ÔåÆ 40'/7"` ÔÇö so a 250,000 sf building defaults to 36'/7" with no input (verified). New pure module `lib/buildingProps.js` (12 unit tests); additive el/settings fields (no migration). Surfaced + editable inline on the selected-building inspector and in the print Options flyout (B199).

### B199 ÔÇö Print options interface: edit global defaults and per-building overrides `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B199)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commit `8e19081`).** The print-frame toolbar gained an **Options Ôû¥** flyout (portal-mounted `AnchoredMenu`, so it escapes the toolbar's stacking context): (a) edit the **global size rules** from B198 (thresholds + their clear-height/slab values, with Reset to defaults) and (b) **override** clear height + slab per building (name editable too), with **auto vs set** surfaced and a one-click revert-to-auto. Editing globals changes defaults for un-overridden buildings; a per-building override pins that building. Live-smoke verified (`ui-audit/verify-print-live.mjs`, 0 runtime errors).

### B200 ÔÇö Print zoom: building/site plan doesn't scale with title block, not a cohesive PDF `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; minted B200)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commit `8e19081`).** Root cause: the plan was a nested SVG but the title block + metrics were HTML flow around it ÔÇö two layout systems, so the print scale slider could scale the chrome while the plan held a fixed size. Fix: the **whole sheet is now ONE `<svg>`** (`lib/printSheet.js` `buildPrintSheetSvg`) ÔÇö title block, the plan (nested `<svg>` keeping its own viewBox), the buildings table (B197) and the metrics share one viewBox and **one scaling transform**, so every layer scales together at any zoom and prints as one cohesive page (`@page margin:0` + intrinsic-inch SVG = exactly one page; inset border clears the printer edge). The on-canvas print frame now matches the printed plan box (WYSIWYG). Headless-rendered: one root SVG, nested plan, letter-landscape 11in viewBox. 15 tests.

### B201 ÔÇö Print filename format `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-5"; minted B201)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commits `8e19081` + follow-up).** Exported PDF (document title ÔåÆ Save-as filename) and PNG use **`YYYY.MM.DD {Project} - {Plan Name}`** ÔÇö e.g. `2026.06.19 Cypress Logistics - Plan 1` (or `ÔÇª - Scheme A` once the plan is renamed). Date = today at export, project = the active site name, **trailing piece = the plan's own label** (`planLabel`). Filesystem-illegal characters are stripped from project + plan (the ` - ` separator and date dots preserved); a blank plan name just drops the tail. `lib/printSheet.js` `sheetFileName`/`sanitizeFilename` (tested).
> **Owner decision (2026-06-19):** the trailing identifier is the **plan name**, not an incrementing number ÔÇö so the filename always tracks whatever the plan is named (today "Plan 1", later e.g. "Scheme A") and won't drift or surprise. (Superseded the initial incrementing-sheet-number default; the per-session counter was removed.)

### B202 ÔÇö "Preparing print" hangs before producing file `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-6"; minted B202)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/sweet-hypatia-itdlvu`, commit `8e19081`).** The stall was the image-inlining step: aerial/overlay `<image>` fetches ran one-by-one with **no timeout**, so any fetch that hung through the TLS-inspection proxy blocked prep for up to ~60s. Fix: each fetch is now **time-boxed** (`AbortController`, 8s) and they run **in parallel**, so worst-case prep is ~8s and a hung image is dropped (PNG) or keeps its remote href (print loads it natively). Added prep **timing instrumentation** (`console.debug` marks for inline + ready) so any future stall is diagnosable rather than a mystery.

### B196 ÔÇö Scale bar + north arrow as DOM overlays so they're always fully visible (B173 follow-up) `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug)  *(owner re-reported on the live site 2026-06-19 ÔÇö "scale still not fully shown, nor the north cardinal direction"; minted B196 ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B195)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/kind-meitner-uy2qeh`). **B173 pinned the app to the viewport (no page scroll) but left two residual defects** the owner still saw: (a) the on-screen scale bar + north arrow were drawn *inside the canvas SVG*, anchored to its coordinate space (`buildScreenFurnitureSvg` at `vh ÔêÆ 40`), so they rode the canvas and could still clip on a short/odd viewport; (b) the north arrow sat directly under the green **"Scaled ┬À county GIS" calibration pill** (a higher-z DOM element at `left:12, bottom:40`) ÔÇö so only the "N" peeked out, the arrow body hidden (the exact "north not shown" symptom). **Fix:** lifted both into **DOM overlays anchored to the VISIBLE canvas corners** ÔÇö new `screenFurniturePlates()` in `lib/sheetFurniture.js` returns the scale bar + north arrow as standalone plates; `SitePlanner.jsx` renders them as two absolutely-positioned `<svg>`s at `bottom:40` (`pointerEvents:none`, `zIndex:7`, `data-export="skip"`), immune to canvas size. Relocated the two colliders: calibration pill `left:12 ÔåÆ 56` (beside the arrow), zoom controls `bottom:78 ÔåÆ 100` (above the scale bar). Also kept the planner root `minHeight:600 ÔåÆ 0` (belt-and-suspenders with B173's `index.css` `100dvh`+`overflow:hidden`, so a sub-600px window clips nothing). The **export** furniture path (`buildSheetFurnitureSvg`) is untouched ÔÇö export parity preserved. **Self-verified headless at 1600├ù800 AND 1600├ù540:** page overflow 0; the "FEET" (scale) and "N" (north) labels both `getBoundingClientRect` fully inside the viewport at both heights; corner screenshots show the scale bar clear below the +/ÔêÆ/Ôñó zoom stack and the north arrow beside (not under) the calibration pill. lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green ┬À `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact.
> **Reconciliation:** B173 (DONE, stays) fixed the page-scroll via viewport pinning and judged a portal overlay unnecessary; the owner's live re-report proved the furniture *placement* (in-SVG + pill overlap) was the remaining cause. B196 implements exactly the "fixed overlay anchored to the canvas corner" mechanism B173's note had set aside. B172/B174/B175 (tabs / header ÔêÆ20% / parcel checkbox) shipped independently on `main` and this branch's duplicates of them were dropped during the merge in favour of `main`'s versions.

### B195 ÔÇö Trailer-parking label stays inside its polygon at every zoom level `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B190, renumbered **B195** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#164/#168 + sibling branches) took B189ÔÇôB193 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B193)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/cool-babbage-mpc9sd`). Zooming out grew the trailer label *relative to its shrinking strip* until it overflowed: the label was sized in fixed **screen** pixels (floored at `lsÔëÑ0.34`), so it stayed roughly constant while the strip got smaller on zoom-out ÔÇö "fit logic exists but the limits are off." **Fix:** a trailer label is now sized as a **fraction of the strip's real-world extent** (EPSG:2278 feet ÔåÆ screen px via the current pixels-per-foot, `view.ppf`), so it scales WITH the area and stays contained by construction. The text runs along the strip's **long** side and the line-stack across its **short** side, each with its own fit bound (the smaller governs ÔÇö this matches `stripLabelRot`, the B186/B188 helper that aligns the label to the long axis). The font is **floored** at a legible minimum (`minPx`, below which a too-small Ôëê2-spot strip takes *controlled overflow* rather than going illegible) and **capped** at the normal label size so it never balloons when zoomed in. The shared label engine `lib/labelLayout.js` gained a **`noLeader`** option: a trailer label that still can't fit overflows **in place** (centred on the strip), instead of being leadered out, and the engine now **prefers dropping a line to fit inside** before accepting that overflow. Per-label font metrics (`fs`/`lh`/`charW`) are threaded through the candidate ÔåÆ layout ÔåÆ render path; every non-trailer label keeps the existing global metrics (defaults unchanged). **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-trailer-label.mjs`): across five zoom levels the label stays **inside** the strip ÔÇö shape **397├ù55px ÔåÆ 102├ù14px**, label/shape height ratio **0.50** (capped when the shape is large) **ÔåÆ 0.96** (floored when small), `inside=true` at every step; `verify-new1to5.mjs` confirms a vertical strip still rotates correctly and other label types are untouched. **2 new `labelLayout` tests** (noLeader overflow-in-place; drop-a-line-to-fit). 354 tests (full suite, post-merge with `main`) ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.
> **Dedup:** extends the **B121** shared label LOD + collision engine (`labelLayout.js`) and complements **B149** (zoom-out decluttering) ÔÇö area-relative sizing is added *on top of* that one engine, not as a parallel renderer. Reuses **B186/B188**'s `stripLabelRot` (label orientation along a strip) rather than re-deriving it. Depended on **B194** for the final two-line label content. Distinct from B188, which fixed label *orientation/consistency*; B195 fixes label *containment under zoom*.

### B194 ÔÇö Trailer-parking label: stall depth in the title, drop the row-dimension line `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B189, renumbered **B194** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#164/#168 + sibling branches) took B189ÔÇôB193 while this was in flight, so this is the real next free ID after B193)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/cool-babbage-mpc9sd`). The trailer-parking label is now **two lines** instead of three: line 1 = the per-stall trailer length + name (e.g. **"50ÔÇ▓ Trailer Parking"**), line 2 = the trailer count (e.g. **"30 trailers"**). The old third line (overall row dimensions, e.g. "360ÔÇ▓ ├ù 50ÔÇ▓" / "355ÔÇ▓ ├ù 50ÔÇ▓") is **dropped entirely** ÔÇö the depth now lives in the title and the overall row length is intentionally gone. The stall depth is read straight off the element's own config (`cfgOf(el).trailerL`, the depth a trailer sits in) ÔÇö **not** the overall row length and **not** recomputed; the count keeps the existing `trailerStalls`/`estTrailers` math (polygon fields still read "(est)"). One code path in `SitePlanner.jsx` (the centred-label builder), so single strips, wide fields, and rotated vertical strips all pick it up. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-trailer-label.mjs` + `verify-new1to5.mjs`): a 360ÔÇ▓├ù50ÔÇ▓ strip reads **"50ÔÇ▓ Trailer Parking" / "30 trailers"** with no dimension line; a wide field reads "53ÔÇ▓ Trailer Parking / 18 trailers"; a thin vertical strip runs the same two lines along its long axis. 354 tests (full suite, post-merge with `main`) ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.
> **Dedup:** distinct from **B188** (which fixed label *orientation/consistency* ÔÇö running the label in-line on a narrow strip via `stripLabelRot`); B194 changes the label *content*. Builds on B188's strip-label work, doesn't duplicate it. Paired with **B195** (containment under zoom), which depends on this item's final two-line dimensions.

### B193 ÔÇö Persist current project before switching `[Header/Nav]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; filed provisionally as B191, renumbered **B193** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#168) took B189ÔÇôB190 for the Site Analysis tranche, so this is the real next free ID after B190/B191/B192)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/awesome-gauss-jlrq5v`).** A breadcrumb switch (to another project or to the Dashboard) flushes the current project first, so a switch can't silently drop unsaved work ÔÇö it routes through the **existing** data-loss machinery: in the Site Planner, opening another project goes through `handleOpenSite` ÔåÆ `flushSite()` and the `key`-change unmount `persistOrDrop()`; in Doc Review, the `useReviewPersistence` unmount/visibility flush already guarantees the local write. On top of that, B193 adds the owner-requested **cloud-at-risk surfacing** (the B124/B125 silent-failure rule): each workspace passes a normalized `saveState` to the breadcrumb, and when it's `offline`/`error`, the switcher shows a **passive ÔÜá line in the dropdown** AND a **transient "saved on this device ÔÇö will sync when reconnected" toast** on the switch. The switch is **never blocked** on a successful cloud save, but is **never silent** about an at-risk one.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to3.mjs`): project switches complete cleanly; the at-risk surfacing is gated on `offline`/`error` (the sandbox is logged-out ÔåÆ `local`, no false warning). **Dedup:** no new persistence path ÔÇö reuses SitePlanner's `flushSite`/`persistOrDrop` + Doc Review's persistence flush; B193 is the routing + surfacing layer over them. 344 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B192 ÔÇö "Dashboard" breadcrumb root ÔåÆ all-projects view `[Header/Nav]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B190, renumbered **B192** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#168) took B189ÔÇôB190 for the Site Analysis tranche, so this is the real next free ID after B190/B191)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/awesome-gauss-jlrq5v`).** The left crumb is a **literal-text "Dashboard"** label with a `ti-layout-dashboard` icon, always visible alongside the project crumb so the **Dashboard > Project** hierarchy is self-evident. It routes to the all-projects landing ÔÇö the **Site Planner map** (the canonical all-projects surface: one marker per project + the YOUR SITES list). From Markup/Schedule it switches into the Site module's map via the shell's `navIntent`; in the Site Planner it sets map mode directly. The **logo + wordmark also route to the Dashboard** (secondary path; its tooltip now reads "Dashboard ÔÇö all projects"), but the labeled crumb is primary and the logo is no longer the only way back. The **"All projects (Dashboard)"** row pinned atop the B191 dropdown is the third, backstop path.
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to3.mjs`): from a loaded project, clicking the Dashboard crumb returns to the all-projects map; the crumb is present in Markup + Schedule. **Dedup:** the logo already routed to the map; B192 makes that route labeled + discoverable + shared across all three workspaces. 344 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B191 ÔÇö Project switcher breadcrumb in header Row 1 `[Header/Nav]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B189, renumbered **B191** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#168) took B189ÔÇôB190 for the Site Analysis tranche, so this is the real next free ID after B190)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/awesome-gauss-jlrq5v`).** Added a left-anchored `Dashboard / <Project> Ôû¥` breadcrumb to the shared two-row `AppHeader`, immediately right of the logo+wordmark, used identically by Site / Schedule / Markup. New shared component `src/shared/ui/ProjectBreadcrumb.jsx`: the project crumb opens a **portal-mounted `AnchoredMenu`** dropdown containing ÔÇö top to bottom ÔÇö a **search field** (filters by name), an **"All projects (Dashboard)"** row pinned at top + a divider, the **recent-projects list newest-edited-first with relative timestamps** ("just now / 5m ago / 3h ago / 2d ago / 3w ago / short date"), and a **"New project"** action at the bottom. The list comes from `listProjects()` (`src/shared/projects/projects.js`) which groups the EXISTING per-user, RLS-scoped `loadSitesList()` store via the pure `groupProjects` helper (`src/shared/projects/projectModel.js`) ÔÇö **no parallel project store**, only the signed-in user's own projects, group-id-backed (name is a label only). Selecting a project loads it **in place** and stays in the current workspace where applicable (Site ÔåÆ opens the plan; Markup ÔåÆ browses that project's files via the Files drawer; Schedule ÔåÆ routes into the Site Planner). **Pure helpers covered by 10 new unit tests** (`test/projects.test.js`).
> **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to3.mjs`, logged-out, 3 seeded projects): breadcrumb renders in all three workspaces; dropdown is portal-mounted **outside `<header>`**, lists projects **newest-first** with timestamps, search filters by name, and selecting a project loads it. **Dedup:** distinct from the shipped B10/B104 (header shell + module tabs). The Site Planner plan-mode **center** siteÔû¥ÔÇ║planÔû¥ switcher (rename + version history) stays as the in-site plan-level detail; the left breadcrumb is the app-wide project switcher. **Known cosmetic follow-up:** in the planner the project name now shows in both the left breadcrumb and the center switcher ÔÇö flagged to the owner (streamlining the center switcher trades off its in-place site-rename affordance, so it's a deliberate owner call, not auto-applied). 344 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B190 ÔÇö Click a constraint card to toggle its layer on the map `[Site Planner / Site Analysis]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B185, renumbered **B190** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#164) took B184ÔÇôB188 for unrelated Site Planner items, so this is the real next free ID after B189)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/bold-edison-h8tfkc`).** Each resolved constraint card now carries a **Ôùì Map** toggle; clicking it shows that category's GIS layer on the planner map, framed to the site; **Ôùë On map** while shown; click again to hide.
> **Built on the existing shared overlay system ÔÇö NO parallel renderer.** Each source in `lib/siteAnalysis.js` gained a `mapLayer` id pointing at the layer that already exists in `lib/layers.js` (`floodÔåÆfema`, `wetlandsÔåÆwetlands`, `pipelinesÔåÆtxrrc_pipe`, `oilgasÔåÆtxrrc_wells`); the finding carries it through. `SiteAnalysis.jsx` renders the toggle **only** for a category that **resolved (present/absent) AND has a `mapLayer`** ÔÇö so UNKNOWN / info / no-source categories (Road authority, Contamination, Jurisdiction, Zoning) show **no blank toggle**, their error stays surfaced (the bug report's rule). The toggle writes the same app-shared **`overlays`/`setOverlays`** state the Layers panel uses (one source of truth ÔåÆ `syncOverlayLayers` paints it), and `SitePlanner.toggleAnalysisLayer` turns the basemap on + **`frameToActiveParcels()`** (active-parcel extent + 60% margin, since FEMA/NWI are scale-gated). Independent per-card toggles ÔåÆ multiple layers at once. If a just-enabled layer's map service fails, the card shows an honest "service isn't responding" hint (the screen result still stands).
> **Verified live (headless):** clicking Wetlands **Ôùì Map** flipped to **Ôùë On map** and added the NWI overlay (`leaflet-image-layer` 0ÔåÆ1), framed to the parcel ÔÇö screenshot `ui-audit/screens/analysis-verify.png`. lint 0 errors ┬À 341 tests ┬À build green ┬À doc-review/site-planner lazy-chunk split intact. **V45.**
> **Dedup:** distinct from **LayerPanel** (the full layer checklist) ÔÇö this is a per-finding shortcut into the same `overlays` state; same explicit-click pattern as the jurisdiction identify (never auto-loads all layers). Builds on **B189** (a card only offers the toggle once its query resolves).

### B189 ÔÇö Site Analysis constraint queries failing (UNKNOWN / "Failed to execute query") `[Site Planner / Site Analysis]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B184, renumbered **B189** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (#164) took B184ÔÇôB188 for unrelated Site Planner items, so this is the real next free ID after B188)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/bold-edison-h8tfkc`).** Filed AND fixed AND verified live the same session. Bug: on Site Analysis, **Wetlands** + **Pipelines** returned "Failed to execute query." ÔåÆ UNKNOWN, while Floodplain worked. The bug report's instinct to **audit every category, not just the two visible**, paid off: a **third** source (**Oil & gas wells**, below the fold) was 400-ing too.
> **Root cause was a different field bug per source ÔÇö confirmed live (HTTP 400 with old fields ÔåÆ HTTP 200 with real features) against a known Katy/Sheldon-Lake parcel before patching:**
> - **Wetlands** ÔÇö NWI split layers 1/2 are **JOINED** layers (`Wetlands Ôïê NWI_Wetland_Codes`), so every field comes back **table-qualified** (`Wetlands_CONUS_West.WETLAND_TYPE`); asking for the bare names 400'd, and the qualifier **differs per sublayer** (East vs West). Fix: request `outFields:"*"` and **`normalizeAttrs()`** strips the `Table.` prefix so the summarizers read plain names.
> - **Pipelines** ÔÇö queried `OPERATOR`/`COMMODITY`, which **don't exist** (real: `OPER_NM`, `CMDTY_DESC`, `DIAMETER`).
> - **Oil & gas wells** ÔÇö queried `LEASE_NAME`, which **doesn't exist** on the Wells layer (real: `SYMNUM`/`API`/`WELLID`/ÔÇª).
> All three fixed + flipped **`verified:true`**, so a now-working empty query resolves to **None found** (not conservative UNKNOWN) ÔÇö matching the bug report's "expected." The silent-error guard is intact: a genuine HTTP/network error still classifies **unknown**, never a fabricated "none." Added **diagnostics** ÔÇö `defaultFetchJson` carries `{httpStatus,url,arcgisCode}` on the Error and `logQueryFailure()` prints the real failure to the console, so an opaque "Failed to execute query." is debuggable instead of silent.
> **Verified live (headless, `ui-audit/verify-analysis.mjs`, real USFWS/FEMA/TxRRC endpoints):** Floodplain **PRESENT (Zone AE)**, Wetlands **PRESENT (Lake, Freshwater Forested/Shrub Wetland)**, Pipelines **NONE FOUND**, Oil & gas **NONE FOUND** ÔÇö zero "Failed to execute query." 341 tests (siteAnalysis 25ÔåÆ36, incl. the qualified-field + corrected-field + normalizeAttrs cases), lint 0 errors, build green. **V45.**
> **Dedup:** this is the per-source debug of **B147**'s "­ƒö▓ Remaining (1)." Distinct from **B133/B135** (those fixed the wetlands *map-overlay image* `/export`; this fixed the Site Analysis `/query` connector ÔÇö a different request path on the same host).

### B188 ÔÇö Trailer-parking label inconsistent + should run in-line on the strip `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-5"; filed provisionally as B184, renumbered **B188** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B180ÔÇôB183 for unrelated items, so this is the real next free ID.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/happy-goldberg-piym6g`). Two trailer-parking instances rendered their labels differently ÔÇö the wide field kept its 3-line label horizontal, while the narrow wall strip overflowed (the collision engine leadered it OUT of the strip). Root cause: there was no orientation logic, so a label wider than the strip was pulled outside rather than turned to run along it. Fix = the **shared label-orientation helper** built for B186 (`stripLabelRot`, applied to `landscape`/`sidewalk`/`trailer` strips): when a label is wider than the strip is narrow, it runs ALONG the strip's long axis (vertical text on a vertical strip), so both instances now render consistently in-line and neither claims more space than the strip itself. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to5.mjs`): a thin vertical trailer strip shows its label running vertically down the strip; a wide trailer field keeps its label horizontal. 286 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B187 ÔÇö Truck court resize must anchor to the dock wall and push/pull trailer parking `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high severity  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; filed provisionally as B183, renumbered **B187**.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/happy-goldberg-piym6g`). Changing a truck court's **Depth** field (e.g. 135ÔåÆ140) resized it from the **centre** ÔÇö losing depth on both sides, detaching the paving from both the dock wall and the trailer parking (a silently-wrong geometry state). **Two root causes fixed:** (1) `resizeSelEl` (the numeric Width/Depth editor) kept `cx/cy` fixed, so it grew/shrank symmetrically ÔÇö it now applies the same **`hostClampOf`/`clampToHost`** host-edge pin the resize grips already use, so an attached strip/court stays flush against the dock wall and only the FAR edge moves; (2) `refitChildren` never moved the court's back-side trailer when the court itself was the resized element (it only handled trailers when the *building* resized) ÔÇö it now registers a resized truck court in `courtBox`, so the `forCourt` trailer follows the moved far edge (pushed out on grow, pulled in on shrink, spacing preserved). Covers both the numeric field and the resize grips. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new4b.mjs`, the owner's exact path ÔÇö Depth 135ÔåÆ170): the building/dock-wall label moved **0 px** (court stays anchored) while the trailer label moved **+54 px** out (pushed flush) ÔÇö court ÔåÆ court paving ÔåÆ trailer stay connected, nothing detaches. 286 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B186 ÔÇö Buffer-strip width labels run along the strip's long axis `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B182, renumbered **B186**; shares its helper with B188/NEW-5)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/happy-goldberg-piym6g`). A "5ÔÇ▓ Landscape" / "5ÔÇ▓ Sidewalk" label rendered horizontally even on a thin vertical strip, eating canvas across the strip. Added one shared, holistic helper ÔÇö **`stripLabelRot(el, lines, charW, ppf)`** (covers `landscape`, `sidewalk` AND `trailer`, no per-type duplication) ÔÇö that returns a rotation when the label is wider than the strip is narrow: the label then runs ALONG the strip's long axis (vertical text on a vertical strip, horizontal on a horizontal one). The shared `labelLayout` collision engine was extended to test the label's **rotated** on-screen footprint, so a vertical label is fitted against the strip's tall height and narrow width (and so it lands INSIDE the strip instead of being leadered out). New unit test in `test/labelLayout.test.js`. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to5.mjs`): vertical landscape/sidewalk/trailer strips show vertical labels; horizontal strips and wide fields stay horizontal. 286 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B185 ÔÇö Site acreage chip is draggable + persisted `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; filed provisionally as B181, renumbered **B185**.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/happy-goldberg-piym6g`). The parcel acreage chip ("32.51 ac") was pinned at the parcel centroid and obstructed the layout. It's now **click-and-drag** (in Select), to anywhere ÔÇö including outside the parcel boundary ÔÇö storing a `labelOffset` (parcel-local feet) on the parcel so it survives geometry edits. New `startAcChip` + an `acChip` drag-move case mirror the B146 dimension-callout drag pattern; the offset persists via the existing Site Model save path (additive field on `parcels[]`, mirrors to Supabase when signed in). **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new3.mjs`): the chip dragged 182 px and `parcels[0].labelOffset` was written to the saved store. 286 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B184 ÔÇö 24ÔÇ▓ road-width dimension drifts left of centre `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B180, renumbered **B184** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B180ÔÇôB183.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/happy-goldberg-piym6g`). The road-width dimension ("24ÔÇ▓") rendered shifted toward the left edge of the road instead of centred on the span. Cause: the dimension line/label were placed at **18 %** along the element's long axis (a sensible default for a building's depth tick, but wrong for a road, where the width tick should sit on the centreline of the length). Fix: roads now place the width dimension at the **midpoint (50 %)** of the length (`posF = el.type === "road" ? 0.5 : 0.18`); the measured value and the perpendicular span anchor were already correct (the label sits at the midpoint of the measured width), so this is a position-only fix ÔÇö no measurement-accuracy change. **Self-verified headless** (`ui-audit/verify-new1to5.mjs`): the "24ÔÇ▓" label sits centred along the road. 286 tests ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green.

### B183 ÔÇö Editable layers decouple from backdrop during pan while loading `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B177, renumbered **B183** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B176ÔÇôB181, so this is the real next free ID after B181/B182.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/nice-heisenberg-r6lmf1`).** On a freshly-loaded site, grabbing and panning before load settled made the drawn layers (massing/parcels/markup) visibly offset from the aerial for a frame or two, then snap back. **Root cause (confirmed, not the pasted race-condition theory):** in Planyr the SVG *is* the source of truth and the basemap follows it, so the layers can't reproject against a stale transform ÔÇö the decoupler was **B65's own ghost snapshot**. A `commit` clones the basemap into a frozen overlay held until the fresh tiles `load`; while loading, that `load` is delayed so the snapshot lingered, and a pan moved the live SVG while the **frozen** snapshot stayed put ÔåÆ the aerial lagged the drawn layers until the snapshot expired (hence "only early on, then snaps back"). **Fix:** drop any lingering snapshot the instant a new gesture starts (`dropGhost()` at the top of the transform path), so a pan is tracked by the live CSS transform (welded to the SVG) and the backfill covers any reveal; a fresh snapshot is taken only at the next commit. **Verified headless** (`ui-audit/diagnose-decouple.mjs`, retina + throttled, pan immediately after load): **0 %** of pan frames have a snapshot on top (idle baseline has one, correctly dropped on the first pan). lint 0 ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.

### B182 ÔÇö Basemap tiles missing at high zoom ("Map data not yet available") `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B176, renumbered **B182** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B176ÔÇôB181 (jurisdictions/tax + cost takeoff), so this is the real next free ID.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/nice-heisenberg-r6lmf1`).** Zooming in deep showed gray "Map data not yet available" placeholder tiles instead of imagery. **Root cause (measured, `ui-audit/diagnose-zoomdepth.mjs`):** Esri World Imagery is native to **z19**; `detectRetina` (added in B170 for sharpness) fetches **one zoom level higher** than the display zoom, so at deep zoom on a retina display the detail layer requested **z20** (168 requests), which Esri answers with the placeholder image. `maxNativeZoom:19` didn't account for the retina offset. **Fix:** drop the detail layer's `maxNativeZoom` by 1 on retina (`L.Browser.retina ? maxNative-1 : maxNative`), so the highest native fetch is z19 and beyond that it **upscales the deepest real imagery** instead of fetching nonexistent tiles ÔÇö exactly the requested "clamp to deepest available zoom, scale the last good tiles up; never put placeholders over the plan." **Verified:** max tile zoom requested now **19** at both retina (DSF 2) and non-retina (DSF 1), **0** placeholder/empty responses (was 168). The coarse backfill (`maxNativeZoom:13`) was already clamping correctly. lint 0 ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.
> **Follow-up ÔåÆ B218 (2026-06-20):** this fix was applied only to the **planner-canvas** backdrop (`SitePlanner.jsx` GEO_BASEMAP). The **map-finder** imagery layer (`MapFinder.jsx`) had the same `detectRetina` (from B170) but never got this retina clamp, so the placeholder still showed there on a retina display. B218 brought the identical clamp to the map finder (every dropdown source) + capped the labels overlay. Both call sites now clamp.

### B180 ÔÇö Verify paving area excludes curb footprint `[Cost]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B180** ÔÇö highest B# across both files was B179, so this is the real next free ID. Prerequisite for B181's paving-area trim.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/determined-feynman-l7c61b`). Investigated ÔåÆ confirmed correct by construction, and locked it in.** There was no pre-existing *cost* paving figure to fold curb into ÔÇö the only paving area the app derived was the **impervious/SF** metric, where including the curb band is *correct* (curb is impervious surface), so that stays untouched. The new cost takeoff (B181) derives paving from the road's **face-of-curb-to-face-of-curb travel width** (`roadTravelWidth` = `min(w,h) ÔêÆ 2┬Àcurb`), which **excludes** the curb by definition: a 30ÔÇ▓ FC-FC road yields 30ÔÇ▓ of paving, never 31ÔÇ▓. So the defect the brief warned about (folding the 6ÔÇ│ curb each side into paving) cannot occur ÔÇö the cost math reads travel width, not the full element rectangle.
> **Locked against regression:** `roadQuantities` (in `lib/costTakeoff.js`) takes the FC-FC width explicitly and a unit test asserts a 30ÔÇ▓ FC-FC ├ù 1000ÔÇ▓ barrier-curb road = exactly **30,000 SF (3,333.33 SY)** of paving with curb **outside** that width ÔÇö so a future change that folded curb back in would fail the suite. **Live-confirmed in-browser:** a seeded 26ÔÇ▓-cross road (FC-FC travel 25ÔÇ▓) reads **1,611 SY** in the Yield panel (25 ├ù 580 ├À 9); had curb been folded in (26ÔÇ▓) it would read 1,675 SY ÔÇö the 1,611 proves the exclusion. 300 tests green, lint 0 errors, build green.

### B181 ÔÇö Curb as a separate linear quantity type with curb-type attribute `[Cost]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B181** ÔÇö next free ID after B180.)*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/determined-feynman-l7c61b`).** A road segment now derives **two independent, separately-priced quantities**:
> - **Paving** ÔÇö an AREA item, **square yards (SY; 1 SY = 9 SF)** = length ├ù FC-FC paving width ├À 9. Width is stored/derived as **face-of-curb to face-of-curb** (the drivable surface), so a back-of-curb dimension can't silently inflate it.
> - **Curb** ÔÇö a LINEAR item, **linear feet (LF)** = length ├ù curbed-side count. **Both sides counted** (a 1,000ÔÇ▓ road curbed both sides = 2,000 LF).
>
> **Curb carries a `curbType` attribute that selects its unit price:** `none` / **`barrier`** (vertical ~6ÔÇ│ lip, no gutter pan) / **`curb-gutter`** (curb + flat concrete gutter pan, typ. 18ÔÇô24ÔÇ│).
