For **curb-and-gutter**, the gutter pan rides with the curb LF item, so **paving trims by the pan width on each curbed side** (`paving = FC-FC ÔêÆ panWidth ├ù sides`); pan width is a property of the curb-and-gutter type (default 2.0ÔÇ▓/24ÔÇ│, editable). Curb LF is split **barrier vs curb-and-gutter** in the rollup so each rides its own price.
> **Implementation:** new pure library `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/costTakeoff.js` (`roadQuantities`, `costRollup`, curb-type readers) ÔÇö no React, fully unit-tested (`test/costTakeoff.test.js`, 15 cases). Road-element fields are additive/optional (`curbType`, `curbedSides`, `panWidth`) and persist losslessly through the Site Model (els pass through untouched; no `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump). UI: a **"Curb & paving (cost)"** group in the road inspector (curb-type dropdown, curbed-sides Both/One, gutter-pan width, live per-segment SY/LF readout ÔÇö inline editors only, no dialogs) and a collapsible **"Road cost (screening)"** section in the **Yield** panel (site-wide Paving SY + Curb LF by type, **user-supplied** unit-price inputs ÔÇö blank by default, never a hardcoded $/LF ÔÇö and a priced subtotal).
> **Hook left for later (not built):** `roadQuantities` tags each curb run `curbCategory: "linear"` so a future **radius/corner curb** category (often a higher unit price) can be broken out as its own priced line without reworking the rollup.
> **Verified:** 300 tests green (15 new), lint 0 errors, build green, lazy-chunk split intact. **Live headless (logged-out preview):** Yield ÔåÆ "Road cost (screening)" renders **Paving 1,611 SY ┬À Curb ┬À barrier 1,160 LF** with the FC-FC caveat and unit-price inputs, **zero page errors** (`ui-audit/verify-cost.mjs`).

### B176 ÔÇö Jurisdictions overlay `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B167, renumbered **B176** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B167ÔÇôB175, so this is the real next free ID after B175.)*
`[x]` Toggleable boundary overlays so you can see, on the map, which districts a site sits in ÔÇö county, city limits, city ETJ, and MUD/water districts. "See which sites are in a MUD around me." No school (per spec). Screening-grade, with a hard "boundary = has jurisdiction, NOT physically served/connected" caveat on every layer.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-19** (branch `claude/busy-carson-1zuiuq`). New **`JURISDICTIONS`** overlay group in `lib/layers.js` + a dedicated **"Jurisdictions"** section in `components/LayerPanel.jsx` (with a one-line "has-jurisdiction Ôëá serves utilities" caption). Four layers:
> - **County boundaries** (`esriFeature`) ÔÇö reuses the **verified** TxDOT county FeatureServer already used by jurisdiction *identify* (B93), so the line you see is the line the identify reports.
> - **City limits** (`esriFeature`) ÔÇö verified TxGIO city-boundaries layer (same source as identify).
> - **City ETJ (Houston region)** (`esriFeature`) ÔÇö verified H-GAC 13-county ETJ layer (same source as identify); blank elsewhere (no statewide ETJ layer exists) ÔÇö honest, not a bug.
> - **MUD / water districts** (`dynamic`) ÔÇö **statewide TCEQ water-district boundaries** (the data behind TCEQ's public Water Districts Map Viewer, republished by HARC: `harcags.harcresearch.org/.../Boundaries/TCEQ_Water_Districts/MapServer`). Covers **Harris + Fort Bend + statewide** ÔÇö not county-gated. (Initial cut shipped Fort-Bend-only; switched to the statewide TCEQ source same day when the owner pointed out TCEQ's mapper.) A `dynamic` image export renders via a CORS-exempt `<img>` even if the probe can't reach the host.
> All ride the existing probe + honest per-layer status + data-age (B96), so a dead endpoint shows a quiet "failed" dot, never wrong data. **Reuses verified endpoints (no new credentials, no parallel renderer); URLs imported from `lib/jurisdiction.js` so there's one source of truth (no drift).** lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green. **Self-verified headless** (`gis-verify/jurisdictions-overlay-verify.mjs`): the Jurisdictions group + all four toggles render in the Layers panel and toggling County boundaries works with **zero JS errors**.
> **Live-render verification = V44 (handed off to a fresh session).** county/city/ETJ hosts (services.arcgis.com, feature.geographic.texas.gov) are reachable + return 200; the MUD host `harcresearch.org` was added to the env egress allowlist 2026-06-19 but is **not live in the session that shipped this** (already-running container keeps the old policy ÔåÆ still 403) ÔÇö so a NEW session confirms the MUD tiles paint over a known Harris MUD parcel. If HARC ever proves unreliable in prod, alternates: City of Houston cohgis MUDs, or `services.twdb.texas.gov` water districts ÔÇö a one-line URL swap in `JURISDICTIONS.jur_mud`.
> **Foundation for B177 (tax breakdown) + B178 (rate choropleth).** Supersedes the GIS-overlay "B" path of the old B165.

### B165 ÔÇö Wire Harris County tax-rate source `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(superseded)*
`[x]` **Superseded 2026-06-19 ÔÇö split into B176ÔÇôB179.** The original "wire a Harris tax rate" ask resolved into the A/B decision (browser GIS-overlay vs. backend exact fetch); the owner's actual goal ("see which sites are in a MUD; the tax statement lists every taxing entity") reframed it into four concrete items: **B176** jurisdictions overlay (shipped, above), **B177** parcel tax-breakdown panel (screening entities + rates), **B178** combined-rate choropleth, **B179** backend per-account exact fetch (the authoritative "A"). The B165 investigation (HCAD parcels carry no jurisdiction fields; official rate tables 403 automated clients ÔåÆ needs spatial overlay or backend) carries forward into B177/B179. Not deleted ÔÇö kept here as the record; tracking now lives on B176ÔÇôB179.

### B65 ÔÇö White flashing on zoom/pan and general redraw `[Site Planner / map]` (bug)
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/nice-heisenberg-r6lmf1`).** Zooming/panning a located site flashed (originally white) as the aerial blanked between redraws. **Root cause, measured not guessed** (headless probe `ui-audit/diagnose-flash.mjs`): the basemap view-lock effect called `map.setView(center, z, {animate:false})` on *every* planner `view` change; Leaflet's `setView`ÔåÆ`_resetView` fires `viewreset`, whose GridLayer handler `_resetAll` **wipes and reloads all tiles**, so each wheel step blanked the aerial for a frame (loaded tiles collapsed **22 ÔåÆ 2**), exposing the backdrop.
> **Fix shipped (two parts):**
> 1. **Mitigation (commit `cf26912`):** the basemap container backdrop was `PAL.paper` (`#f4f1ea`, near-white) ÔåÆ any gap read as a *bright* flash. Changed to a neutral mid-dark gray (`#3f3f3f`) **only while the aerial is on** (stays `PAL.paper` with the aerial off so the SVG-off background still matches). Bright flash ÔåÆ dim blink.
> 2. **Real fix ÔÇö seamless transform-based zoom (commit `af10d1c`):** stop re-`setView`-ing on every frame. Hold Leaflet at a committed center/zoom and, for the small deltas of a live gesture, CSS-`transform` (translate+scale) the whole map container so the already-loaded tiles (and the shared FEMA/NWI overlay layers, which live on the same map) scale to track the gesture ÔÇö no tile reload, no wipe, no flash. A debounced (180 ms) `setView` re-renders crisp once the gesture settles. First paint / resize / >1.5-zoom-level jumps commit immediately so the aerial is never scaled into a blurry mess. Transform math: `scale = getZoomScale(z, committed)`; translate so the desired center maps to the container centre (matches the SVG, which renders at true scale every frame), `transform-origin:0 0`. Container CSS transforms don't affect Leaflet's coordinate math (it uses cached `_size`/pixel-origin, and `clientWidth` is layout-box, unaffected by transform), so alignment is preserved.
> **Tested + rejected on the way:** flipping the map to `zoomAnimation/fadeAnimation:true` made it *worse* (gap frames 1ÔåÆ4, min loaded still 2) and reintroduces SVG-desync risk ÔÇö not used.
> **Verified headless:** `diagnose-flash.mjs` now reports **0 gap frames** (min loaded 8, was 2); `ui-audit/verify-zoom-align.mjs` confirms the drawn building/parcel stay locked to the aerial through the transform and after the crisp commit (no jump). lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green. **Live confirmation on planyr.io still nice-to-have** (a one-frame flash can't be captured headless), but the mechanism + alignment are proven. **Note:** Document Review's PDF viewer uses a different (canvas raster) renderer, not Leaflet ÔÇö if zoom-flashing shows there too it's a separate fix, tracked if/when reported.
> **Follow-up 2026-06-19 (same branch) ÔÇö kill the blank-backdrop reveal during the gesture.** After the flash fix shipped, the owner saw (screenshot) large flat cream patches while panning/zooming a project. Cause: the CSS-transformed basemap container (`geoWrapRef`) slid/scaled and exposed the canvas's `PAL.paper` (`wrapRef`) behind it. Fix (no new dependency): wrap the basemap in a **static clip box** (`inset:0`, `overflow:hidden`, dark `#3f3f3f` bg, never transformed) holding an **oversized inner map div** (`inset:-GEO_OVERSCAN`, `GEO_OVERSCAN = 320` px). The map now overhangs the viewport by 320 px on every side with `keepBuffer` tiles already loaded, so a normal pan/zoom transform reveals **real imagery**; anything beyond the overhang shows the **static dark backdrop**, never the cream page. The transform math is unchanged (it already uses `map.getSize()/2`, which adapts to the larger container, so basemapÔåöSVG alignment is preserved). Verified headless: `ui-audit/pan-reveal.mjs` (a hard mid-pan drag) now shows imagery edge-to-edge with no cream; `verify-zoom-align.mjs` still shows the geometry locked to the aerial through the transform + crisp commit. lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.
> **Follow-up 2 2026-06-19 (same branch) ÔÇö kill the whole-screen "flash to black" on zoom-out.** Owner: zooming out from a building flashed the entire screen dark, even parts already loaded. Cause: the crisp re-render (`commit`) calls `map.setView`, whose Leaflet `viewreset`ÔåÆ`_resetAll` **removes ALL tiles and reloads them**, so for one frame the dark backdrop showed everywhere. Fix = **ghost-buffer the commit**: before the `setView`, clone the current basemap container (with its live CSS transform baked in) into a frozen overlay (`geoGhostRef`) pinned on top; the fresh tiles load underneath; on the tile layer's `load` event (or a 1.4 s fallback) the ghost is removed ÔÇö so the old picture stays visible until the new one is ready and the wipe is never seen. Also dropped the old non-buffered ">1.5 levels ÔåÆ hard commit" branch; the crisp re-render now fires on settle (160 ms) OR once accumulated zoom drift passes ~0.75 levels, both **ghost-buffered** so a mid-gesture re-base no longer flashes. Verified headless (`ui-audit/zoomout-frames.mjs`, samples a continuous wheel zoom-out and measures the fraction of dark-backdrop pixels per frame): worst-case dark coverage **1.6 %** (UI edges only) ÔÇö no full-screen black at any frame (was a brief whole-screen wipe). Alignment + crisp settle unchanged (`verify-zoom-align.mjs`). lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.
> **Follow-up 3 2026-06-19 (same branch) ÔÇö fix the slow gray initial load (regression).** Owner: after the above, a freshly-opened site sat on the gray backdrop for ~10 s before the aerial appeared. Cause: the overscan (2.5├ù viewport) **├ù** detectRetina (2├ù density) Ôëê **4├ù the tile bytes** on first paint, so on a real connection the detail tiles took ~10 s to cover (the sandbox is too fast to feel it ÔÇö reproduced with Playwright CDP network throttling at ~1.2 Mbps: time-to-coverage **9.1 s**). Fix = a **coarse "instant" backfill layer**: a second `L.tileLayer` under the detail layer capped at `maxNativeZoom: 13` (a handful of large-area tiles, no detectRetina) that loads near-instantly and blankets the view with blurry imagery, then the heavy detail layer is added **only after the backfill paints** (`bf.once("load", addDetail)` + a 600 ms fallback) so its many retina tiles don't flood the connection and starve the coarse ones. Result (same 1.2 Mbps throttle): time-to-coverage **9.1 s ÔåÆ ~3 s** (blurry), detail then sharpens on top to the full 45 tiles; on a normal connection the coarse coverage is ~instant. No regression to the interactive fixes (zoom-out flash still 1.6 %, pan still full-imagery). `addDetail` guards against the aerial being toggled off mid-wait. lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green. Harness: `ui-audit/initial-load.mjs` (optional `THROTTLE=1`).

### B175 ÔÇö Parcel active/inactive checkbox in the Parcel menu `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; filed provisionally as B170, renumbered **B175** ÔÇö #152 (B167/B168) + #153 (B169ÔÇôB171) collisions, so this is the real next free ID after B171. Core already shipped under B100; this filed + closed the residual.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/optimistic-wozniak-4iltkx`). Per-parcel **Active checkbox** on each row of the left Parcel menu (checked = active; unchecked = inactive). B100 (2026-06-16) already shipped the underlying `active` flag, the `activeParcelsOf`/`siteSqft` active-only math, the dimmed-on-canvas render, and per-parcel persistence (Site Model `data` ÔåÆ Supabase). This item added the **literal checkbox affordance** the owner asked for (the prior control was a `Ô£ô Active / Ôù» Inactive` chip on the *selected* parcel only; now every row carries a checkbox, and the row + label dim with a "┬À inactive" tag) and **closed the merge-candidacy gap**: `toggleMerge` now refuses to add an inactive parcel to the combine set, and `mergeParcels` filters inactive parcels out of the chosen group ÔÇö so an inactive parcel is excluded from yield **and** from the working merge, matching B100's intent. `SitePlanner.jsx` only; reuses the existing `toggleParcelActive` (pushes history + autosaves). **Self-verified headless (Chromium, logged-out preview):** two-parcel site ÔåÆ both checkboxes checked ÔåÆ unchecking parcel A dims its row + canvas polygon and stamps "┬À inactive"; the other parcel stays active. 257 tests green, lint 0 errors, build green.
> **Dedup note:** core was B100 ÔÇö this is the affordance + merge-candidacy residual, not a re-implementation. No `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump (the `active` field already exists, defaulted-at-read).

### B173 ÔÇö App pinned to the viewport; map scale bar always visible `[Site Planner / UI + Shell]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B168, renumbered **B173** ÔÇö #152 + #153 collisions; next free after B171.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/optimistic-wozniak-4iltkx`). Root cause: nothing guaranteed the app couldn't grow taller than the window, so the page itself could scroll and push the bottom-of-canvas scale bar below the fold (and the header off the top). Fix is structural, not a scale-bar move: `index.css` now sets `overflow:hidden` on `html, body, #root` and pins them to `100dvh` (dynamic viewport height ÔÇö handles mobile browser chrome) via `@supports`; `Shell.jsx` adds a defensive `overflow:hidden` on the shell column. The in-canvas measurement-grade scale bar (bottom-right, `lib/sheetFurniture.js`, shared with print/export) sits a fixed 40 px above the canvas bottom, so once the page can't scroll it is **always** visible ÔÇö no need to fork a second portal-mounted overlay (kept export parity; the owner's "bottom-left fixed overlay" was the suggested mechanism, the real need was "always visible," now met). **Self-verified headless at 1440├ù900 AND 1280├ù600:** `document.scrollHeight === innerHeight` (no page overflow) at both sizes, and the scale bar + north arrow render at the canvas bottom with both header rows visible simultaneously.
> **Dedup note:** distinct from completed **B144** (scale bar clipping/measurement-grade in print/export) ÔÇö this was on-screen viewport layout.

### B172 ÔÇö Module tabs read as inactive, not disabled `[global UI / Shell]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B167, renumbered **B172** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B167/B168 (#152, map items) and B169ÔÇôB171 (#153, basemap-blur trio), so this is the real next free ID after B171.)*

New `ModuleTab` component in `AppHeader.jsx`: inactive tabs are **full-opacity `#c9c3b4`** (muted-but-legible, AA on the `#14110e` bar), the **module accent reveals on hover** (Site `#1D9E75`, Schedule `#7F77DD`, Markup `#EF9F27`), and the **active** tab keeps the accent text + 2 px underline. ### B170 ÔÇö Fix retina/HiDPI tile requests so the aerial basemap renders sharp `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B168, renumbered **B170** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (`claude/brave-galileo-wm8gti`) took B167 + B168 for unrelated map items, so this is the real next free ID; was gated on B169)*
`[x]` Gated on B169 (the diagnostic), which confirmed the dominant blur causes were **(a) HiDPI tiles never requested** and **(b) the planner's fractional zoom** ÔÇö so the source-swap fix path (B171/NEW-3) was correctly NOT triggered, since the imagery source isn't the problem.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-19** (branch `claude/awesome-darwin-7ui0qm`). Added **`detectRetina: true`** to **both** Esri World Imagery tile layers ÔÇö the map finder (`MapFinder.jsx`, `L.tileLayer(bm.tiles, ÔÇª)`) and the planner backdrop (`SitePlanner.jsx`, `GEO_BASEMAP`). On a HiDPI display (`devicePixelRatio > 1`) Leaflet now requests **one-zoom-higher native tiles and renders them at half size** (downsampled = crisp) instead of upscaling 1├ù tiles (= soft). This also sharpens the planner's *fractional* zoom (`zoomSnap:0`, driven by `ppfToZoom`) because it prefers downsampling a higher-zoom tile over upscaling a lower one. **Kept the Esri source** (per the NEW-2 constraint) and **did NOT touch the planner map's `zoomSnap:0`** ÔÇö snapping that map to integer zoom would desync the aerial from the feet-space SVG (the deliberate ppfÔåÆzoom scale lock); `detectRetina` only changes which tiles are fetched + their display size, never the map's CRS zoom, so the lock is untouched. **Self-verified in a real headless browser** (`gis-verify/retina-basemap-verify.mjs`, simulating `deviceScaleFactor` 1 vs 2 over a Katy parcel): at standard density the map requests **z11** tiles; at retina density it requests **z12** (one level higher = 2├ù pixel density), with **0 failed/gray tiles** at either density (no coverage regression) ÔÇö logged **VERIFICATION V43**. lint **0 errors** ┬À **257 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. (The sharpness gain is invisible at the sandbox's `devicePixelRatio = 1`, so the *visible* improvement is best confirmed on Michael's own retina/4K display ÔÇö but the higher-density tile request is proven engaged.)

### B169 ÔÇö Diagnose basemap blur: retina/DPR vs. fractional zoom vs. source resolution `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B167, renumbered **B169** ÔÇö concurrent `main` (`claude/brave-galileo-wm8gti`) took B167 + B168 for unrelated map items, so this is the real next free ID)*
`[x]` Diagnostic only ÔÇö instrument + report the root cause of soft aerial imagery **before** any provider change (the fix paths are B170 + B171).
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-19** (branch `claude/awesome-darwin-7ui0qm`). Root cause established from the two tile-layer call sites (both the **Esri World Imagery** source, `server.arcgisonline.com/.../World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}`) plus a live headless instrumentation:
> - **(a) Retina/HiDPI NOT requested ÔÇö CONFIRMED, primary.** Neither `L.tileLayer` set `detectRetina` (nor any `@2x` / `tileSize:512`): the map finder (`MapFinder.jsx:291`) and the planner backdrop (`SitePlanner.jsx:1043`) both fetched **1├ù tiles**, which the browser then **upscales** on any `devicePixelRatio > 1` display (most modern laptops / 4K monitors) ÔåÆ soft. This is the most likely "it looks blurry on my screen" cause.
> - **(b) Fractional zoom ÔÇö CONFIRMED for the planner backdrop.** The planner map runs **`zoomSnap: 0`** (`SitePlanner.jsx:1031`) and its zoom is driven **continuously** from `ppfToZoom(view.ppf, lat)`, so the raster is almost always displayed at a **fractional** zoom = interpolated = blurred (independent of DPR). The map finder uses Leaflet's default integer zoom, so it's not affected by (b) ÔÇö only (a).
> - **(c) Genuinely low-res source ÔÇö RULED OUT as primary.** Esri World Imagery is **native to z19** (`maxNativeZoom: 19`, ~0.3 m/px) with good Houston-metro coverage; both layers merely *display* past native (21/24), so the only "source" softness is upscaling past z19 at very tight zoom ÔÇö not a stale/low-res source. Verified live (Katy parcel): tiles return **HTTP 200** with no gray/missing tiles at the working zooms.
> - **Live instrumentation:** `gis-verify/retina-basemap-verify.mjs` drove the headless map at `deviceScaleFactor` 1 vs 2 and recorded the **zoom level of every World_Imagery tile actually requested** + load status. Confirmed the pre-fix behavior (1├ù tiles, no DPR awareness) and gave the before/after baseline for B170.
> **Outcome ÔåÆ fix routing:** causes (a)+(b) are real and fixable **without** changing the imagery source ÔåÆ **B170** (add `detectRetina`, keep Esri, don't snap the planner's locked zoom). Cause (c) is not the issue, so **B171** (license-clean high-res source evaluation) stays **gated** ÔÇö only revisit if B170 proves insufficient by eye on a real retina display. lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green (no app-code change in this diagnostic item beyond the verify harness).

### B168 ÔÇö Map-viewer project card: kill single-click delete, move to a right-click context menu `[Site Planner / map]` (bug + feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B168 ÔÇö next free ID after B167)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-19 (branch `claude/brave-galileo-wm8gti`). The "floating project card on the map canvas" is the **Your sites** panel (rendered over the map in `MapFinder.jsx`); each card row showed an inline Ô£ò that opened a delete dialog. ### B166 ÔÇö Geometry check reads wrong county acreage (homesite carve-out, not total) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B166)*
`[x]` **Repro (owner-reported):** parcel `0440110000065` ÔÇö Appraisal panel + drawn boundary agree (17.00 AC vs 17.06 ac) but the geometry check screamed **"county 0.50 ac vs 17.06 ac (3,312% off)."** The 0.50 ac was the **HOMESITE carve-out** (Legal: "PT TR 4D (HOMESITE)"), not the total tract.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-19** (branch `claude/busy-carson-1zuiuq`). Confirmed root cause against the **live HCAD parcels layer** field list: the record carries **both** `Acreage` (total) **and** `acreage_1` (a sub-acreage) ÔÇö both match the acres regex, and `countyAcres()` used `keys.find(...)` which returned whichever came first (the homesite ~0.50). Fix in `SitePlanner.jsx` `countyAcres()`: collect **all** acreage-named fields, drop any whose name marks it a sub-acreage (`home_site|homestead|hs_|hmst|exempt|imprv|improv`), and take the **MAX** of what remains ÔÇö the total tract is always the largest, so a homesite/ag/exemption carve-out can never win. County-agnostic (fixes the statewide carve-out pattern, not just this parcel). Distinct root cause from B75 (that was a m┬▓-vs-acres unit misread; the TxGIO unit handling is untouched). lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.

> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-19** (branch `claude/busy-carson-1zuiuq`). The **Parcel panel already had** a setback-visibility toggle (Boundary ÔÇ║ "Setbacks per edge" ÔÇ║ the "Show" checkbox, same `settings.showSetback` state) ÔÇö so the fix was to remove the **duplicate** from the Setup/Site-defaults tab (`SitePlanner.jsx` ~L6579) and relabel the parcel-panel one to the full **"Show setback line"** (with a tooltip). Net result: the control now lives only with the parcel object, exactly as asked; state/behavior unchanged. lint 0 errors ┬À 257 tests ┬À build green.

### B157 ÔÇö Added-detention area label seats on the NEW ground, not the whole-pond centre `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived 2026-06-18 in chat ÔÇö owner: when expanding an existing pond, show the added surface area + acreage at the centre of mass of the NEW area, because the whole-pond centroid often stays inside the existing basin. Refinement of B139/B140; filed provisionally as B151, renumbered **B157** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B150ÔÇôB156 (easement tool, multipart-parcel + Delete-key bugs, markup hit-testing), so this is the real next free ID.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-18 (branch `claude/brave-noether-rwzkqk`). B140 stacked the "+added" increment as a third line on the pond's single centred label, so on a one-sided (or uniform) expansion the added figure floated over the EXISTING basin ÔÇö the exact case the owner flagged, where the whole-pond centroid stays inside the old pond. Now the added detention area gets its OWN on-map label, seated on the **thickest part of the new ground**:
- **New `lib/pondGeom.js` (`addedAreaLabelPoint`, unit-tested):** the pole-of-inaccessibility of (expanded footprint ÔêÆ baseline footprint) via a coarse grid + local refine ÔÇö the interior point of the new area farthest from any edge. Handles a one-sided strip (lands mid-strip), a uniform "push banks out" ring (lands in the band, never the centre), and a corner push; returns null on no-expansion / pure shrink / degenerate ring. Pure world-feet geometry (no React/DOM), so it unit-tests without a browser.
- **Label (`SitePlanner.jsx` element-label builder):** in expansion mode the centred label reads **"Existing Detention Pond"** + the existing area, re-anchored over the **existing basin** (baseline centroid); a second label titled **"Additional Detention"** + `"+X.XX ac ┬À +Y sf"` is pushed at the added-region point (owner wording, 2026-06-18). A plain (non-expansion) pond label is unchanged ("Detention Pond" + area). The added area is the **footprint / top-down** area (the metric behind the owner's "20ÔÇô25 % of the site" pond-coverage rule ÔÇö NOT water-surface). Both ride B121's existing LOD/collision pool via a per-label id (`<id>` / `<id>#add`) ÔÇö no parallel renderer. A net shrink / sub-`POND_ADD_MIN_SF` (50 sf) change falls back to the inline "+/ÔêÆ" increment so the delta still shows.
- **Verified (self, headless Chromium, logged-out preview build, 2026-06-18):** seeded a one-sided expansion + a concentric expansion; the existing-area label seats over the old basin and the "+added" label seats over the new ground (one-sided: to the right of the basin; concentric: out on the new ring, 183 px off the centre that sits inside the old pond), correct sf/ac, **zero console/page errors**. Re-verified after the owner wording change ÔÇö labels read **"Existing Detention Pond"** and **"Additional Detention"** with the +ac/sf in place. lint **0 errors** (14 pre-existing warnings) ┬À **257 tests** (6 in `test/pondGeom.test.js`) ┬À build green ┬À `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Browser pass ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION V42**.
> Refines **B140** (stays Done as the acres+sf label); builds on **B139**'s `det.baseline`. No `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump ÔÇö reads the existing persisted baseline, adds no field.
> **Follow-up 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/brave-noether-rwzkqk`) ÔÇö two-tone color + per-zone controls.** Owner: "make it so there's a slight color difference between the existing and additional and that I can change the settings for both." In expansion mode the **existing basin** keeps the cartographic water gradient (from **B231**) while the **added ring** paints in a lighter solid tint (`POND_ADD_FILL_DEFAULT #A7D3DD`, a lighter shade of the new teal pond), so the two read apart at a glance. Two color pickers ÔÇö *Existing basin* / *Added area* ÔÇö in the Expanding panel write `det.existFill` / `det.addFill` (live, saved with the element, both overridable); a normal (non-expanding) pond is unchanged. Reconciled cleanly over **B231**'s pond restyle (gradient default for existing, solid override for added); the pond label's dark-slate + white-halo carto style (B231) already reads over either fill, so no per-zone label ink needed. Both polygon + rect render branches updated. Build green; B157 headless self-check still 7/7 (wording + added-area seating intact).

### B154 ÔÇö Delete key intermittently needs two presses to remove a selected element `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B151, renumbered **B154** ÔÇö concurrent `main` took B150ÔÇôB153 (easement tool) plus a second B151 (multipart parcel), so this is the real next free ID; the implementing commit references the bug as NEW-1)*
`[x]` Repro: click an element to select it, press Delete ÔÇö sometimes it deletes on the first press, sometimes it takes two ("works when I was already on the canvas; fails when I came from a panel/button first"). **Root cause (confirmed, but NOT the report's first guess):** the keydown handler is **already** `window`-level ÔÇö so it isn't a focus-scope problem. The real cause is a **stale closure via passive-effect re-binding:** `onKey` is created inside a `useEffect`, and although `sel`/`multi` are in its deps, the effect re-binds the listener **after** the commit/paint. A Delete pressed in the brief window right after a selecting click runs the *previously* bound `onKey`, which closed over the old `sel` (often `null`) ÔåÆ the guard `(sel || multi.length)` was false ÔåÆ no-op, no `preventDefault`; the next press (listener now re-bound) works. Under a heavy render (this is a ~6k-line component) the window widens enough to be hit intermittently.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-18** (branch `claude/gallant-lovelace-82konu`). Added live `selRef`/`multiRef` mirrors (synced during render ÔÇö a passive effect would lag the same window) and made the **Delete/Backspace branch read the refs**, and `deleteSel` read `selRef.current`/`multiRef.current`, so deletion always acts on the *current* selection no matter which listener instance fired ÔÇö no second press. The handler stayed `window`-level (no scope change). Also **hardened the existing typing guard** ÔÇö it already bailed when `document.activeElement` is INPUT/SELECT/TEXTAREA (so Delete never nuked canvas elements while editing a field); extended it to **`contentEditable`** too. The lone same-tick `setSel(x); deleteSel()` caller (the element context-menu Delete) is unaffected ÔÇö the element is already selected when that menu opens, so the ref holds it. lint **0 errors** ┬À **230 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. **Self-verified in a real headless browser** (built app): (A) select an element ÔåÆ one Delete removes it (2ÔåÆ1); (B) touch a tool/panel button, then select an element, then a **single immediate** Delete still removes exactly one ÔÇö the reported flow, now first-press reliable; zero console errors. Logged as **VERIFICATION V40**.

### B150 ÔÇö Easement drawing tool (core): first-class Easement object, input modes, attributes, editing, render, persistence `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; minted B150 ÔÇö next free ID after B149)*
`[x]` New first-class **Easement** object on the editable layer (immutable-backdrop model preserved): a `markups[]` entry `kind:"easement"` with a stable UUID and a display label derived from type + width (cosmetic, editable override). **Mode A ÔÇö Centerline + width:** digitize a polyline centerline, enter total width ÔåÆ strip generated by offsetting the centerline ┬▒width/2 with flat end caps; width is editable after creation and re-offsets the strip live. **Mode B ÔÇö Boundary polygon:** draw a closed ring directly. Attributes panel (Element tab): type (Utility/PUE, Sanitary Sewer, Storm/Drainage, Water, Pipeline, Access/Ingress-Egress, Aerial/Overhead, Temporary Construction, Other), holder/beneficiary, width (auto-filled in mode A), recording reference, exclusive (y/n), status (existing/proposed), restrictsBuildings (default true), restrictsPaving (default false), notes. Editing: draggable centerline/polygon vertices (´╝ï add, Shift-click delete), move, delete, relabel; reuses the existing snap system + persistent toggle. Rendering: semi-transparent hatched fill color-coded by type, centerline shown for strip easements, proposed rendered dashed; per-easement area readout + total encumbered area in Yield. Persistence: Supabase, editable layer, private by default, RLS-protected.
> Ô£à **Done (branch `claude/clever-franklin-7ds6kg`).** **Engine:** inspected the codebase first per the gate ÔÇö an offset/buffer utility already existed (`bufferPolyline` in `lib/metesAndBounds.js`), so **no JSTS added**. Generalized it to be asymmetric-ready (`{leftW,rightW}`, default still symmetric ┬▒w/2) so a future asymmetric-strip UI needs no geometry rework, and factored out a shared one-sided `offsetPolyline` primitive. **Domain logic** in new `lib/easements.js` (`EASEMENT_TYPES`, `easementLabel`, per-mode `deriveEasementRing`, `easementArea`). The **type dropdown uses the shared portal `AnchoredMenu`** so it can't render behind the rail/zoom rail. Easement is a markup ÔåÆ persists through the existing markups[] save path (Supabase, private, RLS) with no schema change. New rail "Easement" section (mode + default width/type via the portal menu). Self-verified in a headless browser (centerline + boundary draw, attributes panel auto-opens, portal type dropdown swaps type + relabels, vertex grips). **246 tests pass** (16 new in `test/easements.test.js`); lint 0 errors; build green.

### B151 ÔÇö Import easement from a metes-and-bounds description `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; minted B151)*
`[x]` Reuse the metes-and-bounds ÔåÆ polygon parser so the user can paste a legal description and spawn an Easement object (boundary geometry), attributes editable afterward. Depends on B150.
> Ô£à **Done.** Reusability gate satisfied with **no parser duplication**: the parser already lives in `lib/metesAndBounds.js` (`parseCalls`/`callsToPath`) and already returns a polygon in the planner's coordinate frame (planar feet), so it wired straight in. Added a **"Plot as easement ÔåÆ"** button to the Title reader / metes-&-bounds modal; on the POB click it builds a `kind:"easement"` (mode B for a closed tract; a `mbWidth` corridor strip for an open traverse) via the shared `makeEasement`/`commitEasement` path, then auto-selects it with the attributes panel open. Self-verified in a headless browser (closed traverse ÔåÆ easement placed, panel shown, 0 page errors).

### B152 ÔÇö Offset-from-parcel-edge easement mode `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B152)*
`[x]` Third input mode: select one or more contiguous edges of an active parcel and enter a width ÔåÆ one-sided strip parallel to and inset from the boundary (the "10ÔÇ▓ utility easement adjacent to the property line" pattern), with corner mitering where the run turns. Depends on B150; shares the offset engine. Designed so the same machinery is reusable for a future setback/building-line tool (no setbacks built now).
> Ô£à **Done.** `buildParcelEdgeStrip` (easements.js) orders the chosen edges into one contiguous run (rejects non-contiguous / whole-boundary selections), auto-picks the **inward** side (offset toward the parcel interior), and miters turns via the shared `offsetPolyline`. Clickable edge hit-targets render over each active parcel in this mode; a floating **"Create easement ÔÅÄ"** bar (mirrors the merge-parcels bar) confirms. The stored easement keeps the edge-run as its editable centerline + `offsetSide`, so width edits and vertex drags re-derive correctly. Self-verified headless (the edge hit-target is the topmost element at the click; strip created).

### B153 ÔÇö Easement exclusion-zone hook for buildable area / yield `[Site Planner]` (task)  *(arrived as "NEW-4"; minted B153)*
`[x]` Expose each easement's geometry + restrictsBuildings/restrictsPaving flags as exclusion zones the yield/buildable-area calc can consume (subtract restrictsBuildings from buildable footprint, restrictsPaving from pavable area). Depends on B150. Integrate if a buildable calc exists; else expose the data + hook cleanly.
> Ô£à **Done ÔÇö data + hook exposed (no premature integration).** Confirmed current state first: there is **no** buildable-area/verdict engine yet (`developableArea()` was a stub; the Yield panel reports drawn areas, not a buildable envelope), so subtracting from existing numbers would be wrong. Added `exclusionZonesOf(m)` (each easement's ring + restriction flags; defaults block buildings, allow paving), made `developableArea(m)` surface them so the future engine consumes them with no rework, added `easementsOf`, and folded `"easement"` into `EASEMENT_KINDS` (so `constraintsOf` includes them). Surfaced encumbered totals (gross / restricts-buildings / restricts-paving) in the Yield panel. Covered by `test/easements.test.js`.

### B147 ÔÇö Markup/shape tools: Bluebeam-style editing (rotate + resize boxes, vertex-edit lines/polylines/polygons) `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived 2026-06-17 in chat: "fix the rectangle and markup tools ÔÇª make them more like bluebeam, not being able to rotate a rectangle is wild"; minted B147 ÔÇö next free after B146)*
`[x]` The neutral markup shapes (drawn with the Line / Rectangle / Ellipse / Polygon / Polyline tools) could only be **moved, restyled, or deleted** ÔÇö selecting one just recoloured it, with **no handles**. You couldn't rotate or resize a rectangle/ellipse, or reshape a line/polyline/polygon. Meanwhile elements have resize/rotate grips and measures/parcels have vertex editing, so markups were the odd one out. **Owner's headline: "not being able to rotate a rectangle is wild."**
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/gallant-lovelace-82konu`). Added Bluebeam-style geometry editing to the markups[] layer, **reusing the existing element/measure transform machinery** (no parallel system):
> - **Rect / ellipse:** on select (Select tool), 4 corner + 4 edge **resize grips** + a **rotate handle** above the top edge ÔÇö mirrors the element handles. Resize is computed in the shape's own rotated frame (opposite side stays fixed); rotate spins about the centre, snapping to 15┬░ when Snap is on, free (whole degrees) when off. The `rot` field already existed on rect/ellipse (rendered via `transform=rotate`), so this is purely the missing UI. New `mkResize`/`mkRotate` drag modes + `startMarkupResize`/`startMarkupRotate`.
> - **Line / polyline / polygon:** draggable **vertex dots**; **´╝ï** on edge midpoints (polyline/polygon) to insert a point; **Shift-click** a dot to delete one (keeps the kind's minimum ÔÇö 2 for line/polyline, 3 for polygon). New `mkVertex` drag mode + `startMarkupVertex`/`addMarkupVertex`, mirroring the measure-vertex handlers (B141). Shared helpers `mkPts`/`setMkPts` normalise line `{a,b}` Ôåö point arrays.
> - **Properties panel:** rect/ellipse gain numeric **Width / Height** and **Rotation┬░** fields (precise entry; kept out of `mkStyle` via a separate `setSelMarkupGeom` so new shapes don't inherit a past size/angle), plus a one-line editing hint per shape.
> - **Scope:** only the 5 neutral annotation kinds get grips; **semantic markups stay move-only** (utilRoute / traced / infwater / encumbrance carry derived geometry ÔÇö corridor/pad/centerline/calls ÔÇö that hand-editing would desync). Locked markups show no grips.
> lint **0 errors** ┬À **225 tests** ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. **Self-verified in a real headless browser** (drove the built app): drew a rectangle ÔåÆ corner+edge grips + rotate handle appear ÔåÆ dragged a corner (300ÔåÆ410 px) and the rotate handle (rotate(0)ÔåÆrotate(53)); drew a 3-point polyline ÔåÆ 3 vertex grips + 2 ´╝ï handles ÔåÆ dragged the middle vertex and the path updated; zero errors from the change. Logged as **VERIFICATION V38**.

### B144 ÔÇö Scale bar clips off-frame and isn't measurement-grade in print/export `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B141, renumbered **B144** ÔÇö concurrent `main` PR #114 had already taken B140ÔÇôB142 and PR #117 took B143, so this is the real next free ID; the implementing commit still references the provisional B141/B142)*
`[x]` Repro: Print/PDF (and PNG export) of the map view ÔåÆ the scale bar's max label ("500") clipped at the right frame edge, the "0" floated above the bar instead of under the start tick, and the segments read as noise rather than a measured ruler. **Root cause (confirmed):** the export reused the *on-screen* scale bar's **screen-pixel** sizing (`scaleBarFt.px Ôëê 120 px` + fixed `28`/`30` px offsets) and screenshotted it into a differently-sized export frame, so it overflowed; labels were also drawn *above* the bar with no plate.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/gallant-lovelace-82konu`). New pure module **`lib/sheetFurniture.js`** draws the furniture **sized in OUTPUT units and anchored to the export FRAME**, not the screen: everything is a fraction of `refS = min(frame w,h)`, so it lands at a fixed physical size on the page (on a letter sheet the short side Ôëê 8.5 in ÔåÆ safe-area inset Ôëê 0.38 in). The scale bar now **snaps to a round real-world distance** (`pickScaleBar`, from 50/100/200/250/500/1000 ft + smaller/larger steps for extreme zoom) that best fills a target width band **without exceeding a hard ceiling** (so it can't clip); **alternating black/white segments**; **tick marks at 0 / midpoint / max with the numbers centered directly under their ticks**; a **"FEET"** unit label; on a **translucent legibility plate** so it reads over light pavement or dark canopy. The graphic bar stays the source of truth (its length encodes the distance, so it survives image rescaling ÔÇö no "1 in = X ft" text scale). Recomputes on zoom. `buildExportSvg` (`SitePlanner.jsx`) composites it into the print/PNG viewBox; the old screen-pixel furniture block + the now-unused `scaleBarFt` were removed. **Also applied on-screen** (owner asked to "fix the site planyr app too"): the live canvas uses the same primitives via `buildScreenFurnitureSvg` (viewport-anchored, fixed modest size) so the app matches the print. **Workspace confirmed Site Planner only** ÔÇö Document Review has no print furniture (nothing shared to fix twice). lint **0 errors** ┬À **222 tests** (18 new in `test/sheetFurniture.test.js`: round-distance snap, no-clip ceiling, safe-area anchoring, plate/label/FEET render, no compass rose, screen variant) ┬À build green; `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. **Self-verified in a real headless browser** (drove the built app: drew buildings ÔåÆ File Ôû¥ ÔåÆ Print / pick frame ÔåÆ Print, captured the actual print page; also captured a PNG export and the on-screen corners) ÔÇö scale bar fully inside the frame, round **0 / 125 / 250 FEET**, no clipping. Logged as **VERIFICATION V34**. Shared root cause + fix with **B145**.

### B145 ÔÇö North arrow oversized and illegible in print/export `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-2"; filed provisionally as B142, renumbered **B145** after the same concurrent-`main` collision as B144)*
`[x]` Repro: Print/PDF (and PNG export) of the map view ÔåÆ the north arrow was oversized for the frame, sat directly on the imagery (illegible over dark canopy), and used generic placeholder styling. **Same root cause as B144** ÔÇö drawn as a screen-pixel overlay (a fixed-radius circle + triangle) screenshotted into a differently-sized export frame, rather than composited in output units.
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/gallant-lovelace-82konu`, same change as B144). The north arrow is now a **clean filled arrow + "N" on the same translucent legibility plate** as the scale bar (`northArrowPlate` in `lib/sheetFurniture.js`), a **modest fixed ~0.5 in tall in the export** (`0.06┬ÀrefS`), **anchored top-left with a fixed margin inside the safe area** so it never clips, and it **rotates to true map bearing** (points straight up on the planner's north-up canvas; `bearingDeg` param wired for any future view rotation). **No ornate compass rose** ÔÇö a simple arrow, the convention for engineering exhibits (a unit test asserts no `<circle>`). Verified together with B144 (same browser run, **V34**) ÔÇö print page shows a modest, legible arrow top-left on its plate.
### B143 ÔÇö Text box edits like Bluebeam: Enter = newline, click away / Esc to finish `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö follow-up to B142
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Owner: starting a text box then clicking out "won't let me" exit. Two causes, both fixed to match Bluebeam's Text Box markup (researched against the Bluebeam Revu docs ÔÇö "finish by selecting away or pressing Esc"; a text box is multi-line):
- **Click-out was trapped at ANY distance:** the canvas pointerdown `preventDefault`s the textarea blur, so `onBlur`ÔåÆ`commitEditCallout` never fired and you stayed in the editor even clicking 400 px away. Fix: a full-canvas transparent catcher rendered under the inline editor (`pointerEvents:"all"`, commits on pointerdown) ÔÇö clicking anywhere outside the box now finishes it.
- **Enter committed (single-line behavior):** Enter now inserts a **newline**; you finish by clicking away or Esc (Esc keeps the text). Placeholder updated to "Type; click away or Esc to finish".
- Empty box is still removed when you click away without typing (matches Bluebeam).
- Verified live (headless): Enter ÔåÆ "Line1\nLine2" (still editing); click away +80 px ÔåÆ finishes + commits two lines; Esc ÔåÆ finishes keeping the text; empty box removed on click-away; zero console errors. lint 0 errors ┬À build green ┬À tests pass. Browser pass ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION V33**.
> Cross-ref: completes the text-box UX started in **B142** (selectable across the whole box) ÔÇö together they make text boxes select / edit / finish like Bluebeam.

### B140 ÔÇö On-map pond footprint area labels (existing & expansion) `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Pond map labels now show **acres + sf** (was sf only): `<ac> ac ┬À <sf> sf` (e.g. "4.78 ac ┬À 208,161 sf"). In expansion mode (B139) the label becomes a 3-line stack ÔÇö name / existing **TOTAL** `ac ┬À sf` / **+added** increment `+x.xx ac ┬À +y sf` ÔÇö so the new total is inferable (mirrors the panel's "+ac-ft"). A focused change in the element-label builder (`SitePlanner.jsx`), reusing `polyArea`/`f2`/`SQFT_PER_ACRE` and B139's `det.baseline`; rides the existing **B121** label LOD/collision engine (no parallel renderer). Verified live (headless): plain pond "3.60 ac ┬À 156,730 sf"; expanded "+1.18 ac ┬À +51,599 sf". **Deferred (polish, noted):** the per-view hide toggle + leader-line for too-small ponds ÔÇö the LOD engine already drops labels that don't fit, so neither blocks. Browser pass ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION V31**.

### B141 ÔÇö Bluebeam-style editable vertices on the measurement tools `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Area / polyline / line measurements are now control-point editable: **drag** a vertex to reshape, click a **´╝ï** on an edge to insert one, **Shift-click** a dot to delete (area keeps ÔëÑ3 pts, line/polyline ÔëÑ2); area + perimeter recompute live. New `startMeasureVertex`/`addMeasureVertex` mirror the polygon-element vertex handlers; a `measureVertex` drag mode updates `measures[].pts`; legacy `{a,b}` records migrate to `{mode,pts}` on first edit. Handles render only when the measure is selected (Select tool), styled like the parcel/element handles. Verified live (headless): selecting an area shows the handles; one vertex drag changed it 166,530 ÔåÆ 257,958 sf. Reuses the established vertex-edit pattern (consistent with Doc Review's shape-aware select). Browser pass ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION V31**.

### B142 ÔÇö Text box not selectable on click `[Site Planner]` (bug)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). The callout/text-box rect now sets `pointerEvents="all"`, so the **whole box** is clickable even when its fill is none/transparent (previously a no-fill box caught clicks only on the painted area / thin border ÔÇö the likely "click does nothing" cause). Filled boxes unchanged; `deleteSel` already removes a selected callout. **Honest note:** a DEFAULT-fill text box already selected fine in headless testing (create ÔåÆ deselect ÔåÆ click reselects, the "Text box" panel opens, Delete removes) ÔÇö I could **not** reproduce the reported "nothing happens" for a normal box, so this is a hardening fix for the no/transparent-fill case that matches Doc Review's shape-aware hit test. If it still recurs on a specific box, reopen with that box's fill setting + exact steps. Resize handles for text boxes were intentionally not added (they auto-size to their text). Browser pass ÔåÆ **VERIFICATION V31**.

### B132 ÔÇö Detention pond expansion: lock an existing pond as a baseline, then read the storage gained `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Owner workflow: trace an existing on-site detention pond, lock it as the baseline, then enlarge it (drag banks out and/or deepen) to read the **added detention** in acre-feet. Built entirely on the existing pond machinery ÔÇö no parallel store, no new schema bump.
- **Panel** (`SitePlanner.jsx`, pond branch of the selected-element inspector): the **Detention storage** section gains a **"Lock as existing pond"** button. Locking snapshots the current footprint ring (world feet: `selEl.points` or `elCorners`) **plus its own depth/freeboard/slope** into `el.det.baseline`, so the comparison is frozen and apples-to-apples. Once locked, an **"Expansion vs. existing"** box shows **Existing** / **Proposed** storage and a live **"Storage gained +X.XX ac-ft"** (green) ÔÇö or **"Storage lost"** (red) when shrunk below baseline ÔÇö plus the cubic-feet delta. **Clear** removes the baseline (keeps depth/freeboard/slope).
- **Increase = `detentionStorage(proposed) ÔêÆ detentionStorage(baseline)`** ÔÇö the *same* prismoidal/taper function for both sides, so the delta can't drift from a method mismatch. Reflects both footprint growth and any depth/slope change.
- **Canvas ghost** (`renderElPx`): the locked baseline ring draws as a faint **dashed outline** under the pond so the expansion is visible. Handled for both the irregular-polygon branch (drawn in world feet directly) and the rotated-rectangle branch (a counter-rotated `<g>` undoes the element's `rotate(el.rot)` so the world-feet ghost lands true). `pointerEvents:none` so it never interferes with selection/drag.
- **Persistence:** `baseline` rides inside the already-persisted `el.det` (additive, lossless) ÔÇö survives save/reload, no `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump. Honest framing kept: "screening only ÔÇö confirm with your civil engineer."
- **Scope (owner-chosen):** "just the increase." Deliberately **excluded** for now (offered, declined): a required-vs-provided "is it enough?" check against added impervious, and a solver ("grow this much to hit a target"). Easy follow-ons if wanted.
- **Verified:** lint 0 errors (14 pre-existing warnings, none in new code) ┬À **191 tests pass** ┬À build green ┬À lazy-chunk split intact (`SitePlannerApp` own chunk). Browser click-through logged as **V25** in `VERIFICATION.md`.

### B123 ÔÇö Restructure building label + make square footage persist on zoom-out `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/determined-brown-bf86l7`). The building label is now a 4-line **level-of-detail stack** built on B121's shared engine ÔÇö name ÔåÆ square footage ÔåÆ "(incl. N bump-outs)" ÔåÆ dimensions ÔÇö so the **square footage survives zoom-out far longer** instead of vanishing early.
- **New ordering + wording** (`lib/labelLayout.js` `buildingLabelLines`, unit-tested): square footage is now its **own line** (was crammed onto the sf line with the bump-out note), the bump-out note is a **separate parenthetical line shown only when the building has bump-outs**, and its wording changed from "+N bump-outs" to **"(incl. N bump-outs)"**. `SitePlanner.jsx`'s building-label branch builds the stack via that helper; every other element label is unchanged.
- **Drop order = dimensions ÔåÆ parenthetical ÔåÆ square footage ÔåÆ name.** The stack is ordered highest-priority-first and B121's `fitLines`/`layoutLabels` drop from the end, so dimensions go first on zoom-out and square footage outlives them (it drops only at extreme zoom-out). Reuses B121's pool ÔÇö not a parallel renderer, as the item required.
- Poly-drawn buildings keep their existing name/sf/ac label; only rect buildings get the 4-line stack.
- **Tested:** `test/labelLayout.test.js` adds a `buildingLabelLines` case (exact order, singular/plural + conditional parenthetical, and a `fitLines` assertion proving sf outlives dimensions). lint 0 errors ┬À **174 tests pass** ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact. Browser-verify logged as **V21** in `VERIFICATION.md`. Completes the B122 ÔåÆ B121 ÔåÆ B123 building-label arc.

### B122 ÔÇö Auto-number buildings with contiguous renumber-on-delete `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/determined-brown-bf86l7`). Each standalone building now shows a sequential **"Building N"** label, numbered by placement order and contiguous (1ÔÇªN) at all times. Deliberately **render-derived, not stored**:
- **New selectors** in `lib/siteModel.js`: `isBuilding(el)` (a `type:"building"` element that is NOT an attached dog-ear/bump-out ÔÇö those carry `dogEar` + `noLabel`) and `buildingNumbers(els)` ÔåÆ `Map(id ÔåÆ N)` assigned in `els` (placement) order. The number is recomputed from list position every render, so **deleting a building renumbers the rest 1ÔÇªN in one pass** with no stored field to migrate; a lone building is "Building 1".
- **Label** (`SitePlanner.jsx` element-label render): a standalone building's first label line is now `Building ${n}` (was the bare type name "Building"); every other element label is unchanged. Dog-ears/bump-outs are excluded (already `noLabel:true`), so the parent's bump-out tally ("ÔÇª (+2 bump-outs)") is untouched.
- **Identity unchanged + safe:** the "stable internal ID, separate from the number" requirement was already met ÔÇö every element has a persisted, collision-seeded `el.id` (`uid()` + `ensureIdAbove` on load), and every cross-reference (`attachedTo` for parking/trailers/dog-ears) already binds to `el.id`, never a display number. So a renumber can't silently re-point anything. No schema change, no migration, no `SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump.
- **Tested:** `test/siteModel.test.js` adds a `buildingNumbers` case (contiguous 1ÔÇªN by placement order, dog-ears excluded, renumber-on-delete, singleÔåÆ"Building 1", null-safe). lint 0 errors ┬À **139 tests pass** ┬À build green ┬À lazy-chunk split intact. Browser-verify logged as **V13** in `VERIFICATION.md`.
> Unblocks **B123** (building-label restructure): it reuses `buildingNumbers`/`isBuilding` for label Line 1, then folds the rest of the 4-line stack into **B121**'s shared label collision/LOD engine.

### B119 ÔÇö Parking orientation flipped: first bay must sit against the building `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/youthful-heisenberg-9w64n4`). **Root cause:** `addParkingRowSide` created building-attached parking with `cfg:{ flipDepth:true }`. The strip's inner (local `y=0`) edge sits against the wall, and `flipDepth` mirrors `carStalls`'s layout so the **drive aisle** landed on that inner edge ÔÇö i.e. the aisle hugged the building and stalls were pushed outward (the inverse of the spec). **Fix (one line):** drop `flipDepth` for building-attached parking. `carStalls` now lays the first stall row at the building edge with the drive aisle outboard, and `growParking` already grows on the away-from-host edge ÔÇö so the field grows **outward, away from the building**, exactly as specced. The per-field "Drive aisle on the far side" toggle (B59) still flips it by choice; existing saved parking keeps its stored `cfg` (only newly-added side parking changes). Lint 0-errors, 152 tests pass, build green. Browser-confirm logged as **V17** in `VERIFICATION.md`.

### B120 ÔÇö Parking +/ÔêÆ stepping: bay-and-aisle increment sequence `[Site Planner]` (bug ÔåÆ was dedupe to B69)  *(arrived as "NEW-2")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/youthful-heisenberg-9w64n4`). The +/ÔêÆ depth sequence (`42 / 60 / 102 / 120 / 162 / 180 ÔÇª`; double-load an aisle before adding a new one) was already implemented + unit-verified as **B69** (`parkDepthForRows`/`parkRowsForDepth`/`growParking`) ÔÇö the math never needed changing. It only *looked* wrong because of **B119**'s flipped orientation: the numerically-correct steps grew toward/into the building. With B119 fixed (stalls hug the wall, growth is outward), the steps now visibly grow in the correct direction. No code change here beyond the B119 fix. Browser-confirm logged as **V17** in `VERIFICATION.md`.

### B128 ÔÇö Split tool: correctly cut concave parcels (general multi-piece straight cut) `[Site Planner]` (bug ÔåÆ enhancement)  *(minted B127 on-branch; renumbered B128 after `main`'s #85 concurrently took B127 for the rail/header dropdown fix ÔÇö the implementing commit still says "B127")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/inspiring-gates-qrhziq`). Closes the QA marathon's last "substantive bug still present" hand-off and supersedes the long-deferred **B31**.
- **The data-corruption fear was already mitigated, and that was verified, not assumed.** The hand-off carried an `[S2/HIGH]` "Split corrupts area on concave parcels ÔÇö saved silently, no error." That premise was **stale** (written against pre-#48 code): `performSplit` already had a concave-cut **backstop guard** (area-conservation + per-piece self-intersection check, from the B74ÔÇôB92 batch) that *refuses* a bad cut with a warning instead of saving corrupted geometry. Confirmed by replicating the live `splitPolygon` + guard and fuzzing **320k random concave cuts** (combs/L/U/stars) against a Monte-Carlo point-membership oracle: **zero** wrong splits slipped past the guard; every 4/6/8/10/12-crossing cut was rejected. So there was no silent `siteSqft`/FAR/coverage corruption to fix.
- **The real remaining gap was capability, not safety.** Because the old `splitPolygon` only paired the two extreme-`t` crossings, a straight cut that crosses a concave lot **4+ times** (which honestly yields **k+1** pieces for `2k` crossings ÔÇö e.g. 3 for a U cut across both prongs) could only ever produce 2 overlapping/omitting "halves," so the guard always **refused** it. Net user-visible behavior: "the Split tool won't cut an L/U/notched lot through its reentrant part."
- **Fix ÔÇö a correct general slice.** Extracted the parcel-split geometry out of `SitePlanner.jsx` into a new pure, tested module **`lib/polygonSplit.js`**: `splitPolygonByLine(points,A,B)` classifies each vertex to a binary side (guarantees an even crossing count ÔÇö no odd-parity tangency bug), inserts a crossing node on every sign-change edge, **pairs ALL crossings evenÔÇôodd along the cut line** (each pair is an interior chord), and traces every resulting piece (walk a boundary arc ÔåÆ hop the interior chord to its partner ÔåÆ continue), dropping zero-area slivers. Returns the natural set of pieces (`>=2`) or `null`. The bent-polyline cut moved over too as `splitPolygonByPath` (unchanged logic). `performSplit` now replaces the one parcel with **N** parcels and keeps the area/self-intersection guard as a backstop, so the result can never be worse than before (a rare near-vertex-grazing degenerate sliver still self-intersects ÔåÆ still refused).
- **UX change to note:** a straight cut through a notched lot now creates **all** the real pieces (e.g. 3 parcels for a U-shaped lot), instead of declining. Convex and 2-crossing concave cuts are byte-for-byte the same two pieces as before.
- **Validation:** new `test/polygonSplit.test.js` (11 cases: convex halving, L 2-crossing, **U ÔåÆ 3 pieces with exact areas + membership**, 3-tooth comb ÔåÆ 4 pieces, line-misses/degenerate ÔåÆ null, bent-path cut, and a 1,500-cut randomized area+membership property check). Standalone prototype passed the 300k-trial oracle (0 area / 0 membership failures; ~0.003% near-degenerate self-int ÔåÆ safely refused). Full suite **163 tests** (was 152, +11) ┬À lint **0 errors** ┬À build green ┬À lazy-chunk split intact. Owner approved shipping; merged to `main` via PR.

### B116 ÔÇö Measurement tools (Length / Polylength / Area) in Site Planner `[Site Planner]` (feature ÔåÆ already implemented)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; committed provisionally as B115 ÔÇö renumbered B116 after main's #72 took B115 for "revisit keyboard shortcuts")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/youthful-heisenberg-9w64n4`). **Dedupe outcome: already implemented ÔÇö this is the "the app already had them" case the PHASE E spec and this very task anticipated.** Site Planner's `measure` tool already ships all three calibration-aware modes; nothing was rebuilt. Evidence in `SitePlanner.jsx`:
- **All three modes present + surfaced:** the **Measure** rail button + `Ôû¥` mode menu (Length / Polylength / Area) ÔÇö `measureMode` state persisted to `localStorage` (`planarfit:measureMode`); two-click **Length**, running-path **Polylength** (accumulate points), closed-polygon **Area** (click the first dot or double-click to close). Click handling, `finishMeasure`, `selectMeasure`, delete, and the in-progress draft preview all exist and work.
- **Area label already matches the spec exactly:** renders `"<sf> sf ┬À <ac> ac ┬À <perim>ÔÇ▓ perim"` (e.g. `12,300 sf ┬À 0.28 ac ┬À 462ÔÇ▓ perim`) ÔÇö perimeter closes the loop; Length/Polylength label the running feet.
- **Calibration-aware, as specced:** labels read from `calibrationState`; when an underlay is `uncalibrated` they render **amber (`#b45309`) with a `ÔÜá`** and the rail shows the **"Underlay isn't calibrated ÔÇö distances may be wrong"** banner (same behavior as Document Review / PHASE E). A drawn/georef/calibrated plan labels in the normal accent.
- **Own editable layer; export behavior consistent:** measurements live in their own `measures[]` layer (migrated `{a,b}`ÔåÆ`{mode,pts}`), persisted in the Site Model. They export alongside callouts/markups ÔÇö consistent with the existing annotation architecture (the only `data-export="skip"` users are transient chrome: grid, basemap, scale bar, north arrow, zoom/layers UI, draft previews). Reuse note: the Document Review takeoff math (`takeoff.js`) and the Site Planner helpers (`polyArea`/`centroid`/`pathLen`) compute the same geometry ÔÇö doc-review in page-units├ùcalibration, the planner natively in feet (the shared EPSG:2278 spine) ÔÇö so no port was needed.
- **Finish UX:** double-click **or Enter** commits (shared `finishActiveDrawing`, per B103); **Esc cancels** the draft. The task floated "Esc to end" ÔÇö deliberately **not** adopted, since Esc-cancels-the-draft is the universal convention used by every other multi-point tool in the app; double-click/Enter already satisfy "click points ÔåÆ end".
- **Only change made (label alignment):** the mode display names were **Line / Polyline / Area**; renamed to **Length / Polylength / Area** ÔÇö Bluebeam's own terms (Planyr is "Bluebeam-style") and the names this task uses, which also **disambiguates the measurement from the markup "Polyline" tool** (`mpolyline`, ÔçºN) that shared the word. Label-only via a module-level `MEASURE_MODES`/`measureModeLabel`; the internal `measureMode` values (`line`/`polyline`/`area`) are untouched, so `localStorage` and all logic are unaffected. Lint 0-errors, 138 tests pass, build green. Browser click-through logged as **V12** in `VERIFICATION.md`.

### B114 ÔÇö Snapping always on; can't be suppressed during a drag `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(arrived as "NEW-1")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/epic-dirac-3ljl49`). Dragging an element (e.g. a road) near a neighbour flush-snapped it to that neighbour's edge with no per-move escape, so you couldn't drop it at a deliberate offset (a 15-ft buffer behind trailer parking). Root cause: the existing "Alt-drop to place free" only skipped the *bond* at drop (`onUp`'s `!e.altKey` guard); the element had already been moved to the flush-snapped position during `onMove`, so Alt never placed it freely ÔÇö and there was no keyboard toggle. Fix:
- **Hold-to-suppress (the standard CAD pattern):** a new `altSnapOffRef`, re-armed from `e.altKey` on every `onBgDown`/`onMove`, makes the live snap = `settings.snap && !altSnapOffRef.current`. `snap()`/`snapPt()` read it (so grid snap, draw, vertex, and placement all honour Alt), and a per-frame `snapOn` gates the inline paths the core helper doesn't cover ÔÇö the ambient neighbour flush-snap (`edgeSnapCenter`), resize/edge-resize-to-grid, and rotate-to-15┬░. Holding Alt bypasses ALL of them for that one move and re-enables on release. Shift-drag (deliberate align+bond) is unaffected.
- **Toggle + shortcut + default OFF:** the toolbar button stays, plus a new **S** shortcut and the Site-defaults checkbox toggle snap; "off" fully disables every snap path above. Per owner request snap now **defaults OFF (free movement)** and is a **global drafting preference** (a tool mode), not a per-site attribute: it lives in `localStorage` (`planarfit:snap`, default off) via `loadSnapPref`/`saveSnapPref`/`setSnap`, seeded at the settings `useState` and preserved across JSON import, so the per-site saved `snap` is ignored on load (every existing site started free, regardless of its baked-in `snap:true`). Turning it on persists across sites/reloads until turned off. (`settings.snap` is still the field every read site uses ÔÇö it just mirrors the pref now.)
- **Visible state:** the toolbar pill now reads `Snap 10ÔÇ▓` Ôåö `Snap off` (green/grey dot, `aria-pressed`), the Site-defaults checkbox is relabeled "Snap to grid & neighbours (S)", the Select-tool hint says "hold Alt to bypass snap & place freely ÔÇó toggle snap with S", and the shortcuts panel gains `S ÔåÆ Toggle snap` and `Alt drag ÔåÆ Bypass snap`. Build green; lazy-chunk split intact. **Browser-verify** logged in VERIFICATION.md.

### B103 ÔÇö Uniform polygon completion across ALL drawing tools `[Site Planner / drawing]` (bug)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (filed provisionally as B95). One shared `finishActiveDrawing()` now drives BOTH Enter and double-click (`onBgDouble`), so finish/auto-close is identical across parcel, every area element (building/paving/parking/trailer/pond/sidewalk/landscape + custom polygons), measure, split, markup poly/polyline, and trace ÔÇö Enter previously had no handler for parcel / element-polygon / mpolygon. Added `removeLastVertex()` so Backspace/Delete undoes the last placed vertex mid-draw (before the delete-selection fallback); Esc-cancel already existed. Road free-draw still finishes as an open path (no forced closing segment). Each finisher keeps its own min-point guard, so Enter/double-click no-ops instead of cancelling a too-short draft; `draftPoly`/`draftElPoly` added to the keydown effect deps so the handler reads the current draft.

### B6 ÔÇö Auto-parking returns wrong dimension after sidewalk resize + delete `[Site Planner]` (bug)
`[x]` Repro: place building ÔåÆ add 30-ft sidewalk ÔåÆ parking within sidewalk ÔåÆ expand sidewalk to 30 ft ÔåÆ delete parking ÔåÆ click "add parking" ÔåÆ tool produced a ~135-ft parking field. Expected: "add parking" drops a **fixed-depth** field every time, regardless of surrounding geometry. Fixed depth is the spec, so the bug is that **something overrides the depth constant with a measured value**. Trace where the parking field's depth is assigned; confirm it can't be contaminated by adjacent or just-deleted geometry (the resized sidewalk and the gap left by the deleted parking are prime suspects).
> Done 2026-06-14 (PR #10). `addParkingRowSide` now offsets by the sidewalk's true *thickness* (`swThick`, which resolves the correct axis) instead of a raw `w`/`h` read that could pick up the sidewalk's *run* (~wall length); the row depth stays the fixed `stallDepth + aisle` constant and can't be contaminated by adjacent or just-deleted geometry.

### B7 ÔÇö Project lifecycle status on map markers `[Site Planner / map]` (feature)
`[x]` Right-click a project marker to set its status. Lifecycle: **Pursuit ÔåÆ Active ÔåÆ On Hold ÔåÆ Complete ÔåÆ Dead** (see B8 for the full state set and visuals). Core of this item:
- Right-click marker ÔåÆ menu of states, current one checked. Mirror the same control in the project detail panel if one exists.
- **Status is a real field** on the project record in Supabase ÔÇö persists across devices, stays private per the existing row-level security (each user sees only their own).
- **New projects default to Pursuit.**
- Companion features: **filter by status** (toggle each state on/off ÔÇö "hide Complete" and "hide Dead" will be the common ones), a **legend**, and **pipeline counts** (e.g., "4 Pursuit ┬À 6 Active ┬À 2 On Hold ┬À 11 Complete ┬À 5 Dead").
> Done 2026-06-14 (this PR ÔÇö B7ÔÇôB12 batch). `status` added to the Site Model (`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 2ÔåÆ3; new sites default to "pursuit"; pre-feature records migrate to "active"), exposed via a `statusOf` selector with ordered `STATUSES` + `STATUS_META`. It persists inside the model `data` ÔÇö i.e. the `sites.data` jsonb in Supabase, so it syncs across devices and stays private under the existing RLS, with **no DB migration / no dedicated column**. Set a site's status by right-clicking its map marker **or** a saved-sites list row (current state checked); applied to the whole site group. Companion legend + per-state show/hide filters + live pipeline counts live in the saved-sites panel.

### B8 ÔÇö Lifecycle states & marker visuals `[Site Planner / map]` (feature)
`[x]` The five states and how each marker reads. Use two redundant cues ÔÇö **color + shape/glyph** ÔÇö so status stays legible for colorblind users and on a busy basemap:
- **Pursuit** ÔÇö amber, dashed ring (tentative, uncommitted).
- **Active** ÔÇö green, solid (the live work; should grab the eye).
- **On Hold** ÔÇö slate/indigo blue, solid ring, pause "ÔÇû" glyph (alive but paused; revivable). Lean slate/indigo rather than sky-blue so it doesn't vanish over blue water on the basemap.
- **Complete** ÔÇö gray, muted, small check Ô£ô (done; recedes).
- **Dead** ÔÇö hollow gray, dimmest, faint Ô£ò, no fill (didn't proceed; kept for the record). **Not red** ÔÇö red reads as urgent/alert; a dead deal is inert.
- Live (green) should pop; both Complete and Dead recede into the background, with shape/glyph telling them apart.
> Done 2026-06-14 (this PR ÔÇö B7ÔÇôB12 batch). Markers carry two redundant cues exactly per spec: Pursuit amber/dashed-ring, Active green/solid (pops), On Hold slate-indigo/pause-ÔÇû, Complete gray/muted-check-Ô£ô (recedes), Dead hollow-gray/faint-Ô£ò/no-fill (recedes; not red). Glyph distinguishes Complete from Dead.
