> - **Genuinely OPEN net-new work:** (1) make **`splitParkingRows` preserve double-loaded modules** (split at module boundaries / keep two rows + one aisle together, not one row + a full aisle each); (2) **free-field auto-orientation** ÔÇö rows parallel to the building face, else the polygon's **longest edge** (today a free rectangle just uses its own local axes; building-attached "first bay against the wall" is already **B119**); (3) the **fuller curb rule** ÔÇö `curbEdgesOf` (~line 499) today only curbs the **terminal depth-ends + a sidewalk transition**, with no full-perimeter / skip-vehicle-opening / dead-end-terminus logic ÔÇö extend it to NEW-1's meets-non-paving / never-internal-seam / no-curb-across-openings / curb-a-dead-end contract; (4) the **button rename**.
> - **Already DONE ÔÇö reconcile against, do not re-do:** **B69** (one-row stepping `parkDepthForRows`/`parkRowsForDepth`/`growParking`), **B70** (derived 6ÔÇ│ curbs: drawn + counted in `curbAreaOf` + excluded from the displayed dimension, never building-side), **B71** (per-element 6ÔÇ│ÔåÆ12ÔÇ│ trailer curb `curbW`), **B119** (building-attached first-bay-against-wall orientation), **B120** (stepping verify), **B59/B29** (per-element `cfg` plumbing), **B6** (sidewalk-resize depth contamination), **B78** (degenerate-config freeze guard). Keep B70's calc/draw/dimension three-way contract and the 6ÔÇ│ default ÔÇö NEW-1 only tightens *where* the curb lands. Polygon/irregular pads are currently out of scope for the auto-curb (B70 deferred nuance); confirm whether NEW-1's perimeter rule should now cover them.
> **Progress 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/blissful-feynman-0ijfr2`): the core split bug + the rename are SHIPPED; the two enhancements stay open.** Fixed the actual repro ÔÇö `splitParkingRows` now splits a field into **double-loaded module elements** (two stall rows sharing one drive aisle, 60ÔÇ▓ at 90┬░) plus **at most one trailing single-loaded row** for an odd remainder, instead of one single-loaded `[row + aisle]` per row. **Total depth and stall count are preserved exactly** (the piece depths sum back to the field's `h`), so yield doesn't change ÔÇö only the split geometry. Extracted the pure math to **`lib/parking.js`** (`parkDepthForRows` / `parkRowsForDepth` / new `splitParkingPieces`) with **`test/parking.test.js`** (10 cases: module split, odd-remainder single row, total-depth preservation, sub-row fold-in, Ôëñ1-module no-op, degenerate-config guard). Renamed the button **"Split into rows" ÔåÆ "Split rows/aisles"** in both places (the selected-element panel + the right-click menu) and tightened its gate to `ÔëÑ3` rows (it now appears only when a split yields ÔëÑ2 pieces). The **un-split** field already double-loaded via `carStalls` (B69), so it's untouched. lint **0 errors** ┬À **185 tests** (10 new) ┬À build green ┬À `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Shipped to `main` (owner said push to main). **Browser pass DONE** ÔÇö self-verified live 2026-06-17 in a headless browser (rename + double-loaded split confirmed on planyr.io / branch preview), logged as **VERIFICATION V24**. **Still OPEN (deliberately deferred this pass):** (2) **free-field longest-edge auto-orientation** ÔÇö interacts with B119's building-attached orientation + the depth-stepping, wants its own careful pass; (3) the **fuller curb rule** (full perimeter / skip internal seams + vehicle openings / curb a dead-end terminus) ÔÇö extends `curbEdgesOf`, which feeds yield math, and has the open design Q above (polygon pads + opening detection), so not safe to guess into production yet.
> **Progress 2026-06-17 (follow-ons SHIPPED ÔÇö B130 now fully DONE):** items (2) + (3) landed via **PR #100**. **(2) free-field orientation** ÔÇö a parking box drawn deeper than it is long swaps `w`/`h` and rotates 90┬░ at draw-commit, so its rows run along the long edge (identical footprint; preset-depth + building-attached draws untouched). **(3) fuller curb rule** ÔÇö `curbEdgesOf` rewritten to curb the whole perimeter where pavement meets non-paving and skip it where pavement meets pavement (drive openings, continuous paving, the seam between split modules), via the new pure, unit-tested `edgeAbutsPaving` (sample-just-outside adjacency). **Building-side curb DECISION (resolved): NO curb against the bare building face** ÔÇö owner-confirmed 2026-06-17 ("no curbs against buildings, that doesn't make sense"); **B70 stands and B130's original "building face" wording is overridden.** A sidewalk between still gets a transition curb. lint 0 ┬À **191 tests** (+6 adjacency) ┬À build green. **Self-verified live** (headless): a tall field orients its rows along the long edge; an isolated pad shows a full-perimeter curb; a split field stays continuous with no seam curbs ÔÇö logged as **VERIFICATION V25**. The only remaining piece ÔÇö clipping the field to the parcel boundary ÔÇö was always out of scope here and is now filed as its own item **B131**.

### B131 ÔÇö Print-dialog toggle to include the site-plan overlay in the printed/exported output `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1"; filed provisionally as B130, renumbered B131 after `main`'s #95 concurrently took B130 for the parking "Split rows/aisles" fix ÔÇö the implementing commit + branch still say B130)*
`[x]` Add a checkbox to the print-frame dialog (the Letter/Tabloid + Landscape/Portrait toolbar) that controls whether the placed **site-plan overlay** (B72 ÔÇö the drag-dropped PDF/image backdrop) is included in the print / PDF / PNG output. **Confirm-before-build resolved:** both the print dialog and the overlay live in **Site Planner** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) ÔÇö the print-frame toolbar's `printPaper`/`printOrient` and the `sheetOverlays` layer ÔÇö **not** Document Review, so it was built here (no retag). All design points met: label **"Print overlay"** (a print-action verb, not the screen-toggle "Show"); the control is **only shown when an overlay is actually loaded** (no dead control); it **defaults to the overlay's on-screen visibility** (checked when a rendered overlay is on screen); and it acts as a **master include/exclude** across multiple overlays, forward-compatible with a future per-overlay hide (`visible !== false`).
> Ô£à **Done 2026-06-17** (branch `claude/adoring-pascal-clrp0k`). **WYSIWYG by construction:** the export path **clones the live SVG**, so an included overlay keeps its exact transform ÔÇö real-world feetÔåÆpixel position (EPSG:2278-anchored origin), scale, rotation, opacity, and page selection (the chosen page is already the rasterized `src`) ÔÇö composited **above the aerial backdrop** in the same z-order as on screen. New pure helper `lib/overlayPrint.js` (`isOverlayPrintable` / `printableOverlays` / `hasPrintableOverlay`) is the single source of truth for the checkbox's visibility + default and the compositing filter; `buildExportSvg(frame, includeOverlay)` tags each overlay raster `data-overlay-image` (kept or dropped per the toggle) and the on-canvas selection chrome + unsynced placeholder `data-export="skip"` (so neither ever prints). Also fixed a **latent inconsistency**: overlays used to **silently vanish from output whenever an aerial underlay was present** (the underlay pass stripped *all* `<image>`s) ÔÇö now overlays are honored in both **Print** and **PNG export**. lint 0 errors ┬À **181 tests** (6 new in `test/overlayPrint.test.js`) ┬À build green; lazy chunk split intact. Browser pass queued as **VERIFICATION V24**. **Shipped to `main` via PR #96** (owner said "ship").

### B108 ÔÇö UI pass: element colours, tool naming, ParcelÔåÆBoundary, Pan remap, one save badge `[Site Planyr / UI]` (task)  *(branch "B98")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`). Five priority UI fixes from the audit:
- **Element colour differentiation (H2)** ÔÇö paving / car parking / trailer / sidewalk / road read as one grey; now distinct hue+lightness + colour-blind-safe textures (sidewalk dot grid, trailer diagonal), parking striping / road centreline carry their own cue. `planStyle` gains a generic `pattern` field; `renderElPx` texFill resolves it. Commit 512fe6e.
- **Tool naming (D10/D11)** ÔÇö right-rail draw tools match the canvas/legend canonical names: ParkingÔåÆ**Car Parking**, TrailerÔåÆ**Trailer Parking**, PondÔåÆ**Detention Pond**. Commit 1138a3f.
- **"Parcel" duplication (C1/D5)** ÔÇö the word was on both rails; right draw/split group renamed **Boundary** (menu still "Draw new parcel / Split a parcel"), left **Parcel** inspector kept (model: left = views, right = tools). Commit 1138a3f.
- **Pan remap (D4)** ÔÇö off `Shift+V` onto **H** + hold-**Space** temporary hand-pan; V always selects. Commit 1138a3f.
- **One save/sync badge (E2 / cross-ref B10)** ÔÇö folded the separate floating "Cloud Ô£ô/off/err" diagnostic into the planner header badge: Syncing / Synced Ô£ô / Saved Ô£ô (device) / Offline / Unsaved (b74894f). **Merge note:** `main` shipped its own header badge in parallel; the merge collapsed cleanly to the richer cloud-aware pill (no duplicate) ÔÇö sanity-check it against main's `onSiteSaved` callback flow on the live preview.
> Verified `Ô£ô already done` (no change needed): undo/redo/zoom-fit tooltips+disabled (audit B6ÔÇôB8), planner scale bar / north arrow / zoom-to-fit (G1ÔÇôG3), switcher dropdown z-index (B66).

### B109 ÔÇö Standardize the right-rail element-row anatomy `[Site Planyr / UI]` (task)  *(branch "B93")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16. The preset-less site-element rows (Paving / Trailer Parking / Detention Pond) now use the same two-line anatomy as Building / Road / Car Parking ÔÇö icon + label + a one-word sub-label ("drive / court", "back-in storage", "detention basin"); they just carry no `Ôû¥` preset menu. All six SITE ELEMENTS rows now read as one uniform column. (Audit D13.)

### B110 ÔÇö Road width preset wording `[Site Planyr / UI]` (task)  *(branch "B94")*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16. The Road width `Ôû¥` menu now reads "NÔÇ▓ travel ÔÇö drag the length" (was "NÔÇ▓ wide"), matching the tool's sub-label and the B70 three-way curb contract (the width is the travel width; the 6ÔÇ│ curb is added on top). **Cross-check `main`'s B102** (Setup Roads "ft" units + "Travel widths"ÔåÆ"Road widths" rename) ÔÇö both shipped; the wording agrees (travel width is the contract). (Audit D-Road.)

### B111 ÔÇö No sign-in affordance when Supabase is unconfigured `[global UI / auth]` (bug)  *(branch "B95")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-16.** The shell account control only renders when `supabaseConfigured()` is true, so a build without cloud env shows **no** sign-in / account entry point at all (the door is simply missing, not disabled). Show a "Sign in" affordance (or an explicit "cloud off" hint) even when unconfigured. (Audit A3.)
> Fix (`src/app/Shell.jsx`): when unconfigured, render a muted **"Ôèÿ Cloud off"** pill in the account corner (never empty) that, on click, opens a small explainer popover ÔÇö "Your work is saved on this device only; signing in / cross-device sync need the cloud connection set up." The configured path (Sign in / account button) is unchanged. Verified headless: `ui-audit/screens/planner-mobile.png` (and all planner shots) now show "Cloud off" top-right, since the CI-less local build is unconfigured. lint 0 ┬À 138 tests ┬À build green.

### B112 ÔÇö Minor polish: File-menu tooltips + Document Review save-badge dot `[Site Planyr / Document Review / UI]` (task)  *(branch "B96", trimmed)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16. (a) **DONE** ÔÇö File `Ôû¥` items got explanatory tooltips (dividers already existed; leading icons skipped ÔÇö they'd clash with the drafting palette). (d) **DONE** ÔÇö Document Review's save badge now leads with a colored status dot (Saving/Saved/Unsaved/Not-saved), matching the planner's unified pill. **Dropped on merge:** the map scale-bar / "Fit all" / instruction-copy parts (b, c) ÔÇö `main`'s B104ÔÇôB106 redesigned that map furniture (collapsible Sites panel, dismissible hint), so my versions were superseded and removed during the merge. (Audit B11 / J2 / J4 / K2.)

> Dropped on merge ÔÇö the branch's **"B97" legend H2-cue:** `main`'s **B101** deliberately removed the element-type colour legend from the Setup page (declutter), so re-adding a legend there was reverted in the merge. The H2 colour-blind textures still render on the canvas elements themselves; only the redundant Setup key is gone.

### B113 ÔÇö Planner is not responsive at mobile width `[Site Planyr / UI]` (feature)  *(branch "B99")*
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-16.** At ~390px the fixed 168px right rail + the left rail/panel consume most of the width and the canvas collapses to a sliver; the header wraps. The planner is effectively desktop-only. Needs a mobile treatment (collapsible/off-canvas rails, a bottom toolbar). Larger effort ÔÇö roadmap, not a quick fix. (Audit L1; see `ui-audit/screens/planner-mobile.png`.)
> Fix (`SitePlanner.jsx`): a `matchMedia("(max-width: 760px)")` `narrow` flag drives a phone treatment ÔÇö **every mobile style is `narrow ?`-gated, so the desktop layout is byte-identical**. When narrow: (1) the right 168px tool palette no longer eats width ÔÇö it **slides in from the right as an overlay** (with a tap-to-dismiss backdrop) summoned by a floating **"Ô£Ä Tools"** button, and auto-closes when you pick a tool so you can draw; (2) the left menu panel (Yield/Parcel/ÔÇª) **overlays the canvas** (`position:absolute`, capped to `min(320px, 100vw-74px)`) instead of pushing it, its drag-resize handle hidden; (3) the thin 54px left rail stays inline; (4) the header scrolls horizontally instead of wrapping (condensed gap/padding). Net: the canvas keeps full usable width on a phone. Verified headless at 390├ù844: `planner-mobile.png` (collapsed + FAB), `planner-mobile-tools.png` (tool drawer), `planner-mobile-panel.png` (yield drawer); desktop `planner-plan.png` unchanged. Live touch pass logged in `VERIFICATION.md` V10. lint 0 ┬À 138 tests ┬À build green.

---

### B15 ÔÇö Import JSON drops callouts & markups, and leaves stale ones on the canvas `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. `importJSONFile` (SitePlanner.jsx ~2459) set parcels/els/measures/settings/underlay but **not** `callouts`/`markups`, although `exportJSON` writes them. Result: (a) imported callouts/markups are discarded; (b) the currently-open plan's callouts/markups aren't reset, so they bleed into the imported plan and get persisted on the next autosave. Fix: `setCallouts(d.callouts || [])` + `setMarkups(d.markups || [])` in import (symmetric with export).

### B16 ÔÇö `isBlankSite` ignores `markups` ÔåÆ markup/encumbrance-only sites are silently deleted on leave `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. `isBlankSite` (SitePlanner.jsx ~795) omits `markups`, so a site whose only work is drawn markups or a plotted metes-and-bounds **encumbrance** is treated as blank: the autosave guard skips it and `persistOrDrop` *deletes* the un-located record on unmount. Fix: include `markups` in `isBlankSite` and in the autosave guard object.

### B17 ÔÇö Doc Review local mirror saved without `updatedAt`; `reconcile` compares mismatched fields `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS risk, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. `buildSnapshot` (DocReview.jsx/Stitcher.jsx) omits `updatedAt`, so `flushLocal()ÔåÆwriteDraft` mirrors a record whose `data.updatedAt` is stale/absent; `reconcile` then compares cloud `updatedAt` vs draft `_localAt` (different events/clocks) and can let a stale local draft shadow a newer cloud copy. Fix: stamp `updatedAt: Date.now()` in `buildSnapshot` so the mirror and the cloud `data` carry a consistent timestamp.

### B18 ÔÇö Last-debounce-window edits flush local-only and are lost when `pullCloud` blind-replaces on next login `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS, high
`[x]` The beforeunload/visibility/unmount/site-switch flushes call `saveSite` (local cache) but **never** `pushSiteToCloud`; `pullCloud` then *replaces* the per-user cache wholesale on next login, discarding any edit made in the final ~400 ms before close that the debounced cloud push missed. Fix options: make `pullCloud` merge by `updatedAt` (keep the newer side), and/or push on `pagehide` via `fetch(..., {keepalive:true})`/`sendBeacon`. (Bigger change ÔÇö left for review.)

### B19 ÔÇö Resume-on-mount re-saves the just-loaded review with a fresh `updatedAt` `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/b19-resume-resave`): `useReviewPersistence` exposes `suspendSave(ms)` which parks a suspend window; the autosave effect early-returns while it's active. `loadSingleReview` arms it once (single commit); `loadStitch` arms it at start and re-arms before its async `setPdfs`/`setPlaced` commits, so a programmatic load never re-stamps just-loaded data with a fresh `updatedAt`. `loadSingleReview`/`loadStitch` set state ÔåÆ the debounce deps change ÔåÆ autosave writes the loaded snapshot back with `updatedAt: now`, so a resume can stamp stale data as newest and (multi-tab) clobber a newer cloud edit. Fix: a `suspendSave` ref set during programmatic load, early-returning from the autosave effect for that tick.

### B21 ÔÇö Down overlay re-probes & repaints the error banner every 45 s forever; background errors share the click-instruction slot `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. `PROBE_TTL` (40 s) < the 45 s re-probe interval, so a genuinely-down enabled layer re-probes and re-fires `onError` every tick, repainting the bottom-left banner indefinitely; that banner is also the only `err` slot, so a background layer hiccup masks the "click a lot" guidance until a user action clears it. Fix: de-duplicate identical error toasts and auto-clear background layer errors after a timeout.

### B22 ÔÇö Rapid parcel clicks race ÔåÆ duplicate selection + leaked highlight polygon `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`) ÔÇö defensive: `handleClick` now removes any pre-existing hilite for a key before overwriting (no orphaned `L.polygon`) and dedupes the `selected` entry by key (no double-counted acreage). NOTE: in the current code the toggle check runs AFTER the `await`, so two clicks resolve sequentially into a correct toggle and the described double-add doesn't actually reproduce ÔÇö these guards close the theoretical gap without an in-flight ref that would drop a legitimate second-lot click. `handleClick` is async with no sequence guard; two fast clicks on the same lot can both take the "add" branch (the toggle check runs before the first `await` resolves), producing a duplicate `selected` entry (double-counted acreage) and an orphaned `L.polygon` left on the map. Fix: guard with an in-flight ref / request id, and remove any pre-existing hilite for a key before overwriting.

### B23 ÔÇö `ditchStats` returns `NaN` profile distance for a single elevation sample `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. elevation.js only guards `length === 0`; with exactly one sample, `i/(n-1) = 0/0 = NaN` flows into the cross-section profile/labels. Fix: treat `< 2` samples as insufficient (`return null`), letting the caller's "no samples" path handle it.

### B24 ÔÇö `measureLabel` prints `"NaN"` / can crash on degenerate measurement points `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. takeoff.js `measureLabel`/`measureValue` have no finite/length guard; an empty `distance` markup dereferences `m.pts[0].x` (throws) and short polys can render `"NaN ft/ac"`. Fix: guard missing/short `pts` and non-finite values.

### B25 ÔÇö Metes-and-bounds: curve calls silently parsed as straight chords; no unreadable-call flagging `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` `CALL_RE` has no `radius`/`arc`/`delta`/`chord` handling; given a curve call it matches the chord bearing+distance and dead-reckons it as a straight segment, with no `dropped`/`unparsed` signal ÔÇö so the traverse is silently wrong and the UI can't warn. (Roadmap already wants "parse curves; flag unreadable.") Fix: detect curve calls and either tessellate the arc or return `{unparsed:true, raw}` so callers can flag them.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from the round-2 correctness PR ÔÇö flagging/tessellating curve calls needs parser + UI work validated against real deed text (risk of breaking the working straight-call parser for Harris/FBCAD); left Open for a focused pass with sample deeds.

### B26 ÔÇö Metes-and-bounds: parser gaps (dash-DMS unparsed, deg>90 accepted, loose closure floor) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): (a) `-` added to the degree/minute separator classes so `S 12-15 W` parses; (b) `parseCalls` skips any call with `deg > 90`; (c) the `pathCloses` absolute floor dropped 25 ft ÔåÆ 5 ft (the 2% relative term kept), so a small lot can't falsely "close" on 25 ft of misclosure. (a) The header advertises `S 12-15 W` but the DMS separator classes exclude `-`, so dash-separated bearings return `[]` (silently dropped). (b) The degrees group `[0-9]{1,3}` accepts a quadrant bearing > 90┬░ (e.g. `N 145 E`) and plots it. (c) `pathCloses` floors tolerance at `max(25, 2%┬Àperim)` so a small lot can be declared "closed" with a 25-ft misclosure, hiding parse errors. Fix: add `-` to the separator classes; reject/flag `deg>90`; drop/shrink the absolute closure floor and always surface the misclosure.

### B27 ÔÇö Enter-to-commit power-line trace uses stale `tracePts`/`traceMode` `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed in audit PR. The keydown effect closes over `traceMode`/`tracePts` but they're not in its dep array, so pressing Enter to finish a trace reads stale values (often `[]`). Fix: add `traceMode`/`tracePts` to the effect deps (double-click already worked because it's a fresh prop).

### B28 ÔÇö `mergeRings` collinear-cleanup threshold is area-based (scale-dependent) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the corner test now thresholds the perpendicular deviation `|cross|/hypot(cÔêÆa)` (> 0.1 ft), so it's scale-independent. The corner test `Math.abs(cross) > 1` compares twice-triangle-area in ft┬▓ (scales with edge length), so on long edges a real slight bend can be dropped (or noise kept), distorting a merged parcel boundary/acreage. Fix: threshold the *perpendicular distance* `|cross|/hypot(cÔêÆa)` against a small foot tolerance.

### B29 ÔÇö Parking +/ÔêÆ shrink guard ignores per-field `cfg` overrides `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `parkBand(el)` and `canShrink` now derive the band from `cfgOf(el)`, matching `growParking`. `parkBand()`/`canShrink` use global `settings.stallDepth/aisle`, but `growParking` uses `cfgOf(el)`; for a field with per-element cfg the on-canvas ÔêÆ handle is enabled/disabled against the wrong row size (guard disagrees with the actual op). Fix: compute the band from `cfgOf(el)` in the guard too.

### B30 ÔÇö Markup/measure minimum size is in feet, not pixels ÔåÆ silently discarded when zoomed out `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the minimums are now `3 / view.ppf` ft (a ~3px drag), so a deliberate drag isn't silently dropped at any zoom. The `dist(a,b) >= 2` / `w,h >= 2` minimums are world-feet; at low ppf a deliberate multi-pixel drag can be < 2 ft and the markup/measure is dropped with no feedback. Fix: make the minimum pixel-based (scale by view ppf).

### B31 ÔÇö Two-point Split returns null when both crossings share an edge `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, low
`[x]` `if (lo.i === hi.i) return null` uses only the extreme-`t` hits, so a valid straight cut across a concave parcel (whose first/last crossings land on the same edge while valid interior crossings exist) silently no-ops. Fix: when `lo.i === hi.i`, pick the two crossings with distinct edge indices that yield two ÔëÑ3-vertex rings.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from round-2 ÔÇö the same-edge multi-crossing case is fiddly concave-polygon geometry (minor/low) that's hard to validate without runtime cases; left Open to avoid shipping a bad split.
> **Superseded 2026-06-17 by B128 (Done):** the whole concave multi-crossing case (not just the same-edge sub-case) is now handled by a correct general slice in `lib/polygonSplit.js` ÔÇö see B128 for the validated fix.

### B32 ÔÇö Undo stack polluted by `pushHistory` on no-op edits `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, high
`[x]` `beginEditCallout` and the dock/type `onChange` handlers call `pushHistory()` before/without an actual change, so cancelling a callout edit or reselecting the same dropdown value adds an undo frame (first Ctrl+Z appears to do nothing). Fix: push history only when a mutation is actually applied.
> Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/correctness-round2`): the **callout** half is fixed ÔÇö `beginEditCallout` no longer pushes history on open, and `commitEditCallout` pushes only when the text actually changes (cancel or commit-unchanged adds no frame). Re-checked the **dock/type dropdown** half ÔÇö it's a NON-ISSUE: a native `<select>` `onChange` fires only on an actual value change (re-selecting the already-selected option doesn't fire it), so it never adds a spurious frame. The callout half was the real bug; B32 is resolved.

### B33 ÔÇö Doc Review redline shapes (rect/cloud/text) are unselectable except at corner points `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/followup-minors`): `hitTest` is now shape-aware ÔÇö rect/cloud select across their whole body (point-in-box ┬▒ tolerance), text selects across its rendered label box, and measures select on nearest vertex OR segment (so a line body selects, not just its endpoints). `hitTest` measures distance only to stored vertices; rect/cloud store two corners and text one point, so clicking an edge or body never selects them (hard to select/delete redlines). Fix: shape-aware hit testing (point-in-rect, distance-to-segment, text bbox).

### B34 ÔÇö Doc Review single-viewer `render()` calls `setScale` internally ÔåÆ re-entrant render race `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-tail-grind`): the fit-to-width scale is computed in its OWN effect (when `scale===0`); `render()` returns early until a concrete scale exists and no longer calls `setScale`, so it's a pure draw and the double-render / `dims` mismatch is gone. In fit-to-width mode `render` calls `setScale(s)` then keeps drawing; the `scale` change re-fires the render effect, so two renders run and `dims`/canvas can briefly mismatch the overlay. Fix: compute fit scale in a separate effect; keep `render` a pure draw at a concrete scale.

### B35 ÔÇö `listProjects` status display is fragile (newest-row-wins; fallback drops status) `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/followup-minors`): `listProjects` returns `status:"unknown"` (not `"active"`) when the value is missing or the status read fell back to the no-status query, and `ProjectLibrary` renders a neutral "Status?" placeholder for an unknown status (still settable) ÔÇö so a failed read no longer silently reports every project as "active." (Group-consistency is unchanged: status is group-wide per B7, so newest-row stays a fair representative.) Status is read from whichever group row sorts first by `updated_at`, and if the `status:data->>status` select errors the fallback query omits status so every project shows the default "active" ÔÇö silently misreporting lifecycle. Fix: resolve status group-consistently and surface "unknown" rather than defaulting to active on a failed status read.

### B37 ÔÇö Cloud/auth hardening `[Site Planner / auth]` (bug) ÔÇö low/minor
`[x]` ­ƒöº partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** `cloudDelete` now scopes by `user_id` AND `id` (was RLS-only). (b) **fixed:** the autosave cloud push had no `.catch`, leaving the save badge stuck on "SavingÔÇª" on a rejected upsert. (c) **fixed:** `metaRef` omitted `county`, so a save whose merge-base lacked county could normalize it to null ÔÇö now self-contained. (d) **fixed:** `testConnection` now accepts any https origin (custom Supabase domains) ÔÇö PR #27; the `pullCloud`-failure-empty-library half was fixed separately as B54 (#21).

### B39 ÔÇö PDF.js documents are never `destroy()`'d ÔåÆ growing memory leak across a review session `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö leak, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): the prior doc is destroyed before swap (DocReview `setPdfDoc` helper) and the Stitcher destroys docs as they leave state via a diff effect, plus an unmount cleanup in both. `loadPdf` returns a `PDFDocumentProxy` (worker + retained ArrayBuffer); every replacement just overwrites `pdfRef.current` / `pdfs[].doc` (openFile, load, resetSingle, resetStitch, loadStitch) with no `.destroy()`, and there's no unmount cleanup. A repo-wide grep for `.destroy()` in doc-review is empty. Heavy construction sets leak MBs per open; the stitcher holds N docs at once. Fix: destroy the prior doc before replacing, destroy each removed/replaced source in the stitcher, and add an unmount effect that destroys the live doc(s). (Deferred from the audit PR only to avoid editing files a parallel agent was reading ÔÇö will fix in the consolidated pass.)

### B40 ÔÇö Superseded PDF render tasks are never cancelled `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö perf, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): `renderPageToCanvas` now exposes its `RenderTask` (an `onTask` callback); `render()` keeps it in a ref, `cancel()`s the prior one before each render and on unmount, and swallows `RenderingCancelledException`. `renderPageToCanvas` creates `page.render(...)` and only awaits its promise; `render()` discards the stale *result* via the `renderTok` token but never calls `task.cancel()`. Rapid page/zoom changes pile up overlapping renders and can hit PDF.js's "cannot use the same canvas during multiple render operations" (throw/torn output). Fix: return the `RenderTask`, keep a ref, `cancel()` before starting a new one and on unmount; treat `RenderingCancelledException` as a no-op.
> **Amendment ÔÇö residual same-canvas race, fixed 2026-06-21 (branch `claude/modest-turing-wixo1y`).** (Folded in from a coworker-chat note, not a new item.) The B40 fix had a hole: `renderTaskRef.current` is only populated AFTER `await pdf.getPage()` inside `renderPageToCanvas`, so during render #1's getPage await, render #2 starts, finds `renderTaskRef.current` still null (nothing to cancel), and both proceed to `page.render()` on the same canvas ÔÇö the exact "Cannot use the same canvas during multiple render operations" error B40 targeted, swallowed, leaving the canvas on a superseded sheet. **Fix:** `renderPageToCanvas` now takes an `isStale` predicate and checks it right BEFORE `page.render()`; `DocReview.jsx` passes `() => tok !== renderTok.current`, so a render superseded mid-getPage bails (returns null) before it touches the canvas ÔÇö only the newest render draws. Verified: lint 0 ┬À 802 tests ┬À build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-b322-b40.mjs` shows 0 same-canvas errors across rapid switches (V85). Shipping via PR #240 ÔåÆ `main` (with the B326ÔÇôB328 batch).

### B41 ÔÇö Global keydown effects have no deps array ÔåÆ re-subscribe on every render (hot path) `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö perf, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): both now bind the `keydown` listener ONCE (`[]` deps) and dispatch through an `onKeyRef` refreshed each render, so the handler keeps live closures without re-subscribing on every render. DocReview.jsx and Stitcher.jsx register their `keydown` listener in an effect with no dependency array (the `}); // eslint-disable-line` pattern), so the handler is removed+re-added after *every* render ÔÇö and `onPointerMove`ÔåÆ`setCursor` re-renders dozens of times/second while drawing. Fix: register once with `[]` and read `draft`/`sel`/handlers from refs inside `onKey`.

### B42 ÔÇö `SitePlannerApp` rebuilds `siteGroups` (a Map over all sites) every render `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö perf, medium
`[x]` Ô£à already addressed (verified 2026-06-15): `siteGroups` is already `useMemo`'d on `[sites, activeSiteId]` in `SitePlannerApp.jsx` (with a "stable identity" comment) ÔÇö the IIFE the audit flagged had since been converted, so no code change was needed. `const siteGroups = (() => { ÔÇª })()` runs unconditionally each render, allocating a fresh array passed as the `sites` prop to `MapFinder` ÔÇö O(n) per render and a referential-stability hazard that defeats child memoization. Fix: `useMemo(ÔÇª, [sites, activeSiteId])`. (Safe, small ÔÇö will fix in the consolidated pass.)

### B43 ÔÇö `applyUser` auth-event handling has no sequence guard ÔåÆ races on fast auth transitions `[Site Planner / auth]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` `applyUser` is async (`setActiveUser` ÔåÆ `await pullCloud` ÔåÆ `refreshSites`); overlapping auth events (INITIAL_SESSIONÔåÆSIGNED_IN, or fast sign-out/in) can interleave so `prevUid.current`/`clearCloudCache` run out of order and clear/point at the wrong user's cache. Fix: capture a monotonic token before the await and bail stale completions (the file already uses a `live` guard elsewhere).

### B44 ÔÇö Doc Review async refresh/flush lack in-flight guards `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/b19-resume-resave`, alongside B19): (a) the flush now gates its cloud upsert on a `dirtyRef` (set on a real edit, cleared on a successful write), so a singleÔåöstitch toggle with no change no longer re-upserts; (b) `ReviewsBar.refresh` is token-guarded (latest wins) and its outside-click listener moved to its own effect; (c) `ProjectLibrary.refresh` is token-guarded too, so an overlapping/late refresh (incl. `fileDrop`'s) can't clobber newer state. (a) `useReviewPersistence`'s unmount cleanup unconditionally fires a cloud `upsert` on every singleÔåöstitch mode toggle (wasteful double-write even when nothing changed) ÔÇö gate on a dirty flag. (b) `ReviewsBar` re-runs `listReviews()+listProjects()` on every `signedIn` flip while open and has no in-flight guard, so a slow resolution can clobber newer state; (c) `ProjectLibrary.fileDrop` calls `refresh()` in `finally` even if the drawer closed, and overlapping refreshes can interleave. Fix: split outside-click vs fetch effects and add an `ignore`/AbortController guard; `useCallback` the refreshers.

### B45 ÔÇö Stitcher keeps large PNG data URLs for every placed sheet in state `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö perf, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `chore/audit-safe-fixes-b39-b45`): `renderPageToImage` now returns a `URL.createObjectURL` blob URL (data-URL fallback), and the Stitcher revokes URLs as sheets leave state via a diff effect + unmount cleanup. `renderPageToImage` returns `canvas.toDataURL("image/png")` at scale 2 and each placed sheet stores that multi-MB base64 string in `placed[].href`; reloads/re-binds regenerate them without releasing the old ones (data URLs can't be revoked). Fix: use `canvas.toBlob` + `URL.createObjectURL`, track and `revokeObjectURL` on remove/replace/unmount.

### B46 ÔÇö `LayerPanel` Mapillary token desyncs across its two instances `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` The token is seeded once via `useState(() => mapillaryToken())` in each `LayerPanel` (map + planner both render one); typing in one doesn't update the other's local `tok`, and an externally-set token isn't reflected on reopen. Fix: lift the token to shared state (alongside `overlays`) or read from a subscribable source.
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from the map/GIS robustness PR ÔÇö minor. Clean fix = either lift the token to shared app state (thread alongside `overlays`) or add a tiny pub/sub in `evidenceLayers.js` (`setMapillaryToken` notifies listeners; each `LayerPanel` subscribes via an effect and updates its `tok`). Left Open for that small refactor.

### B47 ÔÇö ArcGIS `where`-clause hardening (LIKE wildcards + auto-detected field names) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö security, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`) ÔÇö **part (b)**: the parcel-lookup `where` builder now validates `idField`/`addrField` against `/^[A-Za-z0-9_.]+$/` before interpolating, so a hostile/compromised (or user-pasted) layer's metadata can't inject a field name that escapes the `scopeWhere` county confinement. **Part (a)** ÔÇö escaping `%`/`_` LIKE wildcards via an `ESCAPE` clause ÔÇö is **deferred** (must verify Harris/FBCAD accept `ESCAPE` before shipping; low risk ÔÇö read-only public data). A security pass found the rest of the app **clean** (Supabase queries are parameterized with no injection surface; RLS/storage policies sound; secrets handled correctly; all text/SVG render paths are React-escaped). The one item: the parcel-lookup `where` builder escapes the user *value*'s quotes (`'`ÔåÆ`''`, blocks the realistic breakout) but (a) does not escape LIKE wildcards `%`/`_` (a value of `%` enumerates rows ÔÇö minor, public read-only data; the statewide TxGIO/Chambers layer is still confined by `scopeWhere`), and (b) interpolates `idField`/`addrField` **unescaped** ÔÇö and those come from live layer metadata (or a user-pasted "Service / layer URL"), so a hostile/compromised endpoint could inject a field name that defeats the `scopeWhere` county confinement. Read-only/public so not critical. Fix: validate the detected field name against an identifier allowlist (`/^[A-Za-z0-9_.]+$/` and/or `meta.fields.some(f=>f.name===idField)`) before interpolating, and escape `%`/`_` with an `ESCAPE` clause (verify the CAD datasources accept `ESCAPE` before shipping ÔÇö don't break Harris/FBCAD search).

### B48 ÔÇö Client-side Anthropic key (title reader) + reusable HTML-escape helper `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö note, low
`[x]` `lib/titleReader.js` instantiates the Anthropic SDK in the browser with `dangerouslyAllowBrowser:true` and the user's own key from `localStorage` (`planarfit:anthropicKey`). This is the documented BYO-key, no-backend pattern (not a committed/bundled secret), but the key is localStorage-resident and reachable by any script on the origin. Acceptable for a personal tool; harden later by moving the call behind the planned `/server` (key stays server-side) or keeping it in memory only. Also: `printPDF`'s `esc` helper only escapes `&`/`<` (fine for its text/title contexts today) ÔÇö make it also escape `"`/`>` before reusing it in any attribute/style context.

### B49 ÔÇö Document Review file-input validation `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö crash/correctness
`[x]` ­ƒöº partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** the single-sheet viewer's open/drop accepted ANY file and read the whole thing into memory via `arrayBuffer()` before failing (OOM risk on a huge/non-PDF drop) ÔÇö now validated by type + non-zero size before `loadPdf`, mirroring the stitcher. (b) **left for review:** the stitcher sniffs only by `.pdf` extension, and its multi-file load loop is in a single `try`, so one bad file silently skips the rest ÔÇö wrap each `loadPdf` per-file and size-check.

### B50 ÔÇö Export/print & prompt robustness `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` ­ƒöº partially fixed in audit PR. (a) **fixed:** the per-edge setback `window.prompt` treated an empty/whitespace confirm as `0` (`+""===0`) instead of cancelling ÔÇö now requires a non-blank numeric. (b) **left for review:** `exportPNG` swallows raster failures (an `image.onerror` rejects with no `.catch`/feedback; `canvas.toBlob`ÔåÆnull silently produces no download) and `printPDF` can leave a stranded blank print window if serialization throws after `window.open` ÔÇö wrap both in try/catch with a user alert (and `win.close()` on failure). `titleReader` base64-encodes with no size/type guard. (Export filename sanitization, object-URL revocation, empty-state guards, and calibration numeric guards were all audited and are clean.)
> Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/export-robustness-b50`): part (b) done ÔÇö `exportPNG` now alerts on an image-load / canvas-taint / `drawImage` failure (was a silent no-op + unhandled rejection) and on a null `toBlob` result; `printPDF` wraps its post-`window.open` work in try/catch and calls `win.close()` on failure, so a serialization/inlining throw can't strand a blank "PreparingÔÇª" window. Remaining: the `titleReader` base64 size/type guard (BYO-key path, low priority).

### B51 ÔÇö Stitcher async handlers have no in-flight guard ÔåÆ re-entrant interleave corrupts placed sheets `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö data-loss, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/async-safety`): `loadStitch` sets a `loadingRef` while it rebuilds `pdfs[]`/`placed[]`, and `openFiles`/`addSheet` bail when it's set ÔÇö so a click during a load can't have its sheet clobbered by the load's blind `setPlaced(out)`. (`bindSource` stays unguarded; it's internal to `openFiles`.) `openFiles`/`addSheet`/`bindSource`/`loadStitch` each `setBusy(true)ÔÇªawaitÔÇªfinally setBusy(false)` but none *reads* busy as a re-entrancy guard, and they mix functional updates (`addSheet`: `setPlaced(arr=>[...arr,ÔÇª])`) with snapshot overwrites (`loadStitch`: `setPlaced(out)`). Clicking "add sheet" while a load is mid-await lets the load's blind `setPlaced(out)` clobber the just-added sheet. Fix: `busyRef` guard at the top of each, or disable the tray/open while busy; prefer functional updates / reconcile rather than overwrite.

### B52 ÔÇö Doc Review load/open paths lack a cancellation token ÔåÆ opening B while A loads mixes the two `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `doc-review/async-safety`): both load paths capture a `loadTok` at start and bail before each post-await `setState` if a newer open superseded them ÔÇö `loadStitch` before `setPdfs`/`setPlaced`; `loadSingleReview`/`fetchSourceBytes` before `setPdfDoc` (destroying the late doc) ÔÇö so opening B while A loads can't land A's PDF/sheets on B. `loadStitch` (Stitcher) and `openReview`ÔåÆ`loadSingleReview`ÔåÆ`fetchSourceBytes` (DocReview) run long await chains (download + loadPdf + render) and then unconditionally `setState` (reviewId, meta, markups, pdfRef, placed) with no "is this still the requested review?" check. Open review A then B quickly and A's late resolution overwrites B's PDF/sheets while ids/markups are B's ÔÇö and the autosave then persists the mix under the wrong id. Fix: capture a token (the requested `rec.id`) in a ref and bail before each post-await `setState` if superseded (mirror the single-sheet `renderTok` pattern). Related to B19.

### B53 ÔÇö In-planner `identifyAt` lacks a token ÔåÆ second click shows/adds the wrong parcel `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/map-gis-robustness`): `identifyAt` captures an `identifyTok` at call start and bails before `setIdentifyRes` if a newer click superseded it (matches the single-sheet `renderTok` pattern). `identifyAt` (SitePlanner) sets `{busy:true}`, awaits `resolveLayerUrl`/`queryAtPoint`, then unconditionally `setIdentifyRes({attrs,ring,ÔÇª})`. Clicking P2 before P1 resolves lets P1 overwrite P2's result, so the panel/`addIdentifiedParcel` use the wrong lot. (Distinct code path from the already-logged MapFinder `handleClick` race, B22.) Fix: a token captured at call start, checked before the post-await `setState`.

### B54 ÔÇö `pullCloud` overwrites the per-user cache with `{}` when a cloud fetch errors `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö data-loss-shaped, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b54-cloud-pull-guard`): `cloudList` now THROWS on a real fetch error (vs. a genuinely-empty result); `pullCloud` catches it, SKIPS the cache write, and returns `{ ok:false }`, so the cache (the user's last-known sites) is preserved; `applyUser` then shows a dismissible "showing your last synced copy" banner instead of a silent empty library. B18's separate half (merge-by-`updatedAt` + `sendBeacon`-on-pagehide) stays open ÔÇö it carries a delete-resurrection subtlety, so it's deliberately left under B18. `cloudList` returns `[]` on *error* (cloudSync.js), so `pullCloud` can't tell "no sites" from "fetch failed" and blind-writes an empty map to the per-user cache; signing in while offline/unreachable thus shows "no sites" and wipes the local cloud cache to empty (data safe in Supabase, but a scary empty state that the user might start recreating into). Fix: have `cloudList` throw/return a status on error; `pullCloud` must skip the cache overwrite on error and surface a "couldn't load" state. (Sharpens B18/B37d into a concrete fix.)

### B55 ÔÇö Fire-and-forget promises without `.catch` ÔåÆ unhandled rejections; async continuations touch torn-down objects `[Document Review / Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` `uploadSource(...).then(...)` (DocReview + Stitcher), the `usePersistence` unmount/visibility `flush()` cloud upsert, and the `probeService(...).then(...)` overlay continuation all lack `.catch`, so a rejected Storage/DB/network call is an unhandled rejection. The `probeService` continuation also does `lyr.addTo(map)` after the await with no map-teardown guard (can throw if the map was removed mid-probe). Fix: add `.catch(()=>{})` to the fire-and-forget calls (matching the `pushSiteToCloud(...).catch` convention) and guard `addTo` with `map._loaded`.

### B56 ÔÇö Assorted async UX/robustness `[Site Planner / Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` Grouped: (a) `goAddress` double-submit ÔÇö Enter isn't gated by `busy` (only the Go button is), so two geocodes can race the `flyTo`; (b) transient `overlapWarn` messages each schedule their own `setTimeout(()=>setOverlapWarn(""))` with the id discarded, so a stale clear blanks a newer warning (and `runXSection` has no in-flight guard); (c) `setProjectStatus` upserts group rows serially and ignores per-row failure, returning `ok:true` even if one plan kept the old status (inconsistent group status) ÔÇö use `Promise.allSettled` and report partial failure; (d) the evidence layers drop `moveend` events that arrive while a fetch is in flight (no trailing-edge refresh), so the last view may never load; (e) the doc-review mutation handlers (`del`/`onStatus`/`fileDrop`) call `refresh()` after an await with no open/mounted guard.
> Progress 2026-06-15: (a) shipped in #30, (d) in #33; **(c) done here** (branch `chore/audit-tail-grind` ÔÇö `setProjectStatus` now writes the group's plans in parallel via `Promise.allSettled` and returns partial-failure counts); (e) covered by the B44 in-flight guards (PR #32). **(b)** ÔÇö the `overlapWarn` stale-timer ÔÇö left open: the clean fix is a single timer-ref `flashWarn` helper across ~12 call sites (or a uniform auto-clear effect that would flatten per-message durations); minor UX, not worth churning 12 sites.
> **(b) done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/keen-curie-2ic2cg`) ÔÇö this closes B56 (a/c/d/e already shipped).** A single timer-ref `flashWarn(msg, ms)` helper now owns the warning toast: it cancels any pending auto-clear *before* showing a new message, so a stale timer from an earlier warning can no longer blank a newer one. All 32 message-setting `setOverlapWarn(...)` sites route through it ÔÇö transient ones pass their duration; sticky instructional prompts pass `ms=0` (no auto-clear, but they still cancel a prior timer so a stale clear can't wipe them either). The bare `setOverlapWarn("")` clears (Escape, Cancel, toggle-off) stay as-is: a later `flashWarn` cancels any lingering timer, and a fired timer just re-blanks an already-empty toast. Also fixed the second half of (b): `runXSection` got the missing in-flight guard (`xsecBusyRef` bails a second cross-section run while one is still sampling, reset in `finally`), plus an unmount cleanup that clears the pending timer. lint 0 err ┬À 174 tests ┬À build green ┬À lazy split intact. Browser-verify logged as **V23**.

### B58 ÔÇö Ditch cross-section drops no-data DEM samples without preserving position ÔåÆ wrong pond depth `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `sampleProfile` now keeps no-data as `null` (position preserved), and `ditchStats` places each surviving sample at its true fractional distance and takes banks from the end-most VALID samples. `sampleProfile` (elevation.js) does `.filter(v=>isFinite(v))`, discarding position; `ditchStats` then assumes uniform spacing (`d = i/(n-1)*lenFt`) and takes `bankFt = (elevFt[0]+elevFt[n-1])/2`. 3DEP commonly returns no-data over water/low ground, so surviving samples aren't evenly spaced ÔÇö the profile x-axis is distorted, and if the true endpoints were no-data the "bank" is taken from interior points, so `depthFt` (the headline number applied to the pond's available depth) is wrong. (Distinct from the B23 1-sample NaN.) Fix: map each sample to its fractional position BEFORE filtering; treat leading/trailing no-data as missing banks rather than substituting interior points.

### B59 ÔÇö Parking render/count/panel ignore per-element `cfg` ÔåÆ "Drive aisle on the far side" toggle is inert `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, high
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): all three `carStalls(...)` calls now pass `cfgOf(el)` (which merges `{...settings, ...el.cfg}`), so a field's per-element `flipDepth`/depth/aisle/angle take effect in count + render ÔÇö the "Drive aisle on the far side" toggle works. The renderer, metrics, and panel all call `carStalls(el.w, el.h, settings)` with the GLOBAL settings, never `cfgOf(el)`, so a parking field's per-element `cfg` (notably `flipDepth`, and side-parking strips created with `cfg`) has no visual/count effect ÔÇö the panel's "Drive aisle on the far side" checkbox writes `cfg.flipDepth` but nothing reads it for drawing. Trailer rendering correctly uses `el.cfg`, so it's an inconsistency. Fix: use `cfgOf(el)` (which merges `{...settings, ...el.cfg}`) in the three `carStalls(...)` call sites.

### B60 ÔÇö Detention bottom area spuriously non-zero when the basin over-tapers `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö correctness, medium
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): `areaAt` now compares the offset ring's SIGNED area to the footprint's and returns 0 on a winding-sign flip (over-taper), so the bogus positive bottom is gone and the "tapers to a point" guard fires. `detentionStorage`'s `areaAt(down)` returns `offsetPolygon(ring, slope*down)` area; when `slope*depth` exceeds the footprint's inradius, `offsetPolygon` yields an inverted (flipped) ring rather than null, and the self-check uses `polyArea` (abs value), so a bogus positive "bottom" passes. Result: overestimated prismoidal volume and the "Basin tapers to a point ÔÇö reduce depth/slope" guard (`aBottom===0`) never fires. Fix: detect collapse via a signed-area sign flip (or offset distance ÔëÑ inradius) and return 0.

### B61 ÔÇö Two-click road drawn shorter than it is wide mis-assigns the length/cross axis `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor
`[x]` Road creation sets `w:len, h:roadWidth+2*curb`, but downstream infers the cross axis from `min(w,h)` (curb render `el.w>=el.h`; `roadTravel` `min`; resize `el.h<=el.w`). A stubby road (`len < cross`) has `w<h`, so curbs draw on the wrong edges and the length/width fields control the swapped axis. Fix: tag an explicit length axis at creation and key rendering/resize off it (or clamp `len>=cross`).
> Progress 2026-06-15: deferred from round-2 ÔÇö a complete fix needs an explicit length-axis tag threaded through render/resize/roadTravel; a creation-time clamp alone leaves resize able to reproduce it. Left Open for that focused change.

### B62 ÔÇö Utility-route corridor leaves a visible gap to the fitting pad `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö minor, cosmetic
`[x]` ­ƒöº fixed 2026-06-15 (branch `site-planner/b58-b62-planner-correctness`): the route now includes the pad centre (`pts=[source, entry, padC]`), so the corridor reaches the fitting pad. `buildUtilRoute` buffers `pts=[source, entry]` where `entry` is on the wall, but the pad sits outside the wall (`entry + normal*(padSize/2+3)`), so the drawn corridor never reaches the pad. Fix: include the pad center in the route (`pts=[source, entry, padC]`) before buffering. (Math/easement-width/overlap checks are otherwise correct.)

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's backlog (its provisional B13ÔÇôB16 were reassigned here; B1ÔÇôB12 + protocol deduped against existing items, which already shipped). -->

### B67 ÔÇö Attach & mark up a site-plan drawing on a parcel `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` **Done 2026-06-16 (browser-only tranche complete: MVP + increments 2a/2b/2c).** Let the user attach an existing site plan (PDF or JPEG; CAD/DWG out of scope for v1) to a selected parcel and draw on top of it with the full existing markup/element toolset, saved as a persistent, named file tied to that parcel. The pattern mirrors Document Review's **immutable-backdrop + editable-layers-above** model and its Supabase persistence ÔÇö reuse those patterns, don't stand up a parallel drawing engine or store. (Related to but distinct from B14, which files drawings under *projects* in the Document Review workspace; this attaches to a *parcel* in the Site Planner and marks up with the planner's own tools.)
- **Entry point.** After clicking a parcel (e.g. the Schiel Road site), offer "Attach drawing" / "Open markup." Allow **multiple drawings per parcel** (a parcel may have several plan sheets).
- **Backdrop = immutable layer.** The imported PDF/JPEG is the locked backdrop (architecture invariant: imported drawing = immutable; user content lives on editable layers above). PDF ÔåÆ rasterize the chosen page to an image for display; if multipage, let the user pick the page.
- **Markup layer = editable.** Reuse the current `els` + `markups` suite (lines, polylines, polygons, text, dimensions, freehand ÔÇö whatever the suite already supports), not a separate drawing engine.
- **Anchoring v1 = pixel-relative to the backdrop.** Store markup coordinates **normalized to the backdrop's intrinsic pixel dimensions (0ÔÇô1 fractions, not screen pixels)** so zoom/pan can't corrupt geometry. Nothing may assume the backdrop is screen-sized.
- **Forward-compat for georeference (deferred).** Persist the backdrop's **intrinsic dimensions** and (for a PDF) **page size/DPI** alongside the markup ÔÇö the minimum needed later to compute a pixelÔåÆTexas-State-Plane (EPSG:2278) transform without re-marking. No georeferencing UI in v1; just don't discard the data that makes it possible.
- **Persistence.** Save as a named file/record associated with the parcel through the existing Supabase save/load + RLS path (**private by default**). Reopening restores backdrop + markup layer.
- **Out of scope v1 (follow-ons):** CAD/DWG import; real-world georeferencing; and **overlaying the marked-up plan onto the live map/aerial** ÔÇö that last is the "overlay onto site" goal that converges with the Ôÿà north-star ("map ÔåÆ drawings ÔåÆ latest set") and the shared coordinate spine. Flagged here as a follow-on, not v1.
> Cross-ref 2026-06-15: the deferred "overlay onto the live map/aerial" follow-on noted just above is now a filed item ÔÇö **B72** (drag-drop a site-plan PDF onto the map, place by hand) + **B73** (scale calibration / precise alignment). B67 stays distinct (parcel-attached markup in *pixel* space), but shares the immutable-backdrop + PDF-rasterize + Supabase machinery ÔÇö build them on the same primitives.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **browser-only MVP shipped + screenshot-verified.** Select a parcel ÔåÆ Parcel panel ÔåÆ **"´╝ï Attach a drawing (PDF / JPG)"** ÔåÆ it rasterizes the file (reusing the B72 `openOverlayFile` engine ÔÇö PDF.js via dynamic import, off the main thread; image path too) and opens a full-screen **`ParcelDrawing`** markup modal: the imported file is an **immutable backdrop** and you draw on an editable layer above it with **Select / Pen / Line / Box / Text**, a colour picker, delete, fit/zoom/pan. **Markups are stored PIXEL-RELATIVE (0..1)** of the backdrop's intrinsic dims, so zoom/pan can't corrupt them (verified: a seeded rect + text land exactly on the backdrop). **Multiple drawings per parcel** (listed in the panel, open/delete). **Persistence:** additive Site Model field **`parcelDrawings`** (`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` 4ÔåÆ5, `parcelDrawingsOf` selector, `slimForCloud` strips the raster like the overlay/underlay); saved via a `saveSite` MERGE + debounced cloud push, so it needs no new wiring through the autosave path and **existing sites are unaffected** (empty field = no-op). **Forward-compat:** the backdrop's intrinsic dims (PDF points) ride with each record for a future pixelÔåÆEPSG:2278 transform. The modal **portals to `<body>`** so its toolbar clears the shell header (B66 stacking floor). **lint 0 ┬À 56 tests (incl. a new parcelDrawings test) ┬À build green.** ÔÅ│ **Deferred to a focused increment 2 (B67 stays `[ ]`):** (a) the **multipage page-picker** (page 1 only today; `pageCount` is stored); (b) **Supabase Storage backing** of the source file so the raster reloads cross-device (today the raster is local + stripped from the cloud row ÔåÆ a re-login shows a "re-attach to view; markups saved" placeholder, exactly like B72's first increment; **markups always persist**); (c) richer markup parity with the planner's full suite (dimensions/measure, freehand smoothing) and on-backdrop markup editing/move (today: draw + select + delete + recolour). Runtime-verified headless via `ui-audit/screens/parcel-drawing.png`; please also try it on planyr.io with a real PDF.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2a ÔÇö page-picker, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **multi-page PDFs now prompt a "Pick a sheet" dialog** at attach (lists all `pageCount` pages; the chosen sheet rasterizes via the B72 `rasterizePage`, reusing the already-loaded PDF for page 1; the record is named `"<file> ÔÇö p.N"`). The PDF proxy is kept alive only for the pick then destroyed (no leak). Single-page PDFs and images skip the dialog. Removes limit (a). lint 0 ┬À 138 tests ┬À build green.
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2b ÔÇö Storage backing, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **the source file now backs the backdrop in Supabase Storage for cross-device reload.** On attach, the original PDF/image is uploaded (background, fallback-safe) to the existing private `doc-review-files` bucket at `<uid>/parcel-drawings/<siteId>/<drawingId>.<ext>` (new `uploadParcelDrawingFile` in `overlayStorage.js`, uid-first so the existing Storage RLS holds); the returned `storageKey`+`ext` persist on the record (kept through `slimForCloud`, which only strips the raster). Opening a drawing whose local raster is missing (cross-device ÔÇö the cloud row's `src` was stripped) **rehydrates** it: download bytes ÔåÆ `rasterizeStoredPdf(bytes, page)` for PDFs, or `downloadOverlayDataUrl` for images, with a "Loading the drawing from the cloudÔÇª" state. Delete now also best-effort-removes the stored object. All fallback-safe (logged-out / >50 MB / error ÔåÆ keeps the local raster, no regression). Mirrors B72's overlay Storage-backing exactly. This **closes limit (b)**. Remaining for B67: (c) richer markup parity (dimensions/measure, move/edit existing markups). lint 0 ┬À 138 tests ┬À build green; **cohort to confirm the cross-device round-trip live** (`VERIFICATION.md` V9) ÔÇö can't be exercised headless (needs a signed-in session + Storage).
> Progress 2026-06-16 (increment 2c ÔÇö richer markup, branch `claude/laughing-babbage-hrxgct`): **closes limit (c); B67 ÔåÆ done.** In `ParcelDrawing.jsx`: (1) **move** any existing markup ÔÇö in Select, press-and-drag a shape/text to reposition it (cursor shows Ôñºmove; a transient `moving` overlay avoids a save per pointer-move, commits once on release, pixel-relative throughout); (2) **edit text** in place (double-click ÔÇö preserved); (3) a **Measure tool + one-time Scale calibration**: draw a **Scale** line and enter its real length ÔåÆ sets feet-per-pixel; the **Measure** tool then labels each line's true length in feet (teal chip), live while drawing. The calibration is stored as a `type:'calib'` markup, so it **persists through the same `onSave(markups)` channel** ÔÇö no extra plumbing. Verified headless (`ui-audit/screens/parcel-drawing.png`: a 0.26-wide measure line reads **124 ft** against a 200-ft / 0.42-wide scale Ô£ô). Optional future niceties (not blocking): resize/rotate handles on a selected shape. lint 0 ┬À 138 tests ┬À build green.

### B69 ÔÇö Paving "+" over-increments from a single-loaded bay `[Site Planner]` (bug)
`[x]` Repro: start from the ~42ÔÇô47ÔÇ▓ paving cross-section (one parking row + one drive aisle ÔÇö a single-loaded bay = parking on one side of the aisle only) and click "+". **Observed:** it added two parking rows plus another drive aisle (a full double-loaded module). **Expected:** "+" adds exactly one parking row ÔÇö from a single-loaded bay it converts to double-loaded (parking both sides of the existing aisle, ~60ÔÇô65ÔÇ▓); a new drive aisle is inserted only once the current aisle is already double-loaded; each click steps by one row, never a two-row module. (Filed from a parallel chat as provisional "NEW-1"; minted **B69** here ÔÇö B67/B68 were already taken on `main`.)
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). **Root cause:** `growParking` stepped depth by a whole `stallDepth+aisle` band per click, but the renderer/counter (`carStalls`) re-flows the field into *double-loaded modules* (2 rows share 1 aisle), so a single band of added depth re-striped into ~2 extra rows + an aisle ÔÇö the jump the report saw. (Distinct from the already-fixed B29/B59, which were per-element `cfg` plumbing in the same area, not the increment model.) **Fix:** two pure helpers ÔÇö `parkDepthForRows(n)= n┬ÀstallDepth + Ôîên/2Ôîë┬Àaisle` and its inverse `parkRowsForDepth(h)` ÔÇö model double-loading explicitly; `growParking` now reads the current row count and sets the depth for `n┬▒1`, so the increment alternates +stallDepth (singleÔåÆdouble-loads the last aisle) / +stallDepth+aisle (adds a new aisle) exactly as specced. `setParkCfg`, `splitParkingRows`, `canShrink`, and the two "Split into rows" `ÔëÑ2` gates were moved onto the same row model so depthÔåörows stays consistent; the "+" button/tooltip relabelled "´╝ï Row" / "Add one parking row" (was "Row + aisle"). Verified: 42ÔåÆ60ÔåÆ102ÔåÆ120ÔåÆ162 (rows 1ÔåÆ2ÔåÆ3ÔåÆ4ÔåÆ5, +18/+42/+18/+42), round-trip `rowsÔåödepth` clean for n=1..8; build + tests pass.

### B70 ÔÇö Curbs: derived auto-placed bands with visual / calc / dimension rules `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Curbs are derived auto-placed thin bands ÔÇö not user-placed, draggable, or deletable; they float to stay attached to their surface as rows are added/removed. Default = 6ÔÇ│ mono curb ("mono" = single monolithic pour, no gutter pan), occupying 0.5ÔÇ▓ of plan-view width. **Placement:** terminal (the outermost band of every section gets a curb on its far/back edge); sidewalk transition (a curb between a sidewalk and the parking/drive it abuts ÔÇö sidewalk behind, pavement dropping at the curb face); never a building-side curb. **Three-way representation, one rule for all sections (standard / truck court / trailer alike):** always *drawn*; always in the *calculations* (area, yield, paving SF, the width feeding any math ÔåÆ 18ÔÇ▓ stall + 24ÔÇ▓ aisle + 0.5ÔÇ▓ = 42.5ÔÇ▓; a 135ÔÇ▓ court = 135.5ÔÇ▓); never in the *displayed dimension* (reads 42ÔÇ▓, 135ÔÇ▓, 185ÔÇ▓ ÔÇö to the face of curb; the 0.5ÔÇ▓ lives only in geometry + calc).
> Inspection first (what already existed): **roads already implemented the exact three-way contract** ÔÇö a road's footprint includes its 6ÔÇ│ curbs (`h = travelW + 2┬Àcurb`), the curbs are drawn as lines, area uses `w┬Àh` (curb included), and the on-canvas dimension shows `travelW` (curb excluded). So the *pattern* was proven; the gap was that **parking, paving/truck-court, and trailer carried no curb at all** (no draw, no calc, no rule). Built only that gap, generalizing the road contract to the other paved types.
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). A single derived-curb model (`curbEdgesOf(el, allEls)`, a **module-level** helper ÔÇö no `cfgOf`/component scope, so it's safe inside `renderElPx`; cf. B68) is the one source feeding both the drawn band and the math: terminal curb on the edge pointing **away** from the host building (so never building-side); a sidewalk-transition curb on the inner edge when a sidewalk/landscape strip sits between the pad and its host; both depth ends for a free-standing field. The element's `w/h` stays the **face-of-curb** size, so (a) the displayed dimension already excludes the curb with no change, and (b) the curb is derived on top and *floats* to the terminal edge as rows change ÔÇö no stored geometry. **Calc:** `curbAreaOf` (╬ú edge-length┬Àwidth) is added to the element's paving/parking/trailer bucket and thus to impervious ÔÇö so a 200ÔÇ▓ bay reads 8 500 sf (eff. 42.5ÔÇ▓) and a 300ÔÇ▓ court reads 40 650 sf (eff. 135.5ÔÇ▓), while the on-canvas label still reads 42/135. **Draw:** a thin band whose width *scales* (so the 12ÔÇ│ option visibly doubles the 6ÔÇ│), kept legible by its stroke when the 0.5ÔÇ▓ is sub-pixel at low zoom. Roads untouched (excluded from the new model; they keep their own curbs). Build + tests pass.
> Deferred nuance (kept minor, not blocking): the sidewalk-transition and free-field-both-ends cases are geometric heuristics (same-host + inboard; ┬▒depth ends) rather than an explicit stored curb edge; polygon (irregular) pads are out of scope for the auto-curb. Revisit if a future case needs a hand-placed/overridden curb edge.

### B71 ÔÇö Per-element curb override on trailer parking (6ÔÇ│ ÔåÆ 12ÔÇ│) `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Selecting a trailer-parking element exposes a curb selector: default 6ÔÇ│, option 12ÔÇ│ (heavier curb to take trailer-tire/dolly abuse). 12ÔÇ│ = 1.0ÔÇ▓ of plan-view width (double the 6ÔÇ│ curb's 0.5ÔÇ▓) ÔÇö real plan width, not a taller face/reveal (height unchanged). Flows through the same three-way rule as B70: drawn in the visual field, included in calculations (a trailer section with a 12ÔÇ│ terminal curb counts the full 1.0ÔÇ▓ in area/width math), excluded from the displayed dimension (185ÔÇ▓ still reads 185ÔÇ▓). Build the property globally underneath (any band could carry it) but surface the control only on trailer parking for now.
> Done 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/nice-wozniak-2k2v9c`). The width is a global per-element field `el.curbW` (`CURB_6 = 0.5` / `CURB_12 = 1.0`) read by `curbWidthOf(el)`, so any band *could* carry it; the **6ÔÇ│ / 12ÔÇ│ control is surfaced only in the selected-trailer panel** (`setCurbW`). It rides the B70 model end-to-end: the drawn band scales to 1.0ÔÇ▓ (visibly double), `curbAreaOf` counts the full 1.0ÔÇ▓ in the SF/width math, and the displayed dimension is untouched (still face-of-curb). Trailer fields show no on-canvas dimension number today, so there's nothing to wrongly include the curb in; the rule holds the moment one is shown. Verified: a 12ÔÇ│ curb on a 300ÔÇ▓ edge = 300 sf vs the 6ÔÇ│ 150 sf; build + tests pass.

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's backlog (provisional "NEW-1"/"NEW-2" ÔåÆ minted B72/B73 here; deduped against B67, which had already flagged the map-overlay goal as its own out-of-scope follow-on). -->

### B74 ÔÇö "New plan, same parcel" drops the site's county ÔåÆ identify/jurisdiction defaults to Harris `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `newPlanSameParcel` (`SitePlannerApp.jsx`) copied origin/parcels/settings/underlay but **not** `county`, so a Fort Bend/Chambers iteration plan normalized to `county:null` and the planner fell back to Harris for click-identify, jurisdiction utility layers, and easement rules. Added `county: src.county || null` to the payload (matches `goPlanFromParcels`). (`duplicatePlan` already spreads `...src`, so it was fine.)

### B75 ÔÇö Chambers county-acreage check ~10.76├ù wrong; flags every correct parcel as a huge mismatch `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The TxGIO statewide source (Chambers) names its already-in-acres fields `GIS_AREA`/`LEGAL_AREA` (with a sibling `*_UNIT`), which the `countyAcres` acres-regex didn't match ÔåÆ it fell through to a projected `st_area` in m┬▓, ├À43560 (ft┬▓/ac) ÔåÆ ~10.76├ù too small; the old "tenx" m┬▓ guard also corrected the wrong way (├À instead of ├ù). `countyAcres` now prefers `gis_area`/`legal_area` when the sibling unit says "Acres"; the geometry-check badge detects an m┬▓-misread Shape area and shows the **corrected** acreage (a correct lot reads Ô£ô, not "900% off Ôû▓"). Badge-only ÔÇö never fed the yield math (which uses the drawn polygon).

### B76 ÔÇö Cross-county OBJECTID selection-key collision mis-toggles parcels `[Site Planner / map]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `MapFinder.handleClick` keyed selected parcels by bare `OBJECTID`, which is unique only within one CAD layer ÔÇö a multi-county assembly with two lots sharing an id would toggle each other off (and corrupt the assembled acreage). Key is now namespaced `"<county>:<oid>"`. (Distinct from the B22 rapid-click race; the dedupe/hilite guards there are unchanged.)

### B77 ÔÇö `fileNewReview` ignores upload failure ÔåÆ silently files an unopenable document `[Document Review]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. A non-oversize `uploadSource` failure (network/RLS/transient 5xx) was stored as a normal source (`storageKey:null, oversize:false`) and `fileNewReview` returned `ok:true` with no feedback. It now returns `{ uploadFailed }`, and `ProjectLibrary.fileDrop` collects failures and alerts ("filed, but the upload failed ÔÇö re-drop on open to view"). (On load a null key already showed the re-drop banner/placeholder; the gap was no warning at file time.)

### B78 ÔÇö Parking/trailer stall layout can infinite-loop (freeze) on a degenerate config `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö critical (latent)
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `carStalls`/`trailerStalls` compute `mod = rowDepth*2 + aisle` then `mods = floor(h/mod)` and loop `mods` times; `mod===0` (0 stall-depth + 0 aisle) makes `mods` Infinity ÔåÆ an unbounded band loop that hard-freezes the tab (and a 0 stall-width/`tw` made `perRow` Infinity ÔåÆ unbounded render loop). The current UI clamps these inputs (`min` 1/8/1), so it's **not reachable from the fields today** ÔÇö the live freeze a sibling audit saw was a transient mid-deploy regression ÔÇö but legacy/`cfg`-merged data could still hit it, so added an early-return guard (`mod>0 && pitch>0`; `tw>0`) to both.

### B79 ÔÇö Negative/zero Grid size infinite-loops the grid render; size fields have no upper bound `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high (latent)
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `gridLines` used `step = settings.gridSize` directly; a 0/negative step made `for (xÔÇª; x += step)` never terminate (freeze). Clamped `step = Math.max(0.1, +gridSize || 10)`. The Grid field's `min={1}` already blocks it from the UI; this hardens the loop itself. Also added `max={MAX_DIM}` (100000 ft) to the element Width/Depth fields so a fat-fingered/pasted size can't produce absurd geometry or SVG-coordinate stalls.

### B80 ÔÇö Underlay calibration divides by zero on a 0 ft/px underlay `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `applyCalibration` guarded `knownFt>0`/`measured>0` but not `underlay.ftPerPx>0`; a degenerate underlay ÔåÆ Infinity/NaN placement (image flies off-canvas). Added `underlay.ftPerPx > 0` to the guard (covers the derived `sy` too).

### B81 ÔÇö Self-intersecting / near-zero polygons accepted everywhere ÔåÆ silently wrong area `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Parcel/element/measure-area close handlers only gated on vertex count; a bow-tie outline closes and `polyArea` (abs shoelace) returns the *net* area (lobes cancel) with no warning, corrupting siteSqft ÔåÆ coverage/FAR/parking/detention. Added `polySelfIntersects` + `flashPolyWarn` on close (parcel / drawn element / measured area): warns on a self-crossing or ~zero-area outline (accepts but flags, so the number isn't trusted silently).

### B82 ÔÇö Calibrate tool is a silent no-op with no underlay `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Calibrate sits in the main toolbar (selectable with no underlay); clicks hit `if (!underlay) return` silently. Now flashes "Calibrate needs an underlay ÔÇö drop an aerial/screenshot first."

### B83 ÔÇö Copy/Duplicate carries dangling host/court metadata `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Paste/duplicate stripped only `attachedTo`, keeping `truckCourt`/`forCourt`/`dogEar`/`oppSide`/side-park/sidewalk tags ÔÇö so a copied child carried court metadata pointing at nothing (refit/trailer logic reads it). Added a shared `detachClone` that strips all relational tags on clone. (Copying a host building's whole attached assembly is a separate enhancement ÔÇö noted, not done.)

### B84 ÔÇö Metes/POB plot silently cancels when no bearings parsed `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `anchorEncumbrance` returned silently when the description produced 0 calls (e.g. an unreadable format). Now warns "No bearings were recognizedÔÇª", with a separate message when the calls can't form a shape. (The underlying curve/dash-DMS parser gaps stay under B25/B26.)

### B85 ÔÇö Empty callout left on Escape; zero-length cross-section makes a degenerate profile `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The global Escape cleared `calloutDraft` but not an in-progress empty *new* callout ÔÇö now calls `cancelEditCallout()` (added `editCallout` to the keydown effect deps so the closure stays fresh). `runXSection` now requires `lenFt > 1` (a same-point cross-section produced NaN profile distances).

### B86 ÔÇö Split tool corrupts area on concave parcels `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16 (complements B31). `splitPolygon` keys off the extreme-`t` crossings, so a straight cut across a concave parcel (4+ crossings) can yield overlapping/omitting halves whose areas don't sum to the original ÔåÆ wrong siteSqft + every downstream yield. Added an **area-conservation + self-intersection guard** in `performSplit`: if the two pieces don't conserve area (┬▒2%) or come out self-intersecting, the cut is rejected with "that cut crosses the parcel ambiguously ÔÇö try a straight cut between two opposite edges," instead of saving corrupted geometry. (A full polygon-clipping rewrite for multi-piece concave cuts stays deferred per B31.)

### B87 ÔÇö `Infinity` / scientific-notation in a numeric field bypasses the min-clamp ÔåÆ NaN geometry, persisted as null `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö high
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `NumInput.commit` rejected only `isNaN`, but `parseFloat("1e999")`/`"Infinity"` return `Infinity` (not NaN) and `Math.max(min, Infinity)===Infinity`, so a non-finite value slipped through ÔÇö Grid=Infinity made `snap` return NaN (every new coord NaN), a size=Infinity drew nothing, and `JSON.stringify` then persisted NaN/Infinity as **null** (permanent corruption). Now requires `Number.isFinite(n)` and applies a default `1e7` upper cap when no explicit `max`; `snap` also guards a non-finite/Ôëñ0 grid (falls back to 10). Closes the persist-as-null source (B91).

### B88 ÔÇö Type confusion: a record whose `parcels`/`els` isn't an array crashes the whole app `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. `createSiteModel` kept any truthy value (`p.parcels || []`), so a tampered/legacy/bad-sync record with a non-array collection flowed into `parcels.reduce(...)` ÔåÆ `TypeError` ÔåÆ blank app on load. Now coerces every collection (`Array.isArray(...) ? ... : []`) and `settings` to an object. Added a unit test (40 tests pass). (The B68 error boundary already contained the blank-screen; this stops the throw so the site actually loads.)

### B89 ÔÇö "Travel widths" free-text feeds road geometry ÔåÆ NaN roads `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö medium
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. The road-width preset list `.split(",")` of the free-text field offered any token (e.g. `abc`) as a selectable width; drawing with it did `+"abc" = NaN` ÔåÆ a NaN road persisted as null. The preset list now keeps only finite, positive numeric tokens.

### B90 ÔÇö No cross-tab reconciliation: a second tab's finder list goes stale `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö mediumÔåÆlow
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Added a `window` `storage` listener that refreshes the finder list when another tab changes the site store. **Re-scoped on inspection:** the audit's "whole-store clobber" doesn't actually reproduce ÔÇö `saveSite`/`deleteSite` already read-modify-write the latest store per call, so a second tab editing a *different* site can't wipe the first's work; the real symptom was a stale list, which this fixes (logged-in tabs now also pick up each other's cloud-cache writes).

### B91 ÔÇö NaN/Infinity coordinates persist as `null` (corruption amplifier) `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Addressed-at-source 2026-06-16. The non-finite *sources* are now closed (B87 NumInput/snap, B89 road widths, plus the B78/B79 freeze guards), so no path feeds a non-finite coordinate into a save. A deep on-save geometry sanitizer was deliberately NOT added ÔÇö rewriting stored points risks corrupting valid geometry, worse than the rare amplified case it would catch.

### B92 ÔÇö Unbounded text/emoji/RTL in annotations bloats the SVG/record `[Site Planner]` (bug) ÔÇö low
`[x]` Fixed 2026-06-16. Callout/text annotations now cap at `maxLength={2000}`. (Injection was already safe ÔÇö XSS payloads render escaped.)

> **Triaged but NOT changed (still open / deferred, by decision):** building bump-outs aren't clamped when the host shrinks (medium, geometry ÔÇö risk-deferred); `deleteSite` cloud delete stays fire-and-forget (zombie only if the delete fails ÔÇö B-class edge); over-quota save still drops the underlay and reports success (graceful-degradation, minor); `currentSite` pointer global-not-per-user (low). County straddle/out-of-coverage messaging stays under **B13/B36**; metes curve + dash-DMS under **B25/B26**; bundle size / Anthropic-SDK / Mapillary-token / Nominatim / deploy-churn remain LOW/infra.

<!-- Filed 2026-06-15 from a parallel chat's GIS batch (its provisional NEW-1ÔÇªNEW-4 ÔåÆ B93ÔÇªB96 here). (RENUMBERED from a stale B72ÔÇôB75 ÔÇö `main` had since filled B72ÔÇôB92 with the overlay tool + an audit batch; IDs are permanent.) Build order for the batch: B96 (cache) first ÔåÆ B93 + B94 ride on it ÔåÆ B95 deferred. One generic ArcGIS-REST connector serves B93 + B94 (parameterized per source; no per-city/per-source code paths). -->

### B93 ÔÇö Jurisdiction identify: city limits, ETJ & county `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) ÔÇö SHIP target (promoted from deferred 2026-06-17)
`[x]` Click any point ÔÇö or, **on explicit request**, run on the active parcel ÔÇö and get back which jurisdictions it falls in: incorporated city (or "unincorporated"), any city **ETJ** (extraterritorial jurisdiction), and county (Harris, Fort Bend, Montgomery, Galveston, BrazoriaÔÇª). **Trigger: click / explicit request only ÔÇö NOT auto-loaded on every parcel.**
