- **Interaction:** a map click runs a point-in-polygon "identify" against the loaded boundary layers; also identify-on-demand for the active parcel. **Test the whole parcel, not just its centroid** ÔÇö flag boundary-straddle (e.g. partly City of Houston, partly unincorporated Harris Co.) and list *every* jurisdiction it touches rather than forcing one answer.
- **Coverage:** Houston-metro priority cities (Houston, Baytown, Missouri City, Sugar Land, Pearland, ÔÇª), but driven by a **source registry**, never per-city hardcoded logic ÔÇö adding a city = adding a registry row, not new code.
- **Source-priority chain (the crux):** query in fallback order per layer ÔÇö authoritative city source where it publishes good GIS (e.g. Houston / COHGIS) ÔåÆ regional backbone (**H-GAC**, the Houston-Galveston Area Council, whose GIS covers the 13-county region) ÔåÆ statewide backbone (**TxDOT Open Data**, which publishes city limits + ETJ statewide). **One generic ArcGIS-REST connector** parameterized per source; a registry row stores endpoint URL, layer ID, and a field map. (Same connector serves **B94** ÔÇö build it once.)
- **Field mapping:** every source names the city field differently (`CITY_NM` / `NAME` / `MUNICIPALITY` ÔÇª). The registry normalizes each source's schema into one internal shape so the UI is source-agnostic.
- **Reuse existing GIS infra ÔÇö do NOT build a parallel system:** same cache (**B96**), same per-layer honest status + visible data-age, same ~45 s self-heal re-probe, same Texas State Plane frame.
- **Screening-only framing:** ETJ is volatile (Texas law now lets landowners petition out of a city's ETJ; annexations move city limits) ÔÇö label results screening-only, always show source + age, never present as a legal/official determination, keep a visible "verify with the jurisdiction" note.
- **Build step before wiring (evidence-first):** confirm each candidate endpoint actually responds and carries the expected fields, then calibrate against a few parcels whose jurisdiction is already known. Don't assume an endpoint/schema exists ÔÇö verify it.
> **Relationship to B13 / B36 (county-resolution theme) ÔÇö not a duplicate, but adjacent.** B13-pt1 and B36(a)/(d) have been *waiting on a county-boundary polygon dataset* to replace the coarse per-county **bbox** pre-filter (true point-in-county CAD routing + straddle merge). B93 introduces exactly that data path ÔÇö county boundary polygons loaded via the new source registry + point-in-polygon. So B93's county layer **is** the dataset those items need: land it and B13-pt1 / B36(a)/(d) can be closed or folded in. They're still distinct items (B13/B36 = *internal CAD-routing*; B93 = a *user-facing* city/ETJ/county identify) ÔÇö build them aware of each other so the boundary data loads once, not twice.
> **Endpoint verification needs network egress (2026-06-15, filing session):** the build-before-wiring step above is blocked from a default web session ÔÇö outbound to the GIS hosts (`server.arcgisonline.com`, `services.arcgis.com`, `gis.h-gac.com`, TxGIO/TxDOT) returns `403 host_not_allowed` from the environment egress proxy. Recipe: in the environment editor set **Network access ÔåÆ Custom** (keep "Also include default list of common package managers" checked so npm/GitHub still work) and add, one per line: `*.arcgis.com` (TxDOT + city AGOL services), `*.arcgisonline.com`, `gis.h-gac.com` (H-GAC), `*.geographic.texas.gov` (TxGIO), plus `www.gis.hctx.net` + `gis.fbcad.org` (HCAD/FBCAD, for calibrating against known-jurisdiction parcels). **The proxy is fixed when a session starts**, so the change applies to a NEW session ÔÇö not the one that saved it. Verify the hosts respond, then do the verify/calibrate pass before wiring.
> **Progress 2026-06-15 (branch `claude/festive-davinci-0oco2n`): egress unblocked ÔåÆ endpoints verified + calibrated ÔåÆ connector built, tested, wired.** The allowlist recipe above took effect in a FRESH session (the proxy picks up the policy at session start, not mid-session): all GIS hosts now reachable ÔÇö `server.arcgisonline.com` / `services.arcgis.com` (AGOL) / `feature.geographic.texas.gov` (TxGIO) / `www.gis.hctx.net` all 200; H-GAC + FBCAD are *allowed* but upstream-503 right now (not a proxy block). **Verified + calibrated against known ground:** city limits = TxGIO `City_Boundaries/Texas_City_Boundaries/MapServer/0` field `city_name` (downtown Houston ÔåÆ "Houston"); county = TxDOT `Texas_County_Boundaries/FeatureServer/0` field `CNTY_NM` (same point ÔåÆ "Harris" / FIPS 48201). **ETJ has no statewide layer** ÔÇö only per-city / regional publishers (H-GAC for the metro); kept as a registry row that honestly reads "source not wired" rather than faked. **Built `src/workspaces/site-planner/lib/jurisdiction.js`:** ONE generic, registry-driven ArcGIS-REST connector on the B96 SWR cache ÔÇö `JURISDICTION_SOURCES` rows (url/layer/field-map/query-kind), `normalizeFeature` (each source's schema ÔåÆ one internal shape), and `identifyJurisdiction(lng,lat,{ring})` which tests the WHOLE parcel via a polygon-intersect so a boundary **straddle** lists every city/county touched (point fallback when no ring); honest per-source status + visible data-age; screening-only. **Wired** into the planner's Identify panel as an explicit-request "ÔÜû´©Ä Jurisdiction & road authority" button (never auto-loaded, per spec). **23 unit tests** (`test/jurisdiction.test.js`, injected fetch + cache); `lint` (0 errors) / `test` (68 pass) / `build` green; lazy chunks still split. **Remaining (kept `[ ]`):** wire a real ETJ source (H-GAC when its server recovers, or per-city COHGIS rows); **live browser confirmation** (CORS from the app origin + on-map render ÔÇö same handoff as B96); and the **B13/B36 fold-in** (this county layer IS the boundary dataset those items were waiting on ÔÇö close/merge in a follow-up so the data loads once).
> **ETJ leg wired 2026-06-15 (same branch):** B93's third leg is no longer a gap ÔÇö wired to the City of Houston's own COHGIS ETJ (`COH_ETJ_view/FeatureServer/1`, reflects SB2038 releases), verified + calibrated (Aldine / Spring, unincorporated near Houston ÔåÆ in the ETJ ring; downtown / in-city ÔåÆ not). Houston-only by design (the priority metro); other cities' ETJ are additive registry rows. `normalizeFeature` gained a `nameConst` fallback so a single-jurisdiction layer with no city-name column still resolves ("Houston"). So **all three legs (city + ETJ + county) are now wired against verified sources**; what remains is live browser confirmation + the B13/B36 fold-in.
> **Amendment 2026-06-17 ÔÇö disposition: DEFERRED ÔåÆ SHIP as a released, user-facing feature** (status/disposition change folded into B93 per the arriving note; **no new B#**). Promote B93 from "deferred until the GIS-caching foundation is solid" to a production capability on planyr.io: ship the **full** item ÔÇö the boundary layer(s) + click/explicit-request identify ÔÇö as a real **user-facing** tool, not dev-only/experimental. (The connector, all three legs, and the tests already exist ÔÇö see the progress notes above; what's promoted is the *intent to ship*, gated only on the points below.)
> - **Build order unchanged:** **GIS layer caching (B96, stale-while-revalidate) stays the immediate predecessor.** Ship caching + jurisdiction identify **together as one unit** rather than leaving jurisdiction open-ended. *Owner option:* if Michael confirms standalone-first, drop the cache dependency, ship against live endpoints now, and retrofit the cache later.
> - **Screening-only contract carries into the shipped feature** (already specified above under "Screening-only framing" ÔÇö restated as a ship requirement, not a new ask): a per-layer **data-age stamp** + a visible **"verify with the jurisdiction"** disclaimer are always shown; **never** present ETJ or city limits as authoritative.
> - **Honest fallback disclosure (sharpens the source-priority chain):** when a city's authoritative source is unavailable and the answer came from the **regional (H-GAC) / statewide (TxDOT/TxGIO) fallback**, surface **which source answered** ÔÇö don't silently present a fallback result as if it were the city's own.
> - **Open technical gates unchanged:** live browser confirmation (**VERIFICATION V1**) + the **B13/B36 fold-in** (B93's county layer is the boundary dataset those items wait on) + a real non-Houston ETJ source (H-GAC when up, or per-city rows).
> Ô£à **SHIPPED + VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-17.** It's live on planyr.io and confirmed working end-to-end in a real headless browser (logged-out): (1) the four GIS queries answer **from the planyr.io origin with no CORS block** ÔÇö county `services.arcgis.com` ÔåÆ 200 "Harris", city TxGIO ÔåÆ 200 "Houston", ETJ COHGIS ÔåÆ 200 (in-city ÔåÆ correctly not in ETJ), road TxDOT ÔåÆ 200 (maint-agency 4 = City); (2) the on-screen flow renders ÔÇö map ÔåÆ planner ÔåÆ **­ƒöì Identify parcel** ÔåÆ **ÔÜû´©Ä Jurisdiction & road authority** shows **County: Harris ┬À City: Houston ┬À ETJ: not in Houston ETJ ┬À Road maint.: City**, each with a data-age + the "Screening only ÔÇö verify with the jurisdiction" disclaimer. Recorded as **VERIFICATION V1 Ô£à** (screenshot evidence). Marking B93 **done** (the user-facing feature ships for the priority metro). **Residual enhancements stay open as their own follow-ups (not blockers):** a real **non-Houston ETJ** source (other cities' ETJ = additive registry rows; today reads "not in Houston ETJ" outside Houston) and the **B13/B36 fold-in** (reuse this county-boundary layer for internal CAD routing so the data loads once). The honest-fallback-disclosure sharpening (show which source answered) is also carried forward under those.
> Ô£à **ETJ is no longer Houston-only (2026-06-17).** Owner pushed back that the feature isn't finished while ETJ is Houston-only ÔÇö fixed. Confirmed there is **no statewide ETJ layer** (TxGIO has city limits + county, not ETJ), then swapped the ETJ source from the City-of-Houston-only layer to **H-GAC's regional ETJ layer** (`HGAC_City_ETJ_Boundaries` on AGOL `services.arcgis.com/su8ic9KbA7PYVxPS`, CORS-clean), which carries **every city's ETJ across the 13-county Houston-Galveston metro** (Houston, Sugar Land, Missouri City, Pearland, Richmond, Katy, Baytown, Conroe, Texas City, ÔÇª). A single **registry-row swap** ÔÇö exactly the design's "add a city = add a row" intent ÔÇö keyed on the layer's `CITY` field with title-casing of its ALL-CAPS values; the UI's empty-state text changed "not in Houston ETJ" ÔåÆ "not in a city ETJ". **Verified live from the planyr.io origin (2026-06-17):** Spring/Aldine ÔåÆ **Houston**, SW of Sugar Land ÔåÆ **Richmond** (a non-Houston city), in-city point ÔåÆ none (ETJ is the ring outside city limits) ÔÇö all HTTP 200, no CORS block. County / City / Road were already statewide (TxDOT / TxGIO), so the whole identify is now metro-wide. lint 0 errors ┬À **206 tests** (33 jurisdiction; 2 new: title-case + non-Houston ETJ) ┬À build green. **Remaining ETJ follow-up (smaller, separate):** cross-REGION coverage outside H-GAC ÔÇö e.g. Dallas via **NCTCOG**'s ETJ layer ÔÇö is another additive registry row when a deal needs it (would benefit from a tiny multi-source-per-role tweak); the **B13/B36 fold-in** is still the other open thread.
> Ô£à **ETJ extended to Austin + DFW, region-routed (2026-06-17).** Owner asked to add the Austin & DFW metros *if it doesn't slow the Houston use case*. Done, with **region routing** so it can't: ETJ is now a bbox-scoped **list** (`ETJ_SOURCES` + `etjSourcesForPoint(lat,lng)` in `jurisdiction.js`), and a click only queries the metro whose box it falls in ÔÇö a **Houston click still fires exactly one ETJ query (H-GAC), unchanged**; Austin/DFW servers are never touched from Houston. Investigated the data honestly: there's **no statewide ETJ** and, unlike Houston (H-GAC's clean regional layer), Austin/DFW publish ETJ **city-by-city** and some authoritative servers aren't CORS-open (Fort Worth's own `mapit.fortworthtexas.gov` is CORS-blocked; NCTCOG has regional city-limits but **no** regional ETJ + no CORS). Per the owner's "big cities now" call, wired the cleanly-reachable AGOL layers: **Austin** = City of Austin 2-/5-mile ETJ (`COA_Jurisdiction/20`, nameConst "Austin"); **DFW** = City of Fort Worth ETJ (`Fort_Worth_ETJ`, 50 polygons, nameConst "Fort Worth"); **Dallas** has ~no ETJ (landlocked ÔÇö empty layer, nothing to add). `identifyJurisdiction` now queries 1-or-many sources per role and **unions** the names (handles a metro-seam straddle). Smaller Austin/DFW suburbs aren't covered yet (additive rows later) ÔåÆ such a point reads "not in a city ETJ"; outside all three metros reads "no ETJ layer here". **Verified live from the planyr.io origin (2026-06-17):** all three layers CORS-clean + return features at real ETJ points (Austin: Del Valle / NW edge; Fort Worth: Alliance / SW). lint 0 errors ┬À **230 tests** (5 routing tests incl. "Houston ÔåÆ exactly one query", + Austin/non-Houston end-to-end) ┬À build green. Still open: more Austin/DFW suburb rows on request; B13/B36 fold-in.

### B129 ÔÇö [HAND-OFF] GIS overlays: NWI wetlands CORS + the cached-vector experiment ÔÇö needs a real browser to finish `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug/task)
`[x]` **The probe-resilience fix below LANDED via PR #60 (2026-06-16); this item now tracks the REMAINING browser-only work** (confirming FEMA renders, the NWI CORS question, and the reverted vector experiment). **Written for a future session that HAS a browser.** This session had none (CI container), and the GIS hosts are egress-blocked from it (`curl ÔÇª?f=json` ÔåÆ HTTP 403 "Host not in allowlist: hazards.fema.gov / fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov"). So map *rendering* could only be judged from the owner's screenshots ÔÇö which repeatedly misled me into saying "it works" when it didn't. **Do not trust a layer's green "loaded" status as proof of rendering: it is set the instant the layer is attached, BEFORE the image loads.** Only a human/browser confirms a visible overlay.

**Symptom (owner-reported on planyr.io + the PR #60 preview):** toggling **Wetlands (NWI)** throws a layer error. First `network / CORS error` (the health *probe*), then after a fix `Could not parse response as JSON ÔÇª CORS` (esri-leaflet's internal *metadata* fetch). **FEMA flood zones** seemed to load (green status; faint hatching in one screenshot) but its *visible* rendering was never actually confirmed ÔÇö the owner's pushback ("what makes you think it's working?") is the honest state.

**Root-cause analysis (UNVERIFIED in a browser ÔÇö confirm before acting):**
- NWI host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov` appears to refuse cross-origin browser requests (CORS): both the `?f=json` health probe AND esri-leaflet's metadata/service-info fetch fail. Its `f=image` export *should* still paint via a CORS-exempt `<img>`, but esri's metadata `requesterror` fires regardless. FEMA host `hazards.fema.gov` appears CORS-clean.
- The (now-deleted) `kind:"vector"` overlay never rendered. Strong suspicion: an esri raster/image layer must be added **directly to the map**, not nested inside an `L.layerGroup`. Reverted rather than debugged blind.

**What PR #60 MERGED (the safe, shippable part of branch `claude/nice-dirac-029jok`). Net diff vs `main` = just `layers.js`:**
1. `probeService` returns `unreachable:true` on a CORS/network throw; `syncOverlayLayers` then adds the image layer anyway ÔÇö a CORS-blocked *probe* no longer kills a layer.
2. A layer `requesterror` no longer drops the layer or fires the red toast ÔÇö just a quiet per-layer "failed" status; `'load'` flips it back to "loaded".
FEMA/NWI are on the proven `dynamicMapLayer` (`f=image`) path. `lib/vectorLayers.js` (pure fetch/paging/style/decide engine, 31 tests, from #58) is on `main` but **UNWIRED groundwork**. (The branch's MapFinder/SitePlanner edits were dropped at merge ÔÇö they pre-dated and would have undone main's B105/tab work; only the `layers.js` fix landed.) `lint` 0 / `test` 138 / `build` green.

**Questions a browser settles in minutes (DevTools ÔåÆ Network / Console):**
1. Does **FEMA** draw a *visible* flood overlay on the preview? (Confirm/deny ÔÇö ignore the "loaded" status.)
2. Does the **NWI** `export?f=image` request fire, return 200, and produce an `<img>` *despite* the metadata `requesterror`? Or does esri block the export?
3. NWI response headers ÔÇö is `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` present on `/MapServer`, `/export`, `/0/query`? Is the block total or endpoint-specific? Did it change recently (the DONE list says wetlands once worked)?

**Next steps (after browser triage):**
- **FEMA:** confirm it draws a *visible* overlay (the merged probe-resilience fix should keep it alive even if its probe is blocked).
- **NWI:** (a) swap to a CORS-clean wetlands endpoint (browser-test alternates ÔÇö a USFWS ArcGIS Online hosted layer, a tiled service, etc.; edit `STATEWIDE.wetlands.url` in `lib/layers.js`); or (b) route via the `/server` proxy (the documented durable fix for flaky agency hosts ÔÇö serves through our own origin, no CORS); or (c) leave it (screening-only).
- **Vector engine (`lib/vectorLayers.js`):** if revived, render via **direct map-add** and browser-test. Remember these overlays are used at area-scale, where the server-rendered image is the right tool; vectors only pay off zoomed in tight. The deleted `vectorOverlay.js` is recoverable from git (branch commits `49e2a1b` / `2f89c19`).

**Key files:** `lib/layers.js` (`STATEWIDE`, `syncOverlayLayers` dispatch, `probeService` `unreachable`, softened `requesterror`), `lib/vectorLayers.js` + `test/vectorLayers.test.js` (unwired engine), `VERIFICATION.md` V20 (the live-test item for the merged fix).

> **Ô£à DONE 2026-06-17 ÔÇö verified in a REAL headless browser (Chromium/Playwright, live on planyr.io), branch `claude/gallant-wright-c3sjwd`.** Prior sessions had no browser and guessed; this one settled the open questions with evidence.
> - **FEMA flood zones ÔÇö CONFIRMED RENDERING.** `/export?f=image` ÔåÆ **HTTP 200 `image/png`**, `<img>` paints (sampled **25.9%** non-transparent at zoom 15: teal `(0,224,224)` Zone AE, orange `(224,128,0)` floodway, red `(224,0,0)` boundaries ÔÇö standard NFHL symbology tracing Buffalo/White Oak Bayou through downtown Houston). **CORS-clean:** `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://planyr.io` on the metadata GET, `/export`, AND the OPTIONS preflight. **Scale caveat (the source of prior "is it even working?" confusion):** NFHL sublayers 27/28 are gated AT THE SOURCE to `minScale ~1:36,112`, so they draw nothing at city-wide zoom (Ôëñ~z13) and only appear at ~zoom 14+. Blank-when-zoomed-out is expected, not a bug ÔÇö added a user-facing layer note + a code comment so it isn't re-misdiagnosed.
> - **NWI wetlands ÔÇö root cause is NOT CORS (the original hypothesis was wrong); the USFWS service is DOWN (HTTP 500).** The configured host `fwspublicservices.wim.usgs.gov/wetlandsmapservice/ÔÇª` returns **HTTP 500 across its ENTIRE catalog** (metadata, `/export`, `/query`, REST root, `/info`), confirmed three independent ways: sandbox `curl` (3/3 persistent), planyr.io in a real browser (layer errors, no `<img>`, zero successful responses), and an out-of-band fetch on a different network (also 500). Genuine **agency-side outage**, hitting production users too. esri-leaflet's "could not parse JSON / CORS" text is a misleading catch-all.
> - **The configured URL is canonical & correct ÔÇö confirmed NOT to swap it.** The official USFWS "National Wetlands Inventory" ArcGIS web map (arcgis.com item `da9a3343ÔÇª`) uses this EXACT MapServer. The only alternates are `www.fws.gov/wetlands/ÔÇª` (older mirror, egress-blocked from the test sandbox so not in-browser-verifiable) and non-authoritative state re-hosts ÔÇö swapping to either would repeat the blind guess this item exists to stop. **Decision: option (c) ÔÇö keep the canonical URL** (it returns when USFWS restores the service) and make the failure honest.
> - **Shipped (this branch):** `lib/layers.js` ÔÇö the `requesterror` status now reads a plain *"the map service is not responding ÔÇö it may be temporarily unavailable (screening only)"* instead of esri's misleading CORS text (the `'load'` handler still flips it back to "loaded" if the image lands, so a healthy-but-CORS-blocked layer is unaffected); documenting comments on FEMA's source-side scale-gating and the NWI canonical-but-outaged endpoint; and the user-facing FEMA "zoom in to see flood zones" note. **lint 0 errors ┬À 175 tests ┬À build green.**
> - **Browser now persists for future sessions** (STEP 3): Playwright Chromium is installed to `/opt/pw-browsers`, a repo SessionStart hook (`.claude/hooks/ensure-browser.sh`) re-ensures it on every web session, and egress to the GIS hosts + `cdn.playwright.dev` is open. Evidence screenshots committed under `gis-verify/`.
> - **ÔûÂ FOLLOW-UP ÔÇö owner found a LIVE sibling host (2026-06-17):** the official USFWS Wetlands Mapper renders fine from `fwsprimary.wim.usgs.gov` (sibling of our down `fwspublicservices`). So the data is live ÔÇö only our specific host is erroring, and the fix is likely a one-line URL swap. Handed off as **B130** (pending an egress allowlist + in-browser verification). `lib/vectorLayers.js` stays unwired groundwork (unchanged).

### B98 ÔÇö Stop the parcel popup from firing on every click; declutter it `[Site Planner / map]` (bug)
`[x]` Verified already satisfied + completed by B99/B100 (2026-06-16). The behavioral asks were already
met in the code: **(1)** `startMoveParcel` early-returns unless `tool === "select"` (SitePlanner.jsx),
so clicking a parcel while a draw/measure tool is active goes to the **tool**, not the parcel ÔÇö it does
NOT fire while drawing. **(2)** Parcel info already shows in the **left side panel** (opened on
select-tool click), not a re-appearing popover; the map uses hover **tooltips** + click-to-open-site
(no Leaflet `bindPopup` on parcels). **(3)** "Trim to essentials": the panel leads with area + geometry
check + **Active/Lock** (added in B99/B100), and the full county appraisal fields are already behind an
"all fields" expand. So no risky rework was warranted; the residual is cosmetic ordering best judged in a
live view. (If a specific popover is still observed on the live site, reopen with a repro ÔÇö couldn't find
one in the code.) Original spec:
inside parcels constantly, so it fires far too often and gets in the way; the on-parcel overlay is also
busy/unclear (line + vertex handles + length labels all at once). **Expected:**
- When a **draw/measure tool is active**, clicks go to the **tool** and do **NOT** open the popup.
- Parcel info opens only via an **explicit action** ÔÇö selecting with the Parcel/Select tool, a
  **double-click**, or a small **"i" button** ÔÇö and shows in a **side or pinned panel** rather than a
  popover that keeps reappearing.
- **Trim the popup to essentials** ÔÇö name/ID, area, status, active/inactive, lock state ÔÇö with details
  behind an **expand**.

> **B6** select tool). The "active/inactive" + "lock state" essentials come from **B100** / **B99**.

`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/blissful-babbage-liboyn`). Most of this was already in place ÔÇö
parcels are created `locked:true` (map import, draw, rect, split, merge, identify), locking already
guards vertex-drag / vertex-add / polygon-move, and a per-parcel **­ƒöô Lock / ­ƒöÆ Unlock** toggle lives
in the Parcel panel. Remaining work landed here: **removed the persistent ­ƒöÆ badge** from the saved-parcel
list row (state now reads from the panel toggle when a parcel is selected, not an always-on glyph). Lock
is a distinct field from active (B100). `locked` already persists per parcel in the Site Model `data`
(no version bump needed ÔÇö it's a per-parcel field, like `fill`/`setbacks`).
Original spec:
- **Remove the persistent lock badge** next to parcels.
- Treat **all parcels as locked by default** so geometry can't be moved or edited by accident.
- Provide an explicit per-parcel **"unlock to edit"** action; **re-lock** when done.
- **Lock (can the shape be edited) is independent of active/inactive (B100).** Two separate properties
  on a parcel.
> Cross-ref: the trimmed parcel popup (**B98**) surfaces lock state in its essentials. Persist lock
> per parcel on the Site Model (additive field ÔåÆ bump `SITE_MODEL_VERSION`, extend `migrate`, expose
> via a selector, per the conformance rule).

### B139 ÔÇö Detention pond "Expand this pond" mode (replaces B132's "Lock as existing pond") `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(arrived as "NEW-1", filed B133ÔåÆB137 in chat; renumbered B139 ÔÇö B137 collided via a concurrent merge with the already-done "Fort Bend parcels" bug, which keeps B137; this is the next free ID. Companion labels item is B140.)*
`[x]` Done 2026-06-17 (branch `claude/practical-noether-ln1f46`). Reframed pond enlargement around the goal and fixed the two confusions the owner hit (a second "Lock" button + no visible way to expand). Reuses B132's volume math + ghost ÔÇö no schema change.
- **Entry point:** replaced the buried "Lock as existing pond" with a primary, accent **"Expand this pond"** button directly under the Stored-volume readout, above the caveat. Clicking auto-snapshots the current pond as baseline (geometry + depth/freeboard/slope) and draws the dashed ghost ÔÇö baseline capture is now an automatic side effect, never its own button.
- **Explicit expand controls (steppers primary, drag secondary, one readout):** "Push banks out (ft)" applies a uniform OUTWARD offset of the footprint from the baseline ÔÇö new `expandPolygon()` (outward sibling of the inward-only `offsetPolygon`, with a self-intersection fallback ÔåÆ "drag instead") for irregular ponds, and `w/h += 2N` about the baseline centre for rectangles; "Dig deeper (ft)" raises Total depth (derived = depth ÔêÆ baseline). Free drag still works and feeds the same ExistingÔåÆProposed delta. "Reset to existing" reverts geometry + depth to baseline; "Done" exits keeping the new size (feeds downstream coverage/detention) and clears the ghost.
- **Naming-collision fixes:** the only "Lock" left is the generic element pin, relabeled **"­ƒôî Pin"/"­ƒôî Unpin"** (panel + right-click menu) ÔÇö no "lock" verb near the expansion entry; footprint plan dimension **"Depth" ÔåÆ "Length"** for ponds (so dimensions read Width / Length; "Total depth" is now the only "depth", and it's vertical).
- **Guards (amber, non-blocking):** warns when the expanded footprint overlaps a building/parking/trailer (`ringsOverlap`) or extends past the property line (new `pointInRing` vs. parcels); the over-taper case reuses **B60** (bottom area 0) and now surfaces "Side slopes meet before full depth". Steppers work even when the pond is pinned. Honest one-liner "Screening estimate ÔÇö confirm with your engineer" under the gain.
- **Verified (self, headless browser, 2026-06-17):** Expand button shown / old button gone / "Length" label / Pin relabel; enter mode ÔåÆ steppers + Existing/Proposed/gain 0; push banks out 40ÔÇ▓ ÔåÆ uniform grow + ghost even on all four sides, **+18.33 ac-ft**; dig deeper +6ÔÇ▓ ÔåÆ **85.17 ac-ft**; Reset ÔåÆ 0; Done ÔåÆ keeps size; Pin toggles; **zero console errors.** lint 0 errors ┬À **202 tests** ┬À build green ┬À `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. Browser pass recorded as **VERIFICATION V30** (supersedes V25).
- **One deviation from the brief:** Pin/Delete kept as the existing buttons (relabeled), not relocated into the Section header ÔÇö that's a shared all-element-types layout change, deferred as not worth the regression risk for this pond feature.
> Replaces **B132**'s entry point (B132 stays Done as the original). Companion **B140** (on-map pond area labels) stays Open. The "Length" rename + Pin relabel touch the shared element panel ÔÇö coordinated with **B121**.

### B100 ÔÇö Active / Inactive parcels driving calculations `[Site Planner]` (feature)
`[x]` Done 2026-06-16 (branch `claude/blissful-babbage-liboyn`). Per-parcel `active` flag (default
**active**; missing = active, so existing sites are unchanged until a parcel is toggled off). New
`activeParcelsOf` selector + a unit test. `siteSqft` now sums **active parcels only**, so every
downstream metric (coverage, FAR, impervious, detention %, open space) follows automatically. Added an
**Ô£ô Active / Ôù» Inactive** toggle in the Parcel panel (beside Lock) + `toggleParcelActive`; inactive
parcels render **dimmed + dashed** on the canvas (visible for context, clearly excluded) and show
"┬À inactive" in the saved-parcel list; the Yield panel notes "Excludes N inactive parcel(s)." No
`SITE_MODEL_VERSION` bump ÔÇö `active` is a per-parcel field (defaulted at read, like `locked`/`fill`),
so no migration is needed. **Scope note:** the *map's* site-acreage label (MapFinder) intentionally still
shows the full footprint (all parcels) ÔÇö B100 targets the planner's yield/coverage/detention math.
Original spec:
- Add a per-parcel **Active/Inactive** toggle.
- **All area-based calculations ÔÇö yield, coverage, detention, and similar ÔÇö count ONLY active parcels.**
- Inactive parcels stay **visible for context** but render **dimmed or with diagonal-line shading** so
  it's obvious they're excluded.
- **New parcels default to Active.**
- Expose the toggle in the **parcel info panel (B98)**, and ideally **inline** on the parcel itself.
> Cross-ref: independent of lock (**B99**). The "active only" rule must thread through the
> yield/coverage/detention math (the `siteSqft` / `developableArea` inputs) ÔÇö additive Site Model field
> ÔåÆ bump `SITE_MODEL_VERSION`, extend `migrate`, expose via a selector; the self-intersection/area
> guards from **B81/B86** feed the same totals, so keep them in step.

### B417 ÔÇö Trailer parking generated on non-dock sides `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-1", with screenshots; first minted B416 ÔåÆ renumbered **B417** after concurrent PR #313 took B416 for Split-a-parcel)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped 2026-06-23 via PR #314 (branch `claude/exciting-allen-i2eq2k`) ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 1285 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V120 (`ui-audit/verify-b416-b417.mjs`).** Branch/PR/commits + code/test tags + the harness filename retain the originally-minted B416/B417 (collision-renumber convention).
- **Repro (owner screenshots):** a 580ÔÇ▓(w) ├ù 664ÔÇ▓(h) building. Because h > w the dock sides resolve to **left/right**, yet a 135ÔÇ▓ truck-court stack (court ÔåÆ trailer ÔåÆ buffer) sat **stranded on the TOP** ÔÇö a non-dock short side. Trailer parking must appear ONLY on dock faces (`dockSidesFor(b).dockSides`): single-load ÔåÆ one dock side, cross-dock ÔåÆ both, never a non-dock side.
- **Root cause (evidence-first ÔÇö the filed hypothesis was off):** the named suspect (the legacy opposite-dock trailer row `OPP_TRAILER_D` / `oppTrailerGeom` / `fitWallTrailer`) was **already dead**, gated on an `oppSide` tag nothing creates. The REAL leak: a dock-zone stack is **pinned to the side it was created on** (`truckCourt.side`); when a building's long-side axis flips (a reshape) or its dock preset changes, `relayoutAllSides` re-laid the stack on the now-non-dock side and nothing pruned it. `trailerSides` from the old inline `dockSidesOf` was confirmed unread and dropped.
- **Fix:** new pure, unit-tested `lib/dockZones.js` helpers ÔÇö `dockSidesFor` (one shared rule for canvas/panel/depth/guard), `strandedZoneIds`, `pruneStrandedZones` ÔÇö prune a stranded stack **on reshape commit** (pointer-up, so one undo restores size + apron), **on dock-preset change** (`changeBuildingDock`), and **on plan open** (`useState` init ÔåÆ existing stranded plans self-heal). Removed the dead opp-trailer path. +10 dockZones unit tests incl. the requested "zero trailer elements on a non-dock side" for BOTH presets; headless V120 (stranded-on-top ÔåÆ pruned; identical stack on a real dock side ÔåÆ kept, so the prune is targeted).
- **Deduped:** net-new; REUSES the B228/B229/B239/B242/B246 dock-zone machinery (consistent, not a fork); shares `dockSidesFor` with B418 but an independent root cause + test path.

### B418 ÔÇö Building "depth" measured against the wrong reference `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-23 as "NEW-2", with screenshots; first minted B417 ÔåÆ renumbered **B418** after the PR #313 collision)*
`[x]` **Fixed + shipped 2026-06-23 via PR #314 ÔÇö lint 0 ┬À 1285 tests ┬À build green ┬À headless V120.**
- **Repro:** the same 580├ù664 building surfaced a 135ÔÇ▓ "depth-like" dimension reading like the truck-court depth, not the building's wall-to-wall depth. Expected: depth = the footprint span perpendicular to the dock face (cross: dock wall ÔåÆ dock wall; single: dock wall ÔåÆ rear wall) ÔÇö never the frontage, never an attached apron / truck court / trailer / buffer span.
- **Root cause (evidence-first):** the building's OWN depth dimension was already correct ÔÇö `min(w,h)` IS the dock-normal extent, because docks always ride the long walls (580ÔÇ▓ here). The bogus 135ÔÇ▓ in the screenshot is the **stranded court's own red depth dimension** (B417's fault) over the building's depth axis. Shared root cause; B417's prune removes the 135ÔÇ▓.
- **Fix:** hardened the building depth so it can only come from the footprint ÔÇö new pure `footprintDepth(el)` reads depth off `dockSidesFor` (the dock-normal span, building-only) and is now the source for the on-canvas depth dimension (was an implicit `min(w,h)`). +4 unit tests incl. "depth excludes an attached 135ÔÇ▓ court".
- **Deduped:** net-new; shares `dockSidesFor` with B417 but an independent code path (the dimension renderer + `footprintDepth`) and test path.

### B149 ÔÇö Hide fine-detail annotations when zoomed out (level-of-detail tiering) `[Site Planner / UI]` (feature) ÔÇö Ô£à DONE  *(arrived as "NEW-3"; minted B149 ÔÇö next free ID after B148. NEW-2, which arrived alongside, was folded into the open B121 rather than separately minted. Includes the 2026-06-23 owner amendment "sidewalk callout still paints at site-overview zoom" ÔÇö folded in here, NOT a new B#.)*
`[x]` At a zoomed-out site-overview level, fine infrastructure dimensions ÔÇö sidewalk widths, road/drive-aisle widths, short edge dimensions ÔÇö no longer render; they reveal progressively as the user zooms in. Only the high-level labels (building name + SF, and the site-summary chip from **B148**) stay visible at overview zoom.
- **Original ask / recommended mechanism:** tag each annotation with a **tier** ÔÇö *overview* (building names/SF, site totals) / *site* (building-footprint dims, parking-module dims) / *detail* (sidewalk, drive-aisle/road widths, sub-aisle dims) ÔÇö and gate visibility by current map scale, made **self-tuning** by a **minimum on-screen length**: a width callout only draws when the feature it measures projects to at least ~40 px on screen (resolution-independent; no hand-tuned zoom-step breakpoints).
- **2026-06-23 owner amendment (folded in):** at site-overview zoom the **"5ÔÇ▓ Sidewalk"** centred label and the fine width/edge dimension callouts (e.g. the "30ÔÇ▓/40ÔÇ▓" drive/aisle ticks bracketing the sidewalk run) **still painted** ÔÇö illegible clutter. Locked decision: **drop the label + width dimension only; KEEP the thin strip geometry** (a 5ÔÇ▓ strip is ~1 px at that scale; pop-in/out of the geometry on zoom would flicker ÔÇö geometry-hiding is a separate scope, not silently added). Edge case respected: this must NOT also suppress the building name/SF or the B148 site-summary chip (overview tier ÔÇö always visible).
> **Ôÿà SHIPPED + VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (branch `claude/nice-cannon-0zbom0`).** Implemented the tier gating on **B121's engine** (`lib/labelLayout.js`), reusing the existing `dimCalloutVisible(ppf)` / `DIM_CALLOUT_MIN_PPF` gate rather than a parallel one:
> - New pure helper **`detailLabelVisible(featureFt, ppf)`** + **`DETAIL_LABEL_MIN_PX`** = `dimCalloutVisible(ppf) && featureFt*ppf >= DETAIL_LABEL_MIN_PX` ÔÇö the shared dimension-callout zoom gate is the FLOOR, refined by B149's min-on-screen-length rule keyed on the feature the callout measures.
> - **Detail tier:** the sidewalk/landscape/buffer **width labels** (the `el.type === "sidewalk" || "landscape"` branch in `SitePlanner.jsx` ÔÇö skipped from the label-candidate pool when hidden, so the geometry stays) **and** the **paving/road** red edge-dimension **widths** (`renderElPx`). **Site tier:** a building's footprint depth stays on `dimCalloutVisible` (unchanged). **Overview tier:** building name/SF + the site-summary chip ÔÇö never zoom-gated. A SELECTED paving/road keeps its dimension so the click-to-edit / drag handle never vanishes mid-edit (buildings keep their exact prior behaviour).
> - **Calibrated in-browser (B149 says tune it live):** the planner hard-caps zoom at **ppf 8**. At 40 px the narrowest real strip (5ÔÇ▓ sidewalk) would only reveal at exactly 8.0 ppf (the max, zero headroom, float-boundary flicker), so `DETAIL_LABEL_MIN_PX` was tuned to **30 px** ÔåÆ the 5ÔÇ▓ strip reveals at ppf 6 with headroom, while staying hidden at site-overview (~0.2ÔÇô0.5 ppf) and working zoom (0.35). Wider strips reveal proportionally sooner (self-tuning).
> - lint 0 errors ┬À **1286 tests** (labelLayout `detailLabelVisible` added, threshold-agnostic) ┬À build green ┬À `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact. **Headless self-verify V119** (`ui-audit/verify-b149-sidewalk-lod.mjs`, 12/12): at overview the 5ÔÇ▓ Sidewalk + 25ÔÇ▓ Landscape labels and the 37ÔÇ▓ paving dim are ABSENT while "Building 1 / 240,000 sf" PERSISTS, and each reveals at its own predicted zoom (paving 0.92 < landscape 1.45 < sidewalk ~6 ppf) on zoom-in, building name/SF present at every step.
> **Dedup / reconciliation:** generalized B121's crude global `dimCalloutVisible` gate into the tier + per-feature min-on-screen-length rule and extended it to the standalone fine-infra callouts; **B121's "Label priority / level-of-detail" bullet was tracked here and is now delivered.** B121's remaining scope is the *collision* pool at detail zoom (separate, still open) ÔÇö good tiering means its anti-overlap now only runs on whatever's visible. Reused **B30**'s pixel-projected (not feet-based) threshold precedent.

### B438 ÔÇö GIS layer IMAGERY caching, browser-side (service worker + IndexedDB) `[Site Planner / GIS]` ÔÇö SUPERSEDED by B445  *(2026-06-24)*
`[x]` Built the browser-side approach (host-scoped service worker `public/gis-sw.js` caching cross-origin ArcGIS opaque exports + IndexedDB blob SWR `lib/gisImageCache.js` + pure matcher `lib/gisSwRules.js`, headless-verified). **Reversed the same session** on the owner's decision ÔÇö "I don't want it to live in the browser": a per-browser cache doesn't follow you between computers and isn't the professional home for "don't depend on a down government server." Replaced by **B445** (server-side, Drive-backed). Retirement (B438 HAD shipped to production via PR #326, so this actively removes the deployed worker): `gis-sw.js` became a self-unregistering tombstone (deletes its `planyr-gis-*` caches + unregisters itself ÔÇö already-deployed browsers re-check the SW file on navigation and self-destruct), `registerGisSw`ÔåÆ`retireGisSw` (unregisters any prior worker on boot, belt-and-suspenders), and `gisImageCache.js`/`gisSwRules.js` + their tests + `ui-audit/verify-b438-gis-sw.mjs` were deleted.

### B445 ÔÇö GIS layer imagery caching, server-side (Drive-backed, cross-device) `[Site Planner / GIS]` ÔÇö SHIPPED  *(2026-06-24, branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`; renumbered from B439 on 2026-06-24 to clear a collision with the breadcrumb rename/delete B439/B440)*
`[x]` Replaces B438. The map points raster (export-image) layers at a same-origin Cloudflare Pages Function `/api/gis-cache/svc/<b64url(serviceUrl)>/ÔÇª` instead of straight at the agency. The function (thin route `functions/api/gis-cache/[[path]].js` + testable core `functions/api/gis-cache/_handler.js`):
- reconstructs the real upstream URL (`gisProxyCore.parseUpstream`, host-allow-listed so it's not an open relay),
- serves a durable copy from Google Drive INSTANTLY when present (survives an agency outage),
- refreshes that copy in the background once past the TTL (stale-while-revalidate; `gisProxyCore.freshness`, default 24h),
- on cache-miss fetches the agency once (server-side ÔåÆ no CORS wall), serves it, stores in the background (`Planyr/giscache/` Drive folder, files named by `gisProxyCore.cacheKey`; create-then-delete-dupes),
- and **FAILS OPEN** (302 ÔåÆ real upstream) on missing Drive creds / agency error / any unexpected error, so a layer can never break.
Client (`layers.js`): raster layers route through the proxy by default (`gisProxyEnabled`, kill switch `VITE_GIS_PROXY=0`), with a **one-shot fall back to the direct agency URL** on `requesterror` so layers render even where the function isn't deployed (e.g. vite preview) ÔÇö caching is a pure enhancement. Age badge: on `load`, `reportCacheAge` reads the proxy export URL + calls the `?meta=1` sidecar ÔåÆ existing onStatus `{ts,stale}` channel (the panel already renders "as of Xm ago"). `layerRequest.js` gained an opt-in `{proxy}` transport swap (still passes the full pinned sublayer set ÔÇö the coverage hard-rule is untouched). `driveClient.findFile` added (one targeted lookup, newest-first). Reuses the live Drive creds (`GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/REFRESH_TOKEN`, server-side only) ÔÇö no Supabase, no new secret. Tests: `gisProxyCore` 15 + `gisCacheHandler` 12 (in-memory Drive + fake fetch + clock: missÔåÆfetch+serve+store, hit-freshÔåÆno-fetch, hit-staleÔåÆserve+bg-refresh, fail-open 302s, meta age, origin/host guards). Full suite 1417 green, lint 0 errors, build green. **Live-verify pending (V129)** ÔÇö sandbox can't reach planyr.io; on a signed-in browser confirm layers paint from the proxy + survive a simulated agency outage.

### B562 ÔÇö Grid keyboard column nav steps onto hidden columns; cell selection outline disappears `[Schedule]` (bug) ÔÇö Ô£à DONE
*(owner-reported 2026-06-29; fixed + verified same session)*

**Symptom:** In the Schedule **Grid** view, on a project whose Columns chooser hides columns that sit
*between* two visible ones (the reporter's layout hides Health/Status/Owner, which live immediately
left of Cost in the master registry), clicking a Cost cell then pressing **ArrowLeft** made the blue
cell-selection outline **vanish** for ~3 presses before it reappeared on Successor. Same defect on
ArrowRight off Successor, on Tab / Shift+Tab, on Shift+Arrow range extension, and on Tab / Shift+Tab
while editing a cell.

**Root cause (`public/sequence/index.html`):** the cell cursor `selectedColIdx` is an index into the
**master** column registry `COLS` (16 cols), but the grid renders only the project's **visible** columns
in display order and draws the outline where `COLS.indexOf(c) === selectedColIdx`. Navigation stepped
`selectedColIdx` by ┬▒1 through the master registry (clamped only at 0 and the highest visible index),
so it landed on hidden columns ÔÇö which have no rendered cell, so no outline. The lower clamp
`Math.max(0, i-1)` also targeted master index 0 (`id`), itself often hidden.

**Fix:** every column move now walks the **visible columns in display order**, not raw `COLS` ┬▒1.
- New pure, unit-tested module `src/workspaces/scheduler/lib/gridColNav.js`
  (`visibleColMasterIdxs` / `snapToVisible` / `stepVisibleColByIdx` / `stepVisibleCol`); the algorithm
  is mirrored inline in `public/sequence/index.html` (`stepVisibleColIdx` + `visibleColIdxs` /
  `stepGridCol`) because that standalone Babel page can't import from `src/`.
- Routed through it: plain Arrow Left/Right, Tab/Shift+Tab, the Shift+Arrow range `c2`, and the three
  in-cell editing Tab handlers (via a `tabStep` helper + a `colMasterIdxs` prop on `Cell`, replacing the
  old end-only `maxColIdx`/`colCap` clamp). `lastGridColIdx` removed (superseded).
- Cursor stays a master index (smallest blast radius ÔÇö `COLS[selectedColIdx]` reads and the
  `selectedColIdx === ci` render check keep working unchanged).
- **Secondary (same root):** a GridView effect snaps `selectedColIdx` to the nearest visible column if
  its column gets hidden via the Columns chooser (or the initial `id` column is hidden), so the outline
  never silently vanishes on a layout change.
- Also fixed the horizontal scroll-into-view calc, which summed *hidden* columns' widths (`COLS.slice`)
  and could scroll to the wrong place ÔÇö now measured from the rendered display order (`colOrder`).

**Reference already correct:** the Dashboard table (`MasterView.handleTableKey`) indexes into
`activeCols` directly (visible-column space) ÔÇö the grid now matches that model.

**Verified:** `test/gridColNav.test.js` (20 unit tests); full suite **1813** green; lint 0 errors;
build green; headless `ui-audit/verify-grid-hidden-col-nav.mjs` **12/12** (seeds the hidden
Health/Status/Owner layout, asserts ArrowLeft from Cost keeps the outline and lands on Successor, plus
clamps, plain Tab, and in-cell Shift+Tab). ÔåÆ V173.
