## ­ƒôª Archived from BACKLOG.md ÔÇö 2026-07-02 (token-efficiency cleanup)

> These items were marked shipped (`[x]`) but still sat in the live `BACKLOG.md` Open section. Moved here verbatim so the live file loads fast. No content changed.

### B610 ÔÇö Silent-failure gap: a filed drawing whose Drive keyÔåöid mapping write failed read as "saved Ô£ô" then went missing on next open `[Server / storage]` (bug)  *(surfaced 2026-07-02 when Michael reopened the old storage-adapter/Drive handoff ÔÇö the NEW-1ÔÇªNEW-4 vision ÔÇö and asked "have we drifted"; the review found this live bug; minted **B610** = highest real B# across both files (B609) + 1; branch `claude/storage-adapter-drive-discussion-6m5236`. Extends the **B209** no-silent-failure guarantee to the durability-mapping layer ÔÇö folds INTO B209, not a parallel mechanism.)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Root cause:** after Drive bytes uploaded OK, the durable Planyr-keyÔåöDrive-id write (`public.drive_files`) was **best-effort** ÔÇö `adapter.save` fired `idMap.bind(...)` and discarded the result (`server/storage/adapter.js`); `idStoreSupabase.set` only `console.warn`ed on a failed write and returned `undefined`; the resumable-upload COMMIT (`functions/api/files/resumable.js`) ignored the outcome too. So a failed mapping write still reported `ok:true` ÔåÆ the upload showed "saved Ô£ô", but nothing could resolve the file ÔåÆ `downloadFromDrive` missed it and the drawing was silently gone next open. Exactly the class B209/NEW-4 exists to prevent.
- **Fix (honest failure + best-effort rollback, no orphan):** `idStoreSupabase.set` now returns `{ok}`; `idMap.bind` normalizes any store result to `{ok}` (a legacy `undefined`-returning set = success ÔåÆ back-compat, so only an explicit `{ok:false}` rolls back). `adapter.save` checks the bind result and, on `{ok:false}`, rolls the just-uploaded bytes back (`backend.remove`) and returns a visible `fail(...)` ÔÇö the client then falls back to Supabase (small files) or flags re-drop (large), truthful and orphan-free. The resumable COMMIT does the analogous Drive-file rollback (`client.del(fileId)`) and returns 502.
- **Also (lower severity):** `reviewStore.deleteReview` swallowed Storage/Drive cleanup failures yet still reported `ok` ÔåÆ orphaned bytes, silently. It now returns `{orphaned, cleanupFailed}` and the Library shows a dismissible "a copy may linger ÔÇö remove it in Google Drive" notice (data isn't lost; the row delete stays authoritative ÔåÆ a notice, not a red banner).
- **Tests:** `test/storageAdapter.test.js` (rollback on mapping-failure + back-compat), `test/idStoreSupabase.test.js` (`set` returns `{ok:false}`/`{ok:true}`), `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` anti-drift guard. lint 0 ┬À full suite **2163 green** ┬À build green.
- **Verified:** everything runnable here is green. The signed-in Drive round-trip (force a `drive_files` write failure ÔåÆ honest failure + no orphaned Drive file) is auth-gated + Drive-backed, so it's logged as **V191**.

### B611 ÔÇö Share-by-link: expose the built link-provider seam (Drive-native link now; Planyr signed links a one-place switch later) `[Server / storage + Library]` (feature)  *(surfaced 2026-07-02 in the same storage-adapter review ÔÇö NEW-3; owner approved building it; minted **B611** = B610 + 1; same branch. Folds INTO the **B208** link-provider abstraction ÔÇö this ships its missing HTTP surface + UI, not a new mechanism.)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.*** `adapter.shareLink` ÔåÆ `linkProvider` ÔåÆ `driveBackend.shareLink` (Drive `webViewLink`) already existed (B208) but nothing reached them.
- **Endpoint:** new `functions/api/files/share.js` ÔÇö `POST /api/files/share?key=ÔÇª` (a POST, not a GET branch: minting the link is a mutation that grants a public "anyone with the link" permission). Auth + backend gating mirror `/api/files`; honest statuses (401 / 503 / 404-no-Drive-mapping / 502). The future `planyr.io/s/<token>` signed link stays a one-place switch via `PLANYR_LINK_KIND`.
- **Client:** `reviewStore.getShareLink(driveKey)` next to the other `/api/files*` fetchers ÔÇö honest `{ok,url,error}`.
- **UI:** a per-file **Share** button in the Library file list (`FileBrowser.jsx`), inline two-step (no `window.prompt`, per the owner rule): a confirm strip stating "anyone with this link can view ┬À Google Drive link ┬À un-share in Drive" ÔåÆ a copyable link (read-only field + Copy) or an honest inline error. Single-drawing only: a multi-sheet (stitched) set gets an honest "share the whole set from Drive directly" message rather than a misleading one-sheet link.
- **Decisions (owner-approvable):** single-drawing share only for now; no in-app un-share (done in Drive); link-gen failure never silent.
- **Tests:** `test/driveShare.test.js` (handler 400/401/503/404) + adapter & driveBackend shareLink round-trip in `test/storageAdapter.test.js`. lint 0 ┬À suite green ┬À build green.
- **Verified:** logged-out here can't mint a Drive link (auth + Drive), so the signed-in click-through (Share ÔåÆ confirm ÔåÆ a `drive.google.com` link copies; incognito open renders the PDF; a Supabase-fallback file shows "not in Drive") is logged as **V192**.

### B603 ÔÇö Elevation / hillshade map overlay never painted: wrong 3DEP rasterFunction name `[Site Planner / GIS]` (bug)  *(owner-dropped 2026-07-01 ÔÇö "debug and fix the elevation layer"; minted **B603** = highest real B# across both files (B602) + 1; branch `claude/elevation-layer-debug-s8xhdx`)*
`[x]` **Fixed + verified this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Root cause:** the `elevation` overlay config (`lib/layers.js`) rendered the USGS 3DEP ImageServer with `rendering: "Elevation Tinted Hillshade"`. That string is the USGS **press-release prose** wording ÔÇö it is NOT one of the service's published `rasterFunctionInfos[].name`. ArcGIS ImageServer `exportImage` **errors on any rasterFunction it doesn't recognise** (returns an error tile), so the overlay came back **blank / went to the "failed" status dot** with a misleading "service not responding" message (the service was fine; the name was wrong). The cross-section tool was unaffected ÔÇö it samples via `getSamples`, which takes no rendering rule.
- **Fix (one line + guard comment):** `rendering: "Hillshade Elevation Tinted"` ÔÇö the exact 3DEP template name. Its siblings on the same service are `Hillshade Gray`, `Hillshade Gray-Stretch`, `Hillshade Multidirectional`, `Slope Map`, `Aspect Map` ÔÇö all `Hillshade ÔÇª`/verb-first order; the prose reorders the words for readability. Confirmed verbatim against a **working USGS 3DEP WMS layer config** (`Hillshade%20Elevation%20Tinted`). Added a comment so nobody "corrects" it back to the prose form.
- **Why it wasn't caught earlier:** the value has been wrong since the layer config was first authored (the monorepo-restructure commit); the request-shaping (`imageLayerOptions`), the proxy transport swap, and the esri-leaflet `imageMapLayer` wiring are all correct ÔÇö only the template name was off, and nothing asserted it.
- **Tests:** new `test/bugHuntGuards.test.js` **B603** source guard (config carries the exact template name, never the prose ordering); the existing `test/layerRequest.test.js` already locks the `rendering ÔåÆ renderingRule:{rasterFunction}` transform. lint 0 ┬À full suite **2148 green** ┬À build green.
- **Verified:** everything runnable without a browser is green (above). The **live tinted-hillshade paint over the aerial** can't be exercised here ÔÇö `elevation.nationalmap.gov` is blocked by the sandbox egress policy (403) ÔÇö so it's logged as **V189** for a teammate with an unrestricted browser (it is NOT auth-gated; the overlay works logged-out).

### B599 ÔÇö Centerline road model + multi-point drawing + legacy migration `[Site Planner / drawing]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-1"; minted **B599** = highest real B# across both files after merging `origin/main` (B598) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B596** that a concurrent `main` took for the parcel-merge-conflict item; the branch `claude/centerline-road-model-hxi45g`, its commits/PR #459, and ALL in-code labels keep the provisional **B596**)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.*** `type:"road"` evolves from a rotated rectangle to a **centerline polyline** ÔÇö `{ type:"road", pts:[{x,y}ÔÇª], travelW, curb, roadClass, vtx:[{treatment,radius?}ÔÇª] }` ÔÇö staying an editable element in `els[]` (the immutable-backdrop model is untouched).
- **Drawing:** click to drop centerline points; **Enter / double-click finishes** (routed through the shared `finishActiveDrawing()`, B103 ÔÇö no separate finish path), **Backspace** removes the last point (`removeLastVertex()`), **Esc** cancels, **right-click** cancels too. A **2-point road is the old straight road** (the fixed-width preset is now the degenerate case). Honors `snapPt` + Shift-45 (`snap45`) like the other poly tools. `pushHistory()` before commit ÔåÆ one undo of the whole road. The free-draw rectangle road mode is left as-is (out of scope; retire later).
- **Migration (schema v9ÔåÆv10, additive):** on load `migrateRoad` converts a legacy rect road ÔåÆ a 2-point centerline ÔÇö endpoints A/B from `cx,cy,w,h,rot` (long axis = `max(w,h)`), `pts=[A,B]`, carry `travelW`/`curb`, `vtx=[]` (sharp), `roadClass` default. Idempotent (skips a road that already has `pts`); a **bonded dock-layer road (`attachedTo`) is left as a rect** (the relayout engine still owns it); un-migrated legacy stays readable. A migrated straight road renders **identically** (the centerline strip == the old rect). The element keeps a **synced AABB bbox** (`roadStripBBox`, rot:0) so every generic geometry consumer (fit / flush-snap / group bbox / ring tests) works unchanged while the road-specific code reads `pts`.
- **Live preview:** the in-progress draft shows the tessellated centerline **+ the provisional pavement+curb offset strip** as points are placed (cursor = tentative next point).
- **Files:** `lib/siteModel.js` (`rectRoadEndpoints`/`roadStripBBox`/`migrateRoad`/`migrateRoads`, `SITE_MODEL_VERSION=10`); `SitePlanner.jsx` (draftRoadPts flow, `finishRoad`, road-aware `shiftEl` threaded through nudge/move/group-move/paste/duplicate, per-vertex render & handles).
- **Dedup:** the existing rectangle-road items (**B61, B70, B102, B110, B121, B146**) all concern the rotated-rect road and keep their curb / travel-width / dimension behavior ÔÇö curved multi-point geometry is **new**, no conflict. The **B70 curb contract is preserved** (width = travel width; 6ÔÇ│ curb each side; 24ÔÇ▓ travel = 25ÔÇ▓ wide) and the **B146 travel-width dimension callout + click-to-edit** still work (see B601).
- **Verified:** lint 0 ┬À **2128 tests** green ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-centerline-road.mjs` 8/8 (migration render + UI draw + vertex-drag). Auth-only cloud round-trip = **V186**.

### B600 ÔÇö Road curve geometry: per-vertex sharp/arc/smooth + feasibility-clamped radius `[Site Planner / drawing]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-2"; minted **B600** = B599 + 1; depends on B599 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B597** a concurrent `main` took for the site-rename-revert item; in-code labels keep the provisional **B597**)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.*** New **pure, dependency-free** `lib/roadGeometry.js` + `test/roadGeometry.test.js`, matching the repo's pure-lib pattern (`metesAndBounds.js`, `polygonSplit.js`) ÔÇö **no JSTS** (B150 avoided it deliberately).
- `roadCenterline(pts, vtx, { defaultRadius })` ÔåÆ a **tessellated dense polyline** (the rendered centerline) that B601 offsets. Per interior vertex `treatment`:
  - **Sharp** ÔÇö radius 0, hard corner (vertex unchanged). `sharp == input polyline`.
  - **Arc** ÔÇö circular **fillet tangent to both segments**, radius **feasibility-clamped** so the run-up `T = R┬Àtan(╬©/2)` never exceeds half the shorter adjacent segment (╬© = deflection angle), emitted as short tessellated segments. **Default treatment.**
  - **Smooth** ÔÇö vertex is a **through-point of a Catmull-Rom-style interpolating curve** (tangent from neighbours), tessellated ÔÇö for tracing curves off an aerial.
- Treatment is **per-vertex and convertible after drawing** (pen-tool style): click a road vertex on the canvas to select it, then flip **sharp Ôåö arc Ôåö smooth** in the Element panel (Arc seeds the class default radius, editable). No global tool mode, no spacing threshold ÔÇö dense clicks + the clamp produce smoothness naturally. `minRadiusOfCurvature` (circumradius of consecutive triples) backs the B602 civil check, uniformly for arc and traced/smooth runs.
- **Tests (21):** clamp never overruns a segment; arc tangent at both ends; spline passes through its points; sharp == polyline; tessellation density scales with `tessDeg`; min-radius recovers a known circle + finds the tightest along a mixed alignment; + the B602 class-threshold math.

### B601 ÔÇö Road surface + curb + dimension render from the centerline (reuse offset; no JSTS) `[Site Planner / drawing]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-3"; minted **B601** = B600 + 1; depends on B599/B600 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B598** a concurrent `main` took for the parcel-draw-mode item; in-code labels keep the provisional **B598**)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.*** The road surface, curbs and dimension all derive from `pts` via the **shared offset primitives in `lib/metesAndBounds.js`** ÔÇö **no geometry dependency added.** The tessellated centerline feeds the offset, so the miter-clamped joins only ever see gentle per-segment angles (their tuned regime).
- **Surface + curbs:** pavement+curb **outer ring via `bufferPolyline(roadCenterline(ÔÇª), travelW + 2┬Àcurb)`**; the two **curb stripe lines via `offsetPolyline(roadCenterline(ÔÇª), ┬▒travelW/2)`** (face-of-curb). Replaces the rect-based `el.type==="road"` render branch with a centerline branch (early-returns before the rect-only code) ÔÇö curb striping **follows the offset edges**, not the old `el.w>=el.h` axis logic. **B70 curb contract preserved** (24ÔÇ▓ travel = 25ÔÇ▓ wide strip).
- **Dimension:** the travel-width callout is **anchored to the centerline midpoint** (perpendicular to the local direction), **excludes the curb** (reads the true travel width), **click-to-edit** (`editDimWidth` ÔåÆ re-offsets live), and **drag-to-move** (free 2-D offset + a dashed leader back to the centerline, since a curved alignment isn't a single rect to slide along). B149 detail-tier zoom gating retained; kept visible while selected.
- **Yield/area:** uses the **generated strip polygon area** (`roadStripArea`, replaces `w├ùh`) in both the yield rollup and the Element-panel footprint; the curb is already inside the strip (no double count). Cost takeoff (`roadLengthOf`) reads the centerline length.

### B602 ÔÇö Road class + civil design parameters: defaults + non-blocking min-radius warning `[Site Planner / Setup ÔÇö Roads]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-4"; minted **B602** = B601 + 1; depends on B600 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B599** (which is now this item's predecessor's neighbour); in-code labels keep the provisional **B599**)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.*** A **road class** on the road element + an editable class config table `settings.roadClasses` (Setup ÔåÆ Roads, alongside `roadWidths`/`roadCurb`). New pure `lib/roadClasses.js`. Each class seeds a **default centerline radius** (the Arc default for new vertices) and a **min-radius warning threshold** ÔÇö **all editable; the seeds are starting points to verify against current AASHTO / local fire code, none authoritative or hardcoded.**
- **Seed classes:** Truck route (default ~120ÔÇ▓, warn < 50ÔÇ▓), Auto drive aisle (default ~25ÔÇ▓, warn < 24ÔÇ▓ ÔÇö the drawn-road default), **Public / collector** (speed-based: threshold from `R = V┬▓/[15(e+f)]`, Ôëê185ÔÇ▓ at 25 mph, editable design speed), Fire lane (default ~50ÔÇ▓, warn < 28ÔÇ▓ inside per IFC App. D), Custom (no threshold).
- **Civil check (non-blocking):** scans the **tessellated centerline for the minimum radius of curvature anywhere** (circumradius of each three consecutive points) and compares to the class threshold. Below ÔåÆ **`flashWarn` on commit/edit** AND a **persistent ÔÜá indicator in the road's Element panel** ("ÔÜá 35ÔÇ▓ radius ÔÇö below 50ÔÇ▓ min for Truck route"). **Never blocks or auto-corrects** ÔÇö surfaces it loudly, the user decides. Works uniformly for arc vertices and traced/smooth runs (it measures the resulting alignment, not how it was drawn).
- **Verified:** headless confirmed the panel shows the class selector + the exact ÔÜá text for a tight truck-class curve (`verify-centerline-road.mjs`).

### B597 ÔÇö Renaming a site snapped right back to the old name after Enter `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-30 on planyr.io ÔÇö "I renamed a site in the site planner module and pressed enter and it went right back to the old name"; minted **B597** = highest real B# across both files (B596) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B596 that a concurrent `main` took for the parcel-merge-conflict item; the commit/PR keep the provisional **B596** label; branch `claude/site-planner-rename-revert-dk6yoq`)*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified this session. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Root cause ÔÇö TWO independent revert bugs, both surfacing as "the name comes back":**
  1. **(primary) The header breadcrumb dropdown (planner view).** The project switcher owns a local list (`internalProjects`) yet the Site Planner ALSO supplies `onRenameProject`. `ProjectBreadcrumb.commitRename` only re-read that local list in its bare `else` branch, so when `onRenameProject` was present it wrote the new name to the store but never refreshed the list ÔÇö and a same-tab store write doesn't fire the native `storage` event ÔÇö so the just-edited row (and the crumb) kept the OLD name. An instant revert on essentially EVERY site rename.
  2. **The map's YOUR SITES list, multi-plan sites.** The list passes a *representative plan* id; `renameSiteGroup`/`renameSite` treated it as a group id. For a site whose newest plan isn't the group anchor, `loadPlansOfGroup(planId)` matched nothing ÔåÆ no save ÔåÆ revert (and, signed in, nothing pushed to the cloud).
- **Fix:**
  - `ProjectBreadcrumb.commitRename` now refreshes the local list + nudges the other `planarfit:sites` surfaces for BOTH branches whenever it is uncontrolled (Schedule's controlled mode still relies on its bridge prop).
  - `renameSiteGroup` (storage) and `renameSite` (SitePlannerApp) resolve the incoming id to its group (`groupOf(loadSite(id))`) before renaming + cloud-pushing, so a group id OR any plan id within it renames the whole site.
- **Tests:** +4 `test/storage.test.js` cases (rename by group id ┬À by a NON-anchor plan id ┬À single-plan ┬À unknown-id no-op).
- **Verified:** lint **0 errors** ┬À full suite **2106 green** (+4) ┬À build green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-rename-revert.mjs` **10/10 logged-out** (planner breadcrumb rename persists + survives reload; map multi-plan rename renames anchor AND sibling, no revert). The signed-in cloud round-trip (the rename reaching Supabase + surviving a cross-device pull) = **V187**.

### B594 ÔÇö Freshly drawn polyline (or any markup) vanishes mid-session: a per-tab id counter let a new item reuse a tombstoned id, which `mergeSiteContent` then stripped `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug) ÔÇö DATA-LOSS, critical  *(owner-reported 2026-06-30 on planyr.io ÔÇö "I drew a polyline and it disappearedÔÇª could it be a save/overwrite issue?"; arrived as "NEW-1"; minted **B594** = highest real B# across both files (B593) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B591 that a concurrent `main` took for the Scheduler-r3 item (and B592, which it took for the dim-slide item); the in-code comments, commit/PR titles, and the `verify-b591-polyline-id.mjs` harness filename keep the provisional **B591**; SAME symptom family as B134 but a DISTINCT, newly-found root cause ÔÇö see the fold note; branch `claude/polyline-save-overwrite-u3l49r`)*
`[x]` **Fixed + unit-verified this session ÔÇö the id-collision root cause. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Investigation (audit-FIRST, as the brief demanded): a 6-dimension parallel auditor sweep + a two-skeptic adversarial verify pass (refute-the-claim + check-the-fix) over the whole persistence layer.** 18 candidates raised, 15 refuted; **3 survivors all converged on ONE root cause** (a CONFIRMED verdict that I could not refute), correcting the owner-session's initial guess (a missing immediate local mirror ÔÇö already shipped as B458).
- **Root cause (NOT B134's #3 debounce-flush or #4 silent-error ÔÇö a sixth, previously-unidentified cause):** drawn-element ids came from a single per-tab module counter `uid() = "e"+_id++` (`SitePlanner.jsx`), and `ensureIdAbove` re-seeded that counter at load from **only `parcels`+`els`** ÔÇö never `markups`/`measures`/`callouts`/`deletedIds`. So the load-bearing invariant the tombstone system rests on (literally commented "ids are never reused, so tombstoning by id is safe") was **FALSE**: (a) after reopening a plan the counter could sit BELOW a markup id already deleted (its id sitting in `deletedIds`), so a freshly drawn polyline was minted with a **tombstoned** id; (b) two tabs of the same plan re-seeded identically and minted **colliding** ids. When `mergeSiteContent` then ran (cross-tab `storage` sync, take-over reconcile, or boot pull), its tombstone filter (`live()`, `siteModel.js`) **stripped the just-drawn polyline** ÔåÆ it vanished mid-session with no reload, and the thinned model was persisted to device + cloud (a later reload lost it again). The immediate B458 mirror is a sole-tab plain replace that KEEPS the polyline, so the B473 read-back's count check passed ÔÇö which is exactly why nothing alarmed at draw time (ÔåÆ B595).
- **Fix (the verifiers explicitly REJECTED the tempting fixes ÔÇö weakening `mergeSiteContent`'s tombstone filter / a `localOnlyIds` carve-out ÔÇö as regressing B276/B556/B480; the endorsed fix is unique ids):**
  - **New pure `src/shared/ids.js` (`createIdMinter(salt)` + `randomIdSalt()`).** `uid()` now carries a per-tab **salt** (6 random lowercase letters) appended to every id, so two tabs/sessions can never mint the same id and a fresh add can never equal a retained tombstone. Salt is **letters only**, so `seedAbove`'s `parseInt(replace(/\D/g,""))` still recovers the sequence number for ordering. Legacy short ids ("e5") keep working; no data migration.
  - **`ensureIdAbove` now seeds from EVERY id-bearing collection + tombstones** (markups/measures/callouts/deletedIds, not just parcels+els) at both call sites (restore + import) ÔÇö defense-in-depth + monotonic ordering.
  - **Comment at the tombstone helper updated** to record WHY reuse is now impossible (the salt), so the invariant can't be silently re-broken.
- **Tests (assertions land with the code, B432):** new `test/idMinter.test.js` (7) ÔÇö sequential salted mint, two-salt non-collision (the two-tab case), `seedAbove` digit-parse across mixed collections + tombstones, a reopen-low-counter case, `randomIdSalt` digit-free, AND an **end-to-end pair through the real `mergeSiteContent`**: a salted new markup whose numeric part matches an old tombstone is KEPT, while a CONTROL with the colliding bare id is stripped (proving the exact mechanism).
- **Verified:** `npm run lint` **0 errors** ┬À full suite **2072 green** (+7) ┬À `npm run build` green (lazy split intact; `src/shared/ids.js` folds into the planner chunk). The signed-in, two-tab live reproduction on planyr.io (sandbox is logged-out, cross-tab needs auth) = **V185**.
- **Dedup / fold vs B134:** same "work disappears" symptom FAMILY, but B134's open candidates were #3 (debounce-flush) and #4 (silent save error) ÔÇö this is a **distinct sixth root cause** (id reuse ÔåÆ tombstone strip). Kept as its own item with its own test path; B134 amended to cross-reference. Ships with **B595** (the detection half).

### B595 ÔÇö The "changes aren't being saved" notice fired AFTER the loss; the on-device read-back verify was count-only, blind to a same-count drop `[Site Planner / Persistence]` (bug)  *(owner-reported 2026-06-30 ÔÇö "the notification popped up too late"; arrived as "NEW-2"; minted **B595** = B594 + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional B592 (a concurrent `main` took B592 for the dim-slide item); in-code comments + commit/PR keep the provisional **B592**; same branch; ships with B594; the open half of B134's #4 + B125's loud-failure scope)*
`[x]` **Fixed this session ÔÇö membership-anchored read-back + a cross-tab merge tripwire. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Why the notice lagged (found in the same audit):** the immediate B458 mirror verifies the on-device write by **count** (`drawnCount got < want`). A same-count swap ÔÇö one item dropped while another lands, exactly the B594 tombstone/id-collision fold ÔÇö leaves `got==want`, so the count check is **blind** to it; the loss only surfaced later when the thinned cloud push tripped a CAS conflict / the user noticed. So the indicator was a lagging symptom, not an edge-of-loss alarm.
- **Fix:**
  - **Read-back now verifies by MEMBERSHIP, not just count:** `writeMirror` collects the ids it just wrote and confirms each one read back; a missing id flips the loud `localSaveFailed` banner **within the ~50ms mirror** (edit-anchored, not push-anchored) + records `save-verify-failed{ missingId }`. No cry-wolf ÔÇö it only fires when an id we literally just wrote is absent.
  - **Cross-tab merge tripwire:** the `storage` handler now asserts the union never drops a live id without a tombstone explaining it; if it ever does (a future regression of the B594 class), it goes LOUD to telemetry (`merge-dropped-live`) instead of silently shrinking the canvas. Reuses the B468 telemetry pattern.
  - **Cross-tab convergence signature is now id-membership, not collection counts** (`SitePlanner.jsx` `onStore`): a same-count remote swap (other tab deleted one item + added another) used to be skipped by the count-only `sig()`, leaving two tabs permanently divergent ÔÇö now they converge (and a pure remote delete re-hydrates).
- **Verified:** covered by the B594 gate (lint 0 ┬À 2072 tests green ┬À build green). The live signed-in confirmation that a genuinely non-landing save now flips the badge promptly is folded into **V185**.
- **Dedup:** folds into **B125** (loud-failure scope) + **B134 #4** (silent save error) rather than minting a parallel mechanism; the net-new ask ÔÇö detection must cover the **silent same-count no-op**, not only writes that throw ÔÇö is what this adds.

### B588 ÔÇö Marquee (box-select) tool: drag a rubber-band box on empty canvas to multi-select `[Site Planner + Document Review / shared selection]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-2"; minted **B588** = B587 + 1 ÔÇö renumbered repeatedly as fast-moving concurrent `main`s kept taking the next ID (B568ÔÇôB586 band) while this branch was open; the branch/commits/PR #442 and in-code `(B570)` labels keep the provisional number; same branch)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session alongside B587. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Audit result:** drag-on-empty-canvas in the default pointer **pans** in both workspaces (not a no-op), so a dedicated tool is the right call ÔÇö no conflict with the default pointer. The Site Planner's pre-existing marquee was a hidden Shift-drag sub-mode of Select (undiscoverable); this promotes box-select to a first-class rail tool in BOTH workspaces.
- **The tool:** a new `marquee` row in the shared `tools.matrix.js` (a pointer **mode**, like select/pan ÔÇö so the e2e conformance harness skips it cleanly, no property schema). A first-class rail button with its own glyph + `data-testid="tool-marquee"` + `aria-pressed` lit state (Site Planner inline rail; Document Review via the shared `ToolRail`, which already emits both). Shortcut **M** in the Site Planner. **Directly fixes the "can't tell which tool is selected" complaint** ÔÇö the active button is a solid lit fill, not a faint highlight.
- **Behavior:** with the tool active, drag on empty canvas draws a rubber-band (solid two-tone border + light neutral fill tint via the shared `SelectionChrome`); on release, every object the box **touches** (crossing ÔÇö the AutoCAD/Bluebeam default) is selected into the **same shared set B569 populates**, so multi-move / multi-delete / single-undo all come for free. After a box-select the tool hands the live set back to Select so you can immediately drag-move it.
- **Flagged, not built (per the brief):** the directional window-vs-crossing convention (left-to-right = enclosed-only) is supported by the shared `pickInMarquee(mode:"window")` but not wired to drag-direction ÔÇö a small follow-on; default stays crossing.
- **Verified:** part of the same two headless harnesses as B587 (Site Planner + Document Review), both PASSED logged-out; lint/test/build green; signed-in cloud-persistence confirm = V182.
- **Dedup:** the Site Planner's existing Shift-drag marquee is kept as a back-compat shortcut; this adds the discoverable rail tool the brief asked for, shared with Document Review.

### B587 ÔÇö Multi-select in the default pointer: Ctrl/Ôîÿ + Shift-click modifiers + multi-selection operations `[Site Planner + Document Review / shared selection]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-1"; minted **B587** = highest real B# across both files (B586) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered repeatedly from a provisional B569 as fast-moving concurrent `main`s kept taking the next ID (the whole B568ÔÇôB586 band: building grid, Road authority B571, scheduler rounds B585ÔÇª); the branch `claude/multi-select-default-pointer-aycvyr`, its commits, PR #442, and in-code `(B569)` labels keep the provisional number)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session ÔÇö built ON TOP of the existing pointer (not a rebuild), in a shared selection model. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Audit result (the gate the brief demanded ÔÇö done FIRST, 6-agent parallel audit):** the Site Planner ALREADY had a working N-object selection set (`multi`), Shift-click toggle, marquee (Shift-drag), multi-move (`groupMove`), and multi-delete-as-one-undo ÔÇö so this was NOT a rebuild. Document Review had **single-select only**. There was **no shared selection model**. So the real work was: (a) the genuinely-shareable LOGIC into one pure module; (b) Ctrl/Ôîÿ toggle (new); (c) the neutral hue-free chrome (was brand-accent); (d) bring Document Review up to parity; (e) extend the Site Planner set to cover measurements.
- **Shared model (the "don't write two implementations" ask):** new pure `src/shared/markup/selection.js` ÔÇö `pickInMarquee` (crossing/window box-test), `nextSelection` (Ctrl/Ôîÿ = toggle, Shift = additive add, plain = replace), `selMods`, `cornerGrips` ÔÇö + `src/shared/markup/SelectionChrome.jsx` (the ONE neutral chrome visual). Both workspaces consume them. **Flagged contradiction, resolved honestly:** a single shared selection STATE isn't possible without rebuilding each canvas's pointer (which the brief forbids), so the *logic/visual* is shared and the *state/wiring* stays per-host (different coordinate systems: feet vs PDF page-units). 16 new unit tests in `test/selection.test.js`.
- **Modifiers (always-on in the default pointer):** Ctrl/Ôîÿ-click toggles one object in/out without clearing the rest; Shift-click additively adds; an empty set seeds from the current single selection so click-A-then-Ctrl-click-B gives {A,B}. (No ordered-list surface exists, so Shift = add on the canvas, not range-select ÔÇö confirmed during audit.)
- **Neutral chrome:** new hue-free `--sel-casing`/`--sel-line` tokens (palette.js + index.css, both themes; not in the contrast-audit PAIRS so CI is unaffected) ÔÇö a light casing under a dark line + solid corner grips, legible on aerial imagery, never colliding with a status/accent hue. Replaced the old `PAL.accent` dashed marquee/multi outlines.
- **Operations:** multi-move (drag any member moves the set, real-world coords preserved) + multi-delete as one undo. Site Planner set now covers **drawn elements + markups + measurements** (measures added to the marquee pick, group-move, multi-delete, arrow-nudge, and chrome); **parcels keep their own merge interaction ÔÇö the one sanctioned divergence.** Document Review gained the whole stack (selSet + group-move + multi-delete, plus a safety-net effect that prunes the set to current-page existing markups). Batch property edit is the documented follow-on (not v1).
- **Adversarial review (1 agent over the full diff) caught one real bug ÔÇö fixed + regression-tested:** marquee-selecting a LONE measurement wrote an id-keyed `sel`, but the legacy single-measure paths are index-keyed (`sel.i`), so that one measure couldn't be deleted, highlighted, or vertex-edited. Fixed with `measureSelRef` (a lone measure pick ÔåÆ index form) + `seedMulti` converting an index-keyed measure `sel` to its id ref when seeding the multi set. The other four flagged areas verified correct.
- **Verified:** `npm run lint` **0 errors** ┬À full suite **1874 green** (16 new) ┬À `npm run build` green (SelectionChrome is its own shared lazy chunk; planner/review chunks intact) ┬À headless **PASSED logged-out, 20/20**: `ui-audit/verify-multiselect.mjs` (Site Planner **12/12**: marquee tool + lit feedback + neutral chrome + multi-move + multi-delete + single-undo + Ctrl-click toggle + the lone-measure regression) and `ui-audit/verify-multiselect-docreview.mjs` (Document Review **8/8** over the fixture PDF: rail tool + lit + marquee select + multi-delete). The signed-in confirm that a multi-move/delete on a CLOUD-loaded site/review survives reload (rides the unchanged autosave; sandbox is logged-out) is **V182**. Single-select keeps its existing resize/rotate handles (richer than grips); only multi-select + marquee use the neutral chrome (deliberate).
- **Dedup:** distinct from B443/B420 (parcel Shift-merge ÔÇö left divergent, as the brief sanctions) and B423/B431 (the shared markup ENGINE ÔÇö this adds the shared SELECTION layer it never had). Depends-on/ships-with **B588** (the marquee tool).

### B590 ÔÇö Column-line spacing on deep cross-docks was uneven (skinny/fat centre bay) `[Site Planner]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-30 on planyr.io ÔÇö two cross-dock buildings (634ÔÇ▓/651ÔÇ▓ deep) showed an irregular middle column; minted **B590** = highest real B# across both files (B589) + 1; branch `claude/building-doors-gridlines-5rawpy`)*
`[x]` **Fixed this session ÔÇö interior bays now size UNIFORMLY within the band. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Repro:** the B568 "fixed interior + flex the leftover into one bay" model pinned every interior depth bay to exactly 50ÔÇ▓ and dumped the remainder into a single CENTRE bay on cross-docks. For a 634ÔÇ▓-deep box (minus two 60ÔÇ▓ speed bays = 514ÔÇ▓ middle) that gave nine 50ÔÇ▓ bays + a **64ÔÇ▓** fat centre; a 651ÔÇ▓ box gave a **~31ÔÇ▓** skinny centre. Either way the middle column read as a mistake.
- **Fix (`lib/buildingGrid.js` `divideSpan`):** restored UNIFORM interior bays ÔÇö pick the bay COUNT whose equal size lands in the band [50,58] nearest the target, so all interior columns are evenly spaced (the 60ÔÇ▓ speed bay stays the only deliberately-different bay). 634ÔÇ▓ÔåÆ ten equal 51.4ÔÇ▓ bays; 651ÔÇ▓ÔåÆ ten equal 53.1ÔÇ▓ bays. Only a narrow "gap" span with no in-band uniform division falls back to the module + one flex bay (the rare case). Doc/comment/settings text updated; the per-building override + door logic are unchanged.
- **Verified:** `npm run lint` **0 errors** ┬À full suite **2065 green** (33 in `buildingGrid.test.js`, incl. a deep-cross-dock-uniform-middle case asserting the 514/531 middles come out all-equal & in-band) ┬À `npm run build` green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b568-b569-grid.mjs` updated to a 634ÔÇ▓-deep cross-dock and **all checks pass** (28 uniform interior lines, 2 speed lines, screenshot confirms even spacing). Pure canvas ÔåÆ nothing pending in `VERIFICATION.md`.

### B568 ÔÇö Auto-generate the structural column grid + dock doors on a drawn building `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-1"; minted **B568** = highest real B# across both files (B567) + 1 ÔÇö the B3340/B5263/B25431 hits in BACKLOG-DONE.md are CSS hex colours, not IDs; branch `claude/building-doors-gridlines-5rawpy`)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session ÔÇö pure-lib grid + render + settings/overrides + door spacing, all verified. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **What the owner gets:** draw a building and it now shows its **structural column grid** + **dock doors** automatically, the way spec industrial is actually framed. Off each dock face is a **speed bay** (the staging strip ÔÇö default **60ÔÇ▓**, the one number the owner cares most about). Behind it the interior bays are **UNIFORM, sized within the 50ÔÇô58ÔÇ▓ band toward the typical** (default **56ÔÇ▓** along the docks ┬À **50ÔÇ▓** deep) so the columns are evenly spaced. **Length and depth typical sizes are independent** (real buildings run e.g. **56ÔÇ▓ ├ù 50ÔÇ▓**, exactly the owner's case). Cross-dock mirrors a speed bay to **both** long walls and divides the middle uniformly. *(Spacing model: see B590 ÔÇö the owner caught a too-rigid version that put a skinny/fat odd column in the middle of deep cross-docks; interior bays are now uniform-in-band.)*
- **Geometry (new pure lib `lib/buildingGrid.js`, dependency-free):** `computeBuildingGrid({length, depth, dock, grid})` ÔåÆ building-LOCAL column-line offsets (`lengthLines` / `depthLines`, each tagged `speed` | `flex` | `std`), bay lists, and a panel summary. `divideSpan` sizes UNIFORM interior bays within the band toward the target (the count whose uniform size is nearest the target); only a narrow "gap" span with no in-band uniform division falls back to a module + one flex bay (ends/rear/centre). `resolveGridSettings(el, settings)` resolves the plan defaults under per-building overrides (the existing `ÔÇªOverride` pattern). 33 unit tests (`test/buildingGrid.test.js`): uniform-in-band sizing, deep-cross-dock uniform middle, speed-bay placement, cross-dock symmetry + too-shallow guard, independent length vs depth targets, dock-`none`, degenerate depth, out-of-band-target safety, gap-span fallback, empty footprint.
- **Render (in `renderElPx`):** maps the local offsets onto the element's axis-aligned frame (the existing rot/ppf transform handles rotation) ÔÇö interior lines **solid 0.5px neutral gray**, the **speed-bay line a stronger solid coral** (a literal drafting hue, deliberately NOT a `--status-*` / `--accent-*` token), flex boundaries **dashed**, the **dock wall the heaviest edge**. Whole grid **zoom-gated** on the rendered footprint px (the `FEAT_BTN_MIN_PX` precedent) so it reveals when legible and never clutters zoomed out. Rectangle footprints only (polygon `el.points` buildings stay out of scope v1, like the docks); never drawn on a dog-ear bump-out.
- **Settings + overrides + readout:** plan-wide **Structural grid** section (showGrid, speed bay, typ bay length/depth, band min/max, door W / o.c.) + a per-building **Column grid** override group in the inspector. The building panel now reads **"Column grid: 56ÔÇ▓ ├ù 50ÔÇ▓ typ ┬À speed bay 60ÔÇ▓ ┬À N ├ù M bays"** and the door line tracks the configured o.c.
- **Door spacing now configurable (was a hardcoded 12ÔÇ▓):** `doorOC` / `doorWidth` settings; the count tracks o.c.; the dog-ear skip + dock-side logic are unchanged.
- **Adversarial review (8-agent workflow) found 5 real defects ÔÇö all fixed this session:** (1, high) an out-of-band per-building bay target drove a NEGATIVE residual bay + off-building column lines ÔÇö `resolveGridSettings` now clamps the targets into the band and `divideSpan` clamps the effective target; (2) `divideSpan` threw / emitted NaN when called without the band ÔÇö it now defaults + sanitises the band; (3) the renderer read raw `el.dockSide` while the axes came from the validated `footprintAxes`, so an axis-flipping resize desynced the grid 90┬░ ÔÇö both now read the validated side from `dockSidesFor`; (4) the grid/readout defaulted a dock-less building to `single` while the rest of the app defaults `cross` ÔÇö standardised to `cross`; (5) the door-count readout overcounted vs the canvas when a dog-ear sat on the dock face ÔÇö both now route through one shared `dockDoorRun` helper (dog-ear skip included). +5 regression tests.
- **Verified (fully, here ÔÇö no signed-in path owed; the grid is pure canvas, identical logged-in or out):** `npm run lint` **0 errors** ┬À full suite **1883 green** (+32 new) ┬À `npm run build` green ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b568-b569-grid.mjs` **12/12** (3 scenarios): grid + speed line(s) + door leaves render on single-load (1 speed line, 24 doors), cross-dock (2 speed lines = mirror symmetry, 48 doors), AND a stale-`dockSide` axis-flip case (still coherent); the grid vanishes when zoomed out below the gate. Nothing pending in `VERIFICATION.md`.
- **Defaults VALIDATED against the owner's real plans (2026-06-30, via Google Drive).** Measured the **Grand Port** approved arch set (1,005,560 SF cross-dock, 40ÔÇ▓ clear): **56ÔÇ▓** is the single dominant grid dimension (~130 callouts) and the slab plan literally labels a **60ÔÇ▓ SPEED BAY** ÔÇö so length-target 56ÔÇ▓ + speed-bay 60ÔÇ▓ are confirmed dead-on. Depth bays read ~45ÔÇô50ÔÇ▓ (the 50ÔÇ▓ depth default sits at the top of that range; an optional band-floor drop to ~45ÔÇ▓ is logged in OWNER-TODO as a product call). Pinnacle/Urban Logistics buildings are small (~85k SF) and Goose Creek's set is 141 MB (beyond the Drive text reader) ÔÇö neither alters the conclusion. Defaults ship unchanged.

### B569 ÔÇö Snap dock doors so a column never lands on an opening `[Site Planner]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-30 as "NEW-2"; minted **B569** = B568 + 1; same branch)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session alongside B568. *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **What it does:** a column line must never bisect a dock-door opening (the real-world rule that the column grid and dock spacing are coordinated). `placeDockDoors(from, to, lengthLines, {doorOC, doorWidth})` (in `lib/buildingGrid.js`) partitions the dock wall by the interior column lines and drops doors at o.c. spacing **centred within each clear sub-bay**, leaving half a leaf clear of each column ÔÇö so openings fall **between** columns. Built only against B568's grid output (never re-deriving geometry). The canvas door leaves + the panel door count both flow through it, so the count honestly reflects the column-avoidance gaps.
- **Verified:** covered by the same 27 unit tests (no door leaf straddles a column; wider o.c. ÔåÆ fewer doors; empty when no room) + the `verify-b568-b569-grid.mjs` headless render. Massing-level approximation, as scoped.

### B566 ÔÇö Schedule: replace the floppy "Save" button with the unified cloud save-status badge (auto-save parity with Site Planner) `[Scheduler / Persistence]` (feature)  *(owner-dropped 2026-06-29 as "NEW-1"; minted **B566** = highest real B# across both files (B565) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered after provisional B562/B565 both collided with concurrent main items; branch `claude/scheduling-auto-save-parity-dlg65g`)*
`[x]` **Shipped this session ÔÇö presentation swap, not a data-loss rebuild (the audit gate came back: Schedule ALREADY auto-saves). *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Audit result (the gate the request demanded ÔÇö done FIRST):** the Schedule embedded Gantt app (`public/sequence/index.html`) is **NOT** manual-Save-only. It already has the full Site-Planner persistence contract: debounced cloud auto-save (`attemptCloudSave`, save on edit ÔÇö never waits for a button), an optimistic-concurrency `__rev`/`knownRev` version guard, a synchronous local mirror + flush on tab close/visibility, retry-on-error, and a LOUD `saveStatus==="error"` surface ("Cloud auto-save is always on"). The floppy "Save" button (the lifted `SaveButton` in `scheduler/components/ScheduleToolbar.jsx`) was already only a **tri-state status indicator** that the owner reads as a manual Save. So removing it is **safe** ÔÇö this is a pure UI re-skin, the persistence engine is untouched.
- **Change:** the Schedule workspace now shows the **same shared `CloudSyncBadge`** (cloud glyph: synced Ô£ô / saving Ôåæ / error Ôâá) in **AppHeader Row-1 top-right** ÔÇö the identical component + slot the Site Planner uses ÔÇö instead of the floppy in the Row-2 toolbar. New pure `scheduler/lib/saveState.js` (`scheduleSaveState`) maps the embedded app's already-bridged `toolbar.saveStatus` (saved/saving/error) onto the badge's vocabulary; `Scheduler.jsx` passes it as `saveState` to `AppHeader` + wires `onRetrySave ÔåÆ post planar:save` (the badge's "Retry now" re-attempts the cloud write). The floppy `SaveButton` was deleted from `ScheduleToolbar.jsx`. **No third badge minted ÔÇö reuse, per the dedup ask.**
- **Nothing lost:** the floppy did double duty (saved ÔåÆ click linked a local `.html` backup file via `openFile()`; error ÔåÆ retry). Retry is preserved by the badge popover. The **"Link a local backup file"** affordance was **relocated into the embedded Settings panel** (reachable in-shell via the lifted ÔÜÖ) so it isn't orphaned. The standalone `/sequence/` page keeps its own save button unchanged.
- **Guardrail honored (never a false-green):** `scheduleSaveState` returns `null` (badge hidden) until the iframe reports, and an unknown status maps to nothing rather than "synced". **An adversarial review (8-agent workflow) caught one real false-green path and it's fixed:** a failed initial cloud READ sets the embedded app's `offlineFallback` while `saveStatus` still reads its initial "saved" ÔÇö which would have shown a green "Synced" badge over the app's own red "working offline" banner. `offlineFallback` is now bridged through `planar:toolbar-state` and maps to the honest amber **"offline"** badge state (a live write still correctly wins ÔåÆ saving/error). Status colors only (no Schedule accent `#7F77DD` encoding save state). Added `data-testid="cloud-sync-badge"` + `data-sync-state` to the shared badge so the B278 harness can assert Cloud-on on the scheduling path the same way as the planner.
- **Verified:** `npm run lint` 0 errors ┬À full suite **1800 green** (7 new in `test/schedulerSaveState.test.js`, incl. the loud-error- AND offline-never-false-green guardrails) ┬À `npm run build` green ┬À embedded JSX compiles (`jsxcheck-sequence.mjs`) ┬À `verify-new1-cloud-badge.mjs` 20/20 (shared badge intact with the new testid) ┬À `verify-b388-toolbar-lift.mjs` updated for the swap and confirms the floppy is **GONE** headless. **The live cross-iframe round-trip (cloud badge lighting up + Settings backup link) needs the Schedule's own Supabase, unreachable in the sandbox ÔåÆ V174.**

### B498 ÔÇö Dog-ear bump-outs don't re-anchor on host resize ÔåÆ overlap the building `[Site Planner / Site Model]` (bug)  *(owner-found 2026-06-26 on planyr.io reading the live element model, provisional "NEW-6"; minted **B498** = highest real B# across both files (B497) + 1; branch `claude/dog-ear-resize-overlap-v4yvit`)*
`[x]` **Fixed + headless-verified this session (model-layer self-heal on load). *Moves to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.***
- **Repro (Jacintoport `smqdxst8pf3g`, Building 1):** host building `e8984` was widened ~27ÔÇ▓ (to 328.49ÔÇ▓) AFTER its two right-side corner bump-outs (`e8986`/`e8987`, `dogEar{side:"right"}`) were placed flush to the old 301.5ÔÇ▓ edge. The bumps never re-anchored, so each froze at `cx 4.19` ÔÇö inner edge `ÔêÆ25.81`, **13.5ÔÇ▓ INSIDE** the host's current right edge (`ÔêÆ12.32`), straddling the truck-court seam. The truck-court child *did* re-anchor; only the dog-ears were skipped (a B362-era gap ÔÇö dog-ear re-anchoring was added to the live `refitChildren` path AFTER this host was widened).
- **Fix:** `createSiteModel`'s bonded-child normalizer (the B363 rotation-repair pass in `lib/siteModel.js`) now also **re-flushes each `dogEar` child's whole box to the host's CURRENT edge** via the pure `dogEarGeom(host, descriptor)` ÔÇö the same function that places it live ÔÇö so a stored record self-heals on load (no migration). Idempotent + churn-free (a correctly-anchored bump re-derives to itself ÔåÆ same object reference back); a user-resized bump's stored span (`along/proj`) is preserved, a bare legacy tag recovers its current rendered size from the box so only POSITION moves. New `isDogEarSide` guard in `lib/dogEar.js` means a tampered `side` can't throw and blank the planner; finite host/child w┬Àh required.
- **Verified:** `npm run lint` **0 errors** ┬À full suite **1690 green** (6 new NEW-6 tests in `test/bondedRotation.test.js`, incl. an assertion on the EXACT Jacintoport stored numbers ÔåÆ re-anchored `cx 17.678`, inner edge on the host edge, zero overlap) ┬À `npm run build` green.
- **ÔÅ│ One signed-in check (V156):** load the real Jacintoport site on planyr.io signed-in, confirm Building 1's right-side bumps sit flush to the building edge (no overlap into the truck court). The sandbox is logged-out so the actual cloud record can't be loaded here ÔÇö the unit test stands in with its exact stored values.
- **Sweep spun out ÔåÆ B499.** The "bonded child follows the host on resize" guarantee is half-implemented on the LOAD path for OTHER child types (sidewalk / dock-zone stack / side-parking); no stale record observed, deferred with a blocker.
- **Dedup:** distinct from B362 (bump-out sizing) and B363 (bonded-rotation repair) ÔÇö this is the bonded-POSITION-on-resize gap neither covered.

### B491 ÔÇö Security pen-test pass (2026-06-27): cross-user PDF read in team storage + OAuth token-page hardening `[Security / RLS + edge]` (security)  *(owner-requested "exhaustive penetration tests"; 5 parallel surface audits ÔÇö edge Functions, edge proxies, OAuth, Supabase RLS, client XSS/secrets; minted **B491** = highest real B# (B490) + 1 ÔÇö renumbered from a provisional **B488** a concurrent `main` (#377) took for the PDF-viewer-quality audit; the original commit message still reads B488)*
`[x]` **Filed + the two real fixes shipped + lower-priority items triaged the SAME session (branch `claude/resume-planner-persistence-36zqh1`, PR into `main`).** Owner SQL/dashboard steps in `OWNER-TODO.md`.
- **Overall verdict (honest, not padding): the app is well-built.** Edge Functions verify a real Supabase JWT and derive the tenant uid from the *token* (no IDOR); the GIS/Mapillary proxies have a robust WHATWG-host allowlist (no SSRF, token only ever goes to the fixed Mapillary host); client XSS is neutralised by React auto-escaping + consistent `esc()`/`escapeHtml()` in the three string-templated SVG/HTML builders; no server-only secret reaches the bundle (the `sk-ant-`/`ANTHROPIC`/`refresh_token` hits in `dist/` are placeholders / SDK var-names / grant-field names); and the team **management/invite** RLS correctly blocks self-escalation (admin-gated invites, SECURITY DEFINER funcs pin `search_path`, recursion avoided). Most surfaces returned SAFE.
- **`[x]` SHIPPED ÔÇö Finding A (the one serious bug): cross-user PDF read via `can_read_shared_review_file` (`doc-review/db/team_storage.sql`).** The team-shared-file Storage SELECT policy granted read on any object whose path appeared in *some* team-shared review's `data->sources[].storageKey` ÔÇö but `data` is attacker-writable on one's OWN rows, so a user could create a review they own, share it to a team they're on, and list a **victim's** path (`<victim_uid>/ÔÇª`) to read the victim's private PDF (confused-deputy IDOR on the private `doc-review-files` bucket). **Fix:** require `(storage.foldername(p_name))[1] = r.user_id::text` so a readable object must belong to the review's true owner; a fabricated source can then only ever resolve to the attacker's own files, while legit shares still resolve. **Live impact today is gated** (needs the victim's exact path ÔÇö uid + project + discipline + a 7-char random `srcId` ÔÇö realistically known only to a *former* teammate; and no teams are live yet) ÔåÆ **run `team_storage.sql` before inviting anyone** (OWNER-TODO, with B486). HIGH severity if exploited; not actively exploitable by an outsider today.
- **`[x]` SHIPPED ÔÇö Finding B (hardening): OAuth consent callback (`functions/api/auth/google/callback.js`)** now HTML-escapes every interpolated value (the refresh token + Google's error strings) and sends `Cache-Control: no-store` + `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` on the one page that can render a secret. Pure-win, zero functional change. (These routes are a dormant one-time owner bootstrap ÔÇö the token is already minted and must NOT be re-minted ÔÇö so this is latent-risk reduction.)
- **Deliberately NOT auto-fixed (real but lower-priority / would risk regressions or are owner/infra actions ÔÇö left here, not shipped, to avoid bloat):**
  - **Email-confirmation trust (config).** Invite-claim trusts the JWT email as verified; if Supabase "Confirm email" were OFF, a user could claim a co-worker's invite. Primary fix = the dashboard setting (OWNER-TODO); a SQL `email_confirmed_at` gate on the signup trigger was NOT added (the trigger fires *before* confirmation, so gating it would break invite-at-signup ÔÇö needs careful design, deferred).
  - **OAuth CSRF `state` + caller authz** on the dormant consent routes (`start.js`/`callback.js` are unauthenticated). Standard hardening, but the routes shouldn't be exercised at all in steady state (don't re-mint the token), and a JWT gate on a top-level browser redirect is architecturally awkward (no Authorization header on a navigation). Deferred as latent-only.
  - **Optimistic-concurrency `version` reset.** A teammate can write an arbitrary `version` on a shared row, defeating the compare-and-swap anti-clobber guard (integrity, within-team only). A monotonic-`version` BEFORE-UPDATE trigger would fix it but risks breaking *all* saves if the client increment ever diverges ÔÇö deferred pending a safe rollout.
  - **`client_errors` telemetry:** anon INSERT has no rate-limit/size-cap (spam/cost; READ side is correctly closed ÔÇö no cross-user read). Length caps could silently drop large stacks ÔåÆ left to platform rate-limiting.
  - **Public proxy rate-limit** (`/api/mapillary/*`, `/api/gis-cache/*`): no code-level rate limit (quota-abuse ceiling only; targets are public agency hosts). Best as a Cloudflare WAF rule (infra, not code).
  - **`underlay.src` import scheme** not validated (a crafted import/shared plan could load a remote tracker image ÔÇö content-spoof/privacy, NOT XSS; React keeps it inert). Optional `^data:image/|^https?:` allowlist if teams share plans widely.
  - **Scheduler separate Supabase project RLS** (`public/sequence/index.html`, ref `ksetjztkplttbcehyicv`): its hardcoded key is confirmed **anon** (not service_role), but that project's table RLS can't be verified from the repo ÔåÆ owner confirm `planar_*` tables have RLS enabled (ties to the B408 consolidation decision).
- **Dedup:** net-new security item; the shipped Finding A is distinct from B486 (re-home guard) ÔÇö both are team-sharing RLS gaps to run before going live, different policies.

### B445 ÔÇö GIS layer imagery caching, server-side (Drive-backed, cross-device) `[Site Planner / GIS]` (feature) ÔÇö roadmap Track-1 #1; SHIPPED  *(2026-06-24; renumbered from B439 to clear a collision)*
`[x]` Government layer *pictures* (FEMA flood, wetlands, utilities) keep painting when the agency server is down, follow the user between devices, and are stored off the user's machine ÔÇö replacing the browser-side B438. The map points raster (export-image) layers at a same-origin Cloudflare Function `/api/gis-cache/*` that fetches the agency server-side (no CORS wall), keeps a durable copy in the existing Google Drive, refreshes in the background (stale-while-revalidate), and FAILS OPEN (302 ÔåÆ agency) on any miss-and-failure / missing creds / error. Client also one-shot falls back to the direct agency URL if the proxy isn't serving, so a layer always renders (caching is pure enhancement). Default ON; `VITE_GIS_PROXY=0` is the kill switch. Pure core `src/shared/gis/gisProxyCore.js` (svc-URL encode/parse + cache key + freshness, 15 tests) + testable handler `functions/api/gis-cache/_handler.js` (SWR/fail-open, 12 tests, in-memory Drive). Age badge via the proxy's `?meta=1` sidecar ÔåÆ existing onStatus `{ts,stale}` channel. Reuses the live Drive creds (`GOOGLE_*`, server-side only) ÔÇö no Supabase, no new secret. DONE 2026-06-24 (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`, PRs #328 + #329); see BACKLOG-DONE.md. **Bring-up fix (#329):** the gov host (`hazards.fema.gov`) 403s a bare server-side User-Agent ÔåÆ every request fail-opened (nothing cached); fixed by sending a browser UA on upstream fetches (root cause isolated live via a temporary `?diag=1` probe; the datacenter-IP-block fear was disproven). **Ô£à VERIFIED LIVE on planyr.io:** real FEMA export ÔåÆ 200 image/png and `?meta=1` ÔåÆ `cached:true` with a ts (fetch ÔåÆ Drive store ÔåÆ serve ÔåÆ age, end-to-end). Only an in-app visual glance remains (V129).

- `[x]` **B436 ÔÇö e2e: open a PDF in the per-tool rail-arm specs so Section B actually executes.** DONE 2026-06-24 (branch `claude/determined-shannon-p7unj4`). (see BACKLOG-DONE.md)

### B360 ÔÇö Title-block intelligence: unify the readers, expand the discipline taxonomy, tune on the real corpus (V79 filing + V67 scale) `[Doc Review]` (task)  *(2026-06-21; filed B356, renumbered **B360** ÔÇö `main` #268 took B356ÔÇôB359 while in flight)*
`[x]` **Done + tuned on the owner's real drawings ÔÇö merging via PR #270 (branch `claude/bold-cori-okbxu5`).** The Drive connector was re-authed to michael@planyr.io mid-session, so the empirical tuning ran here (no second session needed). 1078 tests, lint 0, build green, lazy chunks intact.

**Unify (one reader, one extractor).** Relocated `parseSheetScale` ÔåÆ `src/shared/files/sheetScale.js` (re-exported from `overlayScale.js` for back-compat); `sheetMeta` imports it from shared (removes the sharedÔåÆworkspace import). `readTitleBlockText` returns `scale` in the same pass; `readSheetMeta` consumes that one read. `firstPagesText` folded into `doc-review/lib/pdf.js`. Civil-only `parseScaleNote` untouched.

**Scale-reader bug (V67).** `parseSheetScale` returned null on an architectural scale when a number (a date) was printed right before it (`ÔÇª10/24/2025  1/8"=1'-0"`) ÔÇö the mixed-number branch swallowed the date digits + fraction. Fixed with `(?<!\d)` + a Ôëñ2-digit mixed whole-inch.

**Discipline taxonomy (owner decision 2026-06-21).** Added dedicated buckets in the owner's order: Architectural, Structural, Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing, Landscape, Fire Alarm, Fire Sprinkler (+ Survey/Environmental/Geotech/CAD/Other). Fire split (Alarm vs Sprinkler); Structural/M/E/P out of "Other"/"MEP". Canonical list now in `titleBlockParse.js`, re-exported by `reviewStore` (+ the walled-off server reader kept in lockstep) so the reader / store / filing-UI can't drift.

**Corpus tuning** (`ui-audit/lib/filingScore.mjs` + `score-filing.mjs`; Jacintoport + Mesa sets read via the Drive connector, ground truth = the descriptive filename). Reader fixes the real sheets drove:
- `classifyDiscipline` ÔåÆ WEIGHTED dominance (a definitive sheet-type like "floor plan"/"foundation plan" Ôë½ a bare cross-reference name like "structural"); dropped bare "elevation(s)" as a keyword (a structural sheet is full of spot elevations). Fixes a real STRUCTURAL set misfiled Civil (deep stray "grading") **and** the inverse ARCH case.
- `findDates` requires a consistent separator ÔåÆ a dimension "5-29/32" no longer parses as 2032 and poisons the latest-date pick.
- `parseRevision` maps "ISSUE FOR CONSTRUCTION" (no "D") ÔåÆ IFC, and no longer reads the heading "REVISIONS" as "Rev S".
**Result (8 readable sheets): project 8/8, discipline 6/7, date 3/6, revision 3/5.** Remaining misses are ground-truth nuances (a sheet's latest revision date vs the owner's package date; a mixed-revision "make-ready" package) or genuine data gaps (image-only scans with no text layer) ÔÇö not reader bugs. Real-snippet unit tests lock every fix in.

**Remaining tail (separate, not a regression):** image-only / scanned sets (e.g. Mesa Plumbing/Electrical have ~no text layer) and the `.dwg` files can't be Tier-1 text-read ÔÇö they fall to the already-built-but-dormant Tier-2 AI/OCR (B299/B352) + the DWGÔåÆDXF server path. Eligible to move to BACKLOG-DONE on merge.

### B121 ÔÇö Site element labels and dimension callouts overlap into an unreadable pile `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) ÔÇö Ô£à DONE 2026-07-02 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
Final tranche shipped (increments 1 / 2a / 2b landed earlier ÔÇö the shared `lib/labelLayout.js` collision + level-of-detail engine, zoom-gating, and leader lines). This session closed the remaining owner re-report:
- **Edge-dimension callouts folded into the collision pool.** The red per-edge dimension numbers (drawn in `renderElPx`) are now tested against the committed centred-label boxes and **HIDDEN when they would overprint one** ÔÇö resolving the "red dimension piling onto the Building name / sf" pile-up. New pure helpers: `dimNumberBox` (`lib/dimSlide.js` ÔÇö the screen box of the number, mirroring renderElPx's anchor / nudge / posF / offset math exactly) + `suppressedDimIds` (`lib/labelLayout.js`); `layoutLabels` now also returns each placement's committed `box`. Computed in the collision pass, threaded into `renderElPx` as a `dimSuppressed` set.
- **Preserves B592** (the dimension stays ON the footprint, slides along the length, NO leader) and the selected-element edit-handle exception ÔÇö we only HIDE an overlapping dimension, never move or leader it. The older B121 ask to pull dimension text off its line with a leader was **superseded by B592 and deliberately NOT built**.
- **"Show dimensions" + "Show areas" toggles** (default ON) added to `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` + the ÔÜÖ Setup panel so a crowded layout can shed the dimension tier or the sf/acre tier. `showAreas` drops only the area line ÔÇö name, dimension line, and the pond "Holds ÔÇª ac-ft" storage line stay.
- Verified: **2215 unit tests green** (new `dimNumberBox` / `suppressedDimIds` / `buildingLabelLines`-falsy / `layoutLabels`-box tests) ┬À lint 0 errors ┬À build green + `SitePlannerApp` lazy chunk intact ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-b121-b148.mjs` (14 checks): dims render, **0 dimÔåölabel overlaps at fit AND zoomed-in**, `showDims` / `showAreas` gate the render, the Setup panel shows both checkboxes, and unchecking "Show dimensions" live-hides the callouts (3 ÔåÆ 0). Screenshot eyeballed.

### B148 ÔÇö Site-summary readout renders as an oversized world-space banner over the map `[Site Planner / UI]` (bug) ÔÇö Ô£à RESOLVED (not reproducible) 2026-07-02 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
Closed after a full code trace + headless verification: there is **no world-space summary banner and no zoom-scaled font anywhere** on the planner canvas ÔÇö the canvas is screen-projected via `f2p` (no global zoom transform), and a grep for a ppf-scaled font-size returns zero hits. The site totals (SF / acreage / perimeter) live in the fixed-size **Site Yield side panel** (`SitePlanner.jsx`) + the print sheet ÔÇö neither balloons. The "stroked-orange monospace" the report described is the **Area measurement tool** (a user-drawn measure), locked at `fontSize=12` at the polygon centroid, which git history shows was always fixed-size. Headless proof (`ui-audit/verify-b121-b148.mjs`): on-canvas text height held **CONSTANT (42.0px ÔåÆ 42.0px)** while the drawing grew 490px ÔåÆ 1522px on zoom-in ÔÇö a world-space text would have scaled with the drawing. The only *unbuilt* reading was an optional on-canvas viewport summary chip (an enhancement, not the bug); **owner declined it this pass (2026-07-02)** ÔÇö a future session can pick it up as net-new, reusing `lib/sheetFurniture.js` `screenFurniturePlates` (the scale-bar / north-arrow corner furniture).

### B490 ÔÇö PDF viewer: optional-content (OCG) layer toggle `[Doc Review / Markup]` (feature) ÔÇö Ô£à DONE 2026-07-03 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
A "Ôûñ Layers" control in the Review toolbar shows/hides a PDF's optional-content groups (e.g. turn off Electrical to see just the site plan). New pure `lib/ocg.js` (`ocgLayerList` + `deriveLayerVisibility`) iterates pdf.js's OptionalContentConfig ÔÇö v6 has **no `getGroups()`**, so it uses the `[id, group]` iterator; `renderInto` threads the retained config via `optionalContentConfigPromise`; `DocReview` extracts the config on load (`readOcg`, guarded on `pdfRef` against a superseded load), lists the groups in a **portaled `AnchoredMenu`** popover (escapes the toolbar row's `overflow:hidden` clip ÔÇö a hand-rolled popover was clipped), and re-rasters both layers on toggle. Visibility is **in-memory** (a view filter, never the immutable markups). **Required a pdf.js polyfill:** the modern ESM build calls `Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed` (a TC39 proposal) but ÔÇö unlike the legacy build ÔÇö doesn't bundle it, so `getOptionalContentConfig()` threw; added a guarded polyfill in `lib/pdf.js`.
Verified: new `test/ocg.test.js` + full suite **2225** green ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green + DocReview lazy chunk intact ┬À headless `ui-audit/verify-ocg.mjs` (**8 checks**, hand-authored 2-layer OCG fixture `e2e/fixtures/sample-ocg.pdf` via `ui-audit/make-sample-ocg-pdf.mjs`): the Layers button appears, the popover lists Electrical+Plumbing and is genuinely **visible (not clipped)**, unchecking Electrical re-rasters redÔåÆ0 while blue persists, re-checking restores it, no page errors. Screenshot eyeballed.

### B489 ÔÇö PDF viewer crispness: sub-pixel seam + continuous-pan re-raster + useSystemFonts `[Doc Review / Markup]` (task) ÔÇö Ô£à DONE 2026-07-03 (batch `claude/backlog-review-batch-vfz167`)
All three held-back refinements from the B488 audit shipped:
- **Sub-pixel seam** ÔÇö `renderInto` now returns the **device-rounded** region in page units (`region:{rx:ox/S,ÔÇª}`, via a new pure `deviceRect` in `renderBudget.js`), and `DocReview.renderDetail` builds the detail tile from `d.region` (not `reg.rect`). The detail canvas's pageÔåÆCSS factor is now exactly `1/density` at origin `ox/density`, matching the backdrop ÔåÆ the Ôëñ0.5px seam/ghosting on thin lines is gone. Proven by algebra in `renderBudget.test.js` (round-trip idempotence + the density-match; the OLD unrounded region provably did NOT display at `density`).
- **Continuous-pan re-raster** ÔÇö a leading-edge throttle (~140ms, pinch-excluded) on the settle effect re-rasters the crisp detail window mid-pan, not only on settle; self-limiting via the existing `tileCovers` no-op + superseded-render cancellation.
- **useSystemFonts:false** ÔÇö set in `loadPdf`'s `getDocument`, so non-embedded fonts render from the shipped `standardFontDataUrl` substitutes (consistent across machines); harmless to the text-extraction callers.
Verified: **2225 unit tests** green ┬À lint 0 ┬À build green. The precise sub-pixel-seam look + pan-feel + font fidelity are logged as **V197** for a browser-equipped eyeball (the harness proves the math + that the paths fire, not the pixel-perfect visual).

### B160 ÔÇö Gantt horizontal width control on PDF/Print Exhibit export `[Scheduler]` (feature) ÔÇö Ô£à REDUNDANT (covered by B392) ÔÇö closed 2026-07-03 during the backlog sweep
